Downtown is not good, and any area outside of Vestal is crime and poverty-stricken or downright dangerous if you end up on the wrong side. Great school — terrible environment.

If they fixed their websites technology or literally anything they would be worth so much more but the technical problems alone make it just the worst thing in the world to deal with. Registering for classes and housing and all of that is harder than some of the classes just because of how much of a nightmare their system is. It is an actual dumpster fire and it is ridiculous that its still like this just pathetic. The school itself has not faults, but around 30% of your time just figuring out how to access anything because it's just an absolute nightmare.
I enjoy my time learning and strengthening my future at Binghamton and have refered this college to many of my friends to come join.

All around, a great school with incredible value. A great pick for people going for a grad degree who want to save on their undergrad. Academics are really good; there's lots to do whether or not you like parties, and there's a genuine sense of community.

Really great academics, everyone is very friendly. Some amazing professors and some not so amazing. overall very well-known and could have good benefits after graduation.
I was out-of-state which some people said you live a different experience, however if you put yourself out there it's very easy to make friends and find your people.
Well-regarded as a party school, the parties are decent, but definitely not as hyped up as they used to be. Additionally, the Downtown scene can be very fun and much people prefer it over the parties.
Food isn't bad, the meal plan is just very limited money regards, even if you try to budget it's really impossible to stay within the schedule.

ai_summary: Biola University is often praised for its strong Christian community and faith-based focus that permeates academics and student life. Many reviews highlight the supportive and caring professors who take the time to connect with students, fostering a welcoming and intimate learning environment . The campus itself is described as beautiful and safe, contributing to the overall positive experience. Student life includes a variety of activities, though some feel more engagement opportunities, especially on weekends, would enhance the experience. While students appreciate the small class sizes and immersive programs, some express concerns about the cost of tuition and the quality of cafeteria food. Overall, Biola is characterized by a nurturing community that encourages academic, spiritual, and personal growth, though opinions on the diversity and vibrancy of campus life vary.

reviews.text: Biola is an amazing University to attend. The environment there is very positive and friendly. With amazing staff who are helpful with any questions you may have. While staying focused on your school life you also get the opportunity to maintain a close relationship with the Lord attending worship services on campus. Biola University also has a variety of academics to chose from. You are able to explore many options you find passionate about.

I would say that Biola University can truly be a worthwhile investment for a prospective student who has a love for God and would like to deepen their faith in Him through higher education. One of Biola's strengths is its theocentric, like-minded, safe community of learners of various majors, as the university emphasizes spiritually equipping its students in character and intellectually equipping them for ministry. The institution's student-to-faculty ratios are overall smaller, which facilitates students' learning and fosters relationships with their professors. Biola offers leading degree programs in various fields, such as business, the sciences, and theology, through its eight schools, evidencing that the institution amounts to a credible, trustworthy, and respectable choice for one's college education. It also enforces and monitors each student's chapel attendance, in striving for a faithful balance between their spirituality and studies.

I found it very faith driven on every subject and topic. I think that the professors really cared about you and what they taught. The food could be better and the housing could be bigger, but the love and respect everyone treats each other with is unmatched.

This has been some of the best community I could have hoped for. The professors care deeply about you as a student and as a person. The campus is built to help one grow spiritually and mentally physically and emotionally in your walk of life!
Biola was a great school, they offered a great integrated education. The professors were amazing and always created great relationships with their students.

Attending Biola was life changing for me and an incredible experience. I recommend Biola to any upcoming students or even graduates considering grad school. I even send friends job postings at Biola because it's a great place to work. The community, the environment, and the campus make it what it is. If you are hoping to grow closer to the Lord, find strong Christian community, and be cared for by professors, mentors, and friends, Biola is for you!
Coming to this school has really helped my faith and academic journey. As someone who thinks faith is very important along with academics this school is perfect!

I loved how my welcoming experience was exciting. The staff at Biola did such a great job at welcoming students and setting up different ways to get to know our peers. It was har for me at first to get used to be away from home, but the atmosphere here has a way to make you feel you are at home. I worked with some of the staff at my part time job, and you can see how passionate the staff is about the students and the school. I loved the clubs, I recommend doing that in order to get into groups of people.

Biola was traumatic.
Lonely, isolated, secular, divided.
My time at Biola was the worst thing that has ever happened to me. There are nights I have nightmares, because of how estranged I felt from the school from the moment of orientation until two years later when I finally made the best decision to leave. Not once did I feel welcomed. The dorms are isolating, discombobulating. If you are coming out of state, or country, prepare to be alone. Prepare to be alone on holidays. Prepare for professors to be annoyed by your existence. Prepare for no one to care about your spiritual walk, soul. If you don’t attend enough, you will get in trouble. You sign a document promising to not drink/smoke/watch porn/practice intercourse….but everyone does. In fact, you can pee in the elevators, black out drunk and vomit every morning in the bathrooms and no one will notice! No one is watching out for you, no one cares for you, no one will help you if you need help.

I am a Freshman at Biola and live in the dorms. I love the classes and community! The problem I have is the food. It is horrible! I feel sick every time I eat in the cafeteria and have already lost ten pounds. Unfortunately, there are no other outside food vendors on the campus. Only $150 flex dollars, for the entire semester, can be used at the small deli Eagle's Nest and coffee shop. Last year, the Journalism department wrote an article about the issues with the Cafeteria and food service, but apparently no one listened. Last week, there were maggots in the salad bar and 2 of my friends got horribly sick with food poisoning due to under cooked pork. Most of us just eat the pizza (who wants chicken curry?) As much as I love Biola, I cannot imagine eating like this for four years! If Biola is not going to fix the food situation, then they need to do away with the mandatory minimum meal plan so we can get good food elsewhere. It will be a shame if I have to transfer over the food issue!
This is my first year at Biola and so far I love it. The transition felt really smooth and everyone is so friendly. There are so many activities going on all the time and clubs to join or even start.

Biola University is a great college! It's a little expensive, but the campus life, thoughtful professors, spirit-enriching classes, amazing chapels, and transformative experiences it offers makes it so, so, so worth it!
Biola has really helped me make sure I was academically successful. I have struggled over the years and they offered pastoral care to get me through it to make sure I seen graduation

Biola university is such an amazing school, it has such a good environment with such good welcoming people. it’s easy to find friends because everyone is so caring and we’re all their for the same thing to grow in our faith, that’s one thing everyone has in common there. it’s overall such an amazing school to be at

Biola is a very quiet school, I feel like not much is ever really happening there. Everyone seems to themselves pretty much, the teachers are really good though. It honestly just depends, i have had my fair share of good and bad professors each semester I have been a student there. The food is pretty bad, they always come up with something weird, but the breakfast is good most of the time. As for parties, there are little boring ones every now and then, the main parties are usually at the colleges baseball house, they are usually just small parties for people who play sports. I really just overall like how supportive the teachers are, and how helpful everyone is, as well as having that welcoming community. I would like for teachers to listen to reviews through.

ai_summary: At Boise State University, students have a mixed experience regarding academics and faculty. While some praise the diverse range of programs and supportive professors , others express frustration over ineffective teaching methods and lack of organization. The campus is noted for its beauty and size, with plenty of resources available, including a free food pantry and helpful student services like advising and tutoring. Student life is lively, with a strong school spirit and a variety of clubs and extracurricular activities that foster a robust community. Some students face challenges with parking and tuition costs being high, but many appreciate the flexibility of online programs , particularly for working students. Overall, Boise serves as a friendly and engaging environment, making it a worthwhile option for prospective students.

reviews.text: Attending Boise State University has been great! It has everything for every kind of students. So, you can expect to find resource's, people, and clubs that support you.

Enrolling into Boise State University has been the best decision of my life. I have met so many kind, incredible people here. From the friends I have made, my roommates, classmates, teachers and everyone else in between, has made Boise State feel like home. All my professors want me to succeed and take the time to help me do that. I don't have anything that I would change about my experience or this school.

Boise State is a great college for students who want to attend a big college, but still feel at home. I really enjoy the community at Boise State, and I feel safe on campus. The food on campus is great as well as the dorms on campus. The campus feels big, but when you get to know your way around it feels small. The staff at Boise State really care about all students, and there are tons of resources for students in need.

I love my school! There are definitely some things that could be improved, and I have had a better experience since not living on campus, but there is a lot of good things. Our athletics are pretty good and there is a ton of support for our football team. And I have had great experiences with most of my professors. I am currently in the nursing program and my experience has been incredible so far. My professors are amazing and I can tell they truly care about their work and their students. My biggest complaint is the greek system, I think there could be a lot of improvements made there.
Some of the instuctors are very understanding. They try their best to be informative but admittedly some good opportunities fly under the radar.

I love going to Boise State University, i love all the people here, i love where i live on campus, and i absolutely love to go to the athletic games. They are so fun and i love to go with my friends. The food is pretty good, a lot better than some other colleges i have toured when i was a senior in high school. But my favorite thing here is how i get to be on my own and be with my friends all of the time.

The advisors actually help you with your degree plan and explain everything thoroughly about things you don't get told at other universities/colleges. The teachers are also very helpful and reply within an appropriate amount of time.

Overall my experience at Boise state so far has been good. I love the campus and being so close to downtown and the river. Something I would change is the campus food as well as the campus life and having more fn campus events to meet more people.
Boise State is a great school. I was given all of the material to succeed and had all the support of my professors and advisors.

I enjoyed my time here at Boise State University. Advisors, professors, and peer mentors are so informative and knowledgeable about what we want out of our time in Boise State University. From someone that lived in an island, Saipan, i never felt home like this. Everyone is so nice and respectful, and they also want you to succeed to your full potential.

It has been pretty amazing! I have a hard time socializing at times and Boise has really helped me with that. I have definitely struggled at times but being out of my normal comfort zone is a win on its own! I am very proud of my experience so far and I am so grateful and blessed to be going to Boise State.
Boise State has a very scenic campus and I enjoyed walking around campus. There are tons of official events around campus for any number of interests.

As a current student, I am loving life at Boise State University. The professors I have had were great at making boring subjects enjoyable! The only thing I would change is increasing the hours the dining halls are open on weekends.

BSU has a lot of great professors who are passionate about their field and invested in student success, particularly in the biology and chemistry departments where I took most of my classes. It's a nice campus and easily walkable with plenty of places to sit down and study in between classes. If I were to pick one thing to change, out would be how they schedule classes. They tend to offer required classes during the same time slots so sometimes you really have to plan ahead to make sure you can fit your classes into the semesters you want them in.

Overall, I have had a great experience at Boise State University. They have everything I need on campus like the health center, food pantry, study rooms, etc. I have felt very supported throughout my first semester and there are so many resources for students struggling in any areas of their lives.

ai_summary: Boston College offers a mix of rigorous academics and a vibrant campus life , making it a compelling choice for many students. Reviews frequently highlight the quality of professors , noting their accessibility and dedication to student success. The sense of community, characterized by a strong school spirit and numerous clubs and activities, fosters connections among students. While many enjoyed the supportive atmosphere and opportunities for engagement, several reviews pointed out challenges related to the school's lack of diversity and the pressures of a predominantly affluent, competitive culture. Additionally, the dining options received mixed feedback, with some expressing concerns about food quality. Although Boston College's administration is noted as caring and responsive in many respects, some students criticized the complicated housing and advising processes, as well as the high tuition costs . Overall, BC is described as a place where hardworking individuals can flourish academically while navigating a full student life.

reviews.text: If you are looking for a work hard, play hard school with a lively campus and student life, DO NOT choose Boston College. Seriously, what is going on at this school? Freshman housing is scattered and unfair (Newton campus, forced triples) Lower campus dorms are better IF you win the lottery (literally)and get to live with friends - and that’s a big if. The sports teams are all struggling and school spirit is waning. With no greek life you would think the school would work hard to create other avenues for social engagement. Nope, clubs are competitive and even intramural sports are hard to get into. But hey, it’s a pretty campus. BC is great for the student who wants to study really hard (great profs)and spend their down time volunteering, training for the marathon or going on mission trips. Don’t know where all the money goes but it’s not going towards any kind of student life. Look elsewhere until BC makes a significant investment in the student experience.

Boston College is a great school for academics, specially in the business school. The school really feels like a tight knit community and there is a lot of involvement around campus. There are still some issues with the school concerning social scene, food, dorms and others.
Boston College was the experience of a lifetime! I enjoyed making lifelong connections, learning new things, and eventually getting into my dream starting job!
Great school. Having a great time. Beanpot is awesome. BU sucks🤣. Best Boston school, no question. Go Eagles

Campus felt safe and professors were nice. Felt like I had to work harder to get into med school compared to peers who went into direct pathway programs or with affiliated med schools at their institutions. Felt like my South Asian ethnicity impacted my experience there. The ethnic makeup of the class did not bother me-but there seemed to be blatant ethnic segregation among the student friend groups. Felt left out of the dating scene-you can wear nice clothes from J.Crew and sing along to Sweet Caroline in the BC bus all you want -but you may still be treated differently based on ethnicity alone. Some girls would talk about "the guys" and specifically only mention the Caucasian guys as a different category? One girl asked to set me up with her friend out in CA just because she was also Indian? found this offensive. Students sit in circles during orientation and politely talk about "acceptance" and "tolerance". I chose BC to be to be treated like everyone else-not "tolerated".

Boston College is the best of all worlds. Students are smart! Serious and focused on career outcomes, but also social and friendly and like to have fun. The Jesuit tradition is real a BC, the Boston location is ideal, and the only drawback is the weather in January and February.

I loved my time at BC and have grown to appreciate it more as I’ve gotten older. The strengths are the gorgeous campus, proximity to Boston (ie so many other schools - plus internship opportunities, cultural events, places to go out, shop etc), the smart, friendly student body, wide variety of activities and school spirit. The sports haven’t been winning a lot recently but football & hockey games are still pretty well attended and very fun. The academics are strong - with great professors & engaging classes - and I love the strong interest in social justice/volunteerism on campus. The racial diversity could be better and the freshman year housing should improve but overall it was a fantastic education and experience that prepared me well for life.

BC is an extremely insular school that is tough academically and does not have the student life outlets to make up for it. The school heavily suppresses the party scene both on and off campus, and the city of Boston also cracks down on bars and house parties. The school is majority white and from rich, suburban backgrounds, with a majority of kids going to prep schools and paying full $95,000 tuition price. Food and housing are lackluster and expensive for what they are. The quality of education is strong, but the general environment is not conducive to student performance.

The academics are rigorous and the professors talented but beyond that, BC feels like a school that’s stuck in the 80s. Freshman year is like boot camp: the dorms are run down with many students placed in forced-triples (this has been happening since 2014 with no attempt to rectify!) and others on a satellite campus nearby. There is no community space other than dated dorm ‘lounges’. It’s as if the school doesn’t understand that kids socialize differently now and if there is not a reason to leave their rooms, they won’t. The kids aren’t cliquey, per se, it’s just hard to expand your circle when there’s literally nothing going on and nowhere to go. Most clubs reject most freshman. If you come in with a group (recruited athlete, ROTC, friends from home) you may feel differently but for many - particularly for those from outside the region - BC can feel very isolating and uninspired. The dining cost/quality is unacceptable. There is a lot of good at BC but changes need to be made.

If you're looking for a liberal arts education, but still have that edge of a top tier research university and sports scene, this is the place for you. I've loved every single class and professor I've had. However, it can be really "cliquey" sometimes. I've never struggled to make friends in the past, but here it takes a lot more effort to find your place.

I am a Freshman at BC and I am so glad I chose to come here. Everyone is interesting (in a good way) and very fun, yet also so intelligent in many diverse ways of being. Teachers are definitely one of the highlights as well.
I love the environment at Boston College. It is a very pretty school and the campus life is truly unique. I recommend!

Good academic school but the party scene is awful. The academic requirements are over the top for interests you don’t really care about. Don’t concern yourself with going out until you’re 21. There’s no party scene. Good campus. The professors take their job too serious. People here are nice but for the price go to a state school. Better social life with close to the same academics and easier classes.

Over all the college is pretty amazing so far, especially as a freshman. The campus is great. Though, you’ll have to try your best to not get lost or walk into the wrong classroom. I have around three years left and I’m going to make sure I make the most of it.
It was not nice attending the school, but we had Health Insurance for Students so we finished the quarter.

ai_summary: Boston University offers a diverse and dynamic experience for students, featuring strong academics and a variety of opportunities for research and professional development. Many reviewers appreciate the knowledgeable professors who are generally supportive and engaging, contributing to a positive learning environment . However, the school’s large size can make it challenging for students to find their niche socially, with some describing the campus culture as lacking in school spirit and traditional college experiences. Student life is bustling, especially in the city, but some feel that the social scene is dominated by those seeking nightlife, which can make it difficult for others to connect. While reviewers highlight the advantages of attending school in a thriving urban setting, concerns around the high cost of tuition and inconsistent administrative support also emerge frequently. Improved housing and dining options are noted as areas needing attention, yet the overall impression of BU remains favorable among those who actively engage with its diverse offerings.

reviews.text: It was a great experience. I enjoyed most of my classes and professors. I was supported a lot by professors, mentors, and my peers. The only thing I wish BU had was more diversity. But around my final year at BU, a first generation center opened that provided a lot of great resources for first gen students.

My experience at Boston University was both exciting and formative. I loved being in the heart of the city and constantly running into interesting events, speakers, and opportunities that made campus life feel dynamic. The professors were incredible, engaging, knowledgeable, and surprisingly accessible despite the large class sizes. I always felt supported academically and encouraged to think critically. At times, though, the size of the university felt overwhelming. Navigating such a large student body and sprawling campus could be challenging, and it sometimes made building community feel less intuitive. Overall, BU pushed me to grow while giving me access to exceptional academic resources and a vibrant city

Boston University was my top choice when I applied, and four years later, I am very happy with my decision. Great academics, tough but good support. The area is amazing, Boston is the best place to be a college student! Never got super involved in campus activities, just went here for the academics, but I know a lot of people who enjoyed the clubs, sports, and Greek life here. Met what I am sure will be my best friends for life, proud to be a Terrier!
Boston University is my dream school! I've really enjoyed my time on-campus with students my age learning and getting to know each other. I met a lot of great people there!
Nice place with best location, although the cost is pretty high. I recommend students who don't clear their academic goal may take the chance to try 100+ majors.

I really enjoy attending Boston University, as everything is extremely convenient. There are coffee shops, restaurants, and study lounges near my classes, which allow me to have little breaks in between my classes. Not only is Boston University a convenient area, but it is also one of the places that has the best views.

I love my experience at BU and while it is urban campus and doesn't have football, we have competitive sports and a unique campus life. It is a special place to attend school and the academics are incredibly rigorous and exceptional.

I loved BU. I made my some of my best friends there who I know will be in my life forever. I was a psych major and I loved most of my classes. BU has great classes, programs, athletics, clubs, and a social scene.
Really good and can't wait to continue. I think atmosphere was very good and professors too I do wish housing was cheaper

I enjoyed how BU felt like a community despite its large campus. There were always new people to meet, and everyone was always friendly and helpful. Classes were rigorous but engaging, challenging, and rewarding.

BU sets you up so well in every regard if you put your mind to it. If you put in some effort into looking for resources, you will find so much you can do/use to help you move forward professionally and academically. Putting yourself out there is pretty crucial to finding friends, and there are so many opportunities to meet people at this school with so many clubs and events. Classes mostly are pretty collaborative, although they can be competitive (mainly if you are a STEM/Business major). Boston is such a wonderful city both in terms of job/internship opportunities and exploring outside of class. I have made so many meaningful connections here at BU, and I wouldn't change it for anything.
Being in triple meant for a double or single is pretty awful. And I’m with a freshman and grad student in this micro space. Terrible housing.

I believe Boston University offers many positive qualities for its students. From the strong professional networks you can build, to the diverse community of people from different backgrounds, to the rigorous academics that set you up for post-graduate success, there is a lot to appreciate. That said, no school is perfect. Aside from its exceptionally high tuition, there are times when it can feel as though the university is not fully aligned with its students’ interests, whether due to limitations on free speech tied to federal funding or instances where certain biases seem to surface.

Terrible school that does value diversity and in fact discriminates on it and seems to support students who spread fear and hate on minorities for no reason other than their skin color. Do not come to this terrible university it seems it’s behind in it’s time, I recommend going to other better more loving schools.

You get what you put into your college experience regardless of where you go, but BU stands out because it effectively provides resources on what you’re interested in and where/how you can pursue it :)

ai_summary: Bowling Green State University (BGSU) offers a friendly and welcoming campus atmosphere where students find a sense of community, engaging in various activities and academic opportunities. Many reviews highlight the supportive faculty who are accessible and willing to help, contributing to a positive academic experience. The campus is noted for its beauty, particularly in the fall, and the small-town feel of Bowling Green adds to the charm. While most students appreciate the close proximity of classes and dorms, opinions vary on campus life—some find it lively and fun, while others feel it lacks excitement. Diversity is a mixed topic, with some students appreciating the community yet noting a need for improvement. The university is also recognized for having a solid range of majors , particularly in business and education, though dining options and housing can sometimes be points of contention. Overall, BGSU is seen as a solid choice for students seeking a balanced college experience with plenty of opportunities for personal and academic growth.

reviews.text: I love how small and clean the Bowling Green State University Campus is. Everything is a short walk away and I think that's allowed me to feel closer to this place. I love meeting new people, so I love it!

BGSU was one of many schools I looked at when choosing a college, including Kent State, University of Toledo, and Marietta. It has been an amazing campus experience here and I couldn’t have picked a better place for my education. I have made incredible friends and gained essential experience to pursue the career I want for myself.

I don’t think Gene Poor is still teaching Entrepreneurship here, but he was amazing. The Digital Art program was alright. If you’re not planning to live here after you graduate, it may be hard to network and find jobs through the schools events and network

When applying for BG on such short notice I was able to get accepted and into the pre nursing program very fast. So far, the professors seem very nice and easy to communicate with. I would like to be able to contact them more effectively though instead of just email. I have yet to be on campus since class starts Monday but am very excited.
Love the people very one is helpful and has everything I need to get my degree. Just hard to find friends. They have very good clubs like Japanese,Honor and a fly program they help with all you need.
BGSU is a great school. Their aviation program and degree will be a strong foundation to reach my goal of becoming a commercial pilot.

As a current Junior, Id say that my experience so far at BGSU has been pretty good minus some minor hiccups. There have been a few professors that have given me and fellow students some trouble but for the most part it's a great university. What originally pulled me into choosing BGSU was the statistics for student satisfaction and the level of diversity, especially given the university's very rural location. Something that I'd like to see change in BGSU is allowing the Black fraternities and sororities to have houses in the greek village. Something else id like to see change is if a professor is consistently ranked poorly on Rate My Professor, Id like to see actions taken by the university to get that professor either talked to or removed from their position if the complaints continue.
BGSU has been a great experience so far! The campus feels very safe and my furthest walk is 25 minutes across campus. I would like to see parking spots change but other than that it’s great!

Based on my personal experience, the Photochemical Sciences program at BGSU did not match its advertised environment. I encountered micromanagement, unclear expectations for exams and graduation, and pressure to work without funding or proper training. Although I applied for computational work, I was pushed toward experiments, and resisting led to loss of support and threats. The graduate coordinator used grades and scholarships as leverage; a grade he said would be a C became an A only after I mentioned a formal appeal. I was also told that recommendation letters might be withheld if I sought to transfer. Some faculty shared information about my academic standing with third parties without my consent. As the only Asian student, I also experienced unequal treatment around funding. Overall, my experience involved coercive supervision, retaliation, and disregard for student boundaries. For these reasons, I cannot personally recommend the program, especially to international applicants.

Bowling Green State University offers an overall experience that feels welcoming, supportive, and full of opportunity. The campus has a strong sense of community, where students can easily connect through classes, organizations, and campus events. Professors are generally approachable and invested in helping students succeed, and the university provides many academic resources—from tutoring centers to career services—to support personal and professional growth. The campus environment is lively but not overwhelming, creating a balance between social life and academics. Whether attending athletic events, joining student groups, or simply spending time around the student union, BGSU offers a well-rounded college experience that encourages involvement, independence, and a sense of belonging.

So far I am having a great experience. Coming from a family of 9 me being the youngest I did not have to work. So, enrolling to BGSU gave me a sense of independency and learning how to take care of myself.
I love the community, the way they always have something fun happening in order to bring students together.

Parent of a student that went to Bowling Green. First year dorm was horrible… has since been closed for massive renos. Second year dorm was very good.
Food was poor. Really quite a lack of healthy food on campus. Think mid west food.
Location is the middle of nowhere off a busy highway near cornfields. The town itself however is charming with lovely old buildings on a main street.
The campus is modern and lacks any buildings with historic charm… this is not a campus where you will see old brick buildings with ivy. Everything is new and clean but lacks character and charm.
The student body looks like you would expect for mid west students going to a public university. If you are looking for some prestige and upper class connections, this is not the school for you.
I am intending to transfer to Bowling Green state University. I have been there a few times and each time I loved it and it seems to be the place for me.

Bowling Green State university is an amazing college to go to for a solid education. The university provides great professors that are kind and respectful. These professors mean it when they say they are here to help you. As long as you ask questions they will drop everything to help when needed. The campus provides solid amount of diversity and helps with people fitting in. BGSU is overall a great place to discover yourself and your goals.

ai_summary: Bradley University generally offers a solid academic experience with supportive and approachable professors , particularly noted in programs like engineering and education. The small campus size fosters a close-knit community where students often feel welcomed and like they belong. There are numerous clubs and activities that enhance campus life, although some students feel that the social scene—particularly for those under 21—can be lacking, and there are concerns about safety in the surrounding area. Housing experiences vary, and while some dorms are deemed inadequate, upperclassman apartments receive better reviews. The dining options have mixed feedback, with some students finding them decent but limited. Financial concerns are frequently mentioned, highlighting the need for clarity around scholarships and funding. Overall, Bradley is characterized by its welcoming atmosphere and opportunities for personal and academic growth, but like many institutions, it has areas needing improvement.

reviews.text: My overall experience at Bradley University has been both rewarding and transformative. As a nursing major, I am constantly challenged academically while being supported by professors who genuinely care about my growth and success. The campus environment fosters learning, collaboration, and a strong sense of community, which has helped me thrive both inside and outside the classroom. Being involved in Greek life has further enriched my experience by allowing me to build meaningful relationships, develop leadership skills, and give back through service and philanthropy. I truly love my school for the opportunities it provides, the supportive environment it creates, and the way it has helped shape me into a more confident and motivated individual.

My experience while attending Bradley University has its strengths, but also growing challenges. As a small school, Bradley offers a more personal environment that fosters close professor and student relationships. However, recent years have made the campus feel increasingly strained. Budget cuts cause professors to leave, programs to disappear, and fewer resources overall. Despite several budget cuts, tuition continues to rise, which makes the changes even more frustrating. The student atmosphere is also discouraging, as gym equipment is often broken, the library is small, and the student center has limited food and seating options. On top of all this, Bradley is located in a rougher part of town where crime is a serious concern. Altogether, these issues have had a noticeable impact on the overall student experience.

Overall bradley is pretty boring. There are a lot of great people here but there isn't much to do with said great people. For every good teacher there's a HORRIBLE one somewhere else. It's really a flip of the coin when it comes to this school

Bradley has a big diverse community, staff are very helpful, professors are great. Facility is great we have a lot of labs. Area is very calm and student friendly and I live off campus but it's pretty close. The engineering program is very strong here I recommend it

I have been at Bradley since August 2024. I was recruited by the softball coach to come play here; however, she was very abusive and ruined my relationship with softball, along with many other girls. I want to go to school here because it is one of the best in the nation for my major, but it is expensive. There is a lot to do in Peoria, and I love the city so much. My professors make me excited for classes and happy to learn.
Being at Bradley University has been a great experience so far and I'm really looking forward to the rest of my time here.
I really enjoyed the people and community I had within my major, but I do wish there was more opportunities announced and guided through

My overall experience at Bradley University has been great. I enjoy the professors and the materials I'm learning in my field. I also appreciate the project I'm working on in my research lab, and the people on campus are great.

My experience at Bradley university the last two years has been incredible and certainly has me excited about my next two. The professors vary from department but all my professors, except the business ones, have been extraordinary. They're very patient with late work, happy to answer questions during office hours and very available through email. I only wish there were less adjunct professors as they don't provide the same care about teaching and student interaction as full time professors do. The business school is notorious for professors like this who put the learning process on you and don't make an effort to reach students. My professors in other departments don't suffer with the same lack of care.

I really enjoy Bradley University as a whole. The campus is very inclusive and close knit. My classes and professors are wonderful; I really have not had any problems with any of them. I love the smaller student population and community feel that Bradley provides.
I like the fact that it’s very inclusive and I’ve never had a problem with Bradley! They all care about you, but it is very challenging

My experience at Bradley University has been incredibly positive and rewarding. From the supportive professors to the vibrant campus community, I have felt welcomed and valued since day one. The small class sizes allow for meaningful connections and personalized learning, which has helped me grow academically and personally. I’ve been able to get involved in student organizations that match my interests, building friendships and leadership skills along the way. Bradley’s focus on real-world experience through internships and hands-on learning has prepared me for future success. Overall, Bradley has become a place where I feel inspired, challenged, and truly at home.

Bradley University's engineering program is very good. To be honest, outside of that a lot of it is just okay. The professors I've had have (For the most part) been very good with meeting with students, answering questions, and teaching. The classes themselves are challenging enough to be interesting but not unnecessarily hard. I have had some issues with my schedule, which isn't great, and the food is below average at the dining halls, but there are some fun clubs run by good people that are fun to go to.
It feels like a great college. All professors I've had so far are very understanding and helpful. The community is great and Peoria has a lot of cool things to do.
Bradley University is where I discovered myself and met supportive teachers. There lectures are also interesting.

ai_summary: Brandeis University offers a unique academic experience characterized by close interactions with dedicated professors and a strong emphasis on social justice and research opportunities. While many students appreciate the diverse and passionate community , they often report challenges with the social environment, citing cliquishness and a lack of vibrant student life outside academics. The tuition is often mentioned as a significant concern , with many feeling that the financial aid offerings are inadequate. A recurring point of criticism is the quality of food and housing, which many students feel do not match the high costs of attending the university. Overall, Brandeis is well-suited for those seeking rigorous academics and a supportive faculty, but it may not be the right fit for students looking for a bustling social scene or high-quality dining options.

reviews.text: It's a great academic school with diversity. Very welcoming place where anyone can thrive. The campus is solid and the surrounding area is nice.

Teaching quality: overall good, with a few instructors being super unhelpful and strict
Freedom of speech: Worst possible. All the political chaos just went crazy for "truth". can't say more but felt neglected
student life: people sucks. purely selfish and mean roommate (a total jerk), and CA never came up with a plan.
food: you know it
dorm: bad, pipes blocked for hours, a week without a water fountain, worst mattress ever.
never worth a dollar and greedy, reluctant to provide basic services. already left the place.

There are some really amazing professors at Brandeis! Most care deeply about their students and are invested in our learning. Sometimes the campus feels dead on the weekends, but I never feel alone or without opportunities for campus life.

The academics at Brandeis are great, and the professors are, for the most part, superb. The issue with Brandeis lies entirely in its administration. Truly a nightmare at every single level. Housing? Terrible. Financial services? Abismol. Academic advising? The worst. I wish I had never gone here simply because of the awful administration. They care more about lining their pockets than the student body. Particularly, the academic advising office is terrible. They don't care about students; they care about themselves. Brandeis' administration is the antithesis of what "Truth, even unto its innermost parts" means and signifies. I am glad to be rid of this place.

Brandeis is a small tight-knit community with great people. The community is diverse with people from every corner of the world, and there is a lot people who are motivated to be the best versions of themselves possible.

I'm a Posse Scholar and first-gen student at Brandeis from NYC. The academics are rigorous and push me to think in new ways. I’ve been lucky to have professors who are approachable and genuinely invested in their students success. The diversity on campus has gave me a space to be comfortable and connect with people from all backgrounds from all over the world. Student life is active with plenty of clubs and organizations to get involved in which has helped me build community and feel a sense of home despite being miles from home.
Housing could be improved and is being improved through the new housing option being built. Campus feels very safe. There are a lot of options to choose from when it comes to food coming from a picky eater, athletics are accessible if students want to get involved but its not a big sports school. Party scene is small but there are plenty of other ways to get involved. Being close to Boston is also a huge advantage with many opportunities to explore the city.

I am jewish myself, and I can confidently say the administration has gone full zionist. . They consider any criticism of isreal antisemitic, which is just not true. This is no longer a leftist-friendly school.
People here are quirky (some in a good way, and some in a bad way). The admin is bad across the board. Look up Brandeis' ranking in 1998 and compare it to today, and you will see how they have destroyed this school.
I will say, the boston area is really nice. Though you can't really walk to boston or anything, its really cool having so much nearby. But there are other better schools in the area that I would recommend over brandeis.

The food is bad, the campus is mid, the location is mid, the parties are bad, and the social dynamics are foul. This school is full of introverts and weirdos (said as a weirdo myself). Not the place to go if you are an extrovert. Also, it's definitely not as diverse as it may seem. There is a large Asian population, though the vast majority of students are Jewish. In itself, it was not a problem, but zionism runs rampant on this campus and the school supports it, silencing pro-Palestine efforts like the Jewish Bund, whose main goal is to peacefully spread information and stand up for their beliefs. Maybe the school for some people but definitely not for me.

My brother goes here. He likes it. It's OK. The buildings are very run-down. And the food is bad. But its very easy to get research. And I think all of the professors are great. There is a lot of left wing students, and a lot of jewish students, and these two sides sometimes conflict due to current global events (you can probably guess what im talking about). But every student I've met from here has just been so nice. I think the people are the best part of this university.

If you’re considering Brandeis, I strongly hope you never have to deal with the Department of Student Rights and Community Standards (DSRCS). While its stated mission is to protect student rights, my experience was the opposite: I encountered a lack of transparency, decisions issued without clear explanations, and repeated failure to respond to reasonable requests for information.
Rather than upholding due process, the system felt more focused on limiting student voice and shielding administrative decisions from accountability. It's deeply disappointing to go through a process that promises fairness but delivers the opposite. In its current state, DSRCS seems more aligned with denying students their rights than protecting them.
It should be nameed, "Department of Student Right Denial and Making Community Life Unbearable."

Overall the school is pretty good. The overall atmosphere of the school is nice during the fall and the spring. During the winter months, the Canadian geese do come back to campus and roam the streets which can be funny at times but that also means a lot of birth feces. Academic wise the library is quite neat and is a good size though it can experience times with minimal ac during breaks. With the freshman dorms being massel and north, they are decent and some have big rooms while some may be smaller. The size of the room and what housing number one gets is up to luck.

I am a transfer student and Brandeis University already feels like a second home. Adapted well to the social and student life. Social life is very interacting with so many athletic clubs and education clubs. As well as clubs that help students identify themselves and help find their people. Student life is also supported with either teachers and peers, or infrastructure with either libraries or study areas. Speaking more on the social life of Brandeis, there isn't a day when something isn't going on. From the festivals going on, to either performances or art shows. It is a wonderful university with lots of diversity.

Brandeis has been a solid experience overall. What I’ve liked most is how invested the professors are in their students and how easy it is to form close relationships in small classes. The academic flexibility lets you explore a wide range of interests and the school really supports students who are driven and curious. I’ve appreciated the diverse thoughtful community and the opportunities for research and internships, especially in premed and STEM fields. It is a place where you can be yourself and grow without the pressure of a cutthroat environment.

My experience at Brandeis University has been both intellectually stimulating and personally enriching. The university fosters a strong sense of community while encouraging academic exploration and civic engagement. I've particularly appreciated the small class sizes, which allow for meaningful discussions and close relationships with professors who are passionate about their fields.
Beyond the classroom, Brandeis offers a wide range of opportunities for growth. I've been involved with the Waltham Group and the Big Siblings program, where I’ve had the chance to give back to the local community—something I deeply value. The university’s emphasis on social justice and service aligns closely with my own goals, making my time here feel purposeful.
Overall, Brandeis has helped me grow as a student, a leader, and a person committed to making a difference.

Poor student athlete experience-- no support from the school financially and not all professsors are accomodating. Aside from this, it is very easy to make friends due to small class sizes and strong student life culture. Quality of education and professors depends heavily on your major.

ai_summary: Brenau University offers a unique environment characterized by small class sizes and a close-knit community, which allows for personalized attention from professors who are generally seen as helpful and supportive. The university is particularly well-regarded for its programs aimed at empowering female leaders and fostering a sense of sisterhood among its predominantly female student body. However, many reviews highlight concerns about the high cost of tuition and the lack of variety in dining options, which detracts from the overall student experience. While some students enjoy the supportive atmosphere and the myriad of clubs available, others express disappointment in campus life, feeling there are limited social activities and opportunities for male students in sports. Additionally, issues with the administration and financial aid processes appear to be a significant pain point for many students. Overall, Brenau is appreciated for its educational commitment but criticized for its pricing and some organizational inefficiencies.

reviews.text: I’m a communications major and I love that the type of experience you can get involved in. The staff are more than happy to help find internships and guide students figure out what to do to be more appealing to recruiters.
Enthusiastic professors and students on campus, slight issues with living on campus (maintenence issues, occasional power outages). Overall, an excellent place to study the liberal arts.
I really enjoy the small classrooms and very involved teachers. i also enjoy how caring and kind everyone is.
This school is a joke. The professors don’t care about you and all the school wants is your money. They almost trap you to stay there

I had an amazing time at Brenau over the course of my undergrad, and loved most of my time there. I loved most of the professors I came in contact with, but the ones I did not like or had problems with, it was hard to get anything done about their bad instruction. It can be sort of a mixed bag at times. The campus itself does not have an abundance of restaurant/bar life, but all of that is within walking distance, so it's not a problem. The overall campus life is great, and very safe to walk around. The food at the cafeteria is mediocre, but the available tea room is useful! Overall, great college.

I have really enjoyed being at Brenau, although this isn't the type of party school. They still try to make it fun for the students, and everyone who attends here has a chance to be one-on-one with their professors if they ever need it. The professors and all the admin really care about students' lives and their academics. It is great going to this school and being around people who care about your well-being other than yourself.
I start the spring semester so I honestly don't know much I'm planning to visit and talk with my counselor while I'm there.

Brenau University has a 100% NCLEX pass rate, but the ELMSN nursing program is extremely unorganized. The professor turnover rate is high. There are a couple of professors who care about students and their success, but there are lots who couldn't care less. I think there are better programs that are less expensive with quality education.

My experience with Breanu University has been wonderful. I was very nervous about going back to school after 20 + years. My enrollment advisor Jessica helped me the through the process and was my cheerleader when I mention my fear about this new chapter in my life. Once I started the online classes again fear sat in, but my program advisor gave me some very encouraging words. The course load was not all the hard, but they did challenge me to do my best. I would recommend Breanu University to family and friends.

As I work as a Paraprofessional in a school system already, I like the fact that they offer their education program all online so that I can get my teaching certificate while also being able to hold a full-time position. The staff and professors there are very responsive and very knowledgeable. Great school.
So far it’s an amazing experience! I love the people they’re so nice. My admissions counselor was very persistent and accommodating.

The people there are very kind and because its such a small school you constantly are running into people you know. I like the small class sizes you get more personalized help from professors. however the food is not good, every week is the same food theres no variety.

Brenau University has provided me with great opportunities. I am in the BSN program and have had nothing but wonderful experiences with the professors and fellow classmates. I look forward to my next semesters of nursing school and the knowledge I will gain!
breanu is a small school which is what I enjoy about it being able to get more help when it come to work because there only up to 25 student in the classroom.

Brenau University has been a really cool but busy experience for me. I transferred here and only take theatre classes, which is great because I love acting. Living on campus is nice since my schedule is super packed, but I have so much work that it’s hard to find time to visit my family. I get caught up in rehearsals and assignments, and before I know it, weeks have passed. It can be a lot, but I really like being around other people who love theatre as much as I do!
My experience in Briar Cliff so far has been really good. I really love their Trio program that helps first-generation students a lot. I find the atmosphere really comfortable and peaceful.

I start briar cliff in the fall and the people there have been nothing but welcoming and supportive. The teacher truly seem to want me to do my best and pursue my dreams and the other students are amazing. my upperclassmen are so helpful.
I have finished 1 year her and I have met some of my best friends here. I am far away from home and so having amazing friends help me forget how far I am from home.
This a best institution for one who want focus his career a ministry and being tuitor with professional in word of God
The small campus allows for more face to face time with professors. As a freshman this was key to my success.

At Briar Cliff, I play volleyball and am a full-time student. Being an athlete is very stressful, especially during peak season. I do love my classes, however. I love the small class sizes. One of my classes next semester only as two of us. The one thing I hate is the limited number of classes and times for each class. In the spring, there was 3 different classes I needed, but all of them were at the same time. Fortunately, I can wait to take them. If I was a senior, however, that would be a big issues. It was also just frustrating trying to schedule my classes with the limited amount of options. My advisor is super helpful. We meet a lot and talk about what the rest of my time at BCU looks like, so I can already start planning for the future.
I enjoyed attending Briar Cliff University. I enjoyed the atmosphere and the professors knowledge helped me get through 4 years and get into my career that I have a passion for.

Bad experience. The teachers are miserable and don't care about students. They are always grumbbling that students are only here to play sports and don't care about courses. Their right! It is sad but I don't think it is a top notch college and I'm afraid I'm not learning anything useful.

I would not recommend this University the buildings and dorms are old and dilapidated and reek of mold and mildew . The office staff don't seem to care much about the students and are basically gone most of the time . There are problems on campus due to security not caring about anything . The President and vice president seem to care more about appearances than they do the students The only thing that seems to natter most around here is the athletics department which isn't the best the athletes here really aren't the best around . And thTne maintenance crew here basically have no clue what they are doing the place is literally falling apart and not very clean . However the nursing department is one of the best and most of the teachers are really really awesome . I would recommend Morningside University in over Briar Cliff it is a much better university . Cause all Briar Cliff cares about is money .
Great private institution. Historically an athletic school but lots of new focus on in demand degree programs.

This school is horrible, the financial aid office is a joke, and when did schools stop caring about the students? I feel like this is a valid question. The financial aid was and is a joke for the 4 years that my son has gone there and for the year and a half that my youngest it's gotten even worse. The dorms are a joke and need to be updated, and so many more things that I could name but am not going to waste my time. The president and vice president are more worried about their appearances and social media following than they are about the school and students and issues. I don't recommend this school to anyone for the simple fact of the financial aid office, they don't care about the students and their well being, the only thing this office cares about is making sure they break the students and making sure that they leave with hundred of thousands of dollars in loans and make sure that they also leave in debt.

At Briar Cliff University, the percentage of student athletes are close to 95%. I am apart of that percentile. I cam into BCU on a softball scholarship. So far, it has been nothing but a rollercoaster ride wether it comes to athletics, academics, mental or physical toughness and even social life. After my first year, if someone were to tell me how it all ended- I would do it all over again. Being at briar cliff has showed me what I can put myself through and come out on the other side stronger than ever before.

Briar Cliff is a nice, welcoming campus. They do a great job at supporting their students in the classroom, ensuring that they do well and wanting them to succeed. The campus community is warm and welcoming to all with its diverse student body.
my experince has been great so far ive learned about diversity and have gone through character develoment , i would say there is nothing to change
Tell staff care about you on a personal level. Academics were geared towards the working person and non traditional student.

ai_summary: Brigham Young University (BYU) consistently stands out for its rigorous academic environment and dedicated faculty . Students report that classes can be challenging, requiring hard work and commitment, but the effort often leads to meaningful learning experiences . The professors are generally described as approachable and invested in students' success, fostering a supportive atmosphere . Student life is enriched by a strong sense of community , where individuals with similar values come together, although many reviewers note a desire for increased diversity. Activities on campus are plentiful, appealing to a wide range of interests, and the environment promotes both academic and spiritual growth. Additionally, the tuition is highlighted as affordable, making BYU an attractive option for those seeking a quality education in a safe, friendly setting. While the absence of a vibrant party scene is acknowledged, many appreciate the overall positive and wholesome social atmosphere.

reviews.text: I loved Brigham Young University. It has a spiritually uplifting atmosphere designed to edify students' intellect as well as their character. I had the opportunity to meet a lot of people different from myself whom I was able to connect with over learning and shared exploration into adulthood. If I could see one thing change I would encourage BYU to build their own student climbing area. Climbing is a huge sport in Utah and many other nearby universities have their own gym. This would be a valuable tool and take pressure off of nearby community gyms getting overcrowded.

I have loved my time at BYU. I have never been happier. The people at this school are the most kind, moral, and intellectual young adults you can find. There is a spirit of faith and goodness on the campus, and I am given all the resources I need to develop in all areas of my life. It's hard to not be your best self at BYU.

I loved Brigham Young University. It provided a safe space where academics met spirituality in which allowed students to progress towards academic and personal growth and development. I loved the kind staff, professors, and overall student experience. The academics were difficult in a way that stretched you to be a better student. I don’t believe that I really would change anything.
Extremely high quality of education. Prepared me well for dental school. Full of good, thoughtful, kind people.
BYU is one of the bests. I love the staff there, all the students are super friendly and supportive.

Overall, my experience with Brigham Young University is amazing! All the staff and faculty I worked with or took classes from had a very infectious passion and love for the things that they taught and the people they taught them too. Brigham Young University is a very safe and uplifting place to be.

I love the variety of classes and times that they were offered. I love how friendly the people were in my ward, but wish there was a little bit more of a friendly environment on campus itself when passing in the halls and you smile at someone they shouldn’t look at you like you’re crazy. I also would change the weather because I don’t like the cold. The married Housing is really old and cylinder blocks, which doesn’t feel super homie, but you couldn’t beat the pricing. It allowed us to save so much money for when we graduated and entered the real world.
I like it here but the only problem is the scholarships, I have a 4.0 and didn’t get any. Otherwise great school I love it

Throughout my first year at BYU I have enjoyed learning and trying new things. All the professors are caring and most people tend to be very respectful of differing perspectives. Most professors do a good job of embodying university values.
I like Brigham Young University. There are many amenities and options of things to do on campus. The dorms are clean and decent.

I love BYU! It is a faith based school with high moral values. I feel like I have not talked to a single unkind person here the entire time I have been here. They have many programs and clubs in place to give students the best experiences and you can feel like care and effort that they put into it.

My overall experience at Brigham Young University was positive and challenging in good ways. I appreciated the strong academic programs, dedicated professors, and the emphasis on ethics and service. The campus is beautiful and there’s a strong sense of community, which made it easy to feel supported. That said, I’d like to see more diversity in perspectives and greater flexibility around policies and student expression. Expanding mental health resources and inclusive spaces would also improve the student experience. Overall, BYU offers a solid education, especially for students who align with its values.

I really have enjoyed my time here at BYU, there are so many opportunities and so many great people. I live the athletics here and they are such great events to attend and join. As a freshman who doesn't live on campus it is difficult to meet people my own age but everyone is so kind no matter the age. Academics are challenging but professors are willing to help always if you are willing to put in the work. It is awesome here!
BYU has been incredible! I feel so loved here and feel like I have people who really care about me and want me to succeed.

Brigham Young University is such a great school. The people and professors here are high quality and strive to perform their best. It’s inspiring to be in a place where so many individuals work hard and genuinely want to succeed. I also love how every class is centered around the gospel, praying before class and sharing scriptures creates a meaningful learning environment. This approach strengthens you spiritually while also pushing you academically. The moral standards at BYU are also very high, which makes it a positive and uplifting place to learn and grow.

ai_summary: Brown University stands out for its supportive and collaborative academic environment , facilitated by its unique open curriculum that allows students the freedom to explore diverse interests without the constraints of a core curriculum. Students frequently note the kindness and helpfulness of professors and peers, creating a strong sense of community that fosters personal and academic growth. While the charming campus and vibrant social life in Providence contribute to a fulfilling college experience, many reviews highlight challenges with housing conditions and dining amenities. Overall, Brown is characterized by its emphasis on intellectual curiosity and the non-competitive atmosphere, making it appealing for those seeking a nurturing yet challenging educational setting.

reviews.text: The academic aspect of the school is very good as are the professors. I will say though that the school can improve in advising/academic/career/and mental health resources outside of the school. Furthermore, safety was definitely lax, below other Ivy league schools ive visited. Still overall a great school- and the students were definitely very motivated.

It's the only school I could've gone to! Open curriculum really lets you focus on the fun of being a student, campus is manageable, great atmosphere from the student body. I recommend anyone that want to go to college comes here!

I have enjoyed my overall experience at Brown. I've only been here for a semester, and I couldn't be more grateful to be a part of such a wonderful community. Everyone has been very friendly and very supportive here, and it has truly felt like a dream come true. Despite the recent situation we were subjected to regarding our safety, I was truly inspired by how united the Brown community was in reaching out to one another and taking care of each other. I truly feel like I belong at Brown, and we are one big family that doesn't hesitate to help each other out when needed.

The best thing about Brown for me is the community they build. Everyone here are always looking for friends which creates a very welcoming environment. The professors are always there when you need them and very understanding. Some changes I would like to see are maybe scanners instead of id cards since the card scanners don't work sometimes (archbron front door never works). I also think they should include paper towels in bathrooms as they are more hygienic and so we are able to clean our messes in bathrooms rather than leaving them for other people.
Amazing school with amazing people—open curriculum is amazing. This school lets you explore your passions like no other.
Brown Univesity is quite great! They have a variety of solid academic fields, and the campus is quite large!

Loved Brown. The professors are amazing and very attentive to students. The student body is really diverse and overall very interesting in kind. Providence is ok as a city-- not a ton going on but charming and relatively safe.
Brown is an incredible place filled with genuine and kind people. The professors are very engaged and care a lot about the undergraduate student experience.

As an international student, Brown University has provided me with a myriad of tools to make my first year truly remarkable. Academically, they have provided me with the opportunity to explore my two passions (mechanical engineering and film production) simultaneously, even providing me with the option of creating my own unique exploration of "film engineering" where I can use my learning to create machines for filmmaking like dollies, camera rigs, lenses, animatronics, stabilizers, and more. Culturally, Brown has provided me the opportunity to come a week and a half earlier to campus (expenses included) in order to participate in an international student orientation where I get to meet other international students and learn all about creating bank accounts, applying for student jobs, and other important information about living in the US. So far, I've felt accepted and included in this community, and I am excited to continue my education until I complete my bachelor's degree.

When you first join Brown as a freshman they have a mandatory bruno week. At first it may seem overwhelming but it allows you to socialize and make friendships that may last the entire year. What helped me adjust was starting the year with some friends I reached out to before classes started.
During the school year my favorite library is SciLi, the sciences library, which may seem a little dull from the outside but it's great. My favorite floors if I want a louder environment while studying is the 5th and 6th floors. When I need to really focuses in absolute quiet I go to the basement or the 13th floor which has an amazing view.

It is an incredibly rigorous school that has a relaxed environment. Students help each other with work, unlike other prestigious universities. It is quite liberal, which can be good or bad depending on your political opinions. Overall a very nice place to go to school.

I believe my time at Brown so far has been a very formative and eye-opening experience. I love interacting with similarly motivated students and being encouraged by engaging professors who truly care about my learning experience.
It's alright I guess, I think the main thing is that Brown is a tiny school, so you get to know a lot of other people.

Brown's academics are really incredible. Professors are excited to teach undergrads, there are always opportunities to talk with them and build relationships. Even in bigger classes, I still got to know my professors pretty well by visiting their office hours and asking questions in class. The student body is also awesome. Brown selects for students who are both excited to learn and excited to connect with others. Everyone on campus wants to meet new people and friends.

I love Brown. The Open Curriculum has been one of the most valuable aspects of my college experience—it’s allowed me to explore a wide range of interests. I originally came in planning to study computer science, but after taking courses in Earth science and economics, I’ve found a passion for space policy that combines technical knowledge with global impact.
The academic environment is incredibly supportive and collaborative. Students are genuinely curious, friendly, and always willing to help each other. Professors are approachable, and it’s easy to form meaningful relationships across departments.
That said, there’s definitely room for improvement when it comes to housing and dining. The amenities aren’t the best, and these day-to-day aspects of student life can feel overlooked at times. Still, the strength of the community and the freedom to shape my own education far outweigh those flaws.

ai_summary: At CUNY City College of New York, students frequently highlight the diversity of the campus and the strong academic support provided by professors, who are generally committed to helping students succeed. The college offers a range of resources, including tutoring, career programs, and accessible faculty , which contribute positively to the overall learning experience. However, some reviews note the need for improvements in administrative efficiency, particularly related to financial aid and advising services. While the campus features beautiful architecture and a variety of clubs and activities, there are mixed feelings on student life, with some feeling it could be more vibrant. The affordability of tuition is often mentioned as a significant advantage , making CCNY an attractive option for students focused on obtaining a solid education without incurring substantial debt. Overall, the college offers a unique environment where dedication to academics and community engagement can thrive, though there are areas that could benefit from enhancements.

reviews.text: My experience at ccny, so far, is relative pleasant. I love the architecture, though the interior of the buildings can be a bit underwhelming - especially in an area with no windows. Most staff are friendly, although this is a CUNY where socializing is not the priority. Food is pretty good since they renovated the dining hall. The area around the college varies in safety.

The most interesting thing is the diversity in the City College of New York and the variety of departments that cover almost every aspiration. Some professors are not very good especially the almost retiring because they tend to be harsh on students with grading but they have so much content and talent which maybe the reason as to why they are still in service. The student life is good with campus groups though international students like non federal work study students struggle to find on campus jobs. Overall, CCNY is a good place for studying and exploring your interests.

While CCNY is prestigious on paper, the actual experience can feel like an uphill battle. The campus suffers from a "broken elevator" reputation, where crumbling facilities and a frustratingly slow bureaucracy—often called the "CUNY Runaround"—make daily life difficult for students. Because it is a commuter school, there is very little traditional "college life," and the academic workload in programs like engineering is notoriously brutal with little support. It is a place where you go to get a high-value degree on a budget, but you have to deal with outdated buildings, difficult commutes, and a "sink or swim" environment to get it.

I think it depends on what you are looking for in a college but CCNY is a commuter school, thus there isn't much party or student life in comparison to a SUNY college. CCNY, however, has many great programs to become apart of, no matter the major! The campus is very pretty, especially in the fall time. The professors are alright, as long as you do your work and reach out to them as well.
Great Summer Programs. Diverse students. Dedicated Teachers . All kids eager to learn. Labs and Projects are really hands-on because of small classes
My overall experience in CCNY is very good, the classes and professors are all very great and extremely helpful, they're always making sure that we succeed and push ourselves to keep going.

My experience at CCNY has been pretty good so far. I am a theatre major and all of the students within the theatre department are very inviting and kind hearted. They try their best to make everyone feel included. I love how there's always an event at my school as well. My professors, as well, have all been very understanding and just nice people in general.

I love the campus, that's one of the biggest admirations I can speak of in terms of my experience at City College. We have one of the best campuses in the city and that says a lot because it's located in New York City. I also feel included in terms of food because there are halal options on campus like those amazing purple food trucks. There's also much to do because we're also located right in the heart of the city, so places around the city aren't too difficult to access. The only reason I'd have to transfer out would be to pursue a major that is better at the college that I am transferring to. I feel as if I belong at CCNY.
A great learning experience both online and in person environment with inspiring professors and a diverse, supportive community.

I loved the sense of community and collaboration among peers and professors. The projects were exciting and memorable. There were plenty of extra-curricular activities to choose from and events to participate in, such as AIAS and NOMAS. I would love to have more guidance on internship applications and how to navigate job hunting. Additionally, I would love it if there were more portfolio reviews and assistance in supplies for model making, or if the school could invest in more machines like laser cutters or clay modeling. City College was a wonderful place to learn and grow as a designer.
