I started at Dominican University in May 2024 for the Nursing Accelerated Program. I've had a positive experience with the professors and the school staff. The professors are very knowledgeable and are very helpful in providing information to the students within their concentration.

I enjoy going to Dominican University because it provides a kind and encouraging environment where students are inspired to develop both intellectually and personally. Professors, who genuinely care about our success, can engage with us in meaningful ways because of the small class sizes. The campus is beautiful and creates a feeling of community, and I value its focus on service and fairness. Additionally, Dominican offers internships, hands-on learning, and participation in a range of student organizations. All things considered, this experience has been beneficial, enabling me to follow my hobbies and create enduring relationships with mentors and peers.
This university is a calm and quiet setting, which is enjoyable but can sometimes get boring. There is nothing to do for campus sometimes. The academics wise this school is great

If I could give this school negative stars I would. Please don't donate your money here. This school is the size of your local high school most likely with nothing exciting or good about the school. The dorms are terrible and they have no clue how to run a school. This place has to get shut down. Residence Life is a disgrace and has no clue what they are doing from the top to the bottom. You will mentally question and rethink a various amount of things in life if you decide to ruin your college experience by going here.

The university can improve on 1) Expanded Academic Programs such as Diversity in Curriculum including Introducing new, in-demand degree programs, particularly in areas like technology, healthcare, and business, could attract a wider student demographic.
2)Interdisciplinary Programs: Offering more interdisciplinary courses or joint degree programs can appeal to students with diverse academic interests
most importantly *Student Support Services
1) Career Services: Strengthening internship opportunities, alumni networking, and career counseling services could improve job placement rates and student satisfaction.
2) Mental Health Resources: Enhancing counseling and mental health support services, particularly around stress management and well-being, is increasingly critical for student success.
3) **Financial Aid Counseling: Providing more financial literacy programs and guidance on scholarships and financial aid opportunities could reduce student debt and dropout rates.

I went to Dominican for my freshman year, and immediately transferred out. At the beginning, I was optimistic and tried to enjoy the environment even though it was in a small town and quiet area. However, the campus life is dead. There’s on-campus events here and there, but you’ll only see 10 people MAXIMUM that attend them after October. The dorms were dirty, run down, and the bathrooms were even worse. There was mold on the walls in the bathroom, the shower heads were old and decrepit, and over the weekends the janitorial staff did not attend to the bathroom so there would be badly clogged toilets left unattended from Friday-Monday morning. Students are very cliquey and the social situation is reminiscent of high school-you’ll only have a great social life here if you do a sport. They do not give you a curriculum for any major, which forces you to only take the classes THEY want you to (the new “Dominican Curriculum”). I came excited for college, and left with meds. Don’t go here.

My experience at DU has been very wonderful. The campus is very well kept and the activities are enjoyable. The professors are accommodating and they want to see you succeed. I don't think that I would change anything, other than tuition.
Dominican is an absolutely wonderful university and I am grateful to have the opportunity to attend it!

ai_summary: Drake University is characterized by its tight-knit community and strong academic relationships where professors are known to genuinely care about student success. Many students appreciate the small class sizes that allow for individualized attention and engaging learning experiences . However, recurring comments about the dining options and a lack of diversity indicate areas for improvement. Student life offers numerous activities and opportunities for involvement, though some find it can be limited, especially for those outside Greek life. The campus is generally viewed as welcoming, yet there are concerns about safety in the surrounding area. Overall, while many find a fulfilling and supportive environment at Drake, there are notable challenges regarding food quality, diversity, and campus culture that prospective students should consider.

reviews.text:
Drake University is recognized for its strong academics, respected Law School, and emphasis on practical learning. The faculty are knowledgeable, engaged, and committed to helping students succeed. Courses are relevant, challenging, and prepare students for their careers.
Drake’s programs focus on leadership, ethics, and making a positive impact in the community. The learning environment values excellence and responsibility. Online courses are well-organized, easy to access, and supportive, allowing working professionals to continue their education without losing quality or engagement.
Overall, Drake University provides a great education and is committed to helping students become leaders who make a difference in their fields.
Drake University has made the new college experience easy to transition from high school. Overall, Drake has been the perfect fit for me.

Overall, I felt right at home in my new environment. For the most part, I felt safe walking alone, especially at night. The teachers there genuinely care about their students' academic and personal lives and make an effort to get to know them. I can't speak on the party scene (because I don't really like parties), but I do know there is always something to do on campus, as each club often hosts an event.

I wouldn't recommend that any prospective students attend Drake University. Admissions here are akin to a used car dealership. The current budget deficit is being poorly handled and the cost of financial mismanagement is being passed off to students. Professors value obedience over learning. Students are frequently pushed along in courses when they clearly fail to meet basic requirements for learning. Standards are regularly lowered here to help those students pass. Independent research is often discouraged for students who wish to work beyond those limitations, with connections and resources being withheld from them. My upper level courses based in mathematics regularly roll back higher-level concepts because students failed to learn them from introductory courses. My non-mathematical classes are almost entirely based on busy work and faculty favoritism - the later determining how arbitrary concepts are graded. Don't be fooled by these charlatans perverting education for profit.

I really enjoy attending Drake University. The students are very welcoming as well as the staff. The campus is easy to get around and is very scenic. They have great educational facilities and are accommodating to all students.

I love Drake University. It really feels like home to me. I chose Drake because of the community and the opportunities it offers me. A 2+4 program for pharmacy is a big deal. The location is great, the professors know me, and I can balance all my extracurriculars and classwork easily.

Absolutely fantastic experience at Drake my friends and I all ended up being very successful. Judges, business executives, ER physicians, business owners, etc. etc. they used to call Drake the Harvard of the Midwest, which is a bit of a stretch, but it’s a really really good school.

I loved my time at Drake. The professors were all so attentive and really pushed me to develop my own interests. I felt more prepared than many of my peers when entering graduate school and the experiences I gained at Drake served me throughout my doctoral studies.

I have loved my time at Drake! It is a great campus community where everyone is there to help you succeed and to do well. The food is still school food but it could be a lot worse. Drake is not very diverse but they have gotten better. The campus itself is not very accessible. When they build new buildings or renovate them they meet the ADA minimum requirements but they need to do a better job of asking students who are actually disabled for their input on how to make it better.
The comunity about Drake it the best thing about it. I adore all my friends and building relationships. The food is probably the worst part of Drake but its not always bad.

I have truly grown as a person since my first year at Drake. There are numerous opportunities for students to get involved on and off campus, whether it be through volunteer work or a professional internship. Because of a small student to faculty ratio, you are able to build relationships with each of your professors. Each faculty member that I have come in contact with truly wants me to succeed. While the party scene is minimal, it's there if you seek it out. Also, Drake relays is one of the most exciting/social events of the year, and some of my favorite memories have been made during this time! I truly feel that I am ready for the professional world because of my experiences at Drake.
I feel more at home as a grad student/worker here in 1.5 years than I did with 5.5 years at my undergrad school. It's good to be a Bulldog.

Drake University was a life changing place to be. I loved every moment of my four years. I made my best friends there, I carved out my life as an adult there. Professors care about me. I was known as a name, not a number. I worked hard, learned a lot, but also had a ton of FUN. It is a BEAUTIFUL campus and there is always a lot of things going on to get involved in. The school is also right in a city neighborhood and Des Moines offers a lot of professional connections and fun things to do as well. With the downtown core just minutes away, you'll have everything at Drake on or off campus you'd want. Set up a visit and check it out, it's a great university!

Overall great school for academics; the classes are quite driven and challenging. The professors are fantastic and I like how intimate classes are where it’s easy to ask questions and grasp concepts better. One thing I wish I could change is the food. It’s not worth the money being spent and I think there needs to be more diversity in food types and better quality food.

I find Drake University to be an excellent place to learn and receive my education from. It is consistently clean or being worked on and improved in different aspects of the campus and I have never once felt not safe while attending there. The professors are highly professional and receptive to feedback and questions that you may have, paying attention and giving one-on-one assistance where needed to assure success. The amount of options I have while attending for my education was a major selling point on top of having room to go further with graduate programs if needed within the same university. I attended during COVID, so some of the policies were not to my liking when we were forced to attend back in person after starting college online, it was very jarring and hard to adapt to after online schooling for 2 years. Lastly, the other aspect I would like to see changed is the accommodations and disability services not providing much besides informing my teachers of my issues than help.

ai_summary: Drexel University stands out as a vibrant, urban institution with a strong focus on co-op experiences , giving students real-world opportunities alongside their academic studies. Many reviews highlight the supportive and engaging nature of the faculty, noting they care about student success and facilitate career readiness. However, the university's quarter system can feel fast-paced and overwhelming, especially for those who prefer a more traditional semester length. The campus is praised for its diversity and location in Philadelphia, providing students with numerous extracurricular activities and cultural experiences, though some students express concerns about the high tuition costs and the quality of dining options. Overall, Drexel offers a unique balance of academic rigor , professional preparation, and city life, making it well-suited for ambitious students.

reviews.text: My overall experience at Drexel has been very pleasant. There are an abundant number of resources available for students to succeed. The community is welcoming and willing to help others, the food/campus/student life is exceptional, and academics and events at Drexel push you to grow.

My experience at Drexel University has been pretty good overall, mostly because of the co-op program. Getting to work real jobs while still being in school is a huge benefit and makes you feel a lot more prepared for after graduation, while also helping with finances. It also helps you figure out what kind of work you actually like before committing to a full-time job. I also like that a lot of the classes try to connect what you’re learning to real applications instead of just theory. One thing I would like to see improved is the cost, because tuition is really high and it can be a big financial stress for students. The quarter system can also be pretty fast and sometimes a lot to keep up with, but it does help you stay productive and get through school quicker.

Drexel University is a great institution with great classes and a walkable and safe area. As a student, in my free time I enjoy walking to center city Philadelphia and finding new places. I have problems with the extreme cost of campus housing for students after freshman year as well as the food options around. Drexel only has one dining hall and the food is very mediocre most of the time or completely empty.

I think Drexel University is a alright college only if your have the funds and is around the area. As a student who commuted and wasn't full tuition I had to pay out of pocket many times just to get the bare-minimum experience as a student. The classes and opportunities are definitely cool, specially co-op. Make sure you do good research on all the programs they have because their study abroad and undergraduate straight to graduate school is a cool opportunity.
Good experience so far, food needs to be fully changed (it's horrible), but a lot to do on campus since it's in the city.
I had a good experience, Philly is amazing, the co-op program is pretty good. I suggest getting an associate's first and transferring in.
I liked the welcoming environment of the campus, its buildings, and professors. Although, from my personal experience, I do not see many appealing activities or restaurants around campus.
I love the campus and the city life. It is very convenient to get to classes and the food and local area around Drexel is safe
Drexel has been a wonderful experience so far, there are so many resources I am able to benefit from. This has made my stay very smooth so far.
I’m enjoying my time here so far. I’ve made good friends, and most of my teachers have been very supportive.

The college life at Drexel University has been a welcoming and supportive environment. The campus has a lot to offer whether through majors or clubs and diversity. I’m looking forward to my last two years here.

Excellent so far, love all the food in the area and there is always something to do. I’d like they have housing specifically for engineering freshman. It has been a good experience, although I am only into my first two weeks the professors are very knowledgeable. Finding classes can be difficult, since the campus is spread out for the city.

Overall, my time at Drexel University was transformative. Academically, the curriculum was rigorous and well-aligned with current industry needs, and professors balanced theory with hands-on projects that deepened my understanding. The signature Co-op program was the highlight, it allowed me to apply classroom learning in real workplaces, build my network, and clarify my career goals. Outside the classroom, Drexel’s student organizations and events offered leadership opportunities and a strong peer network. The university also provided practical support through career services, tutoring centers, and research opportunities. If I had to point to an area for growth, it would be campus navigation and advising consistency across departments, but even there, faculty and advisors I connected with were committed and helpful. Overall, Drexel prepared me well for the next step in my career.

My experience at Drexel has been full of growth and opportunities. I love the hands-on learning through co-op and the diversity on campus. However, I wish there were more community-building events to help students connect, especially during freshman year

My experience at Drexel was not entirely smooth. Coming to a new city and adapting to the schedules,, workloads, and finding new friends was a whole lot of work. But Drexel has so many support systems there for you, and most of the professors I had always wanted you to succeed and grow.

ai_summary: Duke University has a lot to offer students, highlighted by its rigorous academics and engaging professors . Many reviewers emphasize the range of academic opportunities, including research and diverse courses that encourage personal growth. The campus itself is described as beautiful, with a lively atmosphere and strong school spirit, particularly around athletics. Student life is noted for its active participation in clubs and organizations, although it can also feel cliquey and somewhat exclusive, particularly regarding Greek life. While the dining options received praise, there's some concern about dorm conditions and the high cost of attending. Overall, students appreciate the supportive community and the balance of hard work and social activities, with many describing their experience as life-changing.

reviews.text: I love Duke, I think we have a great balance of rigorous academics and social scene/student life. There's so many different kinds of Duke students from different places involved in different things, so everyone is able to really find their people

Duke is a University in a league of its own. Every aspect from the student body and professors the course offerings and athletics are incredible and inspiring. The campus is beautiful and Durham and its surrounding areas are constantly developing to offer more to the community. There is s use of oneness when you walk onto Duke's campus that only grows as you spend more time there.
The BEST school ever. I truly will always and forever be a blue devil. Unparalleled opportunities for growth across all domains.

I applied to Duke hoping to find a diverse learning community and lifelong friends. Instead, I found an egotistical prep school where your social status is directly related to how much money your parents make and whether you're from NYC, Miami, or New Jersey. Professors care more about flexing their accomplishments and knowledge than actually teaching, and take pride in giving students poor grades. People will ask you probing questions about your family's finances at parties (has happened multiple times) then scoff at you once they're sure they're richer than you. Incredibly cliquey and toxic place, applying here ED is the biggest regret of my life.
Fantastic education, quality of life, social scene, athletics (won men's college basketball championship during my attendance). Would go again if I could!

As a BME major, I have loved pretty much every single professor that I have had. The learning environment is incredible and enriching. You can tell how much your professors care about both your academic and personal growth. The athletic culture is awesome, and students have many opportunities to become engaged with their peers. The people, including other students, are phenomenal.

I liked that Duke offers a really diverse scene and you learn a lot from you peers who come from all around the world.
While at Duke though the school has one main objective: Profiteering out if your well being. In my last semester of graduate school I went through an injury that impaired my movement of an arm. At the time I had my own health insurance and provided many medical records and doctor notes asking to Duke to keep my plan. They denied every single one of my requests because I am an international student. I switched to Duke’s plan and had to pay two premiums at the time and an entire out of pocket maximum again of $2K. Additionally I had to switch clinics and pause my treatment for about 20 days. Thanks for ruining my health and finances Duke University.

Good athletics and with that, great school spirt. So many academic opportunities and resources, including opportunities to conduct research. A little more STEM oriented, lots of pre-meds here as we do have a strong program in medical research but I do feel like there is something for everyone here. I will say the list of available majors is not super large-for example, if you already know you want to specifically major in something like chemical engineering or architecture, this is probably not your place because we don't have those certain majors here. I think a little more neutral politically than its peer institutions, but still liberal-leaning. Dorms can vary a lot in quality but most are good. A lot of people double major and a lot of people study abroad. Very diverse student body who are all generally very inspiring and ambitious but not socioeconomically diverse.

I really enjoyed the people there, a very diverse community, excellent professors. Advising was a little less hands on than I would have wanted, you kind of have to have things figured out very early to succeed. Great dining hall, many options for all different kinds of diets.

My experience with Duke University has been incredibly meaningful. Being accepted into the Master of Nursing (MN) program was a dream come true for me, especially as someone who was born at Duke Hospital and always admired the university's commitment to excellence in healthcare. From the beginning, I was impressed by the program’s academic rigor, the dedication of the faculty, and the diverse, passionate student community. As a first-generation Nigerian-American, I felt seen, challenged, and supported in ways that have helped me grow both personally and professionally. One area I hope to see improvement in is financial support and accessibility for students from underrepresented and lower-income backgrounds. Overall, I am incredibly proud to be part of the Duke nursing community and hopeful that my time here will lead me to create positive change in both local and global healthcare settings.
The professors are amazing and care about students. There are so many opportunities for different experiences and research.
Although its online I do get many emails about different events on campus which I think help create a sense of community within students, especially if they are not originally from Durham.
Duke offers opportunities for every type of student. People from all backgrounds and with many different interests can find their place and fit in!

Duke offers a place for everyone and endless opportunities to explore both academically and socially. The sports culture is vibrant and helps bring the community together during important games, such as the Duke vs UNC Men's Basketball game. The Greek life plays a prominent role in the on-campus party scene, and is mostly widely accepting to people of all backgrounds. Professors are always willing to make time for their students, and often are pursuing their own research and service initiatives. Using them as a resource to network and get involved in their work is key to being a successful Duke student. Overall, Duke offers a diverse range of activities, from sports and academics to parties and community service, catering to everyone's interests.
I have not yet begun my experience at Duke, but so far, they have been very helpful and responsive to emails. Current students consistently share how much they love the program

ai_summary: Duquesne University offers a unique and supportive environment for students, emphasizing a close-knit community and accessible professors. The campus is often described as beautiful and well-located in downtown Pittsburgh, providing ample opportunities for internships and city engagement. While many students appreciate the strong academic programs and the commitment of faculty to student success, there are recurring critiques about the dining options, outdated housing, and the high cost of tuition . Student life is described as friendly but somewhat limited in nightlife and diversity, with some students noting that social activities often lead them to other parts of the city. Overall, Duquesne is seen as a solid choice for those seeking a personalized educational experience within an urban setting, though prospective students should weigh the costs and campus culture against their personal preferences.

reviews.text: I love it here it’s nice people and also nice atmosphere. Only annoyances are the snowy weather but otherwise perfect.

My experience at Duquesne has been amazing! I transfered here after my freshman year and It was truthfully the best decisions I have ever made. The staff and professors here are awesome. I love the nursing program. The professors within in it are just outstanding. I feel so comfortable going to talk to them and they actually make learning fun and exciting. I have truly made some of my best friends here. I joined a sorority when I got to Duquesne, and it let me meet some great people. After graduation, I plan on attending grad school to get my masters as a nurse practitioner. Thank you!

I thoroughly enjoyed my 4 years spent as an undergrad at Duquesne University. The campus is walkable, beautiful, safe and feels secluded inside of Pittsburgh's downtown area. With typical class sizes of 30-40 students, it gives students the opportunity to connect with their professors on a deep level. I would recommend joining some form of Greek life, for friendships and night-life activities.

Duquesne is a very friendly school with a wide range of students, professors are alright if you get one who has been teaching long enough and has it down. It is an expensive school from tuiton, food, housing, books, and even parking.

I have enjoyed my time at Duquesne and look forward to all then opportunities the College has to offer. I want to get more involved in the experiences of College life and can’t wait to grow and expand my studies there.
I would like more opportunities to know my career paths better and sooner than after the fact. As a freshman, it is some times hard to know what and where to get all the information. I am finding my way, I just wish it was a bit easier to do so.
I can’t wait to keep growing on my own and learning new ways every day.

My experience at Duquesne has been a mix of good moments and real challenges. The campus is beautiful and the community feels welcoming, especially when you first arrive. Some professors truly care and put effort into helping students learn, and that support makes a difference.
At the same time, other parts of the university feel unorganized. Communication can be slow, and getting the right help when you need it is not always easy. Some classes feel strong and engaging, while others lack structure or clarity. That imbalance has shaped my overall experience.
Duquesne has potential and there are resources here, but they are not always easy to access unless you already know where to look. I’ve grown a lot as a student, but I also think the school could improve how it supports students and handles academic services.
Overall, my time here has been okay. There were positives, but also enough frustrations to make it a solid three out of five.

I transferred from Cedarville University to Duquesne University after my freshman year of college. After transferring, I believe my experience at Duquesne University is a much better one. The professors are much more understanding and care more about the students. Each one is fully invested in the student's future and how they can help. While I do commute so I do not see much of the campus life, I hear that it is a good experience. The students are very welcoming, and I have been able to join several clubs to join the community.
I enjoy it overall. I think it is very fun and I njoy my major but being a commuter I feel overwhelemed with both my responsibilies in and outside of school.

For students seeking strong professional programs, smaller class sizes, and a city campus environment, Duquesne offers a reputable education with strong career outcomes. However, prospective students should be aware of some of the negative feedback surrounding the graduate experience, costs, and social life. Visiting the campus and speaking with current students can provide a more personal and balanced perspective on what to expect.

Duquesne stands out from the other Pittsburgh universities by its small, tucked-away feeling in busy downtown Pittsburgh. They offer numerous rigorous academic programs. It is not the greatest school for diversity, nor nightlife as majority of the students go down to the South Side, which can be unsafe.
I like the campus I think it is nice but there needs to be better teachers in the STEM department. The teachers there are not very good especially in the chemistry and biology classes.

I transferred here from the west coast and it feels like stepping into another nation in the worst ways. Everyone here is either in a clique or doesn't want anything to do with you. The food is repetitive and there is no life on the campus besides all the cliques leaving to Pitt or the southside for drinking and partying. If your looking to transfer from back in the west stay where you are, it was a mistake for me and I wouldn't want you to follow in my footsteps.

So far, my experience at Duquesne University has been good, despite funding for attending every semesters has been stressful. The professors are nice and the area feels comfortable and safe. I think something that I would want to be changed at the university is the funding opportunities. Although the school provides scholarships on the school website, it doesn't provide enough scholarships for the upperclass students, such as myself. I would also change the food options provided. For someone who has a specific eating diet, there is not much options for me when it comes to food.
It’s a fantastic school. They really make an effort to orient freshman and their families to everything they offer. The campus is beautiful and the entire staff is very engaging.

I love Duquesne University. My freshman year flew by so fast, I enjoyed all of my classes and my year. My professors were all amazing and so much better than high school teachers. They were all kind, and engaging as well as easy to understand and learn. I also met so many friends on the campus through Duquesne's student events. Orientation week for my freshman year was a great way to ease into college and meet tons of friends. The campus is gorgeous and the perfect mix of nature, homey, and not too much city. Overall my experience so far has been amazing, and I can not wait to go back for my sophomore year.

ai_summary: At East Carolina University, students frequently highlight the beautiful and diverse campus , noting a solid sense of community and school spirit, especially during football season. Academically, while many praise the dedication and support of professors, experiences can vary, with some classes being larger and less personalized. The vibrant student life offers numerous activities, clubs, and a lively social scene , although some feel the party culture can overshadow other events. Students appreciate the variety of majors and the helpful resources available, but there are mixed feelings regarding the dining options and dorm conditions. Overall, ECU is described as a welcoming place with many opportunities for personal growth , despite some logistical challenges related to campus facilities and services.

reviews.text: I loved East Carolina University! You had to put yourself out there, but it’s like that for every school!! 10/10 would highly recommend!
The off-campus student living is great. The food on campus is good. A lot to do around Greenville. The campus is walkable and entertaining.

I love ECU! I have been able to get involved with so many opportunities that I would’ve never thought I would’ve been apart of. Getting involved is so easy here, as there’s so much to do! Everyone on campus is genuinely nice and the school looks out for your wellbeing. Everyday when I walk on campus I genuinely feel so grateful to be apart of an amazing school!

I have genuinely enjoyed my time here at ECU as a biology major! I work an on campus job and have lots of leadership positions. If you just put yourself out there, you get rewarded with so many experiences! I have done study abroad through ecu, and it was an amazing program! I love the campus culture and the southern charm :)

ECU has given me so many amazing opportunities and I’m forever grateful for the people and community that I’ve found here! From the professors to the friends I made in the dorms I’ve never felt more surrounded by love and support for my feature! The Greenville community is one I’m proud to call my new home!

East Carolina University honestly is my home away from home, it’s amazing to be a Pirate. From the football games, the classes, the friends you make along the way, you’ll find it all. I have met some of my best friends of whom I consider like family to me at ECU. ECU also has the most helpful faculty and staff that I’ve ever met. Everyone is friendly at ECU and even the Professors and Graduate/Teaching Assistants are there to help you along the way no matter what questions you have. The dorms are very big with enough space for you to live comfortably, you’ll always feel safe in Greenville as well since there are also ECU Police patrolling alot of the time. ECU is a blast and it’s truly a pirates life for me!

I would like to see more diversity at East Carolina University. The STEM programs specifically I feel have the most attention at this institution and as someone who is partaking in a STEM major I am grateful for that. The housing there is not the greatest it is very unclean and outdated.

The Campus can be fun; however, the dorms are a mixed bag. Start early and do not wait. You could end up in a horribly old dorm. The science programs are great. Biology and Chemistry are amazing. I cannot speak for the non-sciences. Physics is hard and the department sucks. Psychology is amazing and fully immersive. You will enjoy yourself if you get involved. Join clubs, do greek life, volunteer and form bonds with like minded people. Don't close yourself off because of where you are from, your faith or religion, or even because your shy. College is what you make it and ECU can be great or it won't be. It all depends on you.
I have had some wonderful professors then I have had horrible experiences with just one. The school itself is good. Its all in what you make it for education and socially.

My experience at East Carolina University this past semester was wonderful. The university truly feels like home and I’ve made some of my closest friends here. I liked how the professors taught their classes and allowed us to truly understand the content.

So far, my time at ECU has been a rush of going to classes, completing assignments, and preparing for exams. I am taking a lot of classes though, so this is just my experience. I'm also a commuting student which sucks. Going to ECU is definitely better if you live on or close to campus. Most school events take place in the afternoon or at night, so I'm not usually able to go since I live 30 minutes away. There are many different things you can do at ECU. There seems to be a different thing going on everyday which is great if you enjoy variety. You'll find people from multiple backgrounds here.

There is absolutely nothing good about the school. They register is incompetent. The advisers are terrible. Financial Aid is a joke and the only thing they’re good at is withdrawing people they loved your money. They ignore you after enrolled treat you like you don’t matter and they don’t care once they got your money they’re done with you.
Drugs, overdoses, tattoos, piercings rapes there’s nothing good in this town nothing debauchery drunkenness some of the most disgusting things you’ve ever thought of. They’re happening in Greenville close around East Carolina University. Yeah don’t waste your money.
Pretty good some moderate safety concerns however. A lot of sex based incidents and fires as a result of carelessness but it’s still a good educational setting

My experience at East Carolina University has been rewarding and transformative. I wasn't my original plan to attend here until I got a scholarship, but it became the greatest opportunity of a lifetime. I’ve met great people, learned from passionate professors, and felt supported both academically and personally.

For someone who is attending ECU, my main thing for them to know is do not go with the flow. There is so much temptation here, clubs, parties, drinking/smoking shops around campus. Please do not follow the crowd or go with the flow, that won’t get you anywhere but lost and confused. Spend time figuring out your purpose and why you went here. When things get hard, great! It’s supposed to be hard. Learn to have endurance and perseverance through it all. Never let yourself go and continue to strive. Make yourself proud.

ai_summary: East Tennessee State University (ETSU) provides a unique college experience , combining a sense of community and personal growth with a diverse array of programs. Many students appreciate the supportive faculty who genuinely care about their success and offer individualized attention, fostering an engaging learning environment . The campus itself is often described as beautiful and friendly, with opportunities for outdoor activities and an active student life , including various clubs, sports, and cultural events. However, there are mixed reviews regarding dorm conditions, tuition costs, and the quality of some professors, with particular concerns about accessibility for disabled students and students of color. Overall, ETSU is seen as a welcoming place where students can establish lasting friendships and find their place in a vibrant community.

reviews.text: I love ETSU. The area is beautiful and provides for amazing abilities to enjoy the outdoors and culture. The school was engaging and campus life and cultural diversity was emphasized despite being in a less diverse area of TN. I would go back for graduate school if I were closer and lived in state. I recommend the school 100%

East Tennessee State University is a great school! The campus is beautiful, there are a ton of diverse groups, and great people. The professors are great and hilarious. I love the options of being able to attend in person or online. I have found my best friends here and love the profession I am going into.

Really good! I have enjoyed being involved in sports and campus activities, which have helped me feel more connected to the university. My professors have been kind, supportive, and encouraging. Being part of the orchestra and practicing the violin also has been one of my favorite experiences, as it is where I feel most included and supported. Other meaningful experience for me was my public speaking class. I began the course feeling very nervous and unsure of myself, but with my professor’s support, I gained confidence and learned that I was capable of more than I thought. ESTU has helped me grow in many ways, especially in independence and discipline. adapting was challenging at first, but I now feel more comfortable and connected. Working on campus, particularly doing translation work, has also been very rewarding.
I believe ETSU was a good choice. They really care about your academics. They also have really good resources to help students succeed.

East Tennessee State University has become not only a school where I have grown so much academically but it has also become a second home. The overall environment here at ETSU is comforting and ever so welcoming! I have developed so much as a person, as well as student, and continue to do so as I continue my education. I have met incredible people and have truly have never felt so welcomed so quickly. ETSU has become a safe place and I am so blessed!

It's what's close by to me so not my first pick if I could afford to go elsewhere. I'm a commuting student because I cannot afford housing at the university. Commuting means you're the last priority for campus experiences, whether it be activities or needing technology to finish assignments. Diversity is an ever growing struggle since a certain politician got into office. Professors are hit or miss, most are alright, some are awful, and some are really great. Safety is a no-go. Be very careful outside at night, homeless people harass students, campus police are questionable. Lots of nuisance protesters, as far as a group even putting up a large billboard of AI generated gore a few years back (and more than once). Digital Media program is great so if you want a local digital media program this is great. Big med studies department. Good computer science department. The studies are overall pretty great but everything else could use work.

I am still a current student at ETSU and I have enjoyed my time there. I did live in the dorm and have a great experience. The janitors always did their best to keep it as clean as possible and were always friendly when I would pass them in the hall. I have not had a “bad” experience so to speak. The only thing that I would complain about is sometimes the food in the dining hall or campus restaurants would taste old. But that was typically a rare occurrence.
ETSU has a great community and supportive professors. I’ve had a positive experience overall, though I’d like to see more campus events and improved parking options.
I love the culture and the scenery around campus. I would like to see the food quality improve. The basic classes are not very challenging.

My overall experience at ETSU I would say is pretty great, the school has a good mix of academic and social life all around. It's never hard to reach out to someone to find something to do or if you need help with any type of school work.

Great, doing well in all my classes in Computer Science. Im not staying on campus as I live only an hour away. My parents couldn't afford the extra to allow for living on campus. If I could get some of the help from these scholarships it would be a tremendous help.
So far so good! They have something going on everyday for students to do when they are free! Most people are very nice!
East Tennessee State offers state school amenities in a smaller class sized setting depending on the class level as well as your major.

I’ve lived in Johnson city, TN for majority of my life so I’m accustomed to the area. I don’t live on campus but when i go on campus there’s always something to see whether it’s food trucks, an ice skating rink being built, or just catching the mascot around.

There are a lot of professors who care about student success; however, there are others who do the bare minimum. There are lots of clubs to get involved in; however, the social life is subpar. Since lots of people live at home or go home on weekends, it is hard to connect with a lot of people.

ai_summary: At Texas A&M University-Commerce, students appreciate the small class sizes and strong relationships with professors , making for a supportive academic environment . While many faculty members are dedicated and helpful, some reviews express concerns about certain professors' effectiveness and the overall quality of academic resources. The campus is noted for its friendly atmosphere and diverse student body, fostering a sense of community. Student life is vibrant, with various activities and events; however, the town of Commerce offers limited options for entertainment, leading some students to travel to nearby cities for more variety. Although many find the tuition affordable, there are mixed feelings about the cost of living and food options on campus. Overall, students highlight the inviting and close-knit nature of the university, making it an appealing choice for those seeking a smaller, community-focused college experience.

reviews.text: I have no complaints about the school or specific program I am in. All the professors are amazing and understanding
This college is really such an interesting place to learn and find out who I am as a person and really this college helps me feel like a second home to learn and meet new people
I really like the rodeo coach and his support. I would like to see more funds into the rodeo program to allow for more rodeo scholarships.

I love the country side, but the partying is geting out of hand. The housing is so expensive, and the meal plan is kind of a rip off. We can't take anything home, we miss cafe times due to our schedules, and so much food is wasted daily on campus that us kids can use and store instead of waste. We could even give out plates to the homeless. It is overly priced and you can find yourself in a loop a lot. However, many of the staff and facualty have helped and guided me to find many solutions to these problems. I am greatful for the resources we have access to because many would be, sincerely, screwed without them.

The tuition is affordable, and classes are easy. The professors are friendly and invested in their students' success. It is easy to submit late work, and professors typically respond to emails promptly. I have had problems with courses not being accurate on my transcripts, so it is important to advocate for yourself. It was easy to be accepted into my bachelor's program, and I have enjoyed the cohort style of the social work program. It is important to understand how the scholarships apply to incoming students. Some scholarships (Blue and Gold and maybe others) only apply to fall students, which I did not receive because I started in the spring. I was really disheartened when I found that out because it said I was eligible. Overall, I have enjoyed my experience and would recommend others to check the school out and see if it is the right fit for you.

There has been its ups and downs at this school but I will say the campus life is amazing I made so many new connections and learned many lessons about fake people and messy individuals the parties the people the overall setting is fun always something to do when you’re with the right people
It's nice and quite, it feels like a family, the people are so nice they welcome you. The campus is not big but not small, is perfect, plus they're changing and improving a lot of things!!
As a online student I selected East Texas A&M because of its success rate of completing a degree. Seek out the school for success.

My overall experience at East Texas A&M University has been positive and rewarding. The professors are knowledgeable and genuinely care about students’ success, and the campus provides a friendly and diverse environment for learning. I’ve appreciated the sense of community and the opportunities for both academic and personal growth.
However, I would love to see more financial support options for international students, especially graduate assistants and those who are just starting out. Improving communication between departments and quicker responses regarding registration or holds would also make the student experience even smoother.
Overall, ETAMU is a great place to learn, grow, and connect with others.

East Texas a&m is a horrible school, who’s priorities are completely messed up instead of making more housing upperclassmen had to be put on an housing lottery because they decided to accept more freshmen with limited dorms, the advisors suck at their job, and they take financial aid without warning and place registration holds on accounts which make so many students not be able to go back to school, and not only that the professors think it’s okay to get into your face and scream at you so overrall commerce is a terrible school!!!

As a smaller four year university, I enjoy how the campus is not over crowded. Additionally, as a commuter there is always parking available even when it is busy. The smaller classes (20-40 from what I have observed) make it easier to connect with professors. Overall I have enjoyed my experience so far at East Texas A&M University.

I think it is a great school. All of my professors have been great so far. The only thing that has made my experience not so great is having to pay. My parents do not make so much and I don’t get my entire tuition covered so there is a portion I have to pay, which is a large amount. Overall, it is a great school.

My experience at East Texas A&M University has been rewarding. The faculty are supportive and committed to student success, and the small class sizes allow for more personalized learning. I’ve enjoyed being part of a close-knit campus community and getting involved in student activities. The environment is welcoming and encourages academic and personal growth. One area for improvement would be expanding course options in specialized subjects and increasing internship opportunities to better prepare students for their careers. Overall, it’s been a great place to learn and grow.

I am a transfer student and this fall 2025 semester will be my first at East Texas A&M University. From what I had gathered all students and faculty are extraordinarily welcoming. Staff and faculty will go out of their way to make sure if you have any question that they will get answer. Students are helpful and are open to talking to new students to help find the right peopler to guide you.

My experience at this University has been amazing. It creates a friendly environment for you to make lots of friends, have fun, and still handle business. I love the small campus events. It brings us together, and we get to meet and see a lot of new people.

ai_summary: Eastern Kentucky University offers a diverse range of academic programs and opportunities for student involvement, all set on a beautiful campus that many find welcoming and homey. Students appreciate the engaging faculty , with most professors being supportive and approachable, fostering a small college atmosphere within a larger university. While the campus hosts numerous activities that encourage socializing and community engagement, there are concerns about outdated dorms and dining options that could use improvement. Many reviews mention the ongoing renovations aiming to enhance the campus, though some feel these updates should prioritize student housing over new facilities. Overall, EKU is viewed as a solid choice for students seeking a supportive educational environment in a picturesque setting.

reviews.text: I've not yet attended but am planning to attend this fall semester. When I have talked with counselors over the phone from EKU, they have all been very helpful.
I start school in the fall 2026 semester. I have had a great experience with the staff so far. They are very helpful!

My time at Eastern Kentucky University has been transformative and full of growth. Academically, I’ve pursued my passion for food and nutrition while exploring culinary management and gastronomic tourism, gaining practical skills that prepare me for the future. Beyond the classroom, I’ve embraced campus life through clubs, events, and community involvement, building meaningful friendships and leadership skills. Each experience challenged me to step out of my comfort zone, whether in group projects, internships, or volunteering opportunities. EKU has not only strengthened my knowledge and confidence but also shaped me into a driven, well-rounded individual ready to pursue my goals. And on a more personal side EKU has given me the friends I used to pray for in High School. I have loved my time here and can not believe its almost coming to an end.

my oveall experirence was great! Besides money being an issue and not having financial help from family as been an issue and caused me to have to take a semester off to get a job full-time and pay out of pocket. I can't wait to continue my studies if i can get enough scholarships and money saved

As a full time, on campus EKU student, it's hard not to find at least one problem. Though, you could say the same for many Universities. I lived in Mattox Hall, and if you are not one to share a small space with another person, I do not suggest it. It can get pretty close and personal in there. Most professors are very welcoming and understanding, but I've had my fair share of hard to work with professors. There's a decent variety of food, but I am from a low-income household which is mostly white, so take that with a grain of salt.
What I like is that I can get to classes pretry fast since the buildings are close for my major. What I would like to see change is more better quality food.

In general, I felt extremely accepted into the overall school, especially as a queer person of color. The people are nice, our professors are easy to work with, and Big E welcome was very fun, if not for all the activities we had to do which would tire us out. The hall food in Casey's is diverse, but it can sometimes become repetitive. if you only ever go there for food. The halls are pretty standard as well, but sometimes (rarely) we may have issues with people running down halls.

I loved my teachers. Professor Maggie Frozena, my English 101 and 102 teacher, did so amazing! The work was actually fun to do and I retained so much that I learned. All the teachers were great so far. BigE the financial office at EKU, has been HORRIBLE! They won't reply, treat you as if you don't matter and that your "stupid". They have been so unhelpful and rude when I am able to get a response. The dean of students helped me so much. My advisor has been useless and very un-interested in anything I said. I had to go to the dean for any help. Financial aid is important and should be way less stressful! SNHU wasn't like that. So great once your IN but if you have financial issues, you'll go through hell with EKU's BIG-E
It is very nice all of the students and professors are very kind and there's lots of fun activities to do in the area.
I love how Eastern Kentucky University is not too small that I feel like I’m not getting a true college experience, but also not too big where I feel just like a number.

My overall experience at Eastern Kentucky University has been positive and rewarding. EKU offers a welcoming campus environment, supportive faculty, and a strong sense of community. Many professors are approachable and genuinely invested in student success, especially within major specific courses. I also appreciated the smaller class sizes, which made it easier to engage in discussions and build relationships with instructors and classmates.

I really enjoy the campus and the scenery. I enjoy the forestry and conversational areas such as Taylor Fork where students can walk around and enjoy the forest. The construction on campus is a lot, though.

My experience at Eastern Kentucky has been pretty good for the most part. I do like how we have little events that happen around campus, students go to and get free stuff. I don't think there needs to be much change to the school because I haven't heard any serious issues about the campus

My experience at Eastern Kentucky University has been good. I liked the friendly atmosphere and how helpful the professors were. The campus feels welcoming, and there are plenty of activities and groups to join, which makes it easy to meet people. I also liked that EKU tries to keep costs lower for students. However, some things could be better. Some dorms and buildings feel old and need updates. Parking can be difficult, and getting around campus isn’t always easy for everyone. I’d like to see improvements to housing, better parking options, and more support for students who need easier access around campus.
It’s overall a good school to go to. They try to make all students feel very included and put on various events

ai_summary: At Eastern Michigan University, students often highlight the diverse and welcoming environment , which facilitates connections among peers from various backgrounds. Many reviews point to the strong support provided by professors and academic advisors, making it easier for students to engage in their studies and seek help when needed. Class sizes are generally smaller, allowing for more personalized attention , although experiences with professors can vary widely. Student life seems vibrant, with numerous activities and organizations available, but some express that the campus can feel more like a commuter school with less weekend activity. While the campus itself is appreciated for its beauty and organization , concerns about housing quality , food options, and tuition affordability are common among students. Overall, EMU offers a supportive academic atmosphere enriched by diversity but is also scrutinized for some administrative and financial issues.

reviews.text: I enjoy it, Eastern is a very diverse university and offer a lot and have an amazing campus and campus life. They have a lot of on campus activities and try to make you feel very included and not left out at all. I really enjoy going here.

I have only toured eastern and heard about it I have not started college there yet. From what I saw though the campus is pretty nice and so are the dorms. It’s not in my favorite area of the city but it’s still okay. The food there is decent. I don’t know anything about the party scene because I am not enrolled there.

Attending Eastern Michigan University for my first year of college I had an interesting experience. I started off my first semester living on campus. My build had done nothing for me to get to know people and I felt like I was isolated in my room, I am not a party person and there was not a lot of building activities to get to know people that also lived there. I felt that my classes were decent and they weren't extremely challenging and I was able to learn a lot. My overall experience for my first semester was okay, but moving home for my second semester has helped a lot, even though I am still paying for my room due to them not allowing me to retract my contract.
I recently transferred to EMU after completing courses at a community college. I’ve had nothing but good experiences so far

There needs to be more dining locations across campus for students that live too far from the commons. I am sad to see this college close so many market locations that I would have relied on in between college classes. I do think that the food on campus tastes really good, but I would like to see more variety in food options. There are a lot of things you can do off campus, such as explore the city, or take a bus to Ann Arbor. Social life here is good if you have roommates or go to clubs. The housing is pretty bad here with broken ovens, thermostats, refrigerators, and doors. Or I just got unlucky with the dorm I got. Don't expect anything to be fixed in a timely manner either, as it takes weeks for anyone to come to your dorm to fix the issues.

I liked experiencing my college classes in person and gaining the knowledge I needed from my professors in order to improve myself in my future career. For the online classes, more communication is needed.

I made some of my best friends here and am very grateful for those past experiences. But with that being said, I would hope Eastern is not your first choice. I transferred from this school for athletic reasons, but after going to a new school I’ll tell you what EMU doesn’t have going for it. There is little to no school spirit and as an athlete it sucks to see. More people wearing UofM merch than their own school and students show out well too homecoming and maybe one other nice weather game. Next was the food. Definitely below average. The teaching wasn’t great in my opinion compared to the school I now go to. The teachers are at least passionate though. Ypsilanti is a nice town but going out at night anywhere besides Depot Town could be sketchy. Also no college party scene at the school or in Ypsi. All the fun will happen 12 mins away in Ann Arbor. This school will do its job for you, but just know you could get a similar college experience for less money or pay more for a better one
This is a great school and have had a very good experience with aviation program. I would highly recommend the school and program to perspective students.
Eastern is an interesting place to go. lots of diversity but not a lot of options when it comes down to food. it gets repetitive over time. and the housing is not that good
So far, as a freshman, I’ve liked college in general. In terms of eastern Michigan university, I have no other experience to compare it to but it’s not awful. A lot of good opportunities and classes
Staff does nothing to protect students from university staff. They also don't maintain FOIA documentation. Not a very safe location for students of varying walks off life.
The overall experience is okay. The university is very big on protecting students; the proffessor teach and classes are not that big so it feels more intimate and less overwhelming.

I have been at Eastern Michigan University for a year and a half now, and overall, it has been pretty good. I have met some wonderful people here, have taken some informative courses, and have been granted some amazing opportunities.
Overall I am not very involved with the university. I enjoyed how close to home it was and all the different educational opportunities. I would like to see them give more funding to the arts.

It's been a really great start to my freshmen year. I've adjusted really well to college and have meet alot of cool people. My professors that I've had thus far are very helpful and informative. There are only two things I wish were available for my college experience. I wish there was and area set aside for commuters where they could rest and take a power nap when there's a long wait between there next class when there's no assignments to work on. Also, if there was a monthly communication that went out that let students know of all activities that are taken place on campus.

ai_summary: Edgewood College offers a unique blend of small class sizes and a close-knit community atmosphere , which many reviews highlight as a strong point. Professors are described as dedicated and approachable, often going out of their way to ensure student success. The campus is conveniently located in Madison, providing access to the vibrancy of a bigger city while maintaining an intimate college environment. Students appreciate the variety of extracurricular activities and opportunities to engage with peers, although some express concerns about affordability and the value of tuition relative to experiences. Comments regarding food quality and diversity among the student body suggest room for improvement. While many students enjoy their time at Edgewood and value the relationships built here, some also report issues with course availability and administration responsiveness, indicating that the experience can vary greatly among students.

reviews.text: Edgewood University had been an excellent place to begin my educational career. Between the small class sizes and personal feel, Edgewood is a supportive environment. There are some drawback of a smaller school but the quality of professors, opportunities, and supportive environment makes up for it.
I personally have loved coming to Madison growing up. I thought I would love living here, but had a bad roommate situation and didn't like it. I've also had not so great experiences with professors.

Edgewood College is a fun place to be! You are right next to downtown Madison, which is great and has so many opportunities for work and fun. If you are looking for a job during the semester, Edgewood has some right on campus!

ai_summary: Elon University garners a mix of sentiments from students, with common highlights centering around its supportive community , beautiful campus , and strong academic relationships with professors. Many students appreciate the small class sizes , which foster personal connections and dedicated teaching. However, there's a notable divide regarding social life; while some find the vibrant Greek life and party scene appealing, others feel excluded if they're not part of these circles and remark on a lack of robust social activities outside of partying. The campus culture is described as friendly and welcoming, though some students express concerns about its socio-economic homogeneity and limited diversity. Additionally, while the university offers many opportunities for engagement and experiential learning, a few reviews point to issues with housing, dining options, and academic rigidity in certain programs. Overall, prospective students interested in a close-knit community with strong academic support may find Elon a fitting choice, but those seeking a more dynamic and diverse environment might want to consider their options.

reviews.text: It's a great university that offers plentiful enriching opportunities such as scheduled internships and study-abroad programs to give students genuine hands-on experiences to enhance their perspective. The professors want to see all students succeed equally as much as the students and parents do. It's a small but friendly campus with a lot of great people.

Spending my four years at Elon was the best experience. I was in the business school, and had incredible professors of all different backgrounds. The campus is beautiful, the food is great, and there is a lot to do! Wouldn't change a thing.

Absolutely loved my time at Elon. I met some of the best people I’ve ever met and cultivated some of my most cherished memories. Elon’s academic opportunities and emphasis on wholistic experiences gave me all the tools I needed to be successful in the real world.
Great business program overall!!! I’m part of business fellows and it’s a great program! I recommend you apply
It was so much fun! I was able to build such great relationships with faculty and staff and got to know them beyond the classroom.
i love elon! the LSB professors are by far the most amazing! they go above and beyond! great mentorship!

Loved Elon! The campus is beautiful and the professors are great! The international programing is incredible and gives students a well-rounded educational experience. I am grateful for my time there and thankful for the experiences I had there as a undergraduate student.

I guess the whole experience was just mid. Not terrible, just mid. Academics mid, professors mid, school spirit...well, you get the point. Probably the worst part is its location. Rural, small town in the South. Not ideal.

Elon was a great school, I deeply enjoyed my time there. My freshman year was a little atypical because we were in the midst of a global pandemic. But Elon managed to keep spirits high and keep students on campus. I joined a sorority my freshman year and was able to experience some great opportunities through that organization. The school has a beautiful campus, that they’re working to expand. I’m also impressed with Elon’s current efforts in expanding the university to Queens College in Charlotte.
Overall good experience. Great professors, student life and academics. For the price housing is ok and would appreciate more visibility regarding program oportunities.

I should have transferred. Some of these professors are so horrible and lack basic human decency/empathy. Campus police don’t do anything to keep students safe, they care more about giving tickets and protecting the locals running the 2 bars in the area. This school is as performative as it gets.

when I visited the campus to see if it was the right fit for me, all the students seemed very outgoing and kind. The tour guides were also amazing, and students were out and about compared to sitting in their rooms all day.
Elon University is so cool. Absolutely adore this campus and what they do. Elon is a place where experiential education and faculty mentorship blend into something so beautiful.
It is a friendly, inclusive, beautiful, and caring community. The classes are small and the professors are great. There is a club/activity for everyone.
Fantastic professors lots of students from the north east. It’s a small town setting, but stunning Campus.

ai_summary: Emory University is known for its rigorous academic environment and strong support from professors , who are generally accessible and genuinely invested in student success. Many students appreciate the diversity within the student body and the wide range of clubs and organizations that promote community engagement. The campus is frequently praised for its beauty and safety , along with opportunities for research and networking. However, reviews highlight some concerns, such as the cleanliness of facilities, limited dining options, and a lack of school spirit, especially due to the absence of prominent sports teams. There's also feedback suggesting that student interactions can be cliquish, and some feel that engagement in campus life often revolves around Greek life. The cost of tuition and limited financial aid remain points of contention for many students. Overall, Emory offers a blend of strong academics and a warm community, albeit with certain areas needing improvement.

reviews.text: While Emory University provides high-level resources and reports strong overall career outcomes, my experience suggests that individual students may encounter significant obstacles in securing internships and employment, particularly when seeking early, industry-specific opportunities. The reported employment statistics may overrepresent positive outcomes due to methodological exclusions. Students should proactively supplement official career services with networking, alumni outreach, and external application efforts.
Some departments have more funding than others and it is very visible. Housing is good, but can be very expensive. Food options change frequently, less accessible for upperclassmen

Challenging academic environment, especially great education for pre-health majors, business majors, and creative writing / arts majors. Class size is small (excluding large lecture classes) and professors are usually very willing to help their students
grew to like it but really rough start. ROUGH. hard to find community because everyone's in cliques. but after 4 years, i was able to find people i vibed with
less workload than other colleges on similar level, great experience studying in the campus, but little sports atmosphere.

My time at Emory was great, and I was able to make a lot of new friends that I still keep in touch with til this day! There were some professors who weren't my favorite, but they pushed me to be a better person!
This school has a great campus and such a beautiful view. There are many other school nearby so it’s easy to run into many different people.

Administration has hijacked the university from its original mandate and drastic change is needed before they get 10 cents of our money.
The expenses of the professors boondoggle, the excessive building on campus, the entire place seems to run to benefit the corrupt administration.
The endowments have lead to hubris. I am deeply ashamed of the place and wish the the alumnus had a real say it the operation of this failed institution

Emory has given me immense clarity about the professional path I want to take now and after college by emboldening me to explore a diverse array of paths within Music and Film, largely thanks to the school's network of incredible educators and academic resources and most importantly the several (former) strangers from all different parts of the country and world who I have grown to adore like my own family. Coming off of a gap year after receiving my Associate's Degree, I am eager to build upon my first two years by grasping at every available moment; I want to graduate knowing that I took every course that may have interested me and then some, or that I sat down and shared a meal or a conversation with someone new and discovered a foundation for a friendship I would've never known otherwise. These are the qualities of Emory that give me the confidence to continue through financial, academic, and personal struggles; This school reminds me to have faith in the path I am on.

My time at Emory University has been both challenging and rewarding. The professors are knowledgeable and genuinely invested in students’ success, and there are plenty of resources and opportunities for research, leadership, and growth. The campus is beautiful, and the diverse student body makes it easy to find a community through clubs and organizations.
While I’ve had a great experience, some administrative processes—especially around financial aid and housing—can feel slow or unclear. Communication from certain offices could also be more consistent. Overall, Emory has pushed me to grow academically and personally, and I’m grateful for the experiences and connections I’ve made here.

Emory University offers a vibrant and intellectually stimulating environment, with passionate professors, diverse perspectives, and strong academic resources. I’ve appreciated the collaborative atmosphere, where students support each other while pursuing excellence. The campus is beautiful and well-maintained, fostering both focus and community. I value Emory’s commitment to global awareness and service, which encourages personal growth beyond academics. Overall, Emory provides a well-rounded, challenging, and inspiring experience that prepares students for success both professionally and personally

My experience at Emory has been both challenging and rewarding. I’ve appreciated the supportive professors, diverse academic opportunities, and strong sense of community. At the same time, I think more resources could be dedicated to easing the stress that comes with rigorous coursework, especially for pre-med students. Overall, it’s been a place that has pushed me to grow academically and personally

Being a part of the athletics program at Emory has given me access to really great support and help in academics. I really appreciate the time Emory puts into the athletic programs however I would like to see a little more support in the nutrition side of that. I would like to see better nutritional options and fueling stations for after life and conditioning

Emory University provides an enriching blend of rigorous academics and real-world application. With close ties to the CDC and top hospitals like Emory Healthcare, students gain hands-on experience in public health, medicine, and research. Emory’s liberal arts foundation fosters critical thinking and interdisciplinary learning, preparing students for global impact. The campus is filled with vibrant spaces. Lull water Preserve offers a natural retreat, while Emory Village and Cox Hall buzz with student life. The Michael C. Carlos Museum exposes students to world cultures, and Atlanta’s booming tech, business, and arts scenes create endless opportunities beyond the classroom. At Emory, learning isn’t confined to books, it’s embedded in community, innovation, and leadership.
Emory is a wonderful university that is continuing to diversify their programs including my nurse anesthesia program. The faculty are very knowledgable and supportive.

ai_summary: Fairfield University offers a mix of experiences that students find valuable and challenging. Academically, many praise the engaged professors and small class sizes that foster close relationships, particularly in popular programs like nursing and business. However, some students express dissatisfaction with the core curriculum's extensive requirements and the perception that not all majors receive the same level of support. Student life is described as both vibrant and cliquey , with a tight-knit community that's further enhanced by a beautiful campus and a quaint town atmosphere. Social opportunities are available, but some reviews note a lack of diversity and a challenging environment for those who don't fit into established social groups. While the university is praised for its commitment to student services and the opportunities available, tuition is a common concern, with calls for more financial aid. Overall, Fairfield University is depicted as a place where the right student can thrive, but experiences vary significantly depending on individual circumstances.

reviews.text: It’s close to home and I’m given the support and resources that I need to financially support myself through

I think Fairfield University is a great school when individuals are considering to pursue a nursing or business degree. Everyone on campus is very nice but for housing they have been accepting too many students leading to overcrowding. So, I would consider what you would want to look for in a dorm. The food is pretty consistent and not bad. If that was a concern you want be getting the same diversity that you may receive at other schools but you get consistency at least.

Fairfield offered great academics and great opportunities to connect and learn more about your future career and networking opportunities. The area is great, and the train is super accessible to go into New York, the beach has great darties. The bars are good, but far too expensive for what they are, but still good options. My complaints about the school are about the lack of campus communities and opportunities to meet others. The club fair was almost just for performance, because a lot of the clubs I signed up for don't seem very active or reliable. Of course, there is no greek life, which would be fine, but I really feel like there isn't anything super distinct that is filling that hole. The sports teams do fun things, and its easy to meet people in your classes, but if you don't like your roommate and didn't have a random situation where you found friends, I find it disappointing the lack of active communities to join and meet others.

I really enjoyed my experience at Fairfield. I enjoyed all of my classes, especially the class sizes. They were small, where I made meaningful connections with both students who are now some of my closest friends and professors, with whom I still stay in touch today.

Fairfield University dining has been a major letdown, not only due to the food quality but also because of how students are treated. The management and staff often appear disrespectful and unprofessional. There have been multiple instances where staff openly mock students, make sarcastic remarks, or laugh within earshot when students ask reasonable questions or express concerns. This behavior creates an uncomfortable and unwelcoming atmosphere in a place students are expected to use daily.
