The food only adds to the frustration. Options are repetitive, poorly cooked, and frequently served cold or overdone. Quality varies widely, and dietary restrictions or preferences are often ignored. Considering the high cost of tuition and mandatory meal plans, the dining experience falls far below expectations. Overall, Fairfield dining feels poorly managed, dismissive of student feedback, and disconnected from what students actually need
I’ve thoroughly enjoyed my time. School is very safe and nice environment. Professors are very nice.
Every school has its good and bad. The issue right now is over admission. Accepting so many students is hindering housing and course selections.

My overall experience at Fairfield has been great, I have been able to not only thrive academically, but also socially. My major requires a heavy course load, but I still have time to do all the extracurriculars I want and have a great social life!
Fairfield University fails to do their part in student advocacy, ask their departments what you need, and the communication and the process is very slow.
I like everything about the school. It is just a great place to be overall. I play for men's soccer and it is just such a good environment to be a part of as well are a family
it has been an amazing experience here at fairfield, the people the place the help is all top notch. i couldn’t ask for a better place ngitonment.
My overall experience at Fairfield has been nothing short of fantastic thus far and am looking forward to the next year and a half
I love my time here at Fairfield. The vibes are amazing and the town and people are top. There is always something to do and the athletics team are so good
It’s amazing such a fun school and an amazing people. Need to less the restrictions on the beaches as that’s half the reason you go to Fairfield.
I’ve really enjoyed my time at Fairfield Univwrsity. Especially the conversations and food at the Tully excellent staff plus good chefs equals gooa experience.

ai_summary: Ferris State University has garnered mixed reviews, particularly highlighting its strong academic support and committed faculty . Students appreciate the small class sizes which facilitate personal interaction with professors, making it easier to get help and succeed. However, there are complaints about teaching quality, particularly regarding clarity and engagement. The campus atmosphere is generally considered friendly and inclusive , with a range of extracurricular activities and events, although some reviews note that the town of Big Rapids can feel limited in entertainment options. Dining options received mixed feedback, with a desire for greater diversity in food choices . Many students have also noted the importance of navigating available resources for a smooth transition, particularly for first-generation college students. Lastly, while Ferris offers a competitive education at a reasonable cost, concerns about rising tuition and financial aid limitations were mentioned by some. Overall, Ferris State University promotes a supportive learning environment but has areas that may benefit from improvement.

reviews.text: ferris was a good college for me. It wasn't a huge college like U of M but not a small size either. The 7500 students was just about right for me. I was able to fine a great group of friends fairly easily from the dorm life and programs to get involved with. The classes were small enough that I felt that I could get to know my professors and navigate academics well. The only draw back for someone with a business degree is that Ferris didn't have large tentacles into the business world that could help me find internships and jobs after college like some of the bigger universities in our area. That was my only disappointment, but I would choose it again. No regrets
Ferris is a very diverse university. It has great professors who are ready to help you with anything. The area is also beautiful and very easy for you to get around and enjoy being off of campus.

Small town, good school. Diverse spectrum of majors and career opportunities. Active campus, small-medium sized classes. Would this university recommend to anyone who cares about finding work straight out of college

I have learned a lot while attending Ferris State University. Not only did I learned much more about the degree that I am pursuing, but I also learned why I desired to pursue my degree. I applied for Ferris near the end of my senior year of high school because I did not know my strengths, nor the purpose that I wanted to fulfill. I felt like I was behind, especially when I felt like everyone went right past high school to fulfill a meaningful life of their own. College has helped me discover who I am and who I am meant to be, and that I am not nearly as close to being behind as I so thought I was. I have learned that everyone around me shares similar thoughts and that we are so much more alike than I wanted to believe. We all have our own issues that we deal with alone. I now see how everyone strives to be their best. It took one professor for my perspective on life to change, and I am grateful for the opportunity to grow. I would like Ferris to focus more on student needs.

This is a good school with good professors and small enough classes to get individual attention. They also offer multiple consortium programs which makes it easier to access for students who live in places other than Big Rapids. A change I would like to see is with scholarships. I have a scholarship based on being full time and unfortunately with scheduling and classes overlapping in my consortium, I won't be able to go full time in the fall of 2026, which means I am probably going to lose my scholarship and won't be able to get it back when I go back to full time the following semester.

Ferris State University is a great University that you should seriously consider attending. The university offers many competitive majors, great student life, and is located within a small town that is friendly and peaceful. I have no complaints about the University, I am a Business graduate student. What I would like to see changed is the community more involved and adapting to the University. Ferris is a major bright spot in the Big Rapids town. I think the community should have more entertainment, and restaurants to accompany this major University that brings lots of diversity both domestically and globally.

Ferris State University is very small which allows for small class sizes and great opportunities to develop relationships with students and professors. The housing is what you'd typically expect from a university; outdated, no ac, and bad dining hall food. Despite that, the more affordable options for single dorms are nice.

The professors largely don't care, and some just assign the zoom videos they made in 2020 while they don't teach. I had three or four good profs in my 3 years there. Administration lies and is unhelpful if you try to speak up in terms of these terrible professors, to the point where you cant do anything. I was required to take a class, pay $455 for a class where we colored, made paper air planes, practiced meditating, etc. Scam. Attendance is also required for 99% of classes ...while you're the one paying them.
I love Ferris State. They helped me get acclimated to college again. Nothing but a positive experience and teachers are helpful and flexible

Beautiful campus, amazing people, great food! I loved many aspects of being there including the many options of clubs, many options for those who like music, and it's just overall a great experience. However something I would love to see changed is the lack of certain amenities and the limitations in advanced degrees and courses.

I am just starting as a freshman, so I don't have much experience yet to tell others about. I however hope to find a modern, up-to-date school, who will help me to develop the technical and personal skills I need to start a successful and purposeful career.

My first semester at Ferris was all online so I haven’t yet got to experience the school. All the counselors are so helpful and knowledgeable. I came first orientation and the school made that lots of fun.
My overall experience at Ferris State has been very good. They offer lots of classes and scholarships.

I am a transfer student into Ferris State University, and this will be my second year there (making me a college senior) and I absolutely love the culture on the campus and all the friends I have made so far. The Big Rapids town is okay, but at least the university has fun activities and events that happen within it to keep college students enjoy their college experience and get involved too. I haven't had any problems yet on the campus grounds, and I hope to keep that the same. Otherwise, FSU is a great school for people to attend to, and the Construction Management program there is very phenomenal.
The campus is very grand and fun to explore. The professors are very helpful for education and other important information you need to become better.

ai_summary: Florida A&M University (FAMU) offers a unique and enriching experience characterized by a vibrant campus culture , a strong sense of community, and a commitment to academic excellence. Many students appreciate the diverse and supportive environment , where they feel safe and welcomed, especially as part of an HBCU. Academic programs in fields like business, pharmacy, and engineering are well-regarded. Students often highlight the dedication of faculty members who genuinely care about student success, as well as numerous opportunities for personal and professional growth . While the campus life is lively, featuring events, organizations, and a notable marching band, some reviews mention the need for improvements in facilities, administration, and financial aid processes. Overall, students feel a strong connection to the institution and recognize its historical significance and legacy of Black excellence , making it a compelling choice for those seeking a culturally rich college experience.

reviews.text: My overall experience at Florida A&M University was incredibly rewarding. The culture, campus life, and sense of community created an ideal environment for a college student. Tallahassee is a great college town where much of the city revolves around student life, so there was always something happening and it was hard to ever feel bored. I appreciated the new dorms, updated facilities, and access to great recreational spaces like the gym and athletic fields. Academically, my courses were both challenging and engaging, which helped me grow as a student and professional. Overall, my experience at FAMU was truly top tier.

My experience at FAMU has been amazing. The students and staff are very welcoming and encouraging, they make it known they are there to help if needed as well as helping you transfer smoothly into the college lifestyle.
My overall experience at FAMU has been great. It has allowed me to connect with many people from different locations and gain knowledge from not only professors but also other students.

Florida A&M University is more than a campus; it is a legacy of excellence, resilience, and purpose. As a proud HBCU student, FAMU creates an environment where students are not only educated but also empowered. From the moment you step on the Highest of Seven Hills, you feel a sense of belonging, pride, and responsibility to succeed not only for yourself, but for your community. FAMU challenges you academically while surrounding you with mentors, leaders, and peers who genuinely want to see you thrive. The culture is rich with history, tradition, and innovation, blending academic rigor with school spirit, service, and leadership. Whether through student organizations, service opportunities, research, or the arts, FAMU pushes students to grow into confident, socially conscious professionals. The FAMU experience is about becoming part of a family that prepares you to lead, serve, and create change long after graduation.

My time at Florida A&M University was a deeply meaningful chapter in my academic and personal development. The university offered an environment rooted in cultural pride, community, and excellence that allowed me to grow with intention. Being surrounded by peers and faculty who valued history, leadership, and collective success strengthened my confidence and sense of belonging. Academically, I was encouraged to think critically, engage deeply with course material, and apply what I learned beyond the classroom. Personally, FAMU taught me resilience, self discipline, and the importance of showing up fully for myself and others. The connections I built and the experiences I gained continue to shape my values, work ethic, and long term goals. My time at Florida A and M University laid a strong foundation that I carry forward with pride.

I have visited this school a couples of times and every time that I been to this school, I have always loved it. I have seen myself attending this school ever since I have stepped on the campus for the first time,
Love the school. I have been around the school since I was a little kid. I have generations of family that graduated from this illustrious university.

I love FAMU, it’s an incredible school that stays deeply connected to its roots. The professors have so much personality and are often very down-to-earth and easy to talk to. They genuinely push you to get involved, step outside your comfort zone, and enjoy the full college experience.

Overall, it is a great university, I feel at home, a great sense of community, and strong in my academics with the professors I have taken thus far. There are many options that have helped me get involved and find the best crowd for me. Along with the staff who have helped me find the right track to be on for my goals post-college.

FAMU is a wonderful school to attend and has been worth every penny. The atmosphere and culture upheld by not only faculty but alumni and students is incredible and teaches you how to become great in whatever career field you have interests In. Campus life is great. The dorms are clean and comfortable. Everyone you meet is nice and friendly. Plus when you’re surrounded by many incredible people who are working hard towards their goal's, it pushes you to strive for greatness as well.

I had a pretty eye-opening experience attending my first semester at FAMU. It is definitely a different scene than I was used to. The campus life is pretty bustling, with events occurring every other week. Campus pride is even greater. The communication at the university leaves more to be desired. Professors and advisors alike are hard to contact, and when able to get in contact, their responses are often lackluster. Be advised that the student fees per credit hour are pretty steep.

My overall experience with Florida A&M University was very positive and transformative. FAMU provided a supportive academic environment that emphasized excellence, leadership, and community engagement. The faculty were knowledgeable and invested in student success, offering guidance both inside and outside the classroom.
Beyond academics, the university fostered a strong sense of pride, culture, and networking opportunities that helped shape my professional growth. Being part of the FAMU community strengthened my communication skills, critical thinking, and confidence, and it prepared me well for my career path.

I enjoyed my experience very much. The environment was very diverse and I was able to learn more about my history as a young black woman in America and connect with other amazing people of color. I was able to obtain my doctorate of physical therapy

My experience with Florida A&M University has been inspiring and welcoming. I loved the strong sense of pride and community on campus—FAMU makes you feel like you’re part of something bigger. The faculty and staff I met were supportive, and the campus environment encourages leadership, growth, and academic excellence. There are always events, organizations, and opportunities that help students get involved and connect with others.
If I could change anything, I’d like to see clearer communication between departments and students, especially with financial aid and other administrative processes. More resources for student support and continued improvements to campus facilities would also make the experience even better. Overall, FAMU offers a powerful, motivating atmosphere that pushes students to aim higher and believe in their potential.

My overall experience at Florida Agricultural and Mechanical University has been good. I enjoy my time on campus and have met a lot of great connections. However, the staff and the professor aren’t the best help. I had to navigate on my own for the most part as a transfer student this Fall; but on the bright side I’ve grown in so many ways since August.

ai_summary: Florida Atlantic University (FAU) offers a diverse and welcoming environment with a vibrant campus life that includes a variety of clubs, sports, and engaging events. Many students find the professors to be knowledgeable and genuinely caring about their success, helping to create strong educational relationships. However, some reviews suggest that the academic rigor varies, with certain courses and professors being less effective in teaching. While the campus is praised for its beautiful location and facilities , issues like parking and limited dining options have been noted. Overall, students appreciate the friendly atmosphere and sense of community , though experiences can vary significantly, particularly for commuters. FAU appears to be a good fit for those prioritizing location, diversity, and supportive faculty, but potential students should consider their academic goals and preferences when making their decision.

reviews.text: So far, my experience at Florida Atlantic University has been unmatched. I love the environment, energy, and people on campus. I have truly found my home here at Florida Atlantic. The surround area of FAU is perfect as well. South Florida has endless career and job opportunities specifically in big cities like Miami and Fort Lauderdale. FAU has offered many opportunities to get involved on campus like Greek life and other clubs. As much as I love FAU, there are few changes that I think could be made. There is limited housing for students which is a big problem. Students also complain about advisors and professors.

Overall I have mixed feelings about my time at FAU. Academically, its a great school. Most professors are very polite and understandable. Courses are tough but there are many resources like SI and tutoring available for free for students, and the tutors there are typically very knowledgeable and helpful.
Socially, this school is mediocre. There is not really a student atmosphere, we dont feel like a "team" so to say. Most people are on campus to just go to class and leave as soon as possible. Theres also not many fun social events; they are either hard to find or have very low attendence. Ive tried reaching out to a few clubs using the website but most of them are abandonded and dont reply. Ive seen this happen first hand since my favorite club closed down because no one would lead it after the main body graduates.
If I could go back, I would not go to FAU. After graduating I realized that I could have got a good education elsewehere that also offers a good social life.

Florida Atlantic is like the breath of fresh air you look for when touring for a college to attend. With fantastic academic opportunities, student life and local culture it’s truly the University to be.

I went to one of the choose your FAU events for admitted students on February 16th. This experience was amazing, I met a lot of great people. I got the opportunity to tour the dorms and I absolutely loved Atlantic Park Towers.

Its a nice school but not well organized, and not enough discipline on the teachers. There are very few interesting clubs and most are inactive or hard to learn anything about. The food quality changes depending on the day. It's not that bad but it's not amazing. It would make everything better if they had better electives to choose from.

I just came this spring semester. It's been a month so far and I can say that I am really having a great time at FAU. All of my professors are great, the lectures are taught well. The dining hall is actually good, their food is fresh and flavorful. The dorms are nice, big, and clean. And everyone here is very nice.

My experience at FAU has felt like a vacation conjoint with my education. Never would I have imagined that I could experience a life where my weekdays consist of class and work but my weekends can be good times with my friends and a large amount of time at the beach. Its safe to say FAU isn't your typical college experience but this is an experience that I want to continue having and pursue my degree.
I have overall enjoyed my time at FAU, the campus is beautiful and south florida is an amazing area to be, I just feel they are slightly struggling to keep up with the schools increasing enrollment.

I truly loved every moment of my experience. I became deeply involved on campus by joining several organizations within the College of Business and serving as a student ambassador for the college. I made the most of every opportunity and am incredibly grateful for the people, experiences, and growth this school has given me.

Florida Atlantic has an amazing campus. Everything was walking distance and it wasn't hard to locate classes. Professors were great as well. The financial side of it wasn't great as I need help financially but they couldn't help. Needless to say, what I was offered with an addition to my FASFA did not cover room and tuition which lead me to doing school full time online.
Most STEM professors are incredibly knowledgeable in their field and are willing to explain topics in depth. FAU also offers many free tutoring services and help centers for classes.

My time at FAU was in 2020-2022 and was heavily influenced by COVID. I had only 2 semesters in person and my online semesters were hardly anything to write home about. I earned my BA in psychology with a philosophy minor. The philosophy program (like many in the country) is criminally underfunded resulting in overworked professors which in turn affects course quality. Overall, I received engaging lectures, decent TAs and an education that somewhat prepared me for grad school. FAU is like many public universities in that it pumps out graduates to prepare them for the workforce rather than emphasizing a quality education. This is demonstrated in their quick adoption of AI tools through canvas among other profit-driven decisions within the administration.

As a junior environmental engineering student, my time at FAU has been challenging but rewarding. The new engineering facility is impressive, as well as the latest equipment and other student-free facilities, such as the student union and its entertainment center. I like the location, Boca Raton, as it is a diverse area with all types of cultural hubs. There are a plethora of student organizations at FAU for which I am a part of (NSBE and ASCE). Things I would like to see change is the parking and some of the approaches in teaching regarding tenured professors. Some professors have been able to teach multiple courses at a time, which is acceptable, but for a concerning portion, can inhibit students from advancing towards completing their degree.
I love it so far. Great place to go to school, I definetley would reccomend it to anyone in the college search.

My journey at Florida Atlantic University has been one of growth. During my freshman year, I struggled to adjust to the demands of college life, which affected my academic performance, social connections, and overall well being. Transitioning from a high-achieving high school student to navigating these challenges was humbling.
In my sophomore year, I experienced a serious car accident that became a turning point in my life. This moment forced me to reevaluate my priorities and commit to meaningful personal and academic change.
Now, as a junior, I am proud to have earned a place on the Dean’s List twice. I have developed strong discipline, time-management skills, and resilience. These experiences have shaped me into a focused, motivated student who approaches education with determination, gratitude, and a clear sense of purpose.

ai_summary: Florida Gulf Coast University (FGCU) offers a beautiful , nature-integrated campus that many students appreciate for its aesthetic appeal and peaceful environment. The small class sizes allow for more personal interaction with professors, who are generally viewed as supportive and invested in student success. However, some reviews mention concerns about the university's academic offerings being limited and the challenges of housing availability. Student life is vibrant, with numerous clubs and events, though some students feel that making connections can be difficult if you're not outgoing. Overall, FGCU provides a friendly , community-focused atmosphere with a strong emphasis on environmental awareness and student support services, but it's also noted that the growing enrollment may be straining resources and infrastructure.

reviews.text: I liked the professor because they made the class interesting and easy to follow. Their explanations were clear, and they always took time to answer questions, which helped me understand difficult topics better. I appreciated how organized the lectures were and how the professor encouraged participation from everyone. The assignments were fair and related well to the material we covered. I also liked how the professor was approachable and supportive outside of class, making it easier to get help when needed. Overall, the class was a positive experience, and I feel like I learned a lot.

FGCU is a beautiful school with great amenities such as the university wellness center in South Village and the large library. The campus is very clean and all of the professors are nice and passionate about their classes.
Florida Gulf Coast is great and I have enjoyed my time here! There are so many different opportunities they offer and I feel as if my path moving forward is clearer than ever.
The school is absolutely amazing I am so happy to go here. The sun shines everyday and the people here treat me so well.

FGCU is a very nice school and it is in a great area. I have loved my time here so far. I think that campus is a perfect size as it is not too small or big. I have met some of my closest friends here and I love it here.

I enjoy all of the events on campus, there is at least one event on campus in the lawn that offers a space where people can be very close and happy without anything negative. They also offer a lot of resources when it comes to helping individuals get through their collage life.

Have some extremely good and intelligent professors but some are bad. I liked the gym and the structure of the campus. Also, the smaller classes make it easy to talk to professors and get additional help.

Overall, my experience at Florida Gulf Coast University has been very positive. I appreciate the strong sense of community, approachable professors, and the real-world focus of many programs, especially in hospitality and business. The campus is beautiful and creates a welcoming environment that makes it easy to get involved and feel connected. I’ve also valued the leadership and professional development opportunities available through student organizations and campus jobs. One area I’d like to see improve is course availability during peak registration times, as some required classes fill quickly. Overall, FGCU has provided me with meaningful academic experiences, personal growth, and preparation for my future career.

Campus and student life are all amazing, but the Housing is the most terrible thing I've ever seen in my life and is the reason I'm leaving this college. Directors and their staff are professional in making people's life difficult and are not worried at all about solving student's problems.
I have only been at FGCU for one semester, but I have already met many kind people. Everyone is very welcoming.

I like the smaller class sizes at FGCU, however advising is terrible here. I really like the on campus housing options. I wish there were more options for food around campus and more transportation off campus for students without cars, especially in summer semesters.

It is an amazing school. There are so many volunteer opportunities as well as things to do. My favorite part about this school is the campus and the beautiful places you can go. If you are an environmental science major or marine this school is for you.

My experience at FGCU has been mainly positive. I did dual enrollment for two years and I have had the pleasure of saying it has been great. My professors have been helpful, advising staff also, and the campus and onboarding staff have really made the University feel like home. My major gripe is that the engineering school is small, and does not have my intended major Electrical Engineering.

I love that they are environmentally friendly, they take into account light pollution by using less harmful lights, and they also turn off the lights late at night to save power. Something else that can be seen as a good or bad thing is that they are all about freedom of speech, on campus there is a preacher who yell his opinions and yes he is annoying, but he is allowed to give his opinions as long as he is not harming anyone. Another thing this university has is busses that take you from your dorm room to the campus, busses that take you on trips to decrease excessive driving, and it is a ever improving university building new structures every year.

Beautiful smaller school ethically designed to limit the negative impact of the school on the gentle surrounding ecosystem of Southwest Florida. Essentially it's all the pros and cons of a smaller/new school with the added benefit of being located in a vacation destination. Most aspects of the school are frankly average for a smaller university: academics, student life, party scene, and professors. Unfortunately, school sports are not of particular emphasis in association with the school, clearly having one of the worst sports programs in the state. On the plus side, besides overcrowding which is definitely an issue, the student housing at FGCU is second to none; private bedrooms for each student regardless of the floor plan!

ai_summary: Florida Institute of Technology (FIT) offers a strong academic environment , particularly for STEM majors, with a focus on small class sizes that allow for personalized interaction with professors. Many students appreciate the university's supportive community , which includes a range of clubs, organizations, and events designed to foster engagement among peers. While the campus is well-equipped and close to the beach, some students find the surrounding area lacks entertainment options. Academics can be challenging, with varying experiences regarding the effectiveness of professors, but the availability of research opportunities is a notable advantage. Many students express concerns about tuition costs and the need for more scholarship options, indicating a desire for more financial support. Overall, students value the unique opportunities for hands-on learning and professional experiences, especially given FIT's proximity to major aerospace employers.

reviews.text:
My overall experience at Florida Institute of Technology has been both challenging and rewarding. As an Aviation Management with Flight student, I’ve had the opportunity to combine academic learning with hands-on flight training, which makes the experience more engaging and practical. The coursework can be demanding, especially when balancing classes with flight schedules, but it has helped me develop better time management and discipline.
One of the things I appreciate most about FIT is the aviation environment. Being surrounded by other students who are also working toward their pilot ratings creates a strong sense of community and motivation. The access to flight resources, aircraft, and instructors has also been a major benefit in helping me progress toward my goals.
Overall, my experience at Florida Tech has been positive because it is helping me build both the knowledge and real-world skills I need for my future in aviation.
FIT has been a good experience in terms of aviation opportunities to learn. It comes with many challenges in terms of flight/Sim availability.

This school is an absolute waste of your time, money, and hope.
Most professors are disengaged with a large portion of their class. Tutors at the student success and support center are hit-or-miss, but most undergraduate technical courses are so general, broad and surface level that an AI could graduate with a 4.0.
All academic quad (main freshman dorm) buildings have black mold issues. Wi-Fi is known to be unreliable, IT is attempting to phase out large portions of their workforce for AI. Student shop is barely usable unless you're a senior. The water supply in the city was chlorinated and undrinkable near the start of the year. This school may have held some credibility at some point, but it has since vanished as the school's actual engineering has fizzled out for the most part. Dining and security are good, but are limited by the area of the school.
Take this school off of your list, their scummy financial aid department and administration should never see a cent from you.

Most general class profs do not care about their classes. Admin would rather hide problems under the rug than charge someone with a crime. Food is awful, housing keeps getting more expensive, and the admin bloat is only growing. Also they don’t have enough housing.
I would like to see more diversity on campus. Its recently gotten hard for me to manage being the one of few black people among my peers
I am very pleased with being a student at Florida Tech, my freshman year. It is going fantastic. I like the campus the teachers are great. My peers are friendly. And the cafeteria food is nice.
I appreciate the specialized majors and no need to pursure an associates degree for a bachelors. The professors are hit or miss and it is very expensive.

(MBA Online)
My experience at Florida Tech was extremely disappointing. From the start, there was no clear guidance, constant IT issues, and conflicting information between departments. I didn’t even have access to my student email for a long time, which made it nearly impossible to stay on top of things.
My assigned advisor never introduced herself, never checked in, and never provided any help with registration or degree planning. I had to contact Student Support just to find out who my advisor even was — and only after I reached out myself did she finally send me my degree plan. Until then, I had absolutely no idea what classes I was supposed to take.
Every department I interacted with dropped the ball in one way or another. Communication is poor, processes are confusing, and there’s zero proactive support for students. Compared to other universities I’ve attended, this was by far the most disorganized and disappointing experience.
Expelled in senior year due to their discrimination against the mentally ill. Do not waste your money on this tourist trap.

I love Florida Tech for all it offers, with its highlights being strong connections with top companies such as Nasa and Boeing. My least favorite part, though, is how conservative the student body is, oftentimes showing hatred towards minorities. The staff, however, are the complete opposite, and prioritize all student success whether that be honing in on academics or mental health.

AVOID the flight program at all costs. Management has favorites and will sideline students they dont like causing them to fall behind. Maintenance issues and delays leave many lessons incomplete because you cannot fly. The weather is also very difficult on the flight program. Campus has good professors who are nice and knowledgeable. Facilities on campus could be improved. They are quite dated.

Half of the professors don’t teach or are extremely unprofessional toward students but the rest of the professors are amazing. Housing is horrible. Maintenance never comes out, homeless people break into the buildings, mold and pests everywhere, expensive for what you get. Campus food frequently gives people food poisoning but sometimes it ends up being really good. I like the surrounding area. It can be a bit dangerous and has a lot of gang activity but as long as you go out in groups it’s fine. Technology on this campus is great and is updated frequently. Clubs and extracurriculars are numerous. Research is hard to get into but if you are top of your class and ambitious it is possible. It is hard to get a good GPA but definitely possible

I'm loving the college life at FIT. The classes are great and Professors keep classes engaging. Outside of class it can get a little boring maybe there could be more activities for filling spare time.

Florida Institute of Technology is a great school. The campus is very pretty although the weather can often get very humid. The party scene isn't the best so if you are not planning on going to parties everyday it is perfect. The multiple clubs and organizations gives everyone a chance to take part in an activity that they like or that they want to try out. There's no problem trying a new activity for the first time as people are very welcoming and will always do their best to include you in the club or the organization. On the topic of housing, I haven't seen every housing block and I have heard a lot of people complaining but honestly my experience on that point is great. I share my room with my roommate and the room is VERY big for a college dorm (I'm talking something like 35m^2) and I only share my bathroom with my roommate. The people from the building are very welcoming and we often watch films together in the common area of our building where we can find a TV on one floor.
It's honestly such a fun place to be and there are so many international students/people of different cultures! Most of the professors here are awesome too. :)

ai_summary: Student reviews of Florida International University (FIU) highlight a vibrant and diverse campus that offers a variety of opportunities for academic and personal growth. Academically, many students praise the supportive professors and a wide range of programs, particularly in engineering and business. However, some express a desire for more engaging classes and better professor availability. Campus life is described as active, with numerous clubs, events, and resources that foster community, although many students note that the school's commuter culture can limit social activities. Though the facilities are generally well-maintained and adequate, there are concerns about parking and administrative processes that can be cumbersome. International students feel the financial burden of tuition and limited scholarship options, suggesting improvements in this area are needed. Overall, students appreciate the friendly atmosphere and the supportive environment, indicating that FIU can provide a rewarding college experience despite some areas for enhancement.

reviews.text: Florida International University is a quality school, although it does have a long way to go in order to match other state schools. It needs a stronger incentive for student participation (like variety in clubs and on-campus activities) as well as a smoother process in interacting with FIU staff (both professors and support staff alike). For example, contacting support staff like advisors, financial aid/OneStop staff, and enrollment assistance is a lenghty, sometimes fruitless process. Before, I have spent over an hour on the phone to contact staff regarding an important registration error, which still wasn’t resolved by the end of the call.
I loved FIU and would want to go there again but want the experience of leaving for college so I want to go to USF to take some courses for a Masters program.
the ambiance was quite nice and the professors were educated but sometimes disrespectful. I enjoyed my overall experience there

Everything is great.
I chose to apply for FIU because it has one of if not the best computer science programs in the state of Florida. I also have spoken to multiple people who have gone to FIU and have all stated that FIU is an incredible school with great courses, this is especially apparent with its computer science courses as FIU ranks in the top 50 schools worldwide in computer science. I have taken multiple classes during high school that teaches Computer Science subjects and most of the teachers that teach those subjects have recommended FIU for its courses.

Overall, my FIU experience has varied depending the semester itself. Last semester, I was on campus on Thursdays which allowed me to see the Farmers Market stands and walk by them, not only that I was on campus more often which allowed me to see the overall life of it. This semester, I am not there as often to see the events but I have gotten a change to experience online classes and see how it can vary with professors.

My experience at Florida International University has helped me grow both academically and personally. As a biology major on the pre-dental track, I’ve had access to strong science courses that challenge me while preparing me for my future career. The professors are knowledgeable and there are many opportunities to get involved, whether through research, organizations, or campus events.

it s good school but horrible housing crises is going on right now. they are forcing upper classes to get an apartment next fall which is over 1000 a month. i just wish i can earn scholarships to help

My overall experience at Florida International University has been positive. I really like the community on and off campus, people are friendly, welcoming, and treat you with respect. The residence halls are clean, and the RA's makes an effort to organize activities and events that help students stay connected. The food is generally good and there are many dining options around campus, but the dining hall meals can become repetitive. If you’re trying to maintain a healthy diet, the options are somewhat limited and you often have to work with what’s available.

I loved my time at FIU, it has easy access, amazing professors and facilitated my learning experience. They were extremely helpful and resourceful whenever I needed assistance and the professors truly made the experience unique for me.

FIU is a really great school. I met some of the best people there and I loved learning at this campus. It is such a gorgeous campus and the academics were better than I expected. I enjoyed all the labs and lectures and the professors really made sure to take the time to explain complex topics so the students could understand. They were also all very approachable and welcomed any questions.

It’s great everyone is very welcoming and you can make a lot of friends for just walking around the more populated areas at the campus. A lot of the times the professors become friends too by sharing hilarious stories and their way of teaching making you want to learn more than what you expected. At first it may seem stressful to fit in but everyone has their niches and their will always be a spot left to fill!

My overall experience with Florida International University has been great. I am a first generation college student and I have to work full-time to support myself. With their FIU online program I am able to pursue my Bachelors degree in Supply Chain Management.
Great college for a balance of everything. Classes are easy and professors are nice, campus is pretty, housing is perfect.

My overall experience at Florida International University has been positive. FIU offers a diverse and welcoming campus environment, with students from many different backgrounds, which makes it easy to feel included. The professors I’ve had were knowledgeable and supportive, and there are plenty of academic resources available, such as tutoring, advising, and career services. I also liked the variety of student organizations and events, which made it easier to get involved and meet people.
One thing I would like to see improved is class availability and scheduling, especially for required courses, as some classes fill up quickly. Parking and transportation could also be better, particularly during peak hours. Overall, FIU provides a solid academic experience with strong opportunities for personal and professional growth, but a few logistical improvements would make it even better.

Overall, my experience at Florida International University has been transformative and positive. The past couple of years has been rewarding in such a good way. I appreciate the diversity of the student body because it created such an inclusive opportunity for me. Within classes, there were so many different perspectives and cultures that helped me gain a new understanding on life as a whole. Professors were knowledgeable, engaging, and understanding. Every single instructor I had motivated and guided me in various ways. I also liked the wide range of resources available. The university offers resources like tutoring, libraries, and opportunities for involvement on campus. Lastly, the biggest help thus far has been the fairs and meetings held regarding graduate schools. While attending these events I have learned a lot about how I want to continue my education and who I am as a person. As a whole, FIU has provided me with strong academic and personal growth opportunities.

ai_summary: Florida State University (FSU) offers a vibrant mix of a beautiful campus , diverse student body, and robust academic programs, with many students enjoying the lively social scene and numerous extracurricular opportunities. Many reviews highlight the supportive atmosphere fostered by dedicated professors who genuinely care about student success, providing valuable mentorship and resources. However, some concerns were raised about campus safety , particularly related to Greek life and the surrounding city, as well as challenges with housing availability and parking. The distinct dual-campus arrangement allows students to experience different environments, contributing to FSU's unique appeal . Overall, students feel a strong sense of school spirit and community, making their time at FSU enjoyable and memorable.

reviews.text: Florida State University has by far the prettiest college campus I've ever been on. Almost every professor I've had has been amazing, and the ones that aren't amazing are just average. The food options on campus are really solid. Finally, Tallahassee has an amazing party scene that has something for everyone.
Very kind professors, all willing to work with their students and help them with their studies. It is a good school.

Attending Florida State University was nothing short of an amazing experience. The people, the professors, the surrounding environment all contributed to my success as a student. Completing my undergraduate degree here was the best decision I have ever made and if I could go back in time I would do it again and again. I would recommend FSU to any student that is looking for a big school, diverse population, and top level academics.

I feel really safe walking around FSU's beautiful campus, there's so much to see and an activity for anybody to participate in. The academics are great and I've had no issues with any professors, and living in Tallahassee is a great experience for any young adult. There's a wonderful party/clubbing scene, more of an introvert scene, and the housing, location, and food all around is just as great as the school
good i havent been but i know i wouldlike to step foot on campus, Fsu is a good college to gwt into .
Attending FSU, there has always been something to do and anywhere for you to fit in. There isn’t anywhere else I would have rather attended!
I took a tour at FSU in the summer. The campus in beautiful, the tour guides were very nice and friendly. I loved the water Fontaine and the information behind it.
FSU was the perfect choice for me. I was extremely shy in high school and got a completely different experience in college. Between the amazing professors and student life, I really came into my own
I have had a great time while at Florida State University. The opportunities provided and campus life are top tier, and I couldn't ask for anything better.
The campus and student life is amazing with so much to do, Tallahassee overall is a sweet place to live, but FSU makes it all so much more fun.

Really enjoyed my three years going straight through and graduating with minimal debt. Still stay in contact with 4 of my class mates that have become life long friends. Always there was something to do socially but didn’t feel obligated to participate all the time. Beautiful campus plus attractive people make for an enjoyable time. Great memories.

Florida State University is a beautiful campus that is very walkable and not confusing. Professors are generally caring and want you to succeed. One thing I'd like to see change is the Academic Advising Office, which is very unhelpful in giving accurate advising information.

I absolutely loved it, I got an amazing education and made life-long friends! The classes were extremely engaging and informative and the professors were very easy to work with. There were many extracurricular opportunities and had a club for everyone. The campus environment felt very welcoming and inclusive. There were also so many food options on campus, each being better than the last. I loved how walkable the campus was and how gorgeous the weather and environment is in Tallahassee. The only negatives were Tallahassee itself. While I loved attending Florida State University for all four years, by the end of it I was absolutely ready to leave Tallahassee. Despite being the capital of Florida it didn't feel like there was much there other than the University. It felt like a pretty small town which had its ups and its downs. The positives being that it was common to run into a friend wherever you went and everything was pretty close by eachother.

The professors are very knowledgeable and the adjuncts are helpful. I am currently taking a heavy workload, but there are plenty of opportunities for tutoring groups and my sisters at my sorority have been a blessing - they provided me with their pearls of wisdom based on their experience in and out of the classroom setting. FSU was not my first choice, but now I am so happy I am here. The education and campus life makes me feel right at home.
It is an eye opening experience, within my faith and persona/academic development, and an incredible chance to begin my collegiate career.

ai_summary: Fordham University offers a vibrant college experience , particularly appealing to students who appreciate a combination of rigorous academics and a strong social life . The university's location in New York City enhances opportunities for networking and internships, which many students value highly. While academics generally receive positive feedback, particularly in liberal arts and business programs, some students express concerns about the resources available for certain majors, especially STEM fields. Campus life features a variety of clubs and organizations, but there seems to be a desire for more campus events and improved student engagement. Dining options receive mixed reviews, with many students calling for better quality and variety, especially given the high meal plan costs . Overall, students enjoy the supportive community of Fordham, the beautiful campuses , and the unique advantage of living in such an iconic city, but they also see areas needing improvement in food services and campus spirit.

reviews.text:
My experience at Fordham University has been both rewarding and formative. I have especially appreciated the strong sense of community and the supportive environment created by many dedicated professors. The university’s emphasis on personal growth alongside academic achievement has helped me develop both intellectually and personally. I have also valued the opportunities to engage in meaningful discussions and challenge myself in rigorous courses.
One area I would like to see improved is the math department, particularly in advising and guidance. More proactive academic support, clearer communication about course sequencing, and stronger advisor engagement would make it easier for students to plan effectively and feel confident about their academic path. Strengthening these areas would further enhance the department and improve the overall student experience. for everyone!!!

OMG Fordham you are killing me! My brother wants to go here and even though our single mom makes $84K per year, with 3 kids remaining, there's no financial aid. Not entirely Fordham's fault, when I started 5 years ago, I received almost a full ride for my first year. They didn't renew my scholarship and I'm one year into the workforce and no job in sight, despite a 3.8 GPA. I thought maybe I'd go to medical school two years earlier but I have to much debt from Fordham. My brother was offered a $50K scholarship but that's less than half! Not worth it at all! He got more money from Cornell (not much but still). Fordham, you're going to figure out this cost thing. I know all about the current financial aid crisis but you're in the Bronx for crying out loud with entitled rich students.
Amazing, the people and athletics made the place the best it could be. Really had a good time and a really good
nice very cool super nice thanks fordham you are cool and nice also. anyways i’m eating dinner and im trying to connect to spotify thats why im doing this

Fordham has been a great experiment so far, I love the community and there are great opportunities present every day! If you are towards the top of your class and maintains a strong GPA the opportunities are amazing, from touring the NYSE to the UN, the opportunities are endless
Great school! There is definitely a place for everyone. Campus dining quality could be improved. Housing is pretty good. Academics are incredible

I love Fordham. The campus is so beautiful and I've met some of the most interesting and intelligent people here. The area outside of RH and LC are both very active areas that offer a lot to do and see, great food, and honestly a good social life for being a relatively "small" school. Of course, if you want to party, New York City will offer you whatever you need. But even in The Bronx, and on campus, there are plenty of students down to have fun. The food options are honestly not that bad, and I eat on campus almost every single day unless me and my friends get dinner in the city. I've had some of the funniest, most compassionate, and most qualified professors as well, who make class bearable and entertaining. Overall, I think Fordham is a wonderful choice for someone who is independent enough to take on New York, but also wanting a quiet space to retreat to with a smaller student body compared to other schools in New York.

Before transferring to Fordham University, I found it very difficult to obtain assistance with my application. It took a long time, along with a lot of effort and perseverance, to get the help I needed regarding my questions about tuition and my personal needs. However, once everything was finally settled, I felt like I had found my place. I felt that the professors genuinely cared about me, and I became excited about school events, school spirit, and the many opportunities the university provides. There is always something to do here, and I truly appreciate that aspect of the school, as it gives students an escape from the stress of our everyday academic schedules.
I have enjoyed the last to years there. I have made a lot of friends and learned a lot in my buddies to complete my degree.

I like being a part of the Gabelli School of Business. They provide us with so many helpful resources and the ability to make so many good connections. Everyone works very hard and it is a very driven environment.

I am currently a student worker and have been able to find a balance between working and school. There are many events for commuters and many clubs that anyone can join. Everyone is super nice, and the professors are also very understanding. They are strict with exams, but there is free tutoring for student,s which really helps!

Fordham is a great school that is well known in the NYC area. My classes for my major (film and visual arts) were great, but the classes for the core requirements (which were a lot) were very mixed. The housing (Lincoln Center) was okay, but had its own problems.
I really enjoy the liberal arts curriculum offered at Fordham, and the professors are very helpful. I also appreciate the sense of community among the students at Fordham.
It’s really what you make of it, freshman year you get to decide who you wanna hangout with, and please make sure you pick people that are motivated to do well
My experience has been fun! I’m graduating soon and so far I’ve liked all the things I’ve experienced at both campuses. Each one is different but I’m glad to be a part of the community

ai_summary: Gallaudet University offers a unique and immersive experience for Deaf and hard-of-hearing students, emphasizing a strong sense of community and access to American Sign Language. Many reviews highlight the supportive and inclusive atmosphere on campus, where students feel understood and connected to their Deaf culture. The social life is vibrant, with opportunities for involvement in sports and activities, but there are also concerns regarding academic rigor, campus safety, and the quality of dorms and food. Some students expressed dissatisfaction with the academic expectations, feeling they are not challenged enough compared to peers at other universities. The university's commitment to accessibility and resources tailored for the Deaf community is a standout feature, although experiences with student services and dining options could use improvement. Overall, Gallaudet is seen as a place where students can thrive socially and culturally, while also encountering some challenges typical of a small university setting .

reviews.text: My overall experience at Gallaudet has been both challenging and rewarding. As a non-deaf student, I am fully immersed in Deaf culture, the Deaf community, and American Sign Language (ASL). Because ASL is my second language, I am learning something new every day. While this experience has been very valuable, I have also faced some challenges. Academically, I sometimes feel that the coursework is not as challenging as I would like. Socially, because ASL is my second language, I occasionally struggle with receiving and understanding the language in conversations.

Since i haven't actually attended Gallaudet yet, let's focus on what i can expect! Gallaudet is renowned for its Deaf culture and ASL-centric education. If I am planning to join, i will likely love the community, academic support, and unique cultural experiences.

My experience at Gallaudet University has been meaningful and eye-opening. I appreciated being in an environment where Deaf culture and ASL are centered, and where communication felt natural and accessible. Being surrounded by other Deaf and hard-of-hearing students helped me grow both academically and personally, and it gave me a strong sense of community and belonging.
I especially liked the opportunities to connect with peers, participate in campus activities, and learn from professors who understand Deaf experiences. Gallaudet helped me become more confident and independent.
That said, there are areas that could improve. I would like to see more consistency in academic support and better communication from administration. Improvements in resources, advising, and campus services would make the student experience even stronger.
Overall, Gallaudet University played an important role in my growth, and I’m grateful for the experiences and connections I gained there.
It is very elitist and it is hard to make friends unless you are a generational Deaf student and have been signing for years

Overall, my time at Gallaudet University has been both interesting and gratifying. I liked how welcoming the school was for deaf and hard-of-hearing kids and how I could grow as a person and in school. The teachers are helpful, and the programs push me to think critically while getting me ready for my future job. I really value the chance to meet with other people who have been through similar things and understand Deaf culture.

I attempted to take an online ASL course. The online interface is terrible, and often doesn't make sense. In addition, there were several years lapsed between when I signed up (during COVID) and when I was finally able to have bandwidth. They were very rigid in their enforcement of "policy" that they would not entertain any possibility of re-instatement, even with a penalty. Their claim that "too much time" has gone by is of course arbitrary. I will gladly spend my $1000 elsewhere, as this kind of rigidity and awkward non-sensical interface does not bode well for the quality of instruction.

This will be my first year into Gallaudet, but I have touch Gallaudet few time but everything was wonderful. Why I want to go Gallaudet because My future plans and ambitions start with attending Gallaudet University. My goal at Gallaudet University is to pursue a business degree. I have a strong interest future and my dream is in entrepreneurship and management, and I believe that a degree from Gallaudet would provide me with the knowledge and skills to make a meaningful impact in the business world

Gallaudet University is such a rich experience, being the only Deaf university in the world. It offers plenty of opportunities for immersion in the Deaf community and culture and encourages development in ASL fluency. Its approach to a bilingual environment is beneficial for anyone who attends this university.

I haven’t yet enrolled at Gallaudet University for the Deaf, but what I’ve understood so far has been impressive: respectful and timely email responses, friendly communication, great chat conversations, and even video calls using American Sign Language.
They also invite me to university seminars, and I’m truly grateful to all the kind and respectful staff at Gallaudet University.
From what I’ve heard, they offer great support for international students, such as scholarships, housing, meals, and more.
I look forward to meeting every single deaf student and staff member, and I hope to soon start my master’s degree classes there.
With respect,
Masoud Ghorbanii
Overall I love the Deaf community and I am so happy to be here. This school makes me feel so happy and I am glad I get to be here. Food is decent and so is housing, but the community is amazing.

Gallaudet University is a community where Deaf and hard of hearing students can be together. I loved that I was able to find my deaf community/ culture here. Growing up in a mainstream school, I always lacked a deaf community- coming to Gallaudet helped fulfill my deaf identity. I would like to see Gallaudet have more of a higher education and challenges.

i'll summarize: cheap education with lousy pay for the staff, horrible food, expensive metro/travel fees, if you're not from a generational Deaf family - you're instantly judged. Professors don't get paid enough to care if a student is using ChatGPT/AI to complete assignments. Freshman have a hard time adjusting on campus and finding jobs to pay off their account balance. I knew I wanted to transfer before fall finals. This University also provides very little support for first generation students. Anytime i expressed frustration to Professors/TA's I was instantly referred to the Mental Health clinic. Dorm life is either full of parties or fire alarms in the middle of the night (occasionally there was a legit fire). I tried applying for Disability services three times. rejected every time. This set me up for failure and I failed the same class twice. I begged, pleaded and cried for help. Once again reffered to the mental health clinic for counseling. Please don't go here

gallaudet university is a party college which is not very welcoming for some students. very elitism involved. i struggle with their food because it is very limited and same food everyday when i have allergies its not easy. but the only nice thing about this college, how deaf accessible it is

Gallaudet University faces several significant challenges that impact student experiences. These issues include low academic standards, substandard living conditions, and safety concerns.
Concerns about academic standards at Gallaudet have been raised, particularly regarding the Satisfactory Academic Progress (SAP) that students must maintain to be eligible for financial aid. This suggests that there may be underlying issues with academic rigor that could affect students' educational outcomes.
Living conditions on campus is substandard. Mold is a serious problem in the dorms. Cafeteria food rated 4 out of 10. Student Affairs isn't responsive to the complaints.
Safety is a critical issue at Gallaudet, as the university is located in a large urban area. The Department of Public Safety emphasizes the importance of being aware of one's surroundings and acknowledges that the campus is not isolated from crime 1.

When I arrived at Gallaudet, I didn't know any sign language. For that, I thanked Gallaudet for the opportunity to learn sign language and enjoy full accessibility on campus. I had a rich experience in sports and internships. Some deaf students were hostile to mainstreamed kids and hearing students. I ignored them, socializing with others who accepted me for whom I was. We partied a lot. Education wasn't challenging. Later I got into Honors program which helped some. Gallaudet was my second home. But I don't recommend it as deaf students have better options. To this day, safety is a serious problem. Rape is epidemic. GU either dismisses the victims or blames them. One of the top 8 universities with highest sexual assaults in the country. Campus security is another problem. They can be rough with students. The atmosphere can be oppressive to some students. At Gallaudet, accountability is lacking.

ai_summary: Gannon University stands out for its strong sense of community , with many students describing it as a welcoming family where professors genuinely care about their success. The academics are frequently praised, particularly in health sciences, where innovative facilities like a Patient Simulation Center enhance the learning experience. While the campus is appreciated for its smaller size and personal interactions between students and faculty, some students express concerns about the urban environment, particularly regarding safety and the need for campus improvement. Student life features a variety of activities and clubs, with many students enjoying Greek life and athletics, although some feel the weekend social scene could be improved. Additionally, while many find the tuition manageable due to good scholarship options, comments about the food quality and administration efficiency suggest there are areas in need of improvement .

reviews.text: My experience at Gannon has been both challenging and rewarding. I have been pushed to grow intellectually while also developing real-world skills through community opportunities. The small class sizes make student-professor connections possible and encourage more involvement and discussion. I would like to see more investment in campus facilities to help support modern learning environments for everyone. I would also love to see Gannon bring back the small cafeterias that were around the academic buildings that would benefit faculty and staff.
Gannon University campus and admission process is excellent. The admissions counsellor has been resourceful all through the admission process.

My overall experience with Gannon University has honestly been amazing. I'd say that this really is just a great college option if you are truly looking for the college full experience, from academics, to activities, to friends, to parties, all depending on who you are you will find your place. Everybody here fits in, everyone here truly is nice. It's like living in a whole other world when at Gannon but it's so welcoming. I will say I wasn't always sure about college because of social anxiety, and other factors, but Gannon University, the community make it so much easier to enjoy, feel welcomed, and do your best here. I say if you have the chance or are considering Gannon University go for it, you won't regret it! Life is all about what you make it!
I lobe taking classes at Gannon, the school it self is very nice. I would love to recommend the school to other student who would like to study here at Gannon.

Gannon University is an okay school. I would not recommend attending Gannon if you are looking for a typical college experience. There is barely a campus. You are located in downtown Erie, which means you are always surrounded by homeless people. It's dangerous to walk around at night. I would recommend Gannon if you are going for the sole purpose of academics.

Gannon University is a great school if you like to know everyone on campus and feel a sense of community. I have found that there are many ways to get involved on campus due to clubs and fraternity/sorority life. I feel like they could be kinder to their teachers, as the students can tell from their teachers' attitudes, as well as campus gossip, that the University has not given the teachers their yearly raises despite the President making a very, very high salary. This being said, the University does have amazing teachers who genuinely care about their students, as well as many resources for students to get academic help.

I would just use it as a way to get an education, that is it. The education is pretty good,, and I have good professors. I would not recommend athletics here and stay away from the food in the dining hall if you can.
Going to Gannon has been an amazing decision. Gannon feels like home to me; everyone is so friendly and welcoming—there's always so much to do around campus to get more involved with your classmates.
I will always remember everyone. When I first got there from being recruited, I decided in my mind that I was already going regardless of my other visits.

I am currently transferring to Gannon this fall semester from the Erie County Community College. I start going to Gannon next week, but so far they have been super helpful. They have made the transition feel less intimidating , and I am excited to start my education journey at this university. It is in the city, which makes parking and finding classes a bit stressful.

My experience so far at gannon was great. I am from The Bahamas and I have had my up and down with the snow but I do love it more than the heat. I like gannon because it gives the calm small town vibe that keeps me in my books and focused on my studies and sometime i get the chance to do fun things in my free time. The professors are very helpful and the athletes and other departments make the campus fun.

My experience at Gannon University was really good overall. I liked that the campus was small and easy to get around, and the people were friendly. The professors were helpful and took time to work with students if we had questions or needed extra help. The small class sizes made it easier to learn and feel comfortable speaking up. Gannon also has a great location in downtown Erie, which gives me chances to volunteer, do internships, and get involved. I liked how the school focused on helping others and encouraged students to join service projects. The programs for health, business, and engineering were strong, and the school cared about making students feel welcome. However, I think Gannon could improve by offering more class choices, especially for electives, and by improving the food and housing. I also wish there were more fun things to do on weekends and more support for students dealing with stress. Even then, I had a great time and would recommend Gannon to others.

From my past 2 years at Gannon University it's been great for the most part, there are tutor available and professors have flexible time. I really love that Gannon University also has their own safety boxes around the school for students in need of immediate help. The location of the college I'm not really a big fan of, it is located in downtown Erie and it feels dangerous being in that area it is not the safest place in erie but thankfully police are right next to the college which does make me feel a lot better. Gannon also I feel like food is lacking even the chick fil a located inside of it is also bad quality food, I always end up going some other place to get my food. I like the coffee they sell in the library also library is amazing due to there being a loud floor and a quiet floor, all places having accessible to books for any class there is also computer access but most bring their laptop because of security reasons.
Located within the city of Erie. Safety is a bit of a concern. Campus facilities are nice. Overall experience is good.

The professors and faculty are great and passionate, but the city lacks a college-town vibe. The campus is close to streets that are relatively dangerous and there is a major lack of lighting across campus.

ai_summary: Gardner-Webb University offers a close-knit, supportive community that fosters connections among students and professors. Many reviews highlight the dedicated faculty who take time to know their students personally, offering help both academically and personally. The small class sizes are appreciated, as they allow for meaningful interactions and networking opportunities. Student life is characterized by a Christian atmosphere and various extracurricular activities, though some students express a desire for more weekend activities and dining options. The campus is noted for its beauty and calm environment, but some reviews mention limited local amenities and housing quality. Overall, Gardner-Webb prides itself on its caring community, which many students feel makes it a unique and welcoming place to pursue their education.

reviews.text: I really like how the school is how it’s small classroom teach only few kids and how I can focus more on learning

Gardner Webb is a very Christian university that takes pride in knowing their students. They take the time to get to know you on a personal level and can even greet your family by name when they see them out in public. I have overall felt very safe and welcomed at Gardner Webb. They had made me feel like it is my home away from home.

I genuinely love Gardner–Webb University because it feels like a place where I truly belong and can grow both academically and personally. The close-knit campus community creates a welcoming environment where professors care about students’ success and take the time to build meaningful connections. Gardner–Webb offers strong academic opportunities while also encouraging involvement, leadership, and service, which has helped me develop confidence and a clear sense of direction for my future. The supportive atmosphere, beautiful campus, and emphasis on faith, learning, and purpose make Gardner–Webb more than just a university to me—it feels like home and a place where I am inspired to become the best version of myself.

I came into Gardner-Webb as a freshman from a neighboring state and figured out pretty soon that it wasn't for me but you may like it better than I did. I will say for one that the community here is something that I don't think you'd get from a lot of other schools. You see the same people every day during meals, in the gym, and in several of your classes even. It's a strong community, and not to say it's toxic or intentionally exclusionary, but if you don't fit the bill you'll have a hard time fitting in, regardless of how welcoming the community is. The surrounding area is pretty barren and there's not much to do, not much in the way of interesting events. Food is limited but the Caf rarely disappoints. Housing is run down and outdated. I loved most of my professors but the academics left some things to be desired. Certain important topics weren't covered in intro classes like they are in other schools. Not worth it unless you have a hefty scholarship and are in-state.

I love Gardner-Webb. I live about 3 hours away but they have made me feel at home. I relaly apprciate the Christian life at Gardner-Webb. So many student walk out there faith here and it is mind blowing.

Gardner-Webb University offers a close-knit, faith-based educational environment with small class sizes and supportive faculty. Located in a quiet, rural area of North Carolina, it emphasizes Christian values, academic excellence, and personal growth. Students benefit from a strong sense of community, accessible professors, and active campus ministries. While the setting is peaceful, it may feel somewhat limited in terms of entertainment or diversity for some. Overall, GWU is ideal for students seeking a values-driven, personalized college experience.

Coming in as a transfer student at Gardner-Webb, I wasn’t really sure what to expect. However, the campus is more than beautiful and the people there are like family. Being that this is more of a community than a college it allows you to create genuine bonds with the people who are there. The professors care about the students and want them to actually learn the material. The campus life can be really good some weeks being that the school constantly holds events that students can attend. However, sometimes it can be really dry on campus being that a lot of people go back home on the weekends. I think that realizing that everyone had experienced the first time being away from home and having to make new friends really helps a lot, because it allows you to step out your comfort zone and put yourself in the situation to do so. I believe that this definitely works at Gardner- Webb because the community that they build there is very strong and caring. They are always looking to help others.

I really haven’t started yet, but i’ve been in multiple activities with them and they are awesome. And extremely helpful, I’m an international student and they have helped me in ways I’ve never imagined.

I am an incoming freshman I would be playing division 1 soccer for the school and I am looking for as much financial help possible . I am a first ever student to go to college in my family so our budget isn’t big .
i like how close knit of a community gardner webb is. with it being a smaller school everyone knows everyone and it can create a very intimate experience because of this.
I absolutely love my school and am so blessed to attend there. The community is amazing as is the student life. It is definitely not a party campus although there are still parties that happen.

Gardner-Webb university is a great small university for students who aren’t interested in big time schools or higher tuition.
