My only complaint would be that parking was very hard to find at the beginning of the school year, but the school fixed that. I have had no negative experiences with the professors and the website we use to do our work, which works how it should majority of the time.

I loved Kennesaw State University. There were more than many attributes as to why this school is amazing. The campus is super walkable and easy to navigate. One can get anywhere on campus within a maximum of 10-15 minute walk. The food in the cafeteria is also really good and highly praised. Not to mention the most important part being the education. The education system here is amazing and we use a platform called D2L for all of our assignments and sometimes quizzes and tests. This keeps the students organized and able to easily maneuver through their classes and assignments; plus it’s convenient and quite easy to use. The school’s football team also just won their conference recently, so there’s all the reason to be excited. Speaking of the football team, there is many fun things to do within sports, or clubs, or Greek life, or anything of that nature, and one can always see this kind of stuff all around campus. It encourages more people to go out and have some fun.

KSU have been a amazing university to start a college career. There are so many activities that I can do like joining clubs or going to fun little. Not only, that KSU also have my major which is mechanical engineering and there are so many class for my major to be prepare for. The students are the school is really friendly and nice and campus is huge and beautiful to live on.
I like Kennesaw State University as it brings a peaceful environment that helps me feel as I can be a productive student.

Overall I think Kennesaw State University is a very good school both academically and socially. Although they have their mishaps here and there, they allow you to get the proper education needed for you to work in the workforce as well as tools to help you pursue your studies and whatever field you're trying to work and have education in.

Overall I like Kennesaw State University its a great college, I'm mainly at the Marietta Campus which is a more stem based area, so its extremely clean and well maintained every time I walk to class. I appreicate how the buildings are and the scenary. The meal prices are quite expensive so I do not eat on campus, but I dislike how long the ChickFilA lines are so they should build more resturant choices on campus.

The program itself and the curriculum for my masters was great. I learned a lot and each course’s material was digested appropriately thanks to the professors’ teaching style and lecture videos. They strongly encourage collaboration with other students which was very helpful.

ai_summary: Kent State University has a vibrant community that many students find welcoming and inclusive . The campus is noted for its beauty and offers a variety of academic programs which students generally find engaging, particularly in fields like fashion and nursing. While professors are often described as helpful and supportive , the quality varies, with some students expressing frustration about inconsistent advising and teaching standards. Student life includes a range of clubs and events; however, some feel there are limited opportunities for socializing outside party scenes, especially during winter months. Dining options receive mixed reviews, with a common desire for better food variety , and concerns about the cost of tuition are frequently mentioned. Overall, Kent State seems to offer a solid educational experience with opportunities for involvement, but its social atmosphere and dining options could benefit from improvement.

reviews.text: Overall, my experience at Kent State University was pretty good. I had a lot of great teachers and met some incredible people. The atmosphere, students, activites and clubs are varied and full of fun. No matter where you come from, there is always something that you will never regret getting involved with. My biggest issue lay with those higher up the ladder who did not put students as the highest priority. Construction inconveniences, lack of any assistance with internships, and an ongoing parking crisis on the campus made things difficult on the day to day.
I love it there, I think everyone should go to Kent State University. Everyone there is so nice and helpful and kind.

Kent State is a wonderful institution. I loved my entire experience there. My professors were experts in their fields. The course work prepared me for a life outside the classroom. The assigned group projects were meant to stretch the practical knowledge and bring the concepts to life. I would highly recommend Kent State University. As a graduate of the Fashion School, you cannot go to a better institution with an outstanding reputation in the field.

It has been good so far nice teachers and nice activities to do. The math department is something I would not recommend however. Most of the teachers do not have English as a first language so trying to learn in a very demanding class like that can be very difficult. There are plenty of things to do around Kent so as long as youre creative you won't be bored which is really nice.

I like it. It's a little hard to make friends, but good student help and programs. They make it easy to find what classes you need for your major. The student life is very lively with lots of events and cool places to go downtown. The student life also has a good party scene with both bars and frats having 18+ parties most weekends. The professors are very qualified, but the skill of each one is very baised off of the class you're taking. They do require you to take a number of Kent cores that can be challenging, depending on the one you're taking. Overall, I've had a good experience at Kent so far.
I really liked it but I would it would be better with more food options on campus, one side of campus doesnt have any many. The community is very rich though.
I enjoyed the exercise science laboratories, it was very neat to get hands on experience. I would like to see the curriculum adjusted to better fit the needs for graduate school.
The rooms are nice. The food and bathrooms could be better, but it's your typical college food and bathrooms.
My experience so far at Kent State has been great. I have had great classes with some amazing professors and no issues so far.

theres a lot of different people here. the food gave me menopause and it made me gag and throw up all over my sister's friend's bf and she thought i was hitting on him so i got my head beat into a sewage pipe. lot of cute older teachers tho. xx
Really like this school and their Fashion Design program is excellent. There are limitations with their food options but the ones they have are pretty decent.

My experience is going well as of right now.Classes are starting back up and the pressure is rising. They always have activities for students to do on campus which makes life a little easier. The security is on point we have patrol cars riding past all the time and tens members on go carts checking surrounding areas.The professors are kind but strict you can tell they are about business this semester not that the last ones weren’t but this semester is definitely gonna be up there. I’m excited to try new things and do my best. I would like a broader food selection we have the same thing frequently. Besides that Kent is a good campus there is plenty of places to go to study , chill and just have some quite time. Everyone here is either super sweet or just quiet and that’s as good as it can get truly. I would say just make the best of this experience it’s once in a lifetime and stay on your studies but make sure you still have a fun. Take advantage of the opportunities while they are here

My overall experience through my first semester at KSU was good. I maintained a good gpa while having a good social life. I met a couple of new people, and my professors are all good and explains their course very well.

I loved going to the College of Architecture and Enviormental Design to get my Bachelor of Science in Architecture. I really enjoyed the campus enviorment and the new architecture building. It was a great school to go to and make good friends and they had many different events to go to.
I'm having a positive experience at Kent State. The dorm bathrooms need more attention. The food is ok but I am tired of the same meal options. Need more vegan options

ai_summary: La Salle University is described as a small , welcoming institution with a strong sense of community where students develop close relationships with professors. Many reviews highlight the intimate classroom setting , which allows for personal attention and engagement in academic pursuits. While the academic programs are generally well-regarded, particularly in fields like nursing, some reviews mention variations in teaching quality among professors. Student life appears to offer opportunities for involvement, such as clubs and Greek life, though there are comments about a lack of overall campus activities. Many students appreciate the diverse environment , but concerns are raised regarding campus safety and the quality of dorms and dining options. The affordability of tuition and available financial aid is also a common theme, indicating that while the education is seen as valuable, the costs associated with attending La Salle can be a barrier for some students. Overall, La Salle University stands out for its supportive faculty and strong community bonds, but there are areas, such as campus safety and living conditions, that need improvement.

reviews.text: La Salle University has been my best choice. As a Latina student, I've felt really welcomed into the diverse environment of La Salle. Every single person I've met there have been so nice, kind, and helpful. My overall experience has been fantastic, and I would definitely recommend La Salle as a good university.
The professors and academic is great. I wish there were more housing options and more scholarships to be able to apply for.

It’s been transformative for me. I can’t see myself being at any other college. I’m engaged in several clubs & have lots of new friends. Teachers have been excellent. Class sizes no more than 20. LaSalle has renewed energy. Their is a good vibe on campus. Lots of interest from potential incoming students. Dorms are subpar, & the food is meh.
I think it is alright as an overall experience, but I do hope I can return soon to finish my bachelor's degree.

La Salle has actually been really nice so far, and I’m glad to be here. Right now, I am struggling financially because I don’t have enough money for my next semester, but being here is definitely worth it. Everyone at this school has been so welcoming, and the professors are such a big help. What makes the experience even better is how much it feels like each, and every teacher truly cares about their students.
La Salle University is a really good college overall. It has a variety of majors and a great community.
My experience here coming from Texas to Pennsylvania has been very rewarding. My experiences here are shaping me into who I want to be.

I’ve had a great experience so far to obtain my MBA degree! I enjoyed my undergraduate years at La Salle that it only felt right to return for my MBA. I’ve met my partner here, my niece just graduated, etc. La Salle is a community and a family none the less. The professors are great and the educational structure is helpful for those with creative mindsets beyond the “classroom.”
The professors are very encouraging and want student to exceed. The campus is always quiet and safe.

La Salle is a nice school overall, it's a small campus with little to nothing to do. If you are a student of color there's not a lot of diversity on campus, also the dorms are very unclean, the water is always cold, and often times the water stops working at times.

My experience at Lasalle University ,well for starters I was very nervous. But who wouldnt be ,going from high school to college is a hugh step. Now it's time to actually get down to major studying . Theres no more extra credit or even taking a test over. I had to really think about this. First thing I said is ,I can't disappoint my mom because she is actuallly paying for my education. So life is moving fast . Its really time to grow up. I know i can do this. I am up for any challenge. I am a go getter. But wants I started going to different classes an meeting different people . I felt very comfortable. What I really like about Lasalle is that they have smaller classes, so if you are having trouble in a particular subject, your professors are willing to stay and help. Lasalle also have different organizations and resources that they are willing to help you as well. Lasalle staff is awesome they actually take the time to listen and to get to know there students experiences.
I think the academics stuff and the environment is really good but for students activities there are not many things to do.

La Salle University offers a supportive and inclusive environment for students, with a strong focus on academic excellence, personal growth, and community engagement. The campus fosters a close-knit community where students can build meaningful relationships with professors and peers. The university provides a range of programs that prepare students for both professional success and responsible citizenship. Overall, La Salle's blend of rigorous academics, dedicated faculty, and diverse student body creates a well-rounded educational experience.

As a commuter, life on campus is very welcoming and easy to navigate. There are always people out there ready to help when you need it, and there is easy access to academic help when you feel like you are struggling. There is a sense of community, and there is always something new and good to find or try out at Lasalle University.

Overall La Salle is a wonerful school that has alot to offer in terms of academics, social atmoshpere, and overal school life. It's brings it's great values along with incredible professors that make all of the environment come together.

ai_summary: Lamar University features a diverse and picturesque campus that fosters a strong sense of community among students and faculty. Many reviews highlight the supportive and approachable professors , especially in smaller class settings, which allow for more personalized interactions . Students appreciate the variety of academic resources like tutoring and writing centers, which help enhance their learning experience. While faculty support is often praised, some reviews mention inconsistencies in teaching quality across different professors. Student life on campus is described as friendly, though some find the options for activities limited. Parking and dining hours are commonly pointed out as areas needing improvement. Despite mixed feelings about the surrounding Beaumont area, the affordability of tuition and the welcoming atmosphere contribute to a positive overall experience for many students.

reviews.text: Lamar is a great school for those who are truly focused on getting a degree and not worried about getting the traditional large school college experience. The dorms are great, and the student body is also great, with a diverse group of people from all over the world. The biggest caveat to me however, is the location of the school. If you are someone who likes to explore your college town or find fun things to do around a city, then Beaumont is not for you. There is not much to do outside of the campus, and the campus is relatively small compared to other universities.

I have had an amazing experience at Lamar University it truly feels like my home away from home. I love the small-town community atmosphere; the campus isn’t too big, and everyone is very friendly and welcoming. The professors genuinely care about their students and truly want to see each and every one of us succeed and thrive. I absolutely love attending football and basketball games and supporting my school everyone always has amazing school spirit . If I could change one thing, it would be adding more parking spaces on campus. Overall, Lamar University has been a wonderful place for both my academic and personal growth.

I would like the professors to be more engaging with the students when teaching certain lessons. I see a bunch of students, yawning, not paying attention, messing around. In general, not taking it too serious because of how the professors teach.

It's a good university for engineering if you want to work in industry. The college of engineering is very involved with their students with amazing faculty and staff, but the actual university is terrible expect terrible security, no transparency with students, and constant implementation of new policies that hurt student life and inhibit student orgs.

My experience at Lamar University has been wonderful! The professors are very helpful and provide all of the resources needed to be successful in their courses. I would highly recommend Lamar University to anyone looking to further their education!

I really enjoy being at Lamar University. The campus is beautiful, the staff are always friendly and helpful, and I love the fun pop-up events that bring everyone together. I’d like to see even more student engagement opportunities and activities added in the future.

While attending Lamar, I’ve currently been experiencing the most inclusive/ welcoming school community I’ve ever attended. The food is great, professors are helpful and treat you with respect, however something I would like to see changed is the information sharing between the college and the students is lacking, leaving students scrambling even if they’ve been staying on-top of all there is to-do. Another thing is I’d like there to be more wvents geared towards commuters like myself, when driving 40 minutes to and from the campus it’s hard to participate when most events begin later in the day.

My experience at Lamar was nothing but great. Everything is in close range nothing is too far . Academics are perfect in my opinion and student involvement is a great highlight as well. I would like to see the library up and running again. In the meantime I will continue to occupy my free time in the recreation center or tending to my studies.
I was saddened that the Smoothie King is no longer in the Setzer Center. I think the dining hall could be a bit cheaper, maybe $6 instead of $10 per person.

Lamar has for the most part awesome professors, I mainly take science and nursing courses. Its a large campus so bikes or so other mode to transportation maybe needed. But overall Ive had a great experience so far.

Lamar is a great place to go to for college. There's a lot of diversity and great professors that will treat you like family. The Lamar dinning hall is amazing with tons of delicious food layed out in a buffet style. My favorite place to go to besides my dorm would have to be the Setzer Center or as everyone calls it the Set. It's were people can study or go out on the balcany to relax in rocking chairs. There is also places for organizations to gather, career fairs, a game room, and it has three different fast food restaurants to eat at, I love panda express. Everyday walking to class is peaceful and you just see people zooming by on bikes, skateboards or scooters doing their own thing. I'm really enjoying my Lamar experience so far.

I just started but I already had issues with them with housing. At Lamar, you HAVE to have been living in the area six months better you enroll to the school. I only knew they existed 6 months ago, did know much about them, applied 4 months ago and graduated high school 3 months ago and had to move to Beaumont for school. We tried getting a house ALL summer but got it last minute due to a couple racist realtors and poorly cared houses, so when I did get a home, they could not except me from housing cuz I didn’t live there 6 months ago so I’m getting billed for something I can’t afford. But that’s the only issue tbh academics is great.

Lamar University is a small school with abundant opportunities. I love the massive library, the well kept grounds, and that a pool is just outside my dorm! Transitioning to a public school after being a lifelong homeschooler was a nerve-wracking experience, and Lamar made it easier with the small class sizes, its student to faculty ratio, and the overall welcoming environment. Go Cardinals!

My experience at Lamar University has been very positive. I’ve appreciated the supportive professors, the diversity on campus, and the wide variety of academic programs available. One of the things I’ve liked most is the nurturing feedback from the professors. They are supportive, approachable, and genuinely want to see students succeed. I value the strong sense of community and the helpful resources available for students, such as tutoring centers, academic advising, and student organizations. These support systems made it easier for me to stay on track and feel more connected to campus life. Being part of the Lamar Dance Team has been a highlight of my time here. It has given me the opportunity to get involved on campus, build meaningful connections, and express myself through something I truly love. The experience has taught me discipline, teamwork, and school pride. I’m proud to be a Lamar student and grateful for all the support and opportunities the university has provided to me.

I am attending graduate school to get my masters in Special Education. I have had the best experience with my online courses. All the professors have been very organized and lessons and curriculum course work is engaging and challenging. I feel LU was the right path for me!

ai_summary: Lehigh University offers a vibrant college experience characterized by a strong sense of community and diverse opportunities for involvement, but there are notable challenges related to diversity and student life. Academically, reviews highlight Lehigh's rigorous programs , supportive professors , and the vast variety of majors, particularly strong in engineering and business. Students appreciate the networking benefits provided by a connected alumni network, which enhances career prospects after graduation. However, many reviews mention concerns about the social atmosphere, heavily influenced by Greek life and a drinking culture that may not appeal to everyone. While the campus itself is described as beautiful and engaging, some students feel the surrounding area lacks entertainment options. Issues with dining services and academic advising also appear in several comments, indicating a need for improvement in these areas. Overall, Lehigh can be a great fit for students who embrace its unique culture and academic challenges.

reviews.text: As a student athlete at Lehigh, I had a great experience attending this university. The athletics program and faculty made it feel like one big family. I am forever grateful for the relationships I made which I hope will carry into my next chapter of my life. However, since this school is smaller, I do think there can not just be one dining hall (Rathbone). I think they need to bring back another dining option that is like Rathbone for students. As an athlete, I was lucky enough to have a dining option on Goodman Campus but for other students, there should be more options.

I would say it has been a great experience. Lehigh campus is very diversed which allow people from all all walks of life to come and pursue their dream program. Professors, Staffs and careers career coaches are very helpful in helping you to navigate the life on campus and also very supportive.

I unexpectedly fell in love with Lehigh. I’m a Bethlehem local so when I dream college was in my backyard I was more than surprised. All of my professors so have been more than eager to help me succeed and the big question Seminar has unlocked new interests I didn’t know I had.

As a freshman, I think my experience thus far has been decent. The classes are definitely more challenging and an adjustment from my high school classes that I took but I think I did my best that I could while managing a new social life. However, campus food and the meal plan can use a lot of improvement.

Lehigh offers a variety of resources and opportunities that any student can take advantage of. The school is concerned with academics but the culture is more of a work-hard play-hard school. Campus life is fun but overall diversity could definitely be improved.

Lehigh is a great school! For the steep price, Lehigh offers a lot for students across all areas. Some highlights include the Center for Career and Professional Development, opportunities for out of the classroom learning, and campus life. Food has been iffy on campus lately because of some renovations, so I would like to see some improvements there. Also the bus system is pretty unreliable, especially for a campus built into a mountain and the busses are pretty much required to get up.

Lehigh is a beautiful campus with great school spirit and outstanding academics. The administrators are very visible around campus and people really go out of your way to make sure you're connected. There are tons of clubs and parties, lots of ways to get involved. Preppy and privileged.

Upon reflecting on my time at Lehigh University, I'm grateful that this is where I ended up. Specifically, the research opportunities on campus have been amazing. I've been a part of the Bioarcheology & Skeletal Biology Laboratory and Phages Laboratory during my time at Lehigh.

If you do not like partying and drinking, this school is not for you. I came into this school thinking that academics was top priority, but as soon as I started studying here, I quickly realized that most students are not academically or professionally ambitious. If you want a better network for alumni and current students, I highly recommend you go to a different school.

I really enjoy going to school at Lehigh. Although the school is academically challenging the community is one in which people collaborate and help one another. I always find myself studying in groups and working together on assignments.
I love the culture at Lehigh. It’s so nice to go to a school where people are proud to be there, driven academically, and competitive but fun. I would like to see better housing and dining options.

I have loved my 3 years at Lehigh and I cannot wait for my senior year. I loved the campus life, and how warm the community is. I would love for more bikes or easier transportation to be offered on campus to make it more accessible, but I love the beauty and liveliness of our campus.

Attending Lehigh University has been one of the most rewarding experiences of my life. As a senior in the College of Arts and Sciences, I've taken full advantage of the incredible travel and research opportunities available through grants, environmental fellowships, and the UN partnership. I'm proud to intern with the United Nations, serve as a campus tour guide, and be an active member of Epsilon Delta Pi and other student organizations. Living in Bethlehem has also been a highlight it's a welcoming town with constant events such as concerts or car shows, and I enjoy working at a local restaurant downtown. I've truly loved my time at Lehigh and the endless ways to get involved and grow. If I could change one thing, I would love to see more upperclassman course options and more large-scale school-wide events rather than mostly club-hosted ones. Overall, Lehigh has given me a community, purpose, and unforgettable experiences on top of a killer education with post grad opportunities.

I have loved getting to attend Lehigh university. I have been able to form such a strong community and the amount of campus involvement available has made it so that I have diverse activities I’m involved in. I could not imagine having as great of a college experience anywhere else than at Lehigh.

I really like Lehigh and feel extremely proud of my school. I think that Lehigh is a very academically prestigious university and even though a lot of people haven't heard about it, it's still ranked very high. I think the opportunities are one of the best accepts about this school, and I feel very confident about being able to get a good job and making a lot of money after graduation.

ai_summary: Lesley University offers a unique blend of academic programs , particularly in the arts and education, and is positioned in a vibrant location near Cambridge, which many students appreciate for its culture and accessibility. The small class sizes allow for personalized attention , and many students feel that professors genuinely care about their success and foster a supportive learning atmosphere . However, reviews indicate significant concerns about the administration, inadequate student services , and issues with housing quality and food options. While the campus community values inclusivity, there are critiques regarding the lack of diversity among the student body and faculty, as well as challenges for commuter students in feeling integrated. Overall, Lesley is seen as a good fit for those pursuing art or education fields, but students advise caution regarding its limitations and the need for improvement in various areas.

reviews.text: Great University, awesome location. Dorm situation is not ideal, but in a great part of the city! Professors and classes classes are good. Overall good experience

I enjoyed lesley as a student athlete. The sports team I was on here was like a family and all the student athletes hung out/became friends. They are very nice people to socialize with. However, the science courses are not as good as I would like. They fired many professors and are starting to remove the science program here. The classes we do have are not challenging enough and I don't like that I'm not learning enough for what my major is. I transferred here and the community college I attended prior had a larger science program and had thorough labs/course lectures than Lesley provides. They have good art, education and psychology courses for those majors but are not accomodating to science students. I can't graduate with my year because they have classes that are only provided every other semester. I have to take one to graduate next spring semester. I have to wait a whole extra year to get my degree.

Lesley is a good college for students who want to major in education and art. As a science major it is very disappointing but I stay for the awesome people I have met here and the athletics they have good dining hall food where you will get all the nutrients you need, on campus housing is well taken care of and there is lots of options for off campus housing as well. If you are ever bored going to lesley it is your own fault because there is so much to do there.

I’m a senior Elementary Education Major at Lesley University who is also apart of the women’s soccer team. The education program is great here at Lesley as there is amazing internship connections as well as small classroom sizes. The classes do a great job with preparing students for their licensure tests (mtels) as well as learning various techniques and tools to develop their own pedagogical practices. The athletics here are in a pretty competitive conference and overall feel supported in their academics.
I completed my bachelor's degree entirely online and enjoyed the experience. I had involved professors, manageable course loads, and accessible resources

Moving from Gilbert, Arizona to Cambridge, Massachusetts at 17 to attend Lesley University was one of the biggest challenges and best decisions of my life. As a student-athlete playing baseball, I’ve learned to balance academics, sports, and independence while being far from home. Leaving behind my family and everything familiar pushed me to grow, adapt, and mature quickly. Lesley has opened my eyes to new perspectives, friendships, and opportunities I never imagined back in Arizona. This journey has taught me resilience, responsibility, and confidence, skills I’ll carry beyond the field and classroom. I’m proud of how far I’ve come and excited for what’s ahead.

I like some of the people, however there are some who are entitled or just cliche. I love some of the professors but others not so much. Overall the school has a bit of a communication issue from staff to departments and even students. But it's a small school so more room for one on onee with professors and everyone kinda knows everyone.

My time at Lesley University has been amazing. From the faculty, staff, and students, Lesley creates an environment for learning and also creating relationships. I would recommend anyone to attend Lesley University.

I have been at Lesley for 4 years and only have good things to say about the college. Everything I wanted in college I was able to get with sports, academics, with Boston just being a train ride away.lesley was the perfect school for me and I couldn’t recommend the college anymore than I already do.

As a recent graduate, and current graduate student. I can honestly say my experience at Lesley University was life-changing. What stood out most to me was how supportive the community is professors don't just teach, they actually care about your growth inside and outside the classroom.
I studied psychology and business administration here while also playing four years of college baseball. Balancing athletics and academics isn't always easy, but the staff, coaches, and advisors went out of their way to make sure I had the resources and support to succeed. Even when I was sidelined by injury, I felt encouraged to stay engaged and come back stronger.
The school creates an environment where students can really find themselves. From the classroom discussions to the close knit campus, you're constantly surrounded by people who want you to grow. I also appreciated that Lesley focuses on real world experiences and practical learning you don't just memorize material, you learn how to apply it

EXCELLENT INSTITUTION! Lesley University has been a great place to pursue my academic endeavors with many opportunities to network and make connections with businesses in the greater Boston area. There is a state-of-the-art facility for all majors, caring faculty, and a good learning environment.
As an undergraduate student-athlete, I've had very positive experiences with all of the professors. Many are willing to take steps to provide extra support when necessary. The libraries on campus are newly remodeled and are great places to study or hang out.
Love my experience at Lesley. Professors are really great and want student to succeed. It’s a close-knit community and there are lots of opportunities get involved and meet people outside of class.

Despite Listing many major options, there are 2.5 fulltime equivalent mathematics and Science teachers, no English Teachers, with no plans to hire more. Unfortunately Steinmayer's 'Better Lesley' Plan has improved some aspects, but by laying off 12% of total staff, 24 fulltime teachers, we no longer have an appropriate educational base for most general subjects.

Lesley is what you make of it. some people have a great experience academically, others don't, you have to put in work to get anything out of it. Cambridge is an awesome place to go to school. There's a lot of talk lately about possible loss of accreditation, and issues with the current president. Faculty is not very happy with administration.

Do not waste your money at Lesley University. It needs to be shut down or a lot of the staff need to be fired. All of the staff at the health center need to be fired. They can't even tell the difference between a lingering cough and Pneumonia. All of my friends and I have been misdiagnosed every single time that we have been there. I was once sick for OVER A MONTH because they kept saying that it was "just a lingering cough" as I just kept getting worse and worse. They have done other things just like that to all of my friends too. Most of the teachers don't care if you are extremely sick or not. If you tell them that you can't attend class because you are extremely sick, they will drop your grade. The therapists at Lesley don't care about your mental health. I told them countless times that my mental health has been plummeting, and they do not care. One of them even talked over me every single time I saw her. She absolutely needs to be fired. There is nothing good about Lesley.

ai_summary: At Liberty University, students appreciate the strong Christian foundation that informs their education and campus life. Many reviews highlight the supportive professors and staff who are genuinely invested in student success, creating a nurturing environment that feels like a community. While the rigorous academic coursework is mentioned frequently, students also value the diverse extracurricular activities , including a wide range of athletic options and social events. The campus is noted for its ongoing improvements and beautiful setting , although the strict rules and policies, including curfews and mandatory meetings, receive mixed responses. Overall, Liberty is seen as a good fit for those seeking a faith-based academic experience with ample support for both spiritual and educational growth.

reviews.text: The Admissions process was confusing. But I love everything else about the university. But i do think that making distance learning/ online learning was a great way to help students. They have discounts for first responders, military/ military departments, and early registerion. They regularly check on you during you the process. I actually was shocked how great of a school it. My friend started attending last year and encouraged me to apply. But she wasnt the first student to attend my pastor was the first out of all of us to graduate.
I like Liberty, but I am an introvert and not a party person. They do have stricter rules (since they are a Christian university) but I have been enjoying it.
Liberty has helped me prepair for starting my college classes .i appreciatethe help because i would be lost if not

My overall experience with Liberty University has been amazing and I appreciate that they are always trying to improve student experience. They are a Christ-centered school with teachers devoted to the success of the students. So far, every professor has proven their knowledge on the course and experience in teaching. The university has accommodated the school setting to improve the learning of the students. Tutoring, writing centers, available therapists on campus and even providing healthy meals are a few ways the school shows their dedication to their mission; Training champions for Christ.

Liberty University is a good school to attend. Hard work and dedication are important to achieve your goals and dreams. The faculty and staff are very helpful. They work with you and communicate very quickly. Admissions is very easy to get into contact with and they walk you through how to get things done.

My overall experience at liberty University was great. I developed long lasting friendships that I still have to this day. The professors and academia of the school was great at preparing you for the workforce. The student life on campus provided a lot of opportunities for engagement and extra critical activities. Lynchburg is a small town but after a while you get accustomed to it and grow to love it if you’re into sports there are many sports opportunities and games to attend.

I have loved the supportive and encouraging Christian community here at Liberty. I have found a wonderful best friend already. There are so many events and games and fun things to do. I love gathering with such a large group of my peers to worship and learn about the Bible. I love how intentional and caring the professors are.
I liked the atmosphere. I would change the dorms to not have mold because literally everyone gets sick from it

My experience in Liberty University was full of so many sweet people. Having given my life to Christ on a Latino event finally feeling at peace with people that want me to flourish. The campus community is one of the biggest things that drew me here and will be keeping me here. There is so much love for each other here everyone has their place here. By vast amounts of events ranging from game nights, study sessions in the library, convocation, campus community, bible study, along with 24 hour prayer days. This is a place where the lord is actively moving.

Online learning was difficult. There was a social and personal disconnect. Relationships with professors and students felt impersonal and disconnected. This is my primary reason for deciding not to pursue my master's at Liberty. It would have been nice to see zoom classes that were virtual at least once per week for each course. Since many online students are working, this could be done similar to how local master's programs are here, offering classes at 4:30pm and 7:00pm. Online forums, virtual presentations/recordings, essays, and powerpoint presentations do not fill in the gap for this missing piece. I was a full-time student and it felt isolating, similar to the Covid-19 pandemic lockdown. However, I found the curriculum/academics portion was rewarding, especially as a Christian. I feel well equipped to pursue my calling both from a secular and Biblical perspective. I really enjoyed my coursework. I just wish the school was closer so I could attend in person.

Liberty University’s online education program offers an exceptional blend of academic rigor, flexibility, and Christ-centered values. The curriculum is designed to prepare students for success in their careers while fostering personal and spiritual growth. Professors are engaged, knowledgeable, and accessible, providing meaningful support throughout the learning process. The online platform is intuitive and allows students to balance education with work and life commitments. Liberty’s commitment to excellence and its emphasis on integrity and character shine through in every course. I wholeheartedly recommend Liberty University online to anyone seeking an affordable, high-quality education that genuinely equips graduates for real-world challenges.

They were very helpful and thorough in the application and getting started process. They answered any questions I had and showed me where to access the information I needed. They were always available to answer any questions I had and easy to get in touch with.

I really enjoyed my time at Liberty. I appreciated the different types of speakers that came to speak at Convocation. The professors genuinely cared; In the 4 years, I only had one or two that I really didn't like. I appreciated the combination of growing my faith and my education.

Having the ability to take classes online made continuing my education possible. It was convenient, however, not easier when it comes to the work. It was still very challenging, which made finishing so much more rewarding.

Liberty University is my home away from home where I have been instructed by professional and helpful teachers, met lifelong friends, have grown in my relationship with Christ and have found my purpose in the Nursing program.

ai_summary: At Lincoln Memorial University, students generally appreciate the welcoming atmosphere , emphasizing supportive faculty who prioritize accessibility and student success. The small campus fosters relationships among peers and professors, which many find beneficial for academic growth. However, there are mixed reviews about specific programs, particularly nursing, which some describe as disorganized despite acknowledging some helpful instructors. Student life appears vibrant, with opportunities for involvement in extracurricular activities and community services, although there is limited off-campus entertainment options. The cafeteria food quality is a common topic, receiving both praise and criticism for being inconsistent. Many students express concerns about tuition costs and the financial management of the university, feeling that more could be done to enhance affordability. Overall, LMU seems to create a close-knit community while facing challenges typical of smaller institutions.

reviews.text:
The best part of LMU is the setting: the mountains, the nature trails, the green space, and the slower pace make it a peaceful place to focus on school and build close relationships. The small community can feel welcoming and personal, especially for someone who prefers a quieter environment or doesn’t want the chaos of a huge university.
On the flip side, the rural location can be a drawback if you’re looking for a lively social scene, big-city amenities, or lots of nightlife. Things like shopping, entertainment, and diverse food options require driving to nearby towns, and the overall campus vibe is more relaxed than energetic
LMU has been a fantastic school! The people are very welcoming, and it is obvious that your professors want you to succeed.

Coming from Ohio, I wasn’t sure what to expect at Lincoln Memorial University, but the transition to a new city and campus was smooth. The welcoming community made it easy to adjust, and I quickly felt at home. The peaceful setting helped me stay focused on my goals as a student-athlete, and the small class sizes made it easier to connect with professors and classmates. I’ve really appreciated the support from both the academic and athletic staff. If there’s one thing I’d like to see improve, it would be more dining options and events that celebrate different cultures. Overall, LMU has been a great place to grow.

I enjoy that the school has several different campuses that are available around the state. I am attending a specialized school that provides one on one training and academics. This has allowed me to excel in my academic and personal life. I appreciate the personalized experience and learning they provide.

Who ever gave the lady at the housing department a job, you should fire her this instant. She does not know what she is doing nor can respond to emails with respect! Only cares about herself and do her job quickly and poorly!

If you are looking for a smaller college to go to Lincoln Memorial University is a great choice! I have had a great experience here; the staff and students have all been great. I have struggled in a subject and the professor did not hesitate with helping me and does the same with all of their students. I choose to go here because I was looking for a smaller school to attend and they have a great pre-vet program that consists of students being able to take the same or similar classes as they would in vet school.
Lincoln Memorial provided me confidence in my abilities and education through ample hands on experience and constant access to a skills lab.

Love the view. The fact it felt as though it was its own town and population just on campus.
I would say I wouldn’t change anything other than more dorms in order to accompany more students to be able to able to enjoy Lincoln memorial like I did

I am currently enrolled for LPN-RN I start summer 2025, but what i have experienced so far from the staff is amazing they are very helpful in your process, I have heard good things about this school and my friend who graduated from here referred me.

I’m a freshman at Lincoln Memorial University. Some of the students and faculty here are racist. Not all are! The professors are here just to get paid. They are very unorganized and disconnected. The cafeteria food is either cold or burnt, most people are sick after eating it. Also ladies be careful this school likes to cover things up. You only get 36 hours of work study each semester. Enough to get you by for one month. Liles is the dirtiest building you could live in. The boys poop in the shower and the girls leave hair EVERYWHERE. The pipes in the shower stinks so you’ll have to hold your nose or breath. The toilet stalls are very small. When your in your dorm do not leave your windows open bugs will appear fast. This town doesn’t like blacks unless you go to this school and that’s barely. Also they have been fixing Liles since the middle- end of first semester.

Overall good experience so far, every professor I’ve had has been very helpful and attentive. School holds quite a few on-campus activities for alumni weekly, including making valentines cards for meals on wheels recipients. However, local area around doesn’t have much to do unfortunately. Other students have mostly been pleasant to interact with, and parking is mostly not an issue. Cafeteria food is good most days, but some are a hit or miss. I am attending for the veterinary health science program, and so far would recommend to anyone considering the university!

As a freshman, everything may be new and unfamiliar at first. Lincoln Memorial University, with its small but beautiful campus and great people, made me feel welcome right from the start. It's a great place to pursue an academic degree, get involved in student life and grow as a person.

I love that the campus is in the middle of a small town. I feel safer, and I love eating at local restaurants and shopping at older grocery stores rather than chain places. The scenery is beautiful because the campus is tucked away in the middle of trees and lush grass. I love the atmosphere on campus. Everyone is so friendly, and it is easy to make new connections and maintain relationships with staff, professors, and peers. I would love to see more diversity on campus. I would love to see the number of international students in attendance increase.

My overall experience has been good and i really enjoy this school. No matter the event or what is going on I always feel involved. The support system thought students, teachers, and staff has been really helpful. They always make sure that I feel comfortable and that I understand what is going on.

I love going to LMU! I live in the village where you will have a full kitchen plus a living room. Some dorms have ceiling fans while others have TVs as well. The school has improved their food since my freshman year, and they constantly ask for feedback. The school has opened new building/programs recently. There are plenty of activities provided by the school and recreational sports. Some activities are festivals, crafts, food, day trips, flag football, beach volleyball and permanent jewelry. The school values community service and provides a lot of opportunities for this. They are planning to build more athletic buildings and a new student center. The area around the school is also growing. There have been 2 new stores, 4 new restaurants, and 3 new coffeeshops opening up in the last 3 years. The drive to Knoxville, Johnson city, and Morristown is not bad if looking to go into a bigger city for a day. Party scene isn't big on campus but students most will go to the bars.

ai_summary: Lipscomb University offers a unique college experience characterized by its tight-knit community and a strong Christian foundation. Many students appreciate the intentional relationships they can build with professors due to small class sizes, leading to personalized support in both academic and personal development. The campus life is thriving, with various events that foster friendships and a welcoming environment. While the academics are regarded as rigorous and high-quality, some students highlight concerns about certain administrative policies and the limited social scene for those uninterested in traditional social clubs. Overall, Lipscomb stands out for its commitment to student success and well-being, although some aspects, like housing and dining facilities, could benefit from improvement.

reviews.text: Professors were wonderful, provided encouraging and constructive feedback. The school tries extrememly hard to keep students informed and involved.

Lipscomb University is a place where you as a student can grow and explore professional opportunities. With professors that encourage you and have the best interests for you is what pushes a student to be the best they can be, and Lipscomb University offers this.

This is my first year at Lipscomb University, and I am a Health and Physical Education major, and it is safe to say that my has experience so far has been amazing! A huge reason I decided on Lipscomb was the enriching Christ centered environment that Lipscomb works for every student to have while on campus. The weekly gatherings and breakout sessions are personal favorites that are always so enjoyable to attend. Nonetheless, the academics at Lipscomb are another highlight. The small student-to-teacher ratio allows professors to really get to know me in every class I have. This creates a more personal and supportive learning environment for me and that was extremely important in my decision to come here. The campus dining options are also great. My favorite spot is Wild Blue, a Japanese restaurant on campus that serves delicious rice bowls and sushi. Overall, I’m so glad I chose Lipscomb—it’s been a fantastic place to grow and I am so glad I chose Lipscomb as my college home!

I’ve only been at Lipscomb for a few weeks, but I’ve loved every moment of it! My friends and professors have been such a blessing, and I have enjoyed being surrounded by the genuinely compassionate community here!

One of the coolest things about Lipscomb University is the professors interest in your individual success. They don't just teach the material to get you by, they truly dedicate themselves to growing you as a student and as a person in general. The Lipscomb community is so genuine and inclusive, and I feel like I have grown more in my first two years at Lipscomb then I have throughout my entire life.

I really enjoyed the atmosphere of the campus. It is always so clean and full of life! I also enjoyed all of the events they would put on around the campus. There was so much variety and celebrations of different cultures. As far as things I would like to see changed, the one thing I can think of is even more food variety in the Bison Cafe. Of course, there is already a lot of foods to choose from, but I would like to see more international cuisine incorporated into the menu.

Everyone is super friendly and willing to help you succeed in your academics. The dorms are some of the nicest I've seen of any college I toured before deciding on Lipscomb. The food is also super accommodating especially as I have many food allergies and am able to eat great food there. Go Bisons!

When I first learned about Lipscomb, I was not sure I even wanted to attend the school, but upon visiting it and getting to tour the campus, I fell in love with the community. The community of people is what really made me decide to attend Lipscomb. The infrastructure of the school itself is great, but that does not compare to the heart of it; the staff, the professors, the counselors, and other students. I am grateful to have found such a space where people genuinely care, where others want to see you grow and encourage and uplift you in this journey called college. My hope is that in the years to come Lipscomb will continue to grow and flourish. I want it to change for the better. I want for incoming students to cherish their time there, the way I do now, to be comforted in knowing they are at their home away from home when they are on campus.

Lipscomb was the perfect balance of a small university (great personalized attention, easy to get involved, intentional class sizes) but had fabulous opportunities through Nashville. Professors came from impressive and experienced backgrounds, internship opportunities were strong, career fairs had many impressive employers, and the fun things in Nashville made my experience even better. I made lasting friendships and keep in touch with many of my professors. My faith was grown not only through chapel and bible classes, but through being surrounded by tremendous people who are all looking to grow together. I felt supported by my other students, faculty and staff. Lipscomb is worth the investment and couldn't have been a better experience!
As a doctoral student at Lipscomb I have felt welcomed in by the community and have easily established relationships with faculty and staff. You can tell everyone here cares for the students!
Lipscomb University is an amazing college. It has so many majors and so many fun activities. They have activists that make you forget that your in college and have fun.

I have been at Lipscomb for 11 years in some capacity. Overall, I could not speak more highly of this institution and this community. Lipscomb emboldened my faith in a way that church never did, and I can point back to my real conviction and passion for the Bible to a class at Lipscomb. The friends I have made at Lipscomb have shown to me time and time again what it means to genuinely and selflessly love someone. The mentors, professors, and bosses I have had at Lipscomb have shown a real concern for my needs as a person, and have cultivated leadership qualities within me through their encouragement and quiet example. Lipscomb has been and always will be a place that feels more like home than any other place. If I ever moved away from Nashville, saying goodbye to the Lipscomb community would be the hardest part.

I have loved my time at Lipscomb. Though the cost is hefty, it's been worth it. I've loved learning in engineering and learning how my life- no matter what field I go to can have a higher purpose than earning a pay check. Seeing how I can use my passions and skills for God's kingdom and sharing His love with others has been really cool. The community and professors supporting you has helped as engineering is really rough sometimes. If I had to pick a school all over again, I'd pick Lipscomb - no doubt.
My experience at Lipscomb University has been very transformative. The housing could use some improvement, but the campus is a very good environment for student life on campus.
While I am still attending Lipscomb University my time here so far has been nothing but positivity and greatness. I’ve always felt safe and the professors are awesome

ai_summary: Long Island University offers a diverse and welcoming environment , particularly appealing for those pursuing careers in health sciences, business, and education. The small class sizes foster personal connections between students and professors, allowing for personalized academic support . However, many reviews point out dissatisfaction with the administration's focus on profit over student welfare and recurring issues with enrollment services and communication. Campus life can be limited, especially for commuters, with fewer social events and activities noted on weekends. While the campus and facilities, especially at LIU Post, are praised for their beauty and ongoing improvements , the high tuition costs are a common concern among students. Overall, LIU is recognized for its strong academic programs and supportive faculty, yet faces challenges in campus engagement and administrative efficacy.

reviews.text: There are many things that I enjoy about Long Island University. I like that the class sizes are small because I feel that I learn better in less crowded environments. This also allowed me to get to know my professors well. I also like how my classes are all within walking distance. The food on campus is great, though it is be a bit expensive. Overall, I am having a great time at Long Island University.

So far, pretty decent. I have had some struggles with being bounced from counselor to counselor in only one month being at the school but each one has been extremely helpful in explaining what I need to do to succeed. I am a transfer student and was nervous about coming in the middle of my college career but they have made the transition easy. Very inviting school and environment, I really feel like I belong.

I’m a student in LIU’s PharmD program, which is by no means easy. I love that all of my professors were always willing to help and offer office hours. We frequently had questions and workshops during lecture time, which were pivotal to my learning.

I loved my time at LIU-Post. I made great, lasting friends. The social scene on the beautiful campus was great. The campus itself was stunning and had everything I wanted. Academically, my courses were interesting and some were challenging, but I always felt it was an enabling environment that allowed me to succeed. In high school I tested well on the standardized tests, but I was not a great student. In college, I wanted to get serious and build a future. LIU-Post proved to be the perfect environment to build my academic confidence before graduate school. Also, LIU-Post is within the New York "Tri-State Area", but I was from hundreds of miles away. LIU-Post introduced me to New York City without forcing me to start in Manhattan. I thought that was awesome and provided me a valuable education alongside my college degree. LIU-Post is a hidden gem - work hard here and you can parlay high-achievement into a better future.

I personally didn’t feel very inclined to join certain events as they made me uncomfortable. I did enjoy some events where we could kind of just keep to ourselves because I’m an introvert. I do wish there would be more lounging areas as the cafeteria isn’t very big.
I only just started, however, the Brooklyn Campus is very beautiful. Everyone who attends is very kind and lends a helping hand in your college experience.
Love it here!! I do wish that there was better and faster communication, but otherwise, the campus is beautiful, and professors are really nice.

Honestly I’m quite surprised how bad LIU Brooklyn truly is the administration is a mess and worse of all they let people cheat. There are way to many nursing students who are no even remotely close to being qualified who are somehow in the nursing program

My experience at Long Island university is the best experience I’ve had in a school since my freshman year of high school. I recently moved from New York to Georgia to start a new school and I didn’t quite like the environment of that school so after doubling up and graduating early Ive decided to go back to New York to continue my education.

LIU seemed like a dream school, but I regret investing in it. The administration seems okay with students struggling and appears more focused on finances than student success. The only redeeming quality is the few teachers who go against LIU policies to teach us what we actually need to know.

My overall experience at Long Island University has been both enriching and transformative. The university offers a diverse range of academic programs, and I’ve found the faculty to be knowledgeable, supportive, and genuinely invested in student success. I especially appreciated the small class sizes, which allowed for more personalized attention and deeper discussions.
Outside the classroom, LIU provides numerous extracurricular opportunities that helped me grow personally and professionally—from student organizations to internships and networking events in nearby New York City. The campus environment is welcoming and inclusive, and I’ve made lasting friendships and connections during my time here.
While there were occasional administrative hurdles, my experience overall has been overwhelmingly positive, and I feel well-prepared for the next chapter of my career.

I've only been at this university for a year, but I can say that I like it. The area is nice, and the professors and academics are up to standard. I'm not too sure about campus life, but I can tell they are good in those areas.
i really enjoy the size of the school the nursing program is amazing and i love all of the professors! This school is amazing and i am very happy with my decision of attending this university!

I have loved my time at LIU so far. I am enrolled in the college of veterinary medicine, which is relatively new compared to others. Because of this, the campus is very up to date and has provided me with more than I need to succeed. I live in the graduate dorms, which I was nervous about because its been years since I'd lived in one, but they are very well maintained and better than expected. I would not change anything about this school, I knew I made the right choice from the day I moved in.

As an incoming freshman at Long Island University, I'm incredibly excited to begin my journey this fall! While I haven't officially started classes yet, my overall experience so far has been very positive, especially during the admissions process and orientation. I was really drawn to LIU because of its strong academic programs in my chosen field and the sense of community I felt during my campus visits. I also appreciate the location, which offers a good balance of a campus environment with access to opportunities in the surrounding area. I'm particularly looking forward to getting involved in student clubs and taking advantage of the resources available to new students. As for what I'd like to see change, it's hard to say definitively before I've even set foot in a classroom. However, I'm eager to see how the university continues to support student well-being and foster a diverse and inclusive environment.

ai_summary: At Louisiana State University, students often highlight the vibrant campus life and friendly atmosphere , with numerous opportunities for involvement through clubs, organizations, and social events. The sense of community is strong, and many have noted the diversity of the student body as a significant positive aspect. Academically, the experience can be mixed; while several students find the professors supportive and accessible, others report that some may prioritize research over teaching. The quality of academic advising also receives varied feedback, with some wishing for more assistance. Campus facilities, especially housing, can sometimes be outdated and overcrowded, leading to critiques regarding infrastructure and safety. Dining options are frequently mentioned as needing improvement, particularly concerning variety and nutrition. While many enjoy the spirited sports culture , particularly around football, the associated costs can be a concern for some students. Overall, LSU offers a dynamic college experience, with plenty of upside amid areas that could benefit from enhancements.

reviews.text: I was initially hesitant on moving to Baton Rouge for college, but the community at LSU has really helped me feel like I belong here. Clubs are amazing, my professors are mostly very responsive and eager to help with me, just a freshman. Athletics are phenomenal, but I already knew that going in. What I didn’t know was that this place really strives to make you feel at home, while showing off Louisiana culture. I love it here
My overall experience with Louisiana State University has been great with a side of normal life issues of course!

I genuinely love my experience at LSU! The professors are great and they really care about their students. You will not have a better campus experience anywhere else. The only complaint I have is regarding the road conditions on and around campus! Best value for your money if you have tops!
Being a student at LSU means being apart of a huge community that is caring and loving. Though this community is helpful, the environment surrounding LSU is not the best as it is a bad area.

Overall, my two years at the university has been great! Even with an open campus I feel safe traveling to/from classes even at different times of the day. Most of the professors are understanding and teach the information well. One thing I would like to see changed is true availability to academic advisors and scholarship/grant resources to students, especially those that have stayed with the university for at least 2 years. Maybe even getting rid of out of state costs for those same students.

LSU is a diverse university that embraces louisana's strong and unique culture and feel, while also being welcoming to those form out of state. The Center of Academic Support is extremely helpful, and thus far all of my professors have certainly cared greatly for their students, and manage to help make a big school feel small. The diverse student body makes it easy to learn about new places, while also being able to find your own social niche. While Baton Rouge is not a traditional college town, you can sense the local pride in the university, and it is an amazing thing to see. The main issues with LSU are a lack of commuter parking and student housing, with growing freshmen classes, the on-campus housing has not been able to keep up. For commuter parking, since upperclassmen and sophomores are essentially required to live off campus, it is difficult to find parking in a timely manner, if at all. These are fixable issues, but can definitely impact your school experience.

I experienced racism from other students, and it felt like LSU didn’t really care. Once you’re no longer a freshman, it seems like the university is more focused on money than student well-being. Campus is overcrowded, and it often feels like students are left to fend for themselves. I wish LSU prioritized inclusion, safety, and support for all students, not just incoming freshmen.
My overall experience with LSU has been very good so far. The professors, the advisors, and the amount of help the school gives the college students are very helpful for us!
I toured the campus and was overall impressed with the tour and opportunities to pursue a culturally diverse place to gain my degree and obtain the education I need to get my degree.

Really like the diversity of the student body. On a big campus, one still needs to feel included and have friend support. The engineering curriculum is tough, but prepares you for your next step in life. Definitely have to grow up and learn to communicate to get help when you need it. Definitely helped me to handle issues in my own.

While I am progressing forward with my degrees, the experience was one of overloaded student ratios to services, teaching, and housing/infrastructure. The university is trying, but there are difficulties in arranging meetings with advisors/professors, customer service during key times, much of the teaching seems to be deferred to assistants or on cruise control just coasting by. If you are looking for a party school, there is opportunity here. It will always be what you make of it. The school tries hard to keep security, but with the area and atmosphere, there seem to be constant threats to remain diligent of.

The classes were very challenging but good very good place to study and has a large campus to walk and to get food and also to hangout with friends. I really like the theatre room very pretty and very well organized within the student union. Really enjoy the experience there and I enjoyed seeing how other people promote about Christianism and showing off their clothes and jewelry.

Overall, the teachers are great! Online program is more challenging than I originally thought it would be however, I’m learning and having fun. They allow you to be autonomous and independent but yet offer support. Nice balance.
LSU has been an amazing experience. The place, people, and environment are great and it’s a place many should go.

Overall LSU has been a pretty great experience so far. My professors thus far have been helpful and clear with the curriculum however, one thing I can say is I have had some difficulties with scheduling exams in our testing center with things such as having certain times displayed as available when they really aren't. I would say a positive is definitely the social aspect and campus life. There is always an event on campus with opportunities to make so many new connections and just get involved on campus. A change I would like to see is honestly the food. While we have a great variety of dining spots on and off campus, the actual dining hall itself isn't very pleasurable. Speaking from the West side of campus, you would love the dining hall if you were vegetarian with many options catering to that. There isn't much variety and when there is meat, it is almost always only chicken. While grateful to have these options, I feel like this is a big change many LSU students would love to see.

ai_summary: Louisiana Tech University offers a welcoming and friendly campus atmosphere , where many students feel a strong sense of community and belonging . The diverse student body participates in a variety of clubs and organizations, fostering engagement and connection. Academics are a strong point, especially in STEM fields, with professors generally being supportive and dedicated to student success. However, housing options can be limited and costly, and parking remains a common concern among students. While the small-town setting of Ruston provides a cozy environment, some students find the local entertainment options lacking. Overall, Louisiana Tech stands out for its personal touch, inclusive culture, and quality of education, leaving many students feeling well-prepared for their future careers.

reviews.text: The staff at the campus does a really good job at organizing activity so you can get to know other students at the college. There are lots of clubs that you can get involved with. A lot of the RA's in the dorms plan game nights and movie nights so you can get to know the other people in your dorm. The food in the cafeteria is really good, lots to choose from. Just about everything is within walking distance, I can go for weeks without needing to use my car. I would definitely recommend LA Tech to anyone looking for a good, safe place to continue their education.

As an international student Louisiana tech is great, it’s always peaceful around here and things are easy to find but that’s in exchange for all of the interesting things I heard about the US being super far away

It was a great experience. I met some new people, reconnected with some old friends, got to grow my religious status as well. Had lots of fun late night adventures. I liked how it is so walkable, I pretty much walked everywhere I could. The buildings are nice and I like the architecture, professors were good, they really seemed to care about how well you did and was there to help if you needed. It was so good that ever since I left I have been trying to go back.

I really enjoyed my time at Louisiana Tech within my classes as well as with on campy events, clubs, and fun! It is hard to find parking occasionally, even harder to find a job in the town of Ruston, LA. I hope tech continues to foster the college experience with education and that we can grow consistently without raising school fees like as of late.

Wonderful medium sized university. Class sizes were great and the area had a ton of outdoor activities. I love going back to visit and see the new buildings and changes to improve athletics and academics.

Living in the surrounding community my entire life, it was inevitable I would be attending Louisiana Tech University. I took a few duel enrollment courses in high school and went to Tech as a Sophomore, which really helped my experience, granting a feeling for the courses before stepping into my first college class. Additionally, I chose to walk-on to the Division 1 Track and Field program at Louisiana Tech University as a javelin thrower. The friends I made at that program alone made it worth it. The balance classes, practice, and travel were beneficial in that it helped me establish grant a nice routine I am thankfully to have kept to this point. Finally, my major of Political Science and two minors of Spanish and Sociology provided experiences I feel made me very well rounded as an applicant to law school this coming fall 2026.
At my time at Louisiana Tech University, I had a great time. The professor are good at what they do and are very available. The food is good, and I overall feel welcome and safe on campus.

My overall experience at Louisiana Tech University has been both enriching and transformative. I have expanded my knowledge not only in the classroom but also through campus life, student organizations, and community service. Being part of this environment has taught me discipline, responsibility, and the importance of balancing academics with personal growth. Campus activities and student life have helped me build lasting friendships and leadership skills, while community service has shown me the value of giving back and making a positive impact. Maintaining my classes has required focus and perseverance, but it has also strengthened my character and prepared me for future challenges. Louisiana Tech has given me more than an education it has provided opportunities to grow as a student, a leader, and a member of the community.
I am having a wonderful time. The courses are stimulating, the events are nonstop and collaborative, and the people are great.

I am currently enrolled at LA Tech as a freshman. It is going well, but I really want a job on campus but there are barely any for freshman’s which sucks! I really tried to reach out almost everywhere on campus, but nothing.

Louisiana tech university is an amazing university, well known for the engineering program, but Tech offers undergraduate programs ranging from forensic psychology to computer science and so much more. The town of Ruston just feels like home, it may not have all the entertainment in the world, but it’s a down to earth town with kind people and many small businesses that are very easygoing and well worth the visit. If anything tech is trying to make the best out of what is available and it’s going great if I say so myself. If I had to choose a college to attend again I would still choose Louisiana Tech University over anything else.

Louisiana Tech has been everything that I have ever imagined. The small class sizes has worked perfect for my academic experience and the involvement opportunities on campus have given me so much to do in my spare time. Overall, Louisiana Tech is a 11/10.

I love Louisiana Tech! The first time I walked on campus I felt like I was home; it felt like the college version of my high school. Once I attended orientation, I became even more excited to attend. The only thing I would change is how the finances are handled at the beginning of each quarter. That is a very stressful time because it feels like everything is put in at the last minute.

Great people, everyone is super helpful and everyone I’ve talked to has all been incredibly nice. There are some really great professors who’s been a good educator and kind. The only thing I would like to see done is more financial aid help, and a transparent overview of what the cost is like for new students. It was more than I expected, and there is so little time for new college students to get used to the system and reach out for help if they need.

My experience with Louisiana Tech University is one that I’ll cherish forever. The academics are beneficial to me and my journey to graduation. I’m in nursing, so getting my pre-requisites are important and so far, my professors and the resources I have been able to use to maintain good grades and understanding in my classes have been nothing but adequate. I love Louisiana Tech!

ai_summary: Loyola Marymount University offers a unique college experience with its beautiful campus and small class sizes , fostering close relationships between students and professors.
