Many students appreciate the sense of community and the supportive atmosphere , highlighting the professors' dedication to student success. However, some reviews mention challenges like a lack of diversity, poor communication from administration, and dissatisfaction with the dining options. The location is a definite highlight, offering easy access to beaches and a vibrant city life . While the party scene is described as smaller compared to larger universities, students generally find ways to engage socially. A recurring concern is the high cost of tuition and the perceived lack of adequate financial aid, leading some to question the university's value. Overall, Loyola Marymount University is seen as a great fit for students seeking an intimate academic environment, though it may not suit everyone’s preferences or expectations.

reviews.text: I love how involved the student life is here. Great programs. The school really likes to focus on the amazing creative side. I got into the top 5 film schools in the nation, and I can guarantee you that it is going to be a successful ride through.

I love the intercultural atmosphere on campus. No ethnicity bias. The environment ever green and refreshing. Lecturers ready to listen and do their parts. But modesty among ladies students may be put into consideration.

Overall, I've had a great experience so far but it did take some time to make friends my first year. Join clubs and don't be afraid to start a new one if you have an interest that isn't covered. Class quality is completely dependent on professor, so shop around. Teachers here really do care about students. No TAs are used. Food is good and housing is comparable to what I've seen at other schools. It is what you make of it.

I loved my experience attending LMU. The professors and classes prepared me well for life in the real world. I met my wife on campus and my friends from LMU are all friends for life. After graduation we all found success in our careers either with or without the help of grad school. I attended cal poly Pomona after for a graduate certificate and found it to be a breeze in comparison to the rigor of LMU. Campus life is fun with lots to do to keep you busy. I was not involved in Greek life but had plenty of friends that did. Overall I couldn’t have asked for a better experience.

Overall my experience at LMU after one semester has been very positive. The school size is small enough to where I know all my professors very well. LMU is a beautiful campus tucked away in a safe area and close to local attractions outside of campus. Best decision I’ve made in life period.

Loyola Marymount University is a very good college because they have so many resources. They have tutoring for those who needed some of them can be late but there is help if you need it. Professors also have office hours to help students if you have any questions. The staff is very nice to you and will help you. I don't live on campus but for the food wise there isn't that much options its the same thing this fall semester keep in mind that this is my first year. The food is very good but it can be pricey. There is a lot of events around campus and even clubs. The campus Is very beautiful there is so much green and color. There is sports and a gym for students. Loyola Marymount University will help you for any guidance that you need there, will support you, even therapist available, and fun activities .

I lived on campus while attending school. I think everyone is very kind and supportive. LMU is a smaller school so I always felt like I could ask for help. The Jesuit curriculum makes you more well rounded.
Loyola is such a beautiful place with so much to discover and see! The people are amazing and it is such a great environment!

The campus is beautiful and walkable, with lots of sunlight but also palm trees for some shade. I've liked all my professors so far; they are helpful during office hours and their class structures encourage me to learn more. I've had so many opportunities on campus just in my second year, more than I imagined I would achieve in 4 years. I also enjoy the diversity of Los Angeles, although I wish there was slightly more Asian representation.
I would like to see more of the dorms having AC and possibly more parking on campus/cheaper parking. I understand how both of these are logistically very difficult, but it's been a very real problem for students for years. With the economy in its current state, LMU has done a passable job in terms of food pricing, nutrition, and variety. If you don't have extra money or a car, you are very limited in terms of what kinds of food you can rotate through during the school year. It does work out, though.
Only good things to say... incredible school and community. Professors truly care about their students and want them to be successful! Location is also great...
Beautiful campus, everyone is nice and welcoming, and there is a lot of support for the students academic life and student life on campus.

My experience at Loyola Marymount University was truly meaningful. The small class sizes made it easy to connect with professors, and I always felt supported academically and personally. The campus has a peaceful, open feel, but there’s also a strong sense of community. I appreciated how LMU encourages students to think critically and act with purpose. My time there helped shape how I see the world and how I want to contribute to it.
LMU has been an honorable experience since it has become a place that I owe a lot of my personal growth to.

I had a wonderful experience in the film program at LMU. The teachers were passionate and realistic about what would be useful in the real world. The resources were plentiful. I felt safe and comfortable there. There were great opportunities to intern in Los Angeles.

I like Loyola Marymount University because of its welcoming campus atmosphere and strong sense of community. The university’s commitment to academic excellence, social justice, and ethical leadership aligns with my personal values. The beautiful campus in Los Angeles offers not only scenic views but also countless opportunities for networking, internships, and cultural experiences. LMU’s small class sizes allow for close relationships with professors, which enhances learning and support. I appreciate the diverse student body and the inclusive environment that encourages open-mindedness and growth. Overall, LMU stands out as a place where students are empowered to thrive academically and personally.

ai_summary: Loyola University Chicago offers a unique blend of urban convenience and academic challenge , situated in a beautiful campus next to Lake Michigan. While many students appreciate the intimate learning environment afforded by smaller class sizes and supportive professors, experiences with faculty can be hit-or-miss, with some feeling disconnected or ineffective. Academically, students find the university generally challenging, especially in non-STEM fields; however, navigating course availability can be frustrating for those in popular majors like pre-med. The campus is lively, with numerous activities and clubs, but the party scene is often described as minimal and influenced by Greek life. Concerns persist around diversity, student safety, and tuition costs, with some feeling the financial aid system could be more supportive. Overall, students enjoy the campus community and the vibrant city life that surrounds it, even if the balance between academics and social opportunities is still a topic of discussion.

reviews.text: I had a great experience at Loyola. People are very friendly and willing to help each other out when needed. Professors and faculty members have a positive nature. The best part is the campus, it is beautifully structured with students convenience in mind. There are free transit resources, such as a shuttle for Loyola students only, to travel between both campuses. On top of that, the CTA station is right in front of the main campus, which makes it very easy to reach.
I really enjoyed my time and got hands on experience with research. Definitely expensive but if you have a good scholarship, its not as bad.

After Northwestern and UChicago, I think Loyola is one of the better schools in the area. It is right next to the lake, and the campus is beautiful. If you like a smaller school, but not so small that you know everyone, Loyola is a good place to go. They have a lot of housing, which is nice. I highly recommend their study abroad program in Rome. Greek life is tiny and not that good, so if partying is important to you, then this is not the place. Chicago has amazing nightlife, though, so you kind of have that trade-off. The professors I have had in the Psychology department are fantastic, but I have friends in other departments who have had bad experiences.

It has been an amazing first year at Loyola. I’ve been enjoying some of the classes with some professors who show that they care about their students. It has been making me feel comfortable in succeeding in their classes

As a commuter student I really appreciate the university for having a lounge with a microwave, refrigerator and other appliances. The admin can make questionable decisions, but the professors and the students are all amazing people and a very beautiful, sustainable campus promoting pollinators and biodiversity! Very Gryffindor Harry Potter type of feeling but also somewhat futuristic.
I love it! I am a first year student at Loyola and bae nothing bad to say about the school! I am forever greatful that I picked this school to grow my career.
I am obsessed with the campus of this school and its tight knit community! The chemistry department is definitely something that takes some time to get used to, but there are amazing resources!

The community and environment are so welcoming and supportive. Staff, students, and faculty are super approachable. It makes going to college worthwhile! I love the scenery and academic atmosphere. I just wish it wasn’t so expensive, I struggle to pull money together every time payments come around. It’s a struggle, especially since im unable to work at the moment. Loyola is a great school for those that can afford it.

I really love almost everything about this school! The professors are truly so nice and do their jobs very well and make sure to accommodate everyone. I really enjoyed the view of the campus itself as well. One thing I dislike, however, is the way I had transferred into this university. I was kind of thrown in blind, without being informed of things such as parking permits for commuter students, and enrollment specifics. There could be a lot more work done to also introduce the websites and other apps needed for classes, as the main website to access classes, called Sakai, I had found out about through google.
Loyola is a great school with a wonderful student body. It has a lot to offer and gives many chances to get involved!
Loyola is a very a school that values diversity and mental health. I've made good friends, lots of opportunities and a very nice campus.

I love the welcoming community of people. The location is perfect in my opinion because you're right on the lake and away from the business of the city life on the Lake Shore Campus, but there is also a downtown Water Tower Campus where you are right in the heart of Chicago, so it is legitimately the best of both worlds. You get a quiet, suburban feel, or you can take the shuttle downtown and be in the best city. Dining hall food is diverse and good, academics are rigorous but manageable, professors are kind and very easily accessible. People here are very nice and diverse so it's easy to make friends and find people to go out or stay in with. There are many campus events and clubs to feel a part of the school. I think Loyola is an extremely expensive school and it definitely doesn't need to be. But overall, I feel very safe and I feel I am getting an amazing, well-rounded education. I love it here and I recommend.
I enjoy it very much but find myself constantly stressing about debt. The party life is dead but clubbing scene is active just not for me.

Loyola is a very money hungry school. I feel as though they don’t truly care about the person, just their money. While I still am a freshman, few of my professors are actually understandable and helpful. Most talk at you and don’t really make sense. Food was decent. Going there gave me extreme anxiety.

I love how diverse campus is and all of the different opportunities that LUC offers students. If I were to change one thing about Loyola it would be to offer more scholarship opportunities for academic achievement.

ai_summary: Loyola University New Orleans offers a unique blend of academic and cultural experiences, highlighted by a diverse student body and accessible faculty. Many students appreciate the small campus size, which fosters a welcoming and community-oriented atmosphere , allowing for meaningful interactions with professors and peers. While academic programs, particularly in the arts and counseling, are praised for their quality, some students have noted inconsistency in teaching effectiveness across departments. Student life is vibrant, closely intertwined with the lively city of New Orleans, although the social scene can be distracting and the facilities sometimes outdated. Complaints about tuition affordability and food quality are common, but many students enjoy the camaraderie and support services provided by the university. Overall, Loyola is characterized as a supportive environment that encourages personal growth amid the unique cultural backdrop of New Orleans.

reviews.text: I thoroughly enjoy my academic lifestyle and professors, however student life isn't it. On top of that something is almost always wrong with the water. Haven't had hot water since first semester

Loyola University of New Orleans is a good school, a great school even. It has an active community, who do events both on campus and off campus, and are actively kind. Wonderful teachers, with most if not all with degrees in their fields of teaching. And with a relatively small campus, the teacher do care about you as an individual.
Loyola is making progress for sure, the dorms are renovated, chick fill a on campus. Academics are prestigious and uptown New Orleans is an amazing and very safe area

The dorms are not good, they do not prioritize your health here. We did not have hot water for over a month because the boiler was broken. It kept breaking and we were stuck with absolutely freezing water. There is mold in the dorms and the refuse to acknowledge it, instead they call it mildew. The money is not worth staying at this school as the educational opportunities are heavily lacking. Athletics are okay, however our gym is outdated and sad. The food is okay here, although there is cross contamination. Overall, I would not recommend this school to anyone unless you are a music major.
Loyola University New Orleans is a really great college for those that want a good education. The professors was you to succeed.

My overall experience so far is that everyone is nice. The campus is clean. I drive myself to school and parking is the worst part about campus. I’m only enrolled part-time, but by next semester I’m expected to be on campus full-time. I haven’t spend time in the gym yet, but it’s a beautiful equipped with workout equipments.

The people at Loyola are very friendly and welcoming (almost everyone is willing to help you, they smile and greet you, they hold open doors for you). There's lots of student programs, clubs, and opportunities for involvement. They're very avid on supporting and aiding all students in any way that they can; whether it be a disability, if you're in need of financial support, or just need tutoring, they have all sorts of organizations for that.

Loyola University focuses on a Jesuit education with an emphasize on diversity. No matter your race, religion, sexual or gender identity, or any other quality, they will meet you where you are. The staff are willing to support you through any and all challenges you face.

My experience so far has been generally enjoyable, the people who work here are understanding and caring towards their students. The university gives a great environment and ensures that students get a fair chance for everything.

My experience has been an roller coaster ride full of highs and lows. From freshman year until now. I like how diverse it is. I would love to see the affordability to change. Loyola gets expensive every year and they also cut aid down. Its hard trying to afford it especially when they enforced strict rules. The fin aid staff hasn't been answering the phones and when they do they put you on hold for hours.
I love it at Loyola. I am constantly challenged so constantly growing and I feel extremely supported.
I will soon be starting at Loyola and I'm very excited for this new life experience. I am ready to see what Loyola has to offer.

My overall experience at Loyola University New Orleans has been a mix of positives and some frustrations. I appreciate the small class sizes and the professors who genuinely care about their students. The campus has a unique charm, and being in New Orleans definitely adds to the cultural experience.
That said, I do wish there were more to do on campus. Events and activities can feel limited, and it sometimes feels like student engagement isn’t a top priority. I also think there’s room for improvement when it comes to student resources; a lot of them exist on paper, but they’re not always accessible or genuinely helpful in practice. It would be great to see more support that truly meets students where they are.
Overall, Loyola has potential, but I’d like to see it invest more in making the student experience fuller and more supportive.

I really enjoy the diversity here, especially with Loyola’s central location in the city. Music students, especially, are shown so much diverse and unique flavors of music that would be hard to get anywhere else. The scene here is utterly unmatched. Students at Loyola can also take classes at Tulane University, which is incredible.

Attending Loyola University New Orleans was a great experience. The campus fostered a strong sense of community, with supportive professors who truly cared about students’ success. Small class sizes encouraged meaningful discussions and personalized learning. The university’s Jesuit values emphasized social justice, critical thinking, and service, which shaped both academic and personal growth. Located in the vibrant city of New Orleans, the culture, music, and food enriched daily life and inspired creativity. Loyola provided not just a quality education, but also lasting friendships and unforgettable memories. It’s a place where passion meets purpose—a truly enriching college experience

ai_summary: Marian University Indianapolis offers a close-knit community where students appreciate the small class sizes and personal attention from professors, which enhances their academic experience. Many reviewers highlight the strong nursing and business programs , with faculty dedicated to student success and a supportive learning environment . Campus life features a range of athletics, clubs, and a vibrant atmosphere , although some students note that the food options and housing can be lacking. The university's location near downtown Indianapolis provides access to additional resources and activities. While tuition is a common concern among students, they feel that the education received and supportive environment make it a worthwhile investment. Overall, Marian University is seen as a welcoming space that fosters relationships among students and faculty.

reviews.text: I have enjoyed my first year of experiences being on my own, connecting with new people, and growing academically.
My experience has been great so far. The professors truly seem to care! I think the professors are very knowledgeable and have a great love for teaching.
I have applied and accepted into Marian University. I am excited for the opportunity. I have made the dance team at Marian and love the campus.
I believe that if you are here to study it will be okay, great fit for those who wants to focus without any distractions.

Marian University Indianapolis offers a truly positive experience marked by academic excellence, strong community, and personal growth. Students benefit from small class sizes and dedicated professors who provide individualized support and mentorship. The campus blends a peaceful environment with vibrant student life, enhanced by the EcoLab, historic spaces, and community events. Its location near downtown Indianapolis gives students access to internships, cultural activities, and career opportunities. With hands-on learning, high job placement rates, and a welcoming atmosphere, Marian equips students with both knowledge and confidence, making it an ideal place to learn, grow, and prepare for a successful future.
There could be some improvements in the enrollment process. More direct and clear communication on orientations , etc
It is a good college campus environment and academics are well taught with good professors. The student section at athletic events is well energized as well.

I’ve enjoyed my experience at Marian. I came from IU East which is a small NAIA school to Marian which is a larger NAIA school but over the years that I have been here I have learned and grown a lot. The campus is small but has a beautiful look to it with a large trail that and forest behind the school called the ecolab. Most of the professors are good people and will work with you if you need help with a topic or if you have circumstances going on where they will understand. With it being a small school still you see a lot of the same people everyday but also you get the chance to really strengthen those bonds and make lifelong friends.
Marian’s a good college, everyone’s nice and understanding, but there’s nothing to do in the area, and there’s no “party scene”.
What I like so far is how small the classes are it gives you a chance to be more connected with the professors and have a better understanding of the subjects

I am sure attending Marian University will be a meaningful journey. The close-knit community has made me secure in not only my education but mt teachers and peers as well. Professors actually care about the success of their student's. They offer both academic rigor and personal support. The small class sizes allowed for more meaningful interactions with my peers and the staff as well as a deeper learning and understanding of the material that was being taught. One of the most strongest aspects about Marian is their strong focus on healthcare and education programs. The college of osteopathic medicine is particularly remarkable, which brings a vibrant sense of purpose and professionalism to the campus. Expanding the dining options would greatly enhance the daily student life. It would also be great to see an increase in support for mental health services. increasing opportunities for internships across all majors would further enrich the academic environment as well.

Attending Marian University was a life changing and crucial experience for me in my academic life. Marian's campus felt like home to me because it offered a safe and welcoming environment that had continuous outpouring of new experiences. I felt like the values of Marian (Reconciliation, Peace and Justice, Dignity of the Individual, and Responsible Stewardship) were shown and worked into all course work. This helped me focus on my values and beliefs that I had as an individual and continue to follow them with the support of Marian's Franciscan Values. The professors worked with each individual student to help them reach their full potential. The provided the best experience they could for the students to be able to take on the curriculum handed to them.

I am currently on the basketball team at Marian, and I am having a hard time adjusting. Our coaches make it hard to adjust and they are not on the same page. My professors have been good this second semester, but the first semester was group work, and everyone in my group was foreign, so the language barrier was difficult to overcome. My dorm always has problems—water going out, heating and cooling problems, and wifi outages making it hard to complete homework. I am looking to transfer and am applying for scholarships that can make transferring a lot easier on me financially.
Overall Marian University is a good school. It keeps expanding with new academic programs appearing every year. When I attended it still was very conservative. I don't know if that has changed.

I was a transfer to Marian from a large big ten school and Butler. I really enjoyed my professors and academic advisor, they really cared for me. Saying that, you have to make an effort to get to know them and participate in the classroom so they can get to know you better. The best part of the school might be the career advisor center but YOU have to use it. They are great helping students with their resumes and interviews to get grads ready for the one reason we all go to college: to get a job. I was not an athlete, but 55% + students are athletes so it was difficult to make friends there as a transfer and I was also a commuter. Some students are very gifted and smart while others could care less about school, more about sports. The party scene is very slim and there is no Greek life. Being in the city of Indianapolis is great for students as many banks, hospitals, schools, etc are around the area for internships. Like many things in life, it is what you make of it.

ai_summary: Marquette University offers a vibrant community experience that combines a strong academic focus with an urban environment. Many students appreciate the supportive faculty who genuinely care about their success and are approachable, fostering good relationships that enhance their learning experience. The campus is situated in downtown Milwaukee, providing students with access to city life and a variety of extracurricular activities, including a spirited athletic culture , especially with men's basketball games. However, some reviews mention challenges such as high tuition costs , limited food options, and concerns around safety and diversity . Although many students feel welcomed and included, there are experiences of cliquishness and incidents related to racism that have been noted. Overall, Marquette's blend of a close-knit community and urban lifestyle makes it a unique choice, particularly for those who value both academic and extracurricular engagement.

reviews.text: Marquette University has great academics, faculty, staff, and overall commitment to giving back to the community. You can really tell that the university was built on Jesuit ideals and continues to follow that. However, there are some downsides. With it being a private Catholic institution, the social scene isn't the greatest. The city of Milwaukee is a great setting for a college, but there are some safety concerns, especially in the areas surrounding campus.

Marquette was a great experience. I liked the classes, the campus, and the campus life. There were some good dining halls and the campus life was vibrant. Marquette has a really big athletics department so there are also a lot of athletic events to attend or participate in.
Awesome, everybody is super personable and friendly, could use some work in the quality of dining hall food but overall dorms are well kept
The area that the campus is in is very dangerous. Additionally there are lots of homeless who will constantly ask you for money. Fun school to party at and the academics are challenging.

I was a commuter student, which made it more difficult to form relationships with peers and form study groups. Professors and Teaching Assistants (TA) were helpful, and there was tutoring available for lower-level courses. College was much faster paced than high school, which required some getting used to. Overall, I liked my experiences there and would recommend this university to others.

Its really a change of scenery of where im from. In my city its always loud and theres cars constantly, but here it's a chance to take a break and relax, focus on studies, go to the beach, and have some fun with friends!
The lack of diversity accelerates passive racism on campus, making it hard for students to feel a sense of belonging.

The sense of community at Marquette- with students, staff, and faculty alike is unparalleled at any other college I've visited. People truly do care about you, your wellbeing, and your success as a student and an individual.
Overall, the school had an open diversity of academics and social life. They support, contribute and explore all the students interest.

My experience at Marquette University has really opened a broad sense of opportunity and diversity. There is a variety of ways to get involved on campus and it is highly encouraged so it is not at all hard, and everyone seems genuinely motivated and puts the effort into their school work and their extracurriculars.

Marquette has a large amount of opportunities for students. There are a plethora of nationally acclaimed programs. As long as you are willing to take advantage of the experiences Marquette has to offer, you can easily grow your network, knowledge, and future career options.

Marquette University is a great private, Jesuit university in Milwaukee. Liked the focus on academics, campus activities. Very clear that professors want to see students succeed, they put plans into action. Marquette is an accepting environment, but the party scene is bad. Underage drinking is too common.

I mainly like marquette because of my program. The faculty and professors are amazing and make it super easy to continue your education. I like how involved Marquette is in community service and for the most part everyone is nice and welcoming. Being good at basketball while I'm here is also an upside.

My freshman year at Marquette was filled with a wide range of experiences. From adapting to the environment of large lecture halls to engaging in discussions in smaller classroom settings, the academic transition was both challenging and rewarding as I had the opportunity to connect with peers from across the country and learn from professors with diverse international backgrounds with enriched my educational journey. One of my most significant areas of personal growth was in developing my study habits and work ethic. Coming from high school where work outside of class was minimal I quickly had to learn how to make time to study efficiently. Marquette's rigorous expectations pushed me to refine my ability to concentrate, manage my time, and study with purpose. Learning these skills was, in many ways, the cornerstone of my first-year at Marquette and hopefully push me further in the future.
I appreciate the commitment to Jesuit values, especially Marquette's commitment to inclusion, diversity, acceptance, and community service.

ai_summary: Marshall University offers a close-knit academic environment where students appreciate the supportive faculty and staff who genuinely care about their success. Many reviews highlight the approachable professors and the small class sizes , allowing for personalized interactions that enhance the learning experience. The campus is frequently described as beautiful and welcoming, with a variety of amenities such as dining options and student resources. However, some students express concerns about the parking situation, the cost of tuition, and the surrounding area's safety. Student life is vibrant, with many activities and a community feel, making it easy for students to forge friendships and engage in campus traditions. Overall, while there are challenges, students feel a sense of belonging and support at Marshall.

reviews.text: Marshall University has been a nice college experience so far. I like the classes and most of the professors. I would like to see a different variety of food options in some of their locations, and I do not care much for the area.

I am a full time student here at Marshall University, living on campus. My favorite thing about Marshall is the campus and opportunities they provide here. There is always something going on and someone here to help you get involved!

It has been the best experience so far in the 3 years I’ve been there. The professors are outstanding and the student body are so very nice and welcoming. The sports teams are good and I enjoy attending games.

I really enjoyed how peaceful and wonderful the campus truly is, especially while living on it. The dorms are lovely and I like the academics they have, specifically for music. Everyone I have talked to and met have been incredibly nice and are willing to help if needed!! Marshall provides a safe space for many, and allows their students to feel like its home. I love Marshall, and it truly has been a great place for me to connect with people, and even feel comfortable with what I am majoring in.

I enjoy Marshall and will continue to attend, as well as attend grad school here too. The classes and majors offered are very spread out with lots of great options, most of the professors are great and fair. I have had some negative experiences with a few professors, but nothing that would affect me enough to alter my furthered education. As for the student life, Greek life, and party scene. There are endless clubs and organizations to join, with lots of programs, jobs, and involvement too. The Greek life is smaller compared to other schools but just as great, they are genuine and inclusive. Then, for the party scene, there are some that are very fun and safe, but not as many as bigger schools. Which is fine in my opinion because we are here for an education and to focus on our futures. Overall this university is the perfect in between and will offer great academics, as well as experiences. Go herd!

I am currently enrolled in Marshall University. I enjoy that it is a D1 but doesn't feel huge. I like its walkability. I am in a sorority so I am involved in on campus activities, and campus tries to get students involved in many different things. I dislike Huntington in general as it is not safe. I also have celiac and I wish food options were more available and edible for me.

Professors lean heavy on group work. Group work grades based on how cooperative and positive a student is, not on actual work completion (literally a requirement for a group evaluation rubric). Grades are inflated, making students that should have earned C's getting high B's. Tuition is too high for the value of education being offered.

The most welcoming place I’ve ever been to. Even the janitorial staff seems interested in seeing the best for all the students. I already have recommended this college to high schoolers and I will continue to do so. Food is amazing, instructors are amazing, someone always has an answer to any questions you might have.

My overall experience at Marshall University has been really positive. The professors genuinely care about their students and go out of their way to help you succeed. The campus feels welcoming and has a strong sense of community. It’s easy to find people who are supportive and encouraging. I also appreciate the smaller class sizes, which make it easier to connect with instructors and classmates. If I could change anything, I’d like to see more parking options and maybe some updates to older buildings. It would also be nice to have more campus events or activities that bring everyone together outside of academics. Overall, though, Marshall has given me a great college experience that’s both challenging and rewarding.
I like everything about Marshall University. I like that the staff are supportive and will do whatever they can to help you, I like that the classes are diverse—there’s something for everyone!

My time at Marshall University has been very positive, with meeting lots of new friends, and finding it to be a very accepting environment. If i would change anything, i would make it mandatory for students to meet with their advisors as part of their orientation classes.

I enjoy being in an environment greater than what my high school was. I feel like I’m in a mostly mature place and I don’t Have to worry about anything terrible happening by accident. I would like to see more of what the money being used for my tuition and such goes to. There’s been a lot of problems in the dorms where power to the outlets just goes out and it’s slow to getting fixed. This actually happened to me and some of the food in our fridge went bad because of it. Other than this, my experience has been fantastic overall. I’m in a supportive music program with easy to access recourses and people who want to see all of us succeed.

There are so many opportunities to get involved on campus. I love how campus is fairly small and is walking distance from one side to campus to the other. This makes walking to the student center and to classes very convenient.

In my experience, the student affairs division at Marshall often felt unsupportive and unprofessional. At the Wellness Center and Women’s Center, I encountered dismissive and unkind behavior that created a hostile environment. It was the most unprofessional workplace I’ve been part of, with a culture that seemed to tolerate bullying.
A concern I raised was brushed off as “anxiety,” which felt stigmatizing. When I asked to pause a difficult conversation, I was told, “You’re an adult,” which I found belittling. Coworkers picked apart my clothing, spoke on my behalf, and suggested “teasing” was how to make friends, leaving me undermined and disrespected.
After my report to HR, they promised follow-up but never did, even after I quit. I resigned because the environment was hostile, and it seemed nothing was done, no justice was taken, and no action would occur. I hope this changes—no one deserves to go through this. Marshall must do better in both the Wellness Center and Women’s Center.

I haven’t experienced much yet at Marshall but so far my experience is pretty good. The only thing I would complain about is how some of the professors mainly the men can be very rude. Other than that the campus is beautiful and you professors can be willing to help.

ai_summary: Mary Baldwin University offers a diverse and inclusive community that many students appreciate, with a strong emphasis on personalized academic experiences due to its small class sizes. Reviews highlight the supportive nature of professors who are accessible and genuinely invested in student success. However, several students expressed concerns about the quality of campus facilities, including housing issues such as mold and deteriorating conditions in dorms. While the campus is often praised for its scenic beauty and close-knit environment, students also mention a need for improvement in food quality and campus security . Many extracurricular activities and clubs help foster a sense of community, although some students find social life lacking compared to larger universities. Overall, Mary Baldwin is seen as a good fit for those seeking a smaller, personalized educational experience, but students are warned to carefully evaluate whether it meets their specific needs, particularly regarding campus amenities and academic rigor.

reviews.text: Overall, I'm just happy to be graduating from MBU this year. The school's higher-ups have been fluctuating constantly, not to mention the drama that was the school's president removing 17 minors, most of which were related to topics like diversity and professional skills. I like the people I have met through the school, and I love Staunton as a town, but I'm ready to graduate and move on from my chapter at MBU.
The professors, especially in my major's department, were generally wonderful. I wish there had been more research opportunities and things to do on campus.
My overall experience with MBU so far is that it is a great school that has a lot of experience that you can go too and community.
I love the sense of community and access to professors. The campus is easy to navigate and parking isn’t an issue as a commuter student. My only wish is a coffee shop for gaps between classes!

My experience at Mary Baldwin University through the Virginia Women’s Institute for Leadership (VWIL) has been extremely positive. VWIL is preparing me well for a future in the Army by building leadership skills, discipline, and confidence. I also take classes at the Virginia Military Institute (VMI), which adds to my academic and military training. The professors at Mary Baldwin are supportive, approachable, and very involved with my major. I’ve built a strong social life here and feel part of a close-knit community. Overall, VWIL has helped me grow academically, socially, and as a future military leader.

Mary Baldwin is situated in the pleasant town of Staunton. Rolling hills, clear sky’s, it’s a beautiful walk to and from class. Friendly faces, and helpful hands, one word that springs to mind is community.
N/A at the moment. Strictly online student at this time. Maybe more professor hours for online help.
I like that Mary Baldwin University provides you with all the tools to succeed. And they they genuinely care for their students.

I liked how small and welcoming the community was at Mary Baldwin; it was easy to connect with professors and classmates. I also appreciated the support for first-generation and minority students. One thing I would like to see change is the modernization of the residence halls and campus facilities to make them more modern and comfortable.

Mary Baldwin has so much potential however, it is so difficult to get ahold of staff. I have had so many issues with housing,food,and our finances that I will try to address it and nothing is fixed or even talked about. I have had no heat or ac, raw chicken more times I could count, black mold, money being said was owed when really they told me it was a mistake and wasn’t after I had to come out with 4,000 dollars cash by myself before the end of the month to apply for classes again. And they help students that don’t care to be there more then the students that actually do.
From the moment the enrollment coach reached out to me, I felt like MBU was where I was supposed to be!
While I am transferring in the fall. They have been staying in touch with me and answering all the questions I have and promptly.

So far my experience at Mary Baldwin has been good. While I don't take part in much in the way of student activities (I'm a strictly online student) I do keep up with emails and events that highlight opportunities from students. I have had positive interactions with professors and other professionals as well.
I am a student athlete and love it here. My professors are amazing. However, the food and housing could be improved.
Absolutely amazing the events put together for students will never have you bored. And with the local town area there are so many areas to eat and shop or even just walk.

ai_summary: Marymount University receives mixed reviews from students highlighting both strengths and challenges. Many appreciate the intimate campus atmosphere , where small class sizes foster close relationships with professors and peers, allowing for personalized support and engagement. The university's location near Washington, D.C. is frequently praised for offering numerous internship opportunities and diverse cultural experiences . However, concerns are raised about student services, particularly financial aid and housing management, which some describe as disorganized and unresponsive . Dining options and campus facilities also face criticism, with reports of inadequate food quality and maintenance issues. While some students enjoy active participation in extracurricular activities, others feel that the overall student life is lacking and dominated by athletes. Overall, Marymount is seen as a supportive environment for academic growth, but there are clear areas for improvement, especially in student support services and campus life.

reviews.text: My overall experience at Marymount University has been pretty good. On campus we just got a new lounge/ coffee spot called Lola’s where it is a good place to hang out with friends and studying for classes. I like how diverse and how nice everyone is on campus including the professors! Our athletic program at Marymount composes of most of our school and all sports are good, so if you like a sport this is the right fit for you. Nevertheless, this has been a great experience for me but, I feel like our shuttle system could be better, on weekends it should run to the Ballston- MU train station instead of the Ballston campus. Other than that Marymount University is a good school to join for anyone who is looking for a home away from home.

It's a nurturing learning environment with excellent professors. The campus is charming. Student body is diverse and down to earth. I'm a picky eater so sometimes I like the selection food, sometimes I don't. Dorms are OK-my friends at other universities aren't thrilled with their dorms either. Ballston has so many good restaurants. Lots to do in the area. Love going into DC on the weekend. Highly recommend!!

My experience in the nursing program at Marymount University was deeply disappointing and, at times, genuinely distressing. What should have been a supportive, structured environment designed to prepare future nurses instead felt chaotic, dismissive, and unnecessarily adversarial. Rather than being mentored, I often felt scrutinized. Instead of encouragement, there was condescension. The culture felt rooted in intimidation rather than education. Clinical experiences, which should be formative and empowering, were frequently overshadowed by anxiety about being belittled or publicly criticized. Feedback often felt less like constructive coaching and more like humiliation disguised as “rigor.” Administrative support was equally discouraging. Instead of feeling advocated for, I felt dismissed. I cannot recommend this program to prospective students who value mentorship, transparent communication, and a genuinely supportive learning environment.

There are some truly incredible, caring and engaging professors who will go above and beyond. They make you excited to attend class. Also, small classes make it easier to learn and participate. The student body is friendly and diverse. Great location with frequent shuttle service to Ballston. Pretty and charming campus. Food varies. Lots of planned activities and events. Love that they hosted the Buddhist monks who did the Walk for Peace. It was wonderful to hear them speak. Michelle Obama visited too, but I missed it. Lots of internship opportunities. (I posted some of this a few months ago but could not update my review so I deleted and reposted with new information).
It was okay. I didn’t have any friends and not a lot of people tried to engage with me. No parties or big events. Food is okay but not great - it’s very repetitive.

They have been great at helping me go through their application process and have been very helpful, And with their support I’ve greatly reduced the cost that will be needed for my degree in engineering.

Attending Marymount University has been a valuable experience, though I’ve faced significant financial challenges in affording my education. Balancing my studies and track commitments while managing expenses has been difficult, but I remain dedicated to both my academic and athletic goals. The campus diversity is decent, offering a range of perspectives, though there is room for improvement. Despite these struggles, I am determined to succeed and contribute positively to the university community. Receiving this scholarship would greatly ease my financial burden and allow me to focus more fully on my growth at Marymount.

Marymount university in Arlington, Virginia is the perfect mix of small school in a big city. I love being able to have access to Ballston and Washington, D.C. the professors here are also really good at connecting with students and having an understanding of our busy lives. They offer help and good office hours and are always available for you. The student life here is good as well. We have an active campus with events happening almost everyday.

So far, I've been loving the Marymount experience. The school, professors, and environment are very welcoming, and people are approachable. My favorite thing so far is the professors; they're really kind and easy to talk to. One thing i would like to see change is that some classes are offered during certain semsters which can be annoying waiting until they open next sesmter. For example, some classes are offered in the fall, notthe spring, vice versa

I have had a great experience overall here at Marymount. I am very involved in my major, and I play a sport too. I've not had a bad experience with any professor so far and find they are all supportive and helpful. I really like how small the campus is, it takes no more than 5 minutes to get to class.
Marymount is a diverse college with a background in the Catholic faith. The students and faculty are super friendly and honest.

My experience at Marymount University has been wonderful. The campus community is welcoming and supportive, and the professors truly care about student success. I’ve appreciated the small class sizes, which make it easy to connect with faculty and classmates, and the university’s location near Washington, D.C., provides so many opportunities for internships and real-world learning.

If you have a disability this is not the school for you. They are completely unequipped to handle academic or physical disabilities. Housing dept is terrible, lies, doesn’t answer emails, and cannot tell you size of housing. I met with SAS ahead of time was told all that I asked for private room with bathroom would be provided along with most of my academic accommodations. Indeed not a single item was actually provided. Communication in general is terrible not only between students and college but the college and professors. There is definitely a mild problem in the dorms and possibly the dining hall. Don’t waste your money.

This school is horrible, DO NOT GO HERE!!
As someone who has taken full advantage of the full small school scene, it gives you absolutely no benefits and does not protect student safety.This school is over priced, will try to rob you, and tries to sham
you into coming! They have robbed my friend who is an RA of her merit scholarship and tries to run an oligarchy here via the president. The only thing that brings students in is our sports culture, but most of them quite after their freshman year anyways. Please do not invest in your education here, you can find better opportunities other places.

I loved living on campus and being so close to Washingtion DC. That is honestly the best part of going here is just being so close to the city. The campus is really small and our classes are very intimate due to that. I enjoy that because it helps me focus. There is not a huge campus life but rather it is just a relaxing place to get work done!

ai_summary: Maryville University of St. Louis offers a unique experience characterized by a welcoming atmosphere , small class sizes , and a strong emphasis on student support . Many students appreciate the inclusive environment and the accessibility of professors, making it easy to receive personalized attention in their academic pursuits. The direct entry programs, particularly in health fields like nursing and speech-language therapy, stand out for their well-structured curricula and dedicated faculty. While the online learning platform is noted as user-friendly and engaging, some students express concerns about the tuition costs and the availability of campus activities, particularly for commuters. Campus life can feel subdued, with a desire for more social events, but the overall community spirit is strong. Many reviews highlight the supportive nature of advisors and faculty , though experiences can vary within different programs.

reviews.text: It was good although i would prefer more nightlife as a young adult. Great school if you just want a college education.

My overall experience with Maryville University of St. Louis has been very positive and encouraging. From the beginning, I have been impressed by the welcoming atmosphere and the clear commitment to student success. The admissions process has been smooth, and the staff have been helpful and responsive whenever I have had questions. That support has made the transition to college feel less overwhelming.
What stands out most to me is Maryville’s focus on career preparation and hands on learning. The strong emphasis on real world experience, especially in technology and cybersecurity, aligns closely with my career goals. I appreciate that the university prioritizes small class sizes and personal connections with professors, which creates an environment where students are not just numbers but individuals.
Overall, my experience so far has confirmed that Maryville is a place where I can grow academically, professionally, and personally while working toward my long term goals

Maryville is overall I nice school, but they definitely need help when it comes to advisors as they are fairly useless. Maryville also needs to work on their class options as there were a few I wanted to take that look like they are available classes, only to find out that class no longer exists. Even with the few things they need to fix I have had more good professors than bad.

I love being at Maryville University! The campus life is really amazing and so are the people there too! Whether it’s faculty, students, and professors, all are always willing to help in any way they can!

So far it has been great being at Maryville University. Although it is a small campus, it is still very pleasant. Professors tend to be good, and campus life and parties are not great. It is a small school where a lot of people live off campus. Food and apartments its also something they have to improve

It is a University on the smaller side. Even though they advertise their diversity they do not do much to encourage it and protect it. They have many student orgs but don’t advertise and encourage students to immerse themselves in the different cultures. However they do have some activities such as during welcome week that are good and immersive. There is some farmer’s market style activities they have sometimes that are very nice and some of them are of vendors from different cultures that represent the student body. Most professor are dry welcoming and helpful specially those for the stem programs. There is manny research opportunities alongside the proffers and they encourage you when you have a specific research interest. The dinning hall food is not the best out there but it is good and they have a lot of baked goods on the daily.

A school for business that hates teaching anything IT or cybersecurity related (besides paperwork). Many of the ISYS (information systems) classes are just business classes in disguise. There are cybersecurity seniors who don't know the difference between a router and switch (which is like a plumber not knowing the difference between a toilet and a sink) because the school used the computer networking course to teach them cable management and color-coding.
TLDR: Might be a good school for things like nursing, business, and MIS, but definitely look somewhere else for cybersecurity and computer science.

I love the atmosphere Maryville offers, people here are so friendly and easy to get along with. I would like to see higher quality in food here since there have been multiple occasions where I have gotten food poisoning but besides that I think this school is very clean when it comes to their bathrooms, classrooms and overall buildings.

I have had a great experience so far. Emails are answered quickly when I have questions, and when I call to talk to someone, they are all kind and helpful and very explanatory. The university sends reminder messages about assignments, checks in with students on how we're doing, both in school and in our personal lives.
I have been very impressed with the level of professionalism and helpfulness of the admissions staff.

Maryville’s online CSD bachelor’s program offers flexible coursework with supportive faculty who genuinely want students to succeed. The program builds a strong foundation for grad school while emphasizing cultural awareness, communication skills, and real-world application. Some students note it moves quickly and requires self-motivation, but the flexibility and quality instruction make it a great option for those needing an online degree.
Overall I love the campus atmosphere , faculty, location, program, recreation center, library , track field and others

I've only just started, but I'm happy with how they work with people returning to school. I have an established life so I was nervous about continuing school and having that be completely upended. So far, it seems to be going well and I feel catered to with my new career path.

My overall experience with Maryville University of St. Louis has been very positive. From the start, the admissions team was supportive and responsive, making the enrollment process smooth and clear. I appreciate the university’s commitment to offering practical, hands-on learning opportunities, especially in the Sport Business Management program, which aligns well with my career goals.What I like most is the strong sense of community and the resources available to students, including dedicated academic advisors and extracurricular opportunities that help students grow both personally and professionally.
I like how the campus is spread out. I can easily get to one place in a few minutes. The class sizes are good. The professors have a well known program that they can follow and teach the courses.

ai_summary: At the Massachusetts Institute of Technology, students generally describe their academic experience as both rigorous and rewarding , with a strong emphasis on collaboration and innovative problem-solving. Many appreciate the passionate and supportive faculty who genuinely care about student success, even amid the challenges of intense coursework. The campus life is vibrant, with numerous clubs, activities, and a close-knit community that fosters friendships and teamwork. However, some students mention that the academic workload can be overwhelming and that managing time effectively is crucial for maintaining balance and mental health. Additionally, while the dining options and campus facilities could use improvement, MIT's strong financial aid and the diversity of its student body stand out positively. Overall, the unique combination of bright, motivated peers and a culture that values both learning and creativity makes MIT a distinctive environment.

reviews.text: It is so good I like how it was so safe the food was pretty good the housing was good and the kids were nice.

I love MIT. It is extremely challenging but extremely rewarding. The students at MIT work hard and play hard, and everyone is so passionate and interesting. The IHTFP dichotomy is a great descriptor of the MIT experience.

Honestly all round horrible college experience. Genuinely the worst 5 years of my life. I graduated with an engineering degree and still have NO JOB AFTER 3 YEARS. I wish I committed to Harvard instead. Also the food here is disgusting and overpriced. Do not recommend.

Massachusetts Institute of Technology is a wonderful place to be. It is the crème de la crème of universities with amazing academics, friendly professors, and a wonderful community. I don't think there is any place like this university. As big as it is, it feels as if it is small enough for every student to be known, cared for, and respected. I love this university, with its amazing research opportunities and a vibrant atmosphere where everyone feels seen and heard.

MIT offers an incredible learning environment filled with innovation, collaboration, and hands-on opportunities. I appreciated the diverse community, access to world-class professors, and the culture of curiosity that encourages students to explore beyond their comfort zones. The campus resources—from labs to student organizations—make it easy to connect academics with real-world problem-solving.
What I’d like to see change:
While the fast-paced environment is motivating, it can also be overwhelming. I’d love to see more focus on mental health support, balance, and resources that help students manage the intensity without burning out. More opportunities for peer mentorship and wellness programs could make the experience even better.

I’ve thoroughly enjoyed my experience at MIT! I’ve been impressed with the overall academic rigor and breadth of classes. I’m so pleased, I’m looking at adding another major because I want to take more classes here.
I liked the overall vibe and how everyone there was unique by themselves. I felt like I really fit in there.

Each class pushed the boundaries of my understanding, from complex algorithms to cutting-edge physics concepts. But it was the UROP program that truly transformed my experience. Working alongside brilliant professors and grad students, I found myself contributing to groundbreaking research as an undergraduate. One semester, I helped develop a new machine learning model for climate prediction. The hands-on experience was invaluable, teaching me not just technical skills but also how to think like a researcher. MIT's blend of intense academics and real-world application through UROP prepared me for challenges I never thought I'd be ready to tackle.

The Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) is widely regarded as one of the premier institutions for science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) in the world. Located in Cambridge, Massachusetts, MIT has built a reputation for its rigorous academic programs, cutting-edge research, and innovative culture.

MIT offers an unparalleled academic experience with world-class faculty, cutting-edge research, and a collaborative culture. The opportunities for innovation, networking, and career advancement are exceptional. I particularly appreciated the supportive community and access to state-of-the-art facilities. However, the rigorous workload can be overwhelming, and balancing academics with personal life is challenging. Financial aid is generous but could be more inclusive for middle-income families. Overall, MIT fosters an environment for driven individuals to excel and make an impact. It’s ideal for those passionate about solving real-world problems, but prospective students should be prepared for its intensity and high expectations.
It is one the top ten colleges in the world and I am happy to be here. I grateful for this college and there are million kind words to describe this college.
Rigorous yet rewarding educational experience, especially in the fields of robotics and mechanical engineering.
Excellent academics, but as the students say, it really is "drinking from a firehose." There are some truly amazing people at MIT, and Cambridge has gotten better over the years.

As a student at MIT, I can confidently say that the experience is both challenging and rewarding. The academic rigor here is unmatched; classes are fast-paced and intellectually stimulating, pushing you to think critically and creatively. The professors are not only experts in their fields but also genuinely invested in their students’ success.
Collaboration is a core aspect of MIT culture, and I love working alongside brilliant peers who inspire me to think outside the box. The resources available—from cutting-edge labs to research opportunities—are incredible.
Outside the classroom, the vibrant campus life offers a variety of clubs, events, and hackathons, ensuring there’s never a dull moment. While the workload can be intense, the support system among students and faculty is phenomenal. Overall, MIT is a place where passion meets innovation, making it an unforgettable journey.
I really like the academic rigor and prestige that comes with attending MIT. There are so many amazing people that I have met, although the food scene could use some improvement.

ai_summary: Mercer University offers a blend of strong academics and a close-knit community that many students appreciate. Reviews commonly highlight the personalized attention provided by professors due to small class sizes, fostering supportive learning environments. Most students feel welcomed on campus, noting the friendly atmosphere and opportunities for involvement through various clubs and activities. While the campus and its facilities receive praise for their beauty, students often express concerns over the high cost of tuition and limited dining options, as well as a desire for improved housing quality . Some reviewers pointed out that student life can feel somewhat limited, especially if you’re not involved in sports or Greek life. Overall, Mercer is viewed as an institution where students can obtain a solid education, though experiences regarding diversity and campus culture vary significantly.

reviews.text: I love the school's small campus. The smaller size makes commuting to classes and the cafeteria much faster and easier. You also see a lot of the same people here, which builds a sense of community on campus. There are lots of campus activities as well to keep you busy.

A great school for undergrad music degrees! There are generous internal scholarships from the university itself, and then music majors/minors are eligible for scholarships via the audition process. I and my peers had trouble with dorms. Mold is a big issue in specific buildings, to the point where my friend was diagnosed with mold poisoning in one dorm, and the year prior I fell extremely ill due to the failure of the wall dehumidifiers in a completely different dorm. The basement of Shorter and specific Garden Apartment buildings are notorious for this issue, so be prepared and buy a small dehumidifier if you are assigned to those buildings. Also be VERY careful inspecting upon move-in. I never had any issues with being charged for pre-existing issues, but you want to identify any issues as soon as possible because maintenance can take forever. Document and report leaks, mold, and other potentially long-term issues ASAP. They will victim blame you for mold, so DOCUMENT.

Mercer's class size is perfect for learning. You are able to get to know your professors and classmates easily. Professors are there to help students learn and they are very open to receive students during office hours. The campus is a great size - you can cross campus in about 15 minutes, making it easy to arrive on time. Their reputation goes far as well. As an alumni, people often remark on how great a university Mercer is, and they are impressed by someone earning a degree from Mercer. Going to Mercer was also a great opportunity for me to meet people from different backgrounds than mine. The student diversity makes it a great place to expand your "bubble."
Since graduating, the school has been many efforts in upgrading the campus. They've modernized it to keep up with competing schools, and the campus now has a more "student life" vibe. I know they have made efforts to continue to revitalize areas of Macon, however I would love to see that continue to grow.
Best college in Georgia! Best professors and academics is very great. Nationally recognized medical program
I have gone to mercer for over a year now and coming from a school big school in miami, mercer was the breath of fresh air i needed. It is a great school with good people and a healthy enviornment.

My experience at Mercer University has been both challenging and rewarding. The curriculum pushed me to think critically, sharpen my communication skills, and apply what I learned to real-world situations. Mercer provided an environment that valued growth and persistence, which helped me strengthen my work ethic and confidence. Through coursework, collaboration, and consistent support from faculty, I was able to develop skills that directly align with my career goals. Mercer University did more than educate me it helped me level up academically and personally, preparing me to make a meaningful impact beyond the classroom.

Mercer University is a school that feels personal, supportive, and student-focused while offering resources that you would receive at a larger institution. The campus has been voted as the one of the most beautiful campuses in the Unites States. It's walkable with a classic brick style Southern feel. It has strong academic programs where professions actually know their students. Student life is active and the Division 1 athletic programs bring a great energy to the campus.
One thing I would like to see change is more variety in food selection. As a student-athlete, it is important for me to fuel my body with clean, healthy foods so that I can complete at the highest level.

My overall experience at Mercer has been everything I could have asked for. Living in GA most of my life, I had never really heard of Mercer but it really is a hidden gem! I have had a great experience academically. The smaller class sizes makes it feel more personal and you really get to know your professors. The campus its self is beautiful and Macon has a lot to offer. I played on one of Mercers athletic teams and it has been a great experience. It has definitely brought me closer to many other athletes. Mercer also provides support to allow for us to succeed on and off the field.

Mercer is a very great university. Mercer probably is the best in Georgia. Its has a excellent medical program which is nationally recognized. The students are very unique, friendly, and hardworking. The professors and faculty are very friendly and helpful.

At Mercer it's all about networking, reaching our max potential, and gaining experience as is any other campus. What I love about Mercer is how into their students lives they are. Even after their students graduate they are always talking about their time there and it makes me proud to be a bear and know there are adults who used to be in my shoes. The campus isn't too bad and it's walkable and very nature like and that is something I also love. What i would like to see changed is the smell. Sometimes our sewage smell covers the campus and it stinks horribly.
The people are nice and welcoming and the professors are all very helpful. The campus is also very beautiful.

I have been affilated with Mercer since 1985. My parent worked here at the Univeristy and forty years later I joined in the same department. I am currently working towards my bachelor's degree by attending online classes. My interactions with departments as a student has been as professional and successful as my interactions with them as a collegue.

Mercer has been a great academic experience. I love the campus and professors and they are very helpful to undergrad students. The area around Mercer could be better and i wish there was a way to get to off campus activities more easily without a car.

During my time at Mercer University, I formed several core memories that I will cherish for the rest of my life. I have made friendships that felt a million times stronger than any friendships I have ever formed before. With the level of diversity the school carries, I learned to hear out and embrace new perspectives and outlooks on life. This overall has rounded my character, as I got to meet people I would have never met otherwise if I remained in the same small community I grew up in. Besides my social life, academics have never been more accessible. There are countless resources to utilize at Mercer that help me academically. From the helpful online services the school provides to study, to the professors who go out of their way outside of office hours to assist any student, there is never a student left helpless. Alongside all of this, safety is the school's top priority, comforting students who feel uncertain about the new chapter in their lives.

As opposed to much larger schools, I enjoyed Mercer's small knit community! It fostered connections between students and faculty, as well as highlight individualized teaching. I formed friendships with my professors that were helpful through academic concerns and future plans.

ai_summary: Miami University receives mixed reviews focused on its academic rigor , engaging professors , and vibrant campus life . Many students appreciate the supportive faculty and a variety of extracurricular activities, contributing to a strong sense of community on campus. The Miami Plan, which allows for diverse course selections , is frequently mentioned as beneficial for academic success. However, there are concerns about the lack of diversity, struggles with the course registration process, and a predominance of Greek life that some find overwhelming. While the beautiful campus and safe environment receive high praise, students express a desire for improved dining options and more resources for independent research. Overall, Miami University stands out for its academic opportunities and active student life, but students note areas for potential improvement in inclusivity and student services.

reviews.text: I chose Miami because I loved the campus and was able to build a relationship with education professors before I even got on campus. My classes and social life were both wonderful and felt easy to navigate as a freshman. It's definitely a lot of white people and can feel like everything is overshadowed by the business school, but if you actively work to find your community, it's there!
I had an unbelievable experience at Miam - from club sports to my fraternity to making lifelong friends. Uptown Oxford is a great college town and the campus is incredible.
I loved my time at Miami. I still keep in close contact with many of my friends, teammates, and roommates.

Miami was one of those colleges that I kind of just heard of on a whim, but ever since I did funnily enough I started hearing more and more about it from people that knew about it and one thing I found really special was the fact that NOBODY had anything bad to say about it. Once I was here the campus was beautiful, the people were nice (even if it is a majority white school, which is my only real complaint), and I was really happy with the dorms and other accommodations. My favorite thing about being here is the professors and staff. They really do care about our success here and I am NOT being taught by ANY TAs (Miami always has professors teaching). One of my biggest fears going into college was being faced with 200+ student lecture halls with inaccessible professors and I haven't faced any of that. i am also receiving extensive advising support with being undeclared, and there are SO many resources on campus for students to use. Overall such a good experience so far, choose Miami!
I absolutely love Miami university. The campus is stunning all year round and it is very walkable. I loved my professors and classes and felt very included in everything that I did!
Overall its really a great school. There are a lot of positives at this school, especially on the academic side and with the professors.

I like the open walkable campus. I also enjoy the wide variety of clubs and activities on-campus. I also enjoy the variety of food options that are available by meal swipe. If there was anything I would change it would be to change the hours of one of the dining halls as it currently isn’t the same as the others.
I've had a great time at Miami so far, the people are very nice, and it is one of the most beautiful campuses I've ever seen.

Miami University has a gorgeous walkable campus. My four years in Oxford were fun and challenging. The professors were great. I would encourage students to join a couple of clubs and get to know their professors. There are so many activities to choose from. The nightlife is vibrant but if that is not your scene there are tons of "non partying" things to do. I had friends who loved to party but others who chose other activities. The school is big enough that there is space to find your place.

I am a current student at Miami, and I was slightly nervous about attending. I remember coming on a tour, and everyone absolutely LOVED this school. At Miami, I feel like I am in my own little bubble in my college sphere. There is always something going on, and all the people so far are really sweet. I did not know if Miami was for me because as much as I like to go out and do things with friends, I love my quiet and peaceful time, BUT it honestly is great. I love this school and the small-town vibe but still decent-sized school. Sometimes the food is repetitive, but you learn to deal with it. Lots of kids take their schooling seriously and you always will find nice study spots across campus.

I would like to see more things to do. The party life is fun, but it gets old very quickly. Very thankful there are activities to do in the campus other than partying, but it is very scarce. Excellent professors and education though. Would 100% come here for the education and environment.

My experience at Miami University has over all been very successful. I have been acclimating to my classes very well. I have discovered that I love learning a wide range of subjects. In High School, what I learned was determined by the schools curriculum, but in college I get the opportunity to choose my classes. I love that all my teachers are very passionate about what they teach, it makes me eager to learn more. I love that in my classes, because I specifically chose them, it makes my learning experience more successful. The campus is beautiful with the nature aspect, the brick roads and buildings, and the delicate interior design. I love how I feel safe at the campus and never have to worry about putting my self in danger. If I had to change something about Miami, it would be giving students the opportunity to join Greek Life in the Fall instead of Spring.All in all, I really am happy with my choice of attending Miami University, but I am more grateful that Miami chose me.

If you are into partying, this is the school for you. If you are not into partying, however, you will struggle to branch out. Many social aspects of this school rely heavily on the partying scene. This being said, however, the natural environment around campus is phenomenal and the Outdoor Pursuit Center is an underrated gem. For all its faults, Miami does have a gorgeous campus.

I love the atmosphere and I feel very supported here. The university works very hard to make sure that the transition into college is comfortable, and that there are many programs that help students to make the most of their college experience.

I loved my four years at Miami. My suggestion to incoming freshmen is to join a club or find some type of extracurricular activity. It will help you make new friends and you will find a niche within the 16,000 students. Go Redhawks!

ai_summary: Michigan State University receives a mix of feedback, with many students appreciating the supportive and welcoming environment on campus. The diverse student body and numerous opportunities for involvement in clubs, sports, and activities are frequently highlighted, creating a vibrant social atmosphere .
