Academically, most students find the courses challenging yet rewarding, with dedicated professors who genuinely care about their students' success. However, there are concerns about tuition costs and the quality of some dorms, alongside issues with parking and navigating the large campus. The campus itself is often described as beautiful and full of green spaces , contributing to a positive overall experience. Some reviews also mention difficulties in obtaining meaningful academic support and a desire for improved communication regarding campus resources and events.

reviews.text: Because I am an out-of-state student, making friends is a challenge. However, I have found that most other students here are extremely friendly and more than happy to interact with others. There are so many clubs here that it would be almost hard not to find one that aligns with a value of interest of yours. Also, the dining hall food is delicious!

Attending Michigan State University has been an incredible experience. From the welcoming campus environment to the supportive faculty, I’ve felt encouraged to grow both academically and personally. The resources here, like the libraries, research facilities, and student organizations, have allowed me to explore my interests and challenge myself. Living at University Village has given me a strong sense of community while balancing academics and social life. Overall, MSU has offered me more than just an education—it’s a place that fosters curiosity, leadership, and personal development, and I’m grateful to be part of it.

Prior to arriving to MSU, I was a very closed off person and if there were events I wasn’t the person who would go out of my way to attend these events. Now that I’ve been at MSU do a semester and a half I’ve come to realize I enjoy these events and have been able to meet new people and make some deep connections. I came to MSU not expecting much but this campus and overall student life has made the college experience really enjoyable.

My experience with Michigan State University has been unorthodox, but through this I have found a ton of great resources for studying abroad, undergraduate research alongside professors, and opportunities beyond graduation for careers, and I am quite happy with what they offer. I wish more funding was put towards natural science research and just in general to attract more students, but overall my professors have been outstanding.

I have had a great experience so far. I met some really nice people who are within my field due to my involvement in Lyman Briggs College. All of my professors seem truly invested within the content of their teaching and so I haven't had a negative experience in terms of grades. I also love the campus and campus life. The architecture of the bell tower, north neighborhood, or even the more modern buildings truly give it a college feel and it makes it all the much more enjoyable. I really like out food combos too. I know I have compared with people from other schools who don't get as many options as we do and so I feel truly thankful for the way ours works. I am personally someone who loves coffee and so having the option to get it as one of my combos is definitely a plus. I can't say I'll every go hungry during my stay here. Another really fun part of this school is the athletics and the team spirit. There truly is nothing more exciting than sitting student section with friends.

I really like Michigan state. Professors want you to graduate and they start you in labs using the expensive equipment your first year. Food is decent, dorms are decent, sometimes the gym is a little too full.

Michigan State University has been a great experience for me so far. I really enjoy the campus and the overall atmosphere, and the food options are very good compared to other schools. The academics are strong, and while some professors are tougher than others, I feel like I am still learning a lot. Overall, MSU offers a good balance of academics, campus life, and opportunities, and I genuinely love being a student here.

What I like about Michigan State University is how easy it is to meet new people and make friends. My first year here has been fun, the classes here are very manageable, east lansing is also a very fun college town.

Being here at Michigan State University is one of the best things to ever happen to me. However, there is some minor things that need to be changed. This biggest one I would say is the freshman welcome week events, at this we went with people from out major and did various activities. This is a good idea in theory, but the groups were too large and constantly changing. So, even after a week of walking to a bunch of people I was unable to become friends with anyone. I think this also impacts a lot of the isolation kids feel here on campus because we have all these clubs, but no one wants to show up all alone to something new.
Overall great experience coming as an out of state student it’s a whole new world, along with the many new people I met and the new things I’ve done here, it’s a great school.

I have greatly enjoyed my time as an MSU student, so far. Through this experience, I was able to grow into adulthood, learn new things about myself, and make several new friends, all things I struggled with before. It has not come without challenges, but I have been able to work through them and overcome the obstacles that have come my way.

I love MSU, I'm getting a great education in multiple different areas as I am double majoring and double minoring. I have met some life long friends here and we can hangout in multiple different areas on campus along with having a good night life scene for when we want to go out and have fun. I am learning another language here and MSU has great resources for me to learn.

MICHIGAN STATE University has given me many opportunities to grow as a person. The academics have challenged me to push beyond what is comfortable to grow as a student and a person. The professors are always willing to help which I am very grateful for. Overall I have really enjoyed my time at MSU.
It was pretty horrid and awful. Probably the worst 4years of my life and if I could take it back and go somewhere else I would.

Michigan State University is an amazing college and I feel lucky to have had the opportunity to attend. The student life is great, but MSU as an institution has disappointing communication skills. The professors are very nice and attentive for the most part, and my classes have been enjoyable.

ai_summary: Michigan Technological University offers a vibrant community and a rigorous academic environment , particularly strong in engineering and STEM fields. Many students appreciate the supportive professors who are often approachable and willing to help, although experiences with faculty can be inconsistent. The campus life is lively, with numerous extracurricular activities, outdoor adventures, and events, making it a great fit for those who enjoy an active, outdoorsy lifestyle. The university is also notable for its beautiful surroundings in northern Michigan, but students frequently mention challenges like the harsh winter weather and the quality of dining services, which could use improvement. Moreover, some reviews highlight concerns regarding campus diversity and parking issues. Overall, while MTU is celebrated for its academic rigor, the campus atmosphere, and the sense of community, students should be prepared for its unique challenges .

reviews.text:
Michigan Tech is definitely an odd school, but shines in most of the right ways. My time here has had its ups and downs, but I've overall enjoyed myself and I'm happy to be here.
The sore spots are definitely evident at times. The food situation is "improving" but still awful--supposedly has been for years and continues to be even with a new catering company. The location is super pretty, but extremely remote with a severe lack of in-town jobs, and off-campus housing is varying degrees of old, worn, competitive, and/or slumlord-infested. Tech is also notably expensive without grants, especially for out-of-state.
But the student life is vibrant and accepting, and you'll make some long-term friends with like-minded people. It feels like there's something (clubs, etc.) for everyone here, too. Professors, while varying in quality, tend to care about you and teach classes well. The campus is small (i.e. everything is only a few minutes away at most), walkable, and packed full of history.

Michigan Tech is a challenging school, but gives good education and rewarding opportunities to hard workers. There are tons of clubs, sports, intramurals, and outdoor activities to enjoy. The school is also expensive, but if you have good work ethic, scholarships, and aid then you can do it.

Michigan Tech provided me with an overall excellent undergraduate experience. I studied under excellent professors in the Biological Sciences department who were truly invested in their students' preparation for their future careers. I am excited to see how STEM subjects besides engineering continue to flourish at this school!

Tech is a great place to study. It is a bit difficult when you are not one of their engineering students (or other big majors) because there are not as many resources. Overall it’s been a great experience with great faculty!

Michigan Technological University has been a ton of fun; the local area is absolutely beautiful. If one goes out to find things to do there is so many different things that they can do, from hiking, fishing, skiing, hunting, sledding, swimming, camping, and many more. The students that you meet here at MTU are all very nice, and eager to do new things. I really have enjoyed my time here.

My overall experience at Michigan Technological University has been very positive. The university provides a strong academic foundation, particularly in STEM fields, with rigorous coursework and a strong emphasis on hands-on learning and research. I valued the access to well-equipped laboratories, small class sizes, and the opportunity to work closely with knowledgeable and supportive faculty.
The campus community is welcoming, and the focused, quiet environment is excellent for studying and personal growth. Michigan Tech also encourages innovation, problem-solving, and independence, which helped prepare me for research and professional opportunities.
One area for improvement would be increased support during the long winter months, especially mental health resources and more indoor social activities. Expanding career networking opportunities, industry partnerships, and internship pipelines would also further enhance the student experience.

I think I have had a great time at michigan tech so far and have really enjoyed the academics and the community that I have found up here in the UP. The surrounding area is super beautiful and I overall just really enjoy it up here.
There have been ups and downs. The food is moldy and the tuition is expensive without much financial aid to help students that come from poorer families. Overall its been pretty mediocre.
I really enjoyed my time at Michigan Tech. It is a very rigorous institution that prepares you greatly for whatever comes next
The College Michigan Technological University is a beautiful college that makes you feel right at home.

If you like nature and the outdoors this is the perfect college and there are plenty of places with in driving distance to hike. We do get a ton of snow close to the most in the US. This is not where to go either if you want to be near cities or urban areas because almost none are within driving distance.

Michigan Tech has been very good at getting students involved and letting them enjoy there college time. While also having a good social life the teaching has been very interesting and engaging. There are so many opportunities and learning centers to help as many students as possible and professors actually want to help their students succeed.

I like that it’s a smaller college. It makes my classes much easier, and I’m able to actually connect with my professors. Since the college is in the middle of nowhere, it can be hard to find stuff to do off campus.

I thoroughly enjoy the area around the school, the nature and outside opportunities are extensive at MTU. The social life is good if you try to socialize, and there is most likely a group of people that anybody could fall into between clubs and other organizations. Reputable academics make the school a great choice.
Overall a great university experience thus far, and I am incredibly excited for the remaining years of my enrollment.

ai_summary: Middle Tennessee State University receives mixed feedback from students, but there are several common themes highlighted in the reviews. Many students appreciate the accessibility and helpfulness of faculty and staff , especially when it comes to academic advising and support services, which help make the transition to college smoother. The campus itself is noted for its beauty and spaciousness , along with a wide variety of majors and resources available to students, particularly in fields like aviation, science, and education. Student life is described as somewhat limited, particularly due to the high number of commuter students, which can make campus events feel less vibrant. While the university offers numerous opportunities for involvement, some students feel that the social scene and engagement during sports events, like football games, could be improved. Additionally, affordability is a consistent point of praise, although parking issues and the condition of some campus facilities have been mentioned as areas needing attention. Overall, MTSU is recognized for fostering a supportive community but is seen by some as lacking in vibrant campus life compared to other universities.

reviews.text: It is such a nice and it is taken wonderful care of. New upgrades every year within every major/minors area. Wonderful food options, good maintenance, beautiful walk between buildings. And the best library

My experience at Middle Tennessee State University has been great so far! Me and my roommate go to events on campus every now and then and they are all extremely well planned and fun! I enjoy the food options we have on campus. I use the recreation center almost every day for the gym, as I enjoy working out after classes. As for my classes, I love them! My major (Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology) has been extremely welcoming. I have made some great friends, and the professors are all amazing and extremely professional. They are always open to help and make class super fun, even if it is tough at times. I am not part of the party scene as much, but I have heard it is pretty fun. I have attended a few sporting events, and they have all been fun. I currently live in the dorms on campus, and they are okay. I have a great roommate, but I would enjoy more privacy and quiet time, which is why we are getting an on-campus apartment next year.

My experience at Middle Tennessee State University has been really meaningful overall. I’ve appreciated how supportive most of my professors are, especially in the psychology program — they genuinely want students to succeed and push us to think critically. I also like the variety of student organizations and research opportunities, which have helped me grow both academically and professionally.
If I could change anything, I’d love to see even more mental health resources and clearer communication about graduate school preparation. As someone preparing for the next step after undergrad, having more structured guidance on applications and interviews would be helpful. Overall though, MTSU has given me a strong foundation and a lot of opportunities to grow.

I transferred here from UTK and it was the best decision. I love going here. I have so many friends and I love my classes and I learn so much from everyone I meet. The part scene is great but it doesn't feel forced and people are generally social and fun. If youre studying music, go to school here. If you want to be in a band, go to school here. If you want to actually have fun but not be in a frat, go to school here.

Even though I haven’t had the chance to attend Middle Tennessee State University yet, everything I’ve learned about MTSU makes me confident it will be an amazing experience. From the welcoming campus community to the wide range of academic programs and student organizations, it seems like a place where I can truly grow both personally and professionally. I’ve heard great things about the supportive professors and the vibrant campus life in Murfreesboro. I’m especially excited about the opportunities for involvement, leadership, and hands-on learning. Even though I haven’t been there yet, I genuinely look forward to becoming part of the MTSU community.
Great class variety and professors. Academic advisors are very helpful. There are a lot of opportunities and resources offered.
I love the environment of this university. You can meet friendly faces around every corner. The food here is absolutely delicious.
Its a ok school the only college ive been to so i cant compare i have a good amount of friends there and the teachers are ok.

I love MTSU. It is a great school in a good community. Everyone there is so nice, and the professors care about your success rather than just dismissing you. The campus is very clean, and I feel very safe when I am there. The only thing that I would like to see changed is the parking on campus.

I really like the community. Everyone is in their own world. No one is paying attention to you if you don’t dress up one day, or you have a bad hair day. College is usually like that obviously, but at MTSU, it feels nicer. Everyone is nice, you can tell based on the slight smiles they give even when they don’t know who you are. But it’s comforting. Minding their business while keeping their sympathy in. And the library is so calming. I love spending my afternoons there. They have so many options on where to be, at different talking levels.

MTSU is a good campus if you want to just study and have some extracurriculars. It is not a party school. there is a strict no alcohol on campus policy, as well as closing the on-campus food options early on Saturday and Sunday.

I have been to MTSU since I was born. My dad had went there for four years playing for football for four years. He also regularly visits the campus and watches the football game while I come with him. I am a Dual Enrollment Student at MTSU too and I did a volleyball camp there too. What I like about MTSU is the food, I remember from when I was there for a volleyball camp, we got to eat in the cafeteria. There was so many options like mac and cheese, pizza, spaghetti, cookies, cakes, ice cream, any fountain soda or sweet tea, etc. All of the food was really good and the staff were super nice. I have never met a rude staff while being on campus. What I would like to see change is probably transitioning from D2L to the Canvas LMS for better tech.

I was really excited about coming to MTSU because of their amazing construction management program. It has certainly lived up to the expectations. Unfortunately, I can’t say the same for the rest of the college. It is very boring, the on campus housing isn’t good, and athletics are not fun to go to because of low attendance and performance.

I like how there are so many organizations and ethnic group organizations just for them in case they feel left out. There are many international students, which is awesome and what I like most. I would want to see more staffs who helps students academically to understand students' needs and talk to them step by step.

My experience attending Middle Tennessee State University, has been a big eye opener for me as a student & as an Adult, because coming from an High School schedule with mom around, you have everything you need and she’s there to help when needed, it doesn’t give you the outlook on how the real world is until you go to college, because attending Middle Tennessee State University, I became more independent during my first semester, and tried not to rely on mom too much, but she always helps when I need her. In addition even though our campus life is good and no trouble happens often, it can become a little boring when there is nothing to do after you finish studying, but with making friends it makes the college experience more fun & something to look forward to. In Conclusion, I would like to see more campus nightlife events to happen during the weekend, because many of my friends tend to go home for the weekends, because nothing really happens on campus during the weekend.

ai_summary: Misericordia University is characterized by its small , tight-knit community that fosters personal relationships between students and professors, often allowing for individualized attention in academics. The university is particularly noted for its strong health science programs , including nursing, occupational therapy, and speech-language pathology, which have high-quality faculty and good reputations in their fields. Student life is described as active with various events, but some students feel the social scene is limited, especially on weekends. The campus is seen as beautiful and safe, although there are mentions of a somewhat lackluster party culture and dissatisfaction with dining options. Overall, while Misericordia provides a supportive academic experience, some students seek a more vibrant college town environment and greater diversity of extracurricular activities.

reviews.text: I have mixed reviews about Misericordia University. I have been here for 4 years. I graduated with my business degree and going to grad school now. Their organization is a little all over the place but they will get the job done at the end of the day. Their faculty are really amazing and they do wanna see you do well. I chose here because it was the closest to me and I have heard good reviews.
Misericordia has great medical programs. The professors care about the students. It is a community that wants everyone to thrive.

Misericordia University immediately felt like home from the welcoming enviornment with friendly faces and the beautiful scenery of campus and Dallas, Pa. I felt like I belonged and that I live up to my dreams attending the University. I have been given so many opportunities and experienced different things and it was all easier witht he help of great faculty.

Overall, I have had a positive experience at his University. I have made friendships that I will carry with me through life while taking my passion of sports into the classroom and onto the field. The community is so welcoming an I truly enjoy being here each and every day.

Misericordia is amazing, tight-nit community. From the advisors, to professors, dining staff and campus life, everyone is there to make the experience better. While I was there, a girl on my dance team was diagnosed with ovarian cancer. The school did such an amazing job at giving back to one of their student's. She passed away and they still are doing so much to celebrate who she was. That is the kind of school I am happy to have been apart of.
Even after I graduated and changed my graduate course plan, my undergraduate advisor and the professors I had were still there to support and help me through that change. It truly meant a lot.

I have struggled for almost four years of my life at this school. There is a complete lack of diversity and at no point did I ever feel genuinely cared about by any alumni. The only good thing this school provided me was my freshman year roommate and all this time later it is still the only thing I can say. It is the same events, food, and problems every single year and if I could go back and change the school I enrolled in, I would. Expect to pay an arm and a leg and not receive help which is where I am stuck at only a semester away from graduating. Everyone's experience is different, but I truly have the same opinion about this school every single semester I have attended.

Since going to Misericordia I’ve had a great and different experience then where I’m from . Coming from the city I didn’t know what to expect but what I have learned about Misericordia and campus life and life up there in general I definitely recommend it as a school to attend . From the help you get outside the classroom through professor and also different academic resources provided , and also a nice friendly environment surround the campus . Misericordia is a place you can obtain a great college experience and also make some life long friends .

I had a couple experiences where there where people who got away with things they shouldn't have on campus. Overall, the actually academic programs including the one I am in have very high standard that they hold everyone too with no exceptions.

I have not yet attended but just from my tours and orientation I can tell that this is going to be my second home the environment and people are so welcoming and accepting it is out of this world. They really care about the academics you receive as well as the whole college experience.

The food isn’t the best, but it also is not the worst. It isn’t a big party school either. The campus is beautiful. The classes are usually small, so it gives you better one on one time with the teachers. There is always stuff happening on campus. It is nice, and there is a lot to do.
I am a high school student looking for colleges. Everything else seems good but its upsetting that they don't have a womens basketball team.

I love having small class sizes and being able to have one on one time with the teachers. Along with this since the classes are so small the teachers will get to know you and assist you with anything they can. They don't just stop helping once you are done with their class. Campus life is also amazing, they hold so many events with free food, activities and more.

My overall experience has been wonderful. All of the faculty and administrators have be exceptional with helping navigate my desire to continue my education. If there was something that I was unsure of they would be right there to help figure it out. I would like to see a change in the cost of trying to continue education. It is hard for a single parent especially myself, raising a family, going to school and working fulltime.

It's been lovely! I've made some wonderful friends, the small campus size works for me, and the staff, faculty, and professors I've encounter are great people as well! I love the residence halls, and the campus has many opportunities to offer for students that I've taken up on quite a lot.

Very nice area that is close to the city of Wilkes Barre while still being far enough away that it did not feel like it was inside of a city. Good combination of community while still being a decently sized school. Administration could use some work as they don't focus on the right things sometimes.

ai_summary: Mississippi College offers a close-knit , community-oriented environment that many students appreciate for its welcoming atmosphere and small class sizes, allowing for personalized interactions with professors. The academic programs are generally regarded as strong, particularly in specific fields like nursing and administration of justice, though some students express concerns about course organization and teaching quality. The college's Christian values deeply influence campus life, fostering friendships and community bonds, but this can also make it challenging for students with different interests to fit in. Many students enjoy the active student life with various clubs and tribes, although some feel the social scene is limited and lacks diversity. While the campus is seen as safe and supportive, there are noted issues with campus facilities and dining options. Overall, students find that Mississippi College is a positive environment for academic growth and spiritual development, best suited for those aligned with its Christian ethos.

reviews.text: Mississippi college was a nice experience, but it just was not for me. The people are great, but I just felt like I was not in the right place. I have enjoyed the Christian atmosphere and the professors and classes have been more than amazing! I also loved being in a tribe. My tribe has been the best part of my time at Mississippi College!
I have had a great experience with Mississippi College, I have made many friends and the science department is amazing. There definitely needs to be more diversity and some costs need to come down.
I love the campus and it's people. I am now a senior at the school and the way the staff has cared for me as a student is unheard of! If I could do it all over I would. That is how much I love it.

I like that MC feels like a family, and I love that it is small because it provides more opportunities to every student. No student is just a number; you will not be overlooked, and you can get involved if you put in a little effort. Nowhere is perfect, but the Lord will lead you where you are supposed to be and for me that was MC. Great community, great professors, great area. 10 out of 10

I truly enjoy my experience at Mississippi College and being part of the nursing program. The instructors are knowledgeable, supportive, and genuinely invested in helping students succeed. The program challenges me academically while also preparing me to provide compassionate, skilled care to patients. I appreciate the hands-on learning opportunities and the strong sense of community among students and faculty. Mississippi College fosters both professional growth and personal faith, creating an environment where I feel inspired every day to become the best nurse I can be. I’m proud to be part of such an excellent program and college.

Former undergrad student, graduate student, and employee. I’ve seen it all at Mississippi College. I’ve worn the gear, cheered at the games, attended the faculty/staff meetings… you name it, I’ve been there.
MC is a well wrapped package filled with dollar store knockoffs led by a man with impulsive decision making, easily swayed by his chosen advisors. Those advisors have limited higher education experience and while it doesn’t seem sexy or cool, people who KNOW what they are doing really matters, golf cart rides or not.
Your professors may (and will!) love you and cherish their time with you, but know that after a decade or two of no research or published papers, they are trapped there.
Some professors will be punished for seemingly insignificant things, while others are allowed massive leeway. It all depends on how much revenue they bring in.
If you want a budget degree for a non-budget price, MC is a perfectly fine option. But don’t expect to get ahead or have doors open.
I the feel of MC it is welcoming and amazing. The only thing I would change about MC is the cafe the food is not the best.

I enjoy Mississippi College it has a good environment and great teachers.
They are really there to help you reach your educational goals.

It’s in a small town but the academics were way better than a D1 school. Loved the friendships and church communities I’ve made over the years! People should go visit my bakery family at Meme’s Brick Street Bakery in Old Towne!!!

I am still a student at MC. Throughout my time here, I have made lifelong friendships and have grown a lot as a person. If you want to go to a college that truly cares about you, your education, and your faith, this is the right place for you.

Mississippi College is a place with strong traditions, a tight-knit community, and professors who genuinely care. The smaller class sizes make it easier to connect with instructors, and if you're driven, there's a lot of opportunity to grow academically and personally.
What needs work? Communication and efficiency. Administrative departments like security and the business office often feel disorganized, and getting clear answers can take more effort than it should. The technology systems could use a serious upgrade, and processes like billing or registration often feel more complicated than necessary. Some policies feel outdated and rigid. A little flexibility would go a long way.
In short, the heart of the school is good. The people are solid. But some of the systems and structures need to catch up to the present day. If you're independent and willing to speak up for yourself, you can make it work.

I’m an excited incoming freshman who has had an excellent experience with the school so far! I’ve been especially impressed with the emphasis on faith, guidance from the staff, and encouragement to get connected on campus & in the local community.

I like the size of the campus. There’s always plenty to do, but it’s small enough that you know and see people and don’t feel like you’ve slipped through the cracks. Academics are very good.
Campus living policies could be improved.

I have loved every minute of my time at Mississippi College. If you are looking for a place that fosters Christian community, provides professors who are the best in their field, showcases the small-town feel of Clinton, and is incredibly safe, MC is for you! It is incredibly easy to get involved at MC, and there are always events happening on campus. If you want to get involved you can, but if you prefer a more quiet college experience MC gives you the freedom to do that too! I have grown in so many ways at MC, as a student, friend, and most importantly a follower of Christ.

I loved the Christian environment of MC. Professors were quick to pray over students in every situation and were always available to help a student. The campus does a great job of fostering social life among students through numerous campus wide events as well as tribes and clubs.

ai_summary: Mississippi State University (MSU) offers a vibrant and supportive environment for students, characterized by a strong sense of community and welcoming atmosphere. Many reviews highlight the dedicated faculty who are willing to help students succeed academically, along with the rigorous curriculum that prepares them for their future careers. The campus itself is noted for its beauty and accessibility , fostering a lively student life with numerous extracurricular activities and social events, particularly during football season, which is a significant part of the culture at MSU. While some students express concerns about specific departments, such as the chemistry program and the parking situation, the overall sentiment is that MSU provides a well-rounded college experience that many students find fulfilling and enriching.

reviews.text: I love all of the opportunities to get involved. From the second you get to campus, there are so many clubs to join, activities to go to, and so much more. If I could change anything, it would be the number of regular dining halls. We only have one traditional one, and it is on the far end of one side of the campus. It can be inconvenient at times to go there.
As a freshman, my experience has been great so far. There are a lot of organizations to get involved in so you'll a place here at MSU.
Overall experience is good except for he academic advising in the Arts and Sciences for new student. Very hard to create your own schedule.

Love the school, love the campus, and the community. Small town that’s tightly grouped, great nightlife and great living areas. I enjoyed my professors and academic advisors, and living on campus freshman year was great.

From my freshman year of high school I knew I wanted to attend Mississippi State University. From the moment I stepped on campus, I felt an immense sense of belonging. Since starting at Mississippi State in the fall of 2025, I have not been disappointed. From the overflowing amount of student involvement organizations to the constant reminders about supplemental instruction and tutoring. From the moment I stepped on campus as a student I have never felt like I was alone or overwhelmed. From the amazing advisors to the A-Class professors I have never doubted that my education was at the forefront of their minds. My main concern about MSU is the food options. As much as they try to accommodate the ever-changing appetites of growing college students, I feel that there should be healthier options. I would love to see a healthy food option in central campus.

Although my social life is literally nonexistent, my overall experience has been wonderful. The food is easily accessible, and the opportunities to improve are as well, even if they are still a bit intimidating.

Mississippi State University has been my dream school all of my life. My associate degree and career didn’t require me to attain any academics past that, so I never really considered going back until recently. I’m 40 years old and I’m trying to set an example for my kids (8 and 5 years old) that it’s never too late to further their education! With the help of my awesome wife, I know we will be successful. And with the admission process being a seamless one for an old timer like me, I’m sure going forward will be no different.
Overall Mississippi state is the best school from the professor to the students even to the town that it’s in I love it so much
Everyone in the town is so nice and all the professors truly care about you and your success. I love it here

I truly enjoy my experience at Mississippi State University because of its diversity and the constant opportunities to connect with others. There is always an event or gathering happening on campus, which makes it easy to socialize and feel part of a larger community. From tailgates that bring people together from all over to cheer on our football team to smaller campus activities, MSU consistently fosters a welcoming atmosphere. Over time, it has become my home away from home, and I know I could not have made a better choice. The supportive resources and strong academic programs further enrich my experience.
My experience has been outstanding, the city and school atmosphere is extremely inviting to everyone.

My experience so far with Mississippi State University has been great. Coming in this year, I was a transfer student, so I was very worried about fitting in and the friends I would make here. So far, my major has helped me connect with so many people I know get to call my friends, as well as my roommates I live with now. The campus is so pretty and big which I love, and during all the seasons, it feels like home. So far, I would not like to see anything change. The staff is also so great and kind, as well as helps you with anything you need, or need help with.
I plan to transfer to Indiana University. I dont really like Mississippi state. Its not very diverse.

Mississippi State has been my home away from home! The environment, the people and the culture that I am surrounded by have been amazing. I look forward to spending the next four years at MSU and growing as a person.

MSU is located in Starkville, Mississippi, so if you're looking for a small town feel within footsteps of the countryside, you'll feel right at home. If you're from a larger city, you might feel out of place for a bit, but the campus itself is very pretty. The professors here are great in every class I've taken, though that is different for every major.
The campus itself is very walkable, and the Downtown Cotton district is not a far walk from the north side of campus. It's definitely a bit smaller than other SEC schools, but that comes with both pros and cons. The traffic and parking services are unfortunately a bit rough here, but if you can live in an area that is walkable it hardly becomes an issue.
Overall, if you're looking for a balanced college experience, you should be able to find it here. There isn't a whole lot to do out in the city itself, but if you get involved with campus life, there is plenty to keep yourself busy.

ai_summary: Missouri State University (MSU) offers a mixed bag of experiences, with many students feeling a strong sense of community on campus while also highlighting issues in areas like academic advising and extracurricular involvement. Academically, students appreciate the helpfulness and approachability of professors, although experiences with faculty quality can vary widely. The campus is described as beautiful and manageable in size, promoting easy accessibility to various resources, but parking availability remains a significant concern. Student life is vibrant, with numerous events and organizations available, although some find that certain social circles can feel exclusive. Additionally, the affordability of MSU is frequently mentioned as a positive aspect, making it an attractive option for many students. Overall, while MSU has its strengths, it also faces challenges that prospective students should consider.

reviews.text: I really enjoyed my time at Missouri State University. I genuinely apply what I have learned in my current career.

This school touts "cultural competence" as a pillar of learning, but it hardly shows. Initiatives furthering diversity, equity, and inclusion were not platformed, marketed, or resourced as much as the athletics and religious initiatives were.

I really enjoy the variety of classes and the different majors to choose from. I wish that there were more things open on campus over the weekends, especially dining halls and the library. Only one dining hall is open, from 10 am to 7 pm, and so food and the options are very limited.

My overall experience at Missouri State University has been extremely positive. As an Animal Science major, I really appreciate how supportive and knowledgeable all the professors are as well as gearing their classes to be more hands-on rather than lecture based so I can apply what I have learned in real settings. They genuinely care about their students and make the material they teach in their class relevant to real world careers and situations in the animal field. The campus community itself is very welcoming and is a place where asking questions and being yourself is always encouraged. Overall, I am very happy that I have decided to attend Missouri State University and believe that this college would be a great choice for anyone looking to continue their education after high school.

I love the tight-knit community that MSU creates. I have been a student for two years, as a community college transfer, and I have loved every week I've been a student here. The student organizations are serious about involvement, and the student athletics are getting better and better every semester. The school's history, the location and the instructors are all so amazing to learn from. I beg everyone who loves a metropolitan campus with small-town vibes to look into Missouri State University.

My experience at Missouri State University so far has been both challeneging and deeply transformative. As an international Psychology student, the transition came with academic pressure, cultural adjustment, and significant financial uncertainty, all while navigating far from home. Despite tehse obstacales, the experience has strenghtened resilience, independence and clarity of purpose. One of the most meaningful and joyful parts of my time at Missouri State has been the football season. Coming from a strimg sports background, being immersed in energy and school spirit, and sense of unity that surrounded game days felt like familiar grounding. The season fostered a connection and belonging. One area i would like to see furthrr growth is in the suppport provided to international students. While the resources are available navigating financial challenegs and mental health can feel overwhwelming. More tailored support for unique situations would make a meaniful difference for success.
I have two degrees already from Missouri State and I am currently working on another degree and my experience at MSU has been amazing.

It has been a great place to learn and meet all kinds of new people. All staff is very helpful and informative and help guide you in the right path. The university also has a lot of tutoring through the library and a lot of different clubs that you can take part in and a lot of extracurricular activities after classes in the evening. Overall, it has been a great experience in my first semester.

Overall, it's been great, I've had a good time in classes and getting to build a community with other students. I've met a lot of wonderful people and felt like I learned a lot from professors. My main one problem now is that it feels like most of the STEM clubs, specifically the computer science and physics ones, just aren't that interesting or engaging. The clubs I participated in high school were more interesting than these, and I hear about stuff like drone teams, Mars Rover teams, and Nuclear Science clubs at other schools, and am just kinda disappointed with what we have here
So far my experience has been wonderful. I have enjoyed all of my courses over the last year and its looking like the rest of my courses will be enjoyable and will flow smoothly.

I really enjoy the choice of teachers however, I believe they should be evaluated more often. There were many complaints about professors that go unchecked. Tuition is also absolutely ridiculous and it is difficult to find help when trying to navigate financial challenges. The school spends so much money on redecorating and making the school look nicer and not so much time on the students themselves.

Missouri State provided me with the most fulfilling years of my life. I was a biz bear so all things considered my academics weren’t too challenging, but my upper level finance and grad school courses pushed me. Professors were always willing to provide that extra support when needed. In terms of campus life you get out of it what you put in. If you join organizations, clubs, or Greek life, or just go to the rec center, odds are you will have no shortage of friends. MSU is cheap so you aren’t forced to work through college. Dorms are decent, dining hall is mid. About what you can expect at any university. Cheap off campus housing. Truly amazing opportunities for students if they choose to use it— you can be a big fish in a small/ medium size pond at MSU/ Springfield. For biz bears you will be offered seats at tables/ dinners with community/ business leaders you would typically not get the opportunity to in any other city. Great university if you utilize the opportunities MSU offers.
Missouri State University was one of the best places I could have chosen. It’s small enough where its not over whelming but big enough where you wont meet everyone in a single day.
I’ve only been here a year, since I transferred from OTC. So far I really enjoy being on campus. Everyone has been so nice and professional.

Missouri State University is a good university if you are looking for a fun college experience. Living on campus your first year truly makes it feel like home and helps build the community. There are also many organizations available for you to join. This is definitely a place that allows you to explore your interests with people that are there to help you. There are also many things offered at Missouri State that can help benefit you as you obtain your degree. Missouri State also makes it very easy to get accumulated to college life with all their information being on their website and a required soar event that takes place in the summer before your first year at the college. They will help walk you though the next few years of your life and notify you of all the aid that Missouri State offers.

school_name: Missouri University of Science and Technology
ai_summary: Missouri University of Science and Technology stands out for its rigorous academic programs , particularly in engineering and STEM fields, where students report high demands and challenges but also significant support through resources like tutoring and LEAD sessions. The campus offers a close-knit environment with various clubs and activities, encouraging community and collaboration among students. Many reviews emphasize the role of networking and strong job placement rates after graduation, largely facilitated by well-regarded career fairs. However, student life can be limited outside of academics and Greek life, with some students noting a lack of diversity and fewer on-campus activities. Facilities like residence halls and food options receive mixed reviews, indicating room for improvement. Overall, while the school's academic rigor may be tough, many appreciate the dedicated faculty and the value of the education received.

reviews.text: This school is a breath of fresh air. It's placement in rural Missouri gives you the best of both worlds - a college town with tons of small local businesses and outdoor spaces.

My experience as a Missouri S&T student has been both challenging and rewarding. The rigorous coursework has strengthened my problem-solving skills, while the supportive campus community and involvement opportunities have helped me grow academically, professionally, and personally.

Missouri University of Science and Technology is the college you need to go to for engineering. It has one of the largest, if not the largest career fair for looking for jobs, co-ops, internships, etc. and also has many resources for students to prepare for jobs and academics. The atmosphere is very friendly and you can always find help anywhere. There are design teams which can help get you experience in your desired field. Academic advisors can help you plan out your entire time studying at Missouri S&T. If you want to do research as a graduate or undergraduate even, it is important to know that Missouri S&T has the highest research designation from Carnegie Foundation. There is so much opportunity for success here.
All of my professors have been exceptional so far. They show that they are care about you and they have great charisma. The campus is really nice and has nice classrooms and buildings.

It is great and their academics and well rounded. They focus on the students and the applied science. They make you ready to work in real life and make an outstanding career while also enjoy your college life and atmosphere.
I liked that Rolla was about the same size as my hometown. And was less than two hours away from it as well. The professors were amazing. And I learned some amazing things there.

I love the amount of help you can get with anything. Everyone wants you to succeed so use every respurce that is offered. There are so many clubs and the community is amazing. The only thing I would like to see change is the food court. The food isnt the greatest but honestly thats every college you go to.
Love being part of fraternity. The brotherhood and giving back to the community I’m especially proud of
I love the people at missouri s&t. Everyone here is a nerd and I fit right in with my people. There's many kinds of people here and its a really fun school!

I really liked how nature oriented the campus is with all of the trees and plants between areas. I believe the university works well to try and improve its student's academic talents. I like that the organization and events are set up for the students regardless of their major. The Havener food during Opening Week was good. Although, I have not experienced Thomas Jefferson Hall, despite the claims of it being bad, I have heard mostly positive things about it. Overall I like how the campus feels lenient and has given many students a gentle welcome.

In my experience the classes were extremely hard. Often the professors had a lot of industry experience but little teaching experience which hindered students heavily. There were a lot of clubs and activities around campus to keep people involved. It could be very expensive depending on the housing and meal options chosen. The town itself didn't have a lot going on, but it was only under 2 hours to St. Louis.
I'm a sophomore football player. I'm studying business management with a emphasis in entrepreneurship.
It was an amazing experience and I wish I had started my college at Missouri S and T I would have enjoyed my time much more there than anywhere else but I'm glad I finished y college there
Very cute little college town, if you are able to join clubs you will make lots of friends. Classes are difficult but good for a challenge.

I do enjoy attending Missouri S&T. All of the professors that I’ve had have been very good and while the classes are hard they have your best interest in mind. The goods pretty decent and there are always so many social events going on.

ai_summary: Montana State University (MSU) is frequently praised for its beautiful campus and the stunning natural surroundings of Bozeman, which offer numerous outdoor activities for students. Many students find the academic environment supportive, highlighting the dedication of professors and the variety of courses available, particularly in STEM fields. Student life appears vibrant with numerous clubs, sports opportunities, and events, although some express a desire for more campus activities and improved parking. Several reviews mention the high cost of out-of-state tuition and critique the dining hall food quality. While many students report a welcoming and friendly community , there are concerns about a lack of diversity and accessibility during snowy months, as well as some discontent regarding residence hall conditions. Overall, MSU is seen as a good choice for those who enjoy an outdoorsy lifestyle combined with a strong academic foundation.

reviews.text: My experience at Montana State University has been great. The academics and professors are great and they care about you and your education. The Bozeman community is awesome and there are endless opportunity for outdoor recreation.
It is a great experience at Montana State University. There is a lot to do in the area of Bozeman, Montana.
I’m a freshman at Montana State University attending my second semester. So far I have great experiences with other students and campus life. It is easy to make friends and connections.
Bozeman is the best college town. The people are very nice and if you love to ski this is the best place to be!

My experience at Montana state University has been relatively good. I ended up taking a couple of years away to truly find what I wanted to pursue, and found that my deep admiration for helping people led me down the medical pathway. I am currently working at Bozeman Health in my free time in the surgical department. Bozeman is truly a great well rounded support area for academic success.

Overall, Montana State is a terrible university. I would rate it, on a scale of 1-10, as a 3, because most of the staff isn’t willing to cooperate with state and federal laws about official accommodations, the school’s tutoring program is a sham, the campus police are very limited in how much they can do, professors and campus police are not allowed to administer a student’s emergency medicine when they need it most of all, the case managers refuse to do their jobs, and the TAs don’t show up for their own office hours. I think that all of this defines a poorly run university! All of these are things that have happened to me in my time here at MSU.

Montana State University offers a strong social environment, outdoor opportunities, and a welcoming community. However, my academic experience has been disappointing, particularly with advising. I have struggled to find consistency, as communication often comes last‑minute or not at all. It feels as though priorities are misplaced, with attention given to minor details rather than student success. As an out‑of‑state student, I would caution others to be prepared for challenges. Advising has left me feeling unsupported and has led to wasted time and money. The nursing program, in particular, has lacked effective communication, planning, and student care. If you are not accepted into the program, there is little guidance beyond being told to reapply and retake courses you have already completed. Overall, while the community and environment are positive, the academic and advising structures need significant improvement to truly support students in achieving their goals.

Dining halls need better food options for people with dietary restrictions.
Great support for people with disabilities and special needs.

I love going to Montana State University, there are so many resources to help students succeed. There is something for everyone, whether it is academically, socially, or professionally. I would like to see more parking.

My overall Experience attending montana state university began as a difficult new experience as I moved Accross the country. I found that I immediatly started making friends due to the very involved campus life they have, esspecially when it comes to welcoming freshman. The local scene and community events in the town of bozeman have made me feel welcome and close to home. I chose this school hoping that I would find people like me and it became the perfect place to meet new friends and even mentors such as professors and staff that have so much knowlage and insights to share about the west coast. I have met some of my closest friends in the interactive learning enviroment that Montana state provides. In almost every single class that I have had so far I have been able to create a relationship with my professor, and quickly learned how important that is to my success. In the Jabs bussiness school professors will always go out of their way to guide or provide you to the right resources

I like the people and events, there's a good student life and i have a good circle here. I'd like more frequent school events like socials and stuff but other than that it's really good, I absolutely love it here.
In relation to that fact that it’s a college, it is a wonderful place to go. Great academics, community, housing, food, etc.

So far I have really enjoyed my experience at Montana State University. I love all the activities that are geared towards getting students involved on campus. There also have been plenty of opportunities to receive help in any area of struggle. The only thing I would change is the food options on campus. They tend to be very repetitive which makes it hard to enjoy.
Good, but I tried to get instate tuition after moving and living there for a year and they denied me.

Great school! MSU has it all, great people, great area, plenty to do, school programs, great athletics and clubs. If you like adventure and outdoors, this is the place for you. Everyone I know that has gone to school here has loved it. Check it out for yourself, you won't be disappointed.

ai_summary: Montclair State University offers a vibrant and diverse environment where students feel supported both academically and socially. Many reviews highlight the approachable and helpful faculty , with professors generally being dedicated to student success , although experiences vary widely depending on the individual instructor. The campus size strikes a balance, allowing for recognition of classmates while still providing a wealth of opportunities , including clubs, events, and activities that foster a sense of community . However, concerns about food quality, limited weekend activities for commuters, and issues with financial aid processes have been noted. Overall, MSU is seen as a solid option for students seeking a supportive academic atmosphere with numerous opportunities for involvement and personal growth.

reviews.text: Montclair state university is the definition of a “back pack” school. Being a transfer from another institution I see major differences in quality of education, campus life, and just overall appeal of Montclair state. Education at Montclair state University doesn’t seem to be up to par in comparison to other institutions especially my previous one and professors while some can be inviting don’t seem to have that same quality. Campus life in general is very different in which there isn’t really any campus life given most of the school seems to all be commuters. While the campus itself can be nice the campus life is a whole different story. It feels like everyone isolates themselves due to this lack of community. Montclair state university has a lot of potential but in order to fulfill that change needs to be made to really build that sense of community and school pride many other schools have especially for commuter students that might make it more difficult to be involved.

Montclair State University has provided me with a very rich learning environment, in which it evident that each of the professors are knowledgeable about the courses they teach not only from their own education but from their life experience working in their fields of teaching. The school emphasizes a healthy academic and life balance which allows students to be full time students while commuting and maintaining part-time jobs.
Montclair is a nice university that is safe and the neighborhood nearby is also very nice. It's a peaceful school and everyone is welcoming.

Very approachable and engaging with a diverse group of professors who taught me more than I could have imagined outside of my major area. The only real issues I felt came from the parking current issues as a postgraduate, with how certain departments are being condensed into inferior and costly versions of themselves that affect students and faculty alike.
Montclair has been a great experience and it got me out of my comfort zone. Montclair is very diverse and has many different opportunities to connect with other students.
The school campus is beautiful. The environment is welcoming. The school anlso provides great academics.

I stilll haven’t started so unfortunately can’t say much but it’s really close to home and I do enjoy being near the city, I like Montclair state because it’s closer to home and it’s affordable for me specially now theses days

I am a student at the John J. Cali School of Music at Montclair State University. I honestly very much enjoy my experience here so far, and I love both the students and faculty here in the school of music. Unfortunately, this school is incredibly expensive, and it is very difficult to pay everything off. The food is also not the best quality, especially for how much I'm paying to be here. Overall, I do like my experience, but it is a bit of a financial hassle.
I loved everyone on campus and a lot of my classes. Classes also aren’t so far but I would love some sort of transportation for students. The food that was offered wasn’t the best

Montclair State University is a great college that really cares about the students. You will get a lot of help from advisors and sometimes professors. They are there to help us succeed, and that's something I've really enjoyed so far. Another thing I enjoyed was the very lively atmosphere; almost every day, something is going on outside the student center. Something I would change, though, is the long waits for some of the food there. I once had to wait almost an hour to get a cheeseburger.
My experience at Montclair State University so far has been great, full of culture-specific events that have allowed me to celebrate my culture.

I​‍​‌‍​‍‌​‍​‌‍​‍‌ had a great and organizing time at Montclair State University. The professors are indeed experts, and they always offer their help. There are a lot of facilities available on campus to support students in their studies. I have been lucky to meet different people, and their way of life has really opened up my horizon. In sum, my time at Montgomery State has contributed a lot to my academic and personal ​‍​‌‍​‍‌​‍​‌‍​‍‌growth.

Montclair is awesome, very good academic programs. My only issue has been the on campus activities, it can be difficult to be active sometimes but the more you put yourself out there, the less deniable you become.

My overall experience with Montclair State University for my first year of college was pretty good overall. The professors are nice and helpful when you need a better understand on something, whether it's a homework or classwork assignment, or even just something that you might want to talk about in general. The other students are also nice and interactive towards me and everyone else. They are also very friendly to approach, so you can always ask them for either an opinion on an assignment or just to make a new friend.

I received absolutely no help from career services when graduation was approaching. I found my first job in my own. I would recommend looking at other schools who have stronger job placement programs.

ai_summary: Morgan State University offers a culturally rich environment that resonates well with its students, particularly due to its status as a historically Black college. Many students appreciate the sense of community and support from peers and professors, which fosters a family-like atmosphere on campus. Students often mention the welcoming energy of the campus and the dedicated faculty who genuinely care about their academic success, although experiences with housekeeping and housing assignments can be frustrating. While students enjoy the diversity and opportunities for personal growth, there are common concerns about the efficiency of the financial aid department and the quality of campus housing and food. Overall, Morgan is seen as a nurturing space , especially for those looking to connect with their heritage and pursue their educational goals in a supportive HBCU setting.

reviews.text: So far as a commuter student I like the university. However, the lack of housing/ priority housing is a real issue.

My overall experience at Morgan State University has been both challenging and transformative, shaping me academically, athletically, and personally. One of the aspects I value most is the strong sense of community and support among students and faculty. I appreciated being part of a diverse and driven student body. As a student-athlete, I also valued the opportunity to compete at the Division I level, however, compared to other facilities in the MEAC conference, I would say is behind, and could definitely be better. As an international student, there were moments where I felt that more support systems could be in place to help us integrate into the campus and academic life. Expanding resources, mentorship opportunities, and community-building initiatives specifically for international students could enhance the overall experience and help bridge cultural gaps. Overall, despite the dangerous location of MSU, my experience has been great.

Overall very informative, while the university unfortunately had questionable characters at every turn my only issue is the financial aid and its housing problems as well as this schools lack of being on time

THE BEST DECISION I MADE. I love Morgan. The experiences I’ve gained as a student both from professors and from my peers are priceless. It was at Morgan where my curiosity about the African diaspora was fed. You leave Morgan, knowing where you came from and with a vigor to charge towards a better future. It was at Morgan where I truly understood that I am my ancestors wildest dreams and appreciate the path they’ve lead for me. The diversity is also unmatched. There’s an opportunity to learn about so many different cultures and children from all over the world attend Morgan. It’s a melting pot of African, Caribbean, Asian and even a bit of European culture.

I love Morgan State University!! It took me 7 years to get my degree in Nutritional Sciences but it was worth it. I was a non-traditional student at the time. When I obtained my degree i was 50 years old. I received so much support from my instructors, the administration and the Morgan family.
It was great. I feel privileged to have attended the national treasure. I learned a lot of things and developed a soft spot for the city of Baltimore.

Morgan State University is a great school because it feels like more than just a campus—it feels like a community. As an HBCU, it has a strong sense of culture, pride, and history that helps students feel seen and supported. The professors genuinely care about student success and are approachable both inside and outside the classroom. Morgan State also offers strong academic programs, especially in engineering, business, and the sciences, while encouraging leadership and service. Located in Baltimore, the university provides real-world opportunities, internships, and connections. Overall, Morgan State prepares students not just to graduate, but to grow.
Morgan is an HBCU for all is welcome. So much experience you can make new friends easily. Everyone is so nice and willing to always help each other out.

At Morgan, students benefit from rigorous academic programs across fields like engineering, business, the sciences, social sciences, and liberal arts, taught by faculty who are invested in their success. Class sizes are often small enough to allow individualized mentorship, yet large enough to offer diverse perspectives and collaboration.
Beyond the classroom, MSU’s dynamic campus life is filled with opportunities — from student organizations and leadership development to Greek life, performing arts, and community service. The school’s location in Baltimore’s urban core means students are constantly connected to real-world experiences through internships, research, and civic engagement. Whether you’re studying criminal justice in partnership with local agencies or conducting research with faculty, Morgan empowers students to apply learning in meaningful ways.
The thing that I like the most about Morgan State University is that once you step foot on the campus it is very easy to make friends and bond with others.
The academic is a great experience and example of what you will eventually see in your careers, although it is a bit different than what most would be expecting especially with the professors.
I honestly feel like Morgan is a very diverse school although it's big it is really a really good school it has a lot of dorms over the capacity

Although I haven’t yet attended MSU in person, exploring the student portal and learning about the university’s campus life, diverse community, and athletic programs has been inspiring. I especially appreciate how rooted and welcoming the MSU community appears to be. One thing I would love to see improved is more detailed guidance for incoming international students, to help them feel even more prepared and supported before arrival.

I liked the professors they definitely make sure they involve all their students and cater to the student needs. Housing is a little lacking as they accept a large volume of students while having limited housing so make sure to apply as soon as possible. Other than that, the people are very accepting and you will find community here. Do not believe everything you hear and you will definitely enjoy!

My experience in the Morgan State University Marching Band has been one of the best parts of my time here. Being part of the band has pushed me musically, mentally, and physically, and it’s helped me grow as a person. The band has a strong culture of discipline, tradition, and excellence, and you can feel the pride every time we step onto the field or into the stands. The energy from the band family and the support we give each other makes the long practices worth it.
What I enjoy most is the brotherhood and sisterhood within the sections. You build real bonds with people who work just as hard as you, and you get to represent the school in a powerful way. Performances, games, and trips create memories you don’t forget. The staff and leadership also push us to be our best, and that’s something I really appreciate.

ai_summary: Mount St. Joseph University is often praised for its tight-knit community and the close relationships students can form with faculty members. Many reviews highlight the small class sizes , which allow for personalized attention and interaction, creating an environment where professors provide significant support for academic success. However, there are ongoing concerns about the high tuition costs and the lack of diversity on campus. While the university offers a variety of extracurricular activities and has a welcoming atmosphere , some students wished for more modern facilities and dining options. Overall, students appreciate the friendly environment and the sense of belonging they find at MSJ, often describing it as a "home away from home."

reviews.text: The Mount experience has been great so far. I have loved all the people I have met on campus, as well as all the professors I have had, who were respectful towards me while also maintaining a professional environment. I also engage in a sport at the Mount, and that experience has not gone as intended. The people are still great, but the coaching is just not the best.
Great school, supportive staff, excellent teachers and a good support system and student body that care about you
It’s a very nice school and It has very caring staff and students. I am loving my time here and the atmosphere.

The experience itself was amazing, my issue was im first generation, which means there's a lot i don't know unless I'm told, to my knowledge for my parent plus loan was good for a year not a semester. Noone told me they just let it expire and I couldn't further my education or my football career.

I love MSJ so much!! I have participated is multiple clubs, events, and programs and can honestly say it is one is of the best decisions I've made. The professors genuinely care about your academic success and personal well-being. Campus life is vibrant and I love the short commute to every location on-campus! The only things I would change are the food and the dorms. The food has improved over the years but it still makes people sick occasionally and they have the same rotation every month. There are not many dining options either....there is the main dining hall (5/3), carry-out (Lion's Grille), and a small convenience store (Market C). The dorms are outdated. Overall, amazing school!

It's so far pleasant not many students that's on campus, butit'ss nice anquiette, and everyone I've methase been friendly :)) made decent friends with my esports group and can't wait to experience it with my broth,er who is joining the college

As you’ll notice the only thing with more than 1 start is diversity. That’s because diversity is the only thing this school cares about lol.. that and shoving their god awful opinions in your face all day. I’d avoid MSJ like it was the plague for any newcomers just go to a much cheaper school right up the road UC.
I like it feels like home and everything is willing to help and be your friend. I would like to see change is having a stove and oven to cook with in the dorms.

At Mount St. Joseph university the main focus is always on education, however there are many options for clubs, organizations, sports, and activities that you can participate in at the school. The community at the schools is positive and people are helpful and friendly.

I spend two full semesters at Mount saint Joseph University. Mostly , I have enjoyed the diversity of the school itself. there is students from different backgrounds and within my second degree program i've met students that have different experiences and different major which makes the interaction between student even more interesting. I have also enjoyed the small intimate classes and that makes a huge different on how easily a student can access the teacher and get help if needed.
I enjoy my time at MSJ! I wish there was more diversity and more things available on campus, but that's okay. It is very very expensive, but it shows in the education.

I liked the fun environment and the welcoming feeling that you get coming through the doors. The teachers make sure to put a smile on your face everytime you walk through the door. They are very understand and have small classes sizes to help really get to know there students.

The whole campus is very close knit and small. I really enjoy being able to know and have a relationship with a lot of my classmates due to this small number and it allows you to meet new people easily. Faculty and staff are amazing and are always there to help you with no matter what. They offer many great resources and connections for whatever you may need. I love the mount

I really like the small class sizes that are offered and the limited number of people in each class. I feel that with the smaller class sizes I am more likely to get help and answers when and where I need them. I however do not like some of the teaching styles and the low amount of graded items that go towards the final grade.
Great school, teachers are awesome, campus is beautiful with so many resources and scholarships available

ai_summary: At National Louis University, students often highlight the supportive environment fostered by faculty and staff, noting their willingness to help and maintain open lines of communication. Many appreciate the small class sizes that encourage personalized attention and build a sense of community among students. Reviews frequently mention the strong academic programs , with particular praise for the quality of teaching in fields like counseling and culinary arts. The Chicago location is viewed as a significant benefit, providing access to a vibrant city atmosphere . While students generally have a positive view of their experience, there is a notable desire for increased outreach regarding financial aid and more robust extracurricular opportunities, including sports and campus housing. Overall, the university is recognized for its diverse and inclusive community that seeks to support students' academic and personal well-being.

reviews.text: My experience at National Louis University (NLU) honestly has been great. Everyone is so communicative and friendly. No one makes you feel left out and they provide a ton of support. I like how the professors actually like to get to know you and respect you. I don’t think I’ll like to see anything change as I had mentioned it is a great academic environment. They provide a ton of well organized information regarding syllabus, class, assignments, everything you can think of that you will need or for help.

National Louis University is a great school it has a lot of majors for students to look at when applying for the university. The college offers online courses as well which is great for students that can’t attend in person. One thing that I would like to see change is when every year they decide to increase the amount of how much the credit hours are to make it more affordable since it’s a private school.

I love the fact that National Louis University because they are open to making sure that everyone can attend school in any manner without judgement. I would like to see the fact of all online classes to be run the same using the content tab to follow what is due and when as it is much more accomodating like a checklist.

Hi, I am new to NLU. This is my first year and first semester, and I take all online classes. I can only speak to this. With that being said, the two Professors I have seem to be nice and helpful. The classwork is aligned with the course of study. Semesters are 10 weeks. The ECE program I am in has several options for adding an endorsement in the field of Education. The ECE program also helps educators obtain the Teaching credentials. All the information you could ever need or find is at your fingertips and can easily be accessed via your student portal.
