Overall I like the environment and how diverse everyone is around me. The professors are really helpful, even outside the classroom. The coursework is pretty easy and I like how we do different things and not just assignment after assignment. The food is really good for the most part, I like the different options they have and I like how they cook food from multiple cultures.

During my first years at National Louis, I am pleased to share how I have been resilient in my personal life. I have had the opportunity to meet new people and create new relationships with classmates. I felt welcomed at NLU right away and it felt safe to come to school. I love the diverse cultures NLU shares and how they support different cultures by hosting events and having students gather to talk. I love their power talks and support groups on campus. NLU has has a huge impact on my life and I plan to continue my career path at NLU. I would love to be part of the NLU staff one day in the future. I was able to find my first career job thanks to my professors at NLU. As a young educator, I plan to keep extending my skills and apply them at work. I am honored to share that I work with Early childhood Elementary and it is all thanks to the team at NLU. I love to share my skills of meeting the needs of each child and creating a safe environment for them to be in.
I started as undergraduate students and now I am working to wards my graduate study. Excellent school of education. Thank you
Overall i have enjoyed my time at National Louis University. My professors have been great and I enjoy my classmates.
Overall, I have had a very positive experience attending National Louis University. The professors are all knowledgeable and responsive, and my counselor has been very helpful.
I like the college much. They are very helpful and answer all my questions. I love the way they respect and take care of all student needs.
I transferred this year, and they have helped me every step of the way. Unlike my last school I felt like I truly belong.

When I transferred over to NLU they made it so easy! On top being able to complete my bachelors in a year and half. So many resources that they offer as well. It’s been an amazing experience and plan on doing my masters at NLU too!

The school is great because there is alot of help for the students. Even the students there are nice and helpful. Teachers are great. The only thing I would change is the chairs in the classrooms they are uncomfortable but everything else is a great experience.

When beginning my studies at National Louis University I was concerned about building a community as a hybrid student. To my surprise and delight, I found fierce and supportive comradery amongst the students with whom I studied. In pursuit of my Master's Degree, I am once again considering National Louis University. While I enjoyed my experience with other students, I would like to see improvement in professor/student communication. As a student with a disability, it is imperative that we are able to work together effectively communicate with one another.

Excellent teachers and since it is a culinary school the lunch is phenomenal. The diversity and inclusion and the dedication of the teachers is outstanding. I love that school and need help to continue going their I’m not blessed with money and would really appreciate it.

ai_summary: At the New Jersey Institute of Technology (NJIT), students often highlight the strong academic environment , particularly in engineering and STEM fields, with many praising the knowledgeable faculty and the hands-on , project-based learning approach. The campus community is recognized for its diversity and supportive atmosphere, making it easier for students to find clubs and activities that match their interests. While students appreciate the proximity of amenities and the availability of resources, there are mixed reactions about campus life, with some wishing for more social events or improved student services like food quality and living accommodations. Though the workload can be intensive, particularly in demanding majors, many participants feel the rewards, such as networking and career opportunities , are worth the effort. Overall, NJIT provides a unique blend of a smaller campus feel with the resources of a larger research institution, though experiences may vary based on individual departments and professors.

reviews.text: My experience with NJIT has been a mix of excitement and frustration, like it’s full of potential but sometimes feels buried under too many wires and old buildings. The professors I met were passionate and knew their stuff, especially in engineering, and I really liked that most classes actually push you to think instead of just memorize. The campus itself has a gritty energy that kind of reflects Newark. Not always pretty, but real and alive. I do wish there were more spots to hang out or grab food between classes, since a lot of time you end up walking around just to find somewhere decent to study. The workload can get intense but it feels worth it if you’re serious about your major. Still, a little more flexibility or support for transfer students would honestly make the experience smoother. Overall, it’s not perfect, but if you’re driven, NJIT gives you the tools to actually build something out of yourself.

I'm having a moderately satisfied moment at New Jersey Institute of Technology (NJIT). The college provides me great academic resources since professors are nice and dedicated, and lectures are comprehensive and practical to learn new things and deal with tough exams. I'm really into the indoor pool when I can relax, and the slightly green environment surrounding encourages me to have a walk every night. Playing pool with couple of friends is fantastic and joyful, and getting into some school events helps me to make new friends and expands the social network. Despite the good insight, it would be nice if I could enjoy the food more happily, depending on the mood of the staffs I believed. The surrounding area is not fascinating and vivid as I expected in Newark, and the location is not an ideal place to hang out late at night, should be careful. I would love to have more flowers and tall trees so that the place would become more appealing and refreshing and cozy fireplace in the center.

I loved attending NJIT in person. I took some courses online as well, which I enjoyed. It was a great place to learn in. The students there were respectful and it was a very diverse environment. I even went to the fitness center there when I had time. Overall, I liked my time there as an undergraduate student. It made me choose to do my graduate studies there as well. So I ended up joining the BS/MS program. I will be graduating with my master of science degree in electrical engineering in a few months. There are a few things that I would like to see improved at NJIT. One of them includes that there should be no smoking allowed on campus to ensure a clean and fresh atmosphere. Another change would be to keep rooms as clean as possible, since many times, there was garbage and debris in some areas.

NJIT is a great campus if you like to not travel so far from one class to another. Food aroudn campus is better than on campus. I originally went to school for biomedical engineering, but changed to a business major. The process was simple and easy and I felt more at home with a change of major. I could join certain clubs and be more social.
its alright you wont be insanely happy but not a bad choice especially if you get into the honors college with full ride

Academics: 3/5
The academics here are average. While some professors are engaging and knowledgeable, others can be a bit hit-or-miss. You get what you put into it — so it really depends on how much effort you're willing to invest.
Food & Campus Facilities: 3/5
The food on campus is okay. it’s edible and not too expensive. However, there are times when the quality can be a letdown. I’ve had my share of undercooked meals or even food that feels like it’s past its prime. The campus itself is decent, with good study areas and recreational spaces.
Location: 4/5
The surrounding area is a real plus. There are plenty of places to grab food off-campus
Overall Experience: 3/5
If you're looking for a college with a solid enough academic foundation and good surroundings, this place could work for you. The food and academic experience leave some room for improvement, but the campus vibe and location are strong points.

Completed my undergraduate here. If you are looking to get a good education and not too worried about partying/college scene, this school is great. Professors can vary from great, caring, fun to difficult and problematic. If you stay within the campus, you should have no issue, but Newark can be rough in some areas. So always be careful and aware of your surroundings. NJIT has many clubs, student organizations, and activities that can be taken advantage of for fun nights, networking, etc.
In NJIT there is a wide variety of activities to do when you don't have class. You have plenty of time between classes to go explore the campus, make new friends, and enjoy life outside of studying.
Great college in terms of academics and sports. Overall recommend the college for partying and getting a great education.

NJIT is a very nice university, due to spacious campus diverse students backgrounds. As for my experience I would probably like more options in the cafeteria since I can only eat halal food. other than that my experience was amazing.
my experience is really great, professors re really knowledgeable and cooperative. I grow a lot in this school.

My overall experience with NJIT so far has been very limited as I just finished my orientation. However, from that alone I have felt very welcomed by the NJIT family and well instructed on what to expect during my first year attending NJIT.

As a current student at NJIT majoring in Business Information Systems, my experience so far has been both academically rewarding and socially enriching. The professors are generally very dedicated and bring a lot of industry knowledge to the classroom. Most are approachable and offer helpful support during office hours, which makes a big difference when navigating challenging courses.
The campus is well-maintained and continues to grow with new buildings and resources. The Wellness and Events Center (WEC) is a standout space—not just for fitness, but also for student events and activities. There are plenty of study areas around campus, including the library and common lounges, which are usually quiet and comfortable.

My experience at NJIT was one of the greatest things ever. College is hard no doubt on that but even for every moment that I found myself struggling I kept pushing forward thanks to NJIT because this school has accepted me giving me that push I needed to continue moving forward. I was in it for all, the campus, the people I met there, my professors, and the degree that will change my life for the better. I loved every aspect at NJIT and with every experience I love it even more!

One of my favorite things from going to school at NJIT has been the community. There are many great people who are full of ambition and goals. The campus is beautiful and comforting. My only concern has been with staff at the math department.

ai_summary: New Mexico State University offers a welcoming and diverse atmosphere that many students appreciate, noting it feels like home with a friendly campus community . Academically, the professors are highlighted as supportive , passionate , and accessible, often employing various teaching methods to engage students. The campus is described as beautiful with ample green spaces , though some students express concerns about the dining services and the need for more on-campus activities. The affordability of tuition is a common positive mention, though students note hidden fees and challenges in managing financial aid. While the overall experience is mostly positive, some students feel improvements are needed in areas like advising, administrative processes, and campus visibility of student organizations. NMSU seems particularly suitable for those pursuing careers in agriculture, engineering, and business, but some students express that the university might lack a strong traditional college experience , particularly in student life and sports.

reviews.text: Excellent school to attend. They will help on everything you do. Excellent staff a d they will put you first to be in the program you want tobe

I enjoy being an online student because it gives me the flexibility to do school while still being able to participate in all types of church and family activities. I like being a part of online classes because I am able to work at my own pace and stay focused without the pressure of being in a classroom. At the same time, it can be challenging because it requires a lot of self-discipline and organization skills some challenges that do arise is it takes longer to receive help from instructors. With those challenges in mind online school works best for me to keep moving and forward with my education while still being able to continue maintaining balance in my everyday life

New Mexico State University has always been very welcoming and accommodating to me and other students. I love the atmosphere and going to school everyday knowing I have people who enjoy teaching and other students who are willing to learn and do their best.

Overall, I like my university, is just that there are some professors that make things more difficult that it should be and offer no help, I have come around professors that excell my expectations and have made me fall in love with my career path and they are part of the reason which I'm still studying.

In my time here at NMSU it has been such an amazing one, as soon as I arrived the first day it felt like they wanted me there and that I was seen as a student and not just another dollar sign. I choose NMSU because i want to go into the cattle field in genetics, and they have not disappointed in opportunities for this. With so many profesores having labs and doing research on the topic there is an abundance of opportunities. As well as every profesor I have had so far has had so much passion for what they teach and they truly want you to understand the concepts they are teaching. All in all New Mexico State University is definitely a top school for Animal and Range science.

The most poorly-run institution I've ever had to deal with in my life. I spent *days* trying to straighten out their records on me. I'd spend *hours* on the phone being passed from office to office, and end up talking to the first person I called, who couldn't help. I'd physically go from office to office and get eye-rolls while people tell me their computer is showing them the correct information, then I'd go online and the information was wrong and I couldn't register. When I finally got 3 out of 4 offices to agree where I was registered and for which degree, but I couldn't sign up for courses because financial aid thought I was still in the associate's degree and wouldn't give me any aid, I gave up. I'll attend another university online, and send them my federal aid instead, and get a more prestigious degree anyway. NMSU is pathetic.
What I liked about this school is how many things are always going on especially in the dorm halls. I would like to see the dorms being taken better care of.

New Mexico State University in Las Cruces is such a special place — it truly feels like a second home. From the moment you step on campus, you’re surrounded by friendly faces, beautiful views of the Organ Mountains, and a sense of community that makes you feel like you belong. The professors genuinely care about their students and go out of their way to help you succeed, both in and out of the classroom. There’s a perfect balance of academic challenge and personal growth, and so many ways to get involved and make lasting memories. NMSU isn’t just a university — it’s a place where you grow, find your people, and discover who you’re meant to be.

My overall experience so far at NMSU has sort of been a roller coaster. The campus is beautiful and active with lots of organizations and clubs. The only real gripe I have is the financial aid office. I would email my financial aid advisor and wouldn’t receive a response for days unless I showed up to the financial aid office when I needed help. Other than that my overall academic and lifestyle change has been positive here at NMSU. Also, the city of Las Cruces is great, it has a large variety of places to eat from as opposed to the small town I came from.

I like that we are all free to be our own person. We do not judge others. We all stay open-minded to everyone around us. The professors are really good. They want us to succeed in life. My life now at New Mexico State University has overall become the best. I have loved every moment that I have been here.

My experience in NMSU has been nothing but amazing. I love the student life here and I love the education in NMSU as I am always thrilled to keep on learning. There is absolutley nothing that I would want to change of this beautiful university!

My experience at New Mexico State University has been positive overall. The campus community is welcoming and diverse, and the professors are generally supportive and willing to help students succeed. I appreciated the smaller class sizes in many courses, which made it easier to connect with faculty and classmates. The university also offers great opportunities for research, student organizations, and cultural events. However, I think NMSU could improve by expanding career services, offering more internship connections, and upgrading some of the older campus facilities. Overall, it’s a supportive place to learn and grow both academically and personally.
I enjoyed the scenery and most people are nice. The only downside is the stuck upness of the Greek life of NMSU.

For a first college experience, it was great. Lots of opportunities for everyone of all interests. The campus is very long and walk able which is great if you are a student who wants to get out but does not have access to a car.
I like that they offer many different resources for all kinds of students, they have tutoring for all majors and many different activities for students to socialize!

ai_summary: At New York University, students often praise the vibrant community , especially within specific programs like Music, but express concerns about the lack of diversity in certain areas, such as the Vocal Performance program. Many appreciate the university's location in New York City, which offers a unique urban experience that feels more integrated with real-world opportunities than traditional college life. Students mention the excellent academic quality , with passionate professors and a broad range of course offerings, though some experience challenges with large lecture sizes. The diverse student body allows for rich cultural interactions , although some find it difficult to establish a tight-knit community due to the urban setting. While the campus lacks traditional features like sports team pride and a defined campus boundary, many enjoy the independence and endless activities available in the city. Financial aid and high tuition costs are commonly noted drawbacks, balancing the otherwise exceptional educational and experiential opportunities.

reviews.text: New York University may not have a campus but New York City makes up for it. NYU being directly in the city allows students to have access to go to many sites like the Whitney Museum, Broadway shows, tasty restaurants, boat rides, and so much more. As a freshman, NYU has given me access to extend my creativity to new heights and be able to meet so many people with innovative and professional personalities.

I transferred into New York University after realizing that I wanted to double major in Political Science and Photography. Being a student in Tisch gave me the confidence to pursue a career in the arts. It was a wonderful experience.

My experience at New York University has been both challenging and rewarding. As a master’s student in computer science at NYU Tandon, I appreciate the academic rigor and how courses focus on practical, real-world applications. The professors encourage critical thinking rather than just completing assignments, which has helped me grow technically and analytically. I also value the diversity on campus — interacting with students from different backgrounds has broadened my perspective. There are many opportunities for research, networking, and on-campus roles if you take initiative. One improvement I’d suggest is clearer communication about deadlines and programs, as some opportunities can be easy to miss. Overall, NYU has helped me grow both academically and personally.

The professors at NYU are incredibly qualified often holding multiple degrees. The academic scene at Stern specifically is a little rough because of the way that each class is curved. Being in the middle of New York City is a big plus as the surrounding food spots as well as things to do are abundant.

I tour NYU’s Manhattan campus as a sophomore in high school. The local scene was the breathtaking architecture of Greenwich Village, a blend of green, historical and skyscrapers views. During the tour our tour guide explained her experience at NYU as a former country girl and how the city and life at NYU changed her. The topic that captivated me the most was the global network of 13 academies across the world and testimonies from undergrads who were fresh from their overseas studies in Europe and Asia free of charge and the great changes and door opened through NYU’s studied abroad program.

Personally, I believe NYU is a good school for me. But many people I have met here don’t enjoy it as much as I do. I think this is primarily because NYU has no campus and isn’t like other colleges. The student lifestyle is more independent, and there’s less of a community. I know many other schools have a strong sense of community and bond between students. But at NYU, I don’t really feel a sense of commodity with my peers. But I’m okay with that. I like the more independent lifestyle and I’m okay with having the few friends I have. If you want to live the fast-paced city life, choose NYU. But you will be sacrificing a traditional college experience.
In my experience, I learned a lot from my first semester at NYU, the professors were kind and taught relatively well.

Really enjoyed my time at NYU. It's definitely a choose-your-own-adventure where you can decide the level of involvement you want to have with the university and New York City at large. The variety of food options was good on the meal plan, and there was always something to do on or around campus. It was easy to find people who shared my interests. The university is notably expensive, as is the surrounding area, so that would be the primary thing to be wary of.

NYU is a great school when it comes to academics. Nice professors, competitive peers that will motive you, and warm faculty. But for me I found its dorm to be quite small, but still its a great place. Their study spots are so good that you will left no choice but to sit there and lock in.

As a new college freshman, I found the social life of New York incredibly daunting. I would consider myself someone who is more on the open, social side, but no one talks about how difficult it is going from having the same classes with all your friends to having the same classes with a few people you met in your first week but don't really vibe with. I found it incredibly frustrating talking to people about making friends because most of them said they met their people during Welcome Week. I didn't meet my people during Welcome Week, and it stung seeing everyone else going out on weekends with their newfound friends while I rotted in my dorm.
I would like to have been told, "Don't worry, you'll find your people over time. Don't stress about every little shallow friendship."
too expensive... sitting here writing this just to try and get a scholarship so I don't have to drop out

NYU is a great school, despite the lack of campus , there is definitely an abundance of community. Although, it can be a struggle if you are a low income student, but by second semester it will feel like home.

My experience with New York University has been very positive so far. The admissions process, academic advising, and communication from different departments have all been supportive and well-organized. I appreciate NYU’s global environment and the diversity of its community, which is one of the main reasons I chose to study here. Even before arriving on campus, I have felt welcomed and guided through every step.
If I could suggest an improvement, it would be to streamline communication across departments for new students, as having more centralized guidance would make the process even smoother. Overall, I am excited to join the NYU community and begin my graduate studies. Overall, I am grateful for the positive experience at NYU.
Although my time at NYU was not perfect, it was definitely the right school for me. My assignments were challenging and aimed at improving my critical thinking skills.

My experience at New York University has been meaningful and challenging. As a commuter transfer student, my path into NYU has been different from the traditional experience, but it has taught me a lot about resilience and commitment. Academically, NYU has pushed me in the best ways, my professors are supportive, the coursework is engaging, and I feel like I’m truly growing in my field.
Balancing long travel times with classes, assignments, and involvement on campus has required strong time management, but it’s also shown me how dedicated I am to my goals. The biggest challenge has been tuition. Navigating the cost of attendance while also covering transportation expenses is difficult. I’m proud of the effort I’ve put in to manage these responsibilities, but additional financial support would make a tremendous difference. It would allow me to focus more deeply on my academics and take full advantage of the opportunities NYU offers.

school_name: North Carolina Agricultural and Technical State University
ai_summary: At North Carolina A&T State University, students highlight the vibrant campus life infused with "Aggie Pride" and a strong sense of community . The atmosphere is welcoming, with many students feeling at home among their peers. Academically, the professors are generally supportive and challenging, fostering a culture of critical thinking and growth. Students appreciate various opportunities for involvement, from clubs and organizations to internships, though some express concerns about housing shortages and food quality on campus. While many celebrate the university's cultural richness , they also note issues with financial aid and administrative responsiveness. Overall, students feel that their experience at A&T is shaped significantly by their engagement in campus life and the connections they make.

reviews.text: My experience at North Carolina A&T has been nothing short of transformational. Coming in through a College of Engineering bridge program, I was able to develop a community early on within my college and department that could not be replicated in any other way. I have been able to foster relationships with faculty that will be instrumental to my matriculation, gain a group of mentors diverse in experience and industry, and seize the opportunity to present myself to corporate partners to further develop my professionalism on every level.

I would change the amount of students that admissions continues to allow in the incoming freshman class. Although it’s great to have people want to come here, every year can’t be a situation of having the “biggest freshman class in history”.

My experience with North Carolina A&T State University was something I’ll never forget. From the moment I stepped on campus, I felt a strong sense of pride and community, like I was somewhere that truly values excellence and culture. The energy of the students, the legacy, and the ambition in the air made me feel motivated about my own future. Seeing people who looked like me striving for success made everything feel more possible. It wasn’t just a visit, it felt like a glimpse into the next chapter of my life.
I want to go but I'm not yet admitted. I'm hoping that I will get in, but I've heard nothing but good things about their school. Hopes and Prayers I get in.
I am glad I went to NCAT. My professors truly cared about my education. There are great relationships to maintain and connections to uphold. I'm proud to be an Aggie.
North Carolina A&T State University is a pretty good school. I would like to see changes in housing, professors and the campus dynamic.

As a student at North Carolina A&T State University, I have had a meaningful and enriching experience. The university offers a supportive academic environment with professors and staff who are invested in student growth and success. NCAT has challenged me academically while also helping me develop personally and professionally, preparing me for my future career and life beyond college. I am grateful to be part of a community that values excellence, leadership, and opportunity.
It’s a great college I just feel like they should change the parking and get more parking spaces for the students

During my first semester at North Carolina A&T State University, I've met many new people. The fall semester has truly taught me how to be more responsible and continues to prepare me for adulthood. Attending college will bring you many opportunities, whether they're academic or social.

Although I am a new student and have not officially started classes at North Carolina A&T State University yet, my experience with the university has already been meaningful. My older sister graduated from A&T, so I have visited campus several times and seen firsthand the culture and sense of community that make the school special. I also lived in North Carolina and visited the campus multiple times while my mom mentored students from the school. Being on campus showed me what it feels like to be part of something bigger than yourself. After completing my fall freshman year at community college, I am excited to transition to a university environment where I can fully engage in campus life, meet my roommate, attend events, and build lifelong friendships. I am especially looking forward to the supportive atmosphere and opportunities for growth. As a new student, I am excited for what’s ahead and hopeful to see continued support for transfer students as they adjust to university life.
It was a Great HBCU Experience!! I cannot see myself attending and graduating from any other school.

The college experience is all about what you make it, but being a student at NC A&T is one of the greatest gifts I could have asked for. I love the campus, am extremely thankful for how the professors push students to succeed and couldn't image getting my degree anywhere else.
I went here for the Pre-College Business Summer Camp, and it was a great experience! I learned a lot about the school and even decided to apply.

North Carolina Agricultural and Technical State University is a school with a great culture. A majority of people are kind and it's easy to feel welcomed. Housing has both good and bad options so it just depends on where you end up. There are some professors who you can count on and are very caring and helpful. Others not so much so it's very important you try to reach out to those in your major to see who to take.

My overall experience at North Carolina A&T State University has been positive so far. The campus culture is welcoming, and there is a strong sense of pride and community among students. Many professors genuinely care about student success and are willing to help if you put in the effort. I’ve enjoyed the opportunities for involvement, networking, and academic growth, especially within my major. However, there are areas that could improve, such as communication from administration and efficiency with advising and registration processes. With better organization in those areas, the student experience would be even stronger. Overall, A&T is a great school that offers solid academics, culture, and opportunities if you take advantage of them.

ai_summary: North Carolina State University (NC State) gets mixed reviews from students, highlighting a vibrant campus life filled with friendly peers and engaging faculty. Many students appreciate the strong academic support and passionate professors who genuinely want to help them succeed, particularly in STEM fields. The campus is praised for its size and beauty, but some dorms are seen as outdated. Student life is dynamic, with numerous clubs, organizations, and events, along with a strong sports culture that fosters community spirit. However, issues such as dining hall quality and parking are commonly mentioned as areas needing improvement. While there is a perceived lack of diversity on campus, the overall atmosphere is welcoming and supportive, making it a suitable choice for high school seniors looking for an inclusive college experience.

reviews.text: Overall, my experience at North Carolina State University has been pretty positive. The campus is beautiful, there are a lot of opportunities to get involved in clubs and leadership roles, and the school spirit around sports is really strong. I’ve met some great people here and enjoyed being part of the Pack community. At the same time, some classes can feel very large and it can take effort to build relationships with professors if you don’t actively reach out. I also think navigating some of the campus systems and housing options can be confusing at times. Overall though, it’s a great school with a lot of opportunities if you’re willing to put yourself out there and get involved.

My experience at North Carolina State University has been very interesting since arriving here in the fall. I am a student-athlete here at North Carolina State. The sports scene shows that it has been vastly expanded, which makes campus life enjoyable. When I first got here, it felt like stepping into something I've never experienced before. As just a student looking outward, I felt uncomfortable first coming here because, as a Black young woman used to being around a lot of people that are the same color as me, it was eye-opening when I got here. I had to search for people who were not Caucasian. I was out of my comfort zone to be short. Since then, I have found my community in my teammates, "Girls Night in", a women student-athlete organization focused on the involvement and community aspect for Women in sports, and the African American Cultural Center. I have grown to find my crowd since being here, which has made my experience only grow positively.

So far, NC State has been a great experience~with the exception of class registration. The classes I have been able to get into have been large, but I've been able to meet with the professor or the teaching assistance any time I've needed help. Class registration is frustrating! You have to wait for your assigned date and time and since I am a W, I'm one of the last to register. I had 9 classes on my wish list and only ended up getting one! I checked back regularly and was able to register for 5 of the classes, but I feel like the registration process could be easier and less stressful! I'm enjoying going to games and hanging out with both old and new friends.

NC State was a great experience 25 years ago. The university community made it feel like a new home. The on campus housing was amazing and encouraged relationship building. NC State Athletics is such a great atmosphere and celebrated with great passion. The school does a wonderful job tying in with the surrounding community of Raleigh. The academic programs are second to none and they really push their student body to be the best and industry leaders throughout the country.

I love going to North Carolina State University. As soon as I toured during my senior year of high school, I knew this was where I wanted to be. I love the overall community and support of all students and staff and the beautiful campus. My favorite part is how the campus is like our own little city. I wish tuition wasn't so costly but other than that, I love NC State!

I have loved my experience at NC State so far, and would recommend it to anyone. The classes and teachers are great, and the other faculty and administrators really seem to care about the students. The biggest thing that bothers me is that I think we have too many people. It can be hard to eat a peaceful meal at the dining hall, and almost every time I've tried to go to the library with any friends, we can't find even two seats next to each other in the entire library. So we try going to Talley student union and it's also completely crowded. I know it seems a great thing to be "the people's college" and let a lot of people in, but there's definitely a limit, and I think we've passed it, by quite a bit.

My overall experience with North Carolina State University has been very positive. The university offers strong academic programs that balance theory with real-world application, and I’ve found the professors to be knowledgeable, approachable, and genuinely invested in student success. The campus has a collaborative, welcoming atmosphere with many opportunities to get involved through clubs, research, and career development resources. I’ve also appreciated the support services such as advising and tutoring which have helped me stay on track academically. Overall, NC State has provided a challenging yet supportive environment that has helped me grow both academically and personally.
I really like that there are many different resources, clubs, and organizations at NC State. I do wish it was a more diverse school.

I am enjoying my first semester at NCSU. The campus is very nice and people are friendly and there is always lots going on. Transitioning here as an out of state student has been a bit challenging since the vast majority of students are in state and already have a community of friends. But after a couple of months I found my people.
The professors I have had in my first semester have been pretty good so far. The problem is that it is very hard to get into the classes you need to take since there are strict limitations on required classes and so many seats held for specific majors and year of seniority. It is like a full time job to try to manage wait lists to even fill your schedule to full time attendance.
The classes with the best rated professors are nearly impossible to get into and you get stuck with graduate students teaching who are often not good at all. It is frustrating if you are paying out of state tuition and getting stuck with terrible professors.

I love college, taking that next step in my education towards learning more about electrical engineering has been awesome. Every day (expect for exam days) I am excited to go to class and learn about the nuances that we take for granted in the technology we use. The social side of college has also been a highlight of my life. The bonds you can make when any weekend could be a surprise sleep over with your best friends is like no other. Even when school get rough you know you can rely on your friends to be there and support you. Genuinely as a whole there is very little I would like to change, sure less 8:30s, better professors, and fewer exams but these pale in comparison to the great aspects of college. Overall I am loving college and I am going to make the most of it!

I have enjoyed my first semester at NCSU. The student life is awesome. There are so many ways to get plugged into campus life. My dorm room is large. The food on campus is great. Classes are tough but professors are always willing to help students find resources to them success. Go Pack!

Overall I have greatly enjoyed my time at NC State! It is a university that truly feels like it has everything, even with many students attending the university there are still so many spaces available for work and studying. There is a large variety of clubs and organizations one can become involved with in order to find their people. I love the athletics here, going to games is definitely a big part of student life and its always fun, even when the team isn't always great. When it comes to academics I have felt a good mix of being challenged while also feeling like I am entirely capable of succeeding. While not every professor is great, I have had so many professors that have been so incredible and made my time at NC State an exceptionally positive one. I truly cannot recommend NC State enough.

I love everything about NCState and the Raleigh area. If there were more classes offered later in the day (not first thing in the morning) and if there wasn't a physical education requirement, that would be nice.
I love this college, I would love to see more student events and involvement on Centennial campus instead of it all being on main campus.

Strong STEM and Engineering Programs: NC State is renowned for its College of Engineering, which is consistently ranked among the best in the nation. Given your interest in Java programming and cybersecurity, you would likely find the Department of Computer Science, housed within the College of Engineering, to be a major highlight. The university offers robust curricula, cutting-edge research, and strong industry connections in these fields.
Research Opportunities: As a leading public research university and part of the Research Triangle (along with Duke University and UNC-Chapel Hill), NC State offers extensive research opportunities for undergraduate and graduate students. Centennial Campus is a unique model that integrates academic buildings with corporate and government research labs, providing students with direct access to internships and real-world projects.

ai_summary: At North Dakota State University, students generally appreciate the welcoming atmosphere and the sense of community , often mentioning how easy it is to feel like they belong. The campus is described as perfectly sized —large enough for diverse experiences but small enough to foster personal connections with peers and professors. Academically, reviews highlight the quality of instruction , with many professors described as helpful and caring, though some classes can be crowded. There are robust student services , including extensive resources for tutoring and extracurricular involvement. While students enjoy campus life and amenities like the wellness center and diverse dining options, some express frustration with parking and the sometimes mediocre dining hall experiences. NDSU is noted for its strong sense of school pride, particularly around athletics, which contributes to a vibrant student life .

reviews.text: Academics are fine and there are a lot of good resources for students, like tutoring, disability services, and a counseling center. The surrounding area also has a lot of great opportunities for internships and employment. My main complaint though: this campus is terrible for navigating in winter. They say it takes 10 minutes to cross campus in tours, but it's definitely longer than that. Bus routes through campus are not efficient for getting to most classes. There are a few sky ways, but not enough connecting relevant buildings. Sidewalks are often poorly maintained. Unless you have a car (and can find parking...) walking across campus in -40 temps with icy sidewalks is like trekking the Gulag. Buildings don't connect well or have any tunnels and there have been numerous occasions campus has remained open despite frostbite warnings. NDSU should better utilize the hybrid format for extreme weather.
I have had a good experience with all my classes, and student life. All the faculty has been very nice and welcoming.

It definitely is a great school that offers a ton of opportunities to learn and grow and expand you career preparedness. Especially in areas such as nursing and agriculture. There is definitely a huge shortage and big need for nursing and doctors, and NDSU is surrounded by plenty of hospitals in the area to provide the necessary hands on learning for those professions.

Its ok, I don't hate it, but it isn't amazing. There is a really good Newman Center there that was just built a few years ago. My dorm is alright; the teachers are good from what i can tell. I have met some friends that are all really nice so far.

Overall, NDSU has a very nice campus with most buildings in a walking distance for classes. Athletics are very good at NDSU and academics are also important. There is some stuff to do in the Fargo-Moorhead area that make weekends exciting.

I have not yet had any issues with professors besides the fact that a majority of professors in STEM class do not speak good English. I would like to see a change in the parking situations. Whenever there was tailgating the college sold parking spots in student lots to tailgating visitors, which was frustrating. The food on campus was good at the beginning of the year, then slowly decreased in quality.

I am loving living at NDSU. It has opened me up to so many new opportunities and helped me meet so many people my age. Living on campus has also taught me a lot of important life lessons in just the first moth, the most important one being that not everyone is going to or has to like you. In high school it felt like everyone had opinions or some kind of friendship with everyone but now being in collage most people don't even know each other. I have learned to put myself out there and talk to new people I realized that even when you enjoyed meeting people, they might not be the people you want to be friends with, it doesn't mean that there is anything wrong with you or them, but it is extremely different than any high school and way more fun!
North Dakota State University campus has a small town feel for being in Fargo. In the early fall the campus is pretty but freezing in the winter.

My experience at North Dakota State University has been really meaningful and has helped shape me both personally and professionally. From the moment I started, I felt like I was part of a supportive community that genuinely wants students to succeed. The faculty in the nursing program are incredibly knowledgeable and approachable—they push us to think critically while also being there when we need guidance or support.
I’ve also grown a lot outside the classroom. NDSU offers so many opportunities to get involved, whether through student organizations, volunteering, or leadership roles. Being surrounded by driven, like-minded students has motivated me to stay focused and keep pushing toward my goals.
Overall, NDSU has helped me build confidence, independence, and a deeper passion for nursing. I’m proud to be a Bison, and I know the education and experiences I’ve gained here will stay with me throughout my career.

NDSU has been great. I have been disappointed by the professors particularly in the Finance and other business classes. I've had many that should not even be allowed to instruct. They are their for research and get thrown into instructing when they are not good at it. Other than that the campus life is good. Good food, good sports, fun things to do, and caring people. West Dining Center is the best.

The campus is very modern and is a nice little "fake town" vibe. However the winter is brutal and not too much fun leaving the campus feeling dead. The academics are amazing and the professors very approachable. The food is really good at the dining centers.

Not only is the campus community dedicated to ensuring academic success, the community is so welcoming and tight-knit. I found so many friends both on and off campus and made so many connections. Bison football games and tailgates were a perfect way to show off school pride. I originally was upset I didn't get into my first choice school, but in attending NDSU I found that I was in the #1 place for me. I made so many friends, feel like I'm ahead of other architecture students who graduated from other universities in terms of education and experience, and my connections run far and wide now. The NDSU Wellness Center was and continues to be a great source of stress relief and tends to be my safe space on campus during hard times. The wonderful staff at NDSU have provided an education I used to think was so out of reach. If I could start all over again, I not only would still choose NDSU, but I'd consider it my #1 pick.

Being a commuter at North Dakota State University saved me money on my college expenses. Events such as the Student Involvement Expo hosted by NDSU gave me the opportunity to get involved on campus, and I recommend any prospective student to go to this event. I like the location of the campus; it is located next to Downtown Fargo so there is a lot of things to do outside of campus. The professors and academic departments are good, and the research opportunities are even better.

NDSU has provided me with new experiences and opportunities, such as greek life and other clubs. I have been able to successfully further my education through the very well planned out courses they offer. Some of the professors can be more difficult to learn from and require a more self-taught type of experience, but this can be seen at any college. I have felt welcomed and accepted across campus since my first day, and I am offered different opportunities each day. I enjoy the countless clubs that are offered to me, as well as on-campus events and activities that keep me involved on campus and throughout my community. I am thankful to have the opportunity to be surrounded by such a positive and supportive campus, and I hope my next three years will bring me further success and opportunities.

The community at NDSU is so unique! We have a strong sense of community with our alumni and the Fargo/Moorhead area. Since my freshman year, there have been some really cool additions to campus and I love seeing campus grow with new buildings and improvements!

ai_summary: Northeastern University stands out for its robust co-op program , which many students find integral to their academic and career success, offering significant real-world experience that enhances their employability. Academically, the courses are described as challenging but manageable, with a diverse range of subjects and generally committed professors. Students appreciate the opportunity for global experiences , including study abroad programs and a vibrant campus life, although some feel the social scene lacks the traditional college party atmosphere. Campus facilities, particularly housing, often draw criticism due to high demand and limited space . While Northeastern's Boston location provides a wealth of extracurricular and cultural opportunities, the university's high tuition and sometimes inconsistent administrative communication are noted concerns. Overall, students tend to feel that the experience is what they make of it, balancing academics, social life, and career preparation.

reviews.text: Overall it's been pretty good. I'm currently a freshman on the Oakland campus so my experience is difference from the students who started off on the boston campus. It's been such a unique experience over here. Since it's a mostly first year campus everyone here is around the same age and also just started college making it easy to befriend and socialize with others. Lots of on campus events and activities (That gives out free food too!). Limited courses here though so chose wisely when building your schedule. On campus services are great as well. Housing dorms are a bit old so that's a bummer. But beautiful and really safe campus. It's not too bad here especially if you're just spending a school year here like most students are.

I did not enjoy my time at Northeastern. Most professors only cared about their research and didn’t put much effort into their teaching. I can count on one hand the number of quality professors I had. The gen ed classes are taught by stressed out, exploited grad students. The student body is very cliquey and I struggled to make friends even though I’m extroverted. Social life is pretty nonexistent. I experienced more racism and misogyny here than I have in my entire life. In my first meeting with my academic advisor, she used the n word and didn’t apologize. Definitely avoid this school, especially if you are black. The anti-blackness was suffocating.
Northeastern’s brand name is also overrated. I have to constantly correct people who think I went to Northwestern. Northeastern’s name means nothing outside of New England. There are much better Boston area schools. Don’t waste your time here.
I enjoyed my time at northeastern. The co-op program was fantastic and really helped set myself up for my career after graduation.

My undergraduate experience at Northeastern University was both academically challenging and professionally enriching. One of the most valuable aspects of the university is its emphasis on experiential learning through the co-op program, which allowed me to apply classroom knowledge in real-world settings and gain meaningful professional experience. The faculty fostered a collaborative and engaging learning environment, and many courses encouraged critical thinking and practical problem solving. Overall, Northeastern provided a strong foundation that combined rigorous academics with practical preparation for future career and academic goals.

I love attending this university, the campus is beautiful and there are a lot of fun events to make new friends. I have found great friends from attending campus events and there are a lot clubs to join such as sports, music and cultural clubs. I am very interested in the schools squash program and the school has great professors, I’ve experienced hands on learning outside of the classroom. For example I worked with toddlers at Eli’s early learning for one of my human services course and i had a great experience and thankful for that opportunity!

I enjoyed aspects of Northeastern. I think the school really operates on a system of what you put in is what you will get out. So if you know what you want, or are willing to just try things, there are a lot of opportunities available.
Northeastern University has many opportunities to learn. Not just with co-ops but they have many interesting classes that bring disciplines together
amazing quality of education for the price we have to pay. I had some great co-op experiences that transformed my professional carreer.

It's been very nice so far. I just transferred here and it has been quite cold. I think that the school is very safe as NU has their own PD. Also, the school is very diverse and the people are all very cool. The orientation has been super fun and welcoming.
Northeastern University was a great college experience all around. The teachers were great and their co-op program was a key differentiator.

I am currently enrolled part-time at Northeastern University through the College of Professional Studies, pursuing an undergraduate certificate while balancing work and long-term professional goals. As a non-traditional student who lives off campus, my experience differs from that of a typical full-time undergraduate, but I have found Northeastern to be an excellent fit for my needs. I've appreciated how structured and straight forward my classes have been, and how approachable and fair professors are. Overall, my experience at Northeastern has been positive and has reinforced my commitment to continuing my education and pursuing a career in healthcare.

Northeastern is an amazing campus, and the co-op program truly gives you the opportunity to experience what working in a real-world setting feels like. It allows you to explore whether a certain career path is something you want to pursue in the future.
One downside is that Northeastern offers many opportunities such as co-ops and study abroad programs, which, while beneficial, can affect your social life. You may make close friends, then they leave for co-op or study abroad, and you have to start building new friendships again. This cycle can become overwhelming at times.
I have been very fortunate with housing and have received my top choices most of the time. However, I have heard other students express concerns about housing, including being placed in forced doubles or triples

In the heart of Boston. There’s so much to do outside of school. You meet amazing and very unique people from all around the world. The CO-OP program is crucial for success. Housing is a bit expensive and cramped but varys on building. Amazing area and amazing school.

Love it!! It was absolutely amazing to experience going to Northeastern’s London campus too. It gave me the opportunity to experience a British-style education system without worrying about transfer credits or any of the “typical” difficulties related to finding a study abroad program at a “typical” university.
Coming back to the Boston campus was a seamless transition and have thoroughly enjoyed life here as well.
Overall, I feel that the great array of opportunities provided at Northeastern with global experiences, co-ops, and other forms of experiential learning, gives you a profound and well rounded background propelling you to pursue future endeavors with a better gauge as to what it is you actually want to do in life.

I love this college,they admitted as I am meritocratic.I am already masters from my country and international sports champion, theatre award winning.Northeastern gave me International Impact award.But , I have financial strains . Please give me some scholarship.

ai_summary: Northern Arizona University is frequently praised for its welcoming campus atmosphere and beautiful location in Flagstaff, surrounded by nature and offering four seasons. The academic environment is generally characterized by small class sizes and approachable professors who care about student success, although some reviews note inconsistency in teaching quality. Students appreciate the various extracurricular activities and clubs, which enhance their college experience, yet some express concerns about limited housing options for upperclassmen and challenges with campus services, particularly regarding financial aid. While the tuition is considered affordable compared to other Arizona institutions, many reviews highlight ongoing issues with the cost of living in Flagstaff and the need for improved communication from the university to incoming students. Overall, NAU is described as a unique and diverse community that provides a supportive educational experience , although there are significant areas for improvement.

reviews.text: My first semester at NAU has been good. My classes are challenging but overall school has been a great experience. NAU is located in the ponderosa pines and a short drive to the Grand Canyon. Just about to begin the second semester. I am very happy with my choice.

My overall experience at NAU has been pretty good. I've been enjoying the weather up here as its from different from down back at the valley. I've enjoyed a lot of my classes and really like how each class offers SI lessons that help go deeper into information discussed in class.
Lot's of campus events happening all the time and lots of different choices on where to eat. The surrounding city is also very nice and walkable except for the mall, but there is a good bus system.
I love the way the faculty has been reaching out since I started my classes. I get to talk with my advisor about changes or updates to my current career goal.

I really like NAU because of its environment physically and socially. Most buildings at NAU are very updated and new, such as the Gym building which is a great place to study and work out. Flagstaff Arizona in general is absolutely stunning and you can get literally anywhere on foot or on a bike. The social life is great there aren't any cliques and it feels like everyone is attending to focus on school and get their degree.

I really enjoyed my time at NAU. Competing on the Division I tennis team was a central part of my experience and gave me the opportunity to train and compete at a high level, supported by some of the best tennis facilities in the country. I loved the Flagstaff area, the outdoor lifestyle, and the close-knit feel of the town. The professors were approachable and genuinely invested in their students, which made the academic experience both engaging and rewarding. Overall, NAU offered an exceptional balance of academics, athletics, and quality of life.

I attended NAU to get my master’s in education, but unfortunately, it did not happen! Which is why I do not recommend this school. I am a good person and I am a hard worker but NAU instructors and the counselor Amy Faull (I think her name was) My instructors and the counselor hated me for no reason or maybe it was because I’m Latina. The instructor was determined to fail me from day 1. She was so rude and always criticizing me. Amy Faull actually told me I should give up on my masters because trying would be a waste of money since I wouldn’t achieve anything anyway. Thanks NAU. Now I have debt, failed grades for my masters and can’t even get another masters now!

I truly enjoyed my academic experience at Northern Arizona University. I thought all my professors and courses were very good, both in-person and online. I feel like I learned a lot in my time there. I, however, did not personally enjoy the campus/Flagstaff. It is a gorgeous campus but I did not find that "fun college campus" experience to be present during my time. It could have just been the abnormally cold and long winter, but I found myself feeling 'stuck' inside my apartment a lot when nothing much was going on, on campus or due to weather shutdowns. Overall, quality of education was good but nothing remarkable about the school or campus itself.
It's an enjoyable school and the campus is beautiful, NAU is near a bunch of nice hiking spots too if you enjoy that

Northern Arizona University is a great college for people who enjoy the outdoors and living in a smaller city. I lived on campus all four years and really had a great experience. There were great academic opportunities, as well as extracurriculars. If you’re looking for a low-key college experience, NAU is for you.

Attending Northern Arizona University has been quite the newfound experience so far. As a college freshman I'm still learning a lot, while falling in love with the independent life NAU offers. However, life on NAU campus can be a real struggle at times.
Absolutely great experience in every aspect of the campus, professors, students & staff. I would recommend anyone thinking of attending.

I loved going to Northern Arizona University!
I love the care and resources this college gives and maintains to each student.

Overall, really great campus and great place for outdoor activities. I enjoy the mountains and forests. The only downside, truly, is the lack of ethnic food diversity. Filled with fast food and diner style stores.

When I visited NAU in November for a school campus trip, the experience felt very personal and meaningful to me. From the moment I stepped onto campus, the crisp fall air and the pine trees surrounding NAU created a calm and welcoming atmosphere. The seasonal change gave the campus a cozy, mountain-town feel that immediately stood out and made the visit memorable. Walking through campus, I felt comfortable and at ease.
The mix of natural scenery and modern buildings made the campus feel balanced—focused on academics while still prioritizing student well-being. I was especially impressed by how the campus blended into its surroundings, making it feel peaceful rather than crowded or overwhelming.
Another aspect that stood out to me was the sense of community. The tour guides were friendly and enthusiastic, and the students I passed seemed engaged and comfortable in their environment. Hearing about student life, clubs, and campus traditions made NAU feel welcoming and inclusive.

ai_summary: At Northern Illinois University (NIU), the academic experience is generally perceived as good, especially in fields like business and engineering, where the programs are well-structured. Students appreciate the supportive and dedicated faculty , though there are mixed opinions about specific professors and their engagement levels. The campus culture is vibrant, offering a variety of extracurricular activities, athletic events, and student organizations that foster community. However, some reviews express concerns about accessibility for disabled students, outdated facilities , and inconsistent academic support services. While campus life is lively, with a focus on social events, some students note a lack of diversity and safety issues. In terms of cost, the tuition is seen as relatively affordable, particularly for in-state students, though some express frustration over additional fees and housing costs. Overall, NIU offers a balance of academic and social opportunities, but students are encouraged to actively engage to maximize their experience.

reviews.text: My overall experience was good , I was a commute student due to the housing options and the prices , I think there should be more and better options than the one the school offers, also with that due to having a lot of commute students there should be more parking lots open to those students. The student life I would say is great, from all kinds of clubs to Greek life which I was also a part of . I love how different clubs and organizations that are multi cultural sell food and things from there culture not only to raise money for themselves but it’s also a great way to share and educate others about the club and organization they are part of. So overall I think this school is a great place to be a part of.

Overall, Northern Illinois is not a bad school. There is always something to do on campus so there is no worry about not feeling a sense of belonging. It is definitely a commuter school, and the people I talk to who live on campus do complain about them, but I cannot say much on that. Many great food choices on campus, and the dining halls are always clean. All my classes are nice too; some buildings though need some remodeling because they are too old. For example, one building is old that the air conditioning and heating are always broken. So, it felt freezing wearing two coats inside of the building. I am happy with my choice of school, and I would not consider transferring.

Northern Illinois University is a great school for connection and diversity among race, sexuality, gender identity, and disabilities (mental and physical). I have met so many people who have helped me find who I am, and continue to make me feel like I am a true Huskie.
im not actually a student here YET but i toured the campus as a prospective student and loved it. very good.

NIU is a nice school, living here obviously makes life easier with going to class, and the campus life is nice, one thing if you are looking for fun its the wrong place, there is nothing much to do out in DeKalb. The food served is alright but not on the healthy side, and not a wide variety of things.

Overall, I've had a pretty good experience at NIU. They offer a lot of support to all students to ensure academic and social success. I especially love that they have involvement fairs every semester to help people get more involved on campus.
Better food options. Jobs opportunities that can help and also get right away. Make the books free for all courses. I like the gym, the walking path, and stores near.

I like the close knit community feeling, recognizing faces in classes based on majors . I like that they offer Greek life opportunities. The food can be improved but it is not terrible by any means. I wish more people stayed and lived on campus which would create a more lively environment.

I am the second generation in my family to graduate from NIU and appreciated being awarded a legacy scholarship that was applied towards my first semester's tuition. I also found the professors to be very accommodating and easily approachable when needing to coordinate testing accommodations in the testing center after I was recovering from spinal surgery. I enjoyed participating in campus activities as a member of the Physical Therapy club, Occupational Therapy club, and Counseling club, where I was elected and performed roles as the treasure or secretary. I enjoyed being nominated and was honored to be inducted into the National Honors Society and for Phi Kappa Phi. NIU has continually upgraded the campus and classrooms to accommodate for state-of-the-art technologies and a changing student body, I would love to see an Occupational Therapy program offered at NIU in Dekalb, IL or at their Rockford, IL extension as our region is lacking opportunities for this professional degree.

Northern Illinois University is a very good school academically. They have a highly rated counseling program which mainly caught my eye when looking for colleges, and that is primarily why I attended. The campus is small but it has life to it and there is always student involvement opportunities. Overall, the campus and school itself is very good and it will be beneficial to anyone who chooses to attend.

My overall experience with Northern Illinois University has been positive. The campus offers a welcoming environment with a strong sense of community, and there are many opportunities for students to get involved both academically and socially. Professors are generally supportive and knowledgeable, and there are resources available to help students succeed. NIU provides a good balance of academics, student life, and support services, making it a solid choice for higher education.

Got my MBA as a part time commuter student. Fantastic professors, smart but humble students and great value for the cost. NIU still has a solid but underrated reputation in accounting and finance. A secret gem at a great price. Went on to have an amaze career in financial services.

My freshman year at Northern Illinois University has been a very humbling experience so far. I is very different from high school and a lot more responsibility. It’s all a learning experience, even outside of class. This first semester had really brought out a side of me I didn’t know existed. I’ve taken advantage of my freedom and made friends, which isn’t that easy at NIU. It’s a very small campus in my opinion and so there isn’t much to do. But, depending on the people you hang around and how you chose to spend your time it can be fun.
I like having my own dorm and living alone and getting to know what it's like to be independent and alone.

Northern Illinois University isn't the best fit for me personally as a commuter but I'm sure it is the right fit for others. More than half of my classes are online which works for my work schedule but next semester I don't have the option for as many online classes.

ai_summary: Northern Kentucky University (NKU) receives mixed reviews that highlight several key themes about the college experience. Academically, many students appreciate the small class sizes and the personal connections they can form with professors, who are typically described as friendly and supportive. The availability of diverse course offerings helps students find their academic path, although some express concerns over communication from administrative staff and the rigor of certain programs. Student life appears to be a prominent point of contention; while some enjoy the active student organizations and events, others note that the campus can feel quiet and uncrowded, especially on weekends. The campus itself is often described as safe and convenient, with modern upgrades and good maintenance, yet issues with food options and dorm living conditions were mentioned. Importantly, the affordability of tuition and financial aid opportunities are frequently seen as strong positives. Overall, while many students feel at home and supported at NKU, there are notable calls for improvements in student engagement and campus activities.

reviews.text: Pretty good and I would like to go again for any future education and any other inspirational experiences

NKU was a great school for my undergrad. However, I will say I wasn’t happiest typically with Uni Housing. Additionally, I wish NKU had more social events and parties. As a music major I had wonderful opportunities to travel and perform, definitely miss that part of NKU!
I like interacting with some students and some of the professors but not all of them. I also like how close it is it home.
Great experience, down to earth University. Friendly people, great professors, close to Cincinnati, best value!

Northern Kentucky University is a diverse student population. When I walk around campus I alwasys feels safe and comfortable. The food on campus is very good. The campus has various different places where you can get food, including the library, science center, and student center.
NKU is a great school with great campus staff, beautiful scenery and an amazing food. I would like to see an improvement in the construction because there is a lot and it seems very slow.

Northern kentucky university is a very welcoming school but you have to go out of your way to do anything. They do not make information or programs easy to find, most of the required school software is heavily outdated to the point of frustration, and the way student jobs work is also very disappointing. Also most the food is undercooked in the dining hall

Northern Kentucky University is an incredible college. They are the perfect mix of making sure everybody has a place to go to be accepted without forgetting to make sure the academics are taken seriously.

My experience their on campus is that everybody is involved with each other and that the courses are integrated with freshman to the upper classes. Safety is really good with the campus police everywhere and having jurisdiction within the whole county that the campus is in. There is nothing that I would like to see change.

This has been a wonderful opportunity for me to get away from my hometown. if you're someone who want's change, this is a good place. I came from somewhere where everybody knows everybody, and this is a very big change from that. It's also very inclusive. Love it!
I like how involve everyone is to their education. There is diversity and support for everyone. The profersor are great prople as well as the concelers they are always there to help you when nedded.

Listen; The professors and education itself are great. I genuinely love the SOTA program, and a great deal of the faculty and students here want you to succeed. But I genuinely only recommend this school if you are going part-time or just want a degree. NKU will screw you over financially every chance it gets. The financial department and student account services will rather steal your money (paying for services you did not ask for and making it nearly impossible for any recompense), and then have the audacity to tell you to take out student loans. I can give you photographic evidence and specific instances in DMs. Do not even THINK about dorming here, I have horror stories just from the application process. They will charge you 5 thousand JUST for applying- I did not even move in. I had to go through hell to decrease it to 2 thousand for a "cancellation fee". You're better off investing in yourself at Gateway or out of state. If I had the funds to sue, I would.

I would say this is a college where nothing is too note worthy. pretty bare bones, which can be a good thing for some. not a ton of campus engagement or events, kind of a get in and get out kind of school. Most professers here are amazing, especially the fulltime staff. I have had issues with some part time faculty. My previous nutrition professor, showed extream unprofessionalism to the point it became offensive, and I dropped the cours, and ill be taking it online next semester.

My overall experience at Northern Kentucky University has been positive. I really appreciate the supportive professors and the opportunities to learn and grow in my political science program. The campus is welcoming, and there are many resources for students to succeed.
One thing I would like to see improve is more support for students who need financial aid and academic guidance. Sometimes it can be challenging to manage everything, and extra resources would make the experience even better. Overall, NKU has been a good place for me to continue my education.

Transferring from Eastern Kentucky University, I was not only ecstatic for Northern Kentucky University, I was overjoyed! I know a bunch of my work friends and friends from earlier in my life who go to NKU and so far my time there has been nothing but the best. I love the feeling while being on campus and in class. It feels like a comforting and safe place with some of the nicest people I have met. The only thing I would want the school to change is the decor of the buildings and make it feel less concrete looking.

ai_summary: At Northwestern University, students frequently comment on the strong academic rigor and diverse curriculum , offering a broad selection of courses that allow exploration across different disciplines. Professors are noted for their dedication and willingness to assist students, though experiences can vary significantly, with some expressing frustration over teaching quality in certain cases. The social scene, while active with clubs and organizations, can be heavily influenced by Greek life, leading to a perception of exclusivity , especially for those not involved in it. Student life is described as competitive, which can contribute to stress and burnout, particularly among those in demanding majors. The campus is lauded for its beauty and proximity to Chicago, although some students find the dining options and on-campus housing lacking. Overall, Northwestern is recognized for its distinguished academic environment, but students highlight a need for more support services and a more vibrant social scene.

reviews.text: My experience at Northwestern set me up for my professional career not only through education but also networking, job and test preparation, and research opportunities.

People are wonderful-- it is such an immense privilege to learn from the stories of students around the world. Even within my first couple months on campus, I knew that my life had forever changed, and my worldview forever broadened. I have learned equally as much from my peers as my professors, and that's saying something because I have certainly learned a lot from my professors (particularly in the chem department).
Going away to college will always feel a little lonely and isolating at first, but at Northwestern, once you start making small talk with people and getting involved on campus, you'll find that you've made some of the best friends of your life: people who are kind, intelligent, driven, curious, and overall a gift to know.

I am approaching the end of my winter quarter at Northwestern University. I have loved the opportunity to connect with like-minded people in my field.
