As a communication sciences and disorders major (CSD), my community is extremely small and tight knit. The clubs, staff, and students in my major have made me feel welcomed. I would like to see more diversity in the field of speech and hearing sciences.

I appreciate many aspects of the college—especially the academic opportunities when they align well—but the lack of structured community has made the campus seem lonelier than expected. It's difficult to join student organizations. The college should not only students’ academic growth, but also their ability to form connections and feel a sense of belonging.

Overall, great school. Great academic with professors who actually care. Strong preprofessional culture. A lot of resources to do research, join labs, travel to conferences, etc. However, the campus is a bit dead and people are not really doing much.

I really enjoy the professors at Northwestern and how they make the material both rigorous and engaging. One area for improvement would be to increase job opportunities for students who are not eligible for Federal Work-Study, so that all students have a fair chance to gain valuable work experience on campus.

My time at Northwestern was easily one of the worst experiences I've ever had. I transferred out mid last year and could not be happier with that choice. NU professors care much more about their research and whatever books they're writing than they do about their students. Greek Life pretty much runs the campus and most of the professional clubs are difficult to get into unless you have friends in them. I didn't find the coursework to be particularly challenging but the lack of non-Greek social life really wore me down especially in the winter. Most students I met (that weren't in theatre or Greek Life) hated their NU experience and just stayed because the school is highly ranked. Yeah NU places well into top consulting firms but you're competing against other NU students for those spots. I now go to a liberal arts school and have the same opportunities without the cutthroat competition.

Northwestern was a great undergraduate experience for me. The academics were superb. The main problem was the performance culture on campus. To be fair to the university, it did a great job to try to encourage students to take care of their mental health with a variety of resources available for students. But the performance culture was giving lots of people stress, anxiety, depression, etc.

Despite the winters and how challenging the academics can be, I love Northwestern. The courses and professors push you to do the best you can do and there are so many career opportunities. Additionally, being in the Chicagoland area as a young person is incredibly fun, although the city can be very pricey.

A top university that is very balanced compared to other top 10 universities. This balance is in terms of everything: academics, sports, culture, location.... Everyone can fit into NU.
Since the NU has students and facilities for many different subjects (broader than those old money ivies), and it is near a metropolitan city full of opportunities, it is great for students who want to do many things not just one, and for students who are open to various opportunities.
Since NU is balanced, you do not see toxic phenomenon that many students are competing for one profession (for example, penn students fighting for finance).
Resources provided by the school are the very best. NU is a top target for almost all top companies in various industries. It is near Chicago downtown, so everything is very accessible.
It is one of the safest in top 10.
NU is trying hard for improvement, so it is getting better (many reconstructions...). However, it is uncertain after the cut.

Overall I love the environment of Northwestern, while the quarter system can be a real struggle especially in the Winter, most students are kind and extremely talented in what they may do whether in STEM or the Arts. I'm very proud to say I go here. The party scene is not super exciting but considering how close to Chicago campus is you could always search for better entertainment. Evanston is a beautiful college town with the varying stores and restaurants in reach. There are always options to take fascinating classes on the history of pirates. Diversity is certainly one of the worst things we struggle with but as long as we advocate for those around us things are sure to improve.

The academics are top-notch, and that's the most important thing for a school. I want my education to be worth the $80k I'm paying your institution, and at Northwestern, it is. The professors' care and the connections they help you make are invaluable in the real world. Partying, food, campus life, or anything else comes second in my mind. The only thing that matters to me is education, and that's why I chose Northwestern.

I liked the flexibility that we’re allowed in choosing courses, majors, the opportunities are endless for sure. I’d like the campus life to change, housing renovates, food should be better, parties are kinda mid

A good place to be overall. Very nice, walkable and bike friendly campus with decent public transportation. Home to some of the smartest and most interesting people you will ever meet. Some negatives were the fact that the social/party scene isn't quite as good as what you'd find at some other places, and the quarter system can get very stressful and depressing in the winter.

Life-altering college experience. Incredible classmates, professors, courses and student life. Northwestern's quarter system and wide breadth of academic disciplines allows students to gain a deeper understanding of multiple areas of interest. No other school offers that time of academic experience alongside a vibrant student life that can be pursued outside of the classroom

ai_summary: At Nova Southeastern University (NSU), students share diverse experiences that highlight both strengths and areas for improvement. Academically, many appreciate the rigorous programs and small class sizes that foster personal connections with professors, which enhances learning and mentorship opportunities. Student life is vibrant, with numerous clubs, events, and a close-knit community , although some students feel it's essential to be proactive in getting involved to fully enjoy the experience. The campus is well-maintained and offers a supportive environment , yet concerns about the quality of campus dining, housing shortages, and administrative challenges are common. Overall, while NSU provides a strong academic foundation and a welcoming atmosphere, there are calls for greater focus on addressing housing and food options to enhance the student experience further.

reviews.text: Overall I love this university. It not only has a great campus life but they offer so many things within the tuition that not a lot of people realize. This is a great school for academics as well and offers many clubs, programs and other things for you to succeed in what you do.

My experience at the university has been overall positive and rewarding. I’ve appreciated the supportive learning environment, especially professors who are knowledgeable, approachable, and genuinely invested in student success. The campus resources, including academic advising and tutoring services, have also been helpful in staying on track. I’ve enjoyed meeting people from different backgrounds, which has made the experience more engaging and broadened my perspective. One thing I would like to see improved is clearer communication about deadlines, events, and available opportunities, as well as more flexible scheduling options for students who work. Overall, the university has provided a solid academic foundation while also helping me grow personally and professionally.
I really enjoyed my experience at NSU and would recommend anyone who could afford it to attend. All my professors were friendly and eager to teach.

The school itself looks pretty but it’s overpriced and over hyped. The best thing to do on weekend is leave and go to downtown Ft. Lauderdale and visit clubs that are also more expensive than they should be. It gets pretty boring on weekends and the only other thing to do is go to the beach

Nova Southeastern University has personally served me well. As a student who wanted to join their nursing program, taking my prerequisites there gave me a step up. The staff are always friendly and available, the campus is clean and maintained well, and there are several resources readily available at all times.
I want to understand first before deciding whethI want to understand first before deciding whether I need it or not.er I need it or not.
Great university! Amazing support system and connections with small class sizes and opportunities. Many opportunities to grow and het involved in extracurriculars. Lots of diversity!

My experience at Nova Southeastern University has been largely positive. The campus is modern and well-maintained, and there’s a strong sense of community among students and faculty. I appreciated the accessibility of professors—they are generally supportive and willing to help students succeed academically. NSU also offers a variety of programs and extracurricular opportunities that allow students to explore their interests outside the classroom.

Nova Southeastern provides decent academics but the campus life leads to there being little to no school spirit. Very few people attend the school events and besides the welcome weeks the only events hosted are by individual clubs. It makes the school feel very divided and not connected at all. I would also like to see more dining options. As someone with a chronic illness that struggles with food having the few dining options makes it hard for me to get all the necessary nutrition. There is no change or excitement with the food and all of it is subpar at best.
I love nova and everything about the food the teachers and homework the new friends I keep on finding everyday

Nova Southeastern University stands out for its vibrant traditions, research excellence, and strong community values. Embracing Shark pride through events like Sharkapalooza, CommunityFest, and “fins up” shows genuine school spirit. NSU’s R1 research designation highlights its academic prestige, especially in health sciences and environmental studies. Programs like U-RISE and Razor’s Edge offer hands-on opportunities for growth and leadership. Commitment to community initiatives, such as All FINS In, reflects the school’s mission to create meaningful impact beyond campus. Aligning personal goals with NSU’s values, traditions, and research focus—while participating authentically—demonstrates deep appreciation for what it means to be part of the Shark family.
Nova is really good, some of us do feel that it is a little expensive to receive the little things that we do.

Nova is an amazing school. Though it can be very disorganized at times. For example, I'm a sophmore living in housing for Juniors and Seniors, which has put a great amount of financial stress on me. I know I'm not the first or last that will say this. They don't accommodate for things being that I'm in Razors Edge Leadership. Additionally, towards the end of my first semester I finally felt a home at NSU. But having to worry about my financial aspect has really hindered me. Nova could offer more scholarships as well to help students.
Overall, NSU provides quality education in an efficient and proactive manner. I’ve had some questionable professors/advisors but all together i’ve obtained a positive experience.

Nova is a good school but definitely not the best for the amount of money you will be paying. The teachers are mediocre and its really a toss up on if your teacher is going to be good or if they're going to be horrible at teaching. Professors are knowledgeable but some know how to explain while others don't. The programs at the school are average what you would expect to see at a college what is really missing is the night life and party life if you're into that you won't find that here which might be a plus or a downside depending on the person. Also, academics are mostly pointed towards the medical field so if you are interested in other fields they still do teach them there but with the huge dentistry and nursing buildings compared to taking computer science classes right next door to my psychology class you can see where most of the budget goes in my opinion and there's nothing wrong with that! but you should keep that in mind when deciding what school to apply to
Oakland City is a great school. The professors, students, and staff are all great people that have great relationships with Jesus Christ. They inspire me to continue my walk with God.

Sure! Here's a 100-word example you can adapt based on your actual experience in Oakland City:
---
Oakland City offered me a unique blend of urban culture and tight-knit community charm. I explored its vibrant street art, local coffee shops, and diverse food scene—each reflecting the city’s rich cultural roots. The people were welcoming, and the city felt alive with creativity and activism. I appreciated the walkability and the sense of history around every corner. From casual conversations with locals to community events, I felt connected. While some areas reflected economic challenges, the city’s resilience and spirit stood out. My experience in Oakland City was both eye-opening and inspiring—a true testament to community strength and cultural depth.

The university itself is small, in a rural area of ​​Indiana. But the university has a lot of diversity of students with people from countries all over the world. We have high level sports. The facilities are not bad at all and every year they improve a lot. The university offers transportation if you need it, whether to the bank, airport...

I have only been at Oakland City for a few months but am enjoying the new friendships I am gaining each day. I have met people here that I would have never met if it wasn't for OCU. I am a member of the OCU Baseball team and spend a lot of my time working out with the team or in the gym working on my own. My classes first semester were good and I am excited to continue taking more courses in my major.

Oakland City University offers a unique small-campus experience that fosters a close-knit, welcoming community for its students. The intimate campus setting creates an environment where everyone knows each other, building a sense of belonging and support. This atmosphere extends to the university's sports teams, which operate like a family, with athletes forming lifelong bonds and receiving personalized attention from coaches and staff. The small size of the campus ensures that student-athletes can balance academics and athletics seamlessly while feeling valued and supported in their pursuits. At Oakland City University, the blend of community spirit and athletic excellence makes it an ideal place for students seeking a personalized college experience with strong team camaraderie.
My time here at OCU has been up and down, I’ve had good times and bad times but overall if your a quiet person who likes peace and quiet and not many things going on this would be the perfect place!

Oakland City University is an amazing Christian Campus. I love it there. There is Chapel every Wednesday at 11am. The dorms are ok, especially the one that I am living in now. The other residence halls are amazing. Oakland City University has a softball team, men and women tennis, men and women basketball, cross country, and are getting a football team next semester.

My time at OCU was amazing, I learned a lot about God and furthered my education. It was a small campus so you got to know everybody and get to know the teachers one on one unlike other colleges. I was easy to find help if needed and speak up on things you think would make it a better experience.

Oakland City University is the most disgusting and discriminatory school I have ever encountered. This school uses religion as an excuse to infringe on the rights of the students, especially lgbtq students. They more often than not refuse to take student complaints into consideration when it comes to professors and how classrooms and grades are handled. The town of Oakland City is known to be home to many racists. Many of the universities foreign students experienced racism around and on campus and their complaints were rarely taken seriously. As someone who was on the student government where these issues were brought up, I saw first hand how quickly they were dismissed. However, whenever it was time for the holiday post cards, OCU loved to pick out the “diverse” students. I chose OCU because it was the only school I could afford. After having graduated, If I could go back and change my decision, I would have taken out as many loans as it took to receive my education elsewhere.
I really like the small class sizes and individual attention. My professors genuinely care about my success. Administration and financial aid services are easy to work with.

Oakland City University is a place in which I have truly met lifetime friends. Most professors are kind and help you on a personal level. But, there has been a few instances in which I was truly ashamed to call this my future alma mater. True hate, professors who do not care, and greed high up have made an absolutely frustrating experience. Also, it’s just way too expensive.
I have had the pleasure of meeting several excellent instructors within the school. The instructors are very knowledgeable, and eager to help students be successful.

My experience at Oakland City University so far has been really great. The classes that I am taking aren't that stressful. The students and professors here are really respectful to all of us. It is a beautiful campus and it makes me feel like I am around good people where we are in a Christian atmosphere. Oakland City University has a lot of things that they offer. I wanted to come to OCU because of what they offered. Oakland City University helps give money to those that are in need of financial help and it's just a great college to attend. Even though the town is small, the school is in a great place and it keeps building and becoming bigger every year. The college is a Christian school and the professors are always telling us to go to the chapel and why we should tell others about the school, which I find really amazing. They offer classes that teach about the Bible and God, which is also really amazing. The atmosphere here is beyond great.

I love attending this college. My professors go out of their way to make sure that I understand the material. They help me with anything that I would need help with. That sit down before or after classes to help students get a better understanding of the material. The best educational program that I have seen. I am truly grateful to be attending such an amazing program at Oakland City University!

Oakland city university so far has been a open eye experience. It has taught me many things, for example their are people always trying to help you succeed. It is a friendly school with many amazing staff. The campus is very eco friendly and very quite. This is a benefit because it allow you to focus on your studies. My favorite part is that you make friends along the way and met people who will always be their for you.

ai_summary: Oakland University offers a supportive and engaging academic environment , appreciated for its smaller campus that fosters a sense of community among students. Many reviews highlight the university's excellent professors who are committed to student success, though experiences with certain instructors can vary. The campus is noted for its beauty and safety , with diverse student organizations providing ample opportunities for involvement, especially for commuters. However, some students feel the social scene is quiet, and there are concerns about the quality of dining options and challenges related to financial aid processes. Overall, Oakland University is seen as a great choice for students looking for personal attention in their education while maintaining a balance between campus life and academic rigor.

reviews.text: My experience at Oakland University was okay. I did not do so well the first time I attended OU, so technically I'm a readmit student, but I did transfer credits from a community college. I did like my experience overall, but my academic adviser this time around has been really helpful, and it's more of me getting used to the environment again, but some of the professors could be better at OU.
OU has been welcoming and homey. I commute. It offers a variety of classes and I’ve been able to line them up making for a nice schedule.
Everyone there was so nice. Very diverse school. I was a psych major and sociology major and I loved my professors!

Most of campus is updated and well thought out. There are plenty of parking lots for students to park in. The buildings are cleaned well. The professors want you to succeed and it creates a positive atmosphere.

My overall experience with Oakland University has been positive. Even though I’ve only been there a few times with my school, each visit was fun and welcoming. The campus felt nice and comfortable, and I enjoyed being there with my classmates. From what I’ve seen so far, Oakland University seems like a great place to learn and explore new opportunities.

Oakland University has been very good to me as a transfer student (currently a senior). My professors for the most part have been great. There’s clubs to join ( I joined the National Society of Leadership and Success). Also, there’s other clubs such as a Swiftie Club, there’s a Best Buddies chapter at OU, etc. I highly recommend OU to everyone who’s interested in going.
It was nice as a backpacking school but campus life is pretty mellow. Also from the professors I’ve experienced none are very enthusiastic about teaching.

(My Experience at Oakland University)
-Marcus Jackson-
Dec 8, 2025
As a 20-year-old growing student, my time at Oakland University has been an eye opening moment for me as a transformative journey. Located in Rochester, Michigan, or auburn hills, Oakland University has provided me with a chance at evaluation with a supportive and inclusive environment to grow academically, professionally, and personally which is meant to turn into a long term career and to teach students to take care of business but not only themselves also.
Being part of the Oakland University made me enhanced as a man and a role model to my son and brother and hopefully my sister in the system this community has also allowed me to engage in various extracurricular activities and organizations. These experiences have not only enriched my academic journey and opened my thought process but also helped me build lasting connections with peers who share similar interests.

My overall experience at Oakland University has been positive. The campus is clean, easy to navigate, and offers a supportive atmosphere for students. Professors in my major have been very knowledgeable and willing to help when needed, and I feel like I’m gaining real skills for my future career. I also appreciate the resources available like tutoring, career services, and student events that help you feel more connected.
One thing I would like to see improve is better communication and consistency across different departments, especially when it comes to advising and scheduling classes required for graduation. Parking can also be stressful at busy times. Overall, OU has given me great opportunities to learn, grow, and balance school with my life outside of class.

Oakland University has been the best school I ever been to. It’s always been my dream to go to college but it’s so hard to afford it. I come from a low income household, and financially already doesn’t cover a lot. But putting that aside, I met amazing people and have already learned so much. My professors made everything so easy to learn. I like the food I like the beautiful campus and honestly I recommend anything doesn’t change it all.

Oakland University is a very safe campus! It is ranked one of the safest campuses in the state, and even in the world. The food here is delicious, in my opinion, and there are plenty of different options for everyone! I have had great experiences with professors so far and have not heard of any issues at all. The hardest part of this university is the student life...because there is none. It is a highly commuter school, so if you are living on campus, this would be harder. Weekends are completely EMPTY and there is basically nothing to do at all on campus. I personally do not have a car, which has made it very difficult, since there is nothing to do on campus but a lot of different places to go to outside of campus. This school has some great factors to it, but it is definitely more for introverted people who like to be alone a lot of the time.

I’m a music student at Oakland University, and I could not be happier with the education I am receiving. The professors are incredibly helpful, and I feel like I’m growing as a musician and an artist every week. I would recommend this school to anyone who is looking to become a serious musician!

My time at Oakland University has been a really positive experience overall. The campus has a welcoming atmosphere, and the professors are passionate about what they teach and genuinely care about student success. I’ve appreciated the variety of student organizations and events that make it easy to get involved and feel part of the community. The campus itself is beautiful, especially in the fall, and there are plenty of quiet spots to study or relax between classes.
One thing I’d like to see improve is more parking availability and better communication about campus resources—sometimes it takes a while to find the right information or service you need. Expanding dining options and having longer hours in certain buildings would also make student life even better.
Overall, Oakland University provides a strong education and a supportive environment that helps students grow both academically and personally.
I like it its diverse and has a really good engineering program. It has good advisors and things like that.

So far, as a commuting student, I’ve enjoyed my time at Oakland University. In fact, I actually very much would consider moving onto campus sometime in my college career. It’s very welcoming, and I feel the overall community is very positive. As far as Academics go, the school is top notch, as I feel I truly am learning a lot from my professors.

ai_summary: Ohio University is recognized for its beautiful campus and supportive community , which many reviewers describe as feeling like home. The diverse range of academic programs and friendly professors who genuinely care about student success are frequently highlighted. Students appreciate the number of clubs and organizations available, contributing to a vibrant student life . However, some students express concerns about the lack of adequate support services, especially for issues like financial assistance and disability resources. While the campus atmosphere is lively and the town of Athens offers a unique charm , it is noted that the social scene can be heavily centered around partying, which might not be suitable for everyone. Additionally, students mention a need for improved diversity and inclusion on campus. Overall, students enjoy the balance of academics and social life at Ohio University, but some express a desire for better management of resources and support systems.

reviews.text: My overall experience at Ohio University, Athens, Ohio, has been amazing and very educational. Once I visited the campus, I fell in love with it, how it is laid out, the distance to everything, and how beautiful the campus itself is. My classes so far have been great, all of the professors are nice and willing to help you out no matter what. I have met so many people who have helped me and made lifelong relationships.

I love the atmosphere of the college, the campus is beautiful, the people are all friendly and kind and cheerful, and the employees are very helpful. There are a few things that could be improved upon, like expanding the bus route to go through south green, but overall I really enjoy attending this school.

I really liked the atmosphere of the Athens Campus. It is a small college town and everything is close by which helped me to commute easily to buy anything. During the Fest season, students and everyone have a great time and enjoy the moment to its fullest.

I started at OU in 1985 and lived in the dorms for 2 years (sooo much fun!) before moving to an off-campus house where we partied every weekend. I made friends surprisingly easily, and I'm still close with 6 of them -- friends for life! Back then, OU was ranked the best party school in the USA, and it earned that reputation easily. We partied 3 nights a week and sacrificed a lot of sleep the rest of the week to keep our grades up, but it was worth it. My major was Political Science with a minor in Journalism. My writing dramatically improved over just 2 years because of the great professors at the Scripps School of Journalism, which helped me move up the ladder quickly after I moved to Washington, D.C. after graduation to take an internship on Capitol Hill. 3 years later I was working in the White House as a domestic policy advisor to President Clinton, and now I'm a professor living in Southern California. I've had a great life, and OU played a huge role in paving that road.

I enjoyed the supportive atmosphere at Ohio University. I like the academic opportunities. Professors are available to students. Career resources are great. I wish there were better resources for mental health support. I wish there were cheaper housing options.

My experience at Ohio University has been nothing but a curse. The student life here sucks, the greek life sucks, and the sports here are low-quality. Im a current out-of-state third-year student here and so far, there is basically nothing good I have to say about this school. I have been bullied, excluded, made so many enemies, been verbally harassed and discriminated. This school just makes you go mentally insane. If you are an ethnic minority, and interested in Ohio University, I am WARNING you, DO NOT even think about this school. People here are very cliquey and making friends here is nearly impossible. You pretty much have to be the fake version of yourself in order to thrive here. If y'all are interested in rushing, go to a different school because greek life quality here is horrible and VERY cliquey. OU is a school for low-income students. Once I graduate, I'm NEVER stepping foot in Athens, Ohio ever again, even if Im invited back for an OU alumni event, I'll bluntly decline.
Great, comfortable, affordable, and small town vibes! I would like more classes to be offered pertaining to expressive arts therapy techniques.

Ohio University is a great school, Athens is small but has a lot to offer students living in the area. You'll be surprised by the diversity of offerings at the local grocers, for a small rural city in Ohio there are lots of international and food-sensitive options both at the grocer and on the menus at local restaurants. Be aware if you have severe or very common food allergens/intolerances you will likely want to avoid living on campus/the dining halls - options are very limited (and many of the "safe" alternatives they have are low quality) and it's very hard to get out of meal plan requirements if you're living in the dorms. Off-campus living costs around the same, especially if you cook part or most of the week.

My experience at Ohio University has been incredible and has great personal significance to me. I have really enjoyed the community atmosphere at OU because everyone seems to want to succeed around here. I have been active in Greek life and other student organizations, which have helped me become a better leader and have provided me with friendships that will last a lifetime. I have also really enjoyed the campus at OU because it’s beautiful and has many traditions that are unique to OU. I think that if I could change anything at OU, I would want to have more opportunities for networking to be brought to campus and additional mental health services to be offered to students during stressful times of the semester. I have grown both personally and professionally at OU.

I graduated in 1973 so my review is somewhat in the rear view mirror. For the most part the campus has remained unchanged except for the demolition of the Old Baker Center. That was a grand old building with a marble lobby. The 1804 room overlooked Washington Street. It had a grand piano and was extremely comfortable. In the basement was the Frontier Room. The Frontier Room was sort of a night club. I saw Jerry Jeff Walker and Doc Watson there. You could get a pitcher of beer for a dollar. The wall and steps were the heart of OU.
The cafeteria food in Shively Hall was terrible. I’ve eaten in Shively recently and the food has improved! As a freshman I lived in Gamertsfelder with two roommates.
The most distressing thing is how OU has fallen in academic prestige. OU used to be a national university and attracted many students from the East. It typically ranked in the top 100 schools in the country. OU ranked above Ohio State. It has turned into primarily a nursing school.

I started Ohio University online at the start of spring semester as a transfer student. Which honestly was the best choice I could have made. I got to come home and be close to my family and do school work online without worry about making it to class on time. It also gives the chance to work some more hours to try and earn some money. I have loved Ohio University online so far and I am so excited to continue the rest of my education with them.
OHIO truly shaped the best years of my life. Between the academics, student life, and social opportunities, it offered an experience I truly believe I wouldn't get anywhere else.

Overall wonderful experience! Beautiful campus, a place where there’s something for everyone. You don’t need to change who you are to fit in. Professors are happy to help during office hours, highly recommend since the lectures are larger. Take advantage of hocking hills nearby and don’t be afraid to be yourself!!

I decided to go to OU to receive my ASN to BSN. I chose this college because it was all online and as I was working full time as an RN I didn’t want to take any in person classes. You could take the ASN to BSN as fast or slow as you wanted which was convenient.

I just got to OU this spring semester as a transfer student. Ohio University has been amazing to me in so many ways. I was nervous about a big campus. However, the professors do an amazing job of teaching very well, even with the 18:1 student-to-faculty ratio. The students and local people are all very welcoming and kind. If you're looking for a place to feel welcomed, get a great education, and have fun, OU is the place to be. I am forever grateful I made the decision to be a Bobcat!

ai_summary: Oklahoma City University (OCU) has a distinctively supportive and close-knit community , particularly appreciated among students in its strong performing arts programs , like music and dance. The small class sizes foster strong relationships between students and professors, allowing for individualized attention and a more engaged learning environment, which many students find enhances their academic experience. However, reviews also indicate concerns about the cost of tuition, with some describing it as high relative to facilities and services. Overall, while OCU offers a beautiful campus and a diverse student body , some students mention issues with organization and dining services, as well as the need for improved transportation options. The community atmosphere, characterized by friendliness and inclusion, seems to be a major draw for prospective students.

reviews.text: I enjoy my time at Oklahoma City University but I think the only issue I find is the small campus. There's not a whole lot to do or see when on campus but when we have organizations have their event weeks or student life events, it becomes more lively.

I have had an unforgettable experience. It has truly morphed me into an adult I can be proud of. Obviously, no school is perfect. The housing, food, and diversity are the biggest things that need to be improved; however, the student life and campus activities make up for that. The highest caliber of students learned how to become better as people and humans in their designated major area.

I really like OCU! The housing options are alright, but not great. However, the professors are incredible and make class interesting and exciting. We have three sources of food on campus, the Caf, Kitchen 1904, and Alvins! The food is what you'd expect of a college campus, not incredible, however the staff is always warm and welcoming!

My experience so far at this institution has proven that what you put into college is what you get out of it as a whole. If you just do class and nothing around campus then you will get and education but perhaps not the experiences that help grow character. I have found that the more I enrich my community the more I have grown. OCU is built on its character and its community, the people make the school.

The school have great instructors, resources, high exam ratings and great faculty, always willing to help student achieve their dreams.
I will the school to allow and give students that for some reason could not pass courses, to be given the chance to retake it and not to be expelled or withdraw from the program. I will be very grateful if this policy can be implemented.

Growing up my family moved quite a bit so I had to move school districts a bit too. In my experience I found it hard to make friends and was more introverted. When I first began my journey at OCU I thought it would be the same as always. I thought that it would take a while for me to make friends and I thought that I would be alone for the most part. At OCU they did a welcome week called "Stars Week" which allowed us to get to know one another in small groups. With the welcome I was able to make friends very easily and I have already feel like I belong at the school. Even though I am only a commuter I found OCU to be a very fun and welcoming. I love the campus and the people.

I liked how intense the performance areas were, but you are at the mercy of the professors. Some are experts in the field but others are stuck in 20th century theatre and musical theatre, and don't always see the direction of where the industry is moving. I do think creatively you are fulfilled, with classes in the arts, homework involving practicing, and being cast in shows during the semester. If you want the "traditional college experience", don't go here. 95% of the people here came for the specific program, not the party life.

OCU has been fantastic! They have helped me through every step required for admission including the application, student aid and contacts for transcript requests. They assigned a counselor for enrollment and another for student aid. My experience thus far has been exemplary. I highly recommend OCU. They are simply the best of the best.
I just love it here so much and I would do anything to stay here at school for forever and all the people are cool
I love it here! I enjoy the small school experience, I think it helps me stay more focused in classes because I feel like the professor actually pays attention to me.

Oklahoma City University is a truly incredible school. I am fortunate enough to be a music theater student and the facilities are beyond incredible. There is deep pride from the alumni, and through the incredible generosity of people connected to the university we have one of the best rated music schools in the country, with our music building being the newest technology and creating the standard for music schools being build all over the world. The faculty are truly incredible and dedicated to the support and success of each individual student. Class sizes are very small and focus on the individual and many lessons are private with the teacher that has been specifically matched to each student based on their needs. There is an exceptional level of dedication and drive from most of the students attending each school within the university, and there is no school more excited to welcome and talk to prospective students. The community is rich, with everyone truly valuing each other.
I loved the small classes, and attentive professors. A small campus in a city atmosphere was exactly what I needed after graduating from a very large high school.

My first semester at Oklahoma City University was a tough one. I was dealing with a lot of mental health troubles like anxiety and depression. The professors though were very understanding and provided me resources to help. Freshman dorms are very small so if you are claustrophobic I would not recommend. The school is very small so you will see people you know all of the time. So if you are looking for a big school this is not the place for you. Food is okay. The dining hall has the basic options like pizza and cheese burgers and we have a diner place on campus called kitchen 1904 which is pretty good and has better options. Overall I would give this school a 3 out of 5. A lot of people love it and Im sure if I was not going through my mental health troubles I would be all for it as well. But I think with Oklahoma City University you will either love it fully or hate it. There is really not and in between.

No matter what department, school, or major a student is apart of, the professors always have your best interest at heart and only want to see you succeed. All of the faculty and professors care so much for their students and are more than willing to help their students when needed.

I have really enjoyed being at OCU. I feel like everyone there is extremely friendly and I am able to cooperate and communicate with my professors well. I also feel like it is a great learning environment.

ai_summary: Oklahoma State University has a welcoming and friendly campus atmosphere that many students describe as feeling like home. The school offers a wide range of academic programs and has supportive professors who genuinely care about students' success. Student life is vibrant, with plenty of events, clubs, and activities that foster a sense of community , although some students feel that it can be overly centered on Greek life. The campus is noted for its beauty and has a large student union with diverse dining options . However, parking can be a challenge, and the surrounding town of Stillwater may not offer much beyond campus activities. Overall, Oklahoma State University is seen as a place where students can thrive academically and socially, but the experience may vary based on individual backgrounds and interests.

reviews.text: My time at OSU so far has been amazing! I am an out of state student originally living in California. The move was hard at first, but I was able to quickly find a community here in Oklahoma. Everyone here was always checking in on me which made my transition so much easier! There are always thing happening around campus and many great services that students are able to access like tutoring, gym, etc.

Oklahoma state university has been great experience. It has transparent professors. The classes are smooth, and interesting. The student life is great. There's always something to do on campus. The campus is very walkable making it easy to get around.

Oklahoma State University is a wonderful campus full of inclusion, opportunities, and positivity. Driven by excellence, the school prides itself on the achievements of the students. Throughout my experience at Oklahoma State University I have grown in my leadership, pursued my passions, and achieved high academics.

It is an extremely large, spacious, and well-ventilated environment. The students are very diverse, which leads to many interesting interactions. College life on campus is also really thrilling if you are willing and have enough time to experience it all.

Oklahoma State University is a pretty standard place. I have made a lot of worthwhile connections and friends. I really do think with the major and education I have and will continue to receive will help me so much with my future.
I will be attending there most likely, but I haven't yet. I took a tour there and it is a great campus to attend college at.
They are very friendly and help you ensure you have everything you need to be successful. There is a sense of community and many ways to be involved.
My experience with OSU has been pretty good, I like that the campus has a lot of ways to help students in many different ways and I would want the price of food to be cheaper.
It has been an educational and personally beneficial experience. I have been able to develop interest in certain areas of my studies, and then follow paths to pursue those studies.

I am currently attending, and I am having a great experience. I am majoring in architecture, and the architecture program is fantastic. I feel like I belong, and I am getting a great education. The sports atmosphere is great even if the teams aren't doing well. The food needs to be better, but the resources available are great. Social life is not amazing, but I have a small group of friends that gets together. Party scene is not fantastic, but there is a lot of other options for social outings, like going line dancing.

My overall experience with experience with Oklahoma State University has been good. With me being a first-generation student and only living 2 hours from home allows me to be able to come home on some weekend and tell my family about how classes and college itself is treating me.
So far it's been an amazing experience especially in the education department! Full of passionate and hardworking professors that want to see their students succeed!

Absolute Nightmare – Don’t Waste Your Time
This campus is a toxic hellhole. Sexual harassment was everywhere—from classrooms to clubs—and dorm staff bullied me when I tried to defend myself. Stuff got stolen, people knocked on my door at all hours, and I was constantly on edge just to survive. The student body is shallow, immature, and high-school-level obsessed with looks. If you’re a model, ambitious, or actually want to do something with your life, don’t even bother. Classes are boring, teachers are hit-or-miss, food is awful, and the weather sucks. I lost 30 pounds in two months from stress. Avoid at all costs.

What i actually like about Oklahoma university are it diversity, educational background and atheletic performance.
What i will love to change in the school is the mode of application by trying to give international student athlete a chance to a fee waiver and also a full scholarship
I joined a multicultural sorority that helped me find my place at Okstate. I’d like to see the graphic design program change.

ai_summary: Old Dominion University offers a diverse environment that many students appreciate, fostering connections across different backgrounds. Academically, experiences vary; while some professors are highly praised for their engagement and support, others are described as less effective, leading to mixed reviews on the overall quality of teaching. Students find value in the numerous programs and activities available, which enhance campus life and provide a sense of community. However, there are concerns regarding the availability and quality of advising, and dining options have received both criticism and praise. The campus's safety has been a topic of discussion, with some feeling secure while others express apprehension due to incidents in the surrounding area. Overall, students enjoy a wide range of extracurricular opportunities , but experiences may depend significantly on personal initiative and specific academic pathways pursued.

reviews.text: Old dominion university has been a pretty good school. I am a commuter so I am only on campus when I go to class, but I can say that the teachers are great and want their students to succeed.

My overall experience with Old Dominion University has been positive and rewarding. Since starting at ODU, I have found the academic environment to be supportive and engaging, with courses that encourage critical thinking and real‑world application. The faculty have been knowledgeable and approachable, making it easier to ask questions and stay motivated. ODU has also provided valuable opportunities to grow beyond the classroom. Through involvement in coursework and exposure to public service–focused learning, I have been able to clarify my academic and career goals, particularly my interest in government and public policy. Overall, Old Dominion University has been a strong foundation for my education and personal development, and it has helped prepare me for future academic and professional opportunities.

My overall experience at Old Dominion University has been magnificent. The campus is very big with a lot of different things to look at and multiple different buildings for people's majors, especially mine. The students and the professors at this college are really friendly and want to see their students succeed in their careers. Overall, this college is one of the best colleges to attend to help kick off students' careers.

My experience at Old Dominion University has been both challenging and rewarding. I appreciate the supportive academic environment, especially the accessibility of professors who are willing to help students succeed both inside and outside the classroom. ODU offers a strong sense of community, and I value the diversity on campus, which allows students from many backgrounds to feel seen and heard. The campus provides helpful resources such as tutoring, advising, and student organizations that support academic growth and personal development.One area I would like to see improved is increased communication and efficiency within certain administrative offices, as processes such as financial aid and advising can sometimes feel overwhelming or unclear.
Current freshman learning his way but so far great campus with wonderful teachers and open minded people coming from many different areas around the world.

I transferred from ODU after my first year due to mental health, and the lack of mental health resources the school had to offer. The school is so big you can never form any type of relationships with the professors, they have 100+ other students to worry about, you're just a small drop in the ocean. It's a large commuter school, so there wasn't much to do and campus events were very sparsely attended despite the large population. My professors were decent, but I don't think any of them enjoyed their job and you could tell. The price is outrageous and they will screw you over for a check. Outside of the dining hall the food is good and the meal plan is decent. There is nothing impressive about ODU, even the campus is incredibly sad. They could invest in some lawn maintenance.
Old Dominion University is an excellent institution to attend, especially as someone who can from a smaller school. There is always something going on and everyone is very welcoming.

I have had an overall great time at old dominion university. My only complaint is the housing department can be a hassle to deal with if you need anything fixed. Other than that the food is great, everyone is so welcoming, and there are a lot of opportunities there for after college plans.

With my first semester, I quickly became very comfortable with my professors and learning, as well as adjusting, to each of their teaching styles. The classes and the overall environment is extremely welcoming and I've had nothing but amazing interactions with classmates, professors, staff, and just other students. I was unsure, initially, with how quickly it would take me to feel at home at Old Dominion University, but after just a week I absolutely fell in love. The campus and classrooms don't ever feel unwelcoming, and the staff are always super helpful.

my experience at Old Dominion University has been welcoming and diverse. As an international student, I’ve appreciated how supportive the professors and staff are, and how much the university encourages growth both academically and personally. I’m still adjusting to the system, but I feel motivated by the community and resources ODU provides.
Old Dominion is located in Norfolk, VA, approximately a 30-minute drive from Virginia Beach. The campus is nice and is usually well-maintained and clean.

ODU is truly a welcoming and exciting school community. I feel genuinely happy here and appreciate feeling included as a person of color. It's important to me that I don't experience discrimination based on my identity in the LGBTQ community, and I also feel respected and safe as a woman involved in student activities. Being part of over four clubs allows me to engage actively with campus life. The events organized by our school are always entertaining and bring a lot of joy, and the games are a fun way to connect and unwind. However, I do feel concerned about the high cost of education. The financial stress of paying tuition can sometimes be overwhelming, especially when I think about the expenses involved, which adds to my worries.
Very nice people, be aware of flooding on Hampton Blvd when it rains but other than that it's a lovely school and I'm so glad I chose it.

I like that ODU has a lot of opportunities and activities, so its easy to get involved in things on campus. I think the thing I most dislike about ODU, is the classes. Its mainly personal preference, but some classes seem unnecessary and some of the professors make the class more difficult.

I’m my experience, this place is hard to transition to. Coming from a small town kid the social scene and overall area is very overstimulating. My professors are wonderful, they teach in a way that is challenging. However, if a student isn’t challenged they will not learn. Coming from high school I did not have to study at all, but the challenging courses forces me out of my comfort zone and I appreciate it.

ai_summary: Oregon State University offers a vibrant and engaging college experience , highlighted by a strong sense of community and a beautiful campus in Corvallis. Students appreciate the supportive faculty and accessible academic advisors who genuinely care about their success, though some mention that large class sizes can limit personal interaction with professors. The diverse student life includes numerous clubs and extracurricular activities, making it easy for students to forge friendships and get involved. While many students praise the variety of academic programs , particularly in engineering and sciences, tuition costs are often seen as high, and there's a sentiment that some classes could be more efficiently managed. Overall, OSU presents a well-rounded university experience with ample opportunities, though challenges such as class size and tuition are acknowledged.

reviews.text: Oregon State is a good school located in a quaint college town. With a strong focus on community, OSU allows for individuals to explore cultures outside their norm. With a variety of different majors, there are plenty of spaces you can explore on campus. One difficult thing however, is finding housing for a decent price when you’re in a town with 25,000 other students. Be prepared for the high prices in rent!

My overall experience at Oregon State University has been very positive. Even though I take classes online, I’ve always felt included and supported. The school does a great job teaching students to understand different cultures and to treat people with respect. I’ve learned a lot here, not just academically but personally. Some classes surprised me with how much they changed my thinking. For example, soil science and environmental science helped me see how connected everything is in nature and how important our environment really is.
My ASL instructor has been one of the best teachers I’ve ever had. he is patient, humble, and always willing to help. Even online, he makes the class feel welcoming and fun.
Overall, OSU has helped me grow in ways I didn’t expect. I feel supported, challenged, and encouraged to think in new ways. I’m grateful for the teachers, the classes, and the environment the school creates for its students.

Hi, I am a freshman at Oregon State University. The things I love the most about OSU is it’s nature focused atmosphere in a small town, like Corvallis. I love the active campus life, surroundings, nature, academic supports, over 450 clubs, academic advisors helped me a lot in figuring out what major would be beneficial for me according my goals. One thing, I would like to see changed is that the food locations have a limited timeline, which makes it harder for me to get food at the times I want to. The other I would like to see changed , and more exciting is the weekend, although many students go back home to see their families, I feel that if there were some activities to participate in during the weekend will make studies and weekends more exciting to the students who aren’t able to go to see their families. I believe it would help students to focus more on studies, and would be beneficial to relief stress and anxiety.

So far, I have enjoyed my time at OSU. It took a while to grow on me but as someone who spends majority of their time in class or the library and going home on weekends, I feel comfortable at Oregon State and there are many academic help resources here.

It took a while for Oregon State University to grow on me but I am very grateful for my experience here. Make sure to participate in clubs and intramural sports! Utilize the gym and other resources. Campus is beautiful in the Fall.

I took all online courses here, I liked most of my teachers but I think some failed to realize how hard it is to go to online school. Yes there are lectures but some classes do not give you a lot of lectures and you had to teach yourself. Beyond that most of my teachers were wonderful and did not waste my time with busy work and made content engaging and worked with me to help me be prepared for my career.

Oregon State has been great as an Electrical & Computer Engineering major. The ECE program is hands on and practical with the labs and projects the actually help you learn. Professors within the program are all amazing. Outside of classes, there are many ways to get involved whether its one of the many clubs or IM sports. Sporting events are also free which is nice. Corvallis is also a super chill town to live in.

My experience at OSU was overall very positive. I enjoyed my classes, campus life, and the community in Corvallis. The party scene was present, but not anything that felt pressured to participate in. The food on campus was okay and there was a variety of options, however there was a learning curve on which places were best suited for individual taste. I experienced needing to take additional classes that were unrelated to my field in order to gain credits, which I would have preferred not to, and rather taken a degree specific course.

The counseling center sets up the degree framework easy and takes off a lot of stress on that end. There are a lot of great professors and people and its easy to make friends. The campus and surrounding area are great to explore.

My time at OSU was incredible. From the moment I stepped onto campus, I felt a genuine sense of belonging. The environment is remarkably safe and inclusive, which allowed me to truly focus on my studies and social life without hesitation. There is a "Beaver Nation" spirit that brings everyone together, making the campus feel like a tight-knit community despite its size. Whether it was participating in campus events, joining student organizations, or just hanging out on the Quad, it was always a fun and high-energy atmosphere. It’s a place where you can truly be yourself.
While the campus culture is amazing, I would love to see the university continue to expand affordable housing options and improve parking accessibility for commuting students. As the community grows, keeping the infrastructure as accessible as the social environment will make the experience even better for future Beavers.

Amazing college town added to overall college experience. Nice housing located within reasonable walking distance from classes. Helpful professors that help build community. I had an excellent time as a student and felt prepared for the real world with the knowledge learned in school.
very safe campus when i was there, would recomend to anyone who wants a slower scene with d1 athletics

My son's experience was unpleasant. As a freshman, he needed emotional support during such a major transition, and as parents, we were attentive. However, when he reached out to the mental health and counseling department, they didn’t follow up. There was a suicide in the building where his dorm was located. I contacted the counseling department to ask questions and seek support, but their response was to refer me to their standardized procedures, with no personal attention at all. Fortunately, my son decided to leave the school and look for another option. Unfortunately, he now has to pay a debt of thousands of dollars. His words were: “It wasn’t worth going". My advice for parents: check the support system in this school.

Oregon State is a nice place and I'm sure many people will call it home. The whole city of Corvallis is based on Oregon State University. There's nothing in corvallis besides the campus. The life here is nice but I just want to transfer to Grand Canyon University.

My first month at OSU has been awesome. So many helpful people. My professors have been great and the dorm activities allowed me to meet and in a short time become great friends with so many of my dorm mates.

school_name: Our Lady of the Lake University
ai_summary: At Our Lady of the Lake University, students frequently highlight the tight-knit community and the strong connections they form with professors, thanks to smaller class sizes. Many reviews point to the professors as a major strength, noting their care for students and dedication to their success. While some students appreciate the welcoming atmosphere and the variety of campus activities, others mention a need for improvements in campus facilities and food options, as well as concerns about tuition costs being on the higher side. The campus itself is often described as beautiful and historic, contributing to a pleasing environment for students. However, while some find the academic experience fulfilling, others express dissatisfaction with the availability of certain courses and the quality of student services. Overall, OLLU is seen as a university that fosters personal connections and academic growth , though it has areas that could benefit from enhancement.

reviews.text: The professors majority of the time are willing to work with you, but there’s a few bad apples that make the experience with the school terrible. It’s difficult at times with the way that professors are not trained. They cut budget at the school so professors are also advisors and aren’t trained to do so. I wouldn’t recommend the college, I just would like to be finished so I can transfer out.

My experience at Our Lady of the Lake has been wonderful! I came in with an associate and my advisor was able to put me on the right track to graduate with my bachelor's degree and graduate in two years. Had amazing professors who helps me excel in my courses. I worked on campus with the Center of Student Involvement, and it created on-campus experiences for me! Additionally, I was also in the OLLU Women's Soccer Team which was an amazing experience that brough a lot of fun in my college experience. Overall, it is great school to learn and find community in!

OLLU is somewhere where I am not afraid to be myself. Everyone is extremely welcoming. I’ve had nothing but good experiences. I’ve grown so much academically and personally. There are so many resources for everything. I love that it is a smaller community because stronger bonds are created there. I am glad that I have the opportunity to attend and pursuit a double major.

Our Lady of the Lake University has been the biggest challenge in my adult life so far; however, I do not regret going here. I have been a part of college life for 3 years now, and I appreciate the help and love my professors have given me, especially those who listen to their students and strive to ensure we will have a better future. The rest of the campus needs some work, but I have faith in the Faculty.

My overall experience was wonderful. I transferred from another state, and Our Lady of the Lake University was very welcoming. I liked that the school had a church attached, allowing us to go and pray anytime we wanted. The teachers were awesome and very informative and very open. It was easy to make friends. I also participated in work-study, which was a great experience. Our Lady of the Lake is a good school. They are serious about academics and student learning and that is their number one goal. The school doesn’t focus on partying; it is very strict when it comes to education..

Our Lady of the Lake University is a private catholic university in San Antonio, Texas, that I have enjoyed going to for these past four years of my life. As a Mass Communication major and Visual Art minor, I benefited from the smaller class sizes by getting one-on-one advice from my professors when struggling with projects. The smaller class sizes also lead to more connections between students, encouraging students to work together and participate in campus life together.
I really enjoy attending this university. I enjoy the people I have met and am excited about all the organizations and athletic choices available for me.

I was an international student-athlete at OLLU for 2.5 years. All faculty is over open friendly and helpful when you arrive. It is a small school so it's easy to make friends; almost everyone knows each other. Classes are never more than 20 people so the contact that you have with your professor is pretty personal. They're willing to help you with whatever you need. Overall it is definitely a good option to consider to get your undergrad!

very beautiful college all student activities are full of community and leadership. Activities on campus such as basketball games or student held festivities were very welcoming and so fun to participate in. Absolutely love my campus and would recommend it to everyone. Professors are very nice and do want to see you succeed at anything you do. Classes are not hard, but if you are struggling professors are very helpful.

I love being a student at OLLU. The classes are pretty easy, given I am a Psychology and Social Work student. The professors are pretty lenient. However, they are pretty unorganized which has led to me missing assignments before (however, they let me make them up). The dorms are ok. They need a lot of maintenance and there are ants and poop from dogs in the hallways of the apartments. However, you don't have too many roommates and you share a bathroom with a maximum of three other people. The area is the west side, so it's unsafe and there's not a lot to do. However, it is pretty close to downtown.

Our Lady of the Lake is a great place to go to. The professors there are all understanding and do their best to listen to their students whenever they need help with anything or if they need something repeated to them. Not only that, but the other staff such as the staff in the cafeteria are very welcoming as well. At the moment, I can not think of any changes I would like to see, but perhaps I’ll realize some things the more I spend time at OLLU.

Our Lady of the Lake University is a great higher education institution. With this in mind, I enjoyed being in an educational institution whose mission was deeply rooted in serving its community. I say this for, I have various opportunities to serve the population around me as well as provide them with information. Moreover, I can collaborate with multiple departments to help said community. That being said, if there was any change I would like to see occur, it would be that of providing more opportunities to those in science degree pathways.

Our lady of the lake was not my first choice, but they did offer me the most financial help regarding scholarships. Some of the professors there are the best you could find; they care about their students and constantly advise them on great opportunities. It is a small campus, which can be positive and negative. Your advisors will pay more attention to you as an individual and will be able to get to know you is a positive. On the other hand, the campus size is small, and you will see the same people most of your 4 years aside from the new faces coming in. There are some clubs, but most of them resemble what you would expect from a high school not really a college. The dorms are not really up to date because it is an old school aside from the new slater apartments, but even then, they do have some issues that can be fixed such as the elevators being renovated. I believe that overall, the university needs an update, and they need to focus resources better than getting media attention.

Our Lady of the Lake University has been a rewarding experience for me, offering both academic and personal growth. The supportive faculty and close-knit community have helped me thrive in my Business Management studies. Engaging coursework, leadership opportunities, and meaningful connections with peers have enriched my journey. The university’s values align with my own, fostering resilience and a commitment to excellence. Beyond academics, campus events and student organizations have provided a sense of belonging. My time here has prepared me for future success, reinforcing my confidence and passion for my goals. I’m grateful for this transformative experience.

My time at Our Lady of the Lake University was really great. The professors and staff are really accommodating and understanding. It is a smaller university, but you get to meet so many new people and become a tighter, friendlier and better experience.

ai_summary: Pace University presents a mix of experiences for students, particularly influenced by its urban location in New York City. Many reviews highlight the accessibility of internships and networking opportunities, especially for those in business and performing arts. While some students find the professors knowledgeable and supportive, others express concerns about the quality of academics and a lack of challenging coursework. The campus atmosphere fosters a sense of community, but some reviewers note difficulties in making friends due to its commuter-heavy population and the bustling city environment. Student life is vibrant with numerous clubs and activities, though some report that the social scene can feel lacking. The high cost of tuition and issues with financial aid are recurring concerns, leading some students to question the value of their education. Overall, while students appreciate the opportunities and resources available, there is a notable desire for improved administration and a more engaging social atmosphere.

reviews.text:
I am a psychology major, and I do feel my education has been compromised in a way due to department cuts that have caused most classes to be taught asynchronously and the few in-person classes to be held at extremely late hours of the night. I do feel that Pace isn't the ideal school for the major, and I would've gone elsewhere had I known what I wanted to pursue. However, Pace has an amazing arts program, most notably the Sands school for performing arts, and a great business program for finance and arts & entertainment management.
The social scene on campus is bustling but cliquey, as I've struggled to make real friends beyond my roommates due to bubbles that have been solidified since week 1. Clubs on campus are the best bet for meeting new people, I love Tonal Recall (my acapella group) and the Honors Advisory Board, but there is genuinely something for everyone.
Food is better than most schools, and is made to order and highly customizable.
I love this school! no were else is as fun and amazing as this place, so many oppurtunities to take advantage of. i love it!

I was so excited to transfer to Pace University! Unfortunately, the transfer student experience was not what I anticipated. I did not find that there was any campus/student life support from the school on the city campus. Anything they do happens at the Westchester Campus so you have to travel there. Academically, I did just fine with a 3.48. Some of the professors were "okay" and others were better. Overall I feel it was not worth the $ it costs to attend. I was placed in a triple with another sophmore who was amazing and a senior who was rather controlling and not nice to us. When we discussed this with the RA and resident director there was little we could do but tolerate it. Didn't feel well supported. I had already taken biology but had to take it again at Pace. I didn't find the professor to be a good teacher and ended up having to withdraw to save my GPA which was 3.65 coming to the school. To summarize, for me it was not worth the cost, travel back and forth and experience.

Overall, my experience with Pace University has been a love/hate relationship. I feel that most of my classes are not very challenging work wise. As for professors, most of mine were extremely helpful except for one, which I consider him as the worst teacher I have ever been a student of. Sports are not high priority for the school, however since the school is in the city, there is never a shortage of things to do in the surrounding area. I would recommend this school to someone who is in business, marketing, finance, or an artistic field.

I'm an acting major, and the Sands College of Performing Arts is excellent.
The academics at Pace are not very challenging, though.
I loved my time at Pace! I was a musical theater major and absolutely feel prepared to enter my profession. GO SETTERS!

My experience overall has been generally good, with my main issues being the school-wide lack of communication and the difficulty it takes for students to get in contact with different administrative/student aid departments.

My overall experience with Pace University has been both inspiring and transformative. The university offers a vibrant learning environment that encourages growth, creativity, and independence. I’ve also appreciated the diversity on campus; it’s a place where you meet people from all over the world, each bringing their own perspective and culture, which makes discussions and collaborations more enriching. Whether it’s through internships, campus events, or project-based coursework, the opportunities to gain practical experience are endless. I think Pace could improve communication between departments, especially when it comes to administrative processes like housing, financial aid, and registration. Sometimes it feels like information gets lost or isn’t clear, which can be stressful for students. I’d also like to see more support for commuter and transfer students, ensuring everyone feels equally connected to the campus community.

My overall experience at Pace University has been positive and full of growth. The professors are supportive and genuinely care about helping students succeed. I’ve enjoyed the small class sizes, which make it easier to participate and connect with others. Being involved in different campus activities has also helped me build confidence and community. Overall, Pace has provided me with strong academic opportunities and personal development.
Love the campus, the city is the campus! So far everything is good. Would love more food variety for dinning hall.

As a transfer student at Pace University from Minnesota, there are a lot of cultural shocks that I have experienced at my time so far. Even in the midst of massive change, I have felt incredibly comfortable at Pace. The social life can be hard to navigate especially as a transfer student, but the inclusiveness of student-run clubs at Pace has made it incredibly easy to settle in. I am now involved in 4 clubs, and have made so many friends in just one month of living here. The dorms are excellent, especially compared to other schools in the area. The dining hall process is unique, accessible and delicious. It is not like traditional college dining experiences and really is a big pro of Pace. What I would like to see change is more connection happening with the hard-working people who make our food. Since it is mostly run with GrubHub, there are less interactions with the cooks. I would love to see more community building happening by making it easier to order directly with the cooks.
A great, diverse and vibrant place. Faculty is alwasy very helpful and willing to help you. They challenge you and want you to be the best version of yourself.

I love the whole vibe of Pace University. The environment is welcoming, energetic, and full of opportunity. It is by Wall street, which is the heart of the Financial district. Another great thing is that it is in Manhattan, where dreams come true. I really appreciate is how supportive Pace is for students who are still figuring out their path. The encouragement of professors to students is what makes Pace different from other Universities. They offer great programs and resources to help students explore different fields before committing to a major. Speaking from personal experience the freshman orientation was what made me feel like I belonged. It wasn’t just informative, but also genuine, fun, and engaging. It made me feel excited and prepared to start college. They introduced us to networking early on, connected us with great peers, and really focused how to thrive and reach your fullest potential. It felt like more than just starting college, it felt like home to me.

I would like to see more involved & accessible professors. I also would like to see student being offered more intern & financial assistance opportunities. I do wish there was more classes offered in different areas of Magazine Publishing. I like the sense of community the school tries to provide departmentally. I also like the various classes offered on book publishing and their formates. Overall I do wish the university did a little more to support students on their academic journeys and set students up for success rather than reminding students that graduate students and programs are centered around independence.
I’m a transfer student so I didn’t experience anything yet, but so far they are very accommodating and helpful.

ai_summary: Pacific University Oregon offers a tight-knit and supportive community , which many students appreciate during their time at the school. Professors are frequently described as accessible and genuinely invested in students' success, creating a personalized academic experience through small class sizes. While the campus is beautiful and well-maintained, some students express dissatisfaction with the quality and variety of food options, as well as the somewhat limited social scene in the surrounding town of Forest Grove.
