The university's tuition is noted as high, leading to some discussions about value for money, although many students feel the unique qualities of the school—like its emphasis on individualized attention and strong professor-student relationships—justify the cost. Overall, Pacific University is seen as an ideal choice for those seeking a smaller, welcoming atmosphere with a strong focus on academics.

reviews.text:
I want to preface this review by saying that the film and graphic design departments are great and provided a lot of opportunities for me. With that being said, the program that I originally came to Pacific for, Journalism, is currently in a bad spot mainly due to the professor they chose to lead it.
The school paper not being independent may be causing some of the issues. There have been times where I think the journalism professor asked the administration about some stories, which might be because he's worried that too much bad press from the paper would get him fired or the paper get defunded. However, this would be exactly why going to Pacific for journalism would be a bad idea, because journalism under the constraints of the school isn't journalism, it's PR. And I have experience with trying and failing to do actual journalism with the Index. So if you want to do actual journalism after college, don't go to Pacific. Sports/culture journalism is okay though.

I came up to Pacific University not knowing what to expect, but my experience so far has been great. I have found a great group of friends, as well as knowing I have many staff and teammates around me to support me. One thing that has been tough for me and my family is financials in affording schooling, but I am working hard to apply for scholarships to help cover costs. Pacific University has helped me in that area, in finding sites and scholarships to apply to so that I can give myself the best chance to continue my studies.

My time was at Pacific Univ. in transition for the institution. Liberal Arts and Optometry were the offerings. Education at Pacific gave me the tools to teach: elem. through college, and gave me the opportunity for grad sch. to become a sports therapist and collegiate/ pro athletic trainer before Pacific had these programs. I’m retired and still see alumni from my time in CA. I played sports for the four years and enjoyed every min.
I would reintroduce frats and sororities for better social life. Don’t just become known as a speciality institution. Liberal Arts has it place and strength in this area makes for overall strong, broadly informed individuals!

ai_summary: At Palm Beach Atlantic University, many reviews highlight a strong sense of community and the supportive role of professors , who are often praised for their approachability and commitment to student success. The small class sizes allow for personalized attention, but some students express frustration with the limited course offerings and quality of the dining options, suggesting that the food can be lackluster and unsanitary. The university promotes a distinctly Christian environment , which some find comforting while others feel may be overly restrictive, with mentions of a curfew and other rules that contribute to a controlled atmosphere. The campus's location in West Palm Beach is frequently noted as a significant advantage, with access to the beach and local attractions enhancing the student experience. However, opinions on the adequacy of the academic programs vary, particularly in fields like business, which some students find lacking in rigor or real-world application. Overall, PBA offers a unique blend of academic and spiritual focus, making it appealing to those looking for a close-knit , faith-centered college experience .

reviews.text: Only attended one semester, wherein I became extremely sick due to mold in the dorms. Housing refused to relocate me to a temporary room or transfer to another building after repeated asking until they were contacted by parents and threatened with being exposed to the news. I attended Fall of 24, and as I write this it is currently Spring of 26, and I am still frequently fighting with the Student Accounts office over random fees that they continually bill me for, even after my withdrawl.

I like Palm Beach Atlantic University so much. My experience so far has been great. I got accepted into the school and offered a scholarship. I like the christian community and invites to events. I feel they are really about community and closure. I also that the school is on a beach. It brings a lot of fun things forth. I would like to see their pace of work definitely. They can move slow at times but it’s not issue for the most part. I do recommend coming to Palm Beach Atlantic University to all students thinking about doing so.

Palm Beach Atlantic is a warm welcoming environment where you can be with likeminded individuals who want to grow their faith. It provides opportunities to learn in a small student to professor ratio where the teachers are invested in their students.

My time at Palm Beach Atlantic University was a mix of good and bad. As every college freshman might experience in the beginning, I was both nervous and excited to start this new chapter of my life. I felt like it was the right place to be, and I looked forward to the start of the new semester. However, being that I was a commuter, I was unable to attend the majority of the school's events, as they were catered more towards the students living on campus. This made it significantly harder for me to make genuine connections with my peers and, in turn, made me feel more alone than a part of one big family. Despite this, I do cherish the many moments of personal growth I gained during my time there, and I’m so incredibly thankful to have been given the opportunity of attending this school, even if just for a year.

The advisors are invaluable and provide the required information. It's a Christian-themed institution, making it easy to desire to serve God, as there are opportunities to serve daily. My Professors are knowledgeable and helpful when one needs assistance

As a minority student in my first year, I feel like the school is not as welcoming as it should be. Even though I’m here on scholarship, the excessive cost for food per semester is outrageous. The food choices are not worth the price. I don’t feel that the students and school are as diverse as it’s made out to be. Making it more affordable would really make a difference for someone like me.

My experience at PBA was good overall. Some of the professors were outstanding and taught difficult concepts in an easy to understand manner. It's also a small campus, so it's easy to find classrooms and get everything done in a timely manner. However, some of the students are not overly friendly and they kinda have their cliques.

My experience at PBA was great as a first-year student. I have been at PBA for one year now but I made amazing friends and had great experiences at my time there. The professors I had were very helpful in assisting me with any of my needs. They answered in good timing. The cafeteria food has room for improvement. The student life was exceptional and very welcoming. Overall, I loved my experience at PBA.
I would like to see better cafeteria food options as well as more funding for the science department.

Attending Palm Beach Atlantic was not the experience I had hoped for. From the outside, it seemed like a beautiful, tight-knit Christian campus by the ocean—what could go wrong? But once I actually lived there, the reality didn’t quite match the brochure.
The biggest issue for me was the housing situation. I was stuck in a dorm with roommates who had little to no sense of respect or cleanliness. I found myself constantly cleaning up after everyone—dishes left in the sink for days, overflowing trash, mold in the bathroom, food rotting in the fridge. It was exhausting, both physically and mentally. What should have felt like a safe, comfortable space to unwind after class became a daily source of stress and frustration.
On top of that, the environment was way more extreme in its religious intensity than I had expected. I don’t mind faith—everyone has their own beliefs—but there was an overwhelming sense of religious pressure that felt less about genuine spirituality.

I am an incoming freshman but I have gone to several welcome days, tour days, accepted student days so I have gotten to see what PBA is all about. They have so much support academically and spiritually. It seems like the professors are very engaged with the students. I love the fact that we have to participate in workshop hours and chapel hours. Everyone is so friendly and helpful!

I cannot wait to start at PBA! I can’t wait for the christian environment, and making new friends! Awhile I have gone to private school my whole life I am looking forward to being on my own and experiencing new adventures.

I'm going into my third year at PBA and I have really liked it so far. The campus, the people, and the location completely make the experience 10x better than any other school. The people that you meet at PBA are some of the kindest humans that you'll ever meet; I've met people that I know I'll have in my life forever. The campus is a tight-knit community if you're looking for a small school. Going to a small school has also been great because you get to make personal relationships with all of your professors. The professors at PBA are super helpful and again, since it's a tight-knit community, most of them know how to help you personally. The location is also AMAZING! Downtown West Palm Beach is so diverse and exciting. There are many places to see and everything is in walking distance of the campus. I cannot forget to mention that the beach is only a 20 minute walk from campus and the walk is gorgeous.
I loved the friendly atmosphere, where you truly felt like a part of a mini chrisitan community. I felt that I now have a second home, grown closer to the Lord, and have lifelong friends.
Absolutely beautiful campus, brilliant teachers, and a very nice educational environment with many clubs to join.

ai_summary: Pepperdine University offers a distinct college experience shaped largely by its beautiful Malibu location and strong Christian foundation. The campus is known for its stunning views and supportive community , where small class sizes allow for meaningful interactions between students and dedicated professors who genuinely care about student success. Academically, the university is recognized for rigorous curricula, particularly excelling in humanities and international programs, with many students emphasizing the outstanding study abroad opportunities . However, some reviews highlight a lack of social life, with many students participating in Greek life leading to cliques, and a generally quiet atmosphere that may not appeal to everyone. The dining options receive consistent criticism for being limited and overpriced, and while the community spirit can be strong, issues around diversity and inclusion persist. Financial considerations are also prevalent, as some students feel the tuition does not align with their overall experience, especially in terms of resources and living conditions.

reviews.text:
Its a great school academically, and esthetically, however its important to keep in mind its a Christian school with a non tolerance policy
In my experience new students look past that and try to get wawy with things but ultimately they end up transferring. That being said it is a great community and there is truly nothing that compares to it.

I love the small class sizes and opportunities to build relationships with professors. I have met some of the best people in the world! One of my favorite things about Pepperdine, though, is the wide range of study abroad opportunities!

This is my first semester at Pepperdine, and it has come with many wonderful and challenging experiences. First, I think any college student can relate to the struggles of dorm-living and being on a meal plan where food options may not always be up to par. However, this is somewhat exasperated by the fact that Pepperdine is located in a remote, non-accessible area where transportation to the nearby stores can be tricky. But Malibu is an unbeatable location, so while accessibility from campus may not be ideal, it is worth the overall location, beach access, view, and proximity to major metropolitan hubs. As well, I would say classes are just the right level of difficulty, where I feel like I am expanding my knowledge and being challenged by the course material, but am still able to succeed. As well, I feel that all the professors at Pepperdine genuinely care for their students and get to know them on a personal level due to the small class sizes, which helps you stay encouraged.

Not much school spirit, no one goes to games or matches.
Food is decent, but could be better.
Professors are fantastic.
Pepperdine has a great faith-based community that cultivates strong and diverse relationships as well as great academics.

Personally, my experience with Pepperdine University has been fulfilling both academically and socially. As a current sophomore, my freshman year taught me how to manage and succeed in my classes while also becoming integrated in the student community. Pepperdine's Natural Science program is challenging, but the faculty and resources provided are very helpful in supporting students' success. Plus, the location of the Malibu campus allows for stunning views and access to both the beaches and the city of Los Angeles. The student life on campus is not quite as sports-centered or party-cultured as state schools or larger universities, which may be disappointing to some. However, I personally believe that Pepperdine's unique, spiritual, close-knit community, with peers and faculty who value connecting with you, makes up for where it lacks and sets it apart from any other school.

If I could go back in time I would have not gone to Pepperdine. I had some really nice professors and some that it was prevalent that they were only there because they were tenured. The administration of this school is absolute joke with zero empathy. Student life is only great if you have money and are able to get off campus otherwise its basically a prison with a nice view. The current president is disgusting and greedy and does not hold a light to his predecessor. When I was accepted into Pepperdine they were ranked 49th in the nation now I think were down to 70 something that just show the direction this university in moving while the cost of attendance continues to rise. The only thing that I am grateful for is my friends I met through my sorority and the chance I had to study abroad. This school does not care about their alum unless they are donors. At the end of the day I ultimately choose this school because of the location which was my biggest mistake.
Professors are nice. Malibu is beautiful. However, during weekends, nobody is here. It’s a little bit boring sometimes. Food is really nasty.
Pepperdine University has a very inclusive community. There is always something for anybody. The academics, professors, and programs are truly amazing.

I wouldnt reccomend coming here if you are anything other then a super religious commuter. EVERYONE goes home on weekends even those who live in washington, the campus is desserted and theres no real on campus events or parties. Most of the freshman dorms have espestos and are very susceptible to mold. The food is the same most days and the library closes at 5pm. There is virtually nothing to do and most clubs meet once a month so theres no real scene there either. For gen eds you can still have a lecture class of 200 students in elkins and resources on campus arent what you are paying for. Student success center will avoid meeting with you and you have to do your own four year plans and register yourself as a freshman just like a big school.
Pepperdine is definitely a beautiful campus, though definetly be aware of the surrounding areas as Malibu is pretty isolating.

Pepperdine is a very beautiful campus with good academic programs. However, if you are looking for a traditional college experience with strong school pride and high engagement with on-campus activities, then I would not recommend this university. It felt like an isolated, commuter school.

DO NOT COME HERE !!!
Pepperdine looks amazing on the outside with its beautiful campus, ocean views, and strong marketing pitch. But once you’re actually a student, the reality is very different. The academics are overpriced for what you get, and the resources don’t match the tuition cost at all. Class sizes are smaller, but that doesn’t necessarily mean better teaching since many professors seem more focused on research or their own projects than on students.
Overall, Pepperdine is more about appearances than substance. Unless you’re going here solely for the location, I’d recommend looking elsewhere where your tuition dollars actually translate into education and career outcomes.
GSEP was fantastic as the day program. Professors were dedicated and helpful. Classes were challenging and translated to real world experiences.

Pepperdine is frequently praised for its stunning campus overlooking the Pacific Ocean, often ranking among the most beautiful in the nation. With strong academics, small classes, and international programs.

ai_summary: Point Park University stands out for its vibrant downtown Pittsburgh location , fostering an urban campus life that many students appreciate. The sense of community is often highlighted, with smaller class sizes enabling close interactions between students and professors, which many find beneficial. However, experiences with academics vary significantly; while some students praise the quality of instruction , particularly in the Conservatory of Performing Arts (COPA), others express concerns about the rigor and relevance of non-COPA programs. Issues such as outdated facilities, expensive dining options, and a lack of support for commuters are recurring themes. Students also report mixed feelings about the social environment, with some enjoying the diversity and inclusivity , while others feel isolated or find social dynamics challenging. Overall, Point Park offers unique opportunities , especially in arts and media, but it's essential for prospective students to weigh these against the potential drawbacks in their specific fields of interest.

reviews.text: I graduated from Point Park University with a Performance and Practices Bachelors Degree. While this Major no longer exists, it was one that allowed us to pursue acting, directing, and playwriting. I would say I had very passionate and caring professors who you could tell were very immersed into their craft which was wonderful to learn from! We went through a lot of changes during Covid and learning to act over zoom was not an ideal time, but the school did what they were able to do during lockdown. The school is located in Downtown Pittsburgh so safety is a big priority. They do have security guards at every building, but if you're walking around you have to look out for yourself, always walk with a group at nighttime. I lived on campus for my first 2 years, the only thing is the elevators can take an extremely long time and break down fairly often also air conditioning is non existent. It can get very hot in the spring / summer time. Other than this I had a great experience!

Point Park isn't a bad school by any means but it was very cliquey and largely priortized the COPA program over any other majors. De to this, there wasn't much emphasis on extracurriculars due to COPA students being expected to spend all their time on their performative majors. Largely, I think campus life suffered.
the communications department is very good, but the food is bad and the school is not good for all majors. It is small and personal so you can get to know professors.

My experience at Point Park University has been largely positive. I have appreciated the small class sizes, which allow for meaningful interaction with professors and classmates. The instructors are knowledgeable, accessible, and invested in student success, and the campus environment is welcoming and supportive. I have also valued the practical, hands-on approach to learning, which strengthens my ability to apply course concepts in real-world situations.
Although my overall experience has been strong, there are areas where improvement would be beneficial. Enhancing communication regarding course scheduling, program requirements, and administrative processes would help students better navigate their academic responsibilities. Additionally, expanding campus resources—such as study spaces, technology access, and career support—would further enrich the student experience.
I like it here. Nice people, beautiful campus and city. Freshman dorms very small and cramped. Maybe better food.
It’s really diverse and a good school, I like the fact that the campus is in the middle of a city and everything is walkable. I would like to see more student activities!

It's a nice college. Very robust arts programs. The dorms need major improvement, considering many people are currently living in hotels, but it's understandable considering the school is in a historic district.
My overall experience with Point Park University is pleasant; the teachers and academics here are great, the dormitories are overall okay, but could definitely use some work.

At Point Park University, I walked into the studio each morning carrying Detroit’s rhythm in my bones. Surrounded by mostly white classmates, I felt both visible and invisible—eyes on me, yet few understood the journey that brought me here. The ballet barres, the mirrors, the long rehearsals—they became my proving ground. Being a Black girl from Detroit in this space meant I carried more than just choreography; I carried resilience, culture, and the weight of representation. Some days it was isolating, but every time I danced, I carved out space for myself, showing that my voice, my movement, and my presence belonged.

Overall I’ve enjoyed my experience at the university so far, I met some amazing people and a wonderful roommate. With it being a smaller university I don’t feel overwhelmed and feel I can make good connections. However the food could be better, with more options and better quality.

I like that the school is in downtown Pittsburg. I've enjoyed working with Dean of the Sports Communication program and I really like how much Coach Williams cares about my success both on and off the pitch.

my daughter graduated from the school in the fall of 2023. I was so proud of her that she graduated a full term early. Recently, we have been receiving voicemail messages, and email saying she never graduated. This is despite my daughter‘s accomplishments and the egregious amount of money I have paid to this university. Despite the turmoil, their messages caused in my relationship with my daughter., apparently they’re please for to get more money from her. This is Trump university.. I plan on reporting them to any accrediting agency that will listen.. I have the messages in the emails.

I chose Point Park because my goal was to find a university with a strong dance program, diverse student life, and rigorous academics. However, I was disappointed by the academic classes. I did not find them interesting or challenging at all. Furthermore, the dance program is way too large to be helpful. I did not develop the close relationships with professors that may have helped my career upon graduation. They barely knew us by name or knew how to advise us on our futures. While I ended up having a fulfilling career in dance, I am not sure that PPU was the right choice for my undergraduate education.

Overall I have had a good freshman year. The community of students is great and most faculty are very professional. I will say, one of my main concerns coming into the school was diversity, and I will be honest and say that we are not the most diverse school. However, I think that, as a COPA student, the training I am receiving is so amazing that it is not as hard to overlook, although it could be better. As far as living situations, the dorms are old and all have their problems no matter which one you are assigned to. The dining opportunities are not the best, only one dining hall and two other places to get food that all close before ten, which is not accommodating to people with classes past 8pm, which is most of the performing arts students. Overall the experience does have its flaws, as most schools do, but the training and student life is great.
I had an excellent time studying at PPU. I became friends with many professors. I learned a lot in terms of professional conduct and it was conveniently located from where I lived, very central.

school_name: Pontifical Catholic University of Puerto Rico--Arecibo

reviews.text: Just finished my first semester and got great grades. Also had a very positive experience with many of the professors. Loved theology class and I extensively appreciate the professor's method of teaching. If you are responsible and do your part in the learning effort, you'll do well!

I took an online class but I think I prefer to have the professor right there to answer all my questions immediately. This class made communication complicated due to the fact that it centered on written communication and rarely any oral communication.

In this first year I have meet a faculty of professors well prepared, with the need experience, and the most positive attitude towards the students. Is a well equipped university and on a great location.
It's pretty good, spacious, gets very crowded. Nice

school_name: Pontifical Catholic University of Puerto Rico--Ponce

reviews.text: My experience at the Pontifical Catholic University of Puerto Rico – Ponce has been very good. The professors are kind and always willing to help. The campus is big, beautiful, and there’s always something to do. Every day there are conferences, talks, and activities for students. They also organize many events to entertain and connect with others. There are lots of spaces to study or just relax.

My overall experience at the Pontificial Catholic University has been amazing so far. The campus is big, but not huge so you don’t have to walk way too much to get to your classes. The services are great and they are always available to help, wether it is through email or at the office. I’m a science major and the professors are incredibly knowledgeable. In general, they are all very nice and are always available too. The classrooms count with the best healthcare equipment like ultrasound machines, microscopes and even interactive activities like anatomage for us science majors. The campus counts with multiple cafeterias with a variety of food choices, so you never get tired of eating here, and it is quite affordable as well. The campus is also very well taken care of. The classrooms and buildings are in optimal conditions, and are constantly going through renovations to fix issues or make the campus look more modern. The classrooms and bathrooms are also clean, in perfect conditions.

My overall experience at the Pontifical Catholic University of Puerto Rico – Ponce has been very positive. The professors are knowledgeable and supportive, and they truly care about students’ academic success. The campus environment feels welcoming, and there are many opportunities to get involved in student activities. I also appreciate the strong focus on values and community. One area that could be improved is the availability of updated technology and resources in some classrooms, as well as faster response times from certain administrative offices. Overall, I feel that the university provides a solid education and a great sense of belonging.

My experience at PUC was excellent. I took a course that I really love, History. The subjects were interesting and the teachers were very good. Some of the content seemed a little basic or not very up-to-date. However, with the material they taught and our conversations, we were able to develop a deeper perspective on the subject. The big challenge is that my training in History was very general. So it was very challenging to cover the entire history of humanity in just 5 years. So, naturally, a lot of things are left behind. However, I had two very important international opportunities. One was being able to do an internship in Alexandria, Egypt during my studies, where I stayed for 4 months. And another was being able to do a year of academic mobility at the University of Coimbra. My training was very good and the environment, along with the library and social spaces, were very good and positive.
It hasn’t been an easy experience—university life is challenging. But I have to say that I’m grateful because it has taught me what life is like without depending on others.
The college is located in an excellent place, the professors are amazing at teaching and they prepare you for the work place, they’re super helpful and professional.

The Pontifical Catholic University of Puerto Rico in Ponce is not only an important University, its an experience. It has so many things to talk about. It has variety of programs thanks to the years it has been founded, and have the only college of education in the south of Puerto Rico, that's my area. It has a lot of buildings for every college including Natural Sciences, Environmental Sciences, Arts and Humanities, Laws, Architecture, Fashion Design, Business administration, and some more. Every month there's a creative activity for the students which it is nice knowing the stress students passed in college. Food is nice, has some beautiful landscapes and a diversity of people, you will definitely will love to study there!
Best place to study and meet friends. Professor are well prepared and ready to impart knowledge. Would not change anything.

I loved being a social work undergrad student there. My professors were very pro-student, dedicated, intelligent individuals with a strong drive to guide future social workers. They were always very dynamic in their classes. The university itself is home to many student associations some of which I had the honor of being a part of. I'd also like to mention the fact that it has a wide variety of services for its students and faculty.
Honestly it is a good university. You will have to take 3 catholic theology courses. Chemistry department is on the deline with low students enrolled.
It is a great University with a well evalued Medicine bachelors. The university has great structure and is awesome for those pursuing scientific research.

The Pontifical Catholic University of Puerto Rico is a private institution that offers a variety of academic programs for the professional development of determined people. It provides students with different areas in which they can engage, such as theater, music, band, dance, cheerleading, sports, and more. Academically, it offers innovative programs that allow students to maximize their time and resources. In addition to offering Puerto Rican youth the opportunity to obtain undergraduate and postgraduate degrees, it also provides opportunities for foreign students to complete cultural exchanges at its facilities. Likewise, it has numerous partner universities where students can participate in exchanges, internships, or other programs. Beyond all that it offers its students, the university focuses on promoting values such as solidarity, justice, prudence, commitment, and other qualities that distinguish a professional.

My overall experience at this university so far has been pleasant and enjoyable. The professors are respectful, friendly and understanding with the students, including the staff. Therefore, what I would like to see changed is a good sewer system because sometimes puddles form when it rains and a more efficient internet because sometimes it gets interfered with. On the other hand, I think student life depends on each student's life and what each university offers to the students. So, for me it is a normal and common experience. On the other hand, it is very nice that there are several cafeterias on campus to buy a variety of food. Otherwise, life on campus covers different aspects that are fantastic such as activities, workshops, conferences and associations.

During my years of study at the Pontifical Catholic University of Puerto Rico for my bachelor's degree in nursing I had the opportunity to take courses with excellent and well-prepared professors, but I think that in the master's programs they should have others such as anesthesia or geriatrics. While I was studying I stayed at the Residencia de Señoritas where I noticed the lack of maintenance. In the kitchen the fans were damaged as well as the electrical system of the residence. In addition to the fact that on rainy days water would come in through the kitchen ceiling. Currently I am still there since I am doing my second bachelor's degree and there is no improvement.

It is the best university in the southern area of Puerto Rico, it offers you so many opportunities to grow personally, spiritually and professionally. It has everything and I am very proud to be part of the blue-blood family, up pioneers.

ai_summary: Portland State University (PSU) offers a distinctive urban campus experience right in the heart of downtown Portland, facilitating easy access to city life , food options, and internship opportunities. Many reviews praise the professors for their dedication and support , often highlighting their commitment to student success and the engaging nature of the classes. However, the university is noted for being primarily a commuter school, which can limit student social life and community engagement outside of academics. The diverse and inclusive environment is frequently highlighted, though some students feel that the campus lacks a strong sense of community and that certain programs, particularly in the arts and architecture, could be improved. Additionally, affordability and tuition costs are common points of concern, alongside mixed reviews about student services, especially in financial aid and accessibility support. Overall, PSU is seen as a solid choice for students seeking a practical, career-oriented education in an urban setting.

reviews.text:
Overall, my experience at Portland State University has been positive. One of the things I appreciate most is the diverse and welcoming campus community. PSU attracts students from many different backgrounds, which makes classes and group projects more interesting because you get a wide range of perspectives. The downtown Portland location is also a big advantage—there are plenty of internships, public transit options, and things to do nearby.
Many of the professors I’ve had are knowledgeable and genuinely want students to succeed. When students take initiative and ask questions, most instructors are very supportive and willing to help outside of class. The university also offers good opportunities for networking, internships, and hands-on experience, especially in programs that connect with local industries.

The professors at Portland State were amazing and I formed close connections with them and classmates. I had issues with the university's choices regarding funding allocation, where they spent a lot of money on things unimportant to students while cutting services and classes and raising costs on tuition and housing.

I'm very happy with my experience so far at Portland State University! The academics are great, however I wish there were more options for teaching education. All of my professors have been great to work with and really take the time to help each student. The surrounding city is fun to explore. There are plenty of places on campus for students to shop/eat and do recreational activities, however they are very pricey.
Personally, I have not been impressed with their costs of academics, nor their resources surrounding getting financial help. I feel that I have been left to flounder this past year in terms of finances.

So far, I love my academics and everything I'm learning. I have faced a few disturbing and racist moments, as well as very interesting events, but overall the school is very great. I just wish they had better food in the dining hall. I haven't eaten from there in 3 weeks since my friends and I keep getting food poisoning every time we eat there.

This is a very diverse, supportive and engaged campus. Also the location in downtown Portland is great and very convenient for all kinds of culture, activities and events. I highly recommend this school.

I have had a good experience with PSU so far. I love the urban environment of the campus which is what I was looking for. The Honors college is very inviting and supportive, with great professors and classmates. I highly recommend applying to the Honors College if you can.

I really enjoy going to psu, some things could be worked on, such as the clarity of their staff because currently theyre quite vague with requirement and it has caused me to now owe 2,000 dollars that i wouldn't have if they were more specific. The party scene is quite lacking, but i believe its due to us only having one(maybe two? I havent seen the other one) fraternities. Food is just okay, but it has a variety of options for people with restrictions so its better than high school.

Very caring and qualified professors, much of the content taught in class is applicable to the fields of research as they are currently growing. I'd like to see more emphasis put on the stem classes because I feel they are quite short and don't cover all of the basics that they should be, leaving about 10-20% up to the student to learn on their own.
Portland State has a variety of programs and classes, and the professors that taught me were all very knowledgeable, and inclusive

I struggled my first term at PSU but that is totally my fault. It’s a pretty good school and so far I’ve had some pretty great professors. I would recommend this school to anyone who takes education seriously because it’s 100% worth it if you put in the work.
Train stops right there. There are tons of busses to get there and back. The school food is good but there is also tons of local places that are delicious
It is right in the heart of Portland and there are new buildings. The majors offered are nice and the professors are great. The area was sometimes unsafe, especially late at night.

Portland State University offers a good undergraduate program for architecture if you are into sustainability and urban design. The studios are rigorous yet creative, with the professors providing good support, and good fabrication labs. The environment of downtown Portland provides a daily real experience with buildings, public spaces, and design inspiration. The courses can be heavy, and the campus is dispersed, but in general PSU provides a valuable and hands-on education that will prepare well for the architectural profession.

The administration is cutting too many programs for selfish reasons. I'm going to graduate and this really was my best option for an affordable education, but I'm so tired of this place. Some professors are incredible and have made things feel worth it.
it is a great place to be especially since i got accepted in winter term i cant wait to continue mt education and see explore the city and campus.

ai_summary: At Prairie View A & M University, students often highlight the strong sense of community , describing the campus atmosphere as familial and welcoming . Many appreciate the accessible professors who genuinely care about their students' success and offer support both academically and personally. The small class sizes enhance learning and interaction, contributing to a positive academic experience . However, there are mixed feelings about campus activities, with some students noting that events often don't coincide well with class schedules, which limits participation. While the campus is described as beautiful and vibrant , feedback regarding housing and dining options suggests there is room for improvement. Many students feel that despite its challenges, PVAMU provides a unique and enriching experience , largely due to its HBCU status and commitment to fostering student growth and empowerment.

reviews.text: some assistance from some of the professors; misleading and misguidance relative to course of study and which classes to take; taking longer to complete course of study due to being a transfer student-which can be confusing and disrupting; housing for students is a great concern, typically students have to get off-campus apartments just to have a place to live near by campus
It’s was a very enriching experience and allowed me to learn more than just and education but about myself as a African American Male.

My overall experience at Prairie View A&M University has been very positive. From the moment I arrived, the campus community has been welcoming and supportive, and the university truly makes it a point to help students feel included and valued. There are plenty of opportunities for academic growth, leadership, and involvement, which makes it easy to find your place on campus. Although Prairie View is sometimes labeled as a “party school,” it is much more than that. Students take real pride in attending an HBCU, and there is a strong sense of tradition, culture, and campus pride that makes Prairie View feel like home.

Prairie View A&M University has a D1 Athletic program for Football that was the main point of interest for me. The school has a rich background in culture and is situated in a great region of Texas which means the student body is composed of well-rounded individuals who are dedicating themselves for the betterment of both them and their community.

The Prairie View A&M University College of Nursing is a rigorous and rewarding program that challenges students to grow academically, professionally, and personally. The curriculum is demanding and prepares students to think critically, develop strong clinical judgment, and uphold high standards of patient care. Many faculty members are knowledgeable, passionate, and genuinely invested in student success, providing guidance, mentorship, and support both inside and outside the classroom. The program also emphasizes professionalism, accountability, and service, which helps prepare students for real-world nursing practice. While the fast-paced nature of the program and occasional organizational challenges can be stressful, these experiences also teach adaptability, resilience, and time management. Overall, PVAMU College of Nursing fosters a strong sense of community and pride, encouraging students to persevere, support one another, and strive for excellence as future nurses.

My experience at Prairie View A&M University was both challenging and grounding. Prairie View was a direct reflection of the world and everything in it. Anything you wanted to find there, like opportunity, struggle, growth, community, or pressure, you could. It required discipline, accountability, and self awareness to survive and even more to succeed. I watched so many people start and not finish. So for me to graduate with a 3.48 GPA meant something real to me. It showed my ability to stay consistent, manage competing responsibilities, and meet high expectations in an environment that did not offer shortcuts or hand holding.

My experience so far as a freshman has been good. Most of my professors were good at lecturing and actually help learn, others were more so just trying to get through the semester it felt like, so you would have to go home and teach yourself with the material they gave you or notes you wrote. As far as the student life, it's really easy to make friends/bond and using friends to help you study or get through the semester together. The only thing I can say PVAMU needs to work on is the advisors or even getting someone specific to answer the phone to get in contact with you.
I am a freshman and had some hardships gaining housing as well as a help financially with tuition and housing cost but overall I love and appreciate my school and professors

My overall experience at Prairie view a&m university is amazing. The feeling of the community, great academics and mentorships, putting you in your best position possible is nothing but expecting. The feeling of the environment is unmatchable, and unlike something I’ve never felt before. Walking outside being able to feel welcome within every space it’s like an award. The mentors help a lot, coming to college its all new experiences, new things to learn and new challenges but having someone to talk to that has already been through that experience is nothing but a blessing.Prairie View general body is genuinely a big family I couldn’t be more happier with my college choice.

Campus life at PVAMU was vibrant and full of tradition. From Homecoming to Panther Camp, there was always something happening to bring students together. I especially appreciated the cultural pride and sense of belonging that permeated the campus. It felt like home.
If I could change anything, I would advocate for more mental health resources and expanded counseling services on campus. As a psychology student, I saw the growing need for accessible support, especially during midterms and finals. Increasing awareness and availability of these services would benefit the entire student body.
Overall, Prairie View A&M helped shape me into a more confident, compassionate, and purpose driven individual. I’m proud to be an alumna and grateful for the experiences that continue to guide my journey.

Prairie View is a great place to be I have visited the campus and now it's at the top of my list for when I graduate May 2026. Prairie View is an HBCU that is close to home i'll be around my people and I have family members there now so I feel like my overall experience will be amazing.

My overall experience here has been great. I’ve been able to meet and connect with so many different people who look like me with different backgrounds. The student life and activities are endless and opportunities always present themselves. If i could change something it would be the surrounding area of the school. It needs more resources like grocery stores, restaurants, urgent cares, etc. It’s very dry and empty to say it’s a college town. other than that I love my school and the traction it brings
It was good there was amazing people and it was easy to make friends. I like the dorm setup and how there are different options for you to choose from.

I am non black at a hbu it was hard getting adjusted to the school since I stood out from the rest. Over the years I have seen more students come to our school of different backgrounds ethnicity i I feel like seeing people with my skin tone they feel as though they too are welcome in a school whose main race is black. I thankfully never experienced any discrimination i always felt welcomed by my peers but when it came to race talk I was always alone I had no one to relate to.

My overall experience at PVAMU has been a disappointment. From the professors to the surrounding area, everything has felt underwhelming, especially since there isn’t much to do locally. One of the few positives is that the campus offers good food options in the MSC, and I enjoy spending time at the gyms, which are well-equipped and convenient. However, I believe one major change PVAMU needs to make is improving the way professors engage with students. Many of them seem more focused on getting through material than actually helping us understand it. Not everyone learns the same way, and it would make a big difference if professors took the time to teach with that in mind.

ai_summary: Princeton University is widely recognized for its strong focus on academics and offers a challenging curriculum that pushes students to excel while providing ample resources for support. Many reviews highlight the dedication of professors and the extensive financial aid available, ensuring that diverse student backgrounds are well-supported. Although the academic environment is intense and can be stressful, especially with the culture of grade deflation, students appreciate the collaborative atmosphere and strong sense of community that develops among peers. Student life is vibrant, with numerous clubs and activities, though some note that socializing can sometimes feel cliquish or isolating, particularly for newcomers. The campus is noted for its beauty and safety, and the surrounding town of Princeton adds to the overall experience. While there are concerns about the pressure to succeed and the social scene, many reviewers still regard their time at Princeton as transformative and fulfilling , emphasizing the unique combination of academic rigor and personal growth that the university fosters.

reviews.text:
Princeton would rather pay full tuition for 5800 undergraduates at around $394,000,000 than lose 8 percent of its endowment to taxes and around $3,000,000,000.
At the end of fiscal 2025, the university’s endowment was valued at $35.7 billion, with a payout for the year of $1.8 billion. That represents a spending rate of a little over 5%, according to the university
PRINCO thought they could outsmart the general market in private equities.
The cuts to services and staff benefits are not important to Princeton as it guards its endowment from impact.
Students receiving full aid and yes, even those with family incomes of $250,0000 are pleased. Pell Grant admits are up not due to generosity but from greed of the business that is Princeton.

Princeton University claims to have one of the best computer science departments yet cannot update its own university software for an efficient meal swap program? “The decision (to decommission the meal swap system) was made by the University. However, we were told that the meal exchange software is antiquated and that due to the cost cutting initiative, the University is not in a position to fund the upgrade,” chairman of the Graduate Interclub Council wrote in a statement to The Daily Princetonian.

Max Weiss, a Princeton professor of history and an outspoken advocate of an academic boycott of Is real, led a walkout of his History of Pales tine /Israe l class into McCosh Courtyard, where an anti-Isra el encampment was established. He taught his lesson from there, making him the first faculty member to lecture from the encampment, the Princeton Alumni Weekly reported. Weiss told the publication he hopes the illegal encampment will one day be commemorated with "a plaque."

9 undergraduates and 3 graduate students since 2022 is a concerning number of students. Student athletes are given baseline concussion studies. Princeton students need pre-admission mental health evaluations to match. The atmosphere is not for everyone.

Princeton Grad Student Treasurer Challenged to Subtract From 10,000.
On Monday, the Graduate Student Government (GSG) released the Budget and Finance Report for the first half of the 2026 fiscal year, which revealed that it has already maxed out or exceeded its annual budget in three categories.
Specifically, Events Board contributions, a body in the GSG offering funding for Graduate Student Events, has already exhausted its $10,000 allotment. Special Events — which covers alcohol purchases, materials for events, and catering services — and the Ivy+ Leadership Summit overshot their budgets by $1,476.84 and $823.60, respectively.
On top of these expenses, the report also noted that of the $23,625.00 the GSG had spent on “Beer Jackets,” only $10,519.75 had been recovered in sales.

From the 2025 Report from the Treasurer: “The University is potentially facing a new 8 percent endowment tax, which applies to universities with endowments of over $2 million per student and a tuition-paying population of at least 3,000. With a projected increase of financial aid spending to $327 million in 2025–26, there is a possibility that the University will avoid the endowment tax by having under 3,000 tuition-paying students. The University has previously declined to comment on the endowment tax and the number of students that pay tuition.”
Student life could be improved, but the academics are very comprehensive. All of the courses I have taken so far are interesting.

Thank you to the professors who filed for my Statements for an A+. I fully appreciate this extra effort by professors and I am grateful that my work has been recognized as deserving of the honor at Princeton University!

Princeton University’s President defends higher education on free speech yet the university fails in hiring and admissions to increase free diverse speech.
Eisgruber said universities often reflect what is happening in the culture. "It's on campuses because it's in society," Eisgruber replied. "we are having trouble talking to one another across political differences. Campuses are part of that society, and they're a place where civil discussions and discussions across difference are especially important." When Professor Robert George retires there will be groupthink because of the limited diversity of faculty or students regarding viewpoint. If you are not far left you can expect to keep your views to yourself or face ostracism by faculty and peers. The majority of Princeton graduates self-report that they plan to stay in academia continuing the cycle.

At Princeton you can elect to take a junk course like “Bad Girls.” If you are not paying tuition perhaps you do not mind wasting your time on similar junk courses. If you are a paying tuition I hope you are taking quality courses that will benefit your growth and future employment outside academia.

Admissions standards are not as expected and are varied. One student aced the most rigorous courses in high school, AP 5s, SAT 1590. Another student in the same class had an SAT of 1300 and does not even know something basic as calculating the consumer surplus as an area. The best and the brightest do not opt for test optional nor wish to stand on a soapbox as an activist instead of being a serious academic scholar. Brilliant peers should be admitted for an entire class with a basic expectation of a high level of academic achievement. Grade inflation is a disservice to all students and acceptances not based on merit devalues a diploma’s worth and the reputation of the university.

A new University policy will ban recording on campus with few exceptions starting Jan. 1, raising serious concerns for journalists, among others. It was passed by a wide margin at the Nov. 10 meeting of the Council of the Princeton University Community (CPUC), with no opposing votes.
The policy, which is slightly longer than a page, “prohibits the installation or use of any device for listening, observing, photographing, recording, amplifying, transmitting, or broadcasting sounds or events occurring in any place where the individual or group involved has a reasonable expectation of being free from unwanted surveillance, eavesdropping, recording, or observation without the knowledge and consent of all participants subject to such recordings.” Public or open meetings also may be covered by the no-recording policy “when it has been explicitly stated that recording is not permitted.”

New Princeton students were met with a table of condoms during orientation for an “I Love Female Orgas m” presentation. The students, particularly young women seeking to further their education do not rank being receptacles as top priority of their education.

Several recent incidents of antisemetic graffiti on campus and around town. Where is campus security as the defacing of property occurs? There are cameras everywhere so why haven’t they been identified and caught? Hate is tolerated by inaction. Yet protestors are protected by campus security and town police as they shut down roads and march through Princeton.

“Here is the statement on disruption from the administration — touted as an improvement.
“Going forward, attendees at University events should expect a single warning not to disrupt an event or prevent an invited guest from speaking. Disruptive behavior may result in immediate removal from the event and disciplinary action or other consequences.”
Let me see if I can crack the code. “A single warning” means “one free shot” to disrupt a speaker. “Should expect” translates to “or maybe more, it depends.” And “disruptive behavior may result in ...” signals passivity.
You are either serious about protecting speech or you are not serious. A serious administration might have communicated something like this: “Those who disrupt a speaker will be immediately removed and may face additional consequences.”

ai_summary: At Purdue University, students find a challenging academic environment , particularly excelling in STEM fields, that fosters personal growth and a strong work ethic . The campus offers a variety of resources, including clubs, study spaces, and supportive professors who genuinely care about student success. While the community is lively with numerous events, athletics, and a friendly atmosphere, concerns about housing availability and parking persist, especially as demand increases. The university's location in West Lafayette, while rich in opportunities, may feel isolating at times for those seeking more off-campus activities. Overall, Purdue's commitment to student involvement and the diverse community allows many to thrive both academically and socially.

reviews.text: I am very excited to attend Purdue University! I feel that its academics as well as social life will help me succeed there.

I absolutely loved attending Purdue University. The course were very diverse, the student population was very diverse, there were many different programs offered. The courses were challenging & the professors were more than helpful in most courses. College definitely prepared me for the real world as far as adhering to deadlines.
Purdue has been a great experience with strong academics, hands-on projects, and good career opportunities, though large classes can make it harder to get help.
I loved everything about Purdue but the housing crisis was a major issue for me. The professors are great and campus life is great.
The transportation system sucks so bad other than that I loved my time here and the school is great just need to work on some things
It was a great experience. The people there are awsome and the academics are top. I would really recommend it to anyone considering applying or studying here.

I loved Purdue! I had so many amazing professors that were very supportive and encouraging throughout my time as a student. The campus is beautiful and there are many study spaces, coffee shops, and great food on every corner of campus. I also loved that there is a club for anything you may be interested in. The Corec has amazing facilities to workout in and offers lots of free workout classes. One downside is that there is currently a housing shortage which has caused rent prices to increase and students have to sign a lease very early on because spaces fill up very quickly. However, there are new apartment complexes being build every year to help combat this issue. Overall I loved my experience at Purdue and would highly recommend it!
Absolutely loved attending Purdue. Very career focused wonderful campus and people. My favorite place and proud Boilermaker.
I think it is a great school for academics with some fun. It is a very safe campus, and everyone is very nice.

I am a freshman at Purdue and have really enjoyed the classes and the environment here, I took a gap year so yeah classes are academically hard but there's also tons of clubs and stuff to do around here! I t has been a great experience and cant wait to go back!
I think Purdue is a place with good academics but some of the professor aren't the best while others are great. The place is kinda borning tho.

I believe that Purdue University was a great and unique experience that challenged me both academically and personally. The university offers strong academic programs, especially in STEM fields, and the professors are knowledgeable and passionate about their subjects. I appreciated the wide range of resources available to students, including research opportunities, career services, and student organizations that make it easy to get involved and build connections. The campus culture encourages hard work and independence, which helped me grow and become more disciplined. That said, I would like to see improvements in areas such as academic advising and mental health support, as navigating requirements and managing stress can sometimes feel overwhelming. Overall, Purdue provided a solid education, memorable experiences, and valuable preparation for life after graduation.

At my time at Purdue I have met many people who have stretched the way I think daily. I am constantly having conversations that are engaging and help me better understand other peoples views as well as my own. My experience so far has been nothing but intriguing and I strive continue learning and improving everyday at Purdue.

Purdue is a great place for your education but besides that there’s not much else to do. The surrounding town is small and there are not many social scenes unless you are at least 21 or are involved in Greek life.

I love being a Boilermaker! The campus is amazing, and there's never a shortage of something to do. There are always activities going on around campus, which makes it a lot easier to meet new people and make friends. I've had a good experience with my classes so far, with many helpful professors. The only thing I would like to see change is the housing process. Students get chosen for housing on a randomized lottery basis, and because of this I have been denied on-campus housing for next year since there isn't enough housing on campus for all students. This is very disappointing as I really wanted to live on campus again.

ai_summary: Quinnipiac University elicits a mix of experiences among students, centered around its campus atmosphere, academics, and student life. Many praise the beautiful campus , small class sizes , and supportive professors who are invested in student success. Specific programs, like nursing and business, are highlighted for their rigor and quality . However, concerns arise over the social scene, with some finding it cliquey or lacking diversity , and complaints about the dining options and high tuition costs are common. Several reviews note the administration's responsiveness to student needs but also point out areas for improvement, particularly in student services and the community engagement of students. Overall, Quinnipiac is described as a place where students can forge lasting friendships and receive a decent education, yet some feel it falls short of its promising image in real-life experiences.

reviews.text: As a current freshman I'm happy at QU. There are a lot of activities to keep me busy, the classes and professors have all been engaging and helpful and there are many resources available to help you in whatever way you need. The food is mediocre as with many colleges but there is always a variety of choices and some of the places rotate their food out. Overall, Quinnipiac is a nice space to be for university. It has a lot of community, welcoming professors, and plenty of activities and things to engage in.
I like it the nursing program is amazing great labs and clinical sites. Teachers are helpful campus is beautiful

I love Quinnipiac University, the only complaints I have is when applying for classes that I need to take, it is very hard to get into them, leaving me to take random classes I don't need to graduate that I have to pay for.

Quinnipiac University provided me with an undergraduate experience that helped me boost my professional career and open up a lot of doors for me. I am excited to see the advancements the school has made overall in the past few years to enhance the student experience.

I really like how Quinnipiac maintains smaller class sizes. It makes it easier to get to know your professors and fosters more collaboration among classmates, making it comfortable to ask for help in academic discussions. Beyond the classrooms they have a diverse array of clubs and organization which helps explore new interest along with connecting with new friends. One thing I would like to see in change is the visibility of campus events. Enhancing more communication to be more proactive would allow students to more easily discover and participate in campus activies.
Really nice school with professors that really care about the students and their success. Always willing to help and meet with you to give proper/helpful advice.

Quinnipiac is an amazing university that foster the growth of your education, well being, and over success as a student, although my time here has been short I learned that the first day I landed on campus. There are many resources that are there to help you out as a student. You have multiple health counselors, academic advisors, student wellness center, and many more programs that are all at your fingertips to help you. It's. a very diverse campus with friendly and helpful people. You can for sure have a great nightlife/party scene with people and join fraternity and sorority life here too. They are more than parties and gatherings. I joined Alpha Sigma Phi recently and became a prospective brother I met some of the most amazing guys ever that I am proud to call my brothers. We really bonded and connected not with just our chapter but the whole Quinnipiac FSL community in general. We are a division 1 school with sports/club sports teams and love our bobcat athletes.

Its like home am an international student but i never felt out of place both students , staff and professors are friendly and welcoming .
When it comes to sports like hockey and basketball it’s the best.
It has been the best experience because they were so kind to give me this opportunity to transfer to this wonderful college.
Overall - Fantastic University - Staff is great - Housing is average to above average - Food is below average.

My experience with Quinnipiac University has been a mix of excitement and challenges. The admissions process was smooth, and the communication from the university has been professional and timely. The merit-based scholarship I received showed they value academic achievement, which I appreciated. The campus looks beautiful and student-centered from what I’ve researched, and their Computer and Information Sciences program seems promising. However, as an international student, the cost of attendance is extremely high, even after scholarships, which creates a financial burden. I’d love to see Quinnipiac increase need-based aid or offer more full-ride scholarships, especially for talented students from low-income backgrounds. Overall, the academic offerings and student support seem strong, but financial accessibility needs major improvement for it to be a truly inclusive university.
Make food more affordable in the cafeteria and provide additional resources and support for commuter students.

My Overall experience at Quinnipiac hasn't been too bad or too good. While I do feel like Quinnipiac is a solid School with lots of academic opportunities to take advantage of I will say the schools lack of diversity and social student events is a big draw back to the overall college experience. Especially as a Person of color going to Quinnipiac I do often feel like being the minority on campus can at times make it difficult to find real people to connect with is a bit of a challenge especially when its difficult for the majority to truly relate to your life experiences which at times leads to a bit of a disconnection to the surrounding campus environment. Because of this I feel Quinnipiac hosting more student events could help build stronger connections between students and the campus they live on.
As a upcoming senior at Quinnipiac, I have really enjoyed my time here. The food isn't great and some of the dorms suck but other than that it is a great school.

My overall experience at Quinnipiac University has been both enriching and formative. The university fosters a close-knit community that supports both academic and personal growth. I’ve especially appreciated the small class sizes, which allow for meaningful engagement with professors who genuinely care about your success. The campus is beautiful and well-maintained, and there’s a strong emphasis on preparing students for real-world careers through hands-on learning, especially in health sciences.
One of the aspects I value most is the opportunity for interdisciplinary learning. As a Health Science student aspiring to become a Physician Assistant, I’ve been able to take courses that combine medical knowledge, ethics, and accessibility. My experiences shadowing professionals and working in the field as a volunteer EMS officer have been encouraged and supported by my professors and peers.

ai_summary: Radford University appears to be a welcoming choice for many students, with a strong emphasis on community and personalized attention from professors. Most reviews highlight the caring nature of faculty , smaller class sizes, and a beautiful campus that fosters connection among students. Many appreciate the support provided to first-generation college attendees and the variety of activities and resources available, including tutoring and clubs. However, there are consistent concerns about the food options, described as limited and often unhealthy, as well as mixed feelings about the party culture and its impact on the student experience. Overall, Radford University offers a blend of academic support, community engagement, and a beautiful setting, making it a unique option compared to larger institutions.

reviews.text: I have truly enjoyed my time at Radford University. The focus on the outdoors that the school has is top-notch. As a freshman, I got hired by the Radford University Outdoors program (RUO), and it was life-changing. I have met so many incredible people, and there have been life long conctions that were fostered through my time in the program already. I am a sophmore and I can not wait to see what is still in store. I have grown in my leadership as well as my sense of responsibility. I have learned many new skills regarding the outdoors, along with life skills. The school is also much more affordable than nearby competitors, if it were not for their lower tution I might not have been able to seek higher education. I do not take that for granted, so I have put myself into my studies, and I comprehend new information in every class. I could not recommend the school more.

As an incoming Freshman, I decided on Radford because of what I experienced at the open house. The campus is beautiful and everything is walkable, however its not so small you don't get the full college experience. The students I met were great and the professors seem genuinely invested.

Radford University is a high standard college with amazing people that are willing to help you navigate further in your career life. Staying at this college has helped me be more open and being more creative. Radford University cares about your safety by having emergency poles to ring whenever you feel endangered.

Radford University has been a really welcoming school for me. I like that the classes are small enough to build relationships with professors, especially in my major. The campus feels close, and there are a lot of opportunities to get involved and find your place. Overall, Radford has helped me grow both academically and personally.

I personally love Radford University, the campus is small enough to know where everything is at and everything is easily at walking distance. It is also big enough for someone to feel like it is the perfect comfortable college size as well. The only things I would like to see changes in are the food options and parking lots. Food options we don't have much of and parking is a constant struggle to find.
I have seen a massive increase in my mental health since coming here. My senior year was rough, but now I'm doing a lot better! Everyone is so nice and accommodating.

Radford University quickly became my home. My sister went here and it drew me to this school and it was one of the best decisions of my life. The professor are knowledgeable and clearly care about the students. The activities and programs here are truly amazing and have taught me so much. I have had hardships here and mental health issues, and I can truly say that the staff and resource centers here have saved my life. I am a Highlander and I believe in this school and its mission!
Radford University has been a wonderful place where I have made so many connections, and new friendships within this community!

I really enjoy all the networking opportunities offered at Radford. Within the first month I was informed about multiple internships, career opportunities based on my major, other majors & minors that could go along with my current major. There were also multiple events that tried to get students involved and were pretty fun and inclusive that helped me gain new friends, coming here by myself.

In my time here I have seen that the university is disorganized in how it handles advising and course alterations. They also do not hold up to their own standards of caring for their students as many are left in bad positions due to the school's policies, choices and actions. It has been my experience that they will withhold the financial aid until after the semester starts leaving those who rely on that money for materials and a roof over our heads in a very bad spot, that they will not help you out of. They allow hate speakers to come in and cancel safe space events from entirely different buildings. Any attempts to communicate to the administrators the issues or create change fall on deaf ears and are often ignored. I have felt completely left behind, set back and unwanted by this university and regret choosing them for my degree.
Radford is a great university! I truly enjoyed my first year, and though it was hard being away from family, they made it possible for me to adjust very well.

Overall, great staff and professors who take their time to acknowledge your academic needs. Student life has shown a courageous outburst of joy through its students organizations. Its board of administrators advocate for student diversity and also commodity by providing gender neutral bathrooms, excellent variety of food and beverage, and space where one can find room to grow as a person.

To begin, the memories, friends, and lessons learned I have gained at Radford will forever remain installed in me. From my freshman year random roommate fiasco, to staying up all night studying for a test and missing it; I learned so many valuable, critical life lessons. Within my first 2 months of moving in, I made friends that I now consider to be my family. I got my first apartment in July of 2024, random room mating again, but I was beyond lucky this time. From paying bills, working nearly full-time, being a full-time student, social time, preparing for dental school, and so much more, made me become a self-sufficient adult without me even realizing. Having to attend to challenges alone, make decisions alone, wake up and go to school alone; I found myself in the midst of the real world. Most will say "The real world will hit you like a train," but for me, I did not even realize that I was in it until I stepped back and looked at all I had accomplished. Radford prepared me a lot.

I enjoy Radford’s Academic standpoint. The professors are easy to connect to and have a great understanding of what it’s like to be a college student. Something I would love to see change is student involvement. There aren’t many diverse activities for all students who attend. You have to fit a specific niche to enjoy the activities they offer.

My experience with Radford University also known as RU has been nothing short of pleasant. There are so many friendly faces and an uncountable amount of people willing to help out. I love the surrounding areas and I feel like the bus going from Radford to Virginia Tech is a small detail but an important one. That 20 minute drive allows students to connect with another student body as well as get their input or share their knowledge. On top of the surrounding areas, lets talk about the actual location. Radford tends to be pretty safe as well as having a easily manageable campus. One thing I would change would be how far back softball and baseball is away from campus. Not only these two sports but all sports including basketball and tennis are pushed to the outer edge of campus. Overall, Radford is the perfect fit for ME.

ai_summary: Regent University is often described as a supportive and welcoming environment , with many reviewers highlighting the kindness and accessibility of professors and faculty members. The university's academic rigor is noted, especially in smaller class sizes that enhance student interaction and engagement, making it easier for students to connect personally with their instructors. The campus is appreciated for its beauty and safety , although some students mention that the student life can be somewhat quiet, lacking a party scene. Those enrolled in online programs mention the flexibility of courses and the strong communication from faculty. While many students feel that Regent fosters a genuine Christ-centered community , some reviews express concerns about administrative inefficiencies and a desire for more financial support. Overall, students appreciate the focus on academic excellence and spiritual growth, often feeling well-prepared for their future careers.

reviews.text: Regent University was a constricted environment, the students are encouraged to "fit-in" and follow the religious rules. Diversity was tolerated through a small lens.

I love my major (animation) and I think the arts departments are pretty well funded. However, the administration and board above the professors seem to be a bit out of touch with what goes on below them. The people are very friendly and the food is reasonably priced and tastes pretty good. The dorms have a bit of a known roach problem, but they are very spacious and otherwise nice.

I like that Regent University offered a biblically-centered education and cares about preparing their students for life beyond college. However, while career is a big focus, I feel like I am not prepared for raising a family, which is important to me.
The most important thing for me during my time here has been the people I have met; almost everyone tells me the same thing, that they are here for a reason.
I do wish a lot of the courses were more up-to-date with current information (especially in industries like film) and that some of the professors were more passionate about teaching. Communication is also lacking and/or jumbled a lot of the time. The campus, however, is beautiful, and I love that its small size makes it "walkable."
Overall, I have enjoyed my time here so far, but I think the university could benefit from listening more to their students and going back to the basics on some things.
Very good education and professors as well as a en easy process to apply and navigate. It is a step up from my previous college Oru that i transferred from
