Excellent school with professors and staff that are dedicated to the students academic success. The professors are well versed in their respective fields and take the time to explain the content of each assignment. The professors also show compassion when the student experiences family hardships and shows grace to the student during those times.
It's a good school Overall, as a black student I would like to see a more diverse student and staff.

I received a call as soon as my application was submitted. My admissions advisor was Luke Hill. Unfortunately a couple of days after I heard that my application was in review. My father died suddenly. Luke called right before we made the decision to take him off life-support. Luke asked if he could pray with me. I knew at that moment I was gonna go to that school.
Regent University has challenged me on all aspects to persevere. I’ve met the challenges! I would recommend Regent University to anyone aspiring to succeed in life.
Regent University Admissions office has been more than helpful and have always responded and called back when needed. They are very helpful and always available to offer guidance and resources.

Unfortunately, my experience at this school has not been a positive one. While I believe the academic side is strong, the administartive side - particularly handling students affairs - has been exptremely disapponting. I enrolled after a friend recommended the school to me, and initially, it seemed like a good decision. However, after that first point of contact, coomunication became nearly nonexistent. When I needed assistance, my phone calls went unanswered, and emails took far too long to get a response - if I received one at all. I finished my last class at the beginning of August and did not receive a single email regarding graduation and we are now on the first week of September. I genuinely did not want this to be a negative experience and I even feel bad writing this as the professors were truly great. However there are some areas that need adjustment and I hope those changes are made because we need strong Christian organizations to thrive especially in times like these.

Regent University is a well structured learning environment with strong Christian emphasis. Professors are well poised and tailor their teachings to meet students academic needs. There is easy access of faculties and administrative members. At Regent, I can feel that someone truly cares about my success.

My experience at Regent University was definitely a unique one. I really appreciated the strong sense of community and the Christian foundation that’s woven into the academics and student life. The professors genuinely cared about the students, and I could tell they were invested not just in our academic success but in our personal growth and faith journey.

If you are thinking about attending Regent University in Virginia Beach, VA or online, please reconsider. They waited until I am 4 classes away to work to hinder me from receiving my MDIV, while facing many obstacles. I put in 3 years of research, reading, studying, writing tons of 12-16 papers and now they are trying to hinder me with lies. What kind of Christian school does that?
I like that its faith based, they have years of experience on the online college scene, and they are willing to pray for their students have been very professional and courteous.

I really love Regent a lot. I made a lot of great friends and it’s a wonderful campus. The most beautiful thing I love about Regent is that it brings me closer to God. I’ve grown more into my faith with the people that share the same beliefs and go deeper into the Word and the life of Jesus. I slowly started to change my ways and grow as person. Regent has been such a wonderful time ever since I started and I would love to continue to be at Regent and continue my major, which is animation, so I can develop more into my art and express my art to people and spread the love and goodness of making stories and entertainment that would be enjoyable for them.

ai_summary: Regis University receives mixed reviews from its students, with notable themes emerging around academics, campus life, and community. Many students appreciate the small class sizes and the opportunity for one-on-one interaction with professors, highlighting instructors who care about their success and provide supportive learning environments. The campus is described as beautiful, promoting a community feel, though some find it lacking in vibrant student life and social events, particularly on weekends. Financial concerns are a common thread, with students noting increasing tuition costs and issues surrounding financial aid that do not align with the rising expenses. While there are positive remarks about specialized programs , particularly in health-related fields, some students express dissatisfaction with administration and student services, including safety concerns and perceived disorganization. Overall, Regis University is seen as a tight-knit community with solid academic offerings, but it faces challenges that may impact the overall student experience.

reviews.text: A wonderful place to find your purpose and a solid direction. Great academics and solid career guidance. Fabulous Jesuit foundation.

I have loved working with Regis University to complete my Bachelor’s degree. The campus is beautiful, diverse, and welcoming. They constantly do safety alert emails and drills keeping real time updates to students and staff. The biggest advantage is they work with Front Range Community College allowing a more affordable option to get a degree from a university. They allow you to take three years at FRCC instead of two and then transfer for one year to get a Bachelor from their university. Without that option I would have had too big of a financial burden and stopped at my associates. In addition, they have 6 and 7 week courses online. Meaning more essays and projects every week but its worth it! The short classes make it easy to push through and focus on only 2 classes at once instead of four meaning you can really retain the information and course material. Also eliminating the mid semester burnout or low motivation because the classes are complete quickly. Highly recommend.
Regis University seems to have really solid academic programs and I am enjoying the nursing program specifically here.
Wonderful scholarship opportunities; all my professors were caring and personable; they improved the student hall significantly from when I first started, it's more modern, diverse, and welcoming.
An excellent university with strong support services and challenging studies. I learned so much there and would complete my PhD there if it was available

Regis University is not a big party school where you can walk around campus and not recognize anyone. It's a close-knit community where everyone is included and the professors know you by name. There is always someone ready to help you, and they have great support systems for anyone struggling.

The staff and advisors have all been knowledgeable and helpful in all steps. The professors have been great for the most part-when you are online it's not as easy as going up to them before or after class-it can be a bit of a waiting game with some, others are quick at responding.
Very hard program, but hopefully to finish successfully. They have the only working program in Colorado, which works for me because I have a mortgage and a whole ton of other bills. I have to pay.

Regis University is a beautiful campus and the professors are amazing. The area surrounding Regis isn’t the best, but it could be worse. Everyone you meet on campus is incredibly nice and admin makes sure that there are a ton of fun things to do around campus.

I’ve loved my time as an accelerated nursing student at Regis University in Denver. The rigorous one-year BSN program combines over 750 clinical hours with the Jesuit values of caring for the whole person, social justice, and ethical leadership. This experience has been both challenging and transformative, preparing me to serve as a confident and compassionate nurse.

I loved the environment and small class sizes. Professors wanted the best for their students and helped in any way they could. I would like to see an increase in safety precautions considering the surrounding area isn't very safe.

Regis has been laying off employees like crazy and cancelling degree programs. It's easy to see why. Admissions told me I qualified for a scholarship in January and give me the scholarship for 7 months, then said they did so in error and charged me for their "mistake." Their mistake screwed up my student loans and now I have late fees and a balance. The staff doesn't return calls. The admission process was awful. Most of the teachers don't even work at Regis, they're all fill in's from other Colleges, mostly community colleges. Was such a mistake going here. I'm so disappointed in every aspect of the "college."

Regis is primarily an education focused school. There is not a large party life, athletic presence, safe community etc. The professors are very kind, and the intimate education you get from the school make a huge difference and is a benefit of the small school environment.

As a current nursing student at Regis University, I am proud to be part of a program that emphasizes both clinical excellence and compassionate, values-based care.
One of the aspects I appreciate most is the small class sizes and personalized support from experienced nursing faculty who truly care about student success.
In addition, Regis has strong clinical partnerships with top hospitals and healthcare systems across Colorado, providing diverse experiences in acute care, community health, mental health, and more. The focus on critical thinking, cultural competence, and evidence-based practice makes me feel confident that I’m being prepared to deliver high-quality care in a variety of settings.
Regis University has provided me with the resources, support, and training I need to succeed in nursing while also encouraging me to grow as a leader and advocate for others.

Regis should be avoided at all costs! This schools bottom line is money and as a student you will feel that in every aspect of your education. The teacher’s, while promised small classes and Hanson, do not care about students wellbeing in any regards. I have watched professors outright bully students in front of the class. The administration does nothing to help students and utilizes funding on things like brand new golf carts and trees rather than student facing changes. I reached out for financial assistance getting a bill paid to be able to register for classes in the upcoming semester - I expressed that I was facing financial hardship and was wondering if they could help me with a payment plan because paying in full would mean choosing to pay that or my rent. The response I received was quote: “I can’t lift the registration hold unless you pay in full, I suggest you find the money somehow so you can register.” So unless you enjoy being tossed aside as a student RUN AWAY!

ai_summary: Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute (RPI) is known for its rigorous academic programs , particularly in engineering and computer science, with professors who are accessible and genuinely care about student success. Many students appreciate the collaborative atmosphere , emphasizing a shared journey through challenging coursework rather than fierce competition. However, this challenging environment often translates to a heavy workload and can be intimidating for some. While the wealth of student organizations and clubs helps foster community, many reviews point out a lack of vibrant student life outside academics, with limited activities in the surrounding Troy area, which some find uninviting. Additionally, there are mentions of accessibility issues on campus and a need for improvement in student services and welfare. The cost of attendance is frequently highlighted as a significant drawback, with students feeling that while the education is valuable, the high tuition could be prohibitive. Overall, RPI is suited for motivated individuals seeking a focused, technically rigorous education in a close-knit community, albeit one that may present challenges in social engagement and campus amenities.

reviews.text: RPI offers a rigorous STEM-focused education with strong research opportunities and industry connections. The workload is demanding, and the campus community is highly driven. Some may find the academic pressure intense, but those who thrive enjoy access to solid resources and a respected degree. Campus life and location in Troy, NY have their pros and cons. Overall, a solid choice for students passionate about engineering and technology.

If you are a woman and understand that you're deserving of safety, respect, and being taken seriously, do not attend this school. Officials at this school will not adequately handle harassment, assault, or rape. In freshman year alone almost every girl I have spoken to, as someone who is highly socially involved, has had an experience with some form of harassment at the very least. ROTC and the athletics departments will be deal with the men who pose threats to safety, but the school will not remove them from their victims lives. At least a dozen men in the co 2029 have allegations and documented harassment towards women noted against them. This school will at the least deny, and allegedly delete, evidence of cornering, harassing, and so on, when relayed. You will not be taken seriously, you will be told you don't have enough evidence for a complaint, and no contact orders will not hold up the way they're supposed to. The scholarships are great, but your safety isn't worth it.

It's a difficult school, obviously, which is something I didn't realize going into it, but they made it very obvious to me very quickly. The classes I take are significantly more challenging than some AP-level or college courses I took in high school, and probably more challenging than they would've been at a state school, but it's definitely worth it. A lot of the professors I've met so far take the time to get to know me and help me understand things if I take the time to meet with them and express my difficulties/grievances first. It's a small campus, which some people may not like because you're seeing the same people frequently, but it's nice in a way. It's very familiar and "homey" for lack of better wording. There are some shortcomings, as every college has, like the overall location and the food, and the housing, but in sum, I think it really is an amazing school with some great opportunities.

I am a current sophomore at RPI. I feel like I will be able to get a job pretty easily coming out of college, but I have to work ridiculously hard to get good grades. I basically have to choose between studying, having a life outside of school, and sleep (I do not sleep much). I am actively working to improve the school through student government, so I have some complaints that I would like to fix. For one, the FIXX services are not amazing. A friend of mine had a mouse in her dorm last year and was told to wait three days before they could remove it. I have texture issues with food and, as a result, lost a lot of weight from not eating last year. Some professors are not very accommodating to my reading learning disability. On the other hand, I feel like I really fit in and have met amazing friends. I have joined the right clubs and feel welcomed and supported by my fellow students. If you come to RPI, your experience is very much what you make of it.
As a HASS major it’s hard to find others who are HASS majors but in the end everyone is a nerd and enjoyable to talk to.

I have found my home here. RPI is very great. Professors are mainly wonderful, with the exception of a few who do not respect students at all and purely view them as someone who provides their paycheck. Food can widely be improved, especially for those with allergies, since RPI claims to offer such a wide variety, make sure that carries over to the allergen/dietary restriction rooms/cuisine options as well.

I absolutely love it here. I really like my program and have found a great community. People are really collaborative and willing to help each other out, even other majors or years. The program is definitely rigorous, but it only brings people together more. There are tons of opportunities for hands-on experience within all majors. Professors are super nice and accommodating to different students and their needs. They're also really passionate about their work and like having undergraduates who are interested in research. Diversity isn't the best, but I've never felt like it's a big problem as a woman. I've always felt welcome. The university fosters a good student/campus life, holding lots of free events to socialize and learn. The food and on-campus housing are eh. They're truly nothing special, but also not terrible. I always found something I liked, even with my allergies. The surrounding area is nice once you get to know it. There's lots of niche cool spots and neighborhoods.

RPI was and is a truly amazing experience. I have truthfully enjoyed every second I have spent here. I have seen a lot of other colleges, and this one takes the cake in every respect. Definitely the school for you if you are up for a little challenge with the perk of meeting the best people you will ever meet.
Great school, every professor is very understanding and kind. The people I've met have been amazing friends!
I enjoy the challenging academics at RPI. It is a great school filled with amazing people. The food and dorms aren't amazing, but I feel like that is the experience no matter where you go to college.
The first year experience was quite good. It made transitioning from higschool to college quite easy
If you want to go into engineering this is the school to be at for challenging and rewarding curriculum. Graduates are in high demand from this school

Consider this school if you are ready for a challenge and want to go into a lot of research or use a lot of labs. Everyone is right about how hard this school can be, but it is for the people that are ready to study, think about their future, and are willing to put themselves out their for opportunities that may come their way. If your major requires a lot of hands-on or lab experience, RPI will make sure you get a lot of that experience. Doing research as an undergraduate student is also very important at RPI and will have many opportunities to do so, but you must be willing to reach out and ask a lot of questions. Professors and researchers love when others will ask questions because it will show interest in what they are doing.

I was a business major at Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute which was a rather niche major at the school as it is a STEM focused school. As a business major you get a lot of exposure in a lot of STEM related courses which I definitely liked a lot and you are going to be more connected to companies that specialize more in STEM. Campus itself is very nice but the buildings are very old with few newer buildings which are only for engineering majors. Business majors definitely feels more neglected compared to other majors while computer science and engineering have very good job outlook for people who graduate as their program is well respected and rigorous. What I would like to see change is definitely more renovations and better food in the dining halls where they keep their consistency everyday. Every building is pretty old and wished that computer science courses are easier with more resources for students to succeed in their courses because as a business major it was a difficult course

Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute offers a rigorous academic experience and plentiful opportunities to engage in research. Student life is easy to get involved with, and there are always activities in and around campus. However, administration is slow to respond to and often dismissive of student body requests for campus improvement and policy change. Despite student protests, a recent policy change added staff oversight to the student government. Overall, RPI is an excellent school where I've made some of my closest friends, but I would like to see some administrative changes to be more responsive to student insight.

ai_summary: Rice University offers a distinct blend of rigorous academics and a supportive community , highlighted by its unique residential college system that fosters a strong sense of belonging among students. Many reviews indicate a collaborative atmosphere where students and professors actively support each other, emphasizing the " Culture of Care ." While the academic environment is described as challenging and competitive , this can also lead to feelings of stress among some students, particularly outside the STEM fields. The campus itself is noted for its beauty and the variety of opportunities available, including access to vibrant extracurricular activities and diverse social events. Students appreciate the resources and support provided for career development, and the financial aid options are recognized as being particularly generous. However, some express concern over the administration's responsiveness to student needs and the challenges faced by non-STEM majors. Overall, Rice is characterized by its diversity, engaging professors, and a community that values innovation and personal connections.

reviews.text: great education, am part of the student government, love the education support but would like more student support though,
Rice University is a great school, from academic rigor, extracurricular activities, and student life.
I’ve enjoyed my time at Rice greatly in the past two years and I settled in, but the first two years were a little iffy due to the student body present at Rice.
Rice has a truly amazing and supportive community. As a premed, it provided me with every opportunity and experience I could have asked for. Truly grateful
The best there is. The atmosphere is sublime. I liked that relations between all kinds of people were respected.

Rice has great academics and so many opportunities to explore whatever field you are interested in. The social scene can be very rigid, but there's so much more to do outside of campus in the Houston area.

Great so far! Campus is very nice and not too big which I like so walking around is not bad. Surrounding neighborhood is safe with many grocery stores, restaurants, and shopping nearby. Professors and classmates are all amazing, kind, intellectual, motivated, and passionate in their work. Many events to help with career development and success after graduating.

The food went downhill but's coming back. No one goes to Rice for the parties, but if you want to go out and have friends to go out with then you can make it happen. Admin is trying to push back against party culture, but student governments are working to prevent that. Rice is really a special place, you can never really "get it" until you're there.

As an international student, I can confidently say that Rice University truly feels like home. The professors here do not just teach; they genuinely care. They want to get to know you as a person.
Everyone at Rice is incredibly smart, and what stands out even more is how supportive the environment is. It is the opposite of cutthroat. Students are collaborative, kind, and always willing to help each other grow. People lift you up, not by showing off their success, but by sharing it with you and bringing you along.
Houston is an amazing city to explore, and there is always something happening on and off campus.
Our current president, Reginald DesRoches, is one of the most hardworking and inspiring leaders I have ever seen. You can feel how much he loves Rice and how committed he is to making it even better.
Every year, Rice climbs higher in the rankings, and as a current student, I can honestly say it feels like it gets better by the minute. I am so grateful I chose Rice.

There’s truly no place like Rice. It’s a school where you’re surrounded by some of the smartest, most driven students in the country—but without the cutthroat energy. People here are brilliant but collaborative, ambitious but kind. Everyone’s chasing something big, but they’ll still help you study for your midterm.
The academics are top-tier—professors actually know your name, classes are rigorous but meaningful, and you’re pushed to think deeply and grow constantly. What makes Rice different is the warmth. The people, the traditions, the way your residential college becomes your weird little family—it all feels intentional and human. There’s space for every kind of student here and somehow, all of those people become friends.
It’s a place full of opportunity, curiosity, and heart. You get the best of all worlds: elite academics, genuine community, freedom to explore, and a sense that you belong. Rice isn’t just a great school, it’s the perfect college experience.
Rice is just great. Best academics, very smart people, a lot of available resources and great student life. My best decision ever!

Rice has been an incredible experience—academically rigorous, yet supportive. The small classes and engaged professors make learning personal and collaborative. I’ve loved the flexibility to explore different disciplines, and the residential college system creates a strong sense of community. Campus is beautiful, and resources for research and internships are solid. However, mental health services need improvement—wait times can be long—and I’d like to see more structured support for entrepreneurial and non-traditional career paths. Overall, Rice helped me grow both intellectually and personally, and I’m grateful for the opportunities it’s given me.

I had the best 4 years at Rice where I've grown as a student in my academics, had fantastic time meeting lifelong friends and got involved in many activities, tons of events with free food and merch and enjoyed the student life to the fullest. I think everyone should come to Rice - it's truly an amazing place.
Overall very good campus atmosphere, great student life, and very nice and non-competitive academic environment. Only gripe may be that Houston, the city itself, can get boring after a while.

ice University strikes a perfect balance between academic rigor and a close-knit, collaborative community. For someone like me—deeply curious, driven by purpose, and with an international background—Rice feels like a place where I wouldn’t just study, I’d thrive.
The small class sizes mean real conversations with professors, not just lectures. And with strong programs in data science, global studies, and leadership development, Rice would be an ideal environment to grow into a thoughtful and skilled changemaker. I especially admire how it values interdisciplinary thinking—blending history, technology, and public impact in a way that mirrors my own ambitions.
Plus, the campus is beautiful, the culture is supportive, and there's a genuine push toward innovation with heart. It’s a university that feels both elite and human—something rare and worth aiming for.

ai_summary: Robert Morris University - Pennsylvania offers a small , close-knit campus environment where students often feel a strong sense of community and support from faculty. Many reviews highlight the accessibility and dedication of professors , who typically know students by name and are willing to provide help outside of class. The campus is described as beautiful and safe, with a variety of clubs and activities that foster social connections, though some students note that weekend social life can be lacking due to many commuters going home. Academically, RMU is recognized for its diverse course offerings and good programs, particularly in fields like business and nursing; however, some students express concerns about the high cost of tuition and the need for improvements in communication and administrative support. Overall, while the college experience at RMU is enriched by opportunities for involvement and personal connections, students also mention challenges related to sports performance, living conditions, and administrative decisions that could impact student life.

reviews.text: I thought I would like this school from online and in-person visit but it I honestly is terrible if you aren’t from the area. It is very local and very little students and on weekends it’s a ghost town. There is absolutely no diversity, you will feel out of place. It’s a very hit or miss university with academics, food, professors, transportation, etc. Also it is not Pittsburgh, it’s 1.5 hours away on bus and 40 mins driving from the city. I overall wouldn’t say to come here unless you’re an athlete or planning on joining a frat/sorority because there isn’t much of social interaction on campus.

My experience at Robert Morris University has been very positive. I like the smaller class sizes because they make it easier to talk to professors and get help when needed. The professors really do care about their students and want them to succeed. I also appreciate that RMU focuses on hands on learning through projects and internships, which has helped me better understand my major and prepare for my future career.
The campus is welcoming, and I have met many friendly people. One thing I would like to see improve is easier access to academic advising during course registration. Overall, RMU has given me a strong education and a supportive place to grow as a student.

I've really enjoyed my time at Robert Morris University so far. The campus is very diverse, welcoming, and offers a plethora of academic resources and guidance. I've found that my professors are engaging and helpful in answering any questions I might have about the class or university in general. There are plenty of opportunities to get involved at Robert Morris, and I find myself taking advantage of those opportunities that they have offered to me! Making friends at Robert Morris is effortless, and it is easy to feel included, as it is a very comforting and friendly campus! Overall, I think Robert Morris University is a great place for a person to learn, and obtain the knowledge they need to find a career for them!

ai_summary: At Rochester Institute of Technology (RIT), students often comment on the strong academic rigor and dedicated faculty , especially in STEM fields, offering a blend of theoretical and practical experiences through its celebrated co-op program . The campus culture is described as "nerdy" and supportive, fostering a diverse community that includes a significant presence of deaf and hard of hearing students, which creates a unique environment for cultural exchange. While many appreciate the inclusivity and numerous extracurricular activities , some note that social life can be quieter compared to larger universities, with critiques about campus events' effectiveness in promoting interaction. The winters and general atmosphere in Rochester are frequently mentioned, highlighting challenges such as cold weather and limited nearby attractions . Overall, RIT is recognized for its academic strengths and opportunities, but potential students should be prepared for its specific social dynamics and environment.

reviews.text:
Being a student at RIT I will say that their are a big gap between students and how the student life acts. Being part of a Tech school the social life from everyday isn't something that every student know but that's ok. People from time to time get out of their comfort zone but unfortunately not common. But from that RIT is a great school with academics but also with what comes after the work, RIT sets you up with a great intern system that can guarantee you a job after school like myself which is something I love.
But I would love to see change is that not all majors don't have this guarantee as some majors their job market isn't need so seeing them being helped more to find a job is something I think that needs to be touched on and fixed.
I would like to see the campus food change for the better. The campus food isn't the best and the options are mediocre.
Rochester may be a dangerous city but the campus is definetely safe and the academics are pretty good as well as the professors. They have some really good programs and they help you with co-ops.

In my experience, RIT has been a great place to get real hands-on experience. Classes are designed to be things you will need in the workforce, and apply learned skills to how they would be used in professions. There is pretty much everything you could need in the area around campus, while still having good and active sports and greek life.

Rochester Institute of Technology, while overwhelming at first, has become a place I can confidently call home after one year. It’s incredibly easy to make friends and find your niches. With all the clubs and Wellness classes available, you’re able to join communities and meet people who share your interests. I joined NOVA, a K‑pop dance crew, which has been incredibly rewarding and has brought me so much joy and friendship.
Academics are strong as well. There are many professors and Teaching Assistants ready to help you explore your interests or deepen your understanding and knowledge. If you ever need extra academic support, there are plenty of helpful resources like the Writing Center and the Academic Support Center, where the staff are always friendly and encouraging. For example, I used to hate presenting and felt nervous every time, but after working with the Academic Support Center, my anxiety faded and my skills improved significantly.

RIT is a good school for those interested in academics and making friend that share your interests. I have join several clubs and organization and everyone has fun there. I learn a number of new skills from the classes, including certifications.
Great school if you’re focused on your career and major, not so great if you want the typical “college” experience that includes partying, going out with friends, and that sort of stuff.

My overall experience is focused and creative, built around hands on classes and strong friendships formed through collaborative study sessions, where i can stay locked in academically while still feeling socially connected.
Rochester Institute of Technology is a place where you meet students who are ambitious and focused on pursuing their careers and entering the workforce

My experience at RIT has been mostly positive. At this school I have been able to meet a lot of very friendly and like minded people. The diversity of the school makes it so anybody can find a group that they mesh with. My first semester classes have been going really well, and my adjustment to college life has gone really well. This is in part to me finding a group of friends that I am with a lot and connect to very well. The food on campus is pretty good, the main dinning hall where you can use meal swipes isn't good so I wouldn't go there. But the rest of the locations are pretty good, and the Ben and Jerry's on campus is really nice. The campus life is a bit lacking. There is usually things going on around campus, I just haven't been super interested in going to them. But there has been some events, especially in my dorm hall that I have gone to and enjoyed. The main thing I would like to see changed is how the class selection for the next semester works. Overall a good experience.

I started back in August 2025 and I honestly have really enjoyed my time so far. Coming from a deep south state, yes it is cold. I absolutely adore the snow, though. Gracie's is really not THAT bad. It's not my favorite and I haven't been back in a few weeks, but I'm not low on dining dollars. The corner store is great, the tunnels connect everything pretty well and they're not hard to navigate. Sometimes it's a little dirty, but I want to blame that on gross college kids. It is pretty expensive to go here, but it's expensive to go anywhere nowadays.

I am a game design major, and I really do like the curriculum that RIT has to offer for it. It's a lot of everything the industry has to offer, such as gameplay mechanics, actual design, 3D modeling, etc. While they also do have a UX/UI class, I wish they had a more intensive one. Outside of classes, the campus is big and pretty nice. The food is alright, however a majority of it is fried or fast food. Brick City offers meals, but they close early, so it's not a dinner place.
The school has very good academics and programs dedicated to student's future. They also have a lot of things for students to do in their free time
It's a great university. They offer a variety of courses and have a very very well co-op program, ranked #4 for their co-ops.

I applied to this school for the co-op opportunities, and it has not disappointed so far. There are always opportunities to connect with companies on campus, and the classes I've been taking my first semester have already been setting me up for success by incorporating real world models into lessons. As a CS major, there has been a lot of support from classmates, my professors, and the student instructors, and I've never felt like there wasn't any way to get help when I needed it. Overall, RIT is a great university with lots of offer!

ai_summary: Roosevelt University offers a diverse and inclusive environment , with many students appreciating the supportive and engaged faculty who are dedicated to their success. The small class sizes are often highlighted as beneficial for personalized learning , allowing students to form connections with professors. However, several reviewers raised concerns about the effectiveness of the administration and financial aid services, mentioning unresponsiveness and communication issues. While campus life isn't vibrant for everyone, particularly for commuters, many find the location in downtown Chicago to be a significant advantage , providing ample opportunities for internships and exploration. Overall, the university fosters a strong sense of community and commitment to social justice, although some students feel that the administration's priorities don't always align with student needs.

reviews.text: The only problem I’ve really had is housing , I’d get charged for damage in the dorm , but I wasn’t the one to do it , it would usually be my roommate but they would leave the dorm first at the end of the semester. It really wasn’t fair I was left with these charges.

Roosevelt University is a great school. I completed my first semester in december and looking forward to starting my second semester in January. I would say that Roosevelt provides a lot of opportunities for many different things. The classes are challenging but it is worth it if you have a good support system. I made lots of new friends and endless memories.

My experience as a Roosevelt University student has been great so far, the teachers and faculty are always ready to help. It's a pretty diverse school, with lots of international students from different countries;even I , myself am an international student. Roosevelt University is in downtown, which is amazing and gives students a great opportunity to explore the city in many different ways, and Roosevelt University gives every student enrolled into the school a U-pass which grants students unlimited train and bus rides, which makes transportation so much easier.

As a full-time junior completing my prerequisites for the Radiography program at Roosevelt University, my experience has been very positive so far. My professors have been supportive and accessible, and the coursework has prepared me well for applying to the program in a couple of months. I really appreciate the encouraging academic environment and the helpful advising resources that keep me on track toward my goals. Roosevelt makes me feel confident about taking the next step into the Radiography program this fall, if accepted, and I feel that the university genuinely supports students pursuing healthcare careers.
Roosevelt is a great school in downtown Chicago. It has everything a student needs in the city and then some.

Roosevelt University is at the heart of Chicago! there is always a lot of places to visit and try around you. The lake makes it fun to be surrounded by and the views are amazing. The academic life is manageable because of how helpful professors and staff are. Easy to make friends in a diverse school. A lot of professors I have met are willing to help you with course work if you are truly putting the effort into your academics.
I love the fact that Roosevelt University is in the heart of downtown Chicago. I am able to experience all that the city has to offer while also completing my degree and preparing for my career.

Roosevelt has a welcoming environment that makes all students feel included. The wide diversity in students and professors makes me feel seen and appreciated. Overall Roosevelt has been a pleasant experience.
I really enjoyed Roosevelt University but I do think they should work on their information because i found myself in situations that was hard to get out because they didn’t give me proper information
My experience with Roosevelt University as a musical theater major was excellent. Great professors and amazing things to learn.

During my time at Roosevelt University, I’ve grown so much both personally and academically. Like many students entering college feeling anxious and overwhelmed is a common thing. This was especially true for me as someone navigating undiagnosed anxiety and other personal challenges. Roosevelt stepped up for me in ways I could never have imagined. From professors checking in on me long after classes ended to see how I was doing beyond academics, to faculty members watching my car so I wouldn’t get a ticket when I was running late. Roosevelt has shown me time and again that it’s more than just a university. It’s a community that truly cares. It’s a place where students are supported, seen, and treated like family.

I am currently a tranfer student from a community college and my experience with Roosevelt has been very welcoming.The faculty is very polite and helpful and makes students feel comfortable and accepted.

My experience with Roosevelt University had been amazing so far, the housing for students are very good with the beautiful view of downtown chicago. They have great, staff and faculty that are always ready to help in anyway they can. It’s a very diverse school, with people from all over the world which makes it very nice as you get to meet different people with different backgrounds.
Lovely school. Their commitment to their mission shines through in the faculty they retain and the treatment of individual students.

My favorite overall experience at Roosevelt university is that it is one of the best colleges in the city of Chicago. There is so much to do both in the school and in the surrounding campus . The study rooms can be both beautiful with scenery or quiet and secluded.

ai_summary: Rowan University offers a diverse and supportive academic environment , praised for its small class sizes and approachable professors who genuinely care about student success. Many students highlight the flexibility of programs , including online options, which cater to different learning styles. The campus is noted for being inviting, with many amenities like updated buildings and a variety of clubs and activities, though some concerns exist about the availability of parking and dining quality. The community vibe encourages student involvement, whether in academics, clubs, or social events, making it easier to form friendships. However, feedback indicates some inconsistency in advising quality and certain academic programs, especially in the STEM fields. Overall, students feel that Rowan University provides a valuable educational experience while maintaining a tight-knit campus atmosphere.

reviews.text: Rowan has great student life. There were endless clubs and activities on campus. I wish they cared as much about their guidance departments as they did their extra curricular activities.

Rowan University is where my passion meets my purpose. Their music education program doesn't just teach students how to perform, but it trains compassionate, skilled educators who transform lives through music. The faculty are deeply invested in student growth, offering hands-on experience in real classroom settings from early on. For me, music has always been more than sound; it has been healing, expression, and community. Rowan's program aligns perfectly with my goal of becoming an educator who reaches students the way music once reached me. I don't just want to attend Rowan University; I want to grow there and give back there.

As a freshman at Rowan University my experience has been wonderful with excitement for new opportunities to grow academically and personally. One thing about Rowan University is the campus environment with many places to enjoy, walking around, and the people there who make this place what it truly is today. A thing that I would like to change is the parking situation with how many student there is on campus every day it is extremely hard to find parking in oder to go to your next class on time especially as a commuter.

I believe that my time at Rowan University, and as a transfer student, has been great with academics but has certain flaws. As a transfer student who has only completed an entire semester along some spring, I have had a great time with my classes. The professors, curriculum, deadlines, and classmates have helped me along my academic journey. I was comfortable with asking questions and not feeling dismissed, I was also given amazing opportunities at Rowan. While the academic life is great, there is one large issue that can be given to future students and commuters. The parking situation in the University is not the greatest. Most students arrive about 1-2 hours before their class in order to find a nearby parking as there aren't many spaces. Not only do I believe that parking is an issue, but also the campus food. There are limited options for on-campus meals, and off-campus meals that are affordable. Overall, my time at Rowan has been very great and can make future students succeed.

The campus is very inviting and self-contained. There are numerous food options and housing options. There are many activity and sports offerings, as well as clubs. The location is ideal for resident students and commuters alike.

Rowan University has a lot to offer, and being part of this community genuinely feels meaningful. The university has grown rapidly over the past decade, and that growth shows in its expanding academic programs, strong professional pathways, and commitment to student success. At the same time, the fast pace of expansion brings challenges that are worth acknowledging.
Strengths
Academic flexibility: Rowan offers a wide range of programs, including strong online options, which makes it accessible for working adults and nontraditional students.
Supportive faculty: Many professors are deeply invested in student learning and bring real‑world experience into the classroom.
Diverse student body: The community includes students from many backgrounds, creating a dynamic learning environment.
Career‑focused programs: Rowan emphasizes practical skills, internships, and professional development, which helps students prepare for real careers rather than just earning a degree.

I have enjoyed my experience at Rowan heavily so far. If you are looking for a place that is not too far from home but still gives you that college experience, Rowan is the place to go. There is a huge variety of majors to choose from, each supported by a wonderful academic staff there to help you succeed.
I enjoyed the campus and the buildings for each department. The professors and student lives were very warming.

Rowan University is a bit much when you first start, but it is a wonderful university. There’s always something going on every other weekend, more than a handful full of groups you can join and make new friends, as well as the many tutors.
I love Rowan! The atmosphere, the student events, the staff, everything! I have been really impressed with my experience here.
I currently just transferred to rowan university from oa community college. My experience has been really good so far. The appartments are nice and rowan boulevard is my favorite!

I think Rowan is a good school depending on the major you want to study. If you are persistent for medicine or engineering, I think it has great academic opportunities. I believe the housing and campus life are average compared to other schools.
I I experienced the diversity of education among professors, lecturers, and even mistresses.There are lots of campus opportunities and Job for Students.

I should have never came here. The only thing was good was tuition was cheaper than other places. The cops here suck. They target college students and ticket them for stupid stuff and look at the college kids as their way to make their quota and make the township money. Housing people here suck also. If you ever need help or have a complaint you are just wasting your time. They will never listen and actually go against you. You will never get help with internships. Don't bother asking or trying.
I have been here for over a year and I would say this uni is pretty chill. As an international student, I have no complaints.

ai_summary: At Russell Sage College, students appreciate the intimate and supportive atmosphere fostered by the small class sizes and tight-knit community. Many reviews highlight the dedication of professors who are accessible and genuinely care about student success. The campuses, located in Troy and Albany, provide a unique blend of college and city experiences , although some students mention challenges with commuting and campus organization. While student life offers various activities and a sense of camaraderie , there are mixed feelings about the dining options and social opportunities on campus. Reviews also suggest improvements are needed in communication regarding course offerings and administrative services, but overall, students feel valued and recognized as individuals in their academic pursuits.

reviews.text: I think a change to the Albany campus dining hall situation would be beneficial. There is a lot of waste and not a lot of good options. More of a buffet style like Troy would be good, where you scan in and have plenty of options. The buffet options in Albany are never very good and you can’t have enough variety from just the grill or sandwich station.
Best school in the world, the people and the atmosphere is what really makes this school special. I definitely recommend any student looking here.

Russell Sage College has been an amazing place for me thus far. I am currently a Junior studying business administration. The best way I can describe Sage is as a stepping tone. With the coursework and all the on campus resources, you can tell that the goal of the institution is to set up students for a successful future.

At Sage I am a bio major and a player for the men’s varsity soccer team. Overall, I have had a very positive experience with the school. The best aspect of this college, in my opinion, is how invested each teacher is in your success.
Great class choices and great people here. There are always some different food choices and if you ever need help with anything there’s people

Russell Sage became my second house after I transferred here. I built such a strong relationship with my professors that they became my friends. All the staff members are helpful and very welcoming, everybody wants to make sure you have the best experience possible. Students are very connected and very friendly, I was part of different clubs and organizations, as well as sports teams on campus, which made me feel part of the school as a whole.
There is a lot to do around Albany and you will never have to worry about being bored or not having fun things to do. Even the party scene is great around Sage.

Sage has a lot of character. They provide many opportunities, and all of the professors just want to see you succeed. The academics are challenging, but in the right way. It does make it difficult having 2 campuses, but there is the shuttle, the option to transport with a friend, or by yourself. As for athletics, it is very welcoming, the coaches are wonderful, teammates are respectful, and they want to see your skills grow. The trainers and other staff are wonderful as well, doing everything they can to help you as an athlete. The food can be hit or miss, just like any college. And as for party life, there isn't really any unless you go to nearby schools. Sage is a welcoming place, it is cozy and small, allowing you to feel comfortable and focus on your studies, athletics, clubs, events, and social groups.

This college is the absolute worst school you could possibly go to avoid it at all costs! Going to Russell Sage College honestly feels like signing up for a long, draining, and overwhelmingly frustrating experience. The campus energy is low, the environment feels depressing, and nothing ever seems to run smoothly. Classes, communication, and campus life all feel dull, and it’s easy to feel stuck and unmotivated here
Only go to sage if you hate yourself. Especially do not go for occupational therapy because you will not pass the boards. Save yourself and go literally anywhere else.

I enjoy the support that comes from the school. Words of affirmation are my love language. It is nice to feel seen by a school rather than dread the instructors. I am excited to continue to work with such loving and knowledgeable staff.

So my experience was horrible. I can even begin to tell you what I had to experience with dorms; it was truly traumatizing. One time we had a whole mushroom growing in the bathroom because of the amount of mold; it was truly traumatizing. Academically, it's okay depending on the teacher and your major. As for me, the education major is really good, and for the most part, the teachers are really helpful and try to accommodate you to the best of their abilities and actually try to make a connection with you.

Attending Sage Graduate School ruined my life/career, I was never informed after graduating that I would be able to obtain my LMHC, why, 1 person stands out, a professor that went out of her way to hate men, she was a toxic monster.

I had a very helpful and informative experience at Russell Sage College, I am starting at this college in the fall 2025 and so far everyone i have been in contact with has been nothing but patient and helpful. Responsive ti phone calls and emails in a decently timely manner, and involved my family in the areas needed. I appreciated the fact they didnt make me feel like i was alone in this process.

The professors. are very encouraging and helpful. My school counselor has been very helpful with my return as a Graduate student. In the past the dinning hall had awesome food. Professor's were helpful. My first semester for my bachelor's degree, I didn't have any books but the professor let me use there textbook to make copies and to use for studying. They are the reason why I want to return to this college.
It was really good and I would not want to see anything change, except maybe the restaurants in the are

ai_summary: At Rutgers University–Camden, reviews highlight a strong sense of community , particularly due to its smaller campus size, which fosters closer connections between students and professors. Many students appreciate the supportive faculty who are dedicated to helping students succeed academically. The university's proximity to Philadelphia offers additional opportunities for internships and exploration, while the diverse student body enriches the social experience. However, some students express concerns about issues like financial aid, campus safety, and limited on-campus activities , especially during weekends, due to its commuter student demographic. The campus itself is generally described as inviting, though some amenities, such as food options and athletic facilities, receive mixed reviews. Overall, while many students feel they are getting a solid education, they also acknowledge areas needing improvement , particularly regarding student life and administrative efficiency.

reviews.text:
My experience at Rutgers University–Camden has been very positive so far. As a first-year student, I appreciate how supportive and welcoming the campus community is. The smaller campus makes it easier to connect with professors, staff, and other students, which has helped me get involved and feel comfortable asking for help.
I also value the school’s focus on student success and mentorship. Professors and staff genuinely care about helping students grow, and there are many resources for academic support and career development.
One improvement I would like to see is more early support for students entering challenging courses or returning to school after time away, such as additional tutoring or preparatory workshops.
Overall, my experience has been rewarding, and I look forward to continuing to grow during my time here.
I had a great experience. The staff was very kind, helpful, and compassionate and made learning enjoyable.

I take online classes. So, it works with my full-time work schedule. I have the support of a counselor and a coordinator to help me succeed here at Rutgers. There are many resources, like on-phone tech support, a librarian, etc. So, it has been a wonderful experience. Many professors open up the classes so you can have early access. Which aids in academic success. I love the fact that you can pretty much plan class time when it works best for you each week. So, better than a hybrid situation. Any issues, you can email the professor or IT.

ai_summary: Rutgers University–New Brunswick offers a diverse and vibrant college experience that caters to a wide range of student interests, from strong academic programs and research opportunities to a lively social scene filled with clubs and events. Many reviews highlight the supportive professors and numerous resources available to help students navigate their academic paths, although large class sizes can sometimes hinder personalized attention. The campus is known for its beautiful scenery and variety of experiences across its multiple campuses, but students often note challenges with the bus system and housing conditions, including issues like outdated facilities and safety concerns. The available dining options are varied but could be improved in terms of quality and hours . Overall, while students appreciate the educational opportunities and campus life, they seek enhancements in housing, transportation, and administrative support to create a more cohesive and satisfying experience.

reviews.text:
I learned a lot in both the Psychology Department and was very fortunate to take advantage of the plethora of internship, lab, and fieldwork opportunities. I thought my college experience would be repetitive and only consist of taking lecture-based courses, but there was so much I was able to take away from the out-of-classroom experiences. It's a huge department, so it's a long shot, but I do wish there were smaller sections and more opportunities to get closer to the professors. I was only able to get close with the staff at my internship, not so much the professors of my courses.
The Japanese Department, on the other hand, is very small and tight-knit. I could not have had a better experience there. I was even able to establish connections with professors whose classes I have never taken. It felt nice to have every professor in the department know my name, even if I was never their student. The content was challenging, but I always felt supported by my friends and instructors.

My overall experience at Rutgers University is one that I will always remember fondly. Every step of my journey taught me patience and personal growth. I truly feel that I took full advantage of the numerous opportunities available at Rutgers. This included utilizing Career Services, receiving internship assistance, accessing resources for graduate school, and finding clinical job opportunities through networking with student organizations.
I also appreciated the vastness of the campus, which offered many places to study, eat, and attend classes. Additionally, there are numerous student organizations that allow you to pursue your passions. For example, I have a strong interest in powerlifting, so I joined the Powerlifting Club, which I thoroughly enjoyed.
My only critiques are to increase the number of staff members in student advising in the arts and sciences department, allocate money towards refining other campuses, and transform the bus system by incorporating certain stops.

The main positives were that RU New Brunswick was a huge campus split into five different campus with their own unique facilities, departments, atmosphere, and activities to do. Cook/Douglass had green spaces, animal farms, etc and College Ave was closest to Downtown New Brunswick and had amazing, diverse food spots much better than the dining halls. You’ll never truly be bored or run out of things you can do or see (including RU Gardens, parks, stores, restaurants, etc). The main drawback would be due to the size of the school, it’s harder for staff and faculty to get to know you. You have to be prepared and willing to go the extra mile to reach out to a professor or academic advisor with a question or concern, and then follow up if they don’t reply instead of just giving up on communication. And if they still don’t reply, follow up AGAIN! Which can feel weird at first but that’s how/when you actually get the help/support, usually even more than you originally expected!

ai_summary: At Rutgers University - Newark, students frequently highlight the college's remarkable diversity and supportive community as standout features. Many reviews praise the availability of resources, such as academic support, career services, and campus facilities, including access to specialized classrooms and study areas. The professors are generally perceived as knowledgeable and helpful, with many having real-world experience, which enriches the learning environment. While academics can be challenging, there’s a sense that faculty members genuinely want students to succeed. Additionally, the campus's urban setting offers convenient access to diverse dining options and local cultural experiences. However, some reviews mention concerns about safety, parking difficulties, and the need for improvement in administrative processes. Overall, Rutgers Newark is characterized as a place for growth and community, drawing students from various backgrounds while providing a focused educational experience .

reviews.text: My experience at Rutgers University–Newark has been enriching and transformative. I appreciate the diversity of the student body and the welcoming environment, which allowed me to learn from different perspectives and build meaningful connections. The professors are knowledgeable and approachable, offering guidance both inside and outside the classroom. I particularly enjoyed the opportunities for research, hands-on projects, and community engagement, which helped me apply what I learned in practical ways. One area for improvement could be expanding campus resources and extracurricular activities to provide more support for students’ mental health and professional development. Overall, Rutgers–Newark has been an inspiring place for personal and academic growth, and I would recommend it to anyone seeking a supportive and dynamic university experience.
Very good. I didn’t join yet. I visited the campus with my friend, staff is friendly and cooperative.

I am still a student at Rutgers- Newark, but what I will say, Rutgers has a hard time helping out students in need, I expressed that I am homeless and cant afford housing and have no parental help and I am uncomfortable with taking out a loan for each semester. The adults and people in the high positions, really don't take my case seriously in my opinion, and I am expected to just figure everything out on my own while still balance school, work, and other life stressors. But nonetheless, I am still giving them their props, I do feel like housing is better than I expected, the food i okay at times, the campus is okay looking given its in Newark, I do think they can definitely do some remodeling when it comes to making Rutgers look as nice as the other campuses, but that's my opinion.

My experience at Rutgers Newark has been going well so far. I am a commuter so it definitely is different but the Business school does a great job with connecting students to future employers and they also offer a lot of opportunities to get involved on campus.

It's an amazing school that is easy to navigate. For the most part, the professors are great at teaching. One thing I'd like to see change is the campus food, not as much options as I would've thought.

Rutgers Newark is without a doubt one of the most underrated schools in New Jersey. This campus has given me opportunities I never would have had elsewhere, and I’ve met some of the best people while growing so much along the way. This university will always hold a special place in my life!

My experience at Rutgers University–Newark has been overwhelmingly positive. From the start, I felt supported by everyone—from my dean, to counselors, to professors—who guided me step-by-step toward a successful graduation plan. They were knowledgeable, accessible, and genuinely invested in my growth.
I appreciated how professors not only taught the material but helped me think critically and apply what I learned beyond the classroom. Counseling and advising were also extremely helpful, making it easy to understand requirements and stay on track.
Rutgers–Newark has a diverse, welcoming environment that encourages ambition and builds confidence. The support systems here truly make a difference. My journey hasn’t always been easy, but Rutgers made it possible for me to move forward with purpose. I’m grateful for the guidance, opportunities, and community this school has given me.

This is my first semester, I love the location in the downtown area of Newark which I refer to as college town, due to Essex County College and NJIT are with close proximity of RU. No to member its within walking distance of Penn Station which allows for a quick connect to NYC by bus or train. About 10 minutes walk to NJPAC and Prudential Center where you can enjoy a variety of entertainment. On campus you'll a selection of eateries as well as off campus within walking distance. This university has a very diverse student population and staff as well, which I feel is a great representation of the United Nation. Rutgers offers a plethora of courses/majors. I'm looking forward to learning and exploring RU student life. I would like to see more available student parking.
this campus advising is horrible and is not helpful. Ai would be better help than these advisors. Do not recommend coming to this campus. I regret it.
I would like to see more security measured being implaced, but other then that the campus is amazing and a fun to be at.

Rutgers University–Newark provides a well-rounded academic experience with dedicated faculty and a strong focus on social impact and community engagement. The student body is diverse and supportive, which creates an inclusive environment. Improvements could be made in administrative efficiency and campus communication, but overall, it’s a rewarding place to study and grow academically and personally.

At the Newark campus there is so much to eat. I have really enjoyed the R U Hungry spot as well as Ramen Gami. The shuttles run very late as well which helps me as a commuter student that lives in Brooklyn. I was concerned because I am originally from Georgia so this is a big move for me. However, everyone is friendly here which makes the adjustment easier.

My experience at Rutgers was great! I learned so much from my classes, and made long-term personal and professional connections. The professors are always available and highly knowledgeable in their areas. The only thing I would like to see improved is the communication between management/faculty and students.

Kelly Terry = Horrible
I had a very disappointing experience with Kelly Terry. As a recruitment counselor, she was unhelpful, dismissive, and often failed to provide clear or accurate information. Instead of guiding me through the process, she made everything more confusing and stressful. Emails and requests went ignored or were only partially addressed, and when I followed up, it felt like I was being brushed off.
I expected support and clarity during such an important process, but instead I felt like I was left on my own. If you’re looking for a counselor who listens, communicates effectively, and genuinely cares about helping students, I would strongly suggest working with someone else.
I loved the diversity of Rutgers Newark. I would like for the dorms to be changed/get rid of carpet.

school_name: SUNY College of Environmental Science and Forestry
ai_summary: At SUNY College of Environmental Science & Forestry, students have a unique blend of experiences marked by a strong focus on environmental studies and a tight-knit community. Many reviews highlight the challenging academic curriculum , particularly for students passionate about the environment, emphasizing that rigorous courses are balanced by supportive professors who are generally available to help. The campus environment is described as welcoming, fostering connections among students who share similar interests, although some comments reveal a sense of isolation for those less engaged in the party culture, especially with Syracuse University next door. While students appreciate the shared facilities and resources from SU, there are concerns about administrative communication and student services, which some find lacking. Opinions vary on the vibrancy of campus life, with the social scene often dependent on SU events, and there are mentions of a need for greater diversity within the student body. Overall, SUNY ESF is praised for its academic rigor and unique environmental focus, though students should be prepared for a demanding workload and an environment that may not cater to everyone's social interests.

reviews.text: It's been great so far. The athletics here are great, and the housing is even better. Each dorm has its own bathroom. however the food kinda sucks but ESF used syracuse univeritys dinal halls. All in all, it's a really great school.

Very strong sense of community. Students and faculty are very passionate about their research and are often more than willing to provide help with rigorous course work. Unfortunately, the SUNY system is attempting to severely cut ESF's budget and reduce the quality of education at the university.

I transferred to SUNY College of Environmental Science and Forestry (ESF) from my local community college. I chose it because it is the only SUNY that offers a Bachelor of Landscape Architecture. I also liked how the college had a strong focus on ecology and the environment. The professors at ESF have been great, I have been learning a lot in different classes. I like that it is a smaller school that is right next to a larger school, Syracuse University (SU), so you get both the benefits of a larger and smaller school as you can join any SU club or participate in Greek life if you want. I also like how there are many niche majors offered at this school that aren't offered in a majority of colleges in the United States, mine included. The people here seem nice and welcoming. I would like to see a change in better office hours for staff as there are sometimes inconsistent office hours. Other than that, the school is solid and has great value for New York residents and people everywhere.

SUNY College of environmental science and forestry is a welcoming community of fellow students, transfer students, alumni, and faculty that all share the same passion and values to study and help the environment.

I appreciated the well-published professors teaching many of the courses, as they had experience in their field and could teach from first hand experience. The student life is very well intertwined in day-to-day classes as there was always something to go see or participate in every week. The housing was very nice, as every room has a personal shower and plenty of space.

ESF has many issues but to sum it up in the shortest way possible: You will not enjoy your time at college and your degree will be worthless.
In the real world the name recognition of your University is far more important than ESF would like you to believe and it is a simple fact that nobody outside of Syracuse knows what SUNY-ESF is. The college stopped releasing job outcome surveys, I suspect because their job outcomes are very poor. If you're considering ESF, please consider what will happen after ESF. You will have a worthless degree that nobody wants to hire.
But beyond that, your time at ESF will also be poor. There is very little school spirit, athletics are a joke and the school ATTEMPTS to make events and school activities but they're really lame and very few people ever went to them. You can dye a shirt once a year and that's really as exciting as it gets. Most students go to the SU events instead.
Please, for your own good, do not go to ESF

I can't share my experience not because it is lacking in details but because of the potentially infinite amount of details it would contain. Thus, the difficulty in expressing one's experiences lies I knowing what can be left out, but as my story is far too unique for the vast majority of people being able to understand it without years of backstory and an intimate relationship with me I will choose to omit any details whatsoever with the exception, for this review seems an unique one, that few others have made for any college or university, much less this one, that it felt simultaneously both infantalizing and negligent of its students.

I would like to achieve my goal and gain more knowledge from friends on campus. I actually like to improve my skills such as baseball, computer and making new friends. I want to more details about this field of study. I'm curious about taking these courses that I have a passion for.

SUNY ESF is a great school for anyone interested in learning about the environment. I love that you can get a small-school education while still getting the large-school campus life by being so close to Syracuse University.

Minimum support for the student. No drive to make your college experience enjoyable. Food is not great unless you go to the Syracuse food court to eat. Student activities were sloppy. Overall, not a great experience
To have more money invested into the landscape architecture department because we don't even have enough money to have printer ink
The professors are incredible and truly care about their work and the students there. Housing is a bit frustrating since it is only guaranteed the first year.
ESF is a really great school!! HOWEVER! Do not come here without being prepared for an extremely rigorous curriculum.

I am a current junior at ESF and I absolutely love it. The academics are challenging, and there are definitely issues with admin and how the school functions, but the people and opportunities at ESF are amazing. It is such a great community, there are so many passionate students and professors that love what they do. There are so many ways to get involved on campus through clubs, activities, jobs, etc.

I have loved my experience with ESF! I transferred in halfway through first year and I really found my place. Having many resources from Syracuse university next door is amazing for such a small school, and you really get the best of both worlds. People complain about the food, but for college food it really is above average. The parties are really chill, mostly small backyard parties unless you want to go to SU parties which are bigger and more frat-centered. It is a hard school though, so be prepared. Even the easiest major (environmental studies) is substantially harder than at other schools and is considered a STEM. The faculty is really amazing though.

ai_summary: At Sacred Heart University, many students appreciate the supportive and close-knit community , often highlighting the helpful and approachable professors as a key aspect of their academic experience. The campus is described as beautiful and well-maintained, with various opportunities for involvement through clubs and activities. However, several reviews point out concerns regarding the high tuition fees and cost of living, with many feeling that the value does not match the price. Dining options on campus receive mixed reviews, with students calling for improvements in food variety and dining hours. While some find it easy to connect with peers, others express frustrations about a lack of diversity and the overall social scene, suggesting that it can feel cliquey. Overall, students seem to have a varied experience, with strong academics but mixed feelings about campus life and expenses.

reviews.text: Scared heart is great place once you get the hang of events and clubs you find yourself in a nice community.

I really enjoy it at Sacred Heart University. The people here are wonderful, everyone is friendly towards one another. The food for university standards in my opinion is also really good. There's many things to do here as campus life always has something up there sleeves for people to attend. The RA's are also really nice and involved in a good way. They host events for students to attend like in recents times for the Super Bowl they had a watch party. Overall Sacred Heart is really enjoyable in my opinion.
I loves my time at Sacred Heart! It is what you make of it! There is so many opportunities in academics and social life as long as you put in the work.

I have had a very positive experience as a student at Sacred Heart University, even though I commute rather than live on campus. As a commuter, I was initially worried that it might be difficult to connect with other students, but I have found it easy to meet people I can relate to and feel comfortable around. The campus environment is welcoming, and there are many opportunities to interact with classmates both inside and outside of the classroom. Additionally, my experience within the education department has been especially strong. The professors and academic advisors I have worked with are supportive, approachable, and genuinely invested in my success. They make an effort to get to know students personally, which helps create a sense of belonging. Overall, Sacred Heart University has made me feel at home and confident that I am receiving the quality education I signed up for.

As a biochemistry student at Sacred Heart University, my college experience has been both challenging and rewarding.
Small class sizes allow me to connect closely with professors who are always approachable. A huge plus is the hands-on laboratory work, it has strengthened my understanding of biochemistry concepts. Of course, the coursework, research opportunities, and academic support has been impeccable.
