Living on campus contributes significantly to my experience. Residence halls are modern and well-maintained providing comfortable spaces for studying and relaxation. The dining options are limited, but mostly healthy.
I strongly believe that the financial aid packages could be more open to parents with middle class income.
Overall, Sacred Heart has helped and is continuing to help me grow academically and prepare for future goals in science.

Overall Sacred Heart has been very helpful with my academic success and readiness for having a job after college. They make sure that there is always things for students to do to be involved in student life and help make a balance between academics and social life.

As an atheist, I was not welcomed by everyone. I wanted to try and work with others, but some students judged me very harshly for my views. Be prepared for anti science and much more conservative faculty and students. Ill say the head of our film program pushed back against too much religion in a college classroom, but wasn't enough. Artist in res spent too much time not teaching when she was supposed to be. We pay so much money to go here but maybe save your money.
I loved the amount of involvement opportunities there are including a wide variety of clubs and intramural sports. I would like to see a more diverse community at school.

If you are low income, don’t come here. First generation, don’t come here. Care about your education, don’t come here. Person of color, DO NOT COME HERE. In the 5 months that I have attended this school, I have never been at an institution that is so unwilling to help students with less fortunate backgrounds. And if you even think the quality of the education you get is going to be good, no, it’s not. Half the teachers here can’t teach, so expect to be teaching yourself the content for all that money that you’ll be paying. And remember, they don’t care about anything except your money. Oh and they are racist AF over here.

When I was applying to colleges about three and a half years ago, I was not fully set on Sacred Heart University. However, the moment I stepped on campus, I knew it was where I belonged. I entered SHU as an excited freshman, a nursing major, and a member of the club figure skating team.
SHU has allowed me to grow in many areas of my life, including leadership, perseverance, and confidence in asking for help. These experiences have shaped who I am today and helped prepare me for my future.
When I think about change at SHU, one experience stands out. During the spring semester of my freshman year, I was dismissed from the nursing program. This was an extremely difficult time, and I felt the support system was limited. I struggled to receive guidance from professors or advisors, which made the start of my sophomore year challenging. Greater academic and emotional support for students during moments like this is a change I would like to see at Sacred Heart University.

I loved my experience at Sacred Heart University. The people, classes, opportunities, and overall experience felt like everything one could have wanted out of their time in college. The social scene is extremely active, and there is something for everyone. Whether you are into partying or not, there are plenty of opportunities to meet people and have a good time. Academics vary from major to major, but overall Sacred Heart University has, time and time again, proven itself as a great academic university with a diploma that holds up well in the professional working world. In my personal experience, I have met people at SHU that I will hold close to me for the rest of my life, same goes for experiences. The opportunity to be an on campus leader in many different facets via greek life, academic clubs, and athletics has helped me progress professionally and personally and it is something that I am eternally grateful for.

I’m a senior at sacred heart and I have really enjoyed the past few years here. SHU really encourages you to be involved and there are clubs and activities for everyone. I’m so glad of all the opportunities I’ve had at SHU and I was accepted into a PA program to start next year. I was set up well and was able to do things I enjoyed while being supported by amazing friends, professors, coaches, and mentors.
I like sacred heart a lot, I think that there are a lot of on campus activities to do. I wish sporting events had more of a turnout so they would be more enjoyable.

I love the school so much, it feels like such a family and everyone has been so nice on campus. Everyone is always willing to lend a hand and for that I am truly grateful. Many professors have taught me it is never bad to ask for help.
I throughly enjoy the layout of the campus, it is visually appealing, and has great placement building wise.

ai_summary: Saint Leo University has a variety of strengths and weaknesses that prospective students should consider. Many reviews highlight the supportive atmosphere , with professors showing genuine care for students and offering personalized attention due to small class sizes. The campus is described as beautiful, fostering a strong sense of community among students. However, some students express dissatisfaction with the dining options, the isolation of the campus, and the challenges related to housing and financial aid. The academic workload varies, with some finding it manageable and others facing difficulty with specific courses. While many appreciate the online learning flexibility , some have criticized the organization of the online platform. Additionally, comments reflect on the religious presence on campus, which resonates well with certain students but may not appeal to everyone. Overall, Saint Leo is characterized as a welcoming environment suitable for those seeking a smaller, faith-based institution, but the location and amenities may leave something to be desired.

reviews.text:
Still attending but I have to say I’m extremely disappointed. So far the professors I’ve met along with the staff of the university have all been ok. It seems like nothing here is over the top like they hype it up to be.
My number 1 main issue however is the dorms. I’m currently living off campus now due to how terrible the dorms are.
I can’t speak for buildings 5 and 6 but I can CLEARLY see why they refuse to show dorm rooms on campus tours and how there is little to no pictures online of buildings 1-4. It’s quite literally the same thing as being homeless.
VERY FIRST DAY, there was mold in the bathroom, bath faucet was basically ripped off the hinges and constantly leaked, and the AC was and still is fully broken. With maintenance being useless and not really helping.
After the 2 week Mark bugs started to appear in spots that I’ve never even touched and it’s absolutely disgusting.
I would never ever recommend the dorms of saint Leo to anyone thinking about going AT ALL.
BEWARNED

This was one of the worst college experiences I’ve ever had from applying to graduation. Not only do the advisors not care if you live or die. It feels like they’re actively working against you in every process. The teachers don’t communicate, the “amazing website” they use sucks and is broken all the time. The school uses so much AI that it’s almost impossible to reach an actual person and when you try to call the school you’ll be put on hold for hours. DONT GO HERE
I had a great experience attending this university online. They worked with my Full time job as a Service member.

Saint Leo is nothing more than a huge overpriced antisemitic scam. My whole financial future and graduation has been ruined by Saint Leo. I was promised that I had enough aid for my program before transferring. I didn't receive my textbook for the course due to their stupid course materials program which ended up with me never being able to get my textbooks. I failed the course and started out on academic probation. During that time I did have a great advisor who left after a year she left the school which left me to go 3yrs with no advisor all. I do not have christian beliefs I have more-so ancient Hebrew and Muslim beliefs and had to deal with very much antisem. I bounced back and was on deans list multiple years, made it into omega nu lambda honors society, was on the student advi. board and wrote for news paper. Despite all my academic achievements my aid was snatched from me in my sr. yr. and I denied funds I qualify for to grad. after finishing my courses.

So far, my overall experience at Saint Leo has been great. Although it is a small school, I have met several amazing new friends from all over the country. I’m so glad I decided to attend Saint Leo for my college experience.

My overall experience at Saint Leo University has been great. The online course room has been extremely user friendly. The instructors respond in a timely manner and assist in coursework difficulties. Their resources such as tutors, librarians, and career services has also been great through my learning experience. I have had the opportunity to grow educationally because Saint Leo has provided me the tools to do so. My student advisor has made class registrations a breeze which has promoted a great balance between my personal life and my school life. All in all, my experience at Saint Leo University has been great.

My overall experience at Saint Leo University has been both rewarding and transformative. I appreciate the strong sense of community, supportive professors, and the values-driven education that encourages both academic and personal growth. Being part of a diverse student body has broadened my perspective and challenged me to think critically. I also value the opportunities for involvement in athletics and campus activities, which have enriched my college life. One area for improvement could be expanding resources for career development and internship opportunities.

Saint Leo University offers a very welcoming and comforting environment for students to attend classes and experience their amenities to the fullest. The dorms are well-kept and on-campus maintenance is always prepared to assist with any student needs. The student life events and programs are numerous and always have a good participation from the student body. All of the professors and staff are friendly and willing to help students meet their collegiate goals and beyond.
Everyone has made it super easy to attend and keep up with work remotely! Of course some professors have been better than others, but overall it has been a great experience.

My college experience has been incredibly rewarding, especially as a member of the Honors Program. The supportive faculty, wide range of activities, and strong academic resources have made a real difference in my growth. I've had the opportunity to get involved in the Italian Club and the theater group, both of which have enriched my time on campus. Studying abroad in Ireland was a life-changing experience that expanded my perspective. The college provides excellent support in scheduling classes and staying on track, and tutoring services are always available when needed. Overall, it's been a positive and engaging journey that continues to prepare me for the future.
Small classes average 30 students per class. Professors know you by name not a number, they truly care about your success.

Saint Leo's online courses have been pretty easy to follow and understand. The professors have been great and understanding when life happens! The student advisors I've had have also been great! I just wish the online classes weren't so expensive! Over $1400 for an 8 week class makes it's a bit of a hard time when you work full time, have a family and coach sports. But, I am pleased with the opportunity to be attending St. Leo online!

Going to Saint Leo University has been amazing experience for me to meet Catholic young adults who have the same values as me and share the same faith! I've met life-long friends here and I wouldn't trade it for the world.
Very pretty campus full of nature if that's your sort of thing. Good parties are non-existent. Very diverse student body. In the middle of nowhere.

The best part of SLU besides the Abbey on Campus is the professors. They keep the university alive. Period. The Fuz got me sick every time because they never change the oil. They messed up my husband and 3 of my friends' graduation because the registrar doesn't accept replacement classes (after they approved the replacement classes). The financial aid department is horrendous and almost gave my friend's scholarships to another student. The pre-recorded voice during graduation messed up my name even after I sent a pronunciation. My best friend took 6 classes and an internship to graduate early (got the approval of her advisor and the school) and now that midterms are coming up they're telling her that she can't walk at graduation because none of those classes will count. Everyone that I know that has stayed in the dorms has had to throw out belongings because by the end of the semester, they are covered in mold. Every building has mold.

ai_summary: At Saint Louis University, students frequently express a strong sense of community and support from faculty and staff, especially in academic settings. Many reviews highlight the accessibility of professors and the variety of academic resources, such as free tutoring and learning communities, which help students succeed in their rigorous programs . However, some reviews mention issues with campus safety due to surrounding violence and crime, although campus security measures offer some reassurance. There's a mix of opinions on student life; while many appreciate the diverse clubs and social activities available, some feel limited by the lack of nightlife and party culture. The dining options are a common point of critique, with calls for improvement in food quality and variety . Overall, SLU is valued for its academically-focused environment and strong support systems, though it's also recognized for its higher costs and inconsistent experiences with certain services.

reviews.text: Overall, I had a good experience at SLU. Good school for healthcare majors! St. Louis is a nice city, however my only complaint is that it isn't very walkable.

The school is really nice, I just don’t feel like I’m getting my money’s worth. The campus is beautiful, there are many clubs to join, and there are many service opportunities. However, the food is really hit or miss, mostly a miss, the dorms could be kept much better, and some the professors could also be a bit better. Sometimes there will be a professor that doesn’t seem to really know what they’re talking about, or they won’t speak English as a first language so the communication can be really confusing at times for both sides.
Pretty good but I studied at the Madrid campus which is probably a different experience than the main campus in Saint Louis.

Slu is an inner city school with caring faculty and idiotic administration. The board of trustees cannot handle money and there is always a budget crisis. They cut everything and make it cheap and flimsy
I am so grateful for my decision to come here-- it's been a phenomenal experience and has grown me so much as a student and person. I have found lifelong friends here.

Currently, I do enjoy Saint Louis university. They have made it more affordable by offering scholarships and scholarships oppourtunites. The new president has been making changes and I can already see improvment. I would like better on campus dining options. I like the location, there is always something to do on the weekends in Saint Louis which makes living on campus fun!

As a senior, I did not expect that I would go to Saint Louis University. However, things definitely worked out; I love it here for the most part. Nowhere is perfect, but this is pretty close. There are plenty of opportunities and resources as an engineering student. I love the Catholic Studies Center. If you're Catholic, you have to check it out, or even if you aren't. Student life is great; there are some big events, such as Paint SLU. Also, show up to the soccer and basketball games; we have some really good teams. The campus is also pretty good, with plenty of places to study, especially outside. Only problems may be with buildings and food occasionally. Also, as it is a smaller campus, it's easy to make friends. I would recommend joining a learning community if possible.

On campus safety and aesthetics are good, but most nearby off campus safety and aesthetics are not, except for Forest park. Athletics are a big thing and games are packed. I am in several campus clubs (biking, environmental, and lgbtq+) they are not very active, and do not have a lot of people participating. Though some clubs are bigger than others (running, competing sports, and sororities), but there are not a lot of options. On campus housing is mediocre and expensive. The campus is not too big and is walkable. There is a south campus with mostly healthcare classes, and the track is there (there is also a smaller track on the main campus I like to use but the red tar is coming up and the concrete is visible below throughout the track). South campus is not too far from main campus and the school shuttle run their for students to get to class. There are shopping shuttles on the weekend, but if it is raining, they leak through the ceiling. Overall though I am happy and would recommend.
It is a very diverse and enjoyable environment with a place for everyone. There is a lot of resources to flourish as an individual but also career wise.

I think SLU has a lot to over in terms of being an accredited university and setting you up for success academically. However, considering student living, my experience at SLU has been absolutely awful.😞 I’m a grad student living in Ignacio Hall (graduate living), and absolutely no air conditioning is provided for students. (although it was advertised it was). Students living here essentially live in bedroom sized rooms with temperature getting up to 80 degrees +. (also the windows are sealed, so we can’t have air ventilation). Students have has to move out against their will due to it being unlivable. I advise to not live on campus (unless you’re a freshman), it’s beyond expensive and they will not provide students with the necessities.

If I could go back to senior year of high school, I’d tell myself not to choose Saint Louis University. Academically, the sciences had strong professors, but the business school did not. The student culture was toxic—more competitive than collaborative. Socially, SLU often felt hostile to its own Catholic identity. When I followed the SLU College Republicans page, students threatened to call out the names of followers during lectures to publicly shame them. Once, walking home from the library, I had condoms thrown at me during a pro-abortion rally. Most heartbreaking of all, there were far too many student suicides during my time there. I'd recommend looking elsewhere.

I love how much the city of Saint Louis has to offer to SLU students. From discounts at local bars, restaurants, stores, and activities to the art, culture, and history in the area. SLU also has an incredible D1 basketball team that students get free admission to all SLU home games! Id like to see my city development for the area to be safer but DPS does a wonderful job.

I went to Saint Louis University for my Bachelor's degree in psychology and I applied for spring 2026 and I will start in January. I plan on doing a career change planning on becoming a registered dietitian. Saint Louis University has an amazing program and many other programs that will set college students up for success. I can't wait to start the nutrition and dietetics program. SLU has amazing professors who will support you along the way.

Academically at SLU, I thrived! All my professors were great and taught the subjects well. There were many resources that were easily accessible if you needed more help. As for STL, once you are familiar with the area it becomes easier to navigate where the fun and safe places are! There are many activities to do in the city!

SLU is a great school. There are so many opportunities and campus is amazing. I would definitely recommend this school to everyone. In fact my GF is attending this school and my younger brother is planning of applying after HS. Feels like students at SLU are all part of one big family. I made so many friends

ai_summary: At Saint Mary's University of Minnesota, students appreciate the close-knit community and the personal attention from professors, often noting how small class sizes foster meaningful relationships. The campus is surrounded by beautiful scenery , contributing to a pleasant environment. While many enjoy the supportive atmosphere and strong academic programs, especially in business and health sciences, some reviews express concerns about limited career services, dining options, and the university's handling of major cuts. Student life can feel a bit subdued, particularly for those seeking a vibrant party scene , and while faculty support is consistently praised, issues related to institutional leadership and communication have raised concerns among some students. Overall, students value the sense of belonging and the opportunities for personal and academic growth at Saint Mary's.

reviews.text: My overall experience here at Saint Mary's has been nothing short of enjoyable. I have come to love my college experience because it gives me the chance to make my own choices, meet new people, and experience new things. For example, the different events that happen around campus give you the chance to meet others who are different than you but share similarities as well. The campus itself is small enough that it's not confined, but big enough that it's not intimidating. To conclude, I enjoy my time here through sunshine or rain, challenging or not.

Saint Mary's is a great place for faith-oriented students. Here it feels like staff members truly care for you, and with small class sizes, they know you as a person. Saint Mary's goes above and beyond to make sure their students are thriving and given the opportunities they need for success. I cannot recommend coming here more.

I really enjoyed my time at Saint Mary’s University of Minnesota. I loved the Public Health program I completed; the courses were engaging and the majority of the professors were supportive and knowledgeable. The only challenge I faced was with one professor who frequently missed class due to personal or health issues, which caused some stress. Aside from that, I had a positive experience overall and appreciated the quality of the coursework and the dedication of the other faculty members.

Saint Mary's has competitive collegiate sports, a beautiful campus and a strong presence in the community. I made an educated decision when I applied and entered the program, but the experience has been even better than expected. I value the emphasis on clinical preparation that is supported by sound academic inquiry. I appreciate my professors who bring expertise, real-life experience, and deep respect for the work with them to their instruction. I feel honored to learn alongside my fellow cohort members and am so grateful for the meaningful connections and future professional networks we are already forging. I know that I am being prepared for licensing and am encouraged by the program's impeccable student passing record. And I can focus on all of these great learning opportunities without worrying about the price tag of the program because I know I am getting a great deal. I could not be more satisfied.

Saint Mary’s has been a one of a kind experience for me. It’s settled it the bluffs which makes the campus truly beautiful and unique. Being a part of an athletic program here has made a huge impact in my life and I very grateful for Saint Mary’s athletics. The academics here are also one of a kind with small class sizes and professors that truly want you to succeed.
I like the campus and the small community feel. The reason I came to Saint Mary's is that my sister attended college here.

Overall, I have loved my experience at SMUMN! Every single facility and staff member truly cares about you as a student and as an individual making sure you have the most opportunities available. While at SMU I've built life long relationships with students and staff! Because of SMU I am prepared for grad school (with all my letters of recommendation coming from a professor or advisor) and have many tools to help me succeed in the future!

The first thing I noticed on my tour was how friendly everyone was. I've never had so many people hold the door open for me! The location is beautiful as campus is nestled in the bluffs and surrounded by the Upper Mississippi River. I like how small campus is because I know a lot of people, but I still see a new face everyday. The on campus housing is really nice and there are a lot of options (dorm, suite, apartment, etc.)

I transferred to Saint Mary's from a previous University and I love it here. Ive found so many kind people and friends. I love the professors, they are always available and ready to help. Advisor's here seem to know a lot about what their doing, and they are very helpful to those concerned about courses.

I LOVED my college professors! They were very caring and understanding. I would have loved to see more diversity. As a religious university, it often felt like non-religious students were less accepted.
It was pretty good, I felt like it was a good school with friendly people and the staff was very nice and professional.

Saint Mary's University ... THE WORST FOUR YEARS OF MY LIFE! This school did absolutely NOTHING for my career. Once you graduate, you're kicked the curb and told good luck. There's zero career guidance, and zero commitment to you once you're gone. Thank god I found a way to get into tech and am getting a degree from a different place so I never have to affiliate with this organization ever again.

As a freshman in saint marys university of minnesota i have really enjoyed my experience here so far. Everyone is very welcoming such as student, professors, workers or anyone around campus. The campus is beautiful and the scenery is very pretty. It is a very safe campus overall and I dont ever feel like i will be in trouble. Professors here are very welcoming they are always eager to show you more and explain to you better thing. i enjoy coming to this school.

I love the welcoming atmosphere that Saint Mary's offers. Every time you leave your room, you are going to see someone you know and have someone to talk to, which does not always happen at a big school.

Being at St. Mary’s University of Minnesota has helped me gain independency, confidence, and amazing knowledge on the field of nursing! I would recommend everyone to visit St. Mary’s University and find what “because of you” means!

ai_summary: Sam Houston State University receives mixed reviews, but several common themes emerge across student experiences. Many students appreciate the supportive and engaged professors who genuinely care about their success, along with the diverse range of academic programs available. The small campus creates an environment where students feel connected and part of a community, although some express concerns about the lack of diversity and limited nightlife in Huntsville. Student life is characterized by numerous clubs and activities, and the campus is praised for being safe with a friendly atmosphere . However, some students report challenges with academic advising and housing issues, including the cost and quality of residences. Overall, SHSU is seen as a solid institution, particularly for those in fields like Criminal Justice and Education, but there are areas, particularly in student services and dining options, that could see improvement.

reviews.text: The campus is very nice and the people there are very sweet. They offer a bunch of help and resources

Sam Houston State University truly embodies a vibrant and energetic atmosphere that feels like family. From the moment you step onto campus, you’re enveloped in a sense of belonging that is hard to find elsewhere. The spirit of camaraderie is palpable, whether you're walking through the quad, attending a lively event, or participating in one of the many clubs and organizations available. Faculty and staff are incredibly supportive, often going out of their way to help students succeed both academically and personally. The campus is alive with activities—from spirited athletic events to engaging cultural showcases—creating a dynamic environment that always has something happening. Sam Houston truly offers a unique college experience, blending a sense of belonging with an energetic spirit that makes learning enjoyable and fulfilling. It's a wonderful place to grow, learn, and create lasting memories!

I will be an incoming Freshman in August of 2026. I have tour Sam Houston State University and I have spent time with friends and family that live there. I love the campus because it is so beautiful and has so many helpful resources for studying and socializing. The town is cute and small but also has everything I need to be comfortable. I am looking forward to starting my journey as a Bearkat!
My experience at Sam Houston State University so far has been amazing. I love the area how it's small and everything is close by walking distance. I like the environment everyone's pretty welcoming.

I like the way my professors teach what we are learning in the classroom, they make us engage with our classmates to know their perspectives or what else we could learn. I feel like they should do better with the food served here by changing up different types of food.

I graduated from SHSU in 2000. I had a great 4 years with some amazing professors who remembered me for a long time after I graduated. Overall it was a great experience. I am very impressed with the new buildings and the growth on campus. Lots of things for students to do and be involved in.
I have a daughter that wants to attend and while I'm excited for her to go to Sam Houston, the admissions process is a mess. The amount of times she has had to call to get help with logging into systems and was told things were resolved is upsetting (the issue was not resolved.) I'm slightly worried about how things will go once she is there, our experience so far hasn't been great. It's almost like the school has grown but the systems haven't.

I really like SHSU. It's a good sized campus, not too small but not too big either. The professors I've had the privilege to study under have all been good teachers who genuinely have a passion for their work. The college communicates well with its students, and there are a multitude of resources for the students.

I was a very dedicated student who also liked to take time to go out with my friends and experience the college and the town. The town was boring but the clubs around were fun. The food places around are not good. The professor and the academics and the school life are always amazing! just get involved and ask questions, everyone on campus is nice!

Sam Houston has a lot to grow on. Their athletics program is not fleshed out neither is their Greek life or extracurriculars; However, the school's students have good hearts, and you can tell the teachers care about the students' grades.
Sam Houston is a great campus for students. They have a great campus life, the dorms are inviting, and the food is delicious.

Sam Houston is a pretty great school. While it wasn't my top choice, it was one of my top choices in Texas. It has a far better faculty and campus than UNT from what I've seen. I don't know much about the sports, but their art and culture is pretty great. The students know how to party, but a lot of them are over very soon. The buildings are fairly standard, but it's obvious SHSU is trying to improve its facility. If you enjoy walking, Sam is a great place. If you need to rush to class in the morning, either chose a dorm not at the bottom of the hill or invest in a good pair of running shoes. The core classes can also be very challenging at time, but if you just put in the work you can achieve good grades. The food halls are good, but the quality has been lacking in the previous semester. Overall, a great school in Texas at a fairly reasonable price!

This is my first concluded semester as a Junior transfer to SHSU. So far I’ve had an overall positive experience. What I liked was the prominence of communication with faculty, as well as the personality and teaching style of my in person and online professors. I wish that Campus life had more social events with more marketing to the student email outside of social media, as well as more social media marketing of student life events
Sam Houston is such a Great university to attend. The professors do so greatly at their job and the students around are super nice to be around even if you don't know them. Such a great environment!!

I am a 29-year-old continuing my education in Healthcare Management and Administration, building upon over five years of experience as a Medical Assistant and two years as a Receptionist. In addition to my professional background, I balance the responsibilities of being a full-time mother and wife, which has further strengthened my organizational and leadership skills. As I envision my future, I know I am driven to pursue roles beyond front-line administrative work. My strengths lie in organization, creativity, productivity, and natural leadership, all of which align with a career in healthcare management. Enrolling at Sam Houston State University has been an invaluable decision, as it allows me to complete my coursework online while earning a degree from a respected local institution close to home. This opportunity positions me to advance my career and make a meaningful impact in the healthcare field.

As an education major I loved the education station in the TEC building. They had so many resources and books for us to book or use. As a trans man, I also loved the group therapy for LGBT individuals. I made some good friends from those sessions. SHSU is very inclusive and loves for others to share their experiences and cultures with the rest of campus. Student life is thriving and there's always some kind of event or get together happening around campus.
In terms of what could change, mainly I'd like for the school to be more vocal about scholarship and grant opportunities for their majors. As an education major I'm now in student loan debt because I didn't know that there were grant opportunities for my specific degree. I don't want that for others going into college degrees.

ai_summary: Samford University offers a welcoming and tight-knit community , recognized for its strong academic programs and committed faculty who prioritize student success. Many reviews highlight the supportive atmosphere , small class sizes, and opportunities for personal and spiritual growth , making it appealing to students looking for a Christian environment. However, students have noted challenges such as limited diversity, issues with housing quality, and mixed experiences with campus food. Despite these drawbacks, the vibrant student life , including Greek organizations and numerous extracurricular activities, provides an engaging experience for those who seek both academic rigor and community involvement. Overall, Samford is characterized by its blend of academics and faith-based values, setting it apart from larger, less personalized institutions.

reviews.text: Samford University was a great place to grow as a person, make new lifelong friends, learn, and grow with God. Samford pushed me to go outside my comfort zone and say yes to things I normally wouldn't have said yes to. I found my best friend through random housing and I wouldn't have changed that decision for the world. Samford has provided me with great opportunities to grow my career. I have become better in my profession because of the academics, but I have opportunities I never would have had without Samford. I would love to see Samford incorporate more emphasis on athletics and a 'game' culture to what is already built.

As a freshman, Samford is a great place. Yeah, it's got its flaws and it isn't perfect, but I have enjoyed my time here. Pros: extremely safe, super nice facilities, mostly excellent professors who care about you, generally kind folks, warm weather. Cons: expensive, it's harder to find a friend group if you aren't in Greek, but not nearly impossible, the food is on the better side of average, and the sports teams are good but not great. Overall, Samford is a great school if you can afford it. If you went to a smaller private school growing up, you can easily replicate that experience here.

I'm loving my experience at Samford so far! I've found amazing like-minded Christian friends, I've been academically stimulated (especially by my peers outside of class, which is wonderful), and I've been able to grow towards becoming an adult. It's been a wonderful time.

Great place to receive a well-rounded education! Its Christian aspects also give students to explore their faith in an academic and spiritual manor. One thing to know is that Samford is a dry campus, and there are visitation hours for opposite genders. Also, you cannot move off campus until you are 21.

I thought the experience is alright overall. The campus is really safe and the caf has good options with Chick Fil A and Moes. The course work can rigorous and difficult. In term of student events and parties, Samford is not it for the most part unless you are in greek life.
I love how much the professors truly care about the students! Samford is also huge on student involvement, and there are so many clubs/organizations to get involved with!
Great university keeps you grounded on life plan. Student activity for students and family engagement.
My time at samford was nothing but amazing. Samford does a great job of putting their students first, whether it be incoming or current students, everything they do is to better our time here.
Samford is great, but parking sucks we need more parking in the Greek life area especially now that we added 2 houses it’s going to be packed and we won’t get a parking spot

I like the people and professors there. The classes offered there are usually informative and well structured. I have had bad experiences with housing and food, however. The admin need to consider the needs of the students first, as they seem more focused on superficial things rather than what’s important.

Overall, my time at Samford University has been a meaningful and formative experience. The campus is beautiful and well-maintained, with a close-knit community feel that makes it easy to build relationships with both peers and professors. I appreciated the small class sizes, which allowed for more personalized instruction and stronger academic support.
One of the things I liked most was how much the university values character, faith, and service. The emphasis on ethics and purpose-driven leadership really helped shape my perspective—not just as a student, but as a person.

Samford is an amazing community that is built on the foundation of Christian principles. It is a tight community that looks out for each other. I have had an amazing experience so far with the ups and downs of college and I would recommend Samford to anyone interested.

Samford was a great place for me! The teachers care about you as a person and want to make sure you succeed. Student engagement is a really big thing here, so be prepped to get involved with greek life/sga/student clubs/etc.

Samford is a very welcoming place to be. I have made so many friends in only a short time, and there is always something to do on campus (when I'm not studying and rehearsing music!). This is a place where you can live and practice your faith in a supportive environment. And our sports teams are great! GO BULLDOGS!
I love the atmosphere of Samford. The class sizes are smaller and the professors care about knowing their students and seeing them excel.

ai_summary: San Diego State University (SDSU) offers a vibrant and diverse atmosphere , allowing students to engage in a variety of activities both academically and socially. Many reviews highlight the welcoming environment , with friendly students and faculty who are approachable and genuinely care about their students' success. The academics are described as challenging yet manageable, with a mix of large lecture classes and smaller, more intimate settings, depending on the course. Students appreciate the variety in majors and resources available, including strong support for career services and opportunities for involvement in clubs and organizations. However, some reviews point to inconsistencies in teaching quality and difficulties with the registration process as areas for improvement. The campus's location in San Diego is frequently praised for its beauty and the lifestyle benefits it offers, contributing to a well-rounded college experience that encourages both personal growth and academic achievement.

reviews.text: I enjoyed my time at San Diego State meeting great people who had mutual interests through extracurriculars and my courses. As a social work major I thoroughly enjoyed my coursework and faculty who showed interest in our professional development. My only difficulties were that of parking issues, parking costs, and increases in fees(tuition, food, parking, etc.)

I really enjoy San Diego State, I am a California native whose family enjoyed consistently going back and forth between my hometown and San Diego. Here feels like home to me no matter where I am located, this is where I comfortably come back to. I have felt very supported in my career goals and the steps to get there academically. Unfortunately, I have not felt the same confidence and support financially, and I would really like to get there to continue with the connections and opportunities I've made here.

San Diego State was a fun school to attend. Professors in certain majors were amazing; others lacked passion. Freshman housing wasn't the best, but it was a great experience anyway. Campus food was decent, but meal plan could have been better. The party scene was great if you were a part of Greek life or knew someone involved. I would not change my decision for any other school. Go Aztecs!

I enjoy the schools atmosphere and staff are extremely helpful with guiding new atendees. Not as social as anticipated, but friendly interactions nonetheless. I look forward to continuing my academic journey at this university.

SDSU's campus is beautiful, with a large variety of places for studying, socializing, and fun events. That being said, housing is not particularly outstanding. Many dorms have recently found to have "black mold," my own included, and raises serious concerns for the health of students living on campus. Furthermore, the students themselves can be rowdy, annoying, and oftentimes juvenile. At the end of the day, though, the education provided is adequate and many professors are delightful. 2.9/5.

I like sdsu so far, its a really beautiful place to be and is really conviently located to public transit which makes it easy to get around. Most of the teachers i've had have been great and easy to follow for the class. My only complaint is about the dorms they're pretty good and i'm in the one that is farthest away from everything which makes certain things difficult.

What I like is the overall experience in the fact that the school doesn't seem empty and is lively and can make friends rather easily. What I would like to see a change in would probably be more online classes

I think that the school has great diversity, some amazing faculty, and an awesome campus. I did about 1.5 years online and then the rest in-person for my undergraduate degree. I felt supported and empowered in seeking my education. I wasn't super connected to student life or parties, and I wish I had used more free time to connect with my peers.

So far, my San Diego State University experience has been full of ups and downs. When I first came here for the first week of school, I got to experience tons of new things I never thought my eyes would see. I got to explore more of what I like to do and things I don't like. Coming to this college thousands of miles away from home made me jump into a different mindset of taking care of myself without my parents. I made decisions like a real grown adult. Throughout this experience of letting go of my parents' hands and being away from my everyday people, I made new friends, I made conversations with random people off the sidewalk. Everyone here is very inclusive but you also have to learn how to be inviting towards others.

SDSU won - it was our son's 1st choice. His classes are excellent, professors are accessible, friends are from around the country. He loves college. He's eager to study abroad - everyone seems to do this and he's all in. Had the option to go to UCSD (free tuition, so that was tough to pass up) but he loved SDSU. He talks about the walkable ease of being in a community where there are always people out and about. It's easy to be recognized and connected. A graduate he knows went to the London School of Economics, who credits this to his internship opportunities through the school's career/internship program. Classes are hard but interactions with professors are easy. He ran into one in the airport where they sat and talked at length. He's happy and joined the hiking club and has time to explore San Diego's food scene. Students are smart and driven. Some balance Greek life, he opted to not do this and has a full group of friends from dorm + classes. Accepted? Join the parent group

I got a B.S. in Biology and have worked in that field and related fields for a couple of decades at this point. SDSU prepared me well and I am very grateful. My son is now a freshman and is having a great experience!

My overall experience at San Diego State University has been incredibly meaningful and transformative. SDSU has provided me with a strong academic foundation while also encouraging personal growth and self-discovery. As a psychology major, I have been exposed to diverse perspectives, rigorous coursework, and opportunities that have deepened my understanding of mental health and human behavior.

After attending San Diego State University for a semester, I can confidently say that I made the right college decision for myself. I love being located in San Diego—the weather is amazing, and we are close to the beach. The food on campus is great, with so many options to choose from. I feel like there is always something to do at SDSU, and most of the people I have met are extremely friendly. As far as housing goes, freshman housing is not the best, but I have made the most of it and formed many strong friendships with people on my floor. Academically, I have enjoyed all of my classes, and most of my professors have been great. Overall, San Diego State University is the best school for me.
SDSU is very welcoming academically and socially. Professors are kind and focus on student growth. It is a little hard to find cultural clubs especially for Hispanic students.

At my time so far at San Diego State, I have been able to enjoy the community. I really feel I am a part of this school and that I made the right choice by committing to this university. San Diego State has many opportunities for students to interact with each other. I have been able to make friends and connections in centers like the LatinX center. It was also a place for me to rest or eat on a long day at school. It is also nice that this university gave me the opportunity of having my first job and it being on campus.

ai_summary: San Francisco State University (SFSU) offers a diverse and vibrant campus experience , attracting students with its strong focus on social justice , ethnic studies, and a variety of academic programs. Many students appreciate the supportive professors who are accessible and genuinely care about their success, though experiences with faculty can vary. The campus provides a rich cultural atmosphere due to its San Francisco location, with plenty of opportunities for internships and community engagement. However, the university is primarily a commuter school, which can make it challenging to form connections and participate in campus life unless students actively seek out activities and clubs. Issues such as class availability and rising tuition also came up in reviews, highlighting some areas for improvement in student services and resources. Overall, SFSU is seen as a place where students can grow academically and personally but requires effort to fully engage with its community and available resources.

reviews.text: Sf State is a good local school to Bay Area students who are looking for a 4 year close to home that is more feasible to get into more than Cal or Stanford. It is right by the water on the border of Daly City and has a ton of different things to do in the city.

The school experience was great. I got to meet a ton of wonderful people and even got to talk with my professors like genuine people. The professors also made us participate in group activities instead of boringly talking for hours on end.

I had an overall great experience at San Francisco State. I played on the women's soccer team and I felt that there was a great community within the athletic program. I took some really interesting classes, all of which had professors that were passionate and fair.

Honest Take on SFSU Mechanical Engineering
I’m a Mechanical Engineering student at SFSU, and my girlfriend is a Business (Marketing) major. After comparing experiences with peers, we both feel SJSU would have been the stronger choice, mainly due to better alumni networks and industry pipelines.
At SFSU, many ME electives aren’t actually offered—often only 4–6 per semester, usually with one section and one professor. This also affects core classes, creating single-professor dependency where one bad fit can delay graduation. A major example is ENGR 410, where the required lab hasn’t been offered since Spring 2024, forcing some students into a harder, more theory-heavy replacement.
The program isn’t “too hard,” but it’s too theory- and exam-heavy relative to industry needs. Many students must self-train outside class. Lower-division courses that aren’t hands-on should be online/hybrid; taking them at community college and transferring often makes more sense.

San Francisco State University is a wonderful institution,people there are so loving, friendly, confident and likeable. I am a foreign, i just won a scholarship and everytime in the school, other students cannot stop laughing, smiling because there are happy to see someone from african. They would sometimes offer me help in terms of finance. What i like most about the institution is that it is always clean, there s always fresh air, clean environment, in general i would say everything is clean there. The institution provide the best hospitality and also the best education. It emphasizes mostly on time management and cleanliness hence makes it easy for the students to study. There is peace and calm silence. All i can i say is that the institution in the greatest.

My experience is super wonderful, because you have professors who really care about their students and success. You have also students who learn and cooperate and contribute with one another. Additionally, the San Francisco s State University fosters students’ independence and professionals who love what they do. I received my Bachelor’s Degree from San Francisco State and they helped me to get into Arizona State University online for my graduate program. Hence, the reason I’m applying for scholarships is to go back to San Francisco State University and get my credentials in special education to help children with disabilities or hardships succeed. Children with disabilities in my own opinion are an essential component of our communities and it’s success.

My overall experience at San Francisco State University has been positive. The campus has a diverse and welcoming environment, and many professors genuinely care about their students’ success. I’ve appreciated the variety of courses offered and the opportunities to connect classroom learning with real-world issues, especially through communication, media, and social justice–focused programs.
However, I think some areas could improve—particularly in communication between departments and how quickly students receive updates about financial aid, class changes, or advising. Campus facilities and housing could also use some modernization. Overall, though, SFSU provides a strong sense of community and personal growth for anyone who’s self-motivated and open-minded.

I like that San Francisco State University has a very vivid campus scene where you can always catch something going on, on campus. I also liked that public transportation was very easy around the campus and the local areas, making it easy to navigate the city. Although, from a commuter standpoint, I wish there was more ways to feel included and get involved in campus life.

San Francisco State University has some incredible professors but an incredibly frustrating administration. During my time there, it felt like there were constant faculty strikes — the leadership seemed disconnected, with administrators receiving large salaries while professors struggled with Bay Area living costs. Campus infrastructure wasn’t great either; desks were broken, and housing was nearly impossible to find when I transferred in. Despite that, once I got into my upper-division classes, the experience improved significantly thanks to passionate professors and smaller class sizes. The campus had its share of chaos and activism — sometimes inspiring, sometimes exhausting — but the new library and rec center were both fantastic additions. Just be prepared for bureaucracy and occasional dysfunction, but also for moments of real connection and growth.

Starting my journey at San Francisco State University last month went way more smoothly than I had previously thought. Orientation leaders were genuinely very inviting and GatorFest made socializing feel like a norm in SFSU's campus culture. Everyone was so approachable and that's what changed my mind about transferring schools after this semester.
In terms of everyday life at SFSU, I notice that there is never a dull moment around campus. It's nearly impossible to run out of things you do in the quad or outside the Humanities building since there is always an event going on that is open to all, from bouncy castles, student markets and vendors, to free food giveaways.
The variety of food on campus is very diverse and extremely affordable than spots elsewhere. Compared to many other campuses and even UC's that I've been to, I have not been this surprised about the price to taste and quality of the food ratio than the food at SFSU.

It's a new and exciting experience for me. Being on my own, I am now making decisions and solving problems, which is both scary and rewarding. I've made some nice friends and look forward to making the most of my college experience

I had a really great experience at San Francisco State University. I made lifelong friends and had the chance to grow, both personally and professionally. In the fashion program, I learned a lot, especially about creative and technical design, sustainability, and patternmaking. I always felt incredibly supported by professors and mentors who seemed to really care about me, my success, and future. The program offered many opportunities: I studied abroad, took part in study tours to New York and Italy, went on industry visits, and had excellent networking through our very active student organization, the Fashion Network Association. The annual fashion show was also incredibly professional and gave me a strong platform to showcase my work.
Overall, my time in the fashion program at SF State was excellent and I look back on my time there as a highlight in both my education and career journey.

San Francisco State is known as a commuters school. Majority of people take public transit because parking is very hard and limited. Most students take Bart and the bus. SF State does provide a free muni pass included into their student IDs which was helpful getting around SF. They have a free shuttle for students that takes them to Bart that runs every 20 min and other muni bus you can also take from or too school. The partying scene is cool, but only if you really dorm or know others who throw the parties. There are sororities and fraternities that are on campus that students can join. My favorite part about SF state is the gym! It features a rock climbing wall, pool, and sauna. They often host events in their too such as roller skating night.

My nutrition science program was mostly rigorous, incredibly curated, and very preparing for my graduate education and future career. I loved SFSU because it was very affordable for my family, allowed many options courses to be taken to complete per core requirement, offered unique classes, had incredibly down-to-earth professors, and taught me curriculum that still persists in my career today. I loved the food on campus and the general liberal campus climate. I mostly felt free to be myself (besides the Middle East conflict starting in 2023). I also felt comfortable speaking up in class, although many don’t because it tends to be incredibly liberal.
My experience was mostly positive. Although the library was under construction I was still able to study in vacant classrooms. My commute was also really long.

ai_summary: Santa Clara University offers a beautiful campus with a sense of community and small class sizes that encourage strong relationships between students and professors. Many reviews highlight the dedication of faculty who are generally helpful and approachable, enhancing the overall academic experience. Student life appears vibrant with various clubs, extracurricular activities, and accessible sports, though there are mixed feelings about the social scene, with some students finding it cliquish. The food options receive criticism, with calls for more nutritious choices and diverse menus. While the university is praised for its excellent academic programs , its high tuition costs and perceived lack of diversity in student culture are notable drawbacks mentioned by several reviewers. Overall, SCU is described as a supportive environment for personal and academic growth, especially for those interested in careers in Silicon Valley.

reviews.text:
My overall experience at Santa Clara University has been nothing short of amazing. From the very beginning, I knew this was the place I wanted to be. In fact, it was the only school I applied to because something about it just felt right. The strong sense of community, the beautiful campus, and the university’s commitment to both academic excellence and personal growth all stood out to me. I took a leap of faith by putting all my focus into one school, and I can honestly say it was one of the best decisions I’ve ever made.
Being here has confirmed that I chose the right path. The professors genuinely care about their students and challenge us to think critically while also supporting our goals. I’ve been able to grow not only academically but also personally. The environment pushes me to strive for more while still making me feel supported and valued. There’s a real sense of belonging here that makes it easy to build friendships and connections that feel meaningful.

Professors are ok, students are extremely racist, elitest, price of college high, social life is horrible if u don’t dorm, but nonetheless bad social life, location is ok but not much to do in Santa Clara.

It's alright, but definitely not worth the full price of tuition. Campus life is probably the worst part. On the weekends, pretty much everyone goes home and all that's left is neighborhood families walking their dogs. Nothing to do in Santa Clara or San Jose (CalTrain to SF is expensive, too). Swig, Dunne, and Walsh are party spots the latter half of the week, but pretty much every other dorm is antisocial 24/7. Campus organizations are a mixed bag. Some of them - like Into the Wild, KSCU, and the diversity clubs in the Shappell Lounge - are great at putting on events and getting people involved. A lot of the student body, though, are just rich suburbanites who spend the whole weekend drinking. There are a LOT of kids who go to a Jesuit high school and college, then end up working for their dad. Don't expect the "driven" and "entrepreneurial" student body that the ads make you believe exists. If you can get a full-ride, it's probably better than SJSU.

SCU is the perfect platform for 'college is what you make of it.' Its not hard to find someone of similar interests. By the end of it, almost every person I met said "wait do you know X?" As an engineering major, academics always came first, but partying/socializing was always extremely accessible - like i was drinking 3 times a week but didnt miss a single class. Freshman year (regardless of major) is really interconnected, take the opportunity to choose a fun dorm (dunne/swig) to make friends outside of your major (super important). Rush a frat or sorority - youll either like it or not, but its important that you at least see what greek life has to offer. If not, there's hundreds of clubs on campus that are super easy to make friends. This is a school that you can bum in the sun drinking a beer, then take a linear algebra quiz, then play ultimate frisbee, and go ski on the weekend. Profs/classes were hit or miss - just ask upperclassmen for advice and youre fine.

This institution is Catholic (which is why there is a Mission on campus) and encourages cura personalis, "care for the whole person". The University offers free mental health and counseling programs and exercise sessions for students. Take ratemyprofessor ratings with a grain of salt.

Overall, my experience was very positive. I appreciated how accessible and supportive the staff were, and I felt that communication was clear and helpful throughout the process. I especially liked the flexibility and organization, which made it easier to balance academics with other responsibilities. One thing I would like to see changed is increased access to financial support or clearer guidance around funding options, particularly for summer or online courses. I believe more transparency and outreach in this area would make the experience even better for students.
Social life revolves around greek life. Professors are happy to see you succeed and offer lots of support. Small class sizes.

I transferred to Santa Clara University from my second year in community college and couldn't have been happier with the 4-year University I'd chosen. I'd joined the first generation college student program (LEAD) which allowed me to get acquainted with the campus and fellow first-gen and transfer students a week before classes had started. LEAD facilitated group activities, games, mentorship which greatly helped with the transition to a 4-year university. The majority of my classes consisted of est. 15-20 students to teacher ratio and all of my teachers and counsellors expressed deep care for my academic development. The university has a grand resource base which fosters academic success, personal development that caters to each student individual degree path a career goals.
The campus is absolutely beautiful located at the center of Silicon Valley. They have great network and internship recommendations and opportunities for you, and everyone is super nice!

I really like how Santa Clara University has small class sizes, with a lot of networking opportunities through both clubs and professional connections. I'm currently a freshman and I've been enjoying it a lot so far !
I went to SCU for undergrad and I’m still here for my graduate degree. I really like this school. They take care of their students well and the environment is overall nice.

Santa Clara is an amazing school in a beautiful area. The professors are truly there to support you and help you be where you need to be. People here are friendly as well because it is a small school and the weather is also amazing.

Class sizes are small. The largest class in size I've ever taken was a Physics class with around 70 students. The rest are much smaller, ranging from 15 to 35 students. Faculty is nice, despite some rare cases of "reading from PPT" style teaching. Housing could be better; they are making their residence halls overpopulated by converting single rooms to double rooms. The cost of living in the area is high, as it's in Silicon Valley. SCU holds at least two career fairs per quarter, which is really nice.

My experience at Santa Clara University has been inspiring and rewarding. From the welcoming community to the dedicated faculty, I have felt supported both academically and personally. I appreciate the opportunities to engage with peers, join campus events, and grow through meaningful discussions. The professors’ mentorship and the university’s focus on ethical leadership have shaped my learning and given me a greater sense of purpose. The campus environment blends academic focus with community connection, making my time here enriching. If I could see change, it would be expanding access to resources for student involvement and career development, ensuring all students can reach their full potential. Overall, Santa Clara has been more than a place to learn, it has been a space to grow, connect, and prepare for the future.

I enjoy and appreciate the quality of the campus food as well as the fact that our communal restrooms are cleaned on a daily basis. The professors here are very helpful and kind. Coming from a public high school, having an interactive professor was a nice change. The students and faculty here are very kind and friendly. However, there are times that I do feel like the odd one out as there seems to be a lack of diversity. However, the meditation rooms and bollywood dance teams, although not very well known, do make me feel like I have found my people. It is a bit of a party school, but you can pick and choose what you would like to do with your time whether it be partying, studying, or both. Overall, I would rate Santa Clara University as a four star institution. I like that they also teach morals along with academics.

ai_summary: Seattle Pacific University (SPU) offers a close-knit community that fosters meaningful connections among students and faculty. Many reviews highlight the small class sizes , allowing for personalized attention and a supportive learning environment . While the faculty is often described as caring and engaged, several reviews express concerns over a lack of diversity among students and faculty, noting that the overall campus culture can feel exclusive or clique-y at times. Student life tends to be centered around campus activities rather than traditional party scenes, which may appeal to those seeking a more subdued atmosphere. However, some students wish there were more events and opportunities to build school spirit. Financial considerations are also a recurring theme, with mentions of rising tuition costs and additional fees for various services. Overall, while SPU is praised for its academics and community, students express a desire for increased inclusivity and diversity in the student body.

reviews.text:
I transferred to Seattle Pacific University from a community college where I already received my associates degree. It was an easy transition and the classes were small which made it a lot easier to get to know the professor and classmates. I appreciated that whenever I needed help in a class, the professors were very helpful and took the time to help me understand subjects that I struggled with.
I was a commuter but I spent a lot of time on campus and it gets sort of lonely during the winter quarter but fall and spring is always pretty fun and campus is beautiful. If you are more introverted, this would definitely be a good school for you!

Seattle Pacific University is a private , faith-based institution that drew my attention because of its smaller community. The environment allows meaningful connections with both students and professors. Unlike the usual sense of dread that comes with a new school year, I felt genuine excitement to begin at SPU. The university fosters a strong sense of belonging and provides valuable opportunities for personal growth. Attending the semester job fair significantly guided me toward my career goals and the university’s close support during my internship process proved its genuine commitment to students success. From my experience, SPU offers an excellent education where professors are passionate about their work and truly invest in their students. I hope to continue my academic journey here and pursue graduate studies at SPU.

My overall experience at Seattle Pacific University has been both meaningful and memorable. From the start, I felt welcomed by a supportive community of students, professors, and staff who genuinely care about student success. The campus has a friendly and inclusive atmosphere, making it easy to connect with others and feel a sense of belonging. The classes are engaging and challenges me to think critically, while professors take the time to offer guidance and encouragement. I've also enjoyed the many campus events and opportunities to get involved, which helped me grow personally and academically. Overall, SPU has been a positive and inspiring environment that motivates me to work hard and pursue my goals.

Most of my issues are issues that have appeared out of my own control. Certain changes to the administration and the budget challenges has caused some of that to fall on the students and increase an already fairly hefty tuition cost. I can only recommend that they offer more in-house scholarships and opportunities to get full-ride scholarships as well.

One thing that caught my attention at Seattle Pacific University was the campus size, the atmosphere of the campus. I love that the class sizes weren't too big, which made it easier to the professors and for them to know you as well. All of my professors were so friendly and easy to approach. I loved how engaging the clubs were, you will be able to find your community and friends there. That's how I found most of my friends whom I'm still friends with.
One thing I would like to see change is how much activities and events the school hosts for fun so that students can socialize and have fun things to do during the day.
I liked how many degrees and concentrations were offered. Also, job openings were always available for students looking to work study or land an internship

SPU has been a welcoming place where I’ve grown a lot. I love the caring professors and community vibe. Would love to see more support with financial aid and internships. The campus life is balanced and a lot of opportunities to get engaged in clubs and team.

Transferring to Seattle Pacific University as a computer science major has been a rewarding experience. In just one year, I’ve grown academically and personally. The professors are supportive and genuinely care about our future, offering guidance and opportunities beyond the classroom. As a Christian university, SPU integrates faith into academics and student life, which has added meaning to my education. The diverse and inclusive community has made me feel welcomed and connected. While class scheduling can be a challenge at times, that’s common in many schools. Overall, SPU has provided a great environment for learning, growth, and networking with professionals. I highly recommend it to anyone looking for a strong academic community grounded in values.

I loved my experience in the psychology program here. The professors were incredibly kind and helpful. The small class sizes (usually between 15-25 students) made it an intimate learning environment, allowing you to take your education into your own hands. The only thing I would change is that living on campus can be a little isolating, due to the large number of commuter students.

Seattle Pacific is a decent school; however, there are several concerns their administration could consider for improvement.
Seattle Pacific University offers strong academics, where professors truly listen and support their students. The campus has a quirky, charming ambiance that feels warm and homey, making it a pleasant place to learn. Its location near major Seattle attractions adds to out-of-state student appeal. However, the school struggles with diversity—most students share similar Christian beliefs and perspectives, which can feel limiting and constricting to the student minority. Tuition costs are extremely high at ~$44,000 annually, especially with the limited selection of majors and course offerings each quarter. SPU lacks thought for commuter students, with short, poorly advertised social events and almost no undergraduate online course options.
Overall I have had an alright experience. Parking is expensive and there should be more food on campus. Security is great. The library is great.

As an SPU undergraduate, I am heavily involved in the campus life, and through this lens, I have been able to see the wonderful student life SPU due diligently works to construct. The home I make at SPU sure makes the extra cost worth it. The positions I am trusted to fill and the opportunities I am given really adds to my college experience. The mission is to invite and welcome everyone, and to do that, staff and faulty work together to reflect on how to best make students feel belonging on campus. Some may say I’m ‘glazing’ my university, yes there are always areas of improvement, but SPU works to improve. In the beginning, when I was a freshmen soaking in the independence of being away from my parents, I didn’t think too much about how the school served me. But through the growth I have had here, I have been able to find peace and character growth that I don’t think I would have found without it coming here. University isn’t for everyone, but it was for me.

SPU is an overall great school, however there is room for improvement in particular areas. Being a PWI, the lack of diversity is not surprising, I just wish there was more representation. The student life is very clique-y, while people are most of the time friendly, it is hard to make friendships. There is not much school spirit and more student events would help fix that.
The professors I had at SPU shaped my experience into a positive one, the class sizes are small enough for you to engage and ask questions and really get the most out of your education.
I'm going to attend in the fall so I have not yet experienced all of SPU's environment yet but I like how it is spacious but not far.

ai_summary: Seattle University is characterized by its intimate community and supportive atmosphere , making it a great fit for students seeking a close-knit educational experience. Many reviews highlight the small class sizes , which foster strong relationships with professors and allow for personalized attention, enhancing the overall learning environment. The campus's urban location in downtown Seattle is frequently praised for providing ample opportunities for exploration and engagement with the city. However, high tuition costs and limited housing options are notable concerns among students, alongside a desire for more diversity and vibrant campus life . While academics receive positive feedback for their quality and the professors’ dedication, some students feel the lack of robust school spirit and athletics does affect their overall college experience. In addition, the availability of resources, like helpful staff and various student services, are seen as beneficial, though there is room for improvement in areas like career services and financial aid opportunities.

reviews.text: I am a current student, and I like the diversity which exposed me to different experiences and information outside of my culture. I would like the professor to be morevigilatny an understiding aout the studentd individual learning styles. even if they are "adults."

My overall experience with Seattle University has been very positive. The admissions and enrollment process was well-structured and supportive, with clear communication at each stage. I was particularly impressed by the university’s strong emphasis on academic excellence, ethical leadership, and practical, real-world learning. Interactions with faculty and staff reflected a genuine commitment to student success, and the program’s alignment with my academic and career goals in data science reinforced my confidence in choosing Seattle University.

The teachers have very different teaching styles that I appreciate, but that lead me to being a little unprepared when taking notes for each class. It's still hard to make friends for me since my schedule is unfavorable to most student events.
i just transferred but its pretty good and lots of support from staff i feel valued. Its a welcoming community and i feel connected with my cohort.
Seattle University is a beautiful campus, a tight knit community, and a great place to get your education!

I think student life is good to meeting new people, but sometimes it is cliché. Academically, it depends on the professors. The food on campus is not the best and could be more affordable and better with expanding more places to eat. Additionally I think the campus is nice, but it could be bigger and expanded more on more classrooms and independent self-study.

Although my experience in Seattle University hasn’t started yet because I am an incoming freshman, current students and faculty members I have met during orientation were very welcoming. People that are attending Seattle University do a great job in making sure others are comfortable and safe. From what I have noticed, people that I have met so far radiate positive energy, which makes me feel like I don’t need to worry that much about my time that I will spend on campus. Even though my time was limited due to only being there for freshman orientation, I thought that there shouldn’t be any changes to be made. This allowed me to remove some stress off my shoulders when I start making new friends when school starts in the fall.
It's decent so far, but I'm a freshman, so I don't have much to say so far. I hope to will be a great year for me and I will have a good time too.
I think the school is wonderful and the faculty is amazing. The small class sizes really allow you to learn the material well and provide numerous opportunities to receive help.

I generally really like the school. If it weren't for the really high tuition, I would be happy with being at this school. I've met a lot of new people, joined new clubs, and felt more at ease to be myself compared to high school. As for the academics, as a college student of course it can be boring at times, but the small class sizes really help in keeping engagement and making sure you're still focused.

I am currently a rising sophomore at SU, and I feel like I've met lots of great people, in and out of the classroom. Throughout my freshman year, majority of the professors I had were very kind and empathetic teachers.
