I personally like how the quarter system allows for me to take more classes during the school year, but at times it felt like the quarter was moving really quickly and my classes had to suddenly pick up in speed towards the end, which was a bit stressful. Overall, I've enjoyed my time at SU and I'm looking forward to going back in the fall.

It's a fun school so far. a lot to do in the Seattle area but not many food options. Professors are nice and the classes have a chill vibe while still being challenging academically. Way too much money though and the administration makes decisions without considering the student body.
I have so far enjoyed my time at Seattle University. I lived in the dorms on campus for the first year which definitely helped my experience at college.
This school is very comfy you will end up being connected to almost everyone one way or another. The education is good and so are classrooms. However housing is really not worth the money.

I really like the class sizes at Seattle University. The largest class I’ve been in had about 30 students, and most of my classes have around 20 to 30 students. This smaller class size makes it much easier to communicate with professors, ask questions, and stay engaged in the material. I also appreciate the diversity on campus—Seattle U places a strong emphasis on multiculturalism and creating an inclusive environment where people from all backgrounds feel welcome and supported. I would like to see change in the dining options since the menu is not that varied, and often the same meals would be provided every few days.

ai_summary: At Seton Hall University, students generally appreciate the close-knit community and small class sizes , which foster strong relationships with professors and encourage personalized attention. Many reviews highlight the robust academic programs and supportive environment , particularly within specific majors such as Diplomacy and Computer Science, where students feel a sense of belonging and have ample opportunities for internships and networking, especially given the university's proximity to NYC. However, concerns about tuition costs are prevalent, with many feeling that the value received does not align with the expenses, especially regarding career services and job placement efficacy. Food quality , diversity on campus, and the campus's social scene, particularly on weekends, are often mentioned as areas needing improvement. Overall, while Seton Hall offers a unique educational experience, some students desire more support, diversity, and recreational engagement.

reviews.text: One of my favorite aspects of Seton Hall is the many opportunities to get involved in campus life, clubs, and leadership. I have enjoyed participating in Greek life, community service, a campus job, and a research fellowship. As a social work student, the professors are always there to support me and help me to feel prepared to enter the field upon graduation. The program has also provided me the opportunity to experience two off-campus internships through my junior and senior practicums. Overall, my education at Seton Hall University has allowed me to grow and formed me as a better leader, professional, and person.

I would say that my overall experience at Seton Hall was not too bad. I really enjoyed the campus life, the people I have met, the friends I have made, and the numerous amounts of career opportunities. On the other hand, the school can definitely improve on the food and safety.

I am a freshman at Seton hall and just finished my first semester. I have enjoyed my first semester here in South Orange. The university offers a lot of events to attend whether it is a university promoted or club/organization promoted there is always something to do. The community is very diverse and the people will make you feel right at home. The academics here is good and the professors are top quality. The South orange village is really nice with lots of restaurants and the train station is not far from campus. It helps that you can take the train into New York City in 35-45 min.

This school charges an outrageous amount of money and does not provide anything of value in return. You cannot do that. You cannot charge a lot of money and then not provide your graduates with a quality education. The curriculum for most classes is from the 1960s where they just have you read from a book. Almost no technology of any kind is used or taught at the school. They also do not provide you with a job when you graduate. You are assigned a counselor to advise you on your classes, but that counselor is as worthless as a guidance counselor in high school who meets you once a year and reads your name off of your file. The counselor I was assigned was worthless and do not give me any advice about anything. I got a degree that did not get me a job. What did I pay for?

As a current senior and Marketing major at Seton Hall University, I am truly grateful for the experiences I have had throughout the last four years. Looking back, I have had the opportunity to study abroad in Austria, meet with several marketing experts, and work closely with outside firms to enhance their marketing strategies. The professors are invested in the success of their students, and I have been very fortunate to be under their tutelage. Go Pirates!

My overall experience at Seton Hall University has been really good. I’ve liked how supportive the professors are and how easy it is to get help when you actually need it. The campus feels welcoming, and I’ve been able to meet a lot of people from different backgrounds, which makes everything more interesting. I also appreciate that the classes are not too big, so it’s easier to participate and understand the material.
Something I would like to see improve is communication in some departments, especially when it comes to deadlines or schedules, because sometimes things can feel a little confusing. I also think adding more study spaces or extending library hours would help students a lot, especially during midterms and finals.
Overall, Seton Hall has given me a good balance of academics, community, and opportunities, and I’m glad I chose it.

Seton Hall has been a great experience. The staff there are nice and the student boy, overall, are a diverse group of people. The school's resources are mainly on the South Orange campus, but they also have other campus's not too far around New Jersey for special majors and programs like their school of law, business and health sciences. I enjoy the campus cafeteria food, there are a range of different kinds of meals including a selection for vegans, sushi stations, grilled foods, pastas and variety of deserts. Calm but well-rounded campus.

As a student attending Seton Hall, this university provides a nice community for students. Campus life is always present with multiple events happening year-round and most of the people on campus, including students, faculty, and security, are very welcoming and personable. Also, as an out-of-state student, I have always felt welcomed. It was very easy to get accustomed to Seton Hall and adapt to the new state and area. My favorite places on campus are the university center and the library, two places that provide different environments depending on what you hope to do. The University Center is a perfect place to meet new people, spend time with friends, and take a break from your classes, while the library is a perfect place to study and get work done, offering study rooms and a common floor for group work. Overall, Seton Hall has a great community, provides a fun experience, and cares deeply about its students.

I have loved my time here at Seton Hall. As a diplomacy major, I instantly felt at home and found my community within my major. Seton Hall makes it really easy to get involved by having clubs for every interest and hosting the involvement fair each semester. Overall, I have really made and found my home at the hall.

Seton Hall was my top choice when looking into colleges. Now that I have spent over year here, I am so glad that this is where I ultimately ended up. The community here is so welcoming and accepting, which made it really easy to feel like I fit in. As an education major, I also really enjoy the fact that I am already in a classroom starting my sophomore year. From what I've seen from both myself and from my friends, Seton Hall really prioritizes hands-on learning. It also has given me many opportunities to get involved, ranging from Greek Life to Student Government to even an on-campus job! I love Seton Hall, and I'm so glad I chose the place that feels right for me.

I love the scenery. If you like nature you will love the school. The teachers are nice, they have helped me gain access to many resources. There are A LOT of resources to help you out like tutoring, writing centers. There are also many places where you can chill especially if you are a commuter. I love the fact that even people without meal plans have access to the dining hall they just have to pay like $12 and there are soo many options and it's buffet style so feel free. I also love the green its literally a nature area where you can sit on the grass under trees with your friends and just hang out. They also always have something going on like events everyday. The only thing I don't like is that you have to pay to park on campus even as a commuter and it is very expensive. I feel if I am not going to leave my car there all day I shouldn't have to pay so since I can't park on campus, I park outside of campus sometimes near and sometimes a bit further.

Often switch assigned professors last minute. Students studying for MCAT that took higher quality profs are now stuck with poor quality professors.
^^ HUGE note for students interested in the BSMD program ^^
College life: Campus life is poor; clubs are watered down; nothing to do on campus and in the surrounding off campus areas. Fraternity life is poor; parties are often in basements of run-down homes.
Food Quality: They asked for a quarter-billion dollars form donators but can't put a penny to improve cafeteria food quality.
Inefficient Budgeting: Student tuition is put towards activities/events students are uninterested in.

The student life and people have always been very polite and nice, and the professors id say are average, some good and some bad. The campus is a perfect size for my preferences, i just wish it exemplified catholic life more.

As a junior at Seton Hall University, I have enjoyed my past two years here! Campus is a lively, welcoming place where students can always find something to do. From stopping by different tabling events to trying new food trucks, Seton Hall students can engage in various activities all over campus. There are a great variety of organizations and clubs for students to join to make new friendships or network. With our smaller class sizes and low student-to-faculty ratio, students are able to create strong relationships with their peers, and professors know their students by name. My professors have always been welcoming and easy to reach if I need to ask them any questions. Being so close to NYC, students have access to many internships and events. Living on campus for two years, I had good experiences with food quality and dorming. In my experience, Seton Hall provides their students with exciting events on campus, a sense of community, and many academic/career opportunities.

Seton Hall University offers a great balance of academics, community, and location. Professors are approachable and truly care about student success, and there are plenty of internships, research options, and leadership programs. Campus life is welcoming and active, with Greek life, clubs, and Division I athletics. I love that it’s in a safe suburban area but only about 30 minutes from NYC, which makes internships and networking easy. Career services are strong—career fairs and alumni connections helped me land internships early. Housing and dining keep improving, and the campus has a close-knit, friendly feel. Overall, Seton Hall combines strong academics, career preparation, and fun campus traditions with access to big-city opportunities.

ai_summary: At Shenandoah University, students often describe the environment as welcoming and tight-knit , highlighting the small class sizes that facilitate close relationships with professors and peers. Many reviews commend the faculty for their dedication to student success, noting that they are approachable and eager to help. While the academics generally receive praise, some students express concerns over food quality and limited dining options. Campus life is vibrant with a range of clubs and activities, though the local area might lack nightlife options. The school's focus on personalized education and community support is often cited as a unique aspect, along with a strong conservatory program for the arts. However, some reviews point out issues with administrative responsiveness and concerns regarding inclusivity on campus. Overall, Shenandoah University offers a distinctive college experience where students feel both supported academically and socially, amid some notable challenges.

reviews.text: Love the Conservatory and all the experience it offers. The Acting professors are top notch and the clubs are so much fun. The food could use more choices, but the campus is beautiful.

Overall, Shenandoah is a wonderful school, great location, great professors who care very much about what they are teaching, and everyone one from the students to staff is very nice, approachable, and personable.

I enjoy how friendly and sociable the professors are. The small class sizes make for a very intimate learning environment. It is a very safe, petty campus, and th Old Town Winchest is lovely. The food is very hit or miss, but the rest of the environment makes up for it. There are so many opportunities for students to have a plethora of experiences.

Every professor I have had so far has been helpful when it comes to answering questions in their class and even about campus in general. I live in Racey Hall Dorm and they aren’t that bad unless the AC stops working and it gets extremely hot in there. One thing I’ve noticed around campus at multiple study locations is that the trash could be combined or taken out a little more often than it is sometimes. The dinning hall is not super super good but there are days where you can have an enjoyable meal. They are always listening to student feedback and are constantly trying to improve their food selection. I would love to see a campus Starbucks or Chick-fil-A as dinning options. I have talked to many other students and they have said the same.I love that SU provides various work study opportunities for every student it allows experience, budgeting, and time management skills to be taught and expanded on for all four years. The area is super beautiful all year round. Let’s Go SU Hornets!!!
I have heavily enjoyed my experience at shenandoah! Highlights include the proffessors, class sizes, work study opportunities, and the sense of community.

My favorite part of my Shenandoah experience was probably the professors and the classes. Academically, my Shenandoah experience was rather decent, however, I did feel it was lacking in certain areas (food, student life, clubs, etc.).

I transferred into Shenandoah my sophomore year and have no regrets! As a full time student athlete it’s the best. The community is beyond welcoming and there are so many ways to get involved on campus whether that’s sports or clubs. The professors are 100% behind you and give the best advice. My classes are challenging but in a good way. There are a lot of opportunities in every major and study abroad as well. The surrounding area has everthing you need all in a 5 minute drive! I can’t imagine myself anywhere else and I’m proud to call myself a hornet!
Best decision of my life. I've thoroughly enjoyed my time as a student and am certainly going to walk away with the knowledge and expertise I came to find

I really do enjoy the classes and academic part of SU. There are also fun campus activities to do every now and then! My big downside is the food… It always feels very repetitive but it’s not the worst that it could be.

Shenandoah University is a nice college if you like small animals and quiet time, but I wish their reporting services were easier. I have to go through a million steps with a professor before I can even report them for inappropriate behaviors. It should just be a simple google form.

Great school with good professors. Everyone is so kind. Still a student at the moment and it has been a wonderful experience. Love the professors, the student life and how students interact in other ways.

It’s a good school! Most of the professors are really good, and you’ll be able to get to know them personally. The food is terrible, the party scene is good if you’re a conservatory major or an athlete.
I love SU with my heart, the food could be a little better, but overall it feels like home. Professors are really nice and the campus is pretty diverse.

I love many of the performance opportunities for music students, and many of the music professors are wonderful. I had one experience with a terribly unqualified and unprepared adjunct that was incredibly disappointing. The local area is very walkable and cute. I love the on campus coffee shop. It would be nice if the gener gym for all students was a bit bigger, had lockers, and a shower.

Shenandoah has an environment of students, faculty, and staff who truly care about you and your education. You will be challenged academically, but will have support and guidance the whole way. There are lots of opportunities for students to get connected and have fun, such as CAN (campus activities network) events and the chance to go abroad for free on a GCP (global citizenship project) trip. Shenandoah can improve in its facilities (some buildings are outdated for their purpose) and especially in its food, but overall I found a community that cared for me and challenged me academically, and I had fun the whole time.

ai_summary: Simmons University stands out for its strong academic programs and dedicated professors who are noted for their supportive approach to student success, particularly in disciplines like nursing and education. The campus fosters a sense of community , especially as an all-women's college in Boston, but some students feel the social scene lacks vibrancy, with few campus activities and a need to connect with nearby co-ed institutions for more engagement. Reviewers often mention the high cost of attendance and dissatisfaction with campus facilities, including dorms and dining options, which are described as lacking quality . While there are positive mentions of a diverse student body and opportunities for internships and study abroad, concerns about inclusivity and adequate support for various student groups are also raised, suggesting that while the school has unique strengths, there are areas needing improvement.

reviews.text: Simmons University has a variety of great accelerated programs, and many very dedicated adjunct professors, however there are a lot of things they keep students in the dark about, and housing and dining are bad.

So far my experience in Simmons has been pretty mid. Something I like about it is the nursing program and the location since it surrounded by other universities as well as the city of Boston. Something I would definitely change is the school spirit, we barely have any, the clubs, the events, and etc

Simmons is in a great location--quiet area of Fenway with easy access to downtown. Professors are amazing and academics are not overwhelming. However, there is zero social scene and school spirit, the school is small and everyone knows one another...it's very cliquey. Also a PWI, the lack of diversity is astounding. Student org life is absolutely dead unless you play a sport and nightlife is wishy-washy with Boston frats. Dorms are bathrooms are pretty mid, and the food is not great, dining hall hours suck also. The school really focuses on STEM/health careers and has no liberal arts/broad education- do NOT come here if you plan on majoring in something artsy or if you want a traditional college experience. Overall I don't hate Simmons but plan on transferring next year to get a better academic, social, traditional and well-rounded college experience.

Simmons is a great school. They provide great scholarships. The doorming could be better but for older building's it’s not too bad. The food is better Han most colleges but could be better. This is a PWI so there is not much diversity.

My overall experience with Simmons University has been positive. I appreciate the strong academic programs, especially in nursing and health sciences, and the professors who genuinely care about their students’ success. The small class sizes create a close-knit, supportive community where it’s easy to connect with both peers and faculty. Being located in Boston is another huge advantage, offering access to top hospitals and professional opportunities. However, I think there’s room for improvement in certain areas. Housing and dining services could be updated, as some facilities feel outdated, and the food options are limited. The cost of tuition is also quite high, which can be challenging. Additionally, the social scene can feel quieter compared to larger universities. Overall, Simmons provides a strong, empowering education with a focus on leadership and social responsibility, but enhancing campus life and affordability would make the experience even better.

Being a student at Simmons University provides me with a lot of opportunities academic-wise. However, I have had some difficulties making friends and struggled with the “clique-y” nature of many of the students. I’ve met some lovely people though, and enjoyed exploring the city!

The academics at Simmons University are really good. This is a great school for science, and it has an impressive nursing program. The housing is a little disappointing, especially since it costs $9,000 per semester, but there will soon be the new housing. The food is okay; it is also pretty disappointing. There are so many great opportunities that this school gives to its students, so if you come here, please take advantage of them.

It’s very comfortable. Great area near Fenway park and the Longwood medical area. Lots of access to internships and jobs at hospitals and more. The professors all care about the success of their students and are all super nice.

The area is nice and enjoyable, I like that it's located in Fenway so there's lots to do if I ever want to go out and explore! The staff and professors are all polite and kind and the academic work is not too much unless you fall behind.

I enjoy the closeness of the residential campus as it creates a sense that the students are their own community as well as the location being a reasonable distance from the academic campus. Both campuses are also in close proximity to other colleges/universities so it's nice knowing a large portion of the area's population are also working on pursuing education.

My experience at Simmons University has been incredibly, going into my sophomore year I feel prepared and excited to take on my courses this year. As a two sport athlete I have been able to make many connections in the simmons community. I am originally from Vermont and the Boston scene was a big difference but the people at Simmons have made it a home away from home.

Simmons is a college centered around creating space for all who enter its doors. Right from the start, during my tour, the light emitting from their campus in incomparable. From the staff to fellow students, it is a friendly environment where your individuality is encouraged, and community is built. In my experience as a latinx student whose faced discrimination before, I have never felt more comfortable at a campus. Truly, every student is treated as equal and all perspectives are welcomed during discussion as we learn from one another about the diverse histories, legacies, and traditions we hold in our hearts. If I were to change anything, I would just like to see more Teacher's Assistant opportunties available. However, I believe that academically, professionally, and personally - Simmons is at the forefront of preparing students for a bright future filled with empathetic, strong-willed, ethics-based leaders.

I love the location of Simmons University, mainly because my major is related to health sciences, and we are right near all of the hospitals. There isn't much to do at the school if you are looking for fun, but since we are right in Fenway and are near other colleges, it doesn't get too dull. The academics are excellent, but I would change the party atmosphere. The school spirit is lacking. There are clubs, but it's not a huge thing on campus. Last school year, there was only one school-hosted party. Other than that I would recommend this school.

Simmons University is in a location surrounded by plenty of opportunities for both work and school. Its central spot in Boston makes it easily accessible by public transportation and close to a lot of what the city has to offer. For commuter students, Simmons offers helpful resources like lounges, lockers, and flexible dining options that make campus life more manageable. These spaces are especially useful when you have time between classes and need a place to study or get work done. It's a thoughtful environment that supports students who don't live on campus but still want to feel connected.
I value the opportunity to pursue further education at an institution where I once served as an adjunct lecturer, deepening my connection to its mission and continuing my journey of growth.

ai_summary: South Dakota State University (SDSU) offers a welcoming and friendly atmosphere for students, with many highlighting the supportive faculty and staff who genuinely care about student success. The campus provides a mix of academic rigor and recreational opportunities, boasting small class sizes that facilitate personal interactions with professors. While many students appreciate the variety of programs and activities available, some express concerns about food options and overall campus amenities, particularly in regard to housing conditions and parking. The sense of community is a strong theme, as students often feel at home in Brookings, which balances a small-town feel with the excitement of college life. However, some reviews indicate a desire for more weekend activities beyond academic commitments and a call for enhanced support for transfer students. Overall, SDSU is described as a good fit for those seeking both education and a vibrant college experience .

reviews.text: I came from a very small community so South Dakota State University was a great place to attend college. The campus is small enough you don't get lost for too long after living there. The professors are receptive and I don't feel awkward talking to them. There is SO much to do that if you're bored it's your own fault. And the town of Brookings is so great to the college. I have never felt unsafe anywhere. The only thing I'd like to see change is the dorm living. Not a lot has been changed from when my friends' parents attended SDSU, so that would be great, but I do understand why it hasn't.

My limited experience as an SDSU freshman has been nothing short of incredible! All colleges/universities claim that they care about student success, but SDSU delivers. The professors and TA's in my classes push me to succeed, offering one-on-one assistance during office hours, tutoring options, and numerous SI's that 'hit home' the material and prepare me for practicals, lab quizzes and tests. The campus is beautiful and easy to maneuver. Dorm life was an easy transition and floor meetings and activities helped build solid friendships. The Brookings community is welcoming and easy to navigate. I am proud to be a Jackrabbit and whole-heartedly recommend South Dakota State.

I love the atmosphere here. All of the students seem friendly, and the town is really nice and easy to navigate. I wish there was more of a selection of food in the dining hall, but there are other options on campus. Most of my professors have really seemed to care about my success and work with me to help me understand the topics.

I currently attend South Dakota State University and I think it is the best university in the area. For my major, I had to choose between Mines and USD. SDSU is close to home and has the degree I am pursing. The campus is great and very walkable. The professors want to see you succeed and offer help after class hours. The food is good and very accessible due to their partnership with the engineering program to deliver food with robots.

My experience has been both positive and challenging. I liked the supportive academic environment and helpful instructors who encouraged learning. However, scholarship availability is very limited, which creates financial stress for many students. In addition, the food quality needs improvement, as options are limited, repetitive, and often lack good taste and nutrition. Improving financial aid opportunities and food services would greatly enhance the overall student experience.

My overall experience at South Dakota State University has been positive and meaningful. SDSU has given me the opportunity to grow both academically and personally while working toward my long-term goal of becoming a registered nurse. I’ve really appreciated the supportive learning environment, especially from instructors who genuinely want students to succeed and are willing to help when you reach out. I also value how SDSU offers strong resources for Native American students, including advising, scholarships, and a sense of community that makes you feel seen and supported rather than just like a number.
I like that SDSU encourages leadership, service, and involvement beyond the classroom. The campus culture pushes you to think about how your education connects to real-world impact, which is important to me as someone who wants to give back to my community. I’ve also enjoyed the flexibility of courses and the ability to balance school with family and work responsibilities.

My hometown of Sykesville, MD is almost 1400 miles away from South Dakota State University, which is at least a 20-hour drive. Why so far in Brookings, South Dakota for school? Two reasons -- Air Force ROTC and my major, which is Geographic Information Sciences. Captain Donahue from the AFROTC program at SDSU reached out to me during my senior year in high school. We emailed back and forth a few times, as I was interested in participating in ROTC in college. I checked to see if by chance the school offered my major that I was interested in studying, and then one Friday night I decided to apply to SDSU. Even though I am so far away from my family, I feel at home at SDSU. I truly can say that I am happy out in South Dakota at SDSU. Do I miss my family? Yes, but I can always go home and visit them during holidays and breaks. They all support me and my decision to attend SDSU for my education. The only thing I would say needing a change would be the dining hall food selections.

Classes give good information and teachers are willing to push you to always do better. Many opportunities are available in specific fields of work. Food in the commons could be better, but is still better than expected.
I love SDSU! I am from South Dakota so it has always been a dream of mine to go to SDSU ever since I was little. I have made so many friends. The academics are amazing.
South Dakota State University offers a hands on experience academically and socially. The professors are very helpful and strives to keep their students ace.

South Dakota State University is a wonderful place to get an undergraduate or graduate degree. They have a beautiful campus with nice walk areas and pretty scenery. They also have great professors that are very willing to help anyone pass their classes.

My overall experience at South Dakota State University is anywhere from average to good. I love how campus is a walking campus and was very accessible for walking to classes and wasn’t too far from a lot of the main buildings to go. I also love the different events, activities, and experiences that I can enjoy and get to be apart of. Something that I would love to be changed about the university is to have more scholarships for juniors and seniors. I feel that juniors and seniors should get the same or a better opportunity than freshman students especially since 15% to 25% of freshman end up dropping out. Also, some of the higher education students end up not being able to afford college and dropping out because most of the scholarships are only for their freshman year. I would also like the school to be more attentive about how many students they accept compared to how much building and parking space they have for accommodating all the students living on campus.
I liked the Athletics at SDSU. I would like to see better food options on campus and more food options. I would like to see more healthy options in the c-store.

My time at South Dakota State University has been a positive and meaningful experience. From day one, I felt welcomed by a supportive community of professors, staff, and fellow students. The campus is beautiful, and he facilities, especially the Wellness Center and Student Union, really enhanced student life.
I appreciated how approachable and helpful the professors were, often going out of their way to support students. There are also many clubs and organizations that made it easy to get involved and meet new people.
However, I think SDSU could improve in a couple areas. Career services could be more active in connecting students to internships and jobs beyond South Dakota. Additionally, more emphasis on diversity and inclusion would help make the campus even more welcoming for everyone.
Overall, I’m grateful for my experience at SDSU and would recommend it to others looking for a strong academic and community-oriented university.

SDSU has been very well for me especially in my field of work. I am apart of the SDSU Theater and Dance and Music department and this area has been nothing but amazing. I have learned so much from all my professors. Adding on, I have also continued to grow in my profession hoping to push me to my end goal. This department has helped me strive as an individual and made me very happy and proud of who I am. What I would change about SDSU though is how they don’t take time to view the diversity of the school. SDSU is a huge college that has tons of diversity and it would be nice if they represented people of different ethnicities, backgrounds, etc. I feel as though some of these areas go dark and are left to fend for themselves instead of being up lifted.

ai_summary: Southeastern University stands out for its strong Christian community and supportive environment , which many students find transformative for their personal and spiritual growth. The professors are frequently highlighted as passionate and dedicated to student success, often being approachable and fostering small class sizes for more personalized learning . While the academic programs are well-received, some students express challenges with tuition affordability and strict attendance policies. The campus itself is praised for its beauty and welcoming atmosphere , though there are mixed reviews about campus life and the variety of events. Overall, students appreciate the strong sense of community , opportunities for engagement, and the integration of faith into their education.

reviews.text: Great school and Christian environment. The faculty and staff care deeply about students and the community on campus is obvious.

I had a very frustrating time at southeastern. I encountered a lot of rude people and a lot of the registrar and student retention staff who would cancel meetings and never return emails. It made you feel very unimportant as a student that the only thing that mattered was their image and getting more students in.

Southeastern University really helps to cultivate Spirit-led relationships, education, and future opportunities. They specialize in spiritual equipment and is amazing to be in such a Christ-centered environment.
I really love the community!! Tye campus is beautiful qnd the professors are amaizngggg it is!! It is such a blessing to be able to attend seu!!

It is a great school, amazing community! The minute i walked on campus it felt like home, everyone was so welcoming and it really felt like the right place to be! The environment is such a fun and God honoring environment with the many opportunities of chapels and events put on by the school! The RA’s are wonderful always there for you through hard times! I have my best friends here! The school is hands down an awesome school with many different people groups growing and coming together as one!

P1If you are reading this and you're excited about college, tie yourself to your chair because this review is a rough ride.
Let's start with housing, because apparently, that's where all your dreams will go and die. I lived in an 8-person dorm, and one roommate managed to STINK UP the ENTIRE place with mold and trash. No, I am not talking "college messy". I am talking health-hazard something out of a freaking bio-lab, levels of FOUL ODOR. Campus life took a week to do anything, and only after she THREATENED us did we get moved into emergency housing. Also, guess what emergency housing was known for at the time? You'll never guess—the place where they stuck people with bedbugs.
Food. Omg. Food, ALLEGEDLY, there were condoms found in the food ALLEGEDLY. The menu will never change so throw that out the window, the eggs are also alledgly fake. If you want something nutritious, you can choose from overpriced acai bowls, Chick-fil-A, pizza, bagels, or hamburgers, which are healthy, right?

P2. We were on lockdown from surrounding conflicts THREE TIMES, and for ambiance, you can hear gunshots sometimes. Outside our dorm window, trash piled up constantly because the end dorms apparently double as community dumpsters. This attracted VERMIN and ANTS. You want any internships that don't include church camps? Good luck. Connections are weak, and support is minimal. Roommates and I were also put in danger by a student who claimed to be a russian spy(born in America). The number of times I have had to talk to cops on this campus is crazy. Chapel deserves a warning label, since you need to line up an hour/half an hour early, in the heat, while you balance A&P or CALCII. Off campus? I was chased and harassed by multiple homeless men, and my car had rocks thrown at it. This all happened less than 10-15 minutes away. I have so much more. This place sells itself as wholesome and safe. RUN WHILE YOU CAN—health hazards, safety issues, etc. Pay money to be stressed, ignored endangered

My experience at Southeastern University has been overwhelmingly positive. I’ve grown academically through engaging classes in business, theology, and composition, and I’ve felt supported by professors who genuinely want students to succeed. Being on the track team has pushed me to grow physically and mentally while building friendships that feel like family. I also appreciate SEU’s focus on faith and community—it’s helped shape my character and discipline while balancing school and work at Palace Pizza and Olive Garden.
One thing I’d like to see improved is quicker communication and processing times in certain administrative offices, especially during busy semesters. Overall, SEU has been a place where I’ve matured, found purpose, and built meaningful connections.
Southeastern University is a Christian University that helps grow faith with a curriculum focused on God and learning.

Great. The community is at SEU. So far, everything seems good. I really enjoyed this experience because it was engaging, well-organized, and gave me a chance to learn in a hands-on way. I appreciated the clear instructions and the supportive environment that made it easy to ask questions and share ideas. The activities were meaningful and helped me understand the material better. I also liked working with others and hearing different perspectives, which made the experience more enjoyable and interactive. One thing I would like to see change is adding more time for discussion and reflection, so we can dive deeper into the topics and learn even more from each other.

Southeastern University is a wonderful community and I am blessed to have had the opportunity to be apart of it! The professors are some of the most genuine, kind-hearted people who want to support and see all of their students succeed. The academics are great, the classes and way they are taught has helped me to grow in every aspect of my life. I would reccomend SEU to anyone considering a Christian college.

SEU is an amazing college, especially online. The professors are quick to respond to questions and concerns. I can say that there is a difference when it comes to the professors, they love God and they have amazing character! I would recommend attending SEU, whether online or on campus. If you do attend online I recommend finding a church that is a partnered site campus with them. Nothing like being relational with others on the same journey and it makes college a little easier.

Southeastern University is a great college each day unfolds with classes, assignments, and new friendships. The campus pulses with eagerly as students hurry between lectures and hanging out with fellow classmates. There are no changes need to be made Southeastern University is wonderful college!

This university is committed to nurturing its students both in their faith and in achieving academic excellence. It serves as a powerful force for good in the community and exemplifies what true excellence and intelligence look like.

Southeastern University is a great school. The academic advisor is awesome, knowledgeable and attentive to your studies. The registrar, financial aid all is helpful. Professors are great and the curriculums make sense. This kind of service makes anyone want to continue their education even when the tough gets going.

ai_summary: Southern Illinois University Edwardsville (SIUE) offers students a supportive, welcoming academic environment with engaged faculty who genuinely care about student success. Many reviews highlight the friendly atmosphere on campus, where students appreciate the beauty of the surrounding nature and the variety of extracurricular activities available. While the academics and programs, particularly in nursing and engineering, are well-regarded, some students have expressed frustration over administrative issues and inconsistent advising. Additionally, diversity on campus has been noted as an area needing improvement, as some reviews indicated that the student body could feel segregated. Overall, SIUE is considered a good option for students seeking a quality education at an affordable price, but the experiences can vary widely depending on individual circumstances.

reviews.text: So far, my academic courses have been pretty good. I have been in all gen eds, and I am slowly integrating classes that are specific for my major. I have liked almost all of my professors so far, and I have made friends in most of my classes. My class schedule is also not bad at all, I can't complain about it. I live on campus in a dorm with a roommate, and the experience overall hasn't been bad. I love the suite style and the fact that I am not in a communal bathroom. Some of my only annoyances are the fact that we cannot control whether we are using heat or air conditioning, the hallway lights do not turn off at night, and people can be pretty loud at night. The dorm room is small, but isn't bad, but the showers are very small. The campus is a good size and none of the buildings are crazy far apart. I don't love the dining hall and its options, but I have been able to find what works for me. I would appreciate some changes in the menu, but there are plenty of other options too.

I love it. It’s very diverse and has many things to do. I meet really great friends who I consider family. I feel like this is my 2nd Home and I am very comfortable with where I am and who i’m becoming as a person
It was interesting, I feel that the school is very diverse but often times lacks some strong foundational classes that build up the careers more.

SIUE was the best choice for me when I was deciding on a school my senior year of High School, and it still is today. I have gotten an all-encompassing experience here on the SIUE campus. I like the location, with it being a nature preserve, but the town is just a short drive. I have had an exceptional experience with the classes and professors. I also work on campus and love it! Whether I am hanging out on or off campus with friends, grabbing food from the MUC, or taking a stroll on the trail, I never run out of things to do and have such a great time. I love SIUE and, unless I could make it free to be here, I would not change anything at all.

I enjoy it at Southern Illinois University Edwardsville. the school has great diversity and there are many programs that you can experience. I was a basketball player in high school and I am able to play basketball here at my own leisure. The staff is excellent and tutoring is available. I feel like I can succeed here with all the possibilities I have.

Looking back now that I’m older, I know this school wasn’t for me. One of the miserable, old white ladies in the office of the Bursar asked 18-year-old me, “Well, why did you decide to go to college when you knew you couldn’t afford it?” Well I don’t know Barb, maybe to get what I thought would be a good education and have a better life for myself in the future…
Glad I used that to motivate me into the person I am today because only 1 instructor (Art Appreciation) cared about me and how I was actually doing in class. The rest were absolutely horrible, money-hungry people. Yes this college is quote-on-quote more affordable than other colleges, but it’s not as great as they like to advertise it as.

My experience at SIUE has been good. I transferred to SIUE from a tiny two year community college. I have made friends, and have had good professors here, so far. The community is good and healthy, the students at SIUE are friendly, and are ready to help complete strangers with directions, help, academic advice, anything. It is also a welcoming environment, and is pro LGBTQ+ which I do like. The only critique I have of the campus is the fact sometimes there will be speakers on campus that are there to spread hate. I don’t like that very much, but the community seems to not pay them any mind and feed into their hate, which is good. A good environment is welcoming to all, which I would say SIUE honors this.
I would love some of their smaller programs be developed, specifically the graphic design and the nutrition program.

My experience as a college freshman at Southern Illinois University at Edwardsville has been both exciting and transformative. Coming to SIUE, I was both eager and nervous about adjusting to a new environment, meeting new people, and balancing the academic expectations of college life. Throughout the year, I have grown not only as a student but also as a young adult preparing for my future. Academically, I have been challenged to think more critically, manage my time wisely, and take greater responsibility for my learning. The professors at SIUE have been more supportive and approachable, creating an environment where I feel encouraged to ask questions and push myself to do better. I have also had the opportunity to explore courses that connect to my career interests, helping me gain a clearer understanding of the path I want to pursue. Over all, my freshman year so far has been a foundation for my personal and academic growth. It has shown me the importance of perseverance.

My overall experience with Southern Illinois University Edwardsville (SIUE) has been very positive. The admission process was smooth, and the staff were supportive and responsive, which made me feel confident about my decision. I truly admire the strong reputation of the School of Engineering, especially in Structural and Earthquake Engineering, as well as the modern research facilities and welcoming academic environment. The diversity and inclusiveness at SIUE also stand out, creating a supportive space for international students like me. One area that could be improved is providing more structured platforms for students to connect with faculty regarding assistantship and research opportunities before starting their program. Overall, SIUE offers a strong, student-focused, and research-driven environment, and I am excited to begin my journey here.

Siue is a pretty good university overall. it’s just certain professors that are less understanding about students learning types. Other than that. They have a pretty good mental health program. That is a very friendly campus and they are very welcoming. The housing has great space too.

As a Black freshman at SIUE in 2025, I feel supported and seen from day one. About 13 % of the student body identifies as Black/African American and programs like the Black Scholar Experience my Scholar Village cohort—and the Black Resource Center give me community and academic guidance. The campus is peaceful and green, with walking paths, Cougar Lake, and cozy spots like the Student Success Center in the MUC I’m participating in clubs, faith events, and exploring majors while making friends who understand where I come from. SIUE may not be huge, but here I already feel like I belong.

I went to SIUE straight out of high school, thinking my parents would move back to southern Illinois. Instead, they moved out of the country, and I stayed stateside alone. I spent holidays with my fiancé and old neighbors, which helped during a tough transition. I lived on campus and had a tight-knit group of four girls, including my roommate. I liked not having to commute and being close to everything, but campus was quiet on weekends and during breaks. The resources at SIUE are amazing if you use them. The Student Development Center helped me with my nursing school application, resume, and career planning. I always tell students to use professor office hours. That connection can save you stress and uncertainty, especially during finals. SIUE helped me become independent and build a strong foundation for the future. I’m grateful for the growth I experienced there.

SIU Edwardsville is a bit like a community college. Not to say that it’s bad but it definitely wasn’t the best it could be. There were nice campus events and the food was good as well but after a while it gets to be a little repetitive, so it’s good to remember to make your own fun (especially on weekends because SIU Edwardsville is a backpack school)!

My experience at Southern Illinois University Edwardsville (SIUE) has been very positive so far. The campus is modern, green, and welcoming, with a great mix of academic focus and community spirit. Faculty members are knowledgeable and approachable, especially in the School of Engineering, where hands-on learning and real-world applications are emphasized. The resources provided—labs, student support centers, and career guidance—are helpful for students at every stage of their education.
I particularly appreciate the support system for international students, including orientation programs and accessible staff who assist with visa, housing, and cultural adjustment needs. The campus feels safe and inclusive, and the student community is diverse and collaborative.
Overall, SIUE offers a great environment for academic growth and personal development, and I’m glad to be a part of it.

ai_summary: Southern Illinois University Carbondale offers a unique blend of academics , student life, and campus culture that many students find appealing. The professors are generally described as caring and involved, contributing to a supportive learning environment . The diverse campus fosters a sense of community, with many students appreciating the various cultural backgrounds present. However, some reviews express concerns about the quality of certain facilities and the dining options, as well as the limited effectiveness of student services like advising and financial aid for lower-income students. The campus itself features beautiful natural surroundings , including lakes and trails, which many students enjoy. Overall, while SIUC has its challenges, students value the opportunities for engagement, networking, and a welcoming atmosphere .

reviews.text: I enjoyed my three years at SIUC. I would like to see enrollment back up. Campus is beautiful, especially in the fall. Most courses are enjoyable, and most of the teachers and staff are kind and helpful. My family enjoys Saluki football games. I would recommend SIUC to future students.
All the professors were really nice and helpful. The material was updated and engaging. Counselors were super helpful in navigating working for full-time and going to school full-time online.
I have had a wonderful experience with this institution. I have enjoyed all of my classes, and I feel prepared and supported by all the institution's staff and educators.
You get out of it what you put into it. SIU has great student life and a lively campus community if you want it. Generally, the professors at SIU care a lot about their students.
Great experience and wonderful professors. I met my husband there. My experiences there helped shaped me as an adult.
Overall, I have truly found my place here and believe just about anyone can find a good community here.

I like learning online, the autonomy of it all! Although not having student life is kinda depressing at times and being a nontraditional student has no perks! Scholarships could be improved and student relations.
Have had a lot of fun of course it’s a pretty big open campus so a lot of walking. The dining hall food isn’t really that good and the Carbondale area isn’t the nicest place.
The school itself is fine, but there is no communication between any of the offices and everything was online and it was very confusing

I really enjoyed my time at SIUC because it felt like more than just a campus it felt like home. Coming in, I wasn’t entirely sure what to expect, but I quickly found a strong sense of belonging through my involvement in student organizations, leadership roles, and the friendships I built. The professors genuinely cared about my success and were always willing to support me both academically and personally. SIUC gave me the space to grow into myself, to take on challenges, and to develop confidence in who I am and what I want to do. It’s a place that shaped not just my education, but who I’ve become.

My experience at Southern Illinois University of Carbondale included both growing pains and positive outcomes during my first year. I’ve learned about the many resources SIU has for new and returning students which makes it a great school to graduate from. SIU is also very accommodating to many situations and tends to the needs of their students to ensure comfort and success. I am a first generation student so the emotions and experiences I’ve had over the past year aren’t some my family can relate to. SIU has many mental health resources that helped me with the adjustment. They hosted many self care events with food, music and activities that helped me get out of my dorm more. When I struggled academically, my advisor recommended tutoring and study groups to get extra help.

With me being from Virginia and going to school in Illinois especially Carbondale was a culture shock. The town is beautiful, the people are nice with open arms, the food is amazing, the night life is great, your not to far from St. Louis or Chicago and other schools or universities. I lived on and off campus. Almost everything is in walking distance.

Going to Southern Illinois was such a great idea and I suggest you go to because there are lots of events for the students to partake in which helps people make connections with others and deepen bonds.

Overall my experience at Carbondale has been amazing. I have had so much success in navigating my major and finding ways to get more involved. While also still having time to have a social life having the opportunity to meet amazing individuals. I think the campus is extremely diverse and inviting, there is definitely a place for everyone to grow and gain new experiences. With only being there for a year I have already been exposed to so many more opportunities than I expected. There are a number of research labs to join to fun and to educational clubs for everyone. The campus has so many amazing programs to help students grow their knowledge while also finding new connections.

As an incoming sophomore for the fall 2025 season, I have genuinely overly enjoyed my experience so far at SIUC. At first, I didn't think I was going to like the school because it's in state, but once I touched down on campus I feel in love with it ever since. I am apart of Trio (Student Support Services), a part of the Black Women's Task Force Club, GEI Anytown Campus Program 2025, I have been invited to several variety of events (Martin Luther King Jr. Dinner at SEMO 2025, Saluki Ball 2025) received awards: Trio "Rising Scholar Award 2025", GEI SIUC/SIUE Anytown Campus Program 2025, I worked in the SSB in the law department (Student Affairs: Student Legal Assistance/Law Firm and I will be working in the fall with Trio with the Upper Bound program where I help high school students in Chicago come and visit SIUC and make it their new home. So, I love it here at SIUC so far.

ai_summary: Southern Methodist University (SMU) offers a vibrant and beautiful campus in Dallas, where students appreciate the small class sizes and supportive professors dedicated to student success. The academic programs are consistently praised, particularly in business and liberal arts, and there are abundant opportunities for networking and internships due to the university's location. Student life includes engaging events , a strong Greek presence, and a fun social atmosphere , but some reviews note that campus life can feel cliquey and that diversity is a concern. While many find the resources and student services helpful, others express dissatisfaction with the administration, particularly regarding financial aid and housing conditions. Overall, SMU is viewed as a place that fosters personal and academic growth, though it does come with a hefty price tag that has generated mixed feelings among students about its value.

reviews.text:
What I thought college would be was an exact match for what I thought should happen at a college within the United States and everything associated with it all the way down to just how I feel about everything regarding campus and everything related to the campus is just as I had originally pictured it before coming here; the most important thing I've taken away from the experience of this place (college) has been the people I have met while attending this university.
One weakness I have observed regarding this campus is its lack of ethnic diversity among students and staff. When there are students and staff members from different cultures and backgrounds, this promotes a greater opportunity to be accepting of one another, thus setting a foundation for each student to excel in the overall learning experience of this university.

Overall I have loved my experience at SMU. I am part of a sorority, so the social scene is very influential here. I think it would be great if there is a healthier mix between academics and social events freshman year. I definitely focused on social events heavily freshman year, while over time I have now found a better balance.

As a parent of an SMU student, I can tell you that this university has been a HUGE disappointment. The greek life is very important if you want to be involved, OH - but you are required to get a fake ID because alcohol consumption is also a basic requirement no matter what your age is. Drugs and very wealthy students seem to go hand in hand along with making cheating on tests easy by the fraternities so that any kind of grade curve will be ruined for the students who chose not to cheat. It's a party school and the professors are cold and not helpful or accessible. Plus, you are next to downtown Dallas, a dangerous city with tons of bars. As a parent, I have not been able to speak to my child's counselor. Everything is extremely hard to understand, from the finance department to academics. My daughter was at a state school before this and everything from the ethics to the professors was much better. BIG disappointment in a college that I hoped was going to be much better.

It is very difficult to make friends. The student dashboard is amazing and allows you to select future classes and plan your entire college career. Campus is pretty compact so classes are easy to get to and from. Parking isn’t an issue.

I love being at this school. I am currently in Sports Management courses and the professors that I have are active in that field as well so it is nice to be learning from people who know what it is like in the industry. There are some professors that are not good educators, but know what they're talking about and it is nice to learn about their strategies when it comes to how to work in the sports environment, but I would like to have a more educator type of person teaching these courses.

Overall, my experience at Southern Methodist University has been positive. I appreciate the strong sense of community, supportive faculty, and the wide range of academic and extracurricular opportunities available to students. I also value the campus environment and the resources that support both academic success and personal growth.
One area I would like to see improved is greater affordability and transparency around costs such as housing and meal plans. Expanding support for students balancing academics with financial responsibilities would make the experience even better. Overall, SMU has provided a welcoming and enriching college experience.

I think SMU is very proficient at making both students and staff feel welcome with all the different clubs and events held there. However, I would like to see more updates on the older dorms and the science building; it feels as if more care is being put into the law and business buildings when more students have STEM classes. Overall, I think it's a very good school that promotes exploration in classes using its common curriculum rather than the school being just focused on majors, and at the same time is a very welcoming school.

A graduate experience at SMU is generally supportive, academically rigorous, and community-focused, with small classes, strong faculty mentorship, and meaningful networking opportunities, though it can be expensive and occasionally bureaucratic.
I love SMU. The academics are great and all of the people are welcoming and kind. Haven’t had anything but great professors.

I have really enjoyed student life and everything about being a student here. The only downside I have experienced is certain professors will give you a grade based on if you used your beliefs and values that differed from theirs.

With my overall experience with SMU, (Southern Methodist University) has been great as I've constructed a great sense mentorship with a few of my professors. which encouraged me to ask any questions or concerns I had about the courses I was taking. Along my classes I found myself making friends everywhere I went within the first couple weeks of school this allowed me to feel less alone and homesick. I have been grateful to be accepted into such a welcoming and comforting environment.

Southern Methodist University is the Howard from the South. From its immaculate buildings and amazing well diverse and well prepared professors I have the outmost confidence that I will succeed in my profession.

SMU is the first place where professors feel like colleagues. I recently grabbed lunch with my finance professor and our school director, Todd Milbourn, and I met one-on-one with the new dean to learn his vision. Those conversations feel like friends talking about ideas and character, not only grades. As a student businessman, I feel like I am living the ideal college life. SMU is not only a top university. It is a community that multiplies opportunities through mentors, classmates, and leaders who open doors. Every week, I leave a class or a coffee chat with a new lead, a mentor, or a friend. I have learned that success is not only about what you can do. It is also who you learn from and grow with, and SMU gives me that environment every day.

As I am beginning my journey at Southern Methodist University, I could already tell that this institution offers great academics and a vibrant student life. I chose to further my education at this university because of the excellent programs that they offer, like the business program. I am excited and thrilled to start my academic pursuits this fall.
Good experience! Professors available and ready to help students. Lots of activities and clubs to get involved with.

school_name: Southern University and A & M College
ai_summary: Southern University & A&M College offers a diverse and inclusive environment , with many students feeling a strong sense of community and support. Academically, the professors are generally seen as dedicated to student success, although communication, particularly from administrative staff, can be inconsistent and frustrating at times. Student life is vibrant, featuring numerous activities, events, and a rich cultural atmosphere , including celebrated traditions like Homecoming and events like Pretty Wednesdays. While the food and campus atmosphere receive positive remarks, there are concerns regarding the need for improved student housing and parking. Overall, many students appreciate the school's welcoming environment, but they also express desires for better resources and infrastructure.

reviews.text: haven't had the experience yet but hoping to get that in the fall when I start my courses. I've seen many videos and heard many stories and I cannot wait to get the full jag experience and have a great time all while earning my degree in nursing.
My overall experience here at southern university A & M college is amazing so far. I love it here and I enjoy my engineering courses so far. The sports team are great. The campus life is amazing.
My experience with southern is good . I like the football time and how very diverse the school . The only thing I would change is the staff.

I love my experience at Southern University & A&M College. I have been welcomed and treated with such generosity and respect. The professors are truly invested. At Southern, I am more than just a student ID number, I am known by my name. My professors welcome dialogue and go above an beyond to assist their students. Campus life is very lively and always exciting. Our student cafe has real southern cuisine, very delicious. Housing is hit or miss, I have been fortunate to have great living accomodations. On Southern's campus, I feel like I am at home away from home. I am thankful for the opportunity attend Southern.

My overall experience at Southern University A&M College is a 6 out of 10, primarily due to the lack of support from the professors. Campus doesn't help out the fact that sometimes you may be stressed because of the unsafe environment around the campus, and the threats we received are just the beginning and not even half of what's endured. Sometimes work will pile upon you but after 4 years it'll be over.
This food is betrayal as a taste. Fire all of your chefs. Burn it down and don't even try again. Pls

My overall experience with Southern University A & M College has been okay. The thing I like most about the school is the band, the organizations and the football games. I would love to see a change in the environment, the staff, the housing. Southern University is in the middle of nothing. Us being around nothing causes students to go 20-30 minutes out just to get food. The staff is horrible in the departments. They do not like helping the students and give us the run around. The housing arrangements are not good as well. Students are living with dirty water and mold in their rooms. The bathroom in the communal dorms seems like they were just thrown together, which caused me to get a membership at the gym just to take a decent shower. There isn't enough washers and dryers so I end up leaving campus to pay for services that I'm already paying for at school. We complain about these things all the time but nothing gets fixed. These are just a few of the issues Southern needs to address.
Southern atmosphere is great but when it comes to the staff and the school actually being there for the students it’s horrible they set us up to fail.
Southern was a great choice for my undergrad degree. The education was valuable and campus life was top notch. There's no experience like an HBCU.

My overall experience at southern university has been a roller coaster of successes and challenges. I marched 4 years ins the human jukebox and that was the experience of a life time, I was able to travel to Georgia, Mississippi, and California during my time and those were very exciting experiences. We practiced from four O clock often to twelve or one in the morning depending on who we played that week. It was challenging to balance my school schedule and band early on but the longer I endured the easier it became.

My overall experience at Southern University is pretty cool. It has opened new and better opportunities to my life. My experience is most definitely better than my High school experience. If Had the choice to do it again I would choose Southern over and over again. My experience could be better due to financial situations but overall the school is a ten out of ten for me.

Southern University and A\&M College, located in Baton Rouge, Louisiana, holds a distinguished place as the flagship of the Southern University System. Founded in 1880, it's a historically Black university committed to providing quality education to a diverse student body.
The university offers a wide array of undergraduate and graduate programs, with notable strengths in engineering, business, and the sciences. Its urban campus is a vibrant hub of academic and cultural activity, fostering a strong sense of community.
Southern University is a great college with an abundance of diversity. The cultural elements in tandem with the academics create a satisfactory experience overall.
So far I have had a great experience at my university. I wasn’t too involved in school except being a member of my schools marching band, and that alone is its own experience.
My matriculation at Southern University is an exciting experience, yet it’s not without trivialities.

ai_summary: Springfield College in Massachusetts is recognized for its close-knit community atmosphere, where small class sizes foster strong relationships between students and faculty. Many reviews highlight the supportive and friendly environment , making it feel like a second home to its students. The college places a significant emphasis on athletics and wellness , with the Humanics philosophy focusing on the balance of spirit, mind, and body resonating throughout the campus culture. While student life is vibrant with various clubs and activities, some reviewers note concerns about the aging campus buildings, limited food options, and a lack of diversity. The academic programs, particularly in health and human services, are regarded as excellent, but some students seek a greater challenge in their courses. Overall, Springfield College is recommended for those who value a strong community and are interested in health-related fields, though potential students are encouraged to consider their specific academic and personal needs when deciding.

reviews.text: I just recently graduated from Springfield in December. A vast majority of the professors on campus are incredible and really willing to ensure you have a positive educational experience. Granted there are certainly a few that are not the best to deal with and do not genuinely care for the students. I will be blunt, each year I spent on campus the worst the environment seemed to get. Some of the students act very entitled and are quite rude. I had to unfortunately file a Title IX report my last semester on campus and nothing was done to the student I had to file it against. To end on a positive note though, the career center is incredible and the Alumni Center is an under utilized/appreciated resource!

Springfield College is such a welcoming environment where the professors care about you, as a person, and you are not just a number. They care about your safety and mental health. Everyone there wants to learn, have a successful future, and really like to stay in shape! Very Close knit...

My experience at Springfield College has been very positive overall. The school has a strong sense of community, and you feel that right away—from professors who genuinely want you to succeed to classmates who are supportive and easy to connect with. The emphasis on hands-on learning has been one of the biggest strengths. Whether it’s classroom work or clinical/field experiences, you’re constantly applying what you learn, which makes the material feel meaningful and prepares you well for your future career.
I also appreciate the small class sizes. It’s easy to get to know faculty, ask questions, and actually build relationships rather than feeling like just another student in the room. Campus life is active, and the environment is welcoming.
Overall, Springfield College is a great place if you value community, personal growth, and strong professional preparation. I’ve had a meaningful experience here and feel well supported in my academic and career goals.

ai_summary: At St. Ambrose University, many students appreciate the intimate, supportive environment fostered by small class sizes, which allows for personalized attention from professors. The welcoming campus community facilitates friendships and encourages involvement in various clubs, activities, and events, helping students feel at home. While the professors are viewed as dedicated and helpful, there's some feedback regarding the need for enhanced campus safety measures and improvements in food quality. Students also note that, despite the enriching academic experience, tuition can be a concern, particularly for those without sufficient financial aid. Overall, St. Ambrose is characterized by a close-knit atmosphere that promotes both academic and personal growth, with opportunities that many find to be transformative.

reviews.text: Its very easy to make many friends + professors are pretty good however the food really sucks + housing is alright

I would say I have had a good experience. I have struggled to make friends, and I do not love the temperature of the school, but overall, I really love the school. I like the activities on campus and the social life.

I am a softball player at Saint Ambrose University, and I truly love being here. I transferred from Kirkwood Community College, and the transition has been a really positive experience both academically and athletically. One change I would like to see is greater appreciation for college athletes. Student-athletes dedicate a lot of time, effort, and commitment to representing the university, often balancing demanding schedules, and that dedication deserves more recognition.
I like that I am able to take my classes online. It provides more flexibility for me to be able to work full time.

The main factor why I chose is because it’s close to my hometown. It’s super nice to be able to come home whenever. St. Ambrose for the most part was really good for my first year but the surrounding area around the campus is sort of bad. I feel as though St. Ambrose is so expensive and it’s not even the greatest school. I really don’t want to transfer because I love how close I am to my family but it’s just so expensive.

I like Saint Ambrose because it is a smaller private college. They are very good and generous about giving out scholarships. Another reason why I like Ambrose is the classroom sizes. They are not super big. This will allow you to have more time to talk to the professor or ask questions if you need. Even though Ambrose is a small university there is always something to do on campus. There are plenty of opportunities for people to meet others. There's nothing I really would change except certain class times. If your an athlete make sure you schedule classes around any practices because there are some late classes but also early practices. Make sure that you are communicating with any coaches if need be. Overall Ambrose is a pretty good school.

Everyone that I was encountered with was very helpful when wanting to switch majors and were very helpful in who to get into contact with and how to get everything rolling. All of my professors were great, my advisor was great and my professors at my grad school I am going to were helpful in the process,

St Ambrose has a really good exercise science/kinesiology department. From there students can choose to go into physical therapy, occupational therapy, or health and human fitness. The food on campus isn't always the greatest but the surrounding area has almost every fast food chain and a lot of good restaurants.

This university has an all encompassing robust student support which is much needed for incoming freshmen as well as seniors who might find college life challenging. I like the diversity and their football program.

My experience at St. Ambrose University has been very positive and engaging. The faculty truly care about student success, and the supportive campus community makes it easy to stay motivated. I’ve enjoyed the smaller class sizes, which allow for more personal interaction and meaningful discussion. The resources available—from tutoring to career services—have been incredibly helpful in my academic journey.
If I could suggest one improvement, it would be to expand evening or weekend course options for working students. Overall, St. Ambrose has provided a welcoming environment where I’ve grown academically and personally.
I would defiantly like to see students feel safer with their environment. I do not like seeing them so afraid.

I absolutely loved my time at st Ambrose university and I wouldn’t have changed it! I’d go back in a heartbeat! I miss the campus vibe, the people, and my professors caring about my overall wellbeing and not just my academic success

I was very nervous about living away from home for the first time and not knowing anybody, however St. Ambrose offers excellent student life activities that have given me so many opportunities to meet new people. The teachers and staff are always willing to help and offer guidance.

I am an off campus student so I do not have much experience living on campus or having a roomate and eating cafeteria food, but I do have experience with athletics since I run track and I have had a work study job, so these have allowed me to socialize with others even tho I am not living on campus.
