I absolutely love it here at Temple! There are so many research opportunities at Temple, which is amazing for students like me who want to go into research in the future. In my experience, the dorms here are pretty good. (I recommend living in 1940 as a freshman.) The food at the J&H dining hall is decent, but the Morgan dining hall and Student Center are much better. They have actual restaurants like Panda Express, Chick-fila-A, Wing Stop, and more. The academics are pretty good. There are a lot of resources such as tutoring, and academic advising. As far as safety goes, the campus itself is pretty safe, however, the area surrounding the campus is not always the safest. If you ever go off campus, (especially at night) do not go out alone, and be vigilant. Personally, I have not had any safety concerns, but just be careful. Overall I think Temple is a great school, and if you like being in a large city, then I think you’ll love it too!

As a freshman, who have finished their first semester in Fall 2025, at Temple University, I will say that my overall experiences were great. Having been a first generation College student, I will say that all of my professor were great people. They help either learn something new or help me refresh my skill at certain topic, such as math or literature. Going to college, I had to improve my time management and work ethic since college give me a lot of freedom, freedom that you usually don't feel in highschool. My professor help me mold that freedom into something that I can help me be successful at my course. I also study primarily within the computer science building and the food truck there were great. They had a lot of option like Korean food, Japanese food, or Sandwiches. Lastly, the library is a great place to relax and do my work.

Temple is great, there's lots to do in the city and it's easy to hang out with friends. The campus events leave much to be desired though, if you aren't partying on the weekends you better have friends who are willing to do other things, or you'll be alone.

The main thing I hear about Temple is that people are scared of the location and that it isn't safe. Obviously you're in a city and things will happen, but there's Temple security everywhere, and generally inside campus it is completely safe. The dorms are absolutely terrible and it's cheaper and nicer to just look for an apartment close by. The library is new and amazing and between there the SERC, and Paley hall there's hundreds of cozy little spots to study.

ai_summary: At Tennessee State University, students often highlight the strong sense of community and cultural connection , particularly being part of an HBCU. Many appreciate the diverse student body and the support from faculty, with some professors being particularly engaging and helpful, fostering meaningful connections . However, concerns frequently emerge about the efficiency of the administration, especially regarding financial aid, with reports of delays and communication issues. While the campus life is vibrant with various activities and organizations, some students express disappointment with the condition of housing and the limited dining options . Overall, the experience at TSU is marked by a mix of camaraderie, challenges with administrative processes, and a desire for improved campus facilities.

reviews.text: I enjoyed having my own freedom and chedule while also balancing work and you have a lot of free time
It’s a diversity University which have any kind of people including professors and students from all over the world and very professional always willing to help the students to archive their goals

Tennessee State University is a place where the friends you make and people you meet make you feel at home. I am from the northern east coast, and this school has left a great impression of what Nashville, Tennessee is like. The campus restaurant selections are rarely disappointing and there truly is always something to do out here. The only thing I would change is to have some of the restaurants open on more days and / or longer. I truly value everything I have learned here as a Mass Communications student and the nursing program here is great as well. There are amazing experiences awaiting anybody who decides to attend TSU.
I loved it, I really see myself going here in the future full time! I can’t wait to be apart of there community!!!
Great opportunity to expand my academics and athletics. I really like the surrounding area and community.

I had a great experience at Tennessee State University. It was an enriching experience that was laced with wonderful memories and opportunities. I cherish my time in undergrad and all thing I would change is having applied for more scholarships but I still would have attended the same school

I love Tennessee state university. Best HBCU in Tennessee. My love for TSU is rooted in pride, purpose, and legacy. It’s the culture that shaped me, the community that lifted me, and the history that inspires me to lead with confidence. TSU represents resilience, excellence, and opportunity—where dreams are challenged, voices are heard, and greatness is cultivated. From the Tiger spirit to the bonds built for life, TSU is more than a university; it’s home, heart, and a lifelong commitment to impact.

The professors make sure that you do your work at your most full potential. The upperclassman students are very welcoming. During welcome week, they tried to make sure everyone felt at home. In the cafeteria, they would sometimes have special days where they would cook certain foods like philly cheese steak, gyros, and they would make special drinks. The days that they would do this was called hydration station.

My first semester at Tennessee State University has been an incredibly rewarding experience. I enjoy the authentic culture and how the TSU community is intentional about nurturing students. Class sizes and getting to know professors is another area I appreciate. If I had to change anything, the freshmen dorm could use some major renovation work. For instance, only one side of the room has an outlet so residents have to use long surge protectors to adequately plug in devices and appliances. The campus Chick-Fil-A has also been closed all semester and I really wish it would open soon. It would be nice to see food services seek more input from students on meal options as well. Overall, I am thankful for my experience and would definitely choose TSU a hundred times again.

Attending Tennessee State University has been an enriching experience that has shaped both my academic and professional journey. I have enjoyed the dedicated faculty, engaging coursework, and opportunities for hands-on learning. The university fosters a welcoming and inclusive environment where students can thrive and find their voice.
One area for improvement would be enhancing administrative efficiency—particularly in advising and financial aid communication—to make student processes smoother. Overall, TSU has given me a strong foundation and pride in being part of a historic institution that values education, culture, and community impact.

Went to visit my friend at the beginning of the month and a bunch of people started throwing stuff and harassing me because i am simply white. Got called cracker and everything within 10 Minutes of being on campus. Definitely will never be coming back here as well my friend who currently attends has made it clear this is his last semester going to such an unethical school full of degenerative students such as already is
Great diversity, live community great staff, smart students great professor, security, security, and good vibes

I will not be sending my son here. You allow mobs of students screaming at people who don't have their same ideologies, an older friend was threatened with a screw driver, etc. They will be transferring. Another school down the tubes. Throwing signs at cars? Unprofessional and disrespectful students and faculty.

They allow racist students to run people off their campus for having different political views! The students threatened a speaker when he attempted to retrieve his property and the people in charge at the school told the public the students acted professionally! This school needs to lose ALL funding!

A bunch of racists at this university and to say your students were professional and respectful to the men that were there as conservative, is a lie. Police should have been arresting those hitting these men, damaging their car also. Also to those stealing signs, thats theft and illegal. You should be ashamed of yourselves. Disgusting individuals.

ai_summary: Tennessee Technological University is commonly noted for its strong sense of community , supportive faculty , and a well-regarded engineering department. Many students appreciate the personal attention from professors and the emphasis on academic success, with several highlights on the accessible faculty who genuinely care about student learning. The campus itself is described as beautiful and easy to navigate , with a small-town feel that's complemented by a lively atmosphere of social events and student organizations. While some reviews mention issues like construction noise and parking challenges, the overall sentiment is positive about the balance of academics and student life, making Tennessee Tech an appealing choice for those seeking a more personal college experience. Additionally, the affordability of tuition relative to the quality of education is frequently praised, making it a great option for students interested in STEM fields and beyond.

reviews.text: Overall Tennessee Tech has been a great fit! The classes are pretty straightforward, and the teachers for the most part care about you and want to help you succeed.

I like Tech,but sometime I wish there were more women here it's like 60% male and 40% female. Also I wish the dorms were cheaper. also I'm art education and art department doesn't have any funding and our building has mold. Other than those things Tech is great I love how walkable to campus is.

I am a current Sophomore and Tech, and I have had a generally good experience. I am studying mechanical engineering here, and it is easily the biggest major on campus. If you are considering engineering, I would absolutely recommend Tech to you. I have had a few professors who have not been great, but the majority of them have been incredibly helpful and engaging. As far as cost goes, Tech is not extremely expensive, at least in terms of colleges, and the in-state tuition is easily manageable.

I think Tennessee Tech can provide a lot of good learning for many majors especially engineering and chemistry. This can not be said for my major. I am a biology major with a marine concentration. They have a lot of fun biology classes like animal behavior, ecology, and field botany to name a few. However, there are very few marine biology classes. They have the marine biology class which is available only in the spring every two years and 5 specific water-based biology classes that focus on freshwater. This is due to Tennessee being a land-locked state but this is the only marine biology degree in Tennessee so it would be nice if they could provide more salt water education. They do provide a lot of fun physical education classes like canoeing and Scuba diving though some do cost money. They are also continuously improving campus to make it nicer for students but this also has a drawback of frequent construction. The dorms aren't the best though.
I really like the climbing gym and gym facility at Tennessee Tech. I like the challenge of the academics but I would like to see the professors make themselves more available to students.

Tennessee Technological University is a college that gives you what you want. You get the knowledge you desire to learn with the professors who are eager to teach you, and the student life that is suitable for everyone. There are so many resources for everything you are looking for. From having fun to studying hard, Tennessee Tech has it all. I love how everyone can feel more connected because the school is not too big, but it is big enough to enjoy the college experience.

Tennessee Tech is overall a very nice college to attend if you are looking for anything in the engineering or math world. While the classes are a big tough the professors are usually very willing to help and there are many resources on campus to help students succeed.

I love all of my classes my professors but everything else Im just not feeling like Tennessee Tech is my home. I am on the TTU dance team so that takes up all my time. I just feel like it is just like my home town. Im really struggling here but I hope that as time goes on I settle and it does not bother me as much as it does now.
Enjoy Tech so much! Best decision I've ever made and happy to continue my education at this university.

Campus is very easy to navigate. Almost zero elevation changes too. Food is good. Classes are small. You can know your teachers on a first name basis. New Fitness center is First Class. There are a couple of newer dorms for guys, BUT the older dorms definitely need an upgrade.

TechExpress labelled the wrong days and times for some of my classes, and I had to email professors to verify the correct days and times. Tech built over the main parking lot and has not replaced the lot. There is a shortage of parking. Paying for a resident pass without receiving resident parking is flawed. I've had to accommodate the accommodation center since they require video calls for consideration. One of the staff gave me a one-word email when I asked if we're still meeting when they didn't send me a link for the video call and gave me the link 5 min late. I was given an advisor by Tech after admission, but they weren't in my TechExpress Advisor Page until after my first semester. I was notified that I haven't signed up for a payment plan for courses and would be "purged." I contacted the bursar's office and they rectified the issue. I have received multiple emails not intended for myself including emails meant for professors.
This is my second semester. More, character limit.

Tennessee Technological University has truly become my home away from home. From the moment I arrived, I felt welcomed into a community that values both learning and personal growth. I love how TTU blends a friendly small-town atmosphere with big opportunities that prepare students for the real world.
The professors genuinely care about their students’ success, and the College of Business has given me valuable hands-on experiences, leadership roles, and connections I’ll carry into my career. I also appreciate the many organizations and events that make campus life exciting and meaningful.
TTU has given me an outstanding education, lasting friendships, and memories I’ll treasure forever. I’m proud to be a Golden Eagle.

for my first ever on-campus college experience, I'm surprised I didn't find it out sooner. When I started Tech, I wasn't prepared to see how engaging it was, it felt like every day of the week there was a special event somewhere on campus, like performances in the music hall, food events in the cafeteria, etc. class-wise, I was astonished, very supportive, accommodating and intelligent teachers, each with their own style of teaching that were all very low-key and little to zero condescension. It's very easy to make friends there, I feel like I made more friendly impressions going for my bachelors degree than my associates degree. I haven't seen a sports event yet, but I can imagine when I do, it will be fun. they also have a super cool fitness center that's easy to enter with a student card. Housing situations can be a tad bit tough, but once you're in, it's not bad, as long as you keep things tidy, which I can do finely. Overall, almost phenomenal.

I will be a freshman at Tennessee Tech University in the Fall of 2025. I will be majoring in Music Performance on the Clarinet. I have participated in numerous activities at Tennessee Tech University while in high school and I look forward to studying and performing at Tennessee Tech University. I will be part of the Tennessee Tech University Marching Band this fall as well. I can't wait to play in the Clarinet choir and also perform with fellow students. When I graduate from Tennessee Tech University in 2029, I plan on performing professionally. I am hoping to be able to travel around the world and performing with different orchestras. I have always loved playing the clarinet and I look forward to seeing how far it will take me in life. Tennessee Tech University seems to have a great music program, and I am looking forward to seeing myself grow over the next few years. I look forward to building new relationships with fellow Tennessee Tech University classmates and professors.

I am so thankful to have been able to attend TTU for my undergraduate degree. I was able to grow so much through the academics and growth in my personal life through the guidance and teachings of those on staff here.

ai_summary: Texas A&M University - Kingsville (TAMUK) is often described as a welcoming, close-knit community where students can form meaningful relationships with peers and faculty. The university is noted for its small class sizes , which facilitate personalized attention from professors who are generally supportive and dedicated to student success, especially in popular programs like engineering and agriculture. However, some students mention limitations in extracurricular activities and local entertainment options, indicating that while campus life is vibrant with events, the surrounding town can feel quiet at times. Financial aid services are highlighted positively, aiding many students in overcoming tuition challenges. Overall, TAMUK provides a friendly atmosphere and a solid academic foundation, but there’s a desire for more amenities and activities nearby to enhance the student experience.

reviews.text: My experience as a student at this school has been very fulfilling for me. Being away from home has definitely helped me grow as a person and coming here as really helped me with that. Especially with having access to one on one with professors and help from my peers. One thing that could change though is the quality of the dorms (especially martin hall). Also, the conditions of the roads as well (especially dorm parking lots). Overall, my current experience while being at Kingsville has been good.

TAMU Kingsville is an exceptional university, especially if you prefer a smaller and more personal environment. The professors seem to genuinely care about their students and are willing to help when needed. The university is especially known for its engineering, agriculture, and music programs, offering solid opportunities for hands-on learning and career preparation.

My overall experience here at Tamuk is a pretty decent experience, the culture is strong and it seems to have a large history. The area here is always hot which isn’t my favorite but it’s definitely worth the drive, the weather is nice and chill and it’s definitely not busy at all. I drive a hour everyday to get here and I would recommend because the weather is so nice and there’s no traffic so it’s better.

I’m excited about Texas A&M University-Kingsville because it seems welcoming and has strong programs in my area of interest. I like that the campus looks friendly and supportive. I hope to see more information about internship opportunities and student resources once I start.
It was okay. It really depends on the professor. Some are hands on while others give you free rein. It’s a matter of preference really.

From registration alone I knew A&M was a well put together school. I loved the environment, the student body, the staff. Everyone is so easy going and willing to help anyone step out of their circle. There isn’t much I’d like to see change, they have events almost every day for the student body and really do everything they can to help out their students. I haven’t seen a university care so much and provide so many different ways for their students to have an easier n’ fun experience while working towards their degrees.

I love Texas A&M University-Kingsville because it provides a home setting. The professors like to get to know students and become mentors throughout your college experience! Student life is wonderful and full of orgs and religious groups so you can find your click.
I have really enjoyed my experience at Texas A&M University. I have nothing bad to say. The people and staff are very sweet and so nice and very helpful!

My overall experience with Texas A&M University Kingsville has been positive in many ways, The campus has a welcoming atmosphere, and most of my professors have genuinely cared about my success, which makes classes feel more supportive and engaging. I have also met some amazing people here and the smaller campus size helps everything feels more connected. Although there is still room for improvement in some aspects like more organization during busier times in the semester but for the most part it is a good place to grow academically and personally.

Overall very nice I’ve met so many new people and enjoying all the events this campus has to offer not to mention the geology department there are so many classes that are offered and have given me a lot of experience and taught me so much and I still have a lot more to learn.

I like how welcoming the kingsville campus is. All my professors are very nice and genuinely want to help you succeed. Furthermore, the campus is lovely and lively every single day. Changes I would like to see consists of fixing the dorms. There are many issues regarding them that should have already been fixed but haven’t.
I like how fun this campus is and how its so easy to get along with everyone and it feels so safe here.

With my time as a dual-credit student with TAMU-Kingsville, I grew my education while continuing to finish my GED. As I walk the graduation stage, I will have 27 credits thanks to TAMUK and their wonderful staff of professors and student-ran help centers. I would recommend for the price and the benefits you get from being with a school who cares about every single one of their students.

Not worth it. The food to the dorms to the parking terrible. Not good. My Lucio dorm room was full of roaches before I even moved and the Ac broke not ones but 3/4 times. My mini fridge smelled terrible and housing was zero help. When I first moved in my restroom light didn’t work and I kept asking and asking when they were going to fix it and everyone I asked used the same excuse which was “they had to cut back on maintenance” which shouldn’t be my issues despite have to pay so much for the dorm itself already.
The food is not Good.
The meal plans are not reasonable at all only $100 which will literally get you nothing you can spend that in less than a whole week.
My vehicle got hit and police did nothing I was the one that had to call them to get information when they were “suppose to call me back” what’s the point of buying a pass if your vehicle isn’t considered safe.
Overall my experience has not been the best.

If you’re looking to peruse a degree in agriculture personally TAMUK is the best for people who want a more directed education. The university isn’t as large at the college station one so it has a few benefits like you actually get to know your professors and they actually get to know you. It’s fairly close to the beach so that comes in handy with this Texas heat. There many diffrent sports and clubs we do have football which means you can still experience the college tailgate experience.

ai_summary: Texas A&M University offers a vibrant and welcoming environment characterized by strong traditions , a supportive community , and a rich academic experience. Many students appreciate the friendliness and helpfulness of their peers and professors, helping to foster a sense of belonging despite the university's large size. The academic rigor , particularly in fields like engineering and agriculture, is often highlighted, though some students note the challenges that come with large class sizes. Campus life is enhanced by numerous student organizations , athletic events, and community activities, creating opportunities for friendship and engagement. While many students express pride in the university's culture and strong alumni network, concerns about tuition affordability, transparency, and diversity are also noted. Overall, Texas A&M is viewed as a place that nurtures personal and academic growth, making it an appealing option for prospective students.

reviews.text: I've been liking my experience so far. I really struggled to make friends in high school, but here I was able to find a group of friends relatively quickly. (But maybe I just got lucky, since I'm in a relatively specialized major where statistically, there are a lot of people who will have similar interests and values to me.) My primary problem here has been with some professors who simply do not know how to teach the class I'm taking from them. Also, as a vegetarian, I find the food options on campus very hit or miss. But in general, I've been enjoying my college experience so far, and I'm excited to see what else is in store for me!
Texas A&M is a great University and campus. The people are friendly, the education is top tier, the food is good, and the culture is unmatched. I wouldn’t change a thing.

Loving studying here, the classes are great and the people are nice! Lots of opportunities for growth and self work, my only complaint would be that some of the teachers really don't teach that well, or explain what is on the tests well, but it's managable with good work ethic and communication.

Good professors and courses: wide range of classes within the animal sciences and ecology majors, majority of professors remain knowledgeable and teach subjects with enough understanding and translation for learning students
I enjoy college at A&M, I'm in a handful of organizations where I have made many of my closet friends. Classes are also very interesting.

oining the Texas A&M Class of 2030 has already been a transformative experience. From my first visit to College Station, I was struck by the unique sense of selfless service and the "Aggie Spirit" that permeates the campus. What I like most is the immediate sense of belonging; even as an incoming freshman, the Aggie Network made me feel like part of a lifelong family. Traditions like Silver Taps and the 12th Man aren't just events—they are a blueprint for character and loyalty that I am eager to uphold.
While the university’s deep-rooted traditions are its greatest strength, I would like to see an even greater push toward interdisciplinary innovation. As the world evolves, seeing more collaborative spaces where engineering, liberal arts, and business students can solve global challenges together would be incredible. I am ready to bring my work ethic to TAMU this Fall 2026 and contribute to this legacy of excellence. Gig 'em!

My time at Texas A&M University in College Station has honestly been incredible. The culture here is something special, and the traditions run deep and are the core of the school. Everyone carries themselves with pride and respect. Professors genuinely care and push us to be our best, while still being approachable. The people are kind, always willing to help others if in need. As well, everyone treats each other with the utmost respect. Making it so that there’s a real sense of community. Being part of different student organizations has made the campus feel like home. My first year here, I have participated in different soccer teams with many friends through the organized Intramural Sports. I’m proud to be an Aggie. Every day I’m reminded why this place means so much to so many of us. It was hard leaving home, but the people here and the environment have made it super easy to adapt and make College Station feel like a new home.

The Texas A&M University engineering program is going great so far. I am currently taking classes at TCC while also working toward becoming an engineer through the Texas A&M program. Balancing both has helped me stay focused and disciplined with my time. The classes are challenging, but they are preparing me for the skills and knowledge I will need in the engineering field. Being part of this program motivates me to work harder every day. My goal is to complete the program, earn my engineering degree, and build a strong career in the engineering industry.
Being an aggie is a amazing experience and there are many resources that help every student. I look forward to graduating from this school.

My overall experience with Texas A&M University has been formative and challenging in a positive way. As a first-generation student, I learned quickly how to navigate a large academic system, manage my time, and take ownership of my education. The structure and expectations pushed me to become more disciplined and independent.
Academically, Texas A&M helped me realize my potential once I had stability and access to resources. Outside the classroom, getting involved in student organizations and leadership roles allowed me to grow personally and professionally. At the same time, my experience clarified what I am looking for in my next step. Texas A&M gave me a strong foundation and showed me that I thrive in environments that challenge me and push me to aim higher.
Great community and school spirit, particularly during the fall semester. A welcoming atmosphere for people of different backgrounds!

Texas A&M University has been a wonderful experience. I have thoroughly enjoyed all my classes and professors. I have been involved in Zeta Tau Alpha and have been a part of various orgs and other service projects on campus, all that have positively impacted my experience.
I am a freshman in my second semester and I really love the school so far. Most professors are very nice and passionate about the subjects they teach. Lots of fun clubs and activities on campus.

Texas A&M University at first was overwhelming and big and scary, but they ended up being the most welcoming and accommodating. They have a place for everyone and they have resources for anyone. It is only my first year here and I am very happy already.

Texas A&M offers a lively community and a fantastic spirit of comradery. The teachers and students alike are all kind, caring, and helpful. Overall Texas A&M has given me the best experiences of my life.

ai_summary: Texas A&M University - Corpus Christi (TAMUCC) offers a unique college experience characterized by its beautiful island location and supportive community atmosphere . Students appreciate the friendly and helpful staff and professors, many of whom are dedicated to their academic success. The campus is noted for its accessibility and manageable size, allowing for personalized interactions in classes that are generally smaller. However, critiques frequently mention issues with parking, dining options, and the need for improved student services. While academics and student activities are positively highlighted, some students express a desire for a more vibrant social scene and greater athletic promotion . Overall, TAMUCC stands out for its scenic environment, community spirit, and commitment to student support.

reviews.text: Housing sucks. This is the worst housing I’ve seen from a university. They talk a big game at orientation and when you’re applying but it’s actually awful. Some professors are okay and the classes are pretty standard. The campus is walkable which is perfect. The people here are kinda iffy though, it’s definitely got some college stereotype to it. The school isn’t that bad. Housing is the worst thing about this school. And if they tell you stuff at orientation, 7 times out of 10 it’s probably not true or it’s exaggerated to make it seem like this school is so perfect. Did I mention that housing sucks?
It was a rocky start but all in all it has been a great experience through out my several years of schooling.
I'm currently in high school ,but I plan on attending Texas A&M University - Corpus Christi. So far from what I have seen the school has had all I can ask for in a school.

My experience at Texas A&M University–Corpus Christi has been genuinely rewarding. The campus being right by the water makes it a peaceful place to study, and it really adds to the overall atmosphere. Most of my professors have been supportive and easy to talk to, and they take the time to help when you need it. I’ve appreciated the smaller class sizes because it’s easier to ask questions and actually get to know people. There’s a strong sense of community, and I’ve felt welcomed and supported throughout my time here. Between academics, campus activities, and career resources, I’ve grown a lot both personally and professionally.
My experience has been great with understanding professional professors and the school offers great amenities.

I went to tour the University and I loved it. I liked how it wasn't to big of a University, but to small. I also liked the people I met there, they were super nice and understanding. I liked all of the actives they had for everyone to do and different clubs to join if you wanted to.

When I visited the Corpus Christi
campus, I was welcomed by an
incredible staff whose warmth
and positivity made me feel truly
at home. Their excitement in
welcoming new and upcoming
Islanders left a lasting impression on me. I have dreamed of attending this university for a long time, and being the first in my family to have the opportunity to leave San Antonio and pursue higher education is something I am deeply proud of. l am
extremely grateful for my family's unwavering support and encouragement, especially for motivating me to keep pushing forward even during the toughest moments.
An amazing college to go to fresh out of high school. Has many events to go to no matter what a person likes. The on campus gym is really good for the size of the school.
It wasn't my personally taste campus is small and so is campus life. The campus is really pretty but it can get boring. The professors I've had are great with a few exceptions.

My time in Texas A&M University - Corpus Christi is an exceptional experience. I enjoy the friendly atmosphere and the low tuition costs, but I love the communication and media arts programs the most, because it's all hands-on, and you'll be taught with experienced professionals. The one thing that could be changed would be they need to modernized their buildings, some of them look outdated in comparison to the new Arts and Media Building that's currently in construction just to support the creative programs the school provides.
It was amazing, I love the private beach and the palm trees. Would attend again, to continue my education.
I have only been at this university for a semester but I have had a good experience so far. The online teachers are excellent, they have been attentive, professional, and very kind!
Overall, it's okay, living off campus was the best decision I made, so of the professor has a langue barrier but other than that everything is ok.
PEOPLE ARE SO NICE I LOVE IT HERE WISH I COULD STAY HERE FOREVER I LOVE THE PROFESSERS, CAMPUS LIFE, CLASSES, CLASSMATES EVERYTHING IS AMAZING. sorry left it in caps lock :)

My time at Texas A&M University–Corpus Christi has been a meaningful chapter in my life, filled with growth, learning, and self-discovery. TAMUCC gave me the chance to develop academically through challenging coursework and dedicated professors who encouraged me to think critically and push myself further. I also learned important life lessons about resilience, time management, and balance, especially when facing personal challenges and responsibilities. Beyond the classroom, the Island campus environment and the sense of community made me feel supported and inspired, reminding me that education is about both knowledge and experience. The friendships I built and the opportunities I received here will always remain important to me. Still, I know that my journey continues beyond TAMUCC. I feel called to seek new challenges and opportunities at another institution, where I can expand on the strong foundation this university has given me and continue pursuing my full potential.

ai_summary: At Texas Christian University, the academic environment is generally seen as supportive, with professors who care about student success and smaller class sizes that foster personal connections. The campus is described as beautiful and vibrant, with plenty of on-campus events and a strong sense of school spirit , particularly during football season. While many students appreciate the welcoming community and the resources available for career development, there are concerns regarding tuition costs and a lack of diversity on campus. Greek life heavily influences the social scene, creating a sense of exclusivity for some students. Overall, TCU offers a comprehensive college experience, but it may not be ideal for everyone, particularly those seeking a more diverse or academically rigorous environment.

reviews.text: TCU is a great school to attend, especially as an undergrad. While it is a smaller campus, the spirit is huge, and you will have the opportunity to meet many wonderful people!

I really enjoyed my experience at TCU, mostly because I worked for the tennis teams. I recommend to anyone to look into working in athletics because it makes the college experience so much more fun. I didn't rush a sorority and don't regret it at all, but that's because I was so busy with my work for the tennis teams. I also play club tennis, which I really enjoyed. TCU offers a lot of clubs that students can be involved in and most of them are great communities to be part of. The forms my freshman year could have been nicer, but since TCU is expanding they are getting nicer. The newer dorms like Hill and Walsh are very nice. Something I loved about campus is that you could pretty much walk anywhere on campus, minus athletics, within 25 minutes. The parking situation is bad, but that's how it is with most universities. Sports games are super fun to attend and as students, you can get into any game for free with your student ID.

I would love to see TCU implement more diversity and less of a focus on Greek life. My undergrad experience was very one-note and I wish I had been exposed to more and learned more about the real world while I was there.

As a student athlete living on campus, I have had a great experience at TCU so far. There were plenty of activities that welcomed me to campus and helped me connect with other students. In addition, the athletics facilities are incredible. My professors have been very accomadating and allowed me to adjust to balancing academics and athletics at a collegiate level. Overall, I find the campus community friendly and I have felt at home since moving in on the first day.
As a freshman at Texas Christian University I am hoping to have a better experience in the coming years and have a great outlook on it when it is all over.

My experience at TCU has been awesome. It is truly such a joy EVERYTIME I go back from breaks. Fort Worth has really grown on me and it feels like home now. The dorms are actually really nice mostly because the majority are relatively new. The food is not bad, we are constantly building new dining halls and new dorms for incoming students so definitely something to look forward to. All of my professors have been awesome and really want to see me grow in my education which is great. Overall the university is so great, I can’t recommend it enough.
Great education but greek is very big so just be aware of that if you’re not interested in joining. Very caring professors and enjoyable campus with lots of stuff walking distance.
it’s okay very hard to fit in if you aren’t rich. I find it very difficult to fit in but the professors are very nice and very knowledgeable

Cost is the biggest problem with TCU. In the four years I've been here the tution has increased by 10K. TCU was already unaffordable and now has become out of reach for all but the 1% and those who have access to a significant amount of need-based aid and merit-based aid. That said if the price is not an issue, TCU does have alot to offer.
Campus - The campus is gorgeous and very safe. Most of the dorms are great too.
Social Life - On campus there is always events going on. There is a vibrant party scene but only if you are apart of Greek Life. Outside of campus, Fort Worth does have many activities and Dallas is only an hour away.
Academics - I can only speak for the nursing program but it is world-class. The professors are great and I feel extremely prepared for the real-world. The career and academic advising services have been extremely helpful.
Overall, I would not attend TCU at the current price. Only Harvard may be worth that much but I enjoyed my time here.

TCU is a mixed bag. On one hand, its student life, athletics, and campus environment are amazing, if that is something that a prospective student cares about. On the other, it has a severe deficiency in its academics. This is a two-pronged issue: First, the general student body lacks any academic interest. There is a severe lack of curiosity and willingness to learn across the majority of the enrolled students, and it shows in the dreadful student in-class discussions, pathetic discussion posts, and the quality of the final academic product. Second, classes are too easy, require insignificant amounts of work, and there is no real demand for productivity or discipline. Frankly, this university does not prepare its common student for a postgraduate-level education, instead, it stunts the development of its alumni. This is not meant to be too harsh of a criticism of the university, although it is. There is also the occasional event where students are shot at in crises around campus.

As a student at Texas Christian University, I’ve really come to appreciate how strong the sense of community is here. From my first week on campus, people went out of their way to make me feel included. The professors are supportive and genuinely care about helping students succeed, both academically and personally. Classes are challenging but engaging, and I’ve been able to build real connections with my classmates and instructors. There’s also a great balance between academics and campus life—whether it’s football games, campus events, or volunteering opportunities, there’s always something happening that brings everyone together. I’ve grown a lot since coming to TCU, not just in what I’ve learned but in my confidence and independence. Being a Horned Frog means being part of a community that values leadership, service, and school pride, and I’m proud to call this place home.
TCU supports, racism, discrimination, and people who are racist and discriminating. I do not recommend this school to anyone unless you’re a a white Christian.

One of the most unique college experiences in the nation. Small campus that's very easy to get around. Housing and rising tuition rates are very frustrating, but the student experience is unmatched to other universities around the country.
I think the dining situation needs to be fixed, which TCU is working towards cause of the new dining hall they opened. But the food is just so gross sometimes. Maybe switch vendors.
TCU is the happiest place on earth! The school always has something going on and to be a part of. A lively school that feels like home.

ai_summary: At Texas Southern University, students often highlight the strong sense of community and the welcoming atmosphere , making it feel like a second home. The campus life is vibrant, with numerous clubs and activities that encourage student involvement. Academic support from professors is frequently mentioned positively, with many praising their approachability and dedication to student success. However, challenges such as inconsistent communication from administration, issues with housing, and frustrations surrounding financial aid processes are common concerns among students. The university’s unique identity as a historically Black college offers a distinct experience compared to predominantly white institutions, providing an environment where students feel understood and supported. Overall, Texas Southern University is seen as a place for personal growth , cultural connection, and academic opportunity, although there are calls for improvements in certain areas.

reviews.text: My overall experience at Texas Southern University is going well so far. What l like about Texas southern is how they give you a lot of good resources & they have an amazing kinesiology program. I wouldn't like to see nothing to change.

Overall, my time at Texas Southern University has been a mix of great experiences and things I hope to see improve. I really enjoy the culture, the sense of belonging, and how easy it is to connect with people. Some professors go above and beyond to make sure we understand the material and feel supported. The campus events, organizations, and community spirit also make TSU feel like home.
On the other hand, I think TSU could definitely work on communication and efficiency. Things like financial aid, advising, and general administrative processes can be slow or confusing. Sometimes it takes multiple calls or visits just to get a simple answer. Updating campus systems and modernizing buildings would also make a big difference.
It’s Great honestly. I’m looking forward to my many more years here. It’s a blessing to be a part of this community.

Texas Southern was a great fit due to them helping me in my junior year with the summer program for a week, mentoring the juniors or seniors of the various expects of college life and as well counseling me on how to start getting good grades, learning study techniques as well as utilizing all the resources I would need to succeed. Now at a Freshman at Texas Southern,
My experience now is great and the expectations of professors is very well appreciated.

Texas Southern University (TSU) boasts an impressive aviation program that equips students with the skills and knowledge needed to excel in the dynamic field of aerospace. The program offers hands-on training combined with strong academic support, preparing students for various careers in aviation. TSU also has active organizations like OBAP (Organization of Black Aerospace Professionals), AAAE (American Association of Airport Executives), and Women’s Aviation, which provide excellent networking, leadership opportunities, and a supportive community for students. These groups play a vital role in fostering professional growth and diversity within the aviation industry. TSU’s commitment to aviation education makes it a great choice for aspiring pilots and aerospace professionals.

My experience at Texas Southern University has been mixed. I really appreciate the respectful professors, the vibrant HBCU culture, great clubs, sports, and campus life. However, it’s very difficult to reach financial aid or academic advisors, and there’s little support for scholarships, grants, or internships. The tuition feels overpriced, the payment plans are costly, and the surrounding neighborhood isn’t the safest. Overall, the culture and community are strong, but the school needs better student support services.

My experience was actually kind of hard. I liked the environment, diversity, different styles, friendly people, even rude people, or people with attitudes it was a pretty cool environment. I would like to see their professionalism and organization change. They are unprofessional and unorganized when it comes to registering for classes, refunds, and class schedules.

It's just been freshman week, and so far it has been more than I've imagined. Its a party everynight. absolutely no reason to be bored. Freshman is pretty hectic for those who don't want to be awake early, but everything else is cool! The food is definitely better than high school food, and the student life is fun.

for 3 years at this school there's been incidents like a couple months ago during the spring 2025 semester there was an "incident" involving the death of someone outside of campus. when that happened the schools safety protocol did help a lot and kept students away from where the incident happened. so overall even if it gets a bad reputation from outsiders my school does has its good moments and even great professors.

Attending an HBCU offers a unique sense of community, cultural pride, and tradition that’s often unmatched, with a strong emphasis on celebrating Black history, fostering leadership, and creating a supportive environment where students feel seen and valued. However, like any institution, there are areas for growth—some HBCUs face challenges with funding, which can impact campus facilities, technology updates, or the breadth of academic resources. Administrative processes, such as registration or financial aid, can sometimes be slow or complex, and expanding career services or internship pipelines could further strengthen student outcomes. The core strengths remain in the deep-rooted culture and support network, but continued investment in infrastructure, resources, and professional development opportunities could make the experience even stronger.
As a first-year transfer student, the staff has been my lifeline, providing invaluable support throughout this journey.

My experience has been both enjoyable and at times stressful at Texas Southern University. Since being a part of the track and cross country team has been so enjoyable because my teammates not only add competition but also amusement. The individuals on campus are kind and friendly, and the school atmosphere is welcoming. The housing situation, however, is one area that I think is in need of improvement. In spite of the fact that I know many HBCUs deal with similar issues, TSU's housing costs seem immoderate considering the conditions provided. For students who cannot afford it, requiring all freshmen to live on campus and all upperclassmen to live off campus can create financial difficulty for students. Regarding the cost of living, students came together to have a small protest to demonstrate how many students are impacted by this problem. Regardless of this, there are plenty of activities and events, as well as organizations to get involved in.

Texas Southern University has been a pretty good experience so far. I am only a college freshman that has only had to do admissions, registration and go to a few Texas Southern freshman welcoming events. It has been seamless to say the least. I am already so proud to be accepted into this school and can't wait to attend their Law School in the future.

I enjoy my time at Texas Southern University. Not only is it an HBCU I’m an honored to attend such a prestigious institution. The staff are excellent when it comes to reaching out to them. The professors are very helpful and knowledgeable. Being a single mom at the age of 40 I am glad to have the experience necessary that one of my children made the decision to attend as well. TSU is dedicated to mine as well as others success and that lets me know that I’m doing the right thing.

My experience at Texas Southern University has been one I’ll never forget. From the moment I stepped on campus, I felt a strong sense of community and culture. TSU gave me the opportunity to grow both personally and professionally. The professors really care about your success and are always willing to help if you show effort. I also met some of the most genuine people here friends who turned into family.
One of the things I appreciated the most was how the school embraces its history and continues to push for excellence in everything from academics to campus life. There’s a lot of pride at TSU, and it shows in the way students support each other.

ai_summary: At Texas State University, a vibrant campus life stands out, with a scenic environment and a strong sense of community. Many students appreciate the supportive and approachable professors who foster engaging learning experiences , contributing to a welcoming atmosphere that encourages personal growth and connection. While the academic programs are often praised for their variety and rigor, concerns about dining options, parking difficulties, and housing availability were common among reviews. The campus offers a good mix of extracurricular activities, but some students noted a desire for improvements in academic advising and resources for challenging classes. Overall, students seem to value the beauty of the campus and the diverse opportunities available, fostering a unique college experience that feels both inclusive and enriching.

reviews.text: My experience at Texas State University has been both rewarding and challenging. I’ve grown academically and personally while balancing school and work. I appreciate the supportive professors and the welcoming atmosphere in San Marcos, which makes campus life enjoyable. However, I would like to see improvements in access to financial resources and more flexibility for working students, such as additional online course options. Overall, Texas State has provided me with valuable opportunities, and with a few changes, it could better support students managing multiple responsibilities while pursuing their education.
Beautiful campus with lots of opportunities to meet new people and get involved in the community. Small town with not much to do outside of campus. Lots of options for food.
Nrhwtjtntnsnjstntnsnsntwjwjtwtjwtjwjwtjwtjwtjwwkyymtssgmsgmstmtwmtmwymwtmwtmwtmwtmwymwtwtnwymwmwkywky

Overall, I have had a pleasant experience since transferring to Texas State University. The staff is so helpful in helping students with any situations they may be dealing with. The overall environment is incredibly welcoming.

I love Texas State Univeristy and it's student life. The people there are very welcoming and very nice. They always want to help someone out. There not much that I can say to change. The dorms wer ethe only thing that maybe needs some improvement , but overall the campus and town is amazing.

I am on my second semester as a freshman. Due to financial issues I was unable to live in a dorm or student housing. I live off campus with my father with hopes of saving up and working while in school to be able to move to San Marcos to gain the experience. So far, I have loved Texas State University because of the professors, study material, diversity, and the people. I have not had any issues here and I am very excited to continue my bachelors degree pathway to become a Nurse.

My overall experience I'd say its been ok. I had a knee injury last year and frankly there wasn't a lot of accommodation on their behalf even though the campus is really hard to navigate around when you're wearing crutches (lots of hills and lots of stairs). The advisors are really hard to get a hold off, and their tutoring hours are not always very time accessible for some. The professors have been pretty good. Not a fan of the parking situation either.

I’m a third year and I’d say I’ve had a generally positive experience with Texas state. There is always things going on on campus to get involved with. The party life is fun, there’s always events happening. I’d like to see some change with the administration however.

I love the campus in San Marcos, it is very peaceful and beautiful.
It is not easy to meet people, so better you join clubs.
It is fun live in San Marcos. I can go with my friends to Austin.

I went to Texas State to visit as an incoming freshman. Everyone was super nice, a very diverse group of people, and tons of student resources. Everyone just seems very eager to help you (the student) succeed. Campus is very pretty and every went to the Texas State football game! Everything seems very clean and cool!!

I have not been an outright student at Texas State, but I have had friends and family attend it. I have also been on campus and been to a lot of events held at or near the Texas State campus and community.
it was very good and i enjoy my time here meeting new people and making new friends and connections it really changed my life
The best part of my experience at Texas State was the professors. They were welcoming, inclusive, helpful, and always pushing you to do your best.

My first semester at Texas State went pretty good. One of the good things about the university, is that they are very welcoming to students. They have hundreds of events and organizations that anyone can be apart of. The square is walking distance from campus so that also helps with going out and exploring. Some things I would change about campus would be the parking. For example, 3 dorms with probably a thousand students share one parking garage. Its not really fair or equal and I do not think it should be expected that freshman just leave their cars at home. Overally though, the community is amazing and I can not wait to spend more time out here.
My experience with Texas State University has been pretty good overall it has a good campus and a nice small town with many things to do with good restaurants.

ai_summary: At Texas Tech University, students generally appreciate the welcoming and friendly campus community , often remarking on the strong school spirit and sense of belonging among peers. The beautiful campus and involvement opportunities, such as clubs and events, are highlighted positively. Academically, while many professors are commended for their dedication and expertise , feedback is mixed regarding course difficulty, particularly in engineering and introductory subjects. Some students feel that the variety of resources is beneficial, though there are calls for better awareness of mental health and academic support services. Overall, students enjoy their time at Texas Tech, finding a balance between rigorous academics and a vibrant social life , while some express concerns regarding diversity and resource accessibility.

reviews.text: Texas tech is an amazing school; there is so many different people there with their own stories. No matter where you're at you can fit in anywhere.
Overall welcoming people. Professor quality depends a lot on major but have had more good professors than bad. Dorms are fairly average but not disgusting and food is about the same.

Texas Tech was a great experience for me when it came to the "college experience". I think I could have made it even better if I were to have done more research on what I wanted to pursue as a career but everything you could ever need was there.
Texas Tech is a great University! They have an exciting football team that brings all the students together.

I’m having a great experience so far. Texas Tech has all the things I need in a college. Great classes and professors, nice dorms, and yummy food. The campus is spread quite far apart but most walks are no longer than 20 minutes! I love this school!

I took a chance and came to a college 8 hours away while my entire class went to the college 45 minutes away. But I can honestly say I feel so welcome and taken care of here at Texas Tech. The environment is so fun and all of the staff I've encountered has been so kind and helpful. When colleges say their main goal is for you to succeed Texas Tech actually enforces that!

Tech is a great school. Some find it difficult being so far away from major metro areas but that is what helps students to learn independence. The professors genuinely care about their students success and do everything possible to help them learn. Some housing dorms need renovations but if you get accepted early enough you will be able to pick the right dorm for you! Food is good & theres 2 Chick Fil A’s that are always busy. If you’re considering at least take the time for a campus visit - it’s worth the effort.

Texas Tech is truly my home away from home, a place where I feel safe, supported, and acknowledged. Even though I live more than six hundred miles away in a completely different state, Tech makes me feel like I belong. From the moment I arrived, I was surrounded by people who truly want to see me succeed and who continue to push me to become the best student and person I can be. The resources available both academically and socially, are incredible. No matter what I need, there is always someone willing to help and encourage me. The environment at Texas Tech is unlike anywhere else, it’s a large campus with a small-town feel, where everyone is welcoming and kind. Choosing Texas Tech was one of, if not the best decision I’ve ever made, and I would choose it again a million times over. It isn’t just where I go to school, it’s where I found my second home.
I am a second-year student at Texas Tech University and I have loved attending this school so far. I have enjoyed all aspects from classes to social events.

I think that Texas tech is a great place for people to continue their post-high school education. It has a great social scene, understanding professors, and a nice environment for students to focus on studying.

I personally loved my time at Texas Tech because they offered so many opportunities for a variety of students. A big thing for me is I wasn't interested in Greek life, so being able to find alternate organizations and connect with others was extremely important to me. I found just that at Texas Tech!
I have loved every single second since I started my first class with TTU. Everyone has been amazing and go red raiders!!!

Just started this year at Tech. My experience so far has been good, but definitely an adjustment from living in DFW. I'm a mechanical engineering major and they start you off right away with ME classes, which is good. The professors are really hit or miss. I'm learning that you really have to register asap to get in the classes with the favorite professors.
The school is super supportive of helping make sure you do well and offer different modes of tutoring, mentoring, a success specialist, life coaching and other resources. Football this season is awesome. The campus is pretty big, but you won't find too much to do off campus, except the couple of restaurants nearby.
Overall, I'm enjoying my experience at Tech and glad I chose this school.

There were small communities that you could find on campus that made the college experience better, you will find your people. Definitely went through every season at the school. I would like to see the alumni check in with recent grads more.
My experience with the school is just starting, as I will be enrolled full time as an online student. Everyone thus far has been very attentive and supportive.

ai_summary: Texas Wesleyan University offers a unique experience due to its focus on smaller class sizes , which allow students to form meaningful relationships with professors and receive personalized attention in their studies. Many reviews highlight the supportive faculty and engaging academic environment , emphasizing that professors genuinely care about students' success. The campus has a friendly and inclusive atmosphere , making it easier for students to connect with peers and participate in various activities and events. However, concerns have been raised about the surrounding area, campus facilities, and the quality of food options, with some reviewers noting the neighborhood's safety issues and the need for improvements in dorm living conditions. While many appreciate the available resources, some feel the school could enhance student life and responsiveness to student feedback regarding events and services. Overall, Texas Wesleyan is viewed as a solid choice for those seeking a close-knit community and individual attention in their academic pursuits.

reviews.text: I haven’t been to Texas Wesleyan but I had. A tour and got to see for my self what it looks like and the campus is small which I can see as a benefit especially if you don’t want to be in a class that has a lot of people and have different buildings for everything
Texas Wesleyan is a beautiful university, holding a lot of diversity and meaningful connections. The atmosphere is just great.
I like everything about Txwes they could lower the prices though for people who are putting two kids through the same university.

This is my third year in TXWES, I have to confess that the first two years my involvement was minimum. But this year I committed to becoming more involved. I have to say, that my experience when it comes in the classroom, has been one of the best. I love the smaller smatter, the close interaction that I can have with my professors. I think what I would change has to do more with the events clubs have. I wish there was more of a community for the students, but I also understand that as a student we also have to have that initiative.

I've transferred to this school, and it feels like everyone is very friendly. The campus itself is easy to navigate and they keep things updated so it doesn't feel like an old school, which it really is.

I really like the smaller class sizes and how easy it is to talk to professors. The campus feels safe and friendly, and there's a strong sense of community. Some of the buildings need renovations and I wish that the administration was a little more organized. Still, it's a great university for students who want personal attention.

As I navigate my junior year at Texas Wesleyan, I can genuinely say my college experience has been fairly well. I appreciate the distinct atomosphere this smaller university offers. making friends has been an interesting aspect of college life I've definitely connected with some great people in my classes, which has been fantastic. However, I can completely understand why others, even my self at times might struggle a bit in this area. since I can say including my self after classes, my default mode often involves heading straight to the library for some focused study time or simply going straight home. That tendency to isolate can make it harder to branch out. However, the campus size fosters a sense of less overwhelming and it's easier to recognize faces and feel like you belong. Another significant benefit that truly elevates my experience is the ability to cultivate meaningful student-professor relationships. Because of the consequently small class sizes.
Texas Wesleyan is a campus that is filled with diversity and is has made me feel more than at home since I have been there.
I really like Texas Wesleyan and its smaller smarter policy. It’s great for getting to know teachers and other students but not great if you want to blend in the back and try and do nothing.

I have been able to grow a lot, specifically as an individual. While there are many flaws in the culture there, the learning aspect is not bad. It provides a lot of opportunities and allows for more closer relations with students and staff.

I've had nothing but the best experience at Texas Wes. The best part about this university and you might here it everywhere but it's the class sizes. The reason they are known for "Smaller.Smarter" is because the class size is small enough for your professors to get to know you one on one and really focus on your academic needs. The professors here are nice and all they want for you is to succeed.

Since I’ve been here at Texas Wesleyan I’ve seen more things grow faster than other schools professors are really involved with students and want to pass successfully. The housing and food is great financial office and library are the best they help with betting you need to know about Texas Wesleyan.

So far my experience at Texas Wesleyan University has been great. There are many resources I can use to improve my academics. Everyone here is so welcoming and makes me feel like I belong. There is almost always something to do on campus which is fun to do with your friends.
The entire process of applying, registering and starting my undergrad as a transfer student has been a seamless transition, even after all these years of being out of school! Excited to start!
When I first started college I didn’t know what to do. I didn’t know why I wanted to go to college I didn’t have a plan or nothing.

ai_summary: At Texas Woman's University (TWU), students often appreciate the smaller campus environment , which fosters a close-knit community and enables easier connections with peers and professors. Many reviews highlight the supportive faculty and personalized attention afforded to students, contributing to a positive academic experience. However, some express challenges with parking and limited dining variety. The campus offers a blend of academic programs, especially in nursing, education, and health sciences, with a strong emphasis on empowering women. While students generally feel safe and welcomed, there are calls for more vibrant campus life , as some find the atmosphere quieter on weekends and suggest increased student engagement opportunities . Overall, TWU is seen as a solid choice for those seeking a supportive educational environment where they can develop both academically and personally.

reviews.text: I highly reccomend this campus for those who do not seek the true college experience. We have no football team and the campus is walkable as it is not that large. Campus remains quiet and calm for about 90% of the time and offers good scenery. I like the events they host at the start of every fall semester where they have vendors and activities for the students; free merchandise and food!

My experience at TWU has been great. I am in a very supportive program with nursing. We are given so many opportunities to get hands on experience in healthcare. For example, I have received a certification in Stop the Bleed. The instructors are very helpful and encourage for questions. It feels like they truly want the best for us students. There are also many groups and organizations to join. Out of experiences so far, I have not been able to make a lot of friends through classes, but through organizations I have been able to make a lot of friends and a lot of connections. One thing I would like to see changes in is the dining hall. There have been issues with uncooked food and no change has been made. Overall, this campus checks out all my wants in my ideal school. I love the small campus, that it is predominantly women and is very supportive.
Great campus, would love to be more involved but due to my current employment, I am limited to the student life.
So far, I have had a great experience. The housing and food are decent, and the professors, for the most part, are very helpful and welcoming. The coursework is challenging but very good.

My overall experience at Texas Woman’s University has been positive. It is definitely not a party school, which makes it feel more quiet and peaceful compared to larger universities. The campus is smaller, so most classes are within walking distance. There aren’t campus buses to take you to class, but that’s because everything is close together. Many students commute or go home on weekends, so campus can feel calm. It’s located in a small town with a few things to do, which is nice if you prefer a relaxed environment.

My overall experience at TWU has been great. I have joined the student union association, the black student council and the Korean dance club. My professors have all been kind to me and they respond quick to emails. I have nothing to complain about!

I have not had a bad experience the only thing I am having trouble with is communication with some of the staff members. I am excited about finishing school and I hope that the staff treats others with respect and kindness.

I love how pretty the campus is and that Texas Woman's University is known as the campus with a heart. It is such a welcoming community here with many resources in academics and health. I just wish there was more life on campus. Often, on the weekends the campus becomes very dry because most students go home.
So far my experience has been amazing I just wish the food was better and we could have cookware, but academics opportunities are amazing

First off, as a first-generation student, it was hard experiencing college life for the first time. I had to go through almost everything alone. But the persistent and determined student I am fought hard and everyday to be where I am. I am now a 4.0 student with all the thanks to many help from TWU's administration who were able to guide me through. I am just so in awe of the campus and its life; I couldn't be more thankful. I have truly enjoyed being in its community every semester.

I am a first time student at Texas Woman's University, So far I am impressed by my professors attention to detail in classes and most of them have a genuine compassion for teaching, which makes the classes more interesting. The dorms are a little chaotic, I would say there's a lack on expectations when it comes to noise and restroom etiquette, but the provided rooms are more than enough to live out of for a semester. The dining hall food is not very accommodating to the Gluten Free community, I struggle to find a variety in foods, the only guaranteed gluten free thing they will have in the line everyday is Pizza. The campus life isn't crazy partying, which I think is good, it puts more focus on the academic part of schooling. The campus feels very safe with patrol officers working 24/7 and also available and willing to help at anytime.

Great campus, even for me who was primary online. I still went to campus for event like Drama night or game night, sometimes even outdoor movie night. They always had engaging activities for student. Academics was great, professors really engaging and accessible.

My experience at Texas Woman's University was nothing short of extraordinary! From the moment I stepped into the lively student union as a wide-eyed freshman, to the exhilarating day I proudly received my diploma, each moment felt like a beautifully unfolding dream. I forged deep, lifelong friendships with incredible individuals and learned from passionate professors who inspired me to embrace a love for learning and strive for excellence.
The campus itself was a picturesque haven, radiating warmth and beauty, while the surrounding neighborhood offered a vibrant and inviting atmosphere that felt like home. This nurturing environment contributed to a sense of safety and belonging. My profound love for TWU was so compelling that it inspired both my husband and son to embark on their own journeys there and ultimately graduate from this remarkable institution. Reflecting on my time at Texas Woman's University, I realize it was a truly transformative experience.

My experience as a Texas woman has been both rewarding and empowering. The resilience and strong sense of community I have witnessed here continue to inspire me. I value the diversity of perspectives and opportunities available, as they have broadened my outlook and shaped my goals. At the same time, I believe there is still work to be done in achieving equal pay, expanding healthcare access, and increasing women’s representation in leadership roles. I am committed to contributing to these changes and using my education to help create a more equitable future.

Overall, I love TWU the teachers are very positive and try helping you succeed in class. The people are genuine and open to new interactions. The campus is beautiful, has a variety of options for food and their coffee shop make really good coffee's. TWU is a school that wants to help you get your education affordably.

ai_summary: The Catholic University of America is well-regarded for its academic offerings and strong community atmosphere . Students appreciate the diverse array of programs available, particularly in nursing, business, and the humanities, often citing helpful and approachable professors . The campus is beautiful, with a suburban feel amidst the urban backdrop of Washington, D.C., providing students with ample opportunities for internships and extracurricular activities. However, some reviews point to concerns about the dining options and costs, noting high tuition rates and inconsistent food quality. The admissions process receives positive mentions for clarity and responsiveness, while student life can feel limited, especially for those not deeply engaged in religious activities or athletics. Overall, students feel supported and connected but express a desire for more vibrant campus life and dining improvements.

reviews.text: Campus life in Catholic University of America is great! Although, I commute back and forth. Extra curriculers are fun and the people of the clubs are enthusiastic to welcome new people in. Courses are on point and professors do a good job of teaching what they know during lecture. The campus itself is pretty big there are many places you can walk to. There is a Trader Joe's, Chipotle, &pizza, and more. I also heard of a TexMex restuarant nearby. The basillica is right next to the university itself which is nice because I can walk in whenever I want and look at all the architecture. Overall the University is pretty good and recommend people to have a visit.

I love my time at Catholic. Being a Catholic I feel such a sense of peace there. Religious isn't really pushed and forced onto students, though freshmen do take a theology course, and it's more interesting instead of forcing students to be part in religious activities. My professors so far have been amazing and so have my peers. I live on campus and didn't know anybody there on the first day of school, so my roommates were randomly selected. We bonded easily and so did me and other people from other classes. I love it and I'm looking forward for the rest of my time there.

The breadth of the professors while there in various departments were stellar. They know their content, how to put it into practice and enlighten the learning experience. The religious aspect is present while attending a Catholic institution to help honor tradition.

This is such a great school, especially if you are willing to stay involved in your faith. So many resources from course based tutoring to counselling office, health center, campus ministry, etc. the professors here are also very committed to their teaching.
So far the experience that I have had at Catholic U has been quite enjoyable there is so me negative but the positive outweighs the negative.

Catholic University is a beautiful campus that is bustling when school first begins, and towards the warmer months of spring. As the weather gets colder, the campus life becomes dull. Due to the sports here not being a D1 level, there is not much of a school spirit around sporting events, even during football season. In terms of party life there are bars accessible via metro and a frat house that occasionally will throw parties. The academics vary based on major. As a current architecture student I would say academics in this particular area are strong and rigorous. Additionally, the professors under architecture are wonderful and understanding, as well as under the theology school. As for other professors I have had they are normal, some better than others. There are a lot of faith based events that run yearly here and campus ministry also holds events. Housing is not fantastic, both in process and outcome. Dining isn't great, but dishes vary enough for different food in the week.
