The Catholic University of America Conway School of Nursing helped me find fast job placement and laid the fundamentals for my professional practice.

I'm enjoying my college experience at Catholic U. I've met many new friends. My classes for my major are interesting, and I've been encouraged to either double-major in Mathematics, or make it my minor, by my counselor.

I like how personal the smaller classes are because you really get to meet your professors and get a connection out of them. The proffessors are there to help you navigate your future careers and I love that. What I don’t like about the Catholic university is the administration. It feels as though they work against you. It feels like their whole entire job is to make money off of students conduct.

Catholic University was an amazing school with a great musical theatre program when I was pursuing my undergraduate degree! The campus was beautiful and, with one or two exceptions, I had some of the most incredible professors. I learned so much more than just music when I was here! There is a cost to such a great school though, and that cost is six figures of student loan debt. If I could change anything at all about CUA, it would be the cost of attending. Although I absolutely loved it there and would have gladly done grad school there as well, there is simply no way that I could afford it. If you can make the financial end work, though, it's a great place to live and study! Being in the heart of Washington D.C. was a dream come true at the time, and there was no shortage of things to see and do.

Looking back on my time at CUA, I made wonderful friendships and had great mentors in my professors, but the University administration wasn't the best. My senior year, it was revealed that the University was $30M in debt. During the first week of the spring semester, the music school held an emergency meeting where we were informed that the music school would no longer exist next year due to the budget constraints. It ended up being incorporated into the School of Arts and Sciences, but the initial messaging was all very negative, confusing, and dismissive of the students who were understandably concerned about their School being closed.

So far, I have had an excellent experience at CUA. My professors are really good, I feel safe on campus, students are friendly, and there are lots of things to do. I like being on a smaller campus because class sizes are small and it's easy to get around campus. I also commute, so it's easy to get to and from school since the train is so close by. I would like to see more diversity on campus, but I understand that some religious colleges and universities may attract students of the same faith. I have attended catholic school before, so I feel really comfortable on campus.

I have loved my time at CUA! I wanted to attend a school that was in a city, but was still quiet and safe. Catholic University had the perfect balance between the two, and I am so glad that I made the choice to attend! I also play volleyball at CUA, which provides me with the perfect balance.

I like everything all people were nice and good teacher were awesome and I did like everything.Attending to catholic university it’s a great experience you get to meet new communities different ethnicities and it’s fantastic.
Its a very welcoming community, sometimes it can be very quiet and empty but many times full of people and activities.

ai_summary: The College of St. Scholastica offers a tight-knit community experience with a focus on student support and professor engagement, which many students appreciate. The small class sizes foster personal connections with faculty, who are generally described as caring and invested in students' success. While the academics are praised, especially in nursing and related programs, some reviews note inconsistencies in teaching quality across different courses. Students find the campus welcoming and involved in various activities, although some mention a cliquey atmosphere that may make it challenging to connect with peers arriving from outside the area. Additionally, the cost of tuition is often highlighted as a drawback, with mixed feelings about the value in relation to available diversity and course offerings. Despite these issues, many students express a strong sense of belonging and satisfaction with their overall experience, thanks to supportive faculty and a beautiful campus setting near Lake Superior.

reviews.text: Overall ok. The professors care a great deal about their students, and go above and beyond in helping them succeed.

I am returning to my pursuit of education after many years of just working. I transferred in credits from previous classes taken at a few community colleges and started this year as a sophomore. St. Scholastica has made online learning very accessible, I have particularly enjoyed my microeconomy class as well as the Professor who teaches that course.
I would like to see the mold in the older apartment buildings be taken care of because it becomes difficult to manage and can be harmful to breathe in the apartment buildings.

As an international student from Ghana, I am deeply grateful for the warm welcome and support I received from The College of St. Scholastica. From the moment I arrived, I felt embraced by a community that values diversity, kindness, and inclusion. The faculty, staff, and fellow students went out of their way to make me feel at home, helping me adjust to a new culture and academic environment. Whether it was through helpful orientation sessions, friendly conversations, or academic guidance, I never felt alone. The college’s commitment to creating a supportive atmosphere made a meaningful difference in my transition, and I am truly thankful for the friendships and opportunities I’ve found here. St. Scholastica will always hold a special place in my heart.
This is a great school that offers many scholarships and will match the University of Minnesota pricing. Small class sizes with great professors.
This school is great I love it. You will have so much fun here as there is so much to do and there is so many nice people with great professors and of course the athletes are good.

I love almost everything about St Scholastica; the luxurious dorm rooms, the good food, the small classes, the beautiful campuses….. I mean everything. I just suggest they offer more interms of scholarships and also their level of sporting. Overall St Scholastica is a 5 star. You won’t regret being there.

The College of Saint Scholastica is a great place to grow as a community, meet new friends, and participate in fun, non-party events. It's really easy to make new friends at any point in your college experience and people who go here are typically kind.

The College of St. Scholastica traditional BSN program was a great fit for me! The professors at this school were very invested in my learning journey and growth. They truly want you to be successful and have a passion for teaching. The facility was beautiful with great hands on opportunities to participate in simulation labs as well as a cadaver lab which is not common to have access to in an undergraduate program. Small class sizes allowed for effective relationships with professors and peers. I did not feel like a number at this school, but instead felt a part of a community of scholars.

I have had a rewarding experience at The College of St. Scholastica. As a chemistry major and student activities coordinator, I’ve found the college to be a great environment for both academic and personal growth. The small class sizes allow for close interaction with professors, which has been invaluable in my studies. Being in a supportive community has helped me thrive, and I’ve had the opportunity to engage in leadership roles through student activities.
The campus is beautiful, with a great balance of academic spaces and outdoor areas near Lake Superior, which makes it an ideal place to study and relax. Additionally, being involved in various initiatives, including tutoring and applying for programs like the UNICEF Youth Foresight Fellow, has allowed me to contribute to the college community and grow my professional skills.
The diverse and inclusive environment makes it easy to feel connected and supported in both academic and extracurricular activities.
The campus is so pretty with a not to do. There is really no place where you will be bored because there is always a ton of different activities to meet new people and just have fun!

My experience with The College of St. Scholastica was a good one. The professors were really nice and, I really enjoyed all of my classes. The only downside I would say is that, I didn’t appreciate that the theatre department was closed down because it was one the main reason I enjoyed being on campus as a commuter. The course load was decent and I many friends along the way.

So to cut things short and give my utmost sincere review I will say college of saint scholastica is the best school and also one of the schools with Good professors to shape your future for the better.

I have really been enjoying my time at St. Scholastica so far. However, there are some changes I would like to see. For instance, overnight parking is about a half-mile walk from my dorm, meanwhile there are several parking lots right outside my building. Additionally, there is no air conditioning in almost any of the buildings, and only RAs are allowed to have AC units.
Despite this, I really like my professors; they are all very enthusiastic about their work and clearly love their jobs, which makes learning that much better.

I love how the college is a very diverse college that has endless opportunities to be about in the community or study abroad options. The campus is also very open to the public which is nice because it opens you more to the community. The downfall is that Duluth is getting a little bad to live in as a college student with rent going up and safety wise.

ai_summary: The Master's University (TMU) presents itself as a unique place where a strong emphasis on a Christ-centered education is foundational to the overall college experience. Most reviews highlight the commitment to integrating biblical principles across all academic programs, with professors who are described as caring, knowledgeable, and deeply involved with their students. The smaller campus size contributes to a tight-knit community where students feel personally connected to faculty and peers. While the environment is generally reported as supportive and safe, some concerns arise about the handling of certain social dynamics, particularly regarding gender roles and inclusivity. Many students appreciate the spiritual growth fostered through regular chapel attendance and the intentional focus on faith, although some express a desire for a more diverse array of extracurricular activities. In terms of academics, TMU is recognized for its rigorous programs and helpful staff, although there are mixed opinions on the general education curriculum and certain aspects of student life, like dining options. Overall, TMU offers an experience that is deeply devoted to faith and academic excellence but may not be the best fit for everyone, especially those looking for a more liberal environment.

reviews.text: The campus is absolutely beautiful, with palm trees and fountains everywhere, beautiful stucco buildings, and the dorms are stinking adorable. One of the most beautiful campuses I've ever been to! They have an adorable coffee shop too, and the food is great!

I am admitted into the Master's University and after touring the campus I have very high hopes. The campus is beautiful and very clean. The community is absolutely wonderful and I admire the commitment and care shown from the professors.

I have absolutely loved my time at The Master's University! If you want to study with professors who genuinely care about your success in life and your personal growth, TMU is the place for you! If you're also looking for a university that prioritizes not just your academic success, but also your spiritual growth, then TMU is the best place for that! I cannot recommend it more highly. It is fantastic!

The devotion to the motto "For Christ and Scripture" is evident in almost everything around campus. It is apparent in the classroom, outside the classroom, through all the athletic activities, and in the lives of staff and professors.

When I came into TMU, I was the happiest person on earth and woke up every single morning grateful to be there. My freshman year was wonderful, and I did well in my classes. Then the sophomore year came around. I was put on these meds for a medical condition that I had that caused fatigue, inability to focus, and horrific episodes of psychosis. I was desperately trying to keep my grades up while working with my doctor to try to find a solution. I thought that a community focused on God's love would help me during this time, but they made life a nightmare for me. They sent me home and threatened to unenroll me during midterms. When I came back, they didnt let me take any of my midterms and made me withdraw from 3 classes. Then, they made me sign contract after contract, once again threatening to unenroll me. Once summer came, they gave me a list of things I had to do or I couldn't come back. Disgraceful. Unchristlike. It took a long time for me to even want to open my Bible after that

The Master's University was the greatest choice school of my life. To be surrounded by like-minded students who had a desire to love and know the Lord more through His Word has grown me in more way than one. TMU served as a launching pad for a life lived to the glory of God ink whatever career or vocation the Lord leads you. I may not know what the future brings, but I do know what it means to seek the Kingdom of God first in all things (Matt. 6:33) and that my life is meant to give glory to my Savior and Lord, Jesus Christ, who saved and loved me by dying on the Cross for my sins and rising on the third day that I may be saved (2 Cor. 15:3-4). I pray the same prayer of Jim Elliot: "Lord, let my life be prosperous. Not that I achieve high station, but that my life would be an exhibit to the value of knowing God."

This University is by far and away the best University in the world. There is no better place to study, learn, and experience college life than here. The environment is superb, the staff and professors are top-notch, and the student life provides a wonderful experience.

I loved the people there, even tho it was hard to get to know people especially in the spring when everyone has already made friends. Over all it was a great experience and I can’t wait to go back next year. I am trying to get enough money to live off campus so I can have a better connection with they students.

The goal of the school is great, but it creates an environment where fake personalities are prevalent. Professors are very close with students, which is great, and advisors help you stay on track. There is not a lot of resources for students on campus. The school, and the students as well, prioritizes being aesthetic and hip. Student/campus life is actual dog water with events being promoted 1-2 days before happening, and events are similar to 5th grade scholastic book fairs. Athletes are seen as king and many exceptions are made for them. With no real reputation you have to ask yourself...is it worth paying $56k for...
The Master's University is such an amazing place that is truly For Christ and Scripture. everything they do is a representation of Christ.

This university is an incredible experience for young Christians who desire to receive an education that is both practical and edifying. You are guaranteed to receive as much investment into your professional and spiritual journey as you put in.
I loved the focus on scripture and how that applies to the major I am studying (accounting). Accounting requires people who are full of integrity and honesty, which is directly tied to the scriptural focus that Masters has.
I would love to see the capacity for this school increase since more and more students are coming. There are currently around 3,200 students here so increasing capacity would benefit both the campus and the community around it.

The Master's University is a beautiful campus right outside the L.A. bowl. I attended one of the Bible Survey classes and chapel. The Professors take great care of Scripture and making sure that the students have a solid grasp in understanding God's Word. I loved getting to know the different students and attending several different events that The Master's University held, such as it's annual Christmas Concert. The Master's University is a great place to deepen one's relationship with Christ and other like-minded believers, and to not have to compromise to enjoy and have fun at university.
This school is great! Campus life is wonderful I suggest living on campus. The people are amazing and really friendly.
It was my only choice because of what they stand for spiritually, academically and socially. Here is where I came achieve the most growth personally and professionally and I want to stay here!

The Master's University is an excellent university that is focused on teaching and equipping students to pursue Christ and scripture. In my experience at the university, they do live up to the expectations they set and I have loved it there. The community is overall accepting and caring, the majority of the professors are excellent in their teaching and how they care for students, the campus is beautiful and safe, and many of the people I meet on campus seem to take their faith seriously and love the Lord.

ai_summary: The New School offers a distinctive educational experience that emphasizes creativity, diversity, and interdisciplinary learning. Many students appreciate the small class sizes , as this allows for close interactions with experienced professors who often bring real-world knowledge into the classroom. The location in New York City enhances the experience, providing ample cultural and professional opportunities . However, several reviews indicate issues with administrative processes, communication, and the high cost of tuition, which can lead to financial stress for students. While some find the flexible curriculum liberating, others express a desire for a stronger sense of community and social engagement. Overall, The New School is praised for its innovative approach but criticized for its administrative shortcomings and financial pressures.

reviews.text: I have been quite pleased with my education at The New School. It is only recently with the new changes that my experience has been less than stellar.

The New School is an amazing place, but also very volatile. Recently we have been dealing with issues in our financial department, and it does speak to the immaturity and lack of preparedness at the school. However, you do get to live in the thick of New York City, which is honestly worth it. New York is an amazing place, and I think most people will find the New School to be worthwhile.
The New School has decent resources with regards to academics if you know where to look but it takes a lot of personal advocacy to get the support you may need or want.

So far of being an international student, I like the diversity and the different cultures that makes up the school. I also enjoy their various events. I will like to see more support when it comes to the structure of some of their courses. It felt very unorganized for the number of students in my cohort

I have learned more at The New School about my craft (Jazz Piano) in four months than I have in my four years of playing Jazz piano. Being from Virginia, it was difficult to find other passionate people and teachers about jazz, but being at this school has exposed me to such great people, musicians and friends that I never would have been exposed to if I went anywhere else.

If I could give this school 0 stars I would. I attended this school with the hopes of a brilliant educations and promising future. I have been subjected to not only discrimination as a Hispanic American student but miscommunication on multiple simple issues I had with things during the entire education I had. Everyone had attitude issues and the admin were constantly an issue during my time and payment here. I wish I had a good college experience as many as people live to enjoy this time in their life, and yet it was one of the worst times in my life. Good riddance to this school and the administration who can’t seem to get their act together.

I enjoy being around other creatives and having the chance to learn more about their creative processes. It’s extremely enriching and despite the difference in creative fields, helps me to be a better artist generally.

I would highly recommend The New School to anyone who loves to be in community with other creative people and professors who are truly dedicated to their art practice while also being intellectually challenged.

I genuinely love being at the new school. It was my top number 1 school. My only issue is the affordability. I am struggling really badly to continue my studies here. I have now only just reached the beginning of my sophomore year.

Studying at The New School was a transformative experience. The university fosters creativity, critical thinking, and social engagement. Professors are passionate and often leaders in their fields, encouraging students to challenge norms and think independently. The diverse student body and interdisciplinary approach made collaboration dynamic and inspiring. Being in New York City added immense value, offering endless cultural and professional opportunities. The curriculum is flexible, allowing students to tailor their education to their goals. Overall, The New School empowered me to grow intellectually and artistically, and to approach the world with curiosity, purpose, and a progressive mindset.

Entering my first semester at The New School has been a breeze. The school information and resources have been vital to my performance and upcoming terms. Faculty and staff are always available when needed. Even though I attend classes online, I feel just as connected as a on campus student. I don’t feel I’m missing out on anything.
Good overall experience, student housing can be improved and campus life is a little lacking but good network.

I appreciate The New School for its innovative approach to learning and creating your own degree plans. I enjoyed my coursework. However, some professors were difficult to get ahold of and some were just downright not helpful. However there were many that contributed to a great learning environment.

My experience at The New School has been great overall. I’ve really enjoyed the creative and diverse community, and the interdisciplinary approach to learning has pushed me to think outside the box. It’s a place where you can really explore different ideas and collaborate with people from all backgrounds.
That said, there are a couple of areas for improvement. Some of the administrative processes can be slow, and it would be nice to see better communication about events and opportunities. Also, while the cafeteria staff does their best, I’d love to see more veggie and plant-based options on the menu—it can feel a bit limited at times.
Overall, it’s been a fantastic place for growth, and I’d recommend it to anyone looking for a dynamic, innovative environment.

I went to The New School for my 5-year undergraduate dual-degree program in Illustration and Literary Studies, with a minor in Psychology. Overall, I enjoyed my experience at TNS, but like everything in life, there are pros and cons to the university, and looking back, if I had to attend all over again, I would have done several things differently.
Pros:
- TNS has fantastic professors and classes who really challenged me intellectually.
- Greenwich Village and the local area around the university is safe and all the college buildings are close by.
- It's in NYC-- the capital of the world. I went to so many places and art museums for my classes!
- The student body is extremely diverse and international.
Cons:
- TNS is very expensive -- avoid student loans whenever possible!
- Pricey room and board (luckily I only commuted)
- Some departments are terribly underfunded and career services did not help me find a job after graduation.
- University administration could use improvement.

ai_summary: The Ohio State University offers a vibrant and diverse college experience marked by a strong sense of community and school spirit. Many students appreciate the welcoming atmosphere and supportive professors who are willing to help with academic challenges. Student life is lively, with numerous extracurricular activities, clubs, and a highly engaged social scene , especially centered around sports like football. However, some reviews mention that the university's size can make it feel overwhelming, and there are calls for improvements in diversity and mental health services. Tuition concerns are also noted, particularly for out-of-state students. Overall, the campus provides a wealth of resources and opportunities for personal and academic growth, making it a notable choice for prospective students.

reviews.text: Brilliant campus and student body. Can be intimidating with how large the student body is, but the camaraderie is strong. Off-campus can be dangerous, but police presence is fairly strong and common awareness goes a long way. Fraternity life was robust, as well as class and extracurricular options. Truly something for everybody if you’re willing to put yourself out there and find it. Ohioans are proud of our history and the culture we share with each other. An amazing place to be as a young adult, with Columbus as an amazing neighbor. Go Bucks, down with TTUN 🌰
OSU has a great reputation and wide variety of majors. By going there, you can receive any type of degree that you desire and learn from highly regarded professors.

My experience at The Ohio State University helped shape both my professional and personal growth as a nurse. The program provided a strong foundation in evidence-based practice, critical thinking, and patient-centered care, which prepared me well for nursing and well-rounded individual. I especially appreciated the knowledgeable faculty and the clinical opportunities that allowed me to gain hands-on experience in different healthcare settings. Those experiences helped build my confidence and strengthened my passion for caring for others.
If there is one area I would like to see expanded, it would be more exposure to specialized fields such as aesthetic medicine and other emerging areas of healthcare. Introducing students to a wider variety of career paths could help nurses discover unique ways to combine their artistic skills and interests.

I went for clarinet camp and it was such a divine experience. All of the students and professors, as well as the other campers were such a joy to be around I would definitely do it again in the future.

I loved going to Ohio State. I went through DV and SA situations and the University Housing, Title IX, and academic staff all were so helpful to me during this time. They connected me with resources, helped me figure out how to stay enrolled, and really worked with me academically to provide flexibility while I was struggling. I was also an RA here and I loved it, for the most part the RA staff and I were all laid back and had great relationships with our residents. OSU is such a strong community, especially on game days! The food is okay, not the best thing I've ever eaten but it's decent, and you get dining dollars you can use to order from regular restaurants around Columbus. Some of the older dorms might not be the prettiest, but they have their charms (your own bathroom, tend to be a more tight knit community, separated bedrooms and study/living room spaces, etc).
Ohio State is a very big school, with lots of activities and strong academics, but old dorms and housing.

I have been at Ohio State for 3 semesters now since transferring from Columbus State community college. I love how many resources Ohio State offers to us. Ohio State does a great job of making resources widely available and known for students. Ohio State has great gyms for working out and plenty of activities like climbing, intramural sports, clubs, etc. The campus is beautiful but is quite large. The dining halls have ok food, but compared to what I've heard from other schools, Ohio State's dining halls seem to be better than most. There is plenty of food on High Street as well for people who want something different to eat. The only thing that I don't love about Ohio State is that the school can feel very big at times. Even in my major classes (finance), I usually only know or recognize one or two people in each class. It is a great school if you are looking to party, be involved, and get a good education.

Overall my experience was excellent! I feel my education was a mixture of challenging and creative. I loved the campus life activities. I was very involved and felt their were multiple opportunities to grow and become a leader. I would highly recommend this university. The have first year experience programs and second year transformation programs

A large school, The Ohio State University is located within an urban area, the state capital Columbus, Ohio. This school has very good academic research and on-campus work opportunities along with a large variety of extra curricular activities and volunteer opportunities. Although the campus is very walkable, The Ohio State University also offers free bus transportation which offers easy access to the vibrant downtown. The professors took a vested interest in students and class sizes ranged from large seminar-type classes to more intimate small groups. There are a variety of on-campus dorms as well as many apartments that are within an easy walk to campus.

It gets loud during football season/games, as expected of a football college. The professors are really friendly, and helpful, especially in office hours. Most will reply quickly to emails as well, and I felt the education was good.

Ohio State is a great place for me to learn and study. It also a great support system for me and my friends to help each other when it comes to projects and classwork. Plus it's a fun place to hang around and walk around the campus too.

Osu has been an amazing place to grow both academically and personally. The professors genuinely care about student success and are always willing to help, which made challenging classes feel manageable. I have had access to great resources, supportive advisors, and plenty of opportunities to get involved on campus. Overall, my experience at Osu has prepared me well for the future and made me proud to be a student here.

My experience at the Ohio State university was very positive. Many of my professors were very nice and cared about getting you to pass and have very helpful office hours. There is decent diversity and the local area is very good.

I found that most of the time professors and students were nice. It was fairly easy to get set up with disability services. You do have to do it a few weeks in advance. Teachers were accommodating and were able to meet my needs.
On and off campus, there are many things to do. You can definitely find your next favorite sport or activity while you are a student. Be sure to get involved!

ai_summary: Penn State offers a vibrant and inclusive campus atmosphere , where students often find a strong sense of community and school spirit. Many reviews highlight the supportive faculty and a wide variety of courses, though experiences with professors can vary significantly, especially in large lecture classes. Students appreciate the extensive resources available, including extracurricular activities and career services, but some have expressed concerns over rising tuition costs and the challenges of navigating a large university setting. The campus itself is frequently described as beautiful and lively, with a notable emphasis on sports, particularly football, which contributes to a unique college experience . While most students enjoy the social aspects and opportunities to engage with diverse interests, some feel overwhelmed by the size of the school and the drinking culture. Overall, Penn State is viewed as a place that offers a fulfilling mix of academic and social experiences, appealing to a wide range of students.

reviews.text: Penn State is an amazing school, and I really enjoy my time here. I highly recommend coming here. It has everything you would need, and I love it!

I recently participated in a campus visit program at Penn State and was blown away by the scale of the university. We toured several major-specific buildings, dorms, and campus landmarks, ending with a great meal at one of the dining halls.
The facilities are really great, and the dorms offer a solid standard of living. If you’re looking for a "big school" feel with high-quality amenities and a bustling social atmosphere, Penn State delivers.

The Penn State World Campus experience is phenomenal because of the personalized, mentor-style attention the advisors provide each student. The quality of education is second to none and cannot be differentiated from actual in-person classes. I recommend Penn State World Campus to anyone seeking to start and complete their degree in a fully online environment.

My time at Penn State has been very eventful. I started at Penn State Berks where I was very involved on campus, I was the treasurer of the psychology club, and a member of the criminal justice club, mental health club, and undergraduate research club. I then transferred to Penn State Harrisburg for better academic opportunities as I switched my major to Criminology. Here I am living off-campus but I still attend campus events such as, movie nights, craft days, and field-related talks. I plan to go to Penn State Main Camous next year to complete my major.

I believe Penn state world campus academic program is five star education. Professors were great, course work was excellent. I would recommend this school to anyone especially working adults or stay at home parents.

Great Experience! Thoroughly enjoyed my professors and friends. It's a large school so it's essential to join clubs and organizations to create a tight knit group. There are a ton of opportunities to do so.

My time at Pennsylvania State University has honestly been exactly what I needed it to be. It’s a big school, and that can feel overwhelming at first, but once you find your people and your rhythm, it becomes a huge advantage. There’s always something happening, whether it’s a club event, a speaker, a game, or just a random Tuesday that turns into a great night with friends.
Academically, Penn State pushes you. Professors expect you to show up prepared and actually think. Some classes are tough, but in a good way. I’ve had courses that forced me to manage my time better, sharpen my problem-solving, and get comfortable asking questions when I didn’t understand something. The resources are there if you’re willing to use them.
Overall, Penn State has given me challenge, opportunity, and community all in one place. It’s not perfect, but if you’re willing to put yourself out there and work for what you want, it gives you a lot back.

I have spent most of my time at Penn State studying, cleaning, spending time with friends, and learning how to manage my time. I wish I could see more exposure of campus events and what else is offered around campus. I didn’t see much advertisement or exposure for my major itself. I think more people would want to come if they say how much more they offered.

Overall my experience with Penn State has been both exciting and challenging in the best way. The academic environment pushes me to think critically, especially in engineering and science courses, while collaborative projects have helped me grow as a problem solver and teammate. Beyond the classroom, campus life has given me opportunities to meet people from diverse backgrounds, join student organizations, and explore new interests. I have learned to manage my time, balance responsibilities, and become more independent. Penn State has helped me build confidence in my abilities and clarify my future academic and career goals for my professional development.

My Penn State experience has been shaped by a strong commitment to the agricultural community, leadership, and public service. As a first-generation student majoring in Animal Science with a minor in Equine Science, I have developed a solid foundation in animal health, research, and evidence-based management. Serving as an Agricultural Sciences Change of Campus Mentor has strengthened my communication and mentorship skills while allowing me to support fellow students in their academic transition. Through coursework, hands-on learning, and preparation for my education abroad program, I have gained a broader perspective on the equine industry and veterinary medicine, reinforcing my goal to contribute meaningfully to animal health and the agricultural community.

I have loved my time at Penn State. The campus is beautiful and the town is very cute. I don't like athletics but sports are huge here. Lots of things for everyone to do, no matter your interests. Housing can be expensive, but I have found a nice place further from campus but still in walking distance for an affordable price. Drinking culture is huge here which can be a positive or a negative. Lots of food options on and around campus.
I love PSU. I am a communication sciences and disorders major, and I have no issues with the program! Professors are all amazing. I am a second semester JR.
I enjoyed my 4 years at PSU. I met my best friends and was academically challenged in my nursing program.

I love Penn State since it gave me the opportunity to stay back home for two years and then move out and find my own freedom for my last two years of college, through their 2+2 program. Now that I'm up here at State College I've gained independence and learned more about my responsibilities as a person; when I was at a branch campus I learned about ways to study, I made friends that I know I'll have after college. The professors at Penn State between both campuses I've been to are knowledgeable and always willing to help, without them I don't know if I'd have learned at much as I have.
Pretty good so far, challenging but like im definitely learning something and people will definitely learning something. And there is several learning opportunities put there.

ai_summary: At The University of Akron, the campus atmosphere is generally described as welcoming and vibrant , with a strong sense of community among students. Many reviewers appreciate the diverse food options available, though some note that certain places may have limited operating hours. Academically, the university is recognized for its solid programs, particularly in engineering and business, with many professors reportedly caring and supportive. However, issues with academic advising and the responsiveness of some faculty are common concerns among students. Student life is active, with numerous events, clubs, and sports, but the campus's commuter nature sometimes leaves non-commuters feeling isolated. Safety remains a significant issue, especially beyond the campus boundaries. While many find tuition relatively affordable, the perception of value for money varies, with some feeling that student services could be improved. Overall, students have had a mix of experiences, finding both strengths and areas for growth within the university.

reviews.text: I’m a transfer student at this university. I’m still learning about the college. So far I really enjoy it, the college campus itself feels pretty safe. Lots of police personnel around. However just outside of the campus has major safety concerns and risks. I’m half online and half in person so I haven’t discovered everything yet because I’m only on campus twice a week. But, I would still absolutely recommend this university!

I’ve really enjoyed my time there and plan to graduate in May 2026. Overall, I felt that my professor genuinely cared about my well-being and wanted me to succeed in the course. There were many opportunities to improve my grade, receive extra help, and grow as a leader. Even as an Early College student, I truly appreciated the support and encouragement I received.

I attended the University of Akron as a Business and Organizational Communications major and lived on campus. Overall, it was a really formative experience for me both academically and personally. Living on campus made it easy to get involved and feel connected to the school.
I joined a sorority during my first year, which helped me meet people, but I later found Pi Sigma Epsilon was a better fit for me. It gave me more opportunities to build practical skills, work with motivated peers, and stay engaged outside the classroom in a way that aligned with my goals.
Through my program I developed strong communication, presentation, and teamwork skills that I still use today. The campus is manageable, and Akron is an affordable place to live compared to many college towns.
Overall, I feel like the University of Akron gave me a solid foundation and helped prepare me for what came next.
It was a good experience and was a great time. I would like to see the diversity get better. More welcoming for freshmen

The University of Akron wants to be a fifth rate college with first rate athletics. Everyone is overworked and unhappy. It is super unclear what the mission of the university is, other than sports. The academics are community college level, but the price is a research-based state university. You can get the same education for much less at a community college.
I love Akron, and my time here has allowed me to meet so many great friends. Spending time with them outside of school is always a blast, while still staying focused on my education.

My Undergraduate experience at The University of Akron was different from the typical experience because I am an adult student with a full-time job. Many times I was not able to take advantage of all the experiences UA has to offer becasue I was working during events. Overall if you are an adult student UA staff and professors fully understand this and are extremely flexible especially in the smaller lesser known programs.

The University of Akron is a wonderful school. Being a commuter student, I find it very easy to focus on my academics while also having a social life. The University of Akron involves its commuter students as much as they can and makes efforts to host social events for student inclusion. I would recommend the University of Akron to incoming students.

It's a pretty average college, nothing super memorable or special. Professors are pretty good, campus life is okay, food isn't great. It would be cool if more effort was put in to the facilities of the campus.

Overall, I have really enjoyed my time at The University of Akron! I have found many lifelong friends and the campus is beautiful. Akron is one of the more affordable options and they are well known for engineering (which is what I am pursuing). I recommend reaching out to Professors to see if there are any undergraduate research opportunities available, as that is how I was able to become a Lab Assistant for a PhD student as a Freshman! There are so many learning opportunities through Akron that can give your resume a big boost, so do not be afraid to reach out! The university is about a 15 minute walk from Lock 3 (a place in Downtown Akron), where they host many free events making it perfect for college students. Also the campus is not too far away from the Cuyahoga Valley National Park so you get to experience both the city and the more rural side. The only downside is that many people are commuters so once the weekend hits there are not many people on campus.
I enjoyed the classes that I took as well as the intramural sports and clubs. I spent a lot of time joining different groups and finding ways to have fun.

The University of Akron has been a good fit for me as a student, especially because of the flexibility it offers. I have completed most of my coursework online, which has allowed me to balance school with other responsibilities while still staying on track toward my degree. The online format has been organized and accessible, making it easy to stay engaged and connected to my classes.
When I do spend time on campus, I have found it to be easy for the most part, besides a few far buildings (Polsky), to navigate and welcoming. Buildings are clearly marked, and getting from place to place feels straightforward, even for someone who is not on campus every day. I have also consistently encountered friendly faces, whether it is staff, faculty, or other students, which helps create a comfortable and supportive environment.

My overall experience with Akron U this year was okay. Everything is generally fine i’ve lived in akron my whole life, so i’m familiar with the area. The food is good it gets tiring after a while, the dorms are small but live able and not cramped, and your community is what you make it. The people here aren’t terrible it’s their actions but i believe life is what you make it, I make the best of everything and everyone i can so my community is great.

I wasn't sure about Akron University at first. After being there for an entire semester I can say that I do enjoy being there. I've met a lot of people and made a lot of connections. The campus life has treated me very good so far, I honestly cannot complain.

I think that college life here is strong in its academic area, and weak in every other aspect. I find it easy to reach out to professors and get help with things and have access to academic resources, which is very valuable as a student. On the other hand, I have not been satisfied with my housing, food, or social life here. I think that we need expanding so are able to have more than one dining hall, and so it can stay open later than 8 pm. It's very frustrating to not be able to eat in a dining hall during later hours and on the weekends it is pretty much the only thing to eat as the dining places in the student union are closed. We need updates to the dorm halls, as there have been numerous hot water outages- one being right now- and issues with maintenance response, janitorial staff, and just overall issues due to aging buildings. As far as socially, there seems to be little unity at this school. Overall, I feel like I've had to give up certain aspects here in exchange for others.

ai_summary: At The University of Alabama, students generally appreciate the vibrant community and supportive atmosphere on campus. Many highlight the rigorous academic programs and the dedication of professors who genuinely care about student success, although some express concerns about the quality of academic advising. The campus is frequently described as beautiful and welcoming , with a variety of clubs, activities, and athletic events like football games that foster school spirit and camaraderie. However, there are criticisms about dining options becoming monotonous and challenges related to parking availability and costs. Overall, students report a rich college experience filled with opportunities for growth and connection, often feeling that they are part of a close-knit family .

reviews.text: I would like to see a change in making sure professors or TA that are teaching a class can speak well enough English to be understood. And that the night life didn’t cost so much just to get in that no one can go because of the outrageous cover charges. I have loved the athletics and events.

So far it has been in-depth, supportive, and straightforward. There are great success coaches that will make time to meet and talk with you. The advisors actually listen to the questions you have rather than give advice that does not correspond with your desired journey.

Academically, my experience at the University of Alabama has been strong. I’ve maintained a 3.75 GPA as a first-generation student, which I’m proud of. As a creative media major, I sometimes feel the environment is better suited for a certain type of student. Being on the campus dance team has helped me stay balanced and enjoy my time mentally, while also giving me a chance to express myself creatively outside the classroom.

The University of Alabama offers a great overall college experience. Academics are overall well-funded, with a wide variety of courses offered, though some degree programs feel barebones in places. The student life is excellent, though Greek Life has a monopoly on the party scene.
As a incoming student already visited and its amazing. Campus is really beautiful! The energy the students have is charming. Don't get me started in game days!
So far at the university, I have made so many new friends and met amazing people. I absolutely love it here, and I would recommend coming here to anyone and everyone.

The experience was great, you really get to enjoy that college student life while also working with others. It was very enjoyable meeting people you’ve never thought existed and just growing a long lasting connection with others. I would’ve never expected that.

The University of Alabama is a highly active campus known for its strong athletic culture and wide range of opportunities, including football, basketball, baseball, dance, band, and even powerlifting. School spirit is a significant part of campus life, especially surrounding its nationally recognized sports programs. The university has a relatively high acceptance rate, making it accessible to many students, and it offers reputable music programs for those interested in the arts.
However, the campus appears to lack diversity compared to other large universities. While the campus size is manageable and not overwhelmingly large, some areas could benefit from improved maintenance and cleanliness. Out-of-state tuition can be expensive, which may be a concern for students traveling from other states. Overall, while the University of Alabama offers strong athletics and solid academic programs, there are areas that could be improved. Based on these factors, I would rate it a 7 out of 20.
it was a sendy one boys, sohan the dawg used to come tickle me when i was getting ferda up at sig nu

The University of Alabama is a great school overall with diverse, welcoming culture. Its a beautiful campus and the wide range of involvement opportunities make is an exciting place to learn and grow.

My overall experience thus far at the University of Alabama, is that it’s a nice school. It’s a great environment especially in the fall, and as an african american student you will definitely find your people. Academically it’s challenging in a good way. I’ve grown a lot as well. Here things aren’t just handed to you they are earned and I appreciate that, especially for future purposes.

I am still a student at The University of Alabama. I transferred from a local community college spring semester 2026. It has been different going from a community college to a university. The BAMA Bound orientation helped to answer any questions I had before classes began. My counselor has been a huge help as well. I can't saw anything negative so far in regard to my transfer experience.

I really enjoy the University of Alabama campus. The scenery for the walk to classes makes my day. The campus itself is really pretty. Student life is filled with so much life. It never gets old. What I would like to see change is not having to pay to wash and dry your clothes.

I truly enjoy and love the University of Alabama because it offers strong academics within a welcoming and spirited community. Professors are supportive and genuinely invested in student success, creating an environment that encourages growth and hard work. The university provides valuable resources that help students succeed both academically and personally.
Beyond academics, the sense of community and school pride makes the University of Alabama feel like home. With many opportunities for involvement and leadership, students can build meaningful connections and feel a true sense of belonging. Overall, the University of Alabama offers an inspiring environment that prepares students for future success while providing a memorable college experience.
I loved when I visited Alabama! Everyone was so inviting and the housing was spectacular. Not to mention really good food options.

ai_summary: At the University of Oklahoma, students generally appreciate the welcoming community and supportive faculty who strive to help them succeed academically. Many reviews highlight the diverse campus life and various opportunities for involvement, including clubs and organizations that cater to a wide range of interests. The campus itself is noted for its beauty and size , though some students mention navigating it can be challenging. While the academic experience is strong overall, opinions vary depending on the specific department, with some expressing dissatisfaction with the quality of certain professors or departmental support. Several reviewers point to the importance of belonging and inclusivity , noting that efforts are made to accommodate diverse backgrounds. However, the cost of tuition and campus life focused on Greek organizations are common concerns among students. Overall, OU is seen as a great place to grow, learn, and form lasting relationships .

reviews.text: we didn’t forget what happened btw. this school so buns. f samantha fulnecky that flop. justice for the TA.
It was an eye-opening experience because the college seems to be very open. Minorities are represented, and college life seems positive.

Current Freshman studying meteorology. The program for meteorology here is amazing, and the college experience is amazing as well. Love the vibe, moved across the country to come here and it was totally worth it.

I had a great time attending school at the University of Oklahoma. The professors and programs made sure we were prepared to succeed in their courses and gave us several opportunities to network and expand our professional experiences.

I am currently in the architecture program at OU and rarely have classes outside of my building, but when I do have classes outside and I’m on campus there is a lot of life. There are always events going on helping students, promoting student organizations, and sometimes free food. I really like the athletics side of Oklahoma as well. They are always finding a way to support their teams and it is so much fun to be apart of. I have only been here 3 semesters and there is not really much I would want to see change I feel like the university and the students are always finding something new to do to fit everyone’s interest.
It is a very beautiful campus and easy to walk to all buildings within 15 minutes. The dorms are also close to campus with lots of food options.

There were a couple of professors along my way that helped me succeed, however, overall it has not been a great experience. I have run into road blocks every step of my education process and have not been met with grace from anyone in the administration. Multiple concerns were raised in my program cohort to administrators and those in charge but nothing ever came of it nor did the issues go away. If it weren't for the select professors that have taken their personal time to be an advisor and advocate for me, I likely would not have made it this far.
Would love to see more food options as the lines can be up to an hour wait and there are few places to eat on campus

My experience at the University of Oklahoma has been positive. The campus atmosphere and sense of legacy are what I appreciate most, and they make OU feel like a place where students are part of something meaningful. I have found the people on campus (faculty, staff, fellow students) to be kind, supportive, and genuinely helpful, which has made my transition back to school feel welcoming and encouraging.
Being part of a community also means caring about how it handles difficult moments. I was saddened by the recent dismissal of a professor whose work and presence mattered to many students. While I understand that institutions face complex decisions, this situation reminded me how important it is for a university to foster an environment where all members feel valued and supported.
I am hopeful that OU will continue to grow in ways that reflect its commitment to respect, inclusion, and community. I am grateful to be part of a campus that encourages reflection, dialogue, and progress.

the University of Oklahoma is a good school but i don’t think it was a good school for me. if you are more extroverted and low maintenance with education and have the money to live on campus you will get along fine.

The University of Oklahoma has been a great experience overall. I love the campus atmosphere, school pride, and how welcoming most people are. Being a Sooner really feels special, especially on game days and around athletics. I’ve liked my classes so far and the resources available for students, especially academic advising and campus facilities. That said, I do think the cost of attendance is really high and can be stressful, especially for students relying on loans. I’d like to see more affordable housing and financial support options. Overall, OU is an amazing school with strong traditions and opportunities.

Starting off my freshman year at the University of Oklahoma I was scared and didn't know what to expect but was surprised to see the inclusion and diversity that OU has to offer. The buildings are surrounded by breathtaking architecture and staff that provides the safety and knowledge for everyone to grow. Something I would like to see change is providing more campus events to connect the community together.

This is a fanstastic school. It provides students with many academic opportunities and makes a strong effort to show that they care about their students and their life. It is a campus that is very accepting and very accesible in terms of being a successful adult. This school has made me feel very welcome and like I belong. It provides me with a very good balance between my academic and social environment where I have already met people who have made a strong impact on my life.
The University of Oklahoma is a school that promotes unreasoning hatred against targeted groups, lacks even the most basic of academic standards, and punishes people who expect better from them.
If you want a degree that is worth nothing please come here. A joke college with no integrity to academics and higher education

school_name: The University of Texas at Tyler
ai_summary: The University of Texas at Tyler provides a distinct college experience characterized by its beautiful , nature-filled campus and supportive community. Students appreciate the close-knit environment , where professors are described as personable and genuinely invested in their students' success. Academically, the university is noted for its nursing program and strong faculty across various disciplines, although some reviews mention inconsistency in teaching quality. While the campus offers a variety of student organizations and activities, there are concerns about the social scene and limited entertainment options in Tyler. Additionally, students have expressed a need for improvements in housing, parking, and dining services. Overall, UT Tyler is viewed as an affordable choice with a focus on undergraduate success and a strong sense of community, making it a suitable option for those seeking a smaller, supportive university experience.

reviews.text: UT Tyler is a dry campus school, so the party scene is next to none. However, in terms of education and opportunity, the professors are nice and available to talk to, the food options are decent with a convenience store and a few stores to buy from in and around campus. The campus police and many safety measures does not make you feel in danger.

The University of Texas - Tyler is a great school because it has a small, intimate campus with lots of nature, great professors that want to help students succeed, and it is located in a community that has increasingly become oriented towards college students in the the last several years. Athletics are not big at this school, nor is partying, leaving room for those who want to focus more on academics.

As a current Freshman Accounting Major at the University of Texas at Tyler, I have had no complaints about the school yet! The academics are great, I've enjoyed all of my professors, and it's a beautiful campus with a thriving student body!
I really love attending the University of Texas at Tyler. I did my undergrad there in the early 2000s and I would highly recommend it to anybody looking for a high-quality university.

UT Tyler is a wonderful school. The population is very diverse. The professors are very accommodating and attentive to your educational progress and success. One stop makes everything easily available and they are very helpful and knowledgeable about the areas that they work in. The food services are immaculate. The campus is very beautiful, peaceful, and serene. I am a returning student for graduate school. I also attended here for my undergrad. I took classes on campus during undergrad, this time it's all online and I am receiving the same if not more knowledge online than in the classroom setting. The faculty and students make you feel like you're a part of the team. Both are very doable. I have attended other universities and I would definitely place UT Tyler as a TOP pick on my list.

Less busy work for online courses… and less required software for classes! We had over 1200 dollars of additional software and materials to buy for one course without prior notification to enrolling. It caused a hardship financially!

The University of Texas at Tyler was a great opportunity for me to advance myself and create the ability to become the scholar I wanted to become. The campus was well laid out and the academic buildings were adequately supplied with materials needed for learning. I enjoyed my professors and overall experience through school. I worked full time and went to school full time so life was very busy. Overall, I’m thankful for what the university life taught me and shaped me into the person I am now.

I attended the University of Texas at Tyler as a duel credit student in the spring semester of 2025. The Professors really care about the students growth and wellbeing. The classes provide an optimal education experience considering the shortened semester time. I will be attending the University of Texas at Tyler again for my undergrade degree in Biology.

UT Tyler is a lovely place, and I have had a very good time attending so far. The campus is nice and connected, the classes are well managed. The student life is very nice, even though I've been a bit too busy to attend such things. No change necessary.
This is a great school to go to because of all the things you can do. Also all the people you can meet as you go throughout college.
It has been great. I have gained valuable connections, and I have excelled academically. I believe that this is a wonderful university, and I would recommend it to everyone

My experience has definitely been a rollercoaster. Going from staying at home, to 3 hours away from my family hasn’t been easy. Always learning new things outside my comfort zone, and interacting with new faces on a daily basis has been my life at UT Tyler for the past couple of years, but it has been a good one.
My experience at UT Tyler has been pretty good overall, but one thing I would like for them to change is their tuition.
I have enjoyed my professors they are very understanding and flexible. The location of the school is great and there is a lot of student involvement in programs. The school is also very diverse.

This last spring was my first semester at Ut Tyler, I really enjoyed it and loved all my professors and classes. I’m majoring in political science so it is a lot of work but I have a lot of resources at school to keep me on track which I really like.

ai_summary: The University of Texas - Arlington offers a diverse and accessible educational experience that many students appreciate, especially for its affordability and variety of academic programs. Reviewers highlight the strong support from advisors and professors , emphasizing their dedication and willingness to help. The campus is described as beautiful and manageable, fostering a comfortable environment for learning. However, some students express concerns about the limited social life , particularly due to its status as a commuter school, and the lack of extensive sports offerings , such as a football team. While many students have positive comments about the resources and opportunities available, there are calls for improvements in food options , parking, and campus services, particularly in departments like nursing and business. Overall, UTA is seen as a good choice for students focused on academics and career preparation, though some would like to see initiatives to enhance the overall student life experience.

reviews.text: Its a great community to be in and everyone there is amazing. The work giving my be a little hectic and uneasy but after few dedication, life becomes better and easy to pave through.

The university of Texas at Arlington was an essential experience with opportunities to enhance my social work practice skills. A student in the advanced standing program, absorbing knowledge via online was smooth transition because of the support from the professors and peers. Eventually, working towards my internship to apply my practice and service learning tools, writing papers about the experiences to display my knowledge. Overall, the experience was great as well as the support and community.
great and i would choose this again. niche is helping me out so much to prepare me for me college life

My overall experience at the University of Texas - Arlington has been wonderful. There are always fun activities and events to participate in. Th e campus life is lively and there is so much diversity.

University of Texas at Arlington is a busy college with decent academic scores and a very diverse student population. They offer sports like basketball, powerlifting, and dance, but they do not have a football team, which might be disappointing for some students. The campus isn’t very big and feels kind of in the middle of nowhere, but it still has a lot going on day to day.
One good thing about UTA is the cost—out-of-state tuition is fairly affordable at around $40,000, which is cheaper compared to many other schools. However, students are required to take two years of a foreign language, which may be challenging for some. The music program and band are not the strongest, and the campus isn’t the cleanest. There are also some safety concerns, but overall it’s manageable if you stay aware. Based on these factors, I give it 8.5/20

I felt that UTA offered many resources that allow students to succeed. While my experience is predominantly in the Kinesiology scene I had friends from different majors that spoke about all the opportunities. The professors were also extremely helpful in making you aware of volunteer opportunities, research, clubs, while also helping you success academically. It felt as if the professors truly cared about your success. While the school is relatively large, most of my classes especially towards the end of my degree felt intimate.
I am seeking admission to University of Texas after my Associate in Information Technology from Tarrant County College.

This is my first semester at UTA, having transferred from CTU, I have been more than pleased with my overall experience so far. The campus, facilities, facilities and staff have all been wonderful to experience, and I truly feel that the goal of the institution is student success.
Everywhere in the campus in walkable so I like that. And I also like the gym part. There are so many other functions I can use!

I loved my time at UT Arlington! The campus is vibrant and diverse, with a lot of opportunities to get involved. I tour campus on my own and with assistance,which helped me build connections and gain leadership skills.
The professors are knowledgeable and supportive, and the coursework is challenging but rewarding. I appreciated the flexibility of online and evening classes, which worked well with my part-time job.
If I could change one thing, I'd say more resources for students navigating financial aid and tuition costs would be huge. Sometimes it felt like the support was there, but it was hard to find or access.
Overall, UTA's got a great energy, and I'd recommend it to anyone looking for a solid education and a supportive community.

UTA is the best school to get your education if you are focused. If you like to party and be super social there is another school better for you, but if your education is your top priority, go to UTA. The campus is beautiful, the facilities, the people, the undergrad/grad programs, the resources, the NETWORKING, everything is amazing there. 10/10 recommend.

Overall, my experience at the University of Texas at Arlington has been positive. I appreciate the diversity on campus and the wide range of academic programs offered, which makes it easy to find opportunities that match different interests and career goals. Many professors are knowledgeable and supportive, and the campus has good facilities like libraries, study spaces, and student centers that make learning more accessible.
That said, there are a few areas that could be improved. Communication between departments and students can sometimes be slow or unclear, especially regarding advising and administrative processes. I would also like to see more campus events and student engagement activities, particularly for commuters, to help build a stronger sense of community. Overall, UTA provides solid academic opportunities, and with some improvements in student support and engagement, the experience could be even better.

Great campus, more of a quiet one though...not much of a party school if that's what you like. Great community, and most professors are nice and understanding! I would say, the school could be more accommodating to students when it comes to the amount of classes offered. I've ran into soooo many scheduling issues.

The main thing i enjoy about UTA is how everything is laid out, so far applying to classes and reaching out to advisors are simple and usually very easy to do. The only thing that i would enjoy to see a change for, would just have to be how some classes aren;t offered in the winter or semester classes so it makes it difficult to get ahead in those areas.
I think there's a lot of diversity here and I like it because you can explore other cultures and experiences. The environment also is very good the people in here are so kind.

ai_summary: The University of Texas at Austin is a large , vibrant institution that offers a balance of rigorous academics and a lively student life , enhanced by its central location in Austin. Students frequently mention the competitive but rewarding atmosphere where they can engage in numerous clubs and activities, fostering a strong sense of community and school pride. While the professors are generally supportive and passionate about their subjects, reviews highlight variability in teaching quality, particularly in large introductory classes. The campus and surrounding area provide a variety of resources, dining options, and social opportunities, although issues like parking, housing costs, and campus safety have been noted. Overall, the university is recognized for its strong academic programs, especially in fields like computer science and business, making it a distinctive choice for students seeking both academic and personal growth.

reviews.text:
Studying at the University of Texas at Austin was both challenging and rewarding. The campus is vibrant and diverse, with endless opportunities to learn and grow. I appreciated the world-class faculty, strong research resources, and the lively student community. Austin itself adds so much—music, food, and culture make student life exciting.
What I liked:
Diverse and inclusive student body
Excellent academic resources and faculty support
Strong school spirit and alumni network
The city of Austin’s energy and culture
What I’d like to see change:
Smaller class sizes for more personalized learning
Better parking and transportation options
More affordable housing near campus
Expanded mental health resources
Overall, UT Austin offers a balance of academic excellence and a dynamic campus culture. It’s a place where you can grow intellectually while enjoying a vibrant social environment.

So far, my experience at the University of Texas at Austin has been wonderful, mostly everyone here is very welcoming or happy. Athletics are a big part of student life and there is always something to do on campus or off campus. I would say a big part about life at UT Austin would be exploring or trying something new, having new experiences is what makes up a large part of student life along with music and food as well.

My overall experience at the University of Texas at Austin has been incredibly positive. I truly love being here and, most of all, I love my major in Architecture. While the program is extremely demanding and challenging, it’s also deeply rewarding. The intensity pushes me to grow creatively and academically, and it has confirmed that I chose the right path.
The professors are knowledgeable, passionate, and genuinely invested in their students’ learning and development.
One area I would love to see improved relates to student safety during late-night studio hours. As an architecture student, staying on campus very late, sometimes as late as 4 a.m. is common. While UT does offer walking safety services, they currently only operate until 2 a.m. Extending these services later into the night would make a meaningful difference for students.
UT Austin has been an amazing place to learn, grow, and challenge myself, and I’m grateful to be part of such a strong academic community.

I am having such an amazing experience in this college. I love my classes, the friends I have made, the projects I have worked on, and all the events I have attended. I am currently in the Longhorn Band, which is such a great organization that has helped me meet some great people. I have been able to learn so much not only for my major, but life experiences as well. I absolutely love this college and I am super excited to continue attending here and experience more.

The University of Texas and the city of Austin are a very warm and welcoming place. No matter who you are or what you enjoy doing there will always be someone or something you can relate to! If you want sports we have it, if you want parties there's plenty, if you're just here for academics there's on overwhelming amount of opportunities! The only thing I could even think to ask to change is to have more financial aid/scholarship opportunities. Not that there isn't plenty at UT but who wouldn't want to pay less for college you know?
Met some great people there. Overall just a wonderful experience. Would definitely recommend this college to someone else!

I’ve loved my time at UT so far! Although it’s a big school, it’s pretty easy to make it feel small and tight knit. The environment here is relatively collaborative, the school population is diverse and supportive. I love our campus and I have I lot of school pride!
Such a great university with lots of opportunities and resources for students. The school makes student feel safe on campus and are always providing safety resources.

I have visited UT Austin many times and I have had a great experience, overall! I would definitely recommend this university for those who are looking for a balanced college experience and diverse students!

The University of Texas at Austin has been a terrific experience, especially for those wanting strong academics, a real "college-feel", yet still be in a walkable city/urban area. There's always plenty to do as a college student in Austin! I do wish I saw more cross-disciplinary collaboration across departments and schools within UT. There are so many possible areas of opportunity for students of different backgrounds and majors to work together on overlapping projects, just like in the real world. However, this is essentially only possible in a few clubs and orgs, many of which require an application and membership. I wish UT saw this potential for collaboration and acted proactively to bring students together.

I enjoyed the academic rigor and the many opportunities for organizational involvement. There are truly so many orgs on campus that it makes it hard to not be involved in one that you find interesting or enjoyable. Austin is an amazing city with so many activities and things to do and being in Austin made college so much better overall.

UT Austin is an amazing school for stepping out of your comfort zone but not putting yourself in dangerous waters. I feel like it is so easy to find your community here because of everyone being so welcoming. I believe that you can find the help you need no matter what the issue is and that this University wants its students to become great things.
My overall experience has been amazing as ive had the best few months in my life. Both meeting new people and experiencing new

I love the 40 acres, I think it's beautiful, and I love going to such a historical campus. A change I would like to see is the number of science credits we have to take as a Bachelor of Arts. It feels unnecessary.

UT Austin provided an exceptional academic and personal experience during my time there. The university’s academic standards were consistently high, and the support offered through resources such as the Sanger Learning Center was invaluable. The wide range of courses allowed me to explore my interests in depth and broaden my perspective across multiple fields. Student life was equally impressive. The campus was highly walkable, student organizations represented virtually every interest, and the sports culture fostered a strong sense of community. The surrounding city of Austin enriched the experience even further, serving as a vibrant hub for music, art, technology, and sports.
I would like to see the development of a more detailed and responsive alert system, along with stronger collaboration with student ambassadors, to ensure clearer communication and address the needs of those in the area. My experience at UT Austin remains overwhelmingly positive.

ai_summary: At the University of Texas at Dallas, students generally appreciate the academic rigor and the diverse, supportive environment. Many highlight the university's strong focus on STEM fields , pleasant campus, and the dedicated professors who often go the extra mile to assist students. While the campus offers numerous resources and organizations to help enhance the college experience, some students feel that social life is lacking, particularly for commuters and those seeking traditional college festivities. The dining options receive mixed reviews, with suggestions for improvement in variety and quality.
