Additionally, the reviews indicate that while there are many opportunities for internships and professional growth, the tuition can be a deterrent, leading some to call for lower costs to promote a more diverse student body. Overall, students find value in the education and opportunities at UTD but express a desire for a stronger social atmosphere and more amenities.

reviews.text: My experience with the University of Texas at Dallas has been overall positive. Most professors have been more than welcoming during office hours to not only help you with homework, but to give you advice on future ventures. Furthermore, there is a large amount of clubs for all kinds of majors, there is never a lack of extracurriculars. The buildings, as well, are all modern, so the study spaces here are abundant, and very comfortable.

I've had a generally decent experience at UTD so far. The academics are solid and the resources are there, but I find myself longing for a more high-energy environment. While the campus is functional, I’d like to see a shift toward a culture that more actively pushes and motivates students to strive for more outside of just the classroom. I'm looking for more of that 'drive' and a competitive yet supportive atmosphere to really level up

I enrolled at UTD as a premed student, and I believe the on campus opportunities and academics set me up very well for medical school. I was able to engage in basic science research for all four years, serve as a teaching assistant, work as a tutor at the student success center, and engage in activities such as the on campus EMS agency. The advising committee is helpful in preparing students for the medical school application process (compiling letters of recommendation, reviewing applications, and offering mock interviews). They are generous with financial aid and take promoting equity in higher education seriously.
UTD strongly supports activities match as chess and e-sports and there are options to explore the arts as well (acapella groups, theatre, choir, orchestra, etc.). If there were one thing I would like to see change in the future, it would be to place more of an emphasis on atheletics because it is a quintessential college experience that UTD students seem to miss out on.

Visiting the University of Texas at Dallas during their scholar's day event felt very welcoming; everyone was very nice, and I enjoyed my experience a lot. There was never a dull conversation, and I learned a lot about the campus and the people there. I plan to return in the fall and hope that I have just as much fun as I did attending their Scholars' Day.

The University of Dallas is at a great location and city. It is in the suburbs not too far out from Downtown Dallas with great food spots and lots of stores around. I have always felt safe and comfortable on campus and I am apart of the University Emergency medical response team which a lot of other schools do not have. UTD has a lot of resources to ensure campus safety. The academics I would also say are pretty well with very engaging professors that care for their students. However I would say that there is not of active campus life or school spirit. A lot of students are unaware of our sports teams or just don't go to events because of lack of advertising. I would like to see more engagement in that aspect and also more resources to help new rising clubs.
Happy with the housing transition process and great professors. I wish the learning module was canvas but that is changing in the fall.

This is a very good college to tour. They provide a well rounded campus tours and welcome you in once you step foot. The location is very serene and peaceful and you are sure to make some great friendships while there.

The University of Texas at Dallas has an amazing campus with high safety. They are academically very competitive and I love that. I never thought I would feel so welcomed and comfortable building my future. They offer unconditional help with anything. Im in love with all they have to offer.

The University of Texas at Dallas is a good place for a quiet and steady campus life. It offers many experiences fitting for anyone's needs and preferences. My personal experience here has been quite enjoyable, as I live in the Vega Hall with my lovely 2 roommates and many friends. The events offered are also quite diverse, and I see all kinds of people participating, and new friendships blossom as we come together.

I went to UTD for 4 years and I unfortunately had a very bad experience. I transferred to another school because I didn't feel safe there anymore due to the escalating political climate (protests, arrests, etc). Especially as a woman, it's not a safe environment. I knew several girls who were assaulted, harassed, or stalked (or worse) on or near campus. I went there for engineering, which it's known for, but after transferring to another school I realized that many of the professors at UTD are not good lecturers or you simply cannot understand them because of how strong their accents are. The advisors aren't helpful and can be discouraging. UTD advertises itself as "diverse", and I suppose it is more diverse than schools that are 80% white US citizens, but when you spend time on campus you realize it's not really that diverse and if you're not from the same background as the students then it's hard to make friendships in class. I think they get good ratings because of research.

My overall experience at the University of Texas at Dallas has been positive and academically enriching. I appreciate the strong STEM and biology programs, supportive faculty, and the availability of research, pre-health, and professional development opportunities. The campus provides a focused environment that encourages learning, growth, and collaboration.
One area that could be improved is increasing hands-on opportunities earlier in undergraduate programs and expanding academic advising support, especially for pre-health students navigating complex requirements. Overall, UT Dallas has helped me grow academically and professionally and has prepared me well for future healthcare and research goals.

As a current full-time student, I love going to UTD. It is a very good school, with a lot of different activities and clubs to get yourself involved with. Overall, a good school with good professors and a good college life.

I am an international student from India at UTD. I really like UTD due to its diversity and how many intelligent people attend the school. One thing I don't like about UTD is that it is a commuter school with a lot of student being from Dallas high schools, which makes a social scene a little dull for outsiders.

As a freshman at the University of Texas at Dallas, my experience has been both exciting and challenging. The campus is modern and well-maintained, with plenty of study spaces, libraries, and recreational areas. Professors are knowledgeable and approachable, and I’ve found that attending office hours really helps deepen my understanding of course material. The class sizes are manageable, which makes it easier to ask questions and participate in discussions. I also appreciate the variety of student organizations and events, which provide opportunities to meet new people and explore interests outside of academics. Navigating campus life and balancing coursework has been a learning experience, but overall, I feel supported and motivated to succeed. UTD has given me a strong foundation as I begin my college journey.
I would go on tours and play soccer on there fields. Very good school and very good academics. Overall I would say the environment at the school is great

ai_summary: At the University of Texas - El Paso (UTEP), students frequently emphasize the strong sense of community , diversity, and accessibility of resources. Many reviews highlight the friendly and supportive faculty who are genuinely invested in student success, along with opportunities for academic growth through engaging courses and extracurricular activities. The campus is noted for its beautiful architecture and stunning views , creating an inviting atmosphere for students. However, some concerns arose regarding the parking situation, as well as mixed experiences with certain administrative offices, such as financial aid and admissions. Students appreciate UTEP's affordability and express satisfaction with the various academic programs, especially those related to nursing and engineering. Overall, the university provides a welcoming environment conducive to personal and professional development , particularly for students from diverse backgrounds.

reviews.text: UTEP is overall a good school if you live in-state/ in El Paso, looking for a college that is cheap/affordable. Only downside I would say is that if you are not from El Paso, the school life looks very different compared to other schools because it is a commuter school. On the bright side, UTEP really does seem to be growing, even developing some greek life so if your interested in social life as someone not from El Paso, from what I saw it is a little difficult but it doesn’t mean it will remain that way.
I loved the self paced online program. This is why I am wanting to go back to to UTEP and get a certification online.

My overall experience at UTEP has been both challenging and rewarding. I have not only learned technical knowledge, but also the importance of compassion, responsibility, and professionalism in healthcare.
The University of Texas at El Paso is a great university with a bunch of free resources to help you achieve a great education.
UTEP is a nice school to attend to if you want to have the bigger campus collage school without having to leave town. The campus and teacher are pretty nice, I like going to school there

This is a great school, and I have had, and am still having, a very positive experience. I believe it offers much more than a community college experience, especially in terms of academic challenge, which is what I expected from a university. That said, I think some professors could benefit from showing more passion and enthusiasm, as a few seem to teach simply to get through the material. I also believe certain courses would greatly benefit from more real-world activities and hands on interaction rather than focusing only on theory. Overall, I do not regret my decision to attend this university at all.

My overall experience at the University of Texas at El Paso has been very positive. UTEP is a welcoming and inclusive campus, especially for Hispanic students, where diversity is not only represented but genuinely celebrated. For me, being close to home is a benefit, and the downtown El Paso location makes the campus feel connected to its community.
UTEP is also well known for its strong engineering programs and research opportunities. The university has close ties with the local community and industry, which provides students with valuable hands-on experiences, internships, and research projects. I’ve enjoyed seeing the campus continue to grow with new buildings and new facilities.
I appreciate the resources UTEP provides, such as the free laptop rental program, which helps ensure students have access to essential technology.
One area that could improve is parking. Permits are expensive and difficult to obtain, and finding parking can be challenging, especially during peak hours.
My personal experience from Utep from my past years of visitation, I would describe are very welcoming, and full of new opportunities.
Amazing and always clean campus with very friendly staff and culture. Administration as always very helpful.

My overall experience was pretty good, I had friends in my classes and my professors were very nice and understanding. They were very helpful if you had any questions or doubts about an assignment or project.
As an upcoming transfer student, who is already very familiar with the student life, UTEP is a school that believes in second chances and giving each and every student the ability to achieve.

My overall experience at UTEP has been nothing but bittersweet, I really enjoy being able to be on campus in my hometown. The environment is great you're surrounded by nothing but hard working and dedicated students who have the same drive and aspirations at you. I’ve enjoyed my classes and professors who also enjoy what they teach. The university is great at providing access to many tools to its students. I didn’t think I would get to enjoy the campus life as much as I do being that I live in El Paso and just commute to and from campus but I got the same experience as any other student has.

The University of Texas at El Paso is a gorgeous campus with a diverse climate. It is surrounded by stunning mountains and the beautiful Spanish language. They offer lot of events and resources for students including health services, financial aid, sports events, clubs and organizations, and much more.
It a great University that has a large and diverse population. I would definitely recommend it to anyone who is considering college.

Easier methods to get disabled people like myself to classes in a timely manner. assistive technology, extended time on tests) Accessibility features of a location or service? (e.g., ramps, accessible restrooms) Resources for disabled students

school_name: The University of Texas--Rio Grande Valley
ai_summary: At the University of Texas Rio Grande Valley, many students appreciate the friendly and supportive atmosphere , noting that both peers and professors are eager to help. While there are praises for the affordable tuition and quality education , some students highlight issues with communication and organization within the administration. The campus is described as beautiful and easy to navigate , though parking can be problematic, with some students facing lengthy walks to their classes. The diverse student body largely reflects the local Hispanic culture, which contributes to a sense of community , but some reviews express a desire for more diversity in student backgrounds. Overall, UTRGV is viewed positively, particularly for its academic programs and student services, although there are varying opinions on the rigor of academics and the efficiency of staff.

reviews.text: The University is rather small and at the time the student athletics was very minimal which made student engagement feel lacking. The professors overall were kind, but every course seemed powerpoint and quizlet motivated. Overall, my experience left me feeling like I missed out on a lot of college life.
Overall, UTRGV has been meeting my expectations. I even have an internship lined up for Summer 2027 at a big 4 firm.
Great school if you already live in the area. Professors and courses feel smooth and easy, not such a great school to dorm/live in, in my opinion.
My experience with UTRGV was average to say the least, though the events they held were very inviting and I felt as though I could really participate with the UTRGV team.

This university is unique in the sense that it is split into two campuses. The main and bigger campus located in Edinburg, Tx and the smaller campus located in Brownsville. Most student do not choose where they live and although we are grateful for the bus, it's draining and unfair to travel over an hour away for a class that is only offered at one of the universities.
I love the community, the help they provide, and overall the culture! I have been attending for a semester already and they have not given me any trouble!
I’m loving UTRGV so far, most of my professors have been great and I feel like it’s a very nice/peaceful environment.

I'm currently a high school student enrolled in University of Texas Rio Grande Valley's 2-year Mathematics and Science Academy, in which students get to take courses within the actual university. It's no surprise I've loved my experience, as UTRGV provides a welcoming atmosphere with a myriad of academic opportunities for all sorts of niches. I'd like to see just how their extracurriculars continue to diversify and create an even greater sense of community.

Although I was at UTRGV for a short time, my overall experience was great! The professors were always very helpful and understanding. I also made a lot of great connections with my peers when I was there. Some will most likely be lifelong friends. I would definitely recommend this college to anyone that is willing to go there.

At UTRGV, it feels like home. My experience has been nothing but great. The student union could most definitely offer healthier options to eat at lunch or dinner time. I do struggle a lot with the parking, especially for Zone 2, where students pay a higher price, and there seem to be limited spaces. More microwaves for students in every building would be awesome. The profesors for the most part, are super understanding and help their students out.
UTRGV provided so many opportunities for us. Great school with great vibes. Especially the college of business

Overall, UTRGV gave me a strong academic foundation, a supportive environment, and a deep appreciation for cultural identity in education. With more investment in research and funding, the university could further empower students to reach their full potential.

My experience in UTRGV is ok so far, originally I went to school in Texas A&M Kingsville, so far the change of scene has been nice. Most of the professors are nice and are professional in their field, and the Brownsville campus is beautiful, but something that does bother me is that since UTRGV has 2 campuses one in Edinburgh and the other in Brownsville only the Edinburgh campus gets the focus and most classes in the Brownsville campus can't go on because no one is signing up for them because most of the classes are being taken to Edinburgh. Tome it seems unfair that only one campus gets the attention while the other suffers because every professor and student has to be in Edinburgh, making it hard for some students to travel from Brownsville to the other campus. The reason why I say that is because the buses used for transportation are often crowded and the schools also use vans and small buses, which often time stink and students cannot have personal space on.

Although the University of Texas Rio Grande Valley was not my first choice, I will say I am glad that I am attending this school. I enjoy going to class and I always feel I can talk to my professors about any questions I may have. I also enjoy all the campus activities they have to offer and how easy it has been to make new friends. There is always something for everyone and the overall campus is very nice. I am happy with my choice to attend this university and can not wait to graduate with my bachelor's degree.

My experience with the University of Texas Rio Grande Valley was extremely positive. A lot of students commute to campus in Edinburg since the Rio Grande Valley is a region where everything is so spread out that it cannot be traveled by foot. Nonetheless, I was very involved in organization events by staying late after classes. I had to manage everything while working, so it was very fun spending time with friends and classmates at the student union eating Chick fil a while playing billiards. For me, my undergraduate experience was all about balance. I would study in my free time, but when it was time to rest or work I would fully devote my attention to those activities. The experience here is what you make the most out of it.

ai_summary: At The University of Texas at San Antonio, students appreciate the diverse and inclusive campus environment , with many events and organizations supporting active student involvement . The accessibility of resources, including helpful faculty and excellent tutoring options , often stands out in reviews, although parking challenges are commonly mentioned. While professors are generally considered dedicated and caring, experiences vary; some students report engaging classes , while others express frustration with larger class sizes and teaching methods. Overall, UTSA offers a variety of academic and extracurricular opportunities, creating a sense of community that many students find rewarding, but some feel overwhelmed by the campus's size and dynamics.

reviews.text: I had a great time at UTSA, and I was sad to leave at the end of my degree. Most of my professors had practical experience and gave great advice. I got very lucky with my academic advisor; other students said they struggled to contact theirs. The advisors were always booked, and the specialized ones for health professions were not very helpful to me. The campus had a ton of great free student resources, the rec center, the clinic, counseling, student success coaching, tutoring, the writing and career centers, etc. It felt like there was always something happening on campus to the point where it was difficult to stay aware of important events. Because of the number of commuter students, the parking situation can be pretty bad. I wish passes were less expensive, but the alternative spots for weekend and evening classes were helpful. They do try to emphasize public transportation and have a bus system I just struggled to understand it.

My overall experience at The University of Texas at San Antonio has been pretty good so far. One thing I really like is the campus because it’s big and modern, and there are a lot of nice places to study or just hang out between classes. The student community is also really diverse, so you get to meet people from different backgrounds, which makes it more interesting. I also like that there are a lot of resources for students, like tutoring and career services, which actually help if you take advantage of them. Some of the professors are really helpful and explain things in a way that’s easy to understand. One thing I would like to see change is parking because it can be really hard to find a spot, especially during busy times. I also think there could be more fun events or activities to get students more involved on campus. Overall though, it’s a solid school and a good place to be.

My overall experience at The University of Texas at San Antonio has been positive and rewarding. I have appreciated the diverse and supportive campus environment. The professors I’ve had were knowledgeable and genuinely cared about student success, which made learning more engaging and meaningful. I also liked the flexibility of course options and the opportunities to connect with classmates from different backgrounds.
One of the things I liked most was the sense of community. Even in larger classes, there were opportunities to ask questions, participate in discussions, and feel supported academically. The campus resources, such as advising and tutoring services, were also helpful when I needed guidance.
If I could suggest improvements, I would like to see more streamlined communication between departments and students, especially regarding financial aid, registration deadlines, and important updates. Sometimes information can feel overwhelming or unclear.

UToledo is a prestigious university that trains in engineering, so I chose to study mechanical engineering technology here. The facilities at the school are very good. Especially the laboratories and practices are highly invested. As an international student, I really like the multinational environment when studying here. There are many events that students can participate in and connect with people. I am very happy to be a part of UT.

I did not involve myself much in the party or campus life scene but do think they offered organizations or groups relating to different peoples needs. I feel it was a diverse campus. They had text alerts sent out regarding upcoming events or safety concerns which was nice. I never was on campus at night but would feel uncomfortable if I was. It was nice to have multiple hangout or areas to study in the library, cafeteria or other seating areas around campus.
I have had a positive experience at the University of Toledo. There is a lot of organizations and I've been able to gain meaningful leadership experience.

ai_summary: Reviews for Thomas Jefferson University highlight a mix of academic strengths and challenges concerning student life and facilities. Many students appreciate the small class sizes and the knowledge of professors, which fosters close relationships and support for personal growth. The university is noted for its rigorous programs , particularly in medical and design fields, with students often feeling prepared for future careers. However, concerns arise around campus housing, with reports of outdated dorms and poor food quality; WiFi access and student services, such as health care support, have also been criticized. Social life appears to be limited, with some students wishing for more opportunities to connect and engage with peers. The university is described as having a welcoming atmosphere and a beautiful campus , but it may not meet everyone's expectations regarding activities and campus culture. Overall, TJU provides a solid academic foundation, especially for those in healthcare and design, but students may encounter challenges in other areas of campus life.

reviews.text: Overall I enjoyed my time at Thomas Jefferson University. I was challenged academically and learned a lot from my classes to prepare me for graduate school for my future role as a nurse practitioner. The friends I made at this university was what made the time here that much better.

The university offers a variety of majors with quality professors. At times, the education can be very rigorous and competitive. The professors do offer many opportunities for help outside of class and good education. The school has cliques, making the social life very small. It is right on the outskirts of the city center, making it easy to find places to go to, but secluded enough that the area feels safe.

It's a small school. The education you get is very personal and it's easy to develop relationships with faculty. Some of the adjunct professors are really bad, especially for the "hallmarks" courses. Besides that I have had positive experiences with all of my professors. If you are looking to party this probably isn't the place. Public transportation isn't great in the area and is being defunded by the state so I would recommend having a car.
My overall experience at Jefferson has been great, very welcoming and supportive with every decision made.

I attend Thomas Jefferson university’s Dixon campus for their accelerated Bachelor of Science in Nursing program. Being on a satellite campus, I was concerned with the resources that would be presented to students and thus far am super disappointed. Orientation week they had a woman from financial aid and a woman from admissions that were available to talk. I had accepted a significant scholarship on BannerWeb (the schools interface) which I was informed I “wasn’t supposed to receive” and that it “would have been caught in an audit”. This was a large factor in why I chose to attend TJU in the first place. I decided to pay the amount and continue, as I already turned down the other schools I had applied to. Randomly, the week before finals in the first semester I became locked out of all my university accounts (canvas, email, etc.) & they told me I was unenrolled. Organization is terrible and people like to pass off responsibility (especially in the summer semester) to anyone they can.

During my first year at Thomas Jefferson University, I had a pretty good experience, achieving a 3.9 GPA and making the Dean's List, which I'm really proud of. I also built a strong network by connecting with professors and students and attending various events. I shadowed various companies, which gave me a head start in understanding what I specifically want to pursue in the future. Looking ahead to this upcoming year, I’d like to improve the student life at TJU by fostering a stronger sense of community. On campus, many students stick to their friend groups, and I'd like to see events going on to bring everyone closer, making our environment feel more like a family. Additionally, as a business major, I want others to see the talents of students in our program and other majors in our university which unfortunately often go unnoticed due to our strong focus on medicine.

TJU is a small school with a strong sense of community. They have unique programs with experts in that area. The campus is beautiful and lined with safety phones. However, don't venture very far off campus because there are bad areas nearby. Somewhat expensive.
Overall, I love the Thomas Jefferson University - East Falls campus. The environment is warming and calm and I never doubt my decision about going here.

As an incoming freshman at Jefferson, I love their security system. They have poles with emergency buttons that you can press at any time you feel unsafe. Another thing that I love is the food. They serve a variety of different kinds of food in the cafeteria. As far as education goes I can’t comment on it because this is my first year and I haven’t even started yet. But these are just a few of the things that I love about Jefferson so far.

The Learning approach at Jefferson sounds like an innovative and effective way to prepare students for success in their careers. By emphasizing real-world collaboration, interdisciplinary learning, and industry partnerships, students can gain valuable skills and experiences that will benefit them in the ever-evolving job market.
Some key benefits of the Nexus Learning approach include:
Preparing students for careers that may not yet exist
Fostering collaboration and teamwork across disciplines
Providing hands-on experience with industry partners and entrepreneurial ventures
Developing adaptable and innovative thinking
Overall, the Learning approach seems like a unique and valuable asset for Jefferson students!

I loved Jefferson for undergraduate and am excited to start graduate school. When choosing Jefferson I went by athletics but academics as well. They had the programs I wanted and even accepted my dual enrollment from high school allowing me to graduate from undergraduate a year early. Everything was easy to apply to, register for classes, parking, access to resources, etc. I admired the work they put into the campus, always having flowers in season, keeping the walk ways cleared in the winter and making sure all the facilities are cleaned. Jefferson gave me the best 3 years of experience and social opportunities. I discovered what I wanted to do with my degree and area of career interest. Learning on hand experiences and having the chance for research. They have amazing connections and individuals to speak to when trying to find jobs, housing, applying to postgraduate programs and more. Being an athlete and student on this campus I always felt safe and valued.

I had to transfer out of this school because it is actually a joke. To start off, the school legitimately doesn’t have WiFi?? How is this
possible when they spend 7 million on a Super Bowl commercial but can’t get us internet access?? Second off, I was facing some health issues and the health services office gave me an incorrect diagnosis and put me on antibiotics for no reason which ended up making my situation worse. Health services closes at 4 and isn’t open on weekends so if you need medical attention you’ll have to spend a bunch of money to go else where. In the dining hall I’ve found mold in my food as well as raw chicken, every vegetable or healthy item is drenched in olive oil so no matter what you eat, you feel awful. I did enjoy some of my time here (in the city OFF campus) but I fear the school is just focused on getting more students, more money and a “name” but they don’t reciprocate that into our education.

I gave the campus food two stars because they have a few microwaves at the whole campus. We are at the center city campus so there’s no real dining hall area for students, usually we just by from restaurants in the area.
Life on campus is nice when you have the right people around you and try to make the most of your college experience as long as you try to put yourself out there
It's a great school with a diverse section of majors. The students are friendly and most of the teachers are really great.

ai_summary: Touro University generally receives positive feedback for its supportive faculty and small class sizes , which foster a close-knit learning environment . Students appreciate the commitment from professors who are invested in their success and offer flexibility in scheduling , accommodating those with busy lives or religious commitments. However, there are noted concerns about the lack of resources and extracurricular opportunities, with many feeling that the campus environment is not vibrant or engaging. While the academics are often described as strong, some reviews highlight inconsistencies in teaching quality among professors and a desire for more diverse course offerings. Additionally, some students express concerns about tuition costs and the efficiency of administrative support. Overall, Touro is seen as a solid choice for students seeking a personalized educational experience , though improvements in community engagement and organizational support are desired.

reviews.text: Overall a very good experience. The school is really what you make it, if you out in the effort to reach out to the administration and professors, they are willing to be flexible and work with you to tailor an education plan to you. Small class size, and the professors really know you.

I enjoy about Touro that they have a great labs and equipment to learn with. The other students here at Touro, we all look out for each other and it is a great environment for camaraderie and friendship, not competition. I think Touro could improve some of their professors, they are really boring.

Touro University stands out in several ways, particularly for students who are motivated to move efficiently through their education. The student body is generally high quality, with many driven, professional, and focused individuals who take their studies seriously and support one another. One of Touro’s biggest strengths is its efficiency—you can complete programs relatively quickly, which is appealing for students who want to enter the workforce without unnecessary delays. That said, while the structure is streamlined, the quality of the academics can sometimes leave something to be desired. Some courses feel more procedural than intellectually rigorous, and the depth of instruction may not always match the pace. Overall, Touro can be a practical choice for students who value speed and efficiency, but those seeking a more robust academic experience may find certain aspects lacking.

My overall experience with Touro University has been very positive. The programs are well-structured, the professors are knowledgeable and supportive, and the learning environment is diverse and welcoming. I’ve gained valuable skills and feel well-prepared for my future career.”

Touro is a wonderful school especially based on academics. I previously was unable to enjoy certain subjects that I enjoy now. The professors care and want u to understand and pass so they make sure to help you in any way. I appreciate all staff working there although I have only been there for a short period of time I can tell that they want you to succeed and I can’t say that about many other schools that I have attended. My class have decided that we are all going to stick together to make sure that we all make it through the program which is a good feeling because I do not feel like I am in a competition. If we see a fellow classmate that needs assistance we make sure to help them instead of leaving them behind and that is the kind of environment that you need to succeed. I enjoy going to school and on the days that I do not have school I am sometimes upset because I wish I could go to school. I appreciate this experience and I will make sure to recommend Touro University to all.

I appreciate the commitment to helping students and the strong connections with alumni. These qualities are not just slogans at Touro University; they are realities evident from the moment you step into its environment. My experience has shown me that Touro is more than a place to earn a degree—it is where growth, community, and opportunity intersect in meaningful ways. One of the most valuable aspects is the small class sizes, which foster personal attention and engaging discussions rather than large, anonymous lecture halls. I always felt like more than just a number; I was part of an intellectual community where my voice mattered. Beyond academics, the university provides excellent student support services that meet academic, personal, and professional needs. Tutoring centers, writing labs, and advisors are readily available to help students navigate challenges, succeed in coursework, and make informed career decisions. Touro truly invests in student success.

Touro university has a great graduate program for education. I am currently taking all my classes online and using the canvas program. The professors are always available to answer any questions you have. The assignments require you to interact with your classmates which makes it feel like you are not doing alone! Again, I believe it is a great university.

did not have a good time here. the administration treats students and faculty very poorly and made the environment unsafe. their social media does not do a good job depicting this university at all. Do not go here

As a new graduate student so far I have had a great experience with the online interviews, the admissions counselors and no problem in contacting the financial aid office. I would love for the information for the 2025 school year to be updated and available and the course catalogue of dates and times to be available sooner.
Very nice experience. Would definitely recommend for someone who wants a rigorous academic education.
Teachers are super friendly and always looking to help you learn and grow as much as possible. Good curriculum as well.

This school has the most careless and disorganized administration I have ever come across. They rarely answer phone calls or emails and when they do they can’t help you. If you are applying to their radiology program be prepared to figure out a lot of things on your own during the application process as there is no guidance. I haven’t gotten into the school yet but if their treatment of applicants is similar to their treatment of their students, I have no desire to attend.

I’ve been trying to enroll to this school for the past month, and so it’s been so frustrating with the staff. I keep sending them my transcripts and and the persons in charge of receiving it, I guess aren’t computer savvy or don’t know how to find emails. Then there’s also a lady I speak to on the phone who’s very rude and it seems like she doesn’t want to do her job. For the past month I’ve been trying to get a link from these people to take admissions test and no one there seems to be available. I have one more day to get this link and no one is there to help. If I lose out on attending the fall of 2025, I’m going to enroll elsewhere. This school is a disappointment.

Attending Touro University has been a meaningful and enriching experience for me. One of the things I appreciated most was the supportive environment created by the professors and staff. Many of the instructors are passionate about what they teach and genuinely care about their students’ success. I also liked the small class sizes, which allowed for more personalized attention and stronger relationships with faculty and peers.
Touro’s focus on both academic rigor and real-world application helped me feel prepared for my future career. The university also offers flexibility, which is a huge plus for students balancing work, family, or other responsibilities.
Overall, my time at Touro was positive and valuable, and I would recommend it to students looking for a school that combines academic values with a strong sense of community.
I had an amazing experience at Touro. The teachers really care for the students and want them to succeed. Touro fosters a warm environment. Everyone at Touro feels like family.

ai_summary: At Trevecca Nazarene University, students often highlight the strong sense of community and the supportive nature of the faculty . Many reviews note that the small class sizes allow for personal relationships with professors who genuinely care about student success, providing a welcoming and engaging learning environment . However, some students express disappointment with the campus amenities, citing a lack of activities and mediocre food options. The university's Christian foundation is a double-edged sword ; while it fosters a tight-knit community for many, it may feel exclusive or overwhelming for non-Christian students. Overall, Trevecca's close proximity to downtown Nashville and focus on personal and spiritual growth are seen as distinctive aspects of the college experience, but some reviewers also mentioned a need for improvement in academic rigor and campus facilities.

reviews.text:
No one hangs out on campus. Students stick to themselves or are cliquey. There's no social life at all. This may be due to flat-rate tuition for up to 18 credit hours. Most students take all 18 to finish before the 4-yr scholarships end. They're exhausted! Financial Aid is unhelpful & adds random fees. Dorms are hot/humid & except for UC & Benson, they're infested with roaches, ladybugs, mice, or mold. Campus food is disgusting & food deliveries are tough because dorms lack addresses.
Only 26% enrolled are Nazarene & sadly many are legalistic. It's difficult to make godly friendships & there's no sense of community. Academics are mediocre, gen-eds fill too quickly & some professors are verbally abusive. Attendance policy is strict! If you miss 1 class too many, it's an automatic fail even if excused or if you have an A. Pros: The campus is pretty, peaceful & walkable. Also, I had more good professors than bad. Unless you're Nazarene or don't want friends, I do not recommend Trevecca.

In an absolutely horrible area. Campusbis easily accessible and spread out over blocks with tons of houses, hourly hotels, and projects surrounding it. It use to be a really good school but has changed so much. They focus so much now on online students, adult learning, dual enrollment that their core undergraduates deal with excessive class sizes, limited professor engagement, and athletics have changed. You're in the same city as Vanderbilt, but you AREN'T Vanderbilt athletically. Women's programs are actually ran well and respectfully. Men's, well just ask about their transfer statistics.

During my time here I’ve observed that some faculty do not consistently reflect the university’s mission, operational goals, values, or educational objectives. Trevecca has strong potential, but current actions don’t always align with that promise. In education, it’s important to set aside personal biases and support every student who is motivated to succeed. If one cannot do that, education may not be the right field.
Academic quality is mixed. Several professors have had a meaningful, positive impact on me through their teaching, while others rely heavily on slides and offer limited real-world application. Housing could be improved; the cafeteria is showing progress. The student body can feel cliquey at times, which sometimes makes the environment resemble high school. Given the considerable cost of higher education today, it’s especially important to treat students with respect and support their goals.

I was a student athlete and felt like I had a very full and fun college experience! The people I met, staff and students, were amazing. When I was there I would have wanted to change the cafeteria scene, but they have recently changed that.
This school is a cult. The deans show pure favoritism and pick and choose who they want to punish. Come here if you want to be controlled and have no freedom.

I was verbally abused my peers and professors. They have a lack of decorum, they really don't care for their students. They are just presenting themselves into a character of false naivety, one which presupposes that there is actually meaning behind getting a college degree. I hope no one comes to this college, you won't change your life.
I love my school, but just like everything else, it has its issues. There is a strong sense of community, but that ends when Friday rolls around. The campus is a ghost town.

Trevecca Nazarene University has impacted me both academically and personally. I am grateful for all the professors who challenged me to enhance my understanding on the lectures. Most professors find a way to apply their lessons to real life scenarios and relate them to Christian principles. The environment in my female resident hall has been overall positive, but the building conditions do need improvement (e.g. the laundry room has a limited amount of machines and the heater doesn't work very well on the basement floor where I lived during the winter). Nevertheless, the women dorm rooms have a pretty decent amount of space for two people and I like how the beds are adjustable. I also like how my roommate and I only share the bathroom with two other people. There isn't really a big party scene, but the Quad has so far been a great place to hold big events. Not much complaints about the food; sometimes they make international dishes which brings diversity into play. Good school overall

Trevecca has a beautiful campus with lots of opportunities to get connected with clubs and make lasting friendships. Trevecca has been a place where my faith grew and I got to know GOD in a new a deeper way.
I love the school, not much to do on campus during the semester and then all events are planned all at the same time

Trevecca Nazarene University is a great Christian college located in Nashville, Tennessee. It's a place where students grow in their faith while making lifelong friends. The campus has a strong sense of community, and it's easy to meet people from different backgrounds. Living on campus is convenient, with residence halls that keep you close to classes and activities. The dining options, like the Apple Dining Hall and The Hub, offer a variety of meals. However, some students feel there could be more variety to accommodate different dietary needs better. Trevecca provides a welcoming environment where students can grow academically, spiritually, and socially.

I love being at dear ole TNU! Trevecca has been the perfect fit for me because the class sizes are small enough for the professors to know your name. I strive for personal relationships, and between the students and professors I have already made life long relationships in the year I’ve attended!

So far, I have had a wonderful experience at Trevecca Nazarene University. My professors have inspired me, and my courses have challenged me. The community on campus is so lively and engaging, and the community off-campus is "neighborly." I have thoroughly enjoyed my time here and can attribute that to the spiritual growth, social groups, and academic progress I have made. I think overall the school could use more full-campus events and time spent together in prayer and praise for the Lord. The college already does so much for spiritual growth, but to see it do more would elevate the campus.

Trevecca offers an outstanding experience for students, particularly in its Business Department and dining services. The Business Department excels in preparing students for real-world success, offering a well-rounded curriculum, experienced faculty, and opportunities for hands-on learning that set graduates up for thriving careers.
The campus dining services are equally impressive, with The HUB standing out as a favorite among students. The variety of fresh, made-to-order meals and the relaxed atmosphere make it a great spot for grabbing a quick bite or enjoying time with friends. The renovated dining hall has also been a game-changer, boasting modernized facilities, improved traffic flow, and a wider range of delicious, high-quality options that cater to diverse tastes and dietary needs. Date night is a must event.
Trevecca truly combines academic excellence with a vibrant campus life, making it a top choice for students looking for a holistic university experience!

Trevecca always seeks out ways to welcome new students. Speaking from my experiences who looking for a comfortable and nurturing place to pursue my studies. I am a student came from the background of international Vietnamese student. Trevecca was so thoughtful in creating an international student council community to host and mentor students in need.

ai_summary: Tufts University offers a unique blend of academic rigor and community engagement, with students generally expressing appreciation for the collaborative spirit among peers. Many reviews highlight the passion and intelligence of both the student body and the faculty, who are often described as approachable and dedicated to their students' success. While Tufts is recognized for its strong academic programs, particularly in fields like International Relations, some students report challenges with the social scene and a perceived lack of inclusivity, particularly regarding race and class dynamics. Facilities, including housing and dining, receive mixed reviews, with some expressing frustration over maintenance issues and the overall student life experience. The location near Boston adds valuable opportunities for exploration and engagement, yet some feel that the college could enhance its social offerings and student support services. Overall, students find that while Tufts has its challenges, it fosters a supportive environment for those willing to engage actively in their education and community.

reviews.text: Really hands off, professors care but don’t understand how to teach or provide reasonable deadlines. Plus is area is nice, biggest draw is the food is by far the worst I have ever had. Frequently just walk out of dining hall and order or find something else. It’s terrible. Overall recommend looking elsewhere

My impression of Tufts University is that it is filled with students that are passionate in whatever they are willing to achieve. Whether that is academics, athletics, arts or music, the student body composing the campus is diverse and welcoming. The professors, at least the ones that I experienced, are all very supportive and classes can demand a high level of academics depending on the course. Tufts is a small liberal private college that offers world class academics and a great environment for a wonderful campus life.

I love this school because everyone has their own unique talents, interests and passions. I have made friends from all over the country, and the world! Campus life, club culture, and academic learning are all vibrant parts of being a student, and I've been more than satisfied with the opportunities and openness of others.

Tufts University is a place I have come to call home. Although the party scene isn’t as big as other schools, college is what you make of it. Reach out and make connections and you will succeed here! Everyone is welcoming and friendly.
The amazing professors that Tufts have been able to get are SO great! I took courses with the commissioner of the DEP and the Director of Strategic Planning for Secretary General Kofi Annan!!

My favorite thing about Tufts was the non-cut-throat atmosphere. Unlike many other schools of similar caliber, I never felt like my classmates were out to get me or that we were trying to push each other down to raise ourselves up. It truly felt collaborative and I think that was a great way to learn. In terms of changes, I would love to see more job/career fairs offered. I think there were only really 2 offered (one in the fall and one in the spring), and if there were others I did not hear about them. I honestly felt kind of lost when it came to finding internships and jobs and I did not really know how I was supposed to go about finding them.

I am a Tufts University freshman 2025 student, and I have been here since this September. It's a great school for anyone who wants to learn arts and sciences; I had picked computer science, and I am enjoying every minute of it. The university is very close to those of Harvard and MIT, and they also offer cross-registration courses. The food is halal, which is my preference, but it gets boring. Apart from that, I have friends from Tufts, Harvard, and Northeastern. Being able to be close to top universities and having countless friends is a good opportunity of the school, and I really enjoy every minute of it. It's worth studying here! I am also under financial aid, and it covers almost everything. If you want to explore and study in Boston, this is a good traditional school with a good community.

Tufts is a great school with a great small school atmosphere. Social life is tough but it is good for a medium sized liberal art university. Dining hall food might be the worst, many accounts of bad hygiene and sanitation with utensils, undercooked chicken and unknown pellets pieces within protein dishes.

So far as a new graduate student in the Civil and Environmental Engineering department, I have been exposed to so many incredible minds. I am getting involved with many graduate student clubs and organizations while ensuring I utilize every resource possible to me. The courses are rigorous and taught very well. The professors are all incredibly sweet and understanding. Tufts is an incredible school that provides its students with every resource they need to succeed.

Do not enroll in the cellular agriculture gradute certificate. The program is terribly run, with issues spanning enrollment to advisors to curriculum. The name that they advertise and David Kaplan and his lab, he will be your advisor, and you will never meet him. He teaches no classes within the program curriculum, the grad students (not even professors) teach the courses. He almost never replies to emails, as well as requests to be advised. The majority of classes are under the nutrition school (how this ties into cell ag I will never know), but you cannot even enroll into them because your program does not give you permission, this is something they have not fixed in years. When asking professors what I should do if I am interested in any related topic, they say go to another school. I hope you will be better than I in heading this warning of staying away from this poorly managed, planned , and money grabbing program. I hope this program gets cancelled, it would save everyone time.

Tufts University offers safe spaces to be open. For a small private university, Tufts' staff offer many resources for students, whether its the career center for figuring out a possible major, to academic advisors who talk to students to develop plans during the school years. Tufts University is has a large network of people where students and staff stand up for one another.

Tufts offers a lot of campus resources for every type of student. I enjoyed that there have identity centers for a wide range of identities including: Indigenous center, Asian American Center, LatinX, First center, and many more. They also have different events going on for even more activities too. One thing I think they need to work on is broadcasting their resources so students can take advantage of them.

I love the supportive nature of Tufts students. As soon as you step foot on campus, you are surrounded by individuals who actually care about you and your well-being. The professors and other faculty are also extremely helpful. And the food is amazing.

Great professors ,beautiful campus, awesome community ,tone of work lol.
Very happy with Tufts University.

My experience at Tufts University has been a mix of positives and challenges. One of the things I really appreciate is how the school empowers students to self-advocate. Whether it's academic adjustments, financial concerns, or personal needs, I’ve felt encouraged to speak up for myself, and I've usually been met with support and flexibility.
Financial aid at Tufts is a major strength. They truly try to meet full demonstrated need, and being a low-income, first-generation student, I’ve experienced firsthand how that commitment makes higher education more accessible. The staff are generally helpful and accommodating, especially when it comes to navigating complicated processes.
Finally, socially, I’ve found it harder to connect with the party scene here. Tufts has a predominantly white student population, and as someone who doesn’t always relate to that environment, it can feel isolating at times. I’ve had to find smaller communities where I feel a stronger sense of belonging.

ai_summary: Tulane University is distinctively positioned in the vibrant culture of New Orleans, offering students a unique college experience that combines rigorous academics with a lively social scene. Many students appreciate the strong commitment from professors, who are often described as caring and invested in student success, although some departments, like chemistry and mathematics, receive criticism for inconsistent quality. While the campus is visually attractive and often noted for its outdoor beauty, students have voiced concerns about dining options and dorm conditions, suggesting there is room for improvement in those areas. Student life at Tulane is characterized by a mix of social engagement, with a focus on community service , although some reviews indicate a need for more inclusivity , particularly for underrepresented groups. The balance of academics and social activities is frequently highlighted, making Tulane appealing for those seeking both educational growth and a dynamic college life.

reviews.text: I am going to be attending Tulane this fall and had the chance to tour it last weekend. I loved the campus and staff whom I got to meet and learned so much about the school!
Strong PHD program for Arts and languages, especially for French. Professors seem very whole hearted, and students are curious

I loved my experience at Tulane University. My professors were thoughtful, dedicated, and consistent in their teaching. Tulane provides a welcoming and supportive environment for students to grow both personally and academically. I gained a well-rounded education through Tulane's core curriculum, but was also able to focus more specifically on my majors and minors. Additionally, Tulane allows students to major and minor across academic schools/areas of interest, which provided me with ultimate academic flexibility!

Tulane University is known for its party-culture and party-scene. The university has the #1 College Campus Bar yards away from the school's campus. As my first-year began, I did not believe this party-culture was an aspect of Tulane I needed to worry about. As the year progressed, I caved more and more to this "play hard" mindset, steering away from academics. I knew this was not a lifestyle fit for myself, especially with the major I chose to study. With that, I have steered away from this party-culture to focus primarily on my studies because of how much it costs to attend this school. After I chose to switch mindsets, social life became difficult to navigate because most students are hooked on this "work hard, play hard" mindset, but I am not. My studies have allowed me to meet and known individuals better, especially individuals with the same mindset as myself. Tulane can be a lot at times, but it provides a wonderful education that continues to be my primary focus.

Overall , this campus can be exciting and challenging at the same time. Courses here push you outside your comfort zone. I like to see the community on this campus come together for different events , gales, clubs, etc. The extracurriculars at Tulane also push community engagement and fun . Overall , this schools academic rigor takes up most aspects of students days and nights.

Tulane PD, President Fitts, the chief and captain, condone racism! They allow ADA noncompliance. They don't respond to emergency calls. They allowed a perpetrator to get away after my vehicle was vandalized. Their shift change and protests were more important than to save a life. Tulane University would rather see an alumni dead than to accept responsibility!
Best choice ever. Tulane has not disappointed me at all. Amazing location, people and overall atmosphere.
The graduate school downtown lacks a space for students to purchase food while on campus. Food options in the area outside the building are limited and do no provide a lot of variety.

Overall, I've had a positive experience at Tulane. Tulane's academics and career services have been the best part of my experience so far. Most of the professors here really care for their students, communicate well, and are genuinely good at teaching. My workload as a Political Science and Economics double-major is typically manageable with my work and social life, although workloads vary with every major. The career services here are far superior to neighboring colleges, although their accessibility differs (Business students are given much better resources). As a New Orleanian, I feel that Tulane culture is very separate from New Orleans culture. The tuition price is the worst part of Tulane. Tulane often pushes increasingly expensive services on its students like overpriced textbook bundles and requiring students to get unlimited meal plans after removing more affordable options. Despite this, I still feel that Tulane has lots to offer and I'm happy to be a student here.
My experience at Tulane University has been very rewarding and challenging. The campus is beautiful, the professors are amazing.

I begin at Tulane this upcoming fall with the baseball team so I have not stated there yet. So far what I have seen of the campus, it looks very nice and I know I am going to have a great experience at Tulane.
Not very diverse but some great professors. I think the new housing and meals are better now. I really enjoyed my friends.

I love the city and I love the academics, those are the two best parts of the university experience. A lot of the time, I find the party scene to be a bit too much for me, so I feel alienated from the people who take partying very seriously. The campus food is pretty awful, but the culinary culture of the city makes up for it.

I enjoyed my time at Tulane University! I loved the campus life, the large amount of events hosted every day, the various services offered for students, the inviting community, and much more. My only qualm with the school is that it is has incredibly intelligent and talented professors that do not align with a lot of students' learning styles.

Tulane is very much a school if you want to work hard and play hard. The party scene is very strong but the academics are also challenging and demanding. If you are someone who prefers staying in for a board game night over exploring the city on the weekends, Tulane is probably not the school for you. However, there is definitely a group of people for everyone. The academics are very straightforward, and demand the ability to fulfill specific requirements over a more holistic learning model. I.E., you need to be able to accomplish the specific tasks that a professor demands of you but having a deeper knowledge of the subject through exploration is not really prioritized. Because Tulane is such a unique place with so many cool opportunities, it definitely attracts a really interesting group of students, which is one of its biggest upsides.

ai_summary: Union University receives a mix of feedback, particularly regarding its academic environment and community feel. Many reviews highlight the dedication of the professors , noting their willingness to engage personally with students and support their academic and personal growth. The small class sizes allow for meaningful connections , which many find comforting in their educational journey. The campus is described as beautiful and inviting, with modern dorms that enhance the living experience for students. However, there are concerns about the high tuition costs and the perceived lack of financial support, such as scholarships. Students also express a desire for more diverse student representation and varied student life activities, as some feel the atmosphere can be exclusive or narrowly focused. Overall, Union is praised for its Christ-centered approach and community spirit, while simultaneously facing critiques about its inclusivity and social dynamics.

reviews.text: Union is an incredible school with incredible faculty and staff! You will be pushed and challenged, but also cared for deeply and looked after by those around you.

My overall experience experience has been great. I love that I get to attend classes online and during the evening. It’s been so convenient for me.
I would like to see more campus life activities for the evening students.
Union is a decent school . I’m literally there cause that’s where God wants me to be . It could use more diversity . The teachers are cool .

For a Christian school this is sick, they treat the students who are different and not book picture perfect as animals rats in cages the edge program students (disabled) gets no life im part of it with lower autism and I get treated like I have no idea what’s going on like a little kid not just me the rest of my buddies here too there’s a student worker very very religious but can never get Sundays off and the school doesn’t trust her bc she has piercings and a tattoo that resembles her dream of becoming a mortician, doesn’t look like a Barbie but has the sweetest heart, do better and treat us like humans because we are humans just like you, I may walk, talk and even sound differently but ik what I say ik how to do simple life things just like you I can communicate just like you stop treating disabled students and students workers with interests that doesn’t hurt anyone but just because you don’t like them and treat them like crap, make a difference, God bless

My experience at Union University has been very positive overall. I really appreciate the supportive faculty, the strong sense of community, and the faith-based environment that encourages both academic and personal growth. The smaller class sizes make it easier to connect with professors and classmates, and I feel like the school truly cares about student success.
One area I would like to see improve is the availability of more flexible scheduling and expanded course options, especially for students who are balancing work, family, and school. More resources for affordability and student activities would also be a great addition.
Overall, Union University provides a welcoming, Christ-centered learning environment, and I am grateful for the education and experiences I’ve received here.
Union has blessed me with an enriching academic experience, forged deep friendships, and, most importantly, deepened my relationship with Christ through the GO trip and inspirational chapel services.

The professors and the entire university staff here genuinely care about their students and want to see them succeed and get the absolute most help they can get- wether that be financial, academic, spiritual or personal.

My time at this college has been a letdown. The experience didn't live up to my expectations, and I often wish I'd made different choices. If you're considering attending, I'd advise you to think twice – especially if you're not pursuing a teaching or social work major. The science programs, in particular, seem to have issues with sexism and ineffective teaching methods.
What really gets me is that the school promised a lot of things that never materialized. It feels like they oversold the experience and underdelivered. The lack of transparency and follow-through on commitments makes me feel like I've been misled. The campus atmosphere feels surprisingly similar to high school, which was a disappointment for me. If you attended a private high school, you might find the similarities jarring. Given my experience, I wouldn't recommend this school to anyone looking for a fulfilling college experience.

I absolutely love Union. It was really important for me to have a Christ-centered higher education experience, and that is exactly what Union offers. Faculty and staff’s love for the Lord seeps into everything on campus, and it is beautiful.

I grew up around Union and am closely connected to Union before even going here. However I didn’t realize just how much I didn’t know about Union until I came. I have built such strong Christian friendships through Union University. I am involved in our student activities council so I get to not only be involved with the students but i get to be involved in the making of the events to bring campus together as well. My classes have been good, some professors better than others, but that’s true wherever you go. I’m a STEM major so I spend a lot of time in lab and the lab facilities are great. Lots of space and equipment, and lots of students there to work together and instructors that want to help you and answer questions!

Union University is a small Christian college known for having the best business department in the area. As a transfer student, I’ve fallen in love with the many opportunities the university offers and am excited to see what I can accomplish during my time here.
Although I enjoy being at Union, commuting to and from campus daily has been challenging—especially since my car is constantly breaking down. With the money, I could put it toward buying a new car, which I am currently saving for.
UU offers a great, Christ-centered environment while maintaining a high standard of education. Professors are gentle and readily-available to help when needed.

I'm in the nursing program. This is the worst school. The professors know you need at least all A's or B's to be excepted in the nursing program so they will give you C's, they never offer any extra credit and never understands emergencies. Do not ho here. They give you low grades to hold you in school longer to keep getting money.

Union has been an incredible experience for me, allowing me to connect with new friends and allowing me to learn effectively in school. Since I am severely hard of hearing, Union has granted me the opportunity to use its resources to learn and keep up with the class effectively. The campus life is also great, giving students many chances to destress during finals week and also allowing students to engage with others. The food is great and the housing is even better, with everyone having their rooms and shared space with three other people. I am very happy with Union University.

In all honesty, in my overall experience as a student at Union University, I have no complaints or concerns at the moment, but one thing that I can say is that the staff that I've had in my corner during my process has been breathtakingly awesome and a breath of fresh air. They were patient and took their time with me when most wouldn't, and for that, I appreciate them greatly.

Ana G Méndez is a Bilingual university where I strengthened my English learn—reinforced my professional vocabulary, which helped me on my interviews for new positions in the industry.
With that degree I obtained new opportunities to grow as a professional.

Iam really is very good experience because the University established acomodo razones because iam diagnosed fallo renal Tips 3 and the University is saved me tranquility mentally and emotionally. Definitely the University whit me reddit apology. Thank you NICHE iam happening because really me situation is serious and your helping me really is logarithmic alcanzar me finish estudien Social WORK CLINIC. Thank you so much

Ana G. Méndez University in Gurabo is an incredible place! I love the supportive professors, diverse academic programs, and the welcoming campus environment. The hands-on learning opportunities and commitment to student success make it an amazing place to grow. The culture, sense of community, and beautiful surroundings truly make it feel like home!

My overall experience at Universidad Ana G. Méndez - Gurabo Campus has been both enriching and transformative. The academic environment is supportive, with dedicated professors who are passionate about their fields and genuinely invested in their students' success. The campus itself is welcoming and provides a sense of community, which has been essential for my personal and academic growth.
One of the most valuable aspects of my experience has been the access to resources, such as tutoring services, academic counseling, and extracurricular activities that have helped me develop both professionally and personally. Despite facing challenges, particularly regarding transportation, the university's accessible trolley service has been a significant help.
Overall, my time at Universidad Ana G. Méndez has been an opportunity to expand my knowledge, build meaningful connections, and prepare for my future career.

La Universidad Ana G. Méndez en el campus de Gurabo ofrece una experiencia enriquecedora. Me gustó la atención personalizada de los profesores y la diversidad cultural del campus. Las instalaciones son modernas y los recursos académicos son excelentes. Además, hay muchas actividades extracurriculares que fomentan la interacción entre estudiantes. Sin embargo, me gustaría ver una mayor variedad de programas de estudio y más oportunidades de pasantías para conectar con la industria. También sería beneficioso mejorar los espacios de estudio y áreas comunes para crear un ambiente más colaborativo. En general, una experiencia positiva.

Loved studying there, hopefully will be doing Master's soon. Proffesors were very proffesional and understanding towards deadlines of materials or exams t9 be turned in. Most of us study after having to work an eight hour shift, have children and they also required care and other activities. Even though minors were not permited in all classes it was a life saver when I had to take them with me. The modules for the courses of each section were very well understandable and had materials as reference. In case you missed a class you could utilize them to get back in track. Some Proffesor made their own and made sure you got those in handy. As for the facilities, everything their was up to date for presentations and class. The blackboard is very user friendly for tests, forums, presentations and essays to be uploaded and turned in. The email provided will keep you in contact with Proffesors and classmates also would alert for the incoming materials and turn ins and accsess to 365 platform

At Ana G. Mendez Gurabo Campus University, I experience a vibrant atmosphere that fosters both academic and personal growth. The campus features modern facilities and engaging classrooms that make learning enjoyable. I appreciate the diverse student body and the numerous clubs that encourage participation and leadership. The faculty is dedicated and approachable, always ready to support us and engage in meaningful discussions. My classes blend theoretical knowledge with practical applications, making the learning experience rewarding. The variety of events, from academic seminars to cultural celebrations, creates a lively social scene that enriches my time here. I’ve formed strong connections with fellow students, fostering a sense of belonging and camaraderie. With a commitment to academic excellence and holistic development, Ana G. Mendez Gurabo Campus truly prepares me for my future career while allowing me to pursue my passions in a supportive and enriching environment.

Its really pretty and big university, not a lot of variety to choose careers. The personal is not that good at making classes schedule as they dont take in consideration the students time. The professors are good at teaching, and making feel comfortable to the students to open up and ask questions of the desire topic they need help with. I have had some professors that are tired of teaching the same course over and over and let the students know about it and its not good to let the students know that since can affect the way the students see you as a professional. I wish they could add more careers, since its the university which is close to me and im used to it. I wouldnt like travel to another town just to be able to get the career i desire to be a professional. I also wish they added more places to eat inside of the university, since everything gets full at lunch time and theres basically no place to sit or enjoy the food, or get a place where people can get snacks .
The professors are for the most part helpful and flexible. The gen ed coursework is pretty reasonable. Little to no electives on most degree programs.
It was really nice being part of the recruitment process. Easy method of aplying. Felt welcomed. Amazing academic program! 100% recomended!!!!

I think a lot of great professors are taken for granted, by faculty and by students (including myself at one point). I had an amazing time with the research opportunity I had, but I think they should talk more about those opportunities because students are left in the dark. I was able to find it because of another student. I applied to the work and study program for 2 consecutive years and they never contacted me. I even went to the library for 2 weeks, almost everyday, because I was trying to get on a list for a job, and they kept telling me to come back later, until the last day when they told me they had just sent the list and I couldn't get on it. I don't think it's a bad university, but just keep in mind the students have to stay on top of things and insist on what they want/need. The faculty, staff, employees are nice (in my experience) and it has a good program variety.
Best system I’ve ever studied in ! Great professors, Great campus, everyone is super helpful, I 100% recommended.

Stay far away from this university. Nobody can graduate in 4 years because no matter how smart the student, almost all students repeatedly fail their classes depending on their major. It is extremely difficult to find a STEM teacher who is truly responsive and dedicated (there are a few but still).
The departments of chemistry and mathematics are the worst I've encountered, with a complete lack of sympathy, little to no grade curving, and almost a disdane towards their students.
After maxing out my efforts with more than 30 hours a week spent on independent study, I still scraped by with barely Bs. I transferred to an ivy league college after where it is much more reasonable, just so you know how difficult it can get here.
Great private school! It has everything a student need to develop his career, the professors are very prepared to enhance you in your path to develop what you need to do for the work force.
Every profesor is very considerate and very understanding. Even if you want to group up after class for problems or projects

ai_summary: At the University at Albany, SUNY, students highlight the campus's beauty and its welcoming environment , often describing it as a community where they feel a sense of belonging.
