Academically, experiences vary; while many appreciate the supportive professors who genuinely care about student success, others express dissatisfaction with the large class sizes and perceived lack of engagement in some programs, especially within the STEM field. Student life is vibrant, with a wide array of clubs, events, and a notable party culture, though some feel that a greater variety of late-night activities could enhance the experience. While most agree that the campus is diverse and offers ample opportunities for involvement, concerns regarding campus safety , the quality of dining options, and the adequacy of support services, particularly in academic advisement, are frequently raised. Overall, students emphasize that success at UAlbany hinges on personal effort and engagement with available resources.

reviews.text: I always wanted to go somewhere far but not to far, since I lived in the city I thought this would be a perfect area to learn my independence. This has be the best decision I have made, I made friends that I call family. I have met my boyfriend of three years now. My academics has been very important to me and I loved every second of it, by supportive professor, getting additional help. Overall, I really glad I put myself out there to go away for college to experience this.

Overall, I truly have enjoyed my experience at Suny at Albany so far. As I was coming from out of state, I was nervous about getting adjusted with academics and finding my place but the environment of the school helped me get settled quickly. With the help of my professors, advisors and the people around me, I was able to have a great time as a student here.

The idea of going away to a new place to call home can be terrifying, but when you arrive on campus and the bright, warm sun shines its rays on your face, you realize that the idea is now your reality. All the work you have put in, which has brought you to this place, was all worth it. This starts to ease your mind because although this experience can be terrifying, it is only that way because it is new, that's the beauty of college it is a new place that you can use to reinvent yourself and grow as a person. Being here has helped me start to become the person that I want to be. From learning the right material that I need to succussed in my major thanks to the wonderful professors, to coming out of my shell with help from the welcoming community I was able to build with my roommates that helped turn this room into a home. I love UAlbany for all that it has to offer, and I wouldn't change anything in the world.

It is a somewhat affordable college to attend. As of this moment, the college does not really give anything new that other colleges do. If you're looking to get your degree without any setbacks, then it's a good choice.

I really appreciate the comradery of the students in classes and the dorms. I think the studies are challenging and the professors are very supportive of my needs. Having so many options for majors is really a great benefit as I am now considering a double major.
My experience at the University at Albany was eye-opening. I have learned a lot while being at the University, and at first it took some getting used to but once I got used to it I adjusted easily.

My experience at the University at Albany has been largely positive. I appreciated the diversity of the student body, the strong academic programs—especially in areas like public policy, business, and the sciences—and the accessibility of many professors who were genuinely invested in student success. The campus offers good research opportunities, a solid library system, and helpful career resources.
There are areas for improvement. Some administrative processes can feel slow or confusing, and communication between offices could be clearer. I’d also like to see continued investment in campus facilities, dining options, and mental health services. Expanding hands-on learning opportunities and improving campus transportation would further enhance the student experience overall.

I love my school for the diversity in students and clubs because they are very flexible and cater to the interests of all people. Academically, assistance in and outside of classes was available, and I never felt that I wasn't being assisted in some form. There are many personalities and people of varying backgrounds, which makes the campus very lively. The only thing I would change is more transportation to campus because of the snow, or extending the tunnels. Also, providing more classes that are after 10 am, because frequently I would get forced to take 8 am classes. I also wish personal resources on campus were advocated for more, so students knew they existed.
UAlbany is a very diverse school. I have made long lasting friendships and have learned a lot. There are endless opportunities at this school and I wouldn't trade it for anything.

I can't recommend this school enough! It was simply wonderful. The people are friendly, lots of wonderful clubs, professors genuinely care about students! 10/10! It could be safer, and the food is mid.

My time at UAlbany has been really good overall. I’m a junior in Computer Science with minors in Business and Informatics. The CS program can be tough, but the projects felt real, and most professors are helpful if you actually reach out. Being here has helped me grow a lot academically and personally.
Living on campus was a big part of my experience. I got to meet a lot of people, and build a small community that made the campus feel like home. Thsocial and convenient, and the late-night conversations, events, and overall environment made it easy to connect with others.
I also used a lot of campus resources like CAPS, TimelyCare, tutoring, and the career center, and they really helped when I needed support or guidance. There are tons of clubs and leadership opportunities if you look for them. I do think things like class registration, and dining variety could be better, but overall I’d recommend UAlbany to students who get involved and make the most out of the experience.
Honestly, it is a real great school for sure, the campus life is great its the college experience in itself I would say but just gotta stay motivated in order to not miss classes etc..

Attending the University at Albany has genuinely been a rewarding experience. One thing that stood out to me right away was the diversity on campus. You meet people from so many different backgrounds, and that makes discussions in and out of class a lot more interesting.
Academically, the professors push you to think for yourself rather than just memorize information. Many of them bring real-world examples into their lessons, which helps you actually understand why the material matters. There are also tons of research opportunities and helpful resources like tutoring and academic advising that make it easier to stay on track.
Campus life definitely keeps you busy. There are always events happening—clubs, sports, guest speakers, cultural activities—and the campus itself is easy to get around. I also appreciate how the school encourages students to explore internships in Albany and even New York City, giving a good mix of campus life and career preparation.

My experience at the University at Albany, SUNY has been largely positive. The university offers a diverse and inclusive community with students from all over the world, which creates a vibrant learning environment. The faculty are knowledgeable and supportive, and many professors are deeply invested in their students success both inside and outside the classroom. I also appreciated the wide range of academic programs and research opportunities available, especially for students who want to explore interdisciplinary studies.
If there’s one area for improvement, I would like to see continued investment in campus housing and dining options, as well as more career development resources tailored to specific majors. Increasing hands on learning opportunities, such as internships and co-op programs, would also enhance the overall student experience.

UAlbany is an academically rigorous institution. It’s relatively diverse and offers a decent amount of support to its students. There are many student groups to join and many on campus events to attend.

ai_summary: The University at Buffalo (UB) offers a dynamic and diverse environment, with a wide array of academic programs and extracurricular activities that cater to a broad range of interests. Many students appreciate the supportive atmosphere provided by professors, highlighting their dedication to student success—though some feel that large class sizes can make personal interaction challenging. The campus life is vibrant, with numerous clubs and events available, although opinions on the party scene vary, with some noting it has been restricted. While students enjoy the many resources and opportunities for experiential learning , including research and community building, some have expressed concerns about the food quality and rising living costs on campus. Overall, UB seems well-regarded for its academic value and community, despite some operational challenges that students believe could be improved.

reviews.text: My experience at this university has had its ups and downs. The campus is large which I personally like, yet at times it feels very inconvenient and spread out, especially during times when the weather is harsh. The winter does get extremely cold, making it hard to stay motivated. There also is not much to do on campus, despite it being large in size. Overall, there has been many great moments I have shared with others while attending this school and I have most certainly met people I will stay friends with throughout my life, it just isn't the place for me.
The University at Buffalo remains a low cost alternative to many other schools while still providing an great education.

I believe that UB is the best school in the SUNY system. Not only has the diverse amount of opportunity allowed me to find my passion of pursuing medicine, but it allowed me to explore other fields as well, while growing as a person. The academic scene, though I have heard depends on the specific department, has been extremely welcoming and many faculty members are there to see you succeed. Though the north campus (my primary campus) is very ugly, it is very functional. There is a bit of disconnect between the north and south campuses, which I have found myself commuting between the campuses, and the downtown campus all in a single day, which can be very draining. Food on campus is very good in comparison to other campuses from what I have had, and heard; though there tends to not be any late night options on campus, leaving those without cars stranded. Overall, it is a very good school to figure out what you would like to do with your life, but some programs are better than others.
Wonderful school, getting into JSMBS is my dream for the program on pursuing my career of being a Cancer Biologist. I know this is the route for me.

I truly enjoyed the experience of meeting new friends and observing the differences in teaching styles compared to my high school days. However, one aspect I would like to see improved is the food options available.

My experience was pleasant. Large classes were difficult to manage but if you make an effort to get to know faculty and students they want to know you. Though in the student life there’s always something to do. The student services always has events planned. Overall it’s an amazing, well rounded school that has a good campus life but also a good area when leaving the campus.

I loved all the resident assistants, and the professors were incredibly helpful. Everyone on campus was supportive and always willing to offer advice. It truly felt like a community where people wanted to see you succeed. It was also very easy to find my people and build a great family here. The environment made me feel welcomed, included, and encouraged to grow both academically and personally.

It's been fine thus far, I've only just started attending the university. It has been a learning experience that I am growing to appreciate because many don't get to experience going to a well respected university. Like the one I'm going to so it has been, A process of getting to know my school and getting comfortable with going there.

UB is a great college with safe campus and great academic background. The college is one of the best part of Buffalo, and while Buffalo itself is not that great, UB makes it brilliant, with lots of diversity, clubs, activities and various events throughout the year. The academic side is also great, with plenty opportunity of research, and career.

One of the best choices I’ve ever made. Great experience to attend Buffalo. I would recommend it to anyone wanting a great environment to learn and grow. It has a beautiful campus that is conducive to studying. It is also very diverse in student body and has wonderful teachers and staff that are supportive.
I think UB is definitely the most valuable university in U.S. especially for international students. Hands down! It hasa a convenient campus filled with diverse student body.

I think my buffalo experience has been interesting being a EOP student I stayed on campus over the Summer before my freshmen year I got to meet new people and make friends that I have to this day. I partied studied and have had a overall pretty good time here away from my parents and all of my family alone to live and thrive. Getting used to communication with many different others and building strong bonds and relationships.

I have really enjoyed my time at the University at Buffalo. I got involved with an on campus job very early on and really found out more of the behind the scenes. I think it is an inclusive, safe environment and really gives the "campus" feel. There is a social group for literally anyone as long as you are willing to look. It gets a little cold in the winter but to remedy the transportation is more developed than most other schools I know in NY. UB has a large presence in the local community and Buffalo is a really good place to enjoy college life. I would recommend my school to anyone who asks.

Here is why I say you shouldn’t go to this school, not only will you meet some of the best teachers you’ll also experience some of the most degrading professors in your life. They feel they hold enough power to literally decide the rest of your life. They will claim to do everything in their power for your best interest and leave you questioning your entire existence. If you are a person of color save your sanity because it is majority white. Let alone don’t go into the medical fields I don’t even just mean they are stimulating they are offensive and you can’t have a social life. The food sucks so learn how to cook or order takeout. Unless you live off campus which isn’t much better (villas on rensch) housing is scarce. No one cares about your safety. The police just intrude on people’s lives having a good time. If you don’t have a car you have to depend on other either making friends that do or ubering which is expensive. There is absolutely nothing to do for nightlife in Buffalo.

Nice party scene, decent sports scene, nice campus. However, the financial aid office and student accounts office here is absolutely no help when it comes to paying off your e-bill. I am trying to register for classes, as well as transfer after my first year is done. Please be cautious of accepting your offer.

ai_summary: The University of Alabama - Birmingham (UAB) offers a diverse and inclusive environment , attracting students from various backgrounds. Many reviews highlight the strength of the academic programs , particularly in healthcare, with dedicated professors who are eager to support students. Student life presents a mix of opportunities , especially through clubs and organizations, although some feel that campus life can be lacking, particularly on weekends. While the downtown location provides access to amenities, students express concerns over safety, food options, and parking. Overall, UAB is recognized for its supportive community and comprehensive educational resources , making it a solid choice for those pursuing careers in health and science fields.

reviews.text: I really love how determined everyone who goes there is, from students to teachers it feels like a driven environment

Nice dorms and good friends and professors. The food in the cafeteria definitely needs improvement. If you are interested in the medical sciences, there are a lot of opportunities to volunteer and gain experience.

I've really enjoyed my experience at the University of Alabama at Birmingham so far this year. I have joined and consistently participated in several service organizations and found good friends. This food at UAB is hit or miss at our dining hall but there are a lot of alternative option, so it balances out! I like the opportunities UAB offers to all students in means of events, sports, and organizations.

I had a great experience at the university and would recommend to other students to go there. I have family who have gone there because I had a positive experience and made some of my best friends there.

UAB is a fantastic college; I've already made friends I feel that get me. The homey feel of the campus is astounding; everyone is so so nice and welcoming, and I feel safe and safe to be myself and not a person that needs to act like a character to try to fit in. The classes are very comforting; that's not how everyone would describe a classroom setting, but it makes me feel like I'm a participant rather than a student that is just setting their taking note. It makes it a lot more fun. Overall, UAB is an experience that I very much enjoy and thrive in.
Overall I love UAB! This college strives to bring comunity and realationships between students. The professors are pretty good too. However it is either they care or they do not care no in between.

Being a student here brought joy into my life. It has opened doors for me to meet new people and learn new things about different cultures. It's very easy to make new friends because of this. Even the professors have been excellent. One thing I would like to see change is the closing hours for certain spots. Other than that, there wouldn't be a thing to change.

Diverse population. Rigorous academics. Research opportunities abound. My engineering class is collaborating with a medical department to design a product that could positively change people’s lives for the better and I get to be a part of that!! Highly recommend.

My experience with UAB was overall satisfactory. Seeing as how I graduated high school a year early and was taking a gap year, I appreciate being able to speak with counselors regarding my scholarship and being allowed an exception to receive a presidential scholarship. However, after the first interactions I did have to remind them of the deadlines to submit (after doing everything I could and was waiting on them). I would appreciate if that could be improved but overall I had a pleasant experience with the University of Alabama at Birmingham.

My time at UAB so far has been filled with joy, chaos, and my fair share of breakdowns. When I first moved onto college, I was greeted with a beautiful and diverse campus. Everyone is very approachable, and southern hospitality is not lost, although we are in the city. Making friends was not hard at all, but I had the backbone of the marching band to help me along. I quickly integrated my way into the lifestyle of this university and was granted the resources to accomplish whatever I needed.

Overall, my experience at UAB has been a mix of learning, growth, and adjustment. Being a freshman has definitely come with challenges, especially balancing classes, volunteering, and personal time but it’s also been really rewarding. I’ve met supportive people, gained a better sense of what I want to do in the future as a CRNA, and started to feel more independent. Even when things get stressful, I know I’m in the right place and moving toward something meaningful.

The University of Alabama at Birmingham is a very diverse environment with multiple opportunities to participate in clubs, internships, shopping, and interventions. When I first arrived to campus, I was greeted with bright smiles and welcoming atmosphere of being accepted. The buildings are very clean, including the green areas all around the school. There are some nearby restaurants, so if you wanted something different you can try new foods. The professors are very kind and helpful, and the library is absolutely beautiful. I liked the diversity and amount of clubs you could join. There were a lot of opportunities to explore yourself and there was a lot of tutoring for people who needed it. I feel like the campus could change the length of the food places closing time because some students get out really late and won't get the chance to eat. I also dislike how the campus will fill their trash in several trash cans when the dorm hall's trash compactor is closed because it's very dirty.

This school is not for those who need a stepping stone into college. It’s not for first generation students who know nothing about navigating higher education. The education itself is both inefficient and difficult for the amount of money you pay. Financial aid is non existent.

I think that UAB was the perfect place for me to learn a grow both in my professional and social life. I couldn’t be happier with the people I had the pleasure of knowing while I attended this incredible university!

One thing I like about UAB is the labs. The biology labs include a variety of materials including live specimens. I have genuinely enjoyed all labs i’ve taken. There are always a lot of events. My favorite event I attended was stargazing and s’mores. One thing I would like to change is the dining options. The UAB dining hall food is not the best, and there aren’t many restaurant options. At least they recently added a Chick-fil-A. I also wish they would build new upperclassmen dorms. They have built several new dorms for freshman, but none for upperclassmen. I think there should be at least one new upperclassmen dorm. UAB classes can be very expensive sometimes.

ai_summary: The University of Alabama in Huntsville (UAH) is well-regarded for its strong academic environment , particularly in STEM fields like engineering, nursing, and science. Many students appreciate the supportive faculty who are dedicated to helping them succeed. The campus is described as beautiful and modern, fostering a friendly atmosphere where students can easily get involved in various organizations and activities. However, some reviews highlight challenges like limited parking , the need for more diverse extracurricular options, and questions about the effectiveness of student services and advising. Students value the university's close ties to local industries , providing ample opportunities for internships and practical experience. While UAH is a great fit for those focused on science and technology, it appears less accommodating for those pursuing humanities or non-STEM majors. Overall, students seem to appreciate the personalized attention they receive, but recognize that improvements could enhance their experience in several areas.

reviews.text: UAH is a pretty small campus, but that does not limit the amount of on-campus activities you may get involved in.

My experience at Alabama A&M University has been a mix of growth, challenge, and real development both academically and personally. Being on “The Hill” has taught me how to balance school, work, and life while still staying focused on my goals. The environment here is supportive, and the faculty really push you to think deeper and take responsibility for your education.
AAMU also gave me a strong sense of community. Whether it’s campus events, conversations with classmates, or support from professors, I always felt like I had people around me who wanted to see me succeed. The culture, traditions, and energy on campus really shaped my experience and made it feel like more than just a place to take classes.

The University of Alabama in Huntsville is a pretty good school to go to, especially if you are near the base. From the base its like a 10 minutes drive from there. The school is overall great: polite staff, perfect garden-scape, quite places in the library. Everyone is very helpful and can anwser any question you might have.
It’s a struggle. The financial aid office comes across like they don’t want you to succeed, rather they just want you to be in debt. That’s America isn’t it? Pay lots of money to get nowhere.
I love UAH, it has a really friendly community and a great campus. Very walkable, reasonably sized, and safe for all students--on foot, skateboard, scooter, etc.
The University of Alabama in Huntsville is a great school with many classes that can be in person or online. The food is amazing and there are always many options.

UAH was an academically focused university. I liked that it did not try to sell itself as something other than what it was. You got what you expected. For those who want to go to college to be educated, it is a great option.

My overall experience at the University of Alabama in Huntsville was largely influenced by the art department, as well as the art teachers' connections with the students. I like that there are many events and clubs to join to be social and productive. What I would like to see changed is a greater presence of various art companies at career service events.

My experience with UAH is that of a prospective freshman so the only real exposure I’ve had is from my orientation. But the experience I did have really stuck with me and made me want to chose this college. The people, atmosphere, food, and even the housing left a good to great impression on me. I like UAH and want to go there for next four years

The University of Alabama in Huntsville is a research school at its core. It is a dedicated to inspiring students to get into their studies and graduate. The school is in a safe city, with wonderful activities nearby off campus for fun. The campus itself isn't too large, and it is easy to navigate.

I liked the community it presented and how safe I felt emotionally and physically. Here they care about you moving forward in your career which I believe is truly important to the development of our minds and practices.

Overall my experience has been okay. Theres not many resources for help and financial aid as compared to a lot of schools. and it is really small and small minded for a university with the exception of the humanities. I would say my learning experience has been good overall.
The campus breathed safety for students and the quality of education is unbelievable for students they stuff us up for success

My overall experience at the University of Alabama-Huntsville has honestly been great. The school has impacted me in three major ways. First, job opportunities. Huntsville is filled with tech companies like NASA, Boeing, and Lockheed Martin. These companies shaped me to think about how I will be making an impact in the world through tech. Second is academic opportunities on campus. I work at the UAH Propulsion Research Center Help Desk and hope to join the Cyber-Physical Research Lab soon. Third, athletics. I was a sprinter and was able to continually be around my friends; train with them, suffer with them, and travel to different universities like Clemson and UAB with them. However, there would be things I would like to see change. Campus life is pretty dull. While there are some activities to do, this is a very academic school. Most events revolve around some sort of STEM concept, not really designed for casual socialization. Regardless, besides those, my time at UAH has been great!

I pretty much love it here so far. It’s a pretty standard commuter university with a lot of potential to grow alongside Huntsville’s growth. Greek life isn’t competitive and unlike how it is at larger universities. The community and social scene is a lot more tight knit as well since it’s a smaller university. Most of the staff is also pretty helpful (besides a few bad professors of course). I would say that overall, it’s a pretty chill place to live and learn.

ai_summary: At the University of Alaska Fairbanks, students frequently highlight the tight-knit community and supportive environment on campus, where both faculty and staff demonstrate a genuine interest in student success. The academic programs have their strengths, particularly in fields like STEM and Native Alaskan languages, with many opportunities for hands-on experience and research. However, some students express concern about limited course offerings, especially within certain majors, and the increasing tuition costs, which have not translated into enhanced resources. While the campus is praised for its beautiful views and welcoming atmosphere, the harsh winter climate can be challenging for some students. The student life is described as active but may lack the vibrancy seen in larger universities, with many wishing for more events and social activities. Overall, UAF offers a unique educational experience shaped by its Alaskan location and community spirit.

reviews.text: The price of attendance as a resident of Alaska to go down includes the materials for classes. The cost of food on campus is expensive.

I have had an amazing experience at UAF! Everyone is very helpful and I have not had any problems. I love the online classes. I have not been on campus much; however, the museum is very cool and Alaska is incredible, especially the weather.

My experience with the University of Alaska Fairbanks has been pretty good so far, though I am only in my first year. I love the third spaces available and the various and diverse activities offered to students on campus. Housing is decent, but sometimes maintenance can take a while and spaces aren't cleaned every day. The wifi is hit or miss, but is generally okay as long as you leave yourself enough time to submit assignments. It could pose a challenge for fully online students at times.
I had to switch my major from fire science to medical coding. Advisors were super helpful and had my best interest at heart
Lots of different student life activities, and multiple made to order food options to choose from if you live on campus with the meal plan.
Art program dismissed real art and displays AI generated art. Doesn’t help teach art or drawing techniques, doesn’t master anything but making prompts to steal ideas from online.
It has been an overall good experience, the advisors and teachers are knowledgeable, and the courses are comprehensive.

Online and in person classes. Social work program and professors were amazing with a lot of support. Registrar and Bursars offices are quick to respond and really helpful with any and all resources and questions.
Social work classes are all online, it made the last couple years feel disconnected from the UAF campus.
Art classes are great as well, the museum has expansive archive of amazing art.

I am very disappointed with my experience at UAF. It has been nothing but a delayed disaster. For one I transferred. I had one great advisor when I started, she retired and I got a new one... Terrible. It was the reason I wasn't one year behind but TWO. Not to mention my professors were not helpful. I had one professor who I had emailed about an extention due to weather. She said no problem, she then never added it to the grade-book when I did turn it in, and made me fail the class. (I had As on all other assignments)
Overall it has been the worst experience of my life. It has ruined my mental health and stirred me away from what my dream career is. I can't stress enough how this has been the worst decision of my life.

The University of Alaska Fairbanks is a well funded university that offers a lot of experience opportunities for engineering students. There is a NASA funded lab here called the Alaska Space Grant which works on extraordinary projects each semester. The courses are generally well created and the professors are passionate. The main downside to UAF is the extreme cold and lack of activities in the small city of Fairbanks.

UAF is a great, well-rounded university. It is really easy to connect to peers because it is a small campus. The classes are small, so it is great to connect with your professors. The student body is always hosting a campus event with tons of engagement. There are also lots of university clubs. My favorite thing is the amount of on-campus resources for students, from medical services to academic advising; they have your back!

UAF has offered me a chance to truly find what it is that I wish to do. Their leniency and understanding of students goals and aspirations is something to behold when it comes to UAF. Nice staff, friendly people, fun events. I couldn't have asked for a better college to start out at.

Honestly as a Native student I expected there to be good recognition of our Native communities being that campus is on Native land. I experienced a lot of racism and was often looked down at because of my culture and knowledge of history.

Online student, new this year so not fully certain of what I would like to see change. The only real issue I have had was with the financial aid department, and it was more like dealing with a rude employee than a school issue.

The University offers a variety of clubs, smaller events, and student recreation areas. Everyone is very accommodating and willing to help students who are struggling. Resources are available, but it may be a bit difficult to find them.

ai_summary: At the University of Arizona, students generally appreciate the supportive faculty who prioritize their success and provide engaging coursework across various academic programs. The campus is often described as beautiful and bustling, with a strong sense of school spirit, lively student life , and numerous opportunities for involvement in sports and campus events. However, some reviews express concerns about the overall safety of the surrounding area, as well as the lack of diversity among the student body. Tuition costs and student housing affordability are also highlighted as significant issues. Despite these concerns, many students find their experiences transformative, valuing friendships and community connection fostered during their time at the university.

reviews.text: My overall experience with University of Arizona was amazing. Tucson is only 2 hours away from the main city of Phoenix, but once you spend more time in Tucson, you start to appreciate and find the hidden gems around town. There are numerous campus clubs to get involved in and find opportunities for your major. I also heard the basketball games are worth! My off-campus stay was convenient and affordable, with four separate bedrooms and bathrooms. It comes furnished and includes a shuttle to campus for only $600-800/month.

I received a late admit into UA for their direct admin nursing program. Even with a trip in the middle of summer and an extreme heat wave… when I step on campus I knew this was the place for me. The campus is huge and felt like a college campus where some are just buildings in a busy city. The dorms are good and the rec center is amazing. Pool great to tan and have some down time. Sometimes it’s hard to get into classes that fit your schedule as a freshman but if you keep trying the classes can open up. Buy the sports ticket and I enjoyed the football and basketball games. The half time shows are great. Greek life is big here, and you don’t have to join to attend some events. Dining options are good and the mini grocery markets have ready to go meals too. Meal plan works well too. They want us to succeed and there’s many student and tutoring services readily available. I love it here!
I really enjoyed being a U of A student. The campus and city are vibrant and culturally diverse. There are amazing opportunities for volunteering and community outreach. Greek life is great.

The University of Arizona is in a small town, but still has rich culture and diverse populations. This school balances a blend between social outings and academic performance. I stay busy with acapella, about 13 hours a week, course work, while also managing to make time for my friends. This school is meant for people who take their academics and career seriously, but also like exploring different extracurriculars, sport events, clubs, etc. The city of Tucson is the perfect size with stunning hikes just an hour drive away.

Great place to live with sunshine throughout the year. The campus life is great while the education experience could be better. Some of the classes are not worth the money; you can get a better experience in some of these classes at a local community college. There is a bit of detachment by the university on payments, cost, and when things need to be paid on your account. Many students are lost in this process unless their parents are covering cost. Better transparency and HELP is needed with this process.

My experience at the UA has been really great so far. Getting enrolled into the university was super easy after being a part of the orientation meetings, they really walk you through the steps on setting up your college account and how to navigate the websites. As for safety there could be more lighting in areas, as well as lights on the cross walks.
I felt that the professors did care about my success as a student. However, the university allows people spread misinformation with their posters when they're at the mall.

My experience at the University of Arizona has been positive and meaningful, with strong academics, supportive faculty, and a campus culture built on tradition, school spirit, and community. I have appreciated the academic resources, student support services, and opportunities for involvement that help students grow both personally and professionally. Parking and pedestrian safety are very important. Parking is limited and expensive, which creates stress for commuting students and can impact punctuality. More critically, crosswalk safety around campus—especially at night—needs urgent attention. On December 10, three University of Arizona students tragically lost their lives after being struck by a drunk driver while they were legally crossing the street in a crosswalk. While the university continues to evolve and grow, prioritizing student safety and accessibility would greatly enhance the overall student experience.

During my application process, the international students officer was friendly and professional. She was patient with my numerous questions and inquiries. I was offered an admission with a huge tuition waiver. But I didn't get the opportunity to travel for my studies. Up till today, I still get emails from the school. Maybe not today but I will be a wild cat some day.

I really like that there are resources always offered and available to you. Whether it's health concerns, finding a club you like, technical support and so much more, the school likes to make sure you getting the help you need. The only thing I would like to change from my experience at the school, is having more online courses so that there is less commutes to classes and more flexibility.

My overall experience at the University of Arizona has been very positive and enriching. The university offers a strong academic environment with dedicated faculty who are approachable, supportive, and genuinely invested in student success. The availability of research opportunities, honors programs, and interdisciplinary learning has allowed me to explore my interests deeply while building a solid foundation for my future goals.
Beyond academics, the campus culture is vibrant and inclusive, with numerous student organizations, leadership opportunities, and community engagement activities that encourage personal growth. The resources available—academic advising, career services, and wellness support—have made navigating college life both manageable and rewarding.
Overall, the University of Arizona has provided a balanced experience that combines academic rigor, personal development, and a strong sense of community, making it a place where students can truly thrive.
I like the opportunities we are given to seek help and tutoring. I would want to see changes in the safety of the surrounding areas of the college.

The University of Arizona is a wonderful place for growth and improvement. lots of advancements and opportunities are just around the corner and is up to students how they like to take advantage of this options for their careers upon graduation. i would say is a great school, although my only complain is to wish they bring back some of its programs from the past. As an Art student myself, i enjoy passing time at the Art building close to the UAMA. is a fun and creative spot for designers and painters alike. aspiring graphic designers like myself look forward for more technical spots and tools where our creative will draw beyond the limits. overall, the University of Arizona is a great school full of improvement and commitment in helping students pursue their dreams and achieve their future. as the first University in Arizona is safe to say that this college marks the start for a variety of dreams coming from a variety of cultures across the world, Bear down.

My overall experience with the University of Arizona was very good, and it was very easy to make a lot of friends and meet a lot of new people. If you put yourself out there, it was easy to experience a lot of new things

My experience at the University of Arizona has been both rewarding and motivating. The supportive faculty, strong academic resources, and collaborative campus environment have helped me grow personally and academically, especially as a pre-nursing student. Being involved on campus has made me feel connected and confident in pursuing my future career in healthcare.

ai_summary: The University of Arkansas offers a vibrant college experience , noted for its beautiful campus and friendly atmosphere . Many reviews highlight the accessibility and dedication of professors, making it easier for students to form connections and seek help when needed. Student life is lively, with a strong emphasis on Greek life , numerous clubs, and social events, although some students wish for more diversity on campus. While the school is recognized for its supportive resources , especially in academics and student services, parking remains a common concern. Overall, the sense of community and the engaging environment at the University of Arkansas stand out, making it an attractive option for prospective students.

reviews.text:
Overall I’ve loved my time here at the University of Arkansas. It’s given me some of my closest friends and favorite memories! When I was looking at going to school here I was very nervous. My decision felt overwhelming at times, but I took the risk and picked Arkansas. I do NOT regret it.
Yes, there have been times where I’ve been frustrated with professors, the university in general, or the stress of classes. But it’s all been worth it! I highly recommend joining a fraternity or sorority if you want to meet people fast and get involved on campus, Greek life is huge here. If not, clubs are a great outlet too!
On top of that, Fayetteville is a great area to live in! There’s plenty to do up here especially if you include Rogers and Bentonville. I’ve seen plenty of concerts, hiked, gone out with friends, and just enjoyed the area as a whole! If you think being a Razorback is in your future, take the leap and come stay in the Natural State for a few years. You won’t regret it!

During my first year of college, joining Sigma Phi Epsilon has been one of the most meaningful parts of my experience. I was looking for a community that would challenge me to grow beyond academics, and the fraternity has provided mentorship, accountability, and opportunities to serve. It has pushed me to step into leadership roles earlier than I expected as a freshman. One experience that stands out is organizing and creating intramural league teams within the fraternity. Rather than simply signing up to participate, I took the initiative to coordinate team members, manage communication, handle registration deadlines, and ensure everyone felt included. What began as a way to stay active quickly became an opportunity to build unity and strengthen relationships within the chapter. Bringing people together toward a shared goal taught me how to motivate others, manage different personalities, and stay organized under pressure.

For me it depends if you are a very social extrovert or an introvert. Me personally i am an introvert and not too social. You kind of just have to put yourself out there and attend school events. As for academics the coursework can be a lot and overwhelming with some classes but it is moderate. Professors are 50/50 but most of them are pretty good and care. Party scene is nice.

It is a very beautiful area, with many things to do with regards to athletics or organizations to join. There are some professors that are hard to understand because of the accent barrier, but other than that all professors show a strong understanding and love of their content.
Had a blast, would love to see tuition lowered and the complete take over of the downtown area done with more class.

The University of Arkansas has honestly been a pretty good experience for me so far. One of the first things I noticed was how nice the campus is. It’s really big but also easy to get around. There’s a lot of school spirit here too, especially on game days, and it kinda makes you feel like you belong even if you’re new.
Academically it’s been solid. Most of my professors have been helpful and actually care if you’re learning, which isn’t always the case at every school. There’s also a lot of support stuff like tutoring, study rooms, and advisors, so if you need help it’s usually there.
There’s always stuff going on around campus. it’s not hard to meet people or find something to do. Fayetteville is also a cool college town with good places to hang out.
If I had to change something, I’d say the registration process could be way less confusing. But overall I’m glad I picked UARK and I think it’s a pretty good place to be.

I went there for a school field trip on engineering. The guides and the students there were very nice, and the campus was amazing. The diversity over there is astonishing. I don't think I ever saw the same face twice.
The classes and profesors are great, parking could be better, but overall is a very great campus. I will restate that the parking is frustrating
I'm a fan of the University of Arkansas. We've got decent food, good student life, and a so-so athletics program, but a great marching band and drumline.
UofA has given me a great experience. The only issue I have with this school is the lack of parking.

I really like how engaging campus life is for students. It can be a little difficult to get engaged if you’re not part of Greek life, but there are many student-run organizations available that anyone can join. I would like to see the parking services change, because they charge for minor/unclear violations and don’t have enough spaces available for the students on campus. There are plenty places around campus where you can study or hangout which I really appreciate.
Phenomenal graduate level campus, and friendly atmosphere~ overall, great, and very organized faculty/staff.

My freshman year at the UofA has been great. I've been able to make new friends through the classes I'm taking. I started out as a Marketing major and recently decided to change to Psychology. I didn't really love the fact that my classes and times were picked for me in the business college because that meant I was not able to work as many hours the first semester but I've made it work. I would recommend the UofA to others.

A good college for its price but the environment can be toxic. I like the online professors better, as they assign things clearly and respond on time. Being on campus is very isolating, and their honors program is hardcore. I would recommend online learning instead of being on campus.

Overall, I believe the overall experience at the University of Arkansas was like many state universities with an extremely large undergraduate population and a diverse array of activities, students, and opportunities.

ai_summary: At the University of Arkansas at Little Rock (UALR), most students appreciate the friendly atmosphere and helpful professors who genuinely care about their success. Reviewers frequently mention the strong nursing and social work programs , along with a diverse range of degree offerings . The campus is described as average but welcoming, with instances of good facilities, although some mention the need for improvements in dining options and diversity in campus events. Many students value the close-knit community and emphasize the accessibility of faculty, particularly in smaller class sizes, while some had concerns about the management of certain programs, particularly in nursing. Overall, UALR is viewed as a solid choice, especially for those seeking affordability and a supportive learning environment .

reviews.text: I like the school but the professors could be better and more engaged. Not all of the but I’ve had several that are not engaging and that does not drive excitement for students to be their best and do their best work.

The University of Arkansas at Little Rock was strong academically. It had smaller class sizes and professors with real-world experience. Outside of academics, the student experience was lacking. The area where the university resides is not safe. It is very much a commuter college and lacks traditional student activities to a degree.
I got accepted at universisty of arkansas at little rock ill be attending in fall 2026. I plan to major in political science and have a minor in music or business.

My overall experience at the University of Arkansas at Little Rock has been positive and meaningful. One of the strongest parts of the university is the professors, who are generally approachable and willing to help when students are struggling. Many classes focus on practical, real-world skills instead of just textbook material, which makes the coursework feel useful and relevant. Academic resources like tutoring and advising have also been helpful when balancing classes and deadlines.
Because UALR is a commuter campus, the social atmosphere is quieter than at larger residential universities, but the campus is still welcoming and diverse. Students come from many different backgrounds, which creates a comfortable and inclusive environment. While campus life requires more effort to get involved, clubs and events help build connections. Overall, UALR has provided a supportive learning environment that encourages both academic growth and preparation for future careers.

As a student at the University of Arkansas at Little Rock, my overall experience has been positive and manageable for someone balancing school with work, family, and other responsibilities. I really appreciate how accessible and supportive many of the professors are. They tend to be understanding of nontraditional students and are willing to work with you if you reach out and communicate. The smaller class sizes have made it easier to engage in discussions and build relationships, which has helped me feel more connected to campus. I also value the diversity at UALR. Many of those students came from different backgrounds, ages, and life experiences, which created a welcoming and more realistic learning environment. There are a few areas I’d like to see improve. Some campus facilities and technology feel outdated and could use modernization. Overall, UALR has been a solid choice for me academically and personally, especially as an adult learner. With a few updates, the experience excels.
I just transfered to UALR and everything seems just fine right now. The staff and professors are very supportive and I can always reach for help.

UALR is a local university and fits my needs to live at home and attend an affordable college with a degree I'm interested in pursuing. I like the small classrooms, the option to attend on-site or on-line and the available internships as a junior and senior. I would like to see more classes in the real estate and construction management areas that provide a real world experience within these fields.

It has been decent. There are a more professors and staff that actually care if you do well and about your future then not. The ones who are bad are like really bad to where it almost doesn't feel worth it to tough it out. They are also a college who side with professor even if it is medical reasons. For example, if you ask to for extension or an incomplete due to medical reasons they can deny you and if you try to ask people of student resources to advocate for you they tell you it up to the professor and to figure it out basically.

great overall school for the price l. I've loved all my professor and classes although the online courses are not great. overall I highly recommend it if you need a cheeper college with good resources

UALR was an excellent university. The staff was extremely supportive and knowledgeable. The professors were experts. The food was excellent quality. The campus was safe. The dorms were clean and well managed. The CWDSA program was superior quality. I really enjoyed my time here. The amenities were marvelous.

My experience at the University of Arkansas at Little Rock has been great! I enjoy the relationships i’ve built, and they have contributed heavily to my overall success. The professors are helpful and the curriculum is educational and comprehensive.

If you are looking for the typical college experience you can get that at UALR, but what I really enjoyed about it is that the people you are surrounded by are multi-faceted. The average student at UALR is not the typical young adult portrayed in films or shows. In all of my classes there are students upwards of 40 years old that come to class and bring so much thoughtfulness. I have never been one to party a lot or do something crazy for the fun of it. I went to college with the specific intention of getting my degree in elementary education. I thought I would see this time as simply transitional to where I want to be, however in my short time at UALR I’ve met so many individuals who have broadened my perspective on what college offers. It isn't so much about what the professor is teaching as much as it is about what the students are asking In short, I’ve received so much joy and fulfillment from my time at UALR. I'm so excited to see what my next semester brings!

When I first started online classes, I was very nervous I was not sure what to expect since it was going to be my first time. Taking classes online has helped me with managing my time better I can have my full time on, and in my free time I can focus on school.
I was having a hard time in Astronomy but the, but the university offers tutoring and assistance at the university to help you keep up with the class and not fall back.
Because of being military member, the professors are very understanding and work with you as long as you communicate with them.
My overall experience has been positiv

My experience so far at UA Little Rock has been enjoyable. I go to college with friends and have made many new friends. I enjoy all of the things I am enrolled in and commit to outside of my scheduled classes. There’s not really anything I can think of that I would like you to see change.
The campus is always clean and well maintained. The professors are very helpful and usually respond to emails in a timely manner. Completing coursework online is a breeze.

ai_summary: The University of Bridgeport offers a vibrant and diverse learning environment, with strong academic programs and supportive faculty that many students appreciate. The campus is noted for its scenic location near the water, which enhances the student experience. There are plenty of extracurricular activities , although some students wish for more engagement during weekends. The dining options and on-campus housing have received mixed reviews, with some describing them as inadequate, and notable safety measures are in place on campus. While the administration is generally seen as responsive, there are calls for improvements in organization and financial aid services, especially for international students. Overall, while students enjoy the sense of community and the opportunity for personal growth, they also express a desire for enhancements in certain areas.

reviews.text: It’s is diverse and I am meeting students from all over the world. I have had a wonderful experience my first semester. The professors are accessible and committed to student success. The campus is close to transit to New York City and Boston. It is a hidden gem.
I haven't started going to class yet. My academic advisor is excellent. The dorms appears nice. The school is very expensive.

The University of Bridgeport sits on a the beautiful Seaside Park with amazing views of the beach. There is a lot of diversity and inclusion. There is a beautiful health sciences building, equipped with state of the arm technology to enhance the clinical learning experience. Overall a great place to attend.
I have been here for 2 semesters as a transfer student. It’s an ok school. Living on campus can be stressful sometimes. Their washing machines are always out of order.

As an international freshman at the University of Bridgeport, my experience so far has been both exciting and challenging. I truly appreciate how diverse and welcoming the campus community is. Meeting students from all around the world has broadened my perspective and made me feel less alone being far from home. The professors are helpful, and the small class sizes make it easier to learn and connect. I also like the beautiful campus by the water—it’s peaceful and motivating.
However, as an international student, navigating financial aid and housing deadlines has been quite stressful. I would love to see more support and clearer communication for international students regarding scholarships, housing, and other essential services. Despite the challenges, I still feel hopeful about my journey here, and I’m grateful to be part of a university that offers such opportunities for growth and global exposure.

Great professors and faculty that care about students. There is not much on campus life, campus food is not the best. A lot of sports teams and sporting games. The campus is on a nice body of water with great views. Only shared bathrooms and limited spacing. Nice gym and pool. Campus does a great job of throwing events.

University of Bridgeport is a diverse college, from the staff to the teachers. UB offered great programs that cater to working adults by creating accelerated courses that were flexible and manageable. With these accelerated courses, I would like to see more "student life" engagements provided to students who are looking to build a more relative professional network.

While no experience is perfect, and there were times I felt overwhelmed or stressed, what stood out to me was how the university empowered me to push through. There were moments when I questioned if I could keep going—especially while juggling kids, classes, and life—but the environment at the University of Bridgeport reminded me of my strength. It gave me the space to grow, the tools to succeed, and the inspiration to never give up.
This journey has made me proud—not just of my progress, but of the person I’m becoming. And for that, I’ll always be thankful to the University of Bridgeport.

My experience with the University of Bridgeport has been encouraging. As an international student from Nigeria, I was excited to be admitted into the BSc in Health Science program. The application process was smooth, and the admissions team was very responsive and welcoming. I admire the school’s strong focus on healthcare, which aligns with my dream of becoming a doctor. The campus seems diverse, safe, and academically supportive. I would love to see more financial aid opportunities for international students and clearer guidance on on-campus work. I’m excited to start this journey and confident that UB will help me grow into a compassionate and skilled medical professional.
So far my experience has been wonderful my professors are great and the staff and facilities at University of Bridgeport are wonderful
I’m an alumni, returning grad student. I love the flexibility of classes, now that I live out of state & can still continue by education there. Professors are supportive & understand life happens.

My overall experience at the University of Bridgeport has been a very good time. As someone who has a history of social anxiety, I feel very welcomed and open to be myself. The professors are very helpful and you can tell that want you to understand the material. I don't eat at the Dining Halls often, but I do like the fact that there is a Starbucks and a Subway on campus for alternative options. UB has a diversity of students from all over the world. Making new connections with people from other countries is so interesting because you get to try new foods, learn new words, and even pick up outfit trends.

This is my first year at UB. I just finished my first semester and I can say that so far I have had such a good experience so far. Since the day I have moved in I have had such great support and mentors. I have great RA's in my building, a great RD, great advisor, wonderful professors, three amazing lacrosse coaches and have met some of the best friends/teammates. I enjoy the purple knight family because its a happy and inclusive environment. Campus life has been very enjoyable so far, I spend most of my time in the weight room, on the lax field, in the common room with friends playing video games, watching movies or studying, in the library, in the gym/field watching the other sports teams, at the bowling alley or E-sports lounge. I don't have many complaints about UB at all, besides that Campus food is not the best but luckily each dorm room in the building comes with a Micro-fridge or we have a communal kitchen. UB was my best decision, Im getting a great education while playing lax

My experience UB is like no other. I typically start off my day by heading to the Heckman center to study on some work overall UB has multiple places for students who grow and learn as well as great resources such as the wall through library and tutoring at the fifth floor of IT. This university has shifted my overall working experience. And is giving me opportunities unlike no other.

The University of Bridgeport offers a wide range of courses for many of the most popular career paths. It is also incredibly diverse which allows people from different races and religions to feel comfortable. The athletics programs are on the rise as well. UB competes at the Division II level and attracts outstanding student-athletes from all over the country and from all over the world. The school has made an investment which is currently ongoing, to update its facilities. UB has committed to improve its athletic facilities as well as certain academics and housing accommodations which is much needed.

ai_summary: At the University of California - Berkeley, students often describe the academic environment as challenging yet rewarding , with many praising the dedicated faculty and rigorous coursework. The campus is noted for its beauty and diversity, fostering a vibrant community with numerous clubs and extracurricular opportunities. However, some students find the large class sizes and competitive atmosphere isolating, which can make personalized support feel lacking. While many enjoy the vibrant student life and the proximity to San Francisco, concerns about safety and the quality of housing are commonly mentioned. The dining options receive mixed reviews, with many suggesting improvements are needed. Overall, UC Berkeley is viewed as a place that offers incredible academic opportunities but comes with its share of challenges regarding student life and support services.

reviews.text:
I was a non-traditional student from an overseas country. While its academic reputation is top-notch, I would warn anyone who comes from OOS, particularly any state that has more balanced political views.
In my honest opinion, the entire state isn't all about liberalism, but rather that's something more selfish.
This is a pure diabolic stronghold that justifies violence; attempts to expand their weird democrat propagandas.
More than 80% Berkeley undergrads are Californians who think they are the center of the world and the US, just because they have racial diversity.
Berkeley needs to remember that they grew and thrived on Fed govt's aid during the Manhattan project too.

My overall experience at UC Berkeley has been great. I am a transfer student, so I think I was able to adjust well with my class, professors, and classmates. There are many opportunities for students to thrive in and explore. I would like to see changes in the dining department, as the food is either good or bad.

UC Berkeley is a phenomenal school to be in; surrounded by so many intellectual people, so many opportunities available. Classes are fine. Housing is okay, could get stuck with poor roommates/suitemates but still manageable. It is also very expensive.

I love Berkeley. The students are so lively and kind for the most part. Some drawbacks are the laundry and the food. As someone who wants to eat healthy and is a bit picky there isn’t much options to eat. I have to go grocery shopping a lot in order to get good food in which sucks because I have a dining hall plan. As well the dorms in Stern are pretty good and spacious. The only thing is the laundry is way to expensive for a low income student and there is only one laundry room for the whole building which sucks because when you want to wash sometimes there isn’t machines or dryers.

I'm very grateful to have had the opportunity to attend UC Berkeley. Although the surrounding area is not the most glamorous and the food scene is rather lackluster, the academic environment has more than made up for the school's shortcomings. There is no shortage of wonderful and brilliant professors, and you will spend your time surrounded by peers who value their education. My courses here at Berkeley have taught me valuable skills in critical thinking, research, and more. A great addition to the student experience has been BayPass, which expands public transit access for students across the entire Bay Area. When I'm not in class or studying, I've enjoyed taking trips to San Fransisco with friends thanks to this addition. Part of me is dreading graduation, because I know that I will miss my time here. Go bears!

My experience here at Berkeley has been a journey for growth, nonetheless. I have been able to overcome obstacles that allowed me to redirect my academic career. I have been fortunate to reach out to my therapist to help with the emotions that allow me to feel balanced both academically and personally.

University of California, Berkeley has so many opportunities for whatever field you are interested in. There are plenty of clubs, organizations, and research opportunities to get involved in. If you have a job, it can make these opportunities inaccessible sometimes, because you typically need to dedicate a lot of time to the interview process and training (even for clubs). It's good to consider that over 70% of the campus comes from a very wealthy background and the opportunities on campus reflect that. Work study jobs are also quite competitive. Basically, the more time and resources you have to spare will greatly impact your experience.

I really like UC Berkeley; the academics and competitive environment is something I really like. At UC Berkeley everyone is constantly pushing each other to be better, everyone is constantly improving and I think that is really cool. Perhaps the worst thing about UC Berkeley is the amenities offered to the students. The dining halls are awful and often have undercooked food and the other free campus eateries often make me sick. The dorms are super outdated too, a lot of the stalls on my floor are broken and the lounges are very uncomfortable. I think the school prioritizes academics over all else, so the libraries are really nice but the actual housing at least in my experience has been pretty bad. The lounges in each floor at least in my unit, I am in unit 3 are very bad and outdated compared to the other UC campuses.

My experience at the University of California, Berkeley was both challenging and formative. Berkeley pushed me intellectually while also forcing me to grow personally, there’s no coasting there. The academic environment is rigorous, fast-paced, and deeply interdisciplinary, which helped me learn how to think critically across domains rather than in silos.
Beyond coursework, Berkeley shaped how I view impact. Being surrounded by students who care deeply about social responsibility, innovation, and equity influenced the way I approach problem-solving and career decisions. At the same time, navigating a demanding environment without a traditional support system taught me resilience, self-advocacy, and independence.
Overall, Berkeley wasn’t easy, but it was meaningful. It gave me a strong analytical foundation, exposed me to diverse perspectives, and helped me develop the grit and curiosity that continue to guide my professional goals.

My experience has been so good at UC Berkeley. Cal has great class choices, good reputable professors, and is all around impressive. The dorm is good too with many food options, clean, and I've made many friendships I hope to be lasting. The Berkeley orientation was an amazing five days filled with information and not only did I have a great time and made so many new friendships, I learn just about everything needed to know about the campus and area. Each day I continue to learn and enjoy this prominent campus and my education.

My experience at UC Berkeley is not typical. I transferred from a community college as a junior studying Integrative Biology. I have loved the integrative Biology seminars and how personable and understanding many of the teachers here have been. As a transfer student, your time at UC Berkeley goes by very quickly, making it a challenge to build meaningful friendships and take advantage of the opportunities this school offers. Before coming, really research what UC Berkeley provides to make your experience the best it can be. The general behavior of people here can be quite antisocial and a little awkward, so it takes time to find your people.

I think that the academics make any downsides completely worth it. Some professors can be harsh but most of them are great and experts in their field. Of course, the food and dorms are not great at all but you only have to deal with that for your freshman year. Campus life is pretty good, there are lots of clubs to join and the party scene is very active
Berkeley has great professors and the campus is beautiful and accessible for commuters who take Bart.

UC Berkeley is defined by a culture of unrelenting academic rigor and discovery. As a premier public research university, it places students at the forefront of their fields, often learning from Nobel laureates. This creates a high-pressure "grind" where the curve is steep and expectations are higher. While the intensity is daunting, it fosters a unique brand of resilience and critical thinking, teaching you not just how to solve problems, but how to question the systems behind them.
The campus is a vibrant, often chaotic melting pot of activism and history. Life revolves around Sproul Plaza, where the legacy of the Free Speech Movement remains palpable through daily protests and spirited debates. This energy extends into the city itself—a colorful landscape featuring iconic landmarks like Telegraph Avenue. Despite the school's size, students find community through niche clubs or late-night study sessions, creating a social fabric that is unapologetically bold.

My overall experience at the University of California, Berkeley has been challenging, eye-opening, and deeply formative. Berkeley pushes you academically in ways few institutions do. The rigor of the coursework, especially in STEM, forces you to develop discipline, resilience, and strong problem-solving skills. While this can feel overwhelming at times, it also prepares you exceptionally well for future academic and professional paths. I’ve grown not only intellectually but also personally by learning to manage time, failure, and pressure in a high-stakes environment.
Overall, Berkeley is not an easy place—but it is a meaningful one. If you are self-motivated, resilient, and willing to advocate for yourself, Berkeley offers immense opportunities for growth, learning, and self-discovery.

ai_summary: At the University of California - Davis, students often highlight the welcoming atmosphere and friendly community , making it a great place for social engagement and forming connections. The campus is beautifully maintained and encourages a bike-friendly lifestyle , promoting sustainability and outdoor activities. Academically, students find the professors knowledgeable and passionate, although experiences can vary widely, particularly in larger classes. The university offers a rich array of extracurricular activities , from over 800 clubs to engaging sporting events, fostering a sense of school spirit. While some students appreciate the diversity and inclusive environment, others express concerns about the quarter system's fast pace and the occasional mismatches in course quality. Dining options and housing costs are frequently mentioned, with opinions on food quality ranging from satisfactory to poor. Overall, UC Davis provides a unique blend of academic rigor and community spirit within a charming college town setting.

reviews.text: My experience at UCD was amazing. It did have a rough start with the pandemic and online courses. However, the classes, students, and professor were helpful. I felt safe in the small town and enjoyed the location. Those four years at Davis taught me a lot about life, education, and overall everything.

Davis was such a fun town to live in and the college made it better. I wanted a college town, close community feel and I got exactly that at Davis. If I had to choose a college all over again, I would pick UC Davis time and time again.
I enjoyed my experience at UC Davis. I met some really helpful professors in my area of study that later helped me with recommendation letters for grad school.
I believe that this is a good fit for those who do not live in California that want to expereicnce the life that is in California

loved the campus, the atmosphere and most of the the education was amazing. I learned so much and the rigor of the school I feel really prepared me for my future studied. A great sense of community and very safe.

I loved attending UC Davis. It is located in a college town, making it perfect to connect with other students your age on campus and off campus. It is in a small, quiet town, but its proximity to Sacramento, CA is perfect for night life and good restaurants. The campus culture is full of community, happiness, and kindness. The staff and faculty are experts in their field and are helpful to students. I loved my experience at UC Davis, and miss it very much.

Coming to Davis has shaped my life in numerous ways, through the people, experiences and food. The people at Davis were the first thing that catched my attention with how kind and welcoming everyone is, I was pleasantly surprised. I have been able to meet many like-minded people through the clubs and opportunities on campus which has expanded my view on life. Moreover, I have been able to explore more about myself and understand what I would like through the material taught in classes. However, I will admit I do wish there was more guidance and support from the admin on resources about majors and deciding more about what a student would like to do in the future. Alongside, the people and opportunities, the food at Davis has truly shaped my experience as well where it has allowed me to taste new cuisines and also learn how to be super creative with my meals.

The students here are extremely hostile, rude, and have no passion for studying and learning. They are all terribly poor mannered and do not respect others' personal space and general human rights. Definitely do not recommend attending this school unless you have some sort of magical power of understanding school material with no effort and do not care about your overall wellbeing and mental health. A 0/10 environment and an even worse social experience.
Quiet college town strong academics in horticulture ( wine making), vet, biology. Close to San Francisco Bay Area and Sacramento. Not much night life options.

My overall experience at the University of California, Davis has been positive. The academics are challenging and encourage critical thinking, and the campus is beautiful, bike-friendly, and has a strong sense of community. Most professors are knowledgeable and supportive, especially if you seek help through office hours.
However, large lecture classes can feel overwhelming, and some courses move very quickly. I would like to see more built-in academic support and clearer communication in difficult classes. Overall, UC Davis is a great school for students who are proactive and motivated.
I believe it was an excellent experience overall. Plenty of on campus or close by housing options and the vibes from the school are very friendly.

UC Davis reminds me of home, which is why I decided to go here. With the thriving life of agriculture here, it has a familiar smell and environment that makes me feel at ease. Life is quiet and calming, but the social relationships I have developed here in Davis makes it much more lively and fun. The professors and the school community is extremely supportive and offers repeated services to students in need of any help -- mental health issues and housing. I enjoy my time at Davis and am grateful for the opportunities it has set out for me.

I have just started my college pathway but its not too bad overall. I heavily recommend choosing your professors carefully because I had some not so great ones and some phenomenal ones. Housing is okay, you get to meet tons of cool people which is nice and the food is pretty good as well. I recommend visiting downtown during free time because its really cool and there are a lot of good eateries down there.
I would recommend Davis to anyone who gets admitted into Davis for their undergraduate education. Very cozy college town.
UC Davis is a top ranked, world class Public Ivy. It is ranked #1-#30 in many fields of study. #1 Agriculture and Environmental Sciences, #2 Ecology and Evolutionary Biology, #1 Vet Science.

ai_summary: UC Merced has a distinctive charm with its close-knit community and small class sizes, which make it easy for students to connect with professors and peers.
