Many reviews highlight the opportunities for academic growth , particularly in research and specialized programs, although some students wish for more major options and resources, especially in pre-health fields. The campus is noted for its environmentally sustainable design and modern facilities, providing a pleasant and safe study environment. However, students express concerns about the limited entertainment and dining options in the surrounding area, making campus life feel somewhat isolated. Despite these drawbacks, the supportive atmosphere , diversity, and commitment to student success through various resources and financial aid are frequently mentioned as positive aspects of the university experience.

reviews.text: Great school with plenty of passionate and effective faculty. Parking is awful especially in the heat, requiring long walks across campus leaving me sweaty by the time I get to class. The campus and downtown area are on opposite sides of the city which is a big downside for me. A car is necessary to enjoy both city and campus.
Over all it’s a great school. There’s always something to do so your never bored unless your finished with all your work. It’s just a very calm campus.

It was alright. The food needs to get better and the surrounding area is not that good. There aren't that many places to go to in Merced and if you don't have a form of transportation you have to take the bus, which limits your options even more. The professors are hit or miss. Some of them are good and some make you not want to attend college anymore, but it seems about average to me.

I personally am quite enjoying my time at UC Merced. I'm not the best person to ask about the campus life or student life, but the academics are pretty good, and I do feel like I am learning a lot from there. The scenery is also really nice, and pleasant to walk through when I have a class or two cancelled. However, one thing I would like to see changed is the removal of the push for AI across campus, from the clearly AI generated posters to the AI workshops.

UC Merced is the perfect school for a first generation student. It is filled with support programs and guidance counselors. It is close to nature and students take advantage of it. I hope to see the continued growth and the Medical School.

My first year at UC Merced was life-changing. I went by so quick. I still wasn't sure of my major but I chose to major in Cellular and Molecular Biology.The classes were complex,for me personally but nothing caught my attention as did science. I made amazing roommates,met new people in my classes, became independent, created memories. I loved the quiet environment in UC Merced. It's a vast and quiet place but the university is filled with many resources and support. Professor's are kind.Many clubs and events on campus. It's not so lively though. Not much to do but I hope to see a change and see more events and social gatherings for students to allow unity and relaxation.

UC Merced has beautiful views all throughout campus, and the faculty are all esteemed in their respective fields. The best part is that you can connect with these professors much more frequently than anywhere else.

Some changes that im seeing, currently on campus is the constant innovation done by the school. Always growing the campus's infrastructure and branches of majors. Along with their plentiful resources for students success.

My classes are wonderful… many dedicated instructors who care about their students … Many activities, research, and service opportunities… They want you to succeed at UC Merced… The only thing I would change would be a speed limit on scooters and a campus shopping village with a few cafes and stores… UC Merced has been a fruitful experience and I am blessed to be graduating this Spring!!!!

My overall experience is a positive one. My peers in Glacier Point are respectful towards one another and offer help if an RA is not available right away. Upperclassman have also been a huge help. They have shown me where I can find certain buildings and give advice on how to succeed. The staff is understanding and offers advice to those who are not comfortable with the area just yet. I am proud to say I am attending UC Merced when everyone here is treated with respect and equally.
As a local, I was very privileged to attend this school. I felt extremely comfortable in all my classes and appreciated a sense of community with my classmates.
UC Merced is located in a quiet and somewhat isolated area which makes it perfect to focus on studying. Everything is new and there is a lot of equipment for scientific research.

I began attending UC Merced in 2015, so the campus was still relatively new. Ultimately, the STEM cirriculum, research, faculty, and resources were top tier and I felt like I was getting a high end education. I met amazing friends who I still talk to and go on trips with, 10 years later. I would say that Merced did lack in social activities because it’s a small town, but I’ve learned it’s the people you meet and what you make of a situation that can influence your experience.
Its nice here. The students are nice and inclusive. Merced has the same opportunities as the other campuses, but with less competition.

University of California-Merced is the newest school in Californias UC system. Due to that it has some of the best facilities and dorms when compared with other schools such as UC Santa Barbara. However due to its remote location in the Modesto valley and its slow build up there is very little for students to do in the area. The school is only a couple hours away from the Bay Area or Yosemite however so that can be seen as a silver lining to its location.

ai_summary: At the University of California - Riverside, students often highlight the supportive and friendly atmosphere of the campus, with many praising the approachable professors who are invested in student success. Academically, UCR offers various resources and research opportunities, particularly for those pursuing majors in the sciences, though some students express a desire for tailored support , especially for transfer students and commuters. While student life is enriched by numerous clubs and activities, a recurring theme is the call for more engagement events and improved school spirit . The campus itself is noted for its beauty and inclusivity , though some reviews mention concerns about the surrounding area and limited nightlife. Overall, students appreciate the diverse student body , the safety of the environment, and the abundance of resources available to help them thrive academically and socially.

reviews.text: If you're okay with walking, parking isn't as bad as people say. People tend to both overhype and underrate professors.

University of California, Riverside is an incredible university with faculty and staff who are genuinely invested in your success. Even after I graduated, my professors and mentors continued to guide me, opening doors to research programs, post-bacc opportunities, and guidance on applying to master’s degree and PhD applications.
Campus life at UCR is welcoming for both introverts and extroverts—there are always events and activities that suit every personality and background. With hundreds of clubs and organizations, every student can find their community and feel supported.
I loved my time at UCR and I’m proud to be a Highlanders alum!
💙 Go Highlanders!

Some Pros : walkable campus, student life and activities, class availability, housing is reasonable, school events and festivals Cons: outside of campus is a little dangerous, commuting school, school pride/culture lacking
absolutely wonderful chem, organic chem, bio and neuro/bio professors. professors are invested in student success. lots of research opportunities and community involvement with the medical school.

My overall experience at the University of California, Riverside has been positive and meaningful. UCR has a very diverse and welcoming community, and I appreciated how inclusive and supportive the campus environment felt, especially as a student from a multicultural background. I found that students were generally friendly, collaborative, and willing to help each other succeed.
One area that could be improved is course availability and enrollment. Some required classes fill up quickly, which can make it stressful to plan your schedule or graduate on time. I would also like to see expanded academic advising and more personalized guidance, especially for underclassmen who may feel overwhelmed navigating requirements and career paths.
Overall, UCR is a strong university that provides a supportive learning environment, valuable academic opportunities, and a diverse community. With improvements in class access and advising, it could become even better at supporting student success.

My experience at UCR has been a journey of growth and self-discovery. I’ve learned to balance academics, work, and personal challenges while building resilience, meaningful friendships, and professional skills that have shaped who I am today.
Overall I love Ucr, I love the atmosphere and diversity the school creates. The school does their best to create an amazing social space for everyone to be a part of and for clubs to succeed.

As someone who is commuting from La to UCR I thought I wouldn't be able to make friends or be part of the school. But I was entirely mistaken the people there are so welcoming and are able to talk to you easy. I have made tons of friends that have helped me see the campus even more and have helped me feel at home even if I'm far from home

My overall experience at UC Riverside has been positive. The campus has a welcoming environment with diverse students and supportive professors who genuinely want you to succeed. I appreciated the strong academic programs, especially in the sciences, and the many resources available tutoring, career services, and opportunities for research or internships.

My experience at UCR overall has been okay. It wasn't until this year I was established a permanent academic advisor. The School of Business is my favorite part of the school and has many different places to study. Though I myself may not be involved in clubs, there is a lot of different clubs to choose from.
It's alright, there's nothing too notable, there isn't much to do around Riverside. The public transport is nice

University of California, Riverside, has been such an amazing experience. The overall diversity is a new experience and creates a community that has led to so many new stories. The professors are very passionate about their subjects, which creates an engaging and meaningful class. The campus environment has a perfect blend of buildings and green space, creating multiple options for studying and relaxing. The UCR support system is very beneficial, from tutoring in specific subjects to mental health resources. However, the limited space in courses leads to stressful times whenever course registrations begin, along with the limited parking options. UCR can make a big difference by providing more course options and transportation between the campus. In the end, UCR is an amazing experience where the personal and academic experiences are memorable and still growing.

As a freshman at the university of riverside I can by far say that it is a very welcoming environment. Starting from the orientation and friendly students which make you feel eased into the new experience and change in scenery and environment for some/most. My first few weeks here have been nice especially since adapting to this school hasn't been difficult whatsoever. As far as events go I think they're very engaging for students and inclusive for the diversity that exists at the university, which also gives the opportunity to try and listen to different things in languages/culture. As an example I will speak about the yearly "Block Party" that happens where big and small artists perform and are chosen by the majority of students which are different genres and different backgrounds which gives rooms for others to explore music in different cultures.

I like the professors as they are all very knowledgeable in their area and are able to relay that information. I also enjoy the big campus and being able to bike and skateboard around campus. One thing I would like to change is possibly having more TA's that speak English a little bit better because its very hard to understand some of the accents and the fluidity of the conversation declines because of constantly saying "huh" or deciphering what they are saying. Other than that, I love UCR.

My experience at the University of California Riverside has been really enjoyable. The campus life especially, with meeting all kinds of new people, and being able to interact with people from all over the world. The campus is massive and there si always things to do or events happening to get people together.

ai_summary: At the University of California - San Diego, students frequently highlight the beautiful campus and its ideal location near the beach, creating a pleasant environment for both studying and relaxation. Academically, the university is recognized for its strong STEM programs and excellent research opportunities, although some students feel that humanities courses can be more challenging and that class sizes can be quite large, making personal connections with professors difficult. The student body is noted for its diversity, contributing to a welcoming atmosphere , though many reviews mention a perception of a less active social scene when compared to other universities. While the campus offers numerous resources and clubs for involvement, challenges such as parking issues , housing shortages, and variability in dining options are common concerns for students. Overall, UCSD is characterized as a rigorous academic institution where supportive faculty and a focus on education are prioritized, but students must actively engage to foster their social connections.

reviews.text: great school, i love it here so much! i enjoy my time here. anyone who says that it’s uc socially dead doesn’t know what they’re talking about. i partied myself out and was not in greek life.

My overall experience at UC San Diego was great. Majoring in Cognitive Science at one of the pioneering institutions for this field really gave a lot of unique classes that provided me an equally as unique background in design. I double majored in Interdisciplinary Computing and the Arts which was another way UC San Diego provided a major and education that's not so common.
Though with these majors at this extremely research oriented university, it was difficult diving into the artistic and design aspect of these majors because a lot of emphasis was put onto the technological and research quality rather than the artistic quality. I've noticed that a lot of work would have to be done out of the classroom that have an internship/work-ready portfolio.
I felt as though I enjoyed the student life better than the academics. As a dancer, the dance community is extremely large and is where I found most of my friends and community.

Overall the experience is good. But University of California - San Diego is a very large school, large size classes, large size campus. You won't have that much access to resources as private school does.

My overall experience at UC San Diego has been challenging but really rewarding. The classes are definitely rigorous, especially in STEM, and you have to stay on top of your work from the first week of the quarter. At first it felt overwhelming, but over time I learned how to manage my schedule and use resources like office hours and tutoring. Professors and TAs are helpful if you make the effort to reach out.
One of the best parts of UCSD is the research opportunities. There are so many labs and projects, and it’s possible to get involved even as an undergraduate. Being part of a research team helped me connect what I learned in class to real-world applications.
The campus is beautiful and close to the beach, which makes studying a little less stressful. Socially, it can feel quiet at times, but joining clubs and student organizations made a big difference. Overall, UCSD has pushed me to grow academically and personally, and I feel well prepared for what comes next.

My overall experience at UCSD was amazing. I started out at UCSD as a freshman and went there all 4 years of my undergraduate degree. UCSD offered an amazing student life style and was a great balance between rigorous academics, but also fun great people.

My overall experience with the University of California, San Diego has been very positive. The campus environment feels both academically focused and welcoming, with strong support for students who are curious, driven, and interested in collaboration. UC San Diego stands out for its emphasis on research, innovation, and interdisciplinary learning, which creates many opportunities to explore interests beyond the classroom. The balance between rigorous academics and a supportive community makes UC San Diego feel like a place where students are encouraged to grow intellectually while also finding their own path.

Overall, my experience at UCSD was positive. The community could be better in terms of more undergraduate activities and socialization, but the educational experience was great. My major, specifically, had amazing professors who were very knowledgeable and supportive which made the experience at UCSD worthwhile.

Transferring to UCSD and leaving home for the first time had its challenges with obtaining housing. The local area is safe, has a variety of shopping and having a school ID gives students the opportunity to access free public transport. The ease of availability of sports and nature spots is one of the university's best attributes. UCSD should support students better with housing.
I would like to have the revelle dorms remodeled and maybe more changes in the food at the dinning hauls because it get repetitive.

UC San Diego is a great college because it offers strong academics, research opportunities, and a supportive learning environment. It is especially known for its science and engineering programs, giving students hands-on experience and access to innovative research. UCSD’s college system helps create smaller communities, making a large university feel more personal. Its location in San Diego also provides many internship and career opportunities, along with a diverse and welcoming campus.
Overall, great school but hard to connect with other students within classes. Really need to get out of your comfort zone and join clubs or social events!

School campus: UCSD is the biggest UC in California. If you live in the west side on campus, then you will have to walk at least fifteen to twenty minutes to get across or down campus, especially if you have classes at Eighth, Revelle, or Warren Lecture Hall. I definetly recommend. Walking around Warren College is a bit creepy at night, esp if you do not have a group with you.
Dorms: Dorms are not that bad. But to be honest, all residence dorms and apartments have their pros and cons. However, I heard that Revelle and Warren can have problems. Also, beware if your window leaks during rainy days. Please do not lose your ID or room keys!!!! IDs cost 25$ and keys 60$ for a replacement.
Students: People are super nice, especially if you are entering as a first year. A lot of individuals I met are diverse and welcoming as they also are looking for new friends.
Teachers: Definitely look up Rate My Professor to see who is the best teacher, what their grading is like etc.

My overall experience at the University of California, San Diego has been very positive. UCSD offers a strong academic environment with challenging courses that encourage critical thinking and independence. The professors are knowledgeable and supportive, and there are many resources available, such as tutoring centers and academic advising. I appreciate the diversity on campus, which creates an inclusive and welcoming atmosphere. The campus itself is beautiful and located near the ocean, making it a great place to study and relax. While the quarter system can feel fast-paced at times, it helps students stay focused and motivated.

I absolutely love going to college at UCSD! The campus is absolutely beautiful, with tons of interesting art installations. Further, most of the professors are super friendly, and there is so much research going on you can get involved in.

UCSD is an amazing school with many opportunities. This university along with any other is what you make it, there are endless opportunities. Getting involved in research here is easy to do if you try your hardest and some professors arent great but thats what you get anywhere.

ai_summary: The University of California - Santa Barbara (UCSB) is frequently praised for its stunning campus and lively atmosphere , with proximity to both the beach and mountains enhancing the overall student experience. Many students enjoy the tight-knit community and the balance between academics and social life, though some note that the party scene can be overwhelming if you prefer a more low-key lifestyle. Academically, the university is recognized for its strong research opportunities and dedicated professors, but the large class sizes can make it challenging to connect with faculty. While the academic programs are diverse and engaging, students express concerns about housing availability and affordability, alongside a desire for better dining options and more campus resources, particularly for mental health. Overall, UCSB is seen as a unique blend of rigorous education and a vibrant social life set against a picturesque backdrop, creating a distinctive college experience.

reviews.text: UCSB is a great school with an uplifting and welcoming student atmosphere. I majored in Sociology and really enjoyed most of my professors and classes. The campus is steps from the beach and Isla Vista is always busy with students biking, surfing, or just hanging out in their front yards. One of the downsides would probably be that Isla Vista housing is pretty pricey and students usually live with 3-10 other housemates, getting a house is competitive, students start looking in Fall quarter of the year before, but the experience is worth it!

UC Santa Barbara is so unique and special. The campus is beautiful and sits right next to the beach. Most students live in Isla Vista, which is a neighboring college town right outside of campus, making it very convenient to walk or bike to campus. The academics and professors are amazing and very accomplished.

I loved my experience at UCSB. I majored in Environmental Studies and English and I found both of my degrees incredibly rewarding. The campus and area is beautiful, and student life is great. The housing however is an issue for many, it is very difficult to find affordable housing and most people have to live with 1-2 people in their same room for all four years.

It was very fun, but somewhat marred by the resources provided to my department. It was very clear who and what they prioritized, simply thanks to the lack of funding that public schools receive here. However, I do not regret going to school at UCSB at all. My exposure to music, to new opportunities, to research, and to study abroad was deeply appreciated and I could not have imagined living my college experience in any other way.

I look back fondly on my time at UCSB. While there is some emphasis on partying, the academic scene is as rigorous. I studied DS and Statistics, which allowed me to get a full-time job immediately after graduation - I felt prepped to step into the corporate world.

Overall I enjoyed my time at UCSB, with some exceptions, mostly the cost.
Academics is obviously excellent, like most UC schools, with several standout STEM programs (physics, engineering, CS). Students are welcoming, diverse, and outgoing. It's a study hard, play hard environment. I'm not a huge partier but still had a great time.
UCSB's natural beauty is unparalleled-- right on the beach, mountains & lakes nearby, great weather. My sophomore dorm was a 5 minute walk from the ocean, and it felt like a students-only getaway. You can actually see the stars at night!
The actual campus architecture itself is meh, but a lot of bike routes and buses (free for students!) makes travel easy. Amtrak connects it to LA and beyond.
Downsides-- off-campus housing is EXPENSIVE & limited, apply MONTHS in advance. The city is small and isolated, so internship/work opportunities are more limited than UCSD/UCI etc. Definitely research the cost of living and ROI before you decide to attend.

My experience at University of California, Santa Barbara as an Economics major (Class of 2024) was incredibly rewarding. I appreciated the campus culture, diversity, and the balance between academic rigor and student life. The curriculum and professors challenged me while still allowing space to grow outside the classroom. CLAS sessions were especially valuable in preparing for exams and strengthening my understanding of economics. Living so close to classes and essential resources created a strong sense of community and convenience. I was actively involved in the Finance and Investment Clubs and helped organize community events. Throughout my time there, I maintained high academic standards. If I could suggest one improvement, it would be expanding and improving off-campus housing options.

Overall, I enjoyed my experience at UCSB and I feel that I got a really good college education experience. The school is large and it takes some getting used to the scale, but after a while you get used to it. There are a lot of resources available to support housing and food insecurities. The professor to students ratio was well balanced and there were always opportunities for office hours. Professors were extremely flexible if that designated time did not work. I worked full time throughout college, so that flexibility with professors and strong communication really helped supplement my education as well.
Santa Barbara has great professors and there is a diversity of students here. The downsides are the Campus Dining and how expensive housing is.

My time at UCSB was overall an amazing experience. Looking back, I wish I were more aware of how research-heavy the institution is and what that meant for my education and training. I think some of the safety and housing issues were beyond the university's control.

UCSB provides a beautiful location to study any subject - located on the beach with mild weather and a local environment populated by its students. Studying an Engineering degree at UCSB I found the professors to be engaging, but the curriculum less hands on than one might expect. As a research institute, the courses build a good foundation in theory but are less approachable from a learn by doing stance. There are opportunities to get more hands-on if students desire, with good club presence across interest groups such as aerospace, automotive, and more.

UCSB has always been my #1 choice for college. The academics are very challenging. The setting of the school is breath taking because you are right on the Pacific Ocean. Greek life is outstanding. There is a great vibe because the sun and beach time bring the best out of everyone.

Before I chose UCSB as my school, I was able to visit the campus beforehand and I fell in love. To the west was the shining ocean and to the east were majestic mountains. The views itself were gorgeous and after touring the campus, I decided that this school was it. There was diversity and clubs that even myself, a minority, could join and I could even stay fit as 90% of students were biking to their classes (I was afraid of the freshman 15 curse). It was different from my hometown and to be able to experience that, opened another world for me.

I had a wonderful experience at UC Santa Barbara, mostly I believe because of my small department. It was close knit, supportive, and easy to get classes, but my peers in larger majors did not have this experience. I also socially thrived and found community in the Student Housing Cooperative.

So far it’s been great, I see my future being bright here. Being only a first year I am provided an abundant amount of support through my advisors. I feel pretty well supported but it can be a bit confusing on what kind of classes would be needed to achieve my desired degree.

ai_summary: At the University of California - Santa Cruz, students often highlight the stunning beauty of the campus, uniquely set within a redwood forest and overlooking the coast. Many reviews praise the supportive and engaging professors who genuinely care about student success, offering an intimate learning experience in smaller classes despite some larger course sizes. Student life is diverse and vibrant, with numerous clubs and activities, although some students note a lack of central community areas and feelings of isolation due to the campus's layout. The school is recognized for its commitment to social justice and environmental sustainability, strongly resonating with many students. However, concerns arise regarding the housing situation, with limited availability triggering frustrations among students about living conditions and costs. Overall, while UCSC stands out for its natural surroundings and academic support, comments reflect a desire for improved campus facilities and more accessible social opportunities.

reviews.text: My experience at the University of California, Santa Cruz has been both challenging and transformative. UCSC offers a unique environment where students can engage deeply with issues related to sustainability, food systems, and social change. I appreciated the opportunity to work closely with faculty, conduct research, and build projects that connect academic learning with real world impact. At the same time, I believe there is room for improvement in how the university supports students navigating complex personal or institutional challenges. Clearer communication, more consistent support systems, and stronger accountability structures would help ensure that all students can fully benefit from the opportunities UCSC provides. Overall, UCSC has been an important place for growth, leadership, and community engagement.

My overall experience at the University of California, Santa Cruz has been both enriching and transformative. UCSC provided a supportive and intellectually stimulating environment that encouraged me to grow academically, personally, and professionally. The collaborative community of faculty, staff, and peers helped me develop critical thinking, problem-solving, and leadership skills, while also fostering a strong sense of social responsibility and engagement.
Through my time at UCSC, I was able to explore diverse perspectives, participate in meaningful campus programs, and contribute to initiatives that aligned with my passions and values. This experience not only prepared me for my future career goals but also inspired me to continue learning, taking on leadership roles, and making a positive impact in the communities I serve.

It's very expensive here and very corrupt. The higher above just keep squishing students into this school without having enough housing for the students. It's literally a lottery and based off luck for housing. It's very scary not knowing where you'll the following year especially with how expensive it is to live here. Also the math department is small, or atleast in the stem area, there's about 2 math teachers per quarter for a math course and they're never good at teaching well. I wish we had a better math department.
Great people, great memories. Limited housing opportunities and high cost of living off campus. Amazing nature, stunning school.
Love it here! The campus is so so pretty, the students are welcoming, the food isn't terrible, and the housing is not as bad a people say, you just have to start looking early.

I’m currently a 3rd year at UCSC, I love almost everything about the campus! The only thing that has been stressful during my experience is financing and finding off-campus housing. Aside from that, I have enjoyed wonderful faculty who genuinely care about their field of research. There’s a vibrant student life with so many friendly people. The queer community on campus has meant a lot to me over the years, and has given me a lot of amazing sustained friendships. If you want to be able to explore your specific interests or create a new niche, you will fit in great at UCSC.

I liked the opportunities to be involved. I felt like how the school was physically set up sometimes left the opportunity for isolation. I was very involved on campus in clubs and different organizations which helped myself not be as isolated. The academics are good, but some professors are hard to connect with.

My experience at UCSC has been amazing so far. The people are very friendly and there is a really good community, and the Santa Cruz town is really cute. The academics have been really good and you can tell the professors are experts in their respective fields. To be honest I was really nervous at the beginning of school because I have heard mixed opinions and it was pretty far from home. Now that I have spent a quarter here I could not imagine going anywhere else.

Overall, I have had a good experience at UCSC. I have had some really great professors and learning experiences, but I have also had a few bad professors that hindered my ability to learn. Most classes are difficult but almost all of them feel fair and understandably difficult, there is just the odd one or two that felt unfair which were the same from said bad professors. I usually eat at the cafeteria and the food overall is good. It nothing incredible but I enjoy it enough to like eating it everyday for the first few years. All in all it has been a great experience just not perfect.

The University of Santa Cruz has overall been a pretty fun experience and the amount of liberty you have here has been insane. I am fortunate enough to have entered a University of California that is very different from my hometown and I enjoy the environment here. There is wild life everywhere and you easily become accustomed to the way of living because everyone here is very independent. Transportation has been very easy to navigate, and the Professors I have had have been very transparent with the students attending. I am grateful for being here, however the cost of living has stopped me from being with my family because it is very expensive. Thus, one thing I would change is the cost of living in this area and the ability to be able to drive to campus without having to be a 3rd year. I only continue to expand my learning here, so therefore I expect to finish my business degree soon and potentially find a job so that it can help me with work experience.

My overall experience with UC Santa Cruz has been alright because as a marine biology major, there aren't many opportunities for marine science work or internships. I like the views and scenery of the forest and some of the buildings for the sciences. I think they need to build more housing, more gyms, and more marine science opportunities.

I transferred to UCSC from USC (had a 4.0 GPA at USC, was just incredibly unhappy down there) and UCSC is a lot more fluid if that makes sense. I'm a Literature major so there's a lot of reading and comprehension but honestly it's not too bad. The campus is gorgeous as are the trees, but if you have off campus housing, like I do, it makes it harder to enjoy since you have to commute and that takes energy out of you. My professors are all cool people, but lectures aren't as organized as I would like, it often feels like there wasn't much planning put into it and readings often aren't addressed in lecture, which would be fine, if we delved into the untouched readings in section, but unfortunately that really depends on your TA.

I attended UC Santa Cruz from Fall 2023 to Spring 2025, before transferring. To begin with, if you’re someone who loves nature itself, UCSC will be perfect for you. However, the campus is very spread out, which makes it extremely hard to bump into people, so it often felt socially dead. If you prefer a quieter, less crowded environment, though, this wouldn’t be a problem for you. Throughout my time there, I always felt safe. Regarding on-campus housing, it is extremely competitive, and finding off-campus housing is difficult too. In my second year I had no choice but to move off-campus because they did a lottery system and I didn’t get a spot. The housing crisis is horrible there. During my first year living in an on‑campus dorm, the room was a lot smaller than I expected, but my roommates were nice. The dining-hall food didn’t taste like the best quality. Some classes I took were decent, but I didn’t enjoy most of them because of the way the professors taught. The clubs there was fun!

When I first got into UC Santa Cruz from, I wasn’t proud to attend UC Santa Cruz, everyone from my high school cared about rankings, and UCSC was seen as “third-tier.” But after spending time there, I realized how special it is. The campus is breathtaking built inside a redwood forest with ocean views that feel unreal. Every walk to class feels peaceful and grounding. The dining halls are surprisingly great (RCC’s my favorite), and professors are chill, supportive, and genuinely care. During my second year I lost on campus housing and moved to Hilltop, which turned out amazing I brought my car, explored beaches, trails, and the city. Driving back along Highway 1 always felt like returning to paradise. Santa Cruz itself is creative, laid back, and full of kind people. It taught me to slow down, be independent, and enjoy learning. UCSC may not top rankings, but it offers something far more valuable, a calm, authentic college experience that stays with you forever.

It is hard to make new friends. If you like forest and the beach then this school is good for you . There is not enough busses and the rent is to high. There are some good teachers but other teachers beliefs are questionable.

ai_summary: At the University of California, Irvine, students generally appreciate the beautiful , well-maintained campus and the overall friendly atmosphere . Academics are viewed as challenging yet manageable , with accessible professors who are knowledgeable and engaged. Many review the availability of internships and research opportunities positively, particularly given the school’s advantageous location in a business hub. While the social scene can feel subdued, especially compared to other colleges, students find ways to connect through clubs and dorm life. The campus offers a calming environment centered around a park, which students enjoy for study breaks. On the downside, some students mention that the organization of services like financial aid could improve and that class registration can be tricky. Overall, while UC Irvine may not be the top choice for everyone, many find it a welcoming and enriching place to grow academically and personally.

reviews.text:
My overall experience at the University of California, Irvine has been positive so far. One thing I really appreciate is the campus environment. The campus is very clean, safe, and well-maintained, and the circular layout around Aldrich Park makes it easy to get around. There are also a lot of study spaces and libraries that make it convenient to work between classes.
Academically, UCI offers strong programs and many passionate professors who care about teaching and research. I’ve found that the classes can be challenging, but they push students to think critically and develop useful skills. There are also plenty of student organizations and opportunities to get involved, which helps make the campus feel more connected.

My overall experience at UC Irvine has been great. I am a third year Biological Sciences major and I have had the opportunity to take interesting courses within and outside my major. UC Irvine is for everyone, there are several clubs and organizations on campus and the area is filled with plenty to do. The campus is walkable since it is a huge circle and the professors and staff provide a welcoming and warm environment. The community is diverse and I feel seen and comfortable to speak my mind and find people with the same values as me. In conclusion, I would definitely recommend students to apply to UC Irvine!

My experience at University of California in Irvine gave me a lot of necessary academic tools and resources to succeed in my career choice of dentistry. However, I would say that the campus life and social connections have room for much improvement, as most students appear to lack a strong sense of school spirit.

My experience with UCI is overall pretty good, I really like the tranquility of how calm the campus is, I like the atmosphere and activity like clubs. I would like to see the change in their offices like the financial office that remain unhelpful.

I loved attending UCI! I was able to meet many amazing people through living in the dorms, class, and clubs. I also love walking through Aldrich Park between classes. Housing was also not too difficult to find because there are many close off-campus options available. I do wish public transportation or the Anteater Express was more accessible for further living options. The OC Bus does not run often and the routes take a while. Otherwise, I wouldn't change anything about my experience at UCI.

My experience at the University of California, Irvine was overall very positive and rewarding. I appreciated the range of academic resources available, including research opportunities, academic advising, and career development support, which helped me grow both academically and professionally. One of the things I liked most about UCI was the campus itself. The environment is beautiful, well-maintained, and welcoming, which made spending long days on campus enjoyable. I also valued the diversity of the student body. If there is one area I would like to see improve, it would be access to certain classes and academic advising during peak enrollment periods. Expanding course availability and advising support would make the experience even smoother for students.Overall, UCI provided me with a solid foundation for my future and a memorable college experience that I am grateful for.

UC Irvine feels like a place designed by committee and optimized for nobody. The campus is pristine to the point of sterility, like an outdoor shopping mall where ambition goes to nap. Everything is beige, circular, and endlessly underwhelming. The social scene is famously dead; people commute in, attend class, and vanish like NPCs despawning. School spirit exists only in brochures and during the one basketball game a year people pretend to care about.
Academically, it loves to market itself as elite while students fight for basic access to classes, advisors, and opportunities. Bureaucracy moves at glacial speed unless it’s collecting fees, which it does with Olympic-level efficiency. Professors range from excellent-but-checked-out to actively hostile to teaching, and good luck getting meaningful mentorship unless you already know how the system works.
Being an out of state student, it took me a while to adjust. I love that the school is quiet, peaceful, and everyone is focused on academics. Irvine is also a very safe area !

My overall experience at the UCI has been very positive. UCI offers a strong academic environment with dedicated faculty and many research and extracurricular opportunities. I especially appreciate the diversity of the student body and the collaborative atmosphere, which makes it easy to meet people from different backgrounds and feel supported academically and socially. The campus is clean, safe, and well organized, with many resources available for students, such as tutoring, career services, and student organizations. One area that could be improved is course availability. Some required or popular classes fill up very quickly, which can make scheduling stressful. Additionally, administrative processes such as advising or financial aid can sometimes be slow or confusing. Overall, UCI provides a high-quality education and a welcoming campus environment, and I feel that it has helped me grow both academically and personally in a meaningful and lasting way for future success, now ever.

I transferred into UC Irvine and it has been the best decision i’ve made. The campus is gorgeous, and the professors care about you doing well. A lot of people don’t realize that UC Irvine is in a great area and even super close to the beach!

In my experience, UC Irvine has been a little underwhelming. I feel like went I entered, people already knew others right away, and I felt lonely during my first year. I did not start to make friends until my Spring quarter of 2025. Other than social, the academic life has been okay, most professors do care about their students and well-being, however, every so often, there would be some professors who did not really care about their students and were only there for research. I think what I would like to see change, is professors trying their best to dumb things down to students. I think that would help others understand the material better.

UC Irvine has a very beautiful campus. The central Aldrich Park was well maintained and many of the buildings on campus were new. The faculty were generally helpful. Dining facilities were well maintained and offered much choice. The campus gym was excellent and offered indoor climbing, an Olympic sized swimming pool, tracks for running, badminton courts, racquetball courts, and much more. However, I found the school to have too much of a party scene, which made it difficult to study.

Overall, I had a great educational experience at UCI. I didn't live on campus so for the 2 years I attended there I was commuting from Pasadena. That was one of the more challenging parts of attending UCI. The campus was so beautiful and there were so many resources for me available on campus especially since I was a commuter. Many of my classes that I took in person were in big lecture halls with a lot of students. I would get a little overwhelmed in big classroom settings, but my professors did a really great reassuring me that they were always there to help and support me.

My experience at the University of California, Irvine has been both challenging and rewarding. UCI has helped me grow academically while clarifying my goals in business and accounting. Through rigorous coursework, I have developed discipline, time management, and a stronger understanding of professional responsibility. As a first-generation college student, I have benefited from UCI’s supportive resources, advisors, and diverse campus community. These experiences have strengthened my confidence and resilience while encouraging me to take ownership of my education. Overall, UCI has provided an environment that fosters growth, accountability, and preparation for long-term academic and professional success.

I think being at UCI has really opened my eyes to a whole new world. Growing up in a small town meant that the only fun activities to do was to go to get fast food with friends, go to Walmart and walk around, or just driving around while chatting. Although I will cherish those times for the rest of my life, being in Irvine has been so fun; meeting new people and seeing how the city life is. I'm very thankful for my new friends and how they have been showing me around, telling me the best food spots, the biggest malls, and most importantly, the best spots to take pictures with my camera. The education at UCI has also been very great. Junior and senior year of high school, I did dual enrollment at SFCC and WSU. I learned a lot from those classes, but I think its very safe to say that the education here is much better, with higher quality professors and a much faster-paced, competitive enviornment.

ai_summary: At the University of California - Los Angeles, students appreciate the vibrant campus life , excellent dining options , and a diverse community , which contribute to an engaging college experience. Academically, while many praise the quality of courses and professors, there's some feedback about large class sizes and the challenge of getting into desired classes, especially in STEM fields. Students enjoy numerous extracurricular activities and find it relatively easy to connect with peers through clubs and organizations. However, some concerns have been raised regarding the cost of tuition and housing, as well as the administration's responsiveness to student needs, particularly regarding support for students of color. Overall, UCLA offers a unique blend of rigorous academics , a lively social scene, and a beautiful campus environment , making it appealing to many prospective students.

reviews.text: Overall my experience with UCLA was really good. The campus was beautiful, and the libraries were very nice and quiet. I loved spending time in the study halls and dining halls on the hill. The student life was good, there was always ways to join clubs and make friends. You just have to put yourself out there.

I am appreciative of my college journey at UCLA and feel imbued with the school spirit with my two years of attending so far. I am ecstatic to continue my journey developing my academic skills as a student on the pre-med track, in addition to pursuing a fruitful experience as a first-generation college student. My parents have always emphasized to take every opportunity, and I believe UCLA allows me to live by experiences and be aspiring for further education.

It was great for the most part but I was there when they had the encampments in 2024. The encampment didn't feel unsafe but it was the other students against them that ruined everything. After that, it felt unsafe on campus.
Would recommend this school as it lives up to both its academic and social reputation: the slogan is true.

UCLA has been a great environment to meet a diverse group of students from all places. The opportunities are wide yet competitive, which may be a downside, but the school pride that is quickly planted in all students is what makes UCLA so enjoyable.

The experience is what you make of it. You definitely have to be proactive and know what you are looking for. Opportunities are not presented to you, as there are a lot of students at the school. It can be competitive but also have some faith in yourself and you will do great here.
The surrounding neighborhood had no nightlife and limited daytime activities that were accessible without a car.

I absolutely loved UCLA. I found that there was so much energy, from the classroom to athletic events to extracurriculars. I thought most professors were very engaged and experts in their fields. Once in a while, however, I took classes that felt completely useless. Yes, sometimes I signed up for them to fill requirements knowing they wouldn't necessarily help my major but a solid number of those types of filler classes just felt like wasted time. I think it's great that UCLA allows for such varied interests, however, and I knew people with all types of majors and interests across my own classes. The food options on campus are incredible and I would feel excited to eat or get coffee on campus, and Westwood offered even more great options. Being in a major city always has some safety issues, but I felt it was far safer than some other schools in Los Angeles
My overall experience has been very positive. I have met so many incredible people who will remain in my life forever. I could not recommend this school enough.

I thoroughly enjoyed the dining hall options while living on-campus, the vast number of student organizations, competitive sports culture, and the stunning campus. The prestige of attending UCLA was unmatched. For constructive criticism, it was extremely competitive to get involved as an undergraduate Research Assistant. I would like to see programs that increase the accessibility of research positions for students. Similarly, lectures were very impacted, making it difficult to enroll in classes you needed to graduate.

I generally enjoyed my time at UCLA. Enrollment can be very difficult due to the first/second pass system, and club applications can make socializing feel more daunting. It takes some effort to find your people, but the transfer community in particular is quite strong.

I say it was akin to Charles Dickens' A Tale of Two Cities. It was the best of times, it was the worst of times. I met some amazing people from all sorts of backgrounds and became great friends with some of them. I found UCLA to be just as academically rigorous as everyone told me it would be. At times I was prepared for that and embraced the challenge, finding fulfillment in my studies. However, there were events in my personal life that really disrupted my studies and severely affected my undergraduate experience. As such I have a lot of praise for UCLA, but am also a bit traumatized by my own unique experience. Also, I have a strong disdain for its administration. This was the case before I even attended, and that sentiment only grew stronger when I attended and after graduating. I really detest the higher ups in the administration. It is not surprising, but they really, really do not care about their students at all.

I had an amazing experience at UCLA! The academic environment was incredibly stimulating, and the professors were always willing to go the extra mile to support me. I particularly enjoyed the research opportunities available, and I was able to work on a project that combined my passion for computer science with real-world applications. The campus is also beautiful, with a great vibe and tons of resources for students. I made lifelong friends and connections there, and I think it's a fantastic place to grow both academically and personally.

I enjoyed my UCLA experience so much. I was in the Dykstra Hall dorms my freshmen year of college. I met many life long friends living in these dorms. The dinning halls had amazing food and variety. There are a lot of clubs on campus that cater to diverse identities and interests. At the activities center at the beginning of the year you can find a community of people with similar backgrounds and hobbies. The professors and research opportunities are also great for furthering your education and interest in your field of study.
I had a great time for UCLA. The environment here is amazing. Had an absolute blast. The culture is amazing and the area around it

ai_summary: The University of Central Arkansas (UCA) offers a vibrant campus life with diverse student organizations and supportive faculty , making it a welcoming environment for students. There are many opportunities for academic engagement, and professors are generally praised for being approachable and dedicated to student success. However, experiences with academics can be mixed, as some students feel teaching quality varies across departments. The campus itself is noted for its beauty and accessibility , though parking can be a challenge. Dining options receive mixed reviews, with some food experiences being inconsistent. Students appreciate the inclusion of various cultures on campus and the robust extracurricular activity options , contributing to a strong sense of community . While some feel the tuition adds up due to additional fees, many believe the college provides a valuable overall experience for its cost.

reviews.text: I'm having a great experience at university of Central Arkansas, and I want to continue to learn and experience everything here. I have had the opportunity to learn new skills, meet new people, and build important connections that will help me in my future career. The university also offers many activities, programs, and resources that help students grow both academically and personally. Overall, my experience at UCA has helped me develop my character, confidence, and goals for the future. Attending University of Central Arkansas has given me many valuable opportunities. The university offers strong academic programs and resources that help students succeed in their chosen fields. Through classes, campus events, and networking opportunities, I have been able to learn new skills and gain knowledge that will help me in the future. The supportive environment at UCA encourages students to work hard and pursue their goals. Working the business department has helped me in my degree field.
This campus is a great campus when it comes to studying as you get to learn your study habits that will help you pass class classes and help maintain a good gpa

Overall, I've really enjoyed being on UCA campus! It's truly an enjoyable experience, and it's evident that the professors here truly do love what they teach. For example, my humanities teacher showed complexity concerning her topic, as well as a confident understanding and enjoyment with that. The people, events, and academics are an all around joy.

University of Central Arkansas is a great school in my opinion. It has a diverse campus with people you really wouldn't see anywhere else, it's easy to make friends if you're looking in the right place. It took me a while to get integrated into the social scene, but I'm a first time collage student and didn't really know what to expect. But the staff on campus are nice if you are nice, just be warned, if you're mean to them, they will likely be rude back. The on site facility workers are super sweet, when they were hanging the lights I told them to be careful and it made them smile, and that made me happy. The students can be rude, but a lot of them just want their degree and don't want to really do much else and that's okay, just find your social setting and you'll be okay. The teachers are SUPER nice, very understanding, and just sweet, be nice to them and they'll be sweet back... Just read the syllabus.
I have enjoyed my time at UCA thus far. I have had several issues with housing and academic accommodations, but upon some provoking, my issues were resolved.
Professors are very strict but some will be leniant if you attend class a lot or participate in class.

Central Arkansas was one of two options for my major in the state, so I appreciate it more than its worth. The dorms are not kept well, there is mold is many of the halls, especially the freshmen halls they require you to live in. The cafeteria food deteriorated almost immediately at the beginning of the year. Constant interruptions from concstruction or tours during test weeks. A general lack of accountability from President Houston Davis.

As an international student, my time at UCA taught me a lot about adaptability and growth. Moving to a new country came with challenges, but the welcoming community and supportive professors made it easier to adjust and find my place. I appreciated how diverse and inclusive the campus felt. It helped me connect with people from different backgrounds and build lasting friendships.
The academic experience was strong, and I gained valuable research and leadership skills that continue to help me today. If anything, I would love to see more mentorship programs that guide international students through career and graduate school planning.
Overall, UCA helped me grow not only academically but personally. It shaped my confidence, independence, and ability to adapt wherever I go.
I felt welcomed the first day I started at UCA. UCA staff Freshman made the transition period seamless. My classes and teachers have been very helpful and understanding.

I have always wanted to go to the University of Central Arkansas. It is the best school in Arkansas for the profession that I want to pursue. I've grown up around Conway, so I know a lot about the town. I love it here!

My overall experience at the University of Central Arkansas, particularly within the graduate school environment, has been profoundly supportive and professionally formative. Having arrived in Arkansas from Liverpool, England, initially on a college tennis scholarship, UCA became the foundation for my transition into a professional career.
The best part of UCA is the genuine, hands on focus of the School Psychology program. The faculty are invested practitioners who prioritize applied knowledge, immediately placing us in school settings for practicum. This real world emphasis, coupled with the department’s strong community atmosphere, makes the rigorous coursework manageable and meaningful. It feels less like distant academia and more like vital training for the challenges I am eager to face as a practitioner in Arkansas schools. The cost of living in Conway also provides an essential practical advantage for an international graduate student like myself.

As a new freshman, I love all the new experiences that are available at the University of central Arkansas , in Conway, AR. There are numerous clubs to join and all professors are sincere and very easy to understand. The police department makes a safe environment on the campus.
As a music major, I feel very accepted and wanted. A very, very welcoming environment for almost all colleges.
I liked how beautiful and clean the campus was. The buildings were nice and there were so many opportunities.

Overall, my experience with the University of Central Arkansas was quite positive. I really felt intellectually stimulated in my courses. I enjoyed learning and growing within my field. There was also a reasonable amount of diversity and inclusivity available for its students. However, there was a fluctuation in the quality of my experiences depending on the semester and the group of professors. I also really resonated with the atmosphere of the campus.

ai_summary: At the University of Central Florida (UCF), students appreciate the vibrant , diverse campus life that encourages personal and academic growth. The university excels in providing numerous extracurricular activities, clubs, and events, creating a welcoming community . Many students highlight the positive relationships developed with professors and advisors, who are generally supportive and engaged, although there are occasional mentions of less effective teaching experiences. The campus has ample resources, from cutting-edge facilities to a strong athletic culture , which collectively contribute to an active student life. While tuition is considered relatively affordable, some students express concerns about the size of the university and challenges with parking and housing. Overall, UCF is seen as a place of opportunity, fostering strong connections and memorable experiences for its students.

reviews.text:
My experience at the University of Central Florida has been both rewarding and motivating. I appreciate the diverse and inclusive environment, as well as the wide range of academic and professional opportunities available to students. The supportive faculty and engaging coursework have helped me grow both academically and personally, preparing me for my future goals.
One area I would like to see enhanced is access to structured mentorship programs. While there are many opportunities available, having more consistent and accessible mentorship connections between students, faculty, and professionals would provide valuable guidance, especially for those preparing for graduate or professional school. Strengthening these programs would further support students in navigating their academic and career paths. Overall, my experience has been very positive, and I am grateful to be part of such a dynamic and forward-thinking institution.
I really like the location of UCF. There’s also just so many people there that you’re able to find a group where you fit it. I love going here and it’d be a 10/10 if the dining hall was better.

My experience at the University of Central Florida was incredibly formative both academically and personally. UCF’s large and diverse student body created an environment where I was constantly exposed to new perspectives, cultures, and ideas. As a first-generation college student, navigating a large university was initially intimidating, but the resources available such as academic advising, tutoring centers, and student organizations helped me build confidence and develop strong study habits. I appreciated the opportunities for involvement in research and healthcare-related activities that ultimately helped shape my path toward medical school. If there is one thing I would like to see improve, it would be expanding personalized advising and mentorship opportunities, especially for first-generation students who may need additional guidance navigating academic and career pathways. Overall, UCF provided a strong foundation for my future goals in medicine!

Great school, but the size makes it hard to differentiate yourself when building applications for graduate programs and for building relationships with professors. No shortage on things to do, but most everything outside of campus is 30 mins away. Downtown Orlando can be fun but a little sketchy at times.

Overall, my experience at UCF was a great one. They have a majority of majors to choose from. I really enjoyed that they have direct connect programs from a lot of community colleges. This makes it easier to transition into UCF. My major was in Education. At the time, I felt that my professors did a fantastic job at preparing me for a career as a teacher. The one thing I would change about UCF is the parking situation. This is definitely the most chaotic part about UCF.

I’m speaking from my personal experience, and I know many people enjoy UCF. But if I could choose again, I would not choose this university.
The campus and location are great. However, I have felt completely unsupported. Professors often do not respond to emails, and when you are paying high tuition, basic communication should not be optional. Students invest serious money and effort to be here.
Yes, it is a large university, and large classes make things harder. But that does not excuse silence or indifference. Feeling like just another number is discouraging.
Too often, you are left to figure everything out alone. A university should offer guidance and accountability, not just a brand name.
This has been my experience, and it has been deeply disappointing.

UCF is a great uni, the social life is really amazing and the people are amazing. There is always something happening on campus. The local area around the campus is all sponsored by the school which is really cool, someplace near campus gives discount to students, which I think reinforces the schools hospitality.

I enjoy attending UCF! It is definitely a unique experience. Orlando is a great place that has lots to do and UCF offers so many amazing amenities. The learning environment is not perfect but I especially enjoy my classes I am taking now once I have gotten into my major.
It was a good school, great people very good food. They were being very stingy with how many marshmallows i could eat at once.

I had an amazing academic experience at the University of Central Florida. I was provided plenty of resources that both helped me gain skills in the field of law and general adulting. The school has many organizations that are focused on character and professional development. The professors are top quality and there are always ways to excel given the opportunities that the school offers to its students and alumni as well.

I enjoyed my overall experience at The University of Central Florida. I think that the academics and social life were pretty good. I do have to say though that the school definitely offers more help to certain majors over others. As a psychology student, the advising program was really bad. Over 2,000 psychology students were not even assigned an advisor and if you were so lucky to have one, it was basically impossible to get an appointment with them. Also, all the carreer fairs and job help was aimed towards the engineering and computer science students. Every one else was left to fend for themselves. Overall though I think it was pretty good.

The professors and classes are great, I really feel my education is worth while and I am getting a great academic experience. However, this is a very costly school to attends as far as housing, food, environment, and the area.

The majority of what I experienced from the University of Central Florida (UCF) was when I would go to visit my sister there, as she was a student for all four years. Personally, my sister speaks highly of UCF and when I saw that beautiful campus for the first time, I fell in love with the beautiful scenery, clever mythological theme, and an overall kind atmosphere that you are greeted with. What I would like to see is more diversity. Although I am only from South Florida as we have a lot of diversity-- which I have gotten kind of used to-- while I am sure that people have many different ways of thinking-- which I would love to explore-- I would like there to be more diversity (physically and mentally) in general.

The campus is very large and you are able to explore while staying on campus. There are issues with garages and lack of parking as well as the prices of things on campus. I think that the on-campus housing is too expensive for those who are trying to live independently for the first time.

I don't recommend going to UCF. It is called U Can't Finish for a reason. My advisor never answers me or will tell you the wrong classes to apply for which prolongs your graduation date. At least half of the chairs, water fountains, etc aren't updated and are squeaky or broken and slanted. Parking is the worst thing ever and you're walking super far to get anywhere on campus. Your advisor will only have options for appointments the first two weeks of the semester and then they go ghost. Seriously it really sucks and is super draining going here. I've done everything myself and have been given no guidance or support and when you do reach out, it does nothing and goes nowhere. They don't care about the genuine safety of the students and make classes still be mandatory when we have a sh00ter threat. I really can't think of much of anything positive to say about this school...

ai_summary: The University of Charleston offers a close-knit community characterized by small class sizes that allow for significant interaction between students and professors. Many students appreciate the faculty's commitment to their success, highlighting their availability and willingness to provide personalized support . Academically, students remark on the challenging coursework and dynamic programs, particularly in areas like nursing and leadership. While the campus atmosphere is described as friendly and vibrant, with plenty of activities and events, some students express concerns about the limited range of majors and the overall social scene in Charleston, noting it's not a typical college town. Additionally, reviews indicate a desire for improvements to facilities and equipment, particularly in athletics, along with a recognition of the school's higher tuition costs . Overall, the University of Charleston stands out for its supportive environment and strong student-teacher relationships, which many find rewarding in their educational journey.

reviews.text: Enjoyed campus experience, now finishing online. Feel like the education is worth the cost and degree will help advance career.

Great school all around. Professors are amazing! I would recommend this college to any student. Especially if you have interest in nursing’s this school is absolutely wonderful and so helpful in providing the necessary information to make you successful.

UC has truly been life-changing for me as an international student. Leaving my home country was not easy, but from the moment I arrived, the kindness and encouragement of the faculty and staff made me feel supported and welcomed. They didn’t just see me as a student—they took the time to understand me, guide me, and cheer me on through every step of my journey. What I’ve found here is more than an education; I’ve found a community that feels like family, one that has given me the confidence to grow and the courage to pursue my dreams
It has changed my life and I am very grateful to be a part of this community. It will always remain a special part in my heart.

Transferring to UC has been such a positive change for me because I get to be closer to home and the people who matter most. The faculty and staff have been so supportive, making me feel welcome and encouraged in pursuing Radiologic Science. What stands out to me most is the sense of community here, everyone genuinely wants to see you succeed.

When I got my first degree I was a non-traditional student on campus. I am all online now finishing up my BSN. Every time I have asked for help, someone was always there. Where be academics, financial or technical assistance was given. You meet students from many different countries and backgrounds, So you get to learn about different cultures for free essentially. The library is great with very helpful librarians. Great food options with beautiful scenery to look at while eating. The building is so beautiful many weddings take place there. Come see for yourself. You won’t be disappointed.

I have really enjoyed my experience at the University of Charleston. Every professor I’ve had has been wonderful and all great communicators. Due to the smaller size of the school, I actually got the chance to connect with my professors. There are a wide range of classes, including online, that fit into my busy schedule.

This school got me through quick and easy. I graduated with my Bachelors with a lot of knowledge to make me feel comfortable starting my new role as a nurse. I am currently in NP school and it is going well also!

University of Charleston has been very helpful for me pursuing another degree. The professors are eager to help and always make themselves available. The Student Solutions center made it very easy for me to enroll back into college.

Balancing a full-time job while pursuing graduate school can be challenging, but the University of Charleston has been an excellent fit. The supportive and accommodating staff have made it possible for me to attend full-time and stay on track. UC’s flexible, student-centered culture truly sets it apart.

Ive been attending UC as a transferr student and I genuinely believe that this has been one of the best educations of my life. Ive gotten very close with my teachers, one of them encouraging me to pursue making my own home computer server. They helped me find resources and a good starting place and I plan on starting this week! UC has a very personal learning structure, you wont get left behind if you speak with your proffesors which I really like as a student.

I am at University of Charleston in the Radiology program and it has been great so far! I have learned so much in the few weeks I have been here. I have also made new friends while being here which has been amazing!
My experience at UC has been filled with good moments. I’ve enjoyed my time as a student athlete because life on UC campus is really what you make of it.

UC is very community-based and promotes the culture of West Virginia while also encouraging us to help improve and better the world around us. The small class sizes mean that professors are more attentive to my needs. Overall, I feel that UC feels like another home.

Coming to UC has been life-changing for me. The soccer program gave me the best trip of my life in the UK, and as a transfer student, I’ve felt nothing but support from professors, advisors, and the international team. Being an international student is not easy, with all the challenges and being far from home, but here I truly feel at home. What really stands out is the people here always welcoming, always willing to help and this make all difference for me. That’s why I know UC is the perfect place for me to grow this year.

ai_summary: The University of Chicago is known for its academically rigorous environment that challenges students to think critically and engage deeply with a variety of subjects. Reviewers highlight exceptional professors who foster a supportive learning atmosphere , making academics both enriching and demanding. While many students appreciate the strong intellectual community and the diverse opportunities available, some express concerns about the social aspects of campus life, feeling that connections can be hard to make and that house culture is underdeveloped. The campus itself is considered beautiful and located in a vibrant neighborhood with access to cultural experiences in Chicago. Additionally, students mention the significant workload , particularly with the quarter system, which some find overwhelming at times. Although the University is seen as a place for serious scholars, opinions vary on the balance between academics and social life, with some finding it rewarding while others feel it can lead to stress and isolation. Financial aid options are noted as generous, but some students comment on the perceived expense of attending the University. Overall, UChicago is characterized by its strong emphasis on intellectual growth and a unique community that encourages exploration and connection among like-minded individuals.

reviews.text:
My experience at the University of Chicago has been challenging but rewarding. I really valued the intellectually curious environment. Students and professors are engaged, and classes encouraged me to think critically and push my limits.
One thing I would like to see change is how academic pressure is handled. While the rigor is a strength, it can feel overwhelming at times, and more emphasis on balance and mental health support would improve the overall experience. UChicago is an incredible place to learn, but greater support could make it even better.
I had an amazing four years at UChicago! I loved the variety of student organizations, and my professors were all very passionate and approachable.

University of Chicago is a school full of wonderful activities, staff, and people. It's easy to make new friends and join in programs or clubs.
It's fairly safe on campus grounds with UIC officers around here and there. It's easy to get to UIC and around campus. There's plenty of places to sit and relax.

Very friendly, supporting environment, even though its also very rigorous academically. Very prestigious school to get into, yet very rewarding and very worthwhile. The campus is gorgeous, the staff is very supporting and understanding, and the financial aid is easy and comprehensive. Definitely apply if you have high goals and ambition!
I like the Career Advancement office; very helpful. The culture here is internships, research and study abroad: these are the amazing things Uchicago make easily accessible.

The University of Chicago has a really beautiful campus that is absolutely breathtaking to anyone going there for the first time. That being said, it's not for everyone. The environment is built for a very specific archetype of people and you'll either love it or hate it.

I enjoy the intellectual atmosphere while attending the University of Chicago, since every student who attends seems to be extremely bright and extremely curious. In my classes, I am constantly challenged to become the best, brightest version of myself.
