As I am enrolled into the Political Science major, I have found my professors to be very well rated, and I enjoy the learning experience.

I like UGA as a school for academics and athletes, but the party scene is not my favorite. Partying is not something I particularly enjoys so thats why I don't like this scene, but for someone else who really likes to do that, they might love this aspect of UGA. Academics wise, I have loved all my classes here so far and my professors have been amazing. They are personable, understanding, and engaging during long lectures and classes. One of my professors in particular I have gotten to know fairly well and that has been sweet to have that personal connection that seems hard to come by in a university so large. I am involved in an on campus ministry as well as a Panhellenic sorority. These outlets allow me to hang out with my friends outside of class, and expand on our shared interests while getting involved in service opportunities, both on campus and in the surrounding community of Athens. I think these are a great way to get plugged in and be a part of the local culture.

The University of Georgia was a great school if you were pursuing a degree within the School of Public and International Afffairs. The teachers were masters in their subjects and were not only passionate but will to work with students to provide them the resources and support they needed to thrive academically. There are so many clubs and organizations for students to be a part of, and lots of study abroad or exchange opportunities if you are interested in living in another country.
For those that like to party, UGA is a great place to be. However, if you've never cared for frat culture it becomes a bit frustrating how much that takes over the school and even day to day interactions with others. For students of color there are little opportunities to feel supported when facing issues like racism or harrassment and the office of equal opportunity is not very qualified or good at their jobs.
UGA has been an amazing experience. The classes are informative and professors are knowledgeable. It has a great balance of rigor and fun.
Great school. Only qualm was with the Public Relations major, because I didn't take an 1102 English class (but had taken a higher level), I had to stay an extra semenster.
I transferred here from a smaller school, and it was the best decision ever. The professors are amazing, I feel like 99% of students are here with true academic goals, and I love Athens. GO DAWGS!

I have had a great experience the past 1.5 years at UGA. Every single professor I have had has been determined to help me succeed and learn as much as I can in the subjects I took. The honors programs has provided me a great experience in teachers that have managed to make me love chemistry and all my classes. Professors have supported me through my courses and I have made many new relationships allowing me to flourish and make the most of my experience at UGA. I am very glad to have come to this school and am excited to see where the rest of of my undergraduate journey leads me.
The student culture in Athens is amazing as there are people from many different backgrounds that all come together for unity, especially during football games.

UGA really tried to prepare you for the transition from high school to college - their orientation really helped to prepare me before I started classes in the Fall of '25 and started my first dorm room experience.

UGA was a great experience, but there were issues when courses transitioned online during COVID. Many students failed including me, a normal Straight A student, and my GPA has suffered as a result making it hard for me to get into grad school.

In my first semester at UGA, I found some of my best friends. I met so many amazing people from different backgrounds and quickly figured out the people I wanted to be around. I love the campus and student life because there are so many opportunities available to me. The school spirit is like no other. UGA is very work hard play hard and I am so happy I ended up here. There is truly no better college.

UGA offers the ultimate play hard/work hard environment. Classes are rigorous but interesting, there are many organizations on campus designed to propel students towards success (ex: the career center), great football, vibrant greek life, and an excellent going out scene. There is something for everyone!

ai_summary: At the University of Hartford, students often appreciate the small class sizes and the genuine care shown by professors, which allows for personalized attention and meaningful academic relationships. While many enjoy the vibrant community and diversity among peers, some students find the campus life and extracurricular options limited, particularly if they are not part of Greek life or varsity sports. Concerns about campus safety and inadequate student services are recurring themes, with complaints about poor dorm conditions and parking issues. The food quality also garners mixed opinions , alongside criticisms about tuition costs and financial aid support. Overall, students emphasize the importance of getting involved and making the most of their college experience, suggesting that enthusiasm and initiative can lead to a more fulfilling time at the university.

reviews.text: This school works for a very specific crowd. It's quiet and eerie, usually peaceful, but the administration will solve any issues if they arise. This school's motto is "just sweep it under the rug and keep smiling." It's VERY anglo-saxon white culture wise with notable racial bias. The academics are truly atrocious. This is the perfect school for someone who wants a boring campus, straight C's, and to just get through an undergrad degree smoothly. I feel like I've thrown away hundreds of thousands here. note- the Hartt school is entirely different and I cannot speak for that

My experience at the University of Hartford has been very positive overall. The professors are supportive, approachable, and genuinely care about students’ success both inside and outside the classroom.
The campus environment feels welcoming and diverse, which makes it easy to meet new people and feel connected. There are many resources available, from academic support to extracurricular activities, that help students stay engaged and motivated.
Academically, the university offers hands-on learning experiences that prepare students for real-world careers. I’ve grown both personally and academically, and I feel more confident about my future because of the opportunities I’ve had here.

The school is amazing. Registrars, advisors, and most professors are always willing to help. The tutors are amazing as well. Online classes have been great. The class work allows you to work together with other classes.

I like it here a lot. A very welcoming community in the arts program I am in, and the school itself is a nice place to be. Housing could be improved but I feel that's true for nearly every college campus.

This has been the worst experience that I have ever had in a school day. They did me soo wrong.I did finish the degree.It was supposed to come with a certification.I'm still trying to get certified.Because they never took the time to call me and tell me if I needed to be enrolled for fall semester then when I found out , I had been enrolled for , three classes Instead of only 2, so when I tried to tell them that I didn't want the 3 class, it was too late. They wanted me to pay for the third class, even though I was going to take that class in spring now for spring, I only have 3 credits and I needed 6, so my fafsa form to be approved. So now I owe them for the last class that was extra in the fall and for the upcoming class, that's in spring that if I want, my certification basically $,6000 more, which I don't know where I'm going to get from my job is Already asking for the certification and now since I owe the last class, this place won't even give me my degree this place is a joke.
Great academic and athletic experience, so many great people and great professors. Overall great school.

THIS IS AN EXPENSIVE COMMUNITY COLLEGE. BUT AT LEAST THEY GIVE DEGREES TO EVERYONE REGARDLESS OF PASSING CLASSES OR GPA (a girl in my class literally never came to a single class or did a single project and got a 65). I would advise anyone not coming for the theater program to pack their bags and run. Multiple programs, like architecture, are on the brink of being un accredited. Food is getting better. Campus is located next to a very rough neighborhood so don’t expect to do anything if you don’t own a car. Also, Hartford is the most expensive place per capita to live in the country. Everything about this location is horrible. If you receive a scholarship for 20-27k, just know, this is the minimum scholarship. They charge 60 and give 20k in scholarships to any highschool graduate with a pulse. This is a marketing strategy that allowed them from charging 35k a year to 60k+ real quick. If you come here, you’ll regret it.
-a student who did 5 years and regrets every little bit

I love my experience at the University of Hartford. I’ve had a really nice time here. My professors genuinely care about you and want to ensure you understand what they’re teaching. They’re motivating, encouraging, and very engaging. The professors know their students, and the school has a calm, relaxed, and safe environment. People are friendly, and I see so many students from different parts of the world, which I really like.
The main thing I would like to see changed is the food and the prices on campus. I’d love the food to taste better, and I think the prices should be more affordable. Snacks, food, clothes, and other items on campus are very expensive, and I really hope that improves.

i need to transfer! The housing situation and location of the campus are horrible. Housing is filthy and smells. Garbage everywhere. The campus is on the edge of a really sketchy area. I made a mistake coming here. Don't make the same one I did. The professors are just okay. So that could work. Lots of people I meet came here because they didn't get into their first choice. That is sad. That is what happened to me.

A sorry excuse for an “academic institution,” a lost cause, in fact, there was nary a cause to begin with. A premier example of the death of education under late stage capitalism. The school has decent programs for the engineering and business professions (technical school for the wealthy trades), the performing arts school is decent (keeping in mind that the Hartt school was absorbed by the university rather than developed by it), though home to many great artists, most attend the art school out of convenience rather than the school’s value, nursing and health sciences programs produce questionable results, and the arts and sciences school hardly does anything. The best thing this school could do for itself is fold. I find the fundamental incompetence and uninspired foundation of this school leeches into all of its victims and I’m truly ashamed at the prospect of possessing a “degree” from this seething pit of despair, unfortunately due to character limits I cannot elaborate further.
Very good overall, there is always activities for the students, classes/professors are good, and pretty campus.

My overall experience at the University of Hartford has been positive and rewarding. The campus offers a welcoming environment with professors who genuinely care about their students’ success. I’ve appreciated the small class sizes, which make it easier to get one-on-one help and build strong relationships with faculty and classmates. The university also provides great resources, like tutoring centers and career services, that help students stay on track academically and professionally. However, I would like to see some improvements in campus communication and more activities that bring students together. Overall, UHart has provided me with a supportive community and valuable learning experiences.

Great communication from the University and all staff made the transition into college less stressful. They offer an incredible Music program with enthusiastic and qualified faculty that will prepare me for my future career.

Huge disappointment. Teachers are just ok. The dorms are the worst. Filthy and full of mold. It never gets cleaned. Social life is not great. If you are into Greek life its ok. Food options are not great. Go here if you can't get in anywhere else. I think a safety school for a lot of people.

The engineering program is really good, but it takes up a lot of your free time. If you're not actively seeking out campus events they will pass you by. Good professors in the engineering program just make sure you talk to people.

ai_summary: At the University of Hawaii at Hilo, students often highlight the small class sizes and supportive faculty , creating an intimate learning environment that fosters personal relationships between students and professors. Many reviews mention the diverse student body and the welcoming community , which enhances the overall college experience. Academically, the university is well-regarded for its programs in sciences like biology, marine science, and Hawaiian studies, though some students express concerns about the availability of certain classes and bureaucratic inefficiencies. Student life is described as laid-back, with limited social activities and nightlife, making it less suitable for those seeking a traditional party atmosphere. While some students appreciate the peaceful surroundings and outdoor activities available in Hilo, others wish for more campus events and improvements in housing and dining services. Overall, UH Hilo offers a unique college experience characterized by its natural beauty , community spirit, and personalized education.

reviews.text: My overall experience at the University of Hawaiʻi at Hilo has been really positive. The campus has a welcoming atmosphere, and the smaller class sizes make it easier to connect with professors and classmates. I’ve appreciated how diverse and supportive the community is, and how the university embraces Hawaiian culture and values. The programs encourage hands-on learning, which has helped me grow both academically and personally. One thing I’d like to see improve is better communication about campus events and job opportunities for students. Overall, UH Hilo feels like a close-knit community that truly cares about its students.
Lots of different people from students to professors. Very nice place and people as well. Easy to understand learning and environment.

I enjoy UH Hilo because of how much they care for their students. They treat everyone like ohana (family). Everyone (especially the staff) are very helpful and will always go out of their way for you when you need it. The student body itself is an amazing experience. They are all outgoing, open minded, and friendly towards their fellow peers.
What I really enjoy about UH Hilo is how welcoming they make you feel, also the way they plan many events to allow for socialization.

My experience at the University of Hawaiʻi at Hilo has been unique and meaningful. The small class sizes made a huge difference; professors actually know your name and really care about how you’re doing. I felt supported in a way I probably wouldn’t have at a bigger school. The programs in science and Hawaiian studies stood out to me the most. Being on the Big Island, surrounded by the ocean and rainforest, makes learning feel real and hands-on. The Hawaiian culture on campus gave me a strong sense of connection to the place and community. Campus life is quieter than in big cities, which at first felt different, but I grew to appreciate it. It gave me space to focus on my studies, enjoy the outdoors, and build close friendships. For me, UH Hilo isn’t just about academics, it’s about connection: to the professors, the culture, and the land. It’s a place where you can grow in both knowledge and perspective.
The counselors and student help resources are amazing, well organized and have a solution for everything
Good Experience overall friendly atmosphere student life is great plenty of things to do on or off campus.
Amazing and would recommend to other students or high schools who are thinking about going to college

My overall experience with the University of Hawaiʻi at Hilo was meaningful, even though I didn’t spend a lot of time physically on campus. What I appreciated most was the university’s commitment to supporting students from diverse backgrounds and its focus on Hawaiian culture and community. The faculty and staff were approachable and genuinely cared about student success, which made a positive impression on me.
That said, I would like to see more resources and opportunities for students to engage on campus, especially in terms of extracurricular activities, networking events, and spaces for creative expression. Increasing access to mental health services and career development programs would also benefit students greatly. Overall, UH Hilo has a lot of potential, and with continued investment in student life and support services, it can become an even stronger hub for education and community connection.

The campus life at UH Hilo is very lively. There are always events on campus almost every other week. There are also available resources both online and on campus if you need help, guidance, or someone to talk to about anything. If there is one thing I would like to see change, it would be the tutoring center. It's only open 4 days a week and it's a first come first serve basis. There aren't many tutors for chemistry and I'm sure that the tutors themselves are also students who are only available at certain times. It would be a lot more helpful if there was another tutor who will always be available and also if the time frame was extended until the library closes. They are there to help as best as they can but honestly speaking, I wouldn't say I had the best experience; it was very rushed.

My experience with UH Hilo has been a rollercoaster of emotions. It is a very different environment from what I have always known, seeing new people everyday, class schedules changing, commuting to each class, it's been a fresh change. I like how different it is, it's definitely been a great way to branch out of my comfort zones.

The University of Hawaii at Hilo is located in an amazing place and contains amazing people but lacks the typical university lifestyle. The campus is pretty but there is not much student's activity happening on campus. The campus is a short drive from the beach and all professors are kind and welcoming. I would like to see the dining hall food changed up a bit, along with more on campus amentities, like hammocks or daily activities.

Although I had chosen the University to diversify my experiences, disappointed to say the least. If you don't plan to stay here permanently, the community does not welcome you. Moreover, the professors and staff members work together to ensure they can juice out as much money from students as possible--forget about getting grants or help with any creative projects. Regardless of merit, they do not show support. Next, the student life is non-existence: No parties, social gatherings and they provide about 2 vans worth for trips (if you get chosen to go). The closest beach is two hours away, they treat students like prisoners after 10pm and you can't host any event unless part of a club/association. Not worth the education.

UH Hilo is a very diverse campus with students from around the world. There is a positive learning atmosphere and supportive faculty that want you to succeed. The location is beautiful and there are many places to explore around the island. Hilo has a lot of local and native people, which allows opportunities to connect, learn and gain insight into the culture, traditions, values, and how one can respect and contribute to the community as a visitor. It was common for students to transfer back to the mainland - it is far from home and family for some. Student opportunities and support services include academic advising, gym, pool, grass field, tennis courts. The campus is walkable to parking permits was affordable. There is a food pantry that students can get groceries, books, produce grown on campus. The international student group held dinners and events for student to meet. There are opportunities to sign up for events to get transportation to hikes, beaches, and other activities.
University of Hawaii at Hilo is a unique, once in a lifetime experience. The University and Big Island community is truly incredible.

ai_summary: At the University of Hawaii at Manoa, students generally appreciate the beautiful campus and diverse , friendly community . The academic experience is a mixed bag; while many professors are supportive and genuinely invested in student success, some are perceived to lack teaching effectiveness, leading to varied educational outcomes. The grading system and curriculum choices receive a mix of praise and criticism, with some students seeking more structured assessments. Student life reflects a laid-back island culture , with an emphasis on outdoor activities rather than typical party scenes, which may appeal to those looking for a relaxed college atmosphere. However, students express concerns about the dorm conditions and the high cost of living in Honolulu, along with limited options for career counseling and advising. Overall, UHM offers a unique opportunity for students to explore their studies in a culturally rich setting, though improvements in facilities and academic support are noted as areas needing attention.

reviews.text: While I feel that the academics could improve dramatically in comparison to other universities across the United States, I do believe that the teachers do exceptionally well in helping their students succeed. The faciltiies at Manoa could also be more up-to-date considering how much the students spend on tuition a year.

Overall, my experience at UH Manoa has been great. I am a commuting student so I wouldn't be able to say much about dorm life, but classes and student life is pretty great. The campus is super well kept; the nature is beautiful; the student life is simple. They have a ton of food options, and they just opened up a Taco Bell. The food price is kind of ridiculous but Hawaii in general has ridiculous pricing. The gym is also great with a ton of options.

It is a good university, but the education school needs to be either clearer about how tough they are on students as far as workload or stricter on acceptance. Campus could be more accessible (not so spread out, elevators not much farther from the main path, elevators need to work).

I like my classes overall and the professors. Each has their own teaching style which allows me to understand my strengths and weakness. I enjoy the campus and its resources. I've noticed there is something new happening that benefits the students from clubs to fairs. The one thing I'd want to change about the campus is having more options regarding to food for those who can't eat majority of the food that's given. for example, a salad bar, those on keto, etc.

The University of Hawaii at Manoa is a large university with great research opportunities, many study spaces, cultural diversity, and is perfectly located. One change I would like to see is more of a campus life.

Overall my experience in person has been good. There is always a place to study, and I look forward to going to my inperson class. Some of my online classes are filled with nonsense readings that do not relate to the discussion's or topics for the week.

My experience so far has been amazing! I love the feeling of a smaller campus, it really helps me get more involved with the community because it is not as big and overwhelming. The housing could be better, many of the dorms are run-down. The party life is very different from other colleges. There are not many house parties that I know of, but there are a lot of 18+ clubs that people go to on the weekends.

I am in the middle of my second semester and I love it here. The professors are amazing and very helpful. Also my academic advisor has been amazing to with providing all kinds of information that I need.

As a freshman at UHM, my experiences so far have honestly been fine. Nothing crazy. My classes and professors are great. My workload is manageable. The advisors at UHM are very helpful which makes me feel more at ease about my future at UHM. I feel safe on campus and can fulfill my education without many worries. This university is the perfect fit for my wants and needs when attending college.

It’s nice. Pretty landscape but just an education. Some professors are amazing and some are mid. Kinda just luck of the draw. Dorming, you need a food plan but no need for dorm apartments. Food is pretty good quality but same things a lot

Manoa offers a variety of classes to take whether or not it belongs in your major. I especially love that it requires a Hawaiian pacific class when coming to study at Manoa. The food here is very sustainable and has a variety. Lectures and labs are interesting but learning can differ from professor and TA’s. Many of the students prefer a more lenient/easy to understand teacher for most of their studies and rely on past student reviews on rate my professor. The campus offers easy access to bathrooms and other facilities like the gym and study areas.

At UH Manoa, I got a good education as the programs were good and rigorous, but sometimes certain aspects were too much. Some teachers could get away with verbally abusing students often, which is my biggest concern. Other than that, the campus is very beautiful and located in a beautiful place, but campus life is pretty dead. There are many initiatives that are being taken to improve that, but they always seem to be of minimal impact.
Very good campus. Safe and lots of opportunities for education and extracurriculars. I personally live far from campus but it is very safe

I like this college because the campus life is very welcoming and all of the professors are there to help you if you need it. UH Manoa has plenty of sources to get help and to help guide you through all of your work. They have many places to hangout on and off campus, and because it is in Hawai’i the beach is always accessible. Most everyone can drive so many of us explore the island and even if we can’t drive, our student id’s give us free use of the city buses and rails.

I would like to see UH providing classes options that can be more accessible to working mature students looking for more flexibility in availability of classes. UH is a great University however it does not cater towards working students. There are not many options available in terms of class schedules and deadlines are imposed in a manner that does not give much room for working students to succeed. There are not much self paced courses and flexibility in program completion or courses class time to choose which can put strain on people who has to work in order to live.

ai_summary: The University of Houston provides a vibrant and diverse environment that supports academic and personal growth. Many reviewers appreciate the variety of academic programs and the commitment of faculty who are generally seen as approachable and supportive, although experiences with professors can vary. The campus is lively with numerous extracurricular activities and organizations, fostering a sense of community. However, some students feel that the large size of the university can make it difficult to connect personally with peers. Student life is enriched by the university's cultural diversity , with many opportunities for involvement and engagement, but some express concerns about safety and campus culture , citing it as primarily a commuter school. Overall, students recognize the value of the education and resources available, despite occasional frustrations with administrative services and dining options.

reviews.text: It is amazing. I am able to create life-long memories and friends. My professors were wonderful and taught me lessons I will keep with me for the rest of my life.

My experience at the University of Houston has been truly meaningful. What stood out to me most was how diverse and welcoming the campus feels. I never felt out of place. It genuinely became a second home. The academic environment challenged me in the best way, and there were so many resources available to support students, from advising to career development. Overall, UH gave me both a strong education and a strong sense of community, and I’m really grateful for that.
I have toured many times and have had many friends and family attend here, and every time I visited the campus I fell more and more in love with the area and the community.

Overall, the University of Houston is a nice campus with good professors and academics, however, sometimes it feels like they forget about other things that have impacts on students. All I'll say is, yes, they have a lot of resources for almost anything but sometimes they fail to tell us students important information that could be saving us time.

My experience at the University of Houston has been positive overall. As an engineering student, I’ve benefited from strong academic programs, supportive professors, and valuable networking opportunities through industry events and student organizations. UH has helped me grow both academically and professionally.
I especially appreciate the diversity on campus and the career-focused environment within the college. There are many resources available for students who take the initiative to use them.
One area that could improve is clearer communication regarding financial aid and billing processes, as adjustments can sometimes be confusing. Overall, I’m proud to be part of the Cougar community and grateful for the opportunities UH has provided.
I've been to the University of Houston as a high school student, and I'm planning on going, but so far I say that they are a pretty good school.

University of Houston stands out as a truly unique institution because it reflects the energy, diversity, and ambition of one of America’s largest cities. UH is a major research university that combines academic rigor with real-world opportunity, especially in fields like engineering, business, health, and the arts. Its student body represents cultures from around the world, creating a dynamic learning environment. Strong industry partnerships, urban location, and a commitment to access and innovation allow students to gain practical experience while shaping the future of Houston and beyond, making UH both distinctive and impactful for modern students everywhere today and tomorrow.
The University of Houston is a good school for its academics and connections to careers. The only improvement is more security/safety measures, but overall a good school!

My experience at the University of Houston so far has been good. What I like about the school is the athletics and events that go on, like matcha pop up shop or little vendors that set up tents at the campus to sell to students. One thing that I would like to see changed is the safety measures around the University of Houston. From what I've heard from the news and others, stepping away from campus is not safe, and seeing robberies happen inside the campus.

My overall experience at University of Houston has been positive. I appreciate the diversity on campus and how welcoming the environment feels—students from many different backgrounds make it easy to feel included. The campus has good resources such as the library, student center, and academic support services, which are very helpful. Most of my professors have been supportive and knowledgeable, and they genuinely want students to succeed.
One thing I would like to see improve is communication and organization within some departments, as it can sometimes be confusing to get clear information. Parking and transportation could also be improved, especially during busy hours. Overall, UH provides a solid academic experience and a strong sense of community, and I’m glad to be a student here.

I like going here for the most part, the worst thing is safety, though. Before the spring semester even started, we had 2 robberies on campus. Last semester, there was at least 1 robbery, and a student died not too far from campus. The surrounding area isn't safe at all, and the campus isn't too much safer. Never walk alone at night. Carry some form of protection, such as pepper spray, and let people know where you're going. I'm not sure I'll stay for all four years due to safety concerns, which is a bummer because I really like the classes and professors so far.

I currently finished my fall semester, and I am about to finish my spring semester at the University of Houston. I love this school! It was easy for me to make friends. I attended most, if not all, the basketball and football games; the environment is truly fun. Cougar Woods and Moody Dining Halls are really good. I would go there all the time if I could. The only thing that I will say is that safety is my biggest concern. We recently had 3 burglaries, and all were on campus, close to each other.
My overall experience at this school has been enjoyable. The people here are welcoming and open to other people and the academics/extracurriculars leave students feeling fulfilled.

Ive grown so much ever since coming to the University of Houston. What i love about such a big school is kinda how small you feel and how your dreams are limitless because your just someone in a crowd full of tons of people will dreams. How diverse people are here is awesome! the food is okay ,but Houston n is awesome. Coming from such a small city its been so nice seeing how far dreams can take you .

The University of Houston is a great school overall, with their sutdent diversity and clubs, you can surely find somewhere you belong. During the week, the campus is full of life and activities, and you see people walking to class or just hanging out in one of the many social areas. The food is also top-notch, even better if you have a meal plan. Safety has been an issue, especially as of late. The campus is located in a rough erea so it's to be expected. If you're not walking around alone during late hours and actually take the time to explore the campus, you are guaranteed to have a good time.

ai_summary: At the University of Houston-Clear Lake, students often appreciate the smaller class sizes , which foster close interactions with professors and allow for more personalized attention in their academic journey. The campus environment is described as beautiful and tranquil , with wildlife and green spaces enhancing the overall atmosphere. Many reviews highlight the supportive community of students and staff, contributing to a welcoming school culture where mental health and inclusivity are taken seriously. While there are numerous positive comments about professors' dedication to student success, some critiques mention issues like limited class offerings , disorganization, and difficulties with advising, especially for those needing flexible course schedules. The campus is seen as a peaceful alternative to larger schools, though some students desire more student life activities and food options on campus.

reviews.text: University of Houston Downtown was a great opportunity for a first generation student like me. It was the best in price but it screams you get what you pay for. A lot of my teachers treated it like it was a pocket money job. Perhaps it was, should student be able to tell? Some of the most hands n and education forward teachers were over talked and could get their teaching methods approved. In my opinion most of those teachers were the most creditable when it came to being a teacher!

Not ideal for undergraduates
As a freshman, I found UHCL’s environment challenging. The college seems primarily geared toward graduate students, and undergraduates often feel overlooked. Administrative staff and student helpers can be rude and dismissive, and phone or in-person support can feel rushed.

My experience overall at the University of Houston Clear Lake has been amazing, I love the people I have meet so far as well as the campus itself. The Housing there is simple and very close to the classes which is good for me since I live 1 hour away from the University.
What I like most about my experience is how smoothly I was able to transfer to UHCL. The advisors and staff were very welcoming and helpful.
Overall UHCL is a very calm academic focused college with many events and gatherings. There are a lot of resources and career fairs for help get you connected before graduation!
UHCL offers a wide variety of resources. However I’ve experienced liberal leftist practices of professors in the classroom and I’m not comfortable with this.

If I could give negative stars, I would. UHCL has been one of the most frustrating, disorganized, and money-hungry institutions I’ve ever dealt with.
I was charged over $700 for three Spring 2025 classes — two of which were already full and inaccessible, and the third I withdrew from after attending once (the second class was even canceled by the professor). I received no instruction, no credit, and no academic benefit, but they still demanded full payment.
When I tried to appeal, I was misled by Financial Aid, falsely told I had paid out of pocket (I didn’t), and pressured not to file an appeal because I was warned it might “cost me more.” After I filed formal complaints with state and federal agencies, UHCL locked me out of my account, refused to release my transcript (which I needed for military enlistment), and now they’ve even revoked my access to the campus gym — while still charging me for it as part of the same $700+ balance.
i had a good experience getting my bachelors degree so I kept at it at the same school and pursued/achieved a master's degree

Clear lake is diverse, everyone there treats you like family. I have entered countless diffrent university campuses in my life trying to find the right one, the one that will nurture and give me access to the life of my dream, and I really believe I found it in uhcl. Every hall i pass is students laughing, the joyfullness reems into everyone around them, you can see departments working to make our campus life funner and bright, advisors that dont think of you as someone little, they bring you up to be equal around everyone and truly help you find what you are looking for. But we also cant forget the food, truly a specticle I may be basic in what I like but clear lake clearly knows their students and what they need. Which is why I love this school so much, they clearly onlg want the best for us all

Studying at the University of Houston-Clear Lake has been a truly enriching journey.
The healthcare administration program has provided a strong academic foundation, with dedicated professors who genuinely care about student success. Their support has made it easier to balance my studies with work and family life.
Working at the Wellness and Recreation Center has been a great opportunity to gain real-world experience. I also had the privilege of serving as Secretary of the Healthcare Student Association and being a Student Associate of the American College of Healthcare Executives. These leadership roles allowed me to engage with students and professionals and gain confidence.
However, one area I found challenging and would like to improve was the limited financial support for international students.
Despite that, UHCL has shaped me into a stronger, more focused individual. I will always be grateful for the learning, leadership, and support.
Overall my journey at Unversity of Houston-Clearlake has been great! Professors and staff are very supportive!

When first applying to the university of Houston Clearlake, I originally intended to take classes at the Pearland campus. Unbeknownst to me the Pearland campus is for graduate courses. Luckily the drive to the other campus was not bad and the school was easy to learn (due to many maps outlining exactly where classes were on their website).

Very relaxed environment, but the Office of Strategic Partnerships didn't really do a lot to ensure graduating students would have a job. Advisory staff was poor (if present), but the faculty here is very interesting and eccentric.
I majored in Computer Science, and basically all my classes were in a building with a single outlet per room, haha.
Overall, not a bad experience, but avoid the Delta building at all costs.

I liked the various clubs and groups that catered towards various majors and cultures that help students become connected with helpful community leaders. As well, the groups offered spread knowledge of ways students can be involved in their majors in regards to internships and job opportunities upon graduation.

I like the ability to complete my degree online as it is convenient with my busy schedule. Between a full time job and running a pet sitting business, being able to earn my degree online has been a game changer.

ai_summary: The University of Idaho offers a blend of traditional college life and academic rigor, appealing to students seeking a vibrant community and diverse experiences. Many reviews highlight a strong Greek life and an active campus with numerous clubs, fostering connections among students. While academics, particularly in hands-on programs like Environmental Science and Agriculture, receive praise for supportive faculty , some express concerns about inconsistency in student services, specifically around financial aid. The campus is frequently described as beautiful, set in a picturesque town with plenty of walking access to various amenities. Tuition discounts for in-state students are noted, but housing quality and costs can be an issue. Overall, the U of I is depicted as a friendly, tight-knit community where students feel cared for and supported in their academic endeavors.

reviews.text: U of I is a great school with a lot of amazing programs and great professors. It is as not as diverse as the coasts, and Idaho is a conservative state. However, the town of Moscow where the university resides is very blue and accepting of all. The university has many different clubs to join and beautiful facilities. The dorms also host a ton of weekly events and activities to meet new people and connect with the people you live near. If you like having all four seasons and a small town feel while still being open minded, this is the college for you.

My overall experience with the University of Idaho has been positive. I’ve met supportive instructors and had access to helpful resources that pushed me to grow academically and personally. I especially liked the sense of community and the opportunities to get involved through clubs, events, and campus support services.
One thing I would like to see improved is a stronger focus on practical, hands-on learning. In some classes, we spend a lot of time on theory, but not enough time applying it through real-world projects, labs, internships, and industry-style assignments. I think adding more project-based learning, skill-building workshops, and real examples from careers would help students feel more confident and career-ready.

I enjoy how passionate my professors have been; my love for theatre has only grown exponentially since being here. My biggest complaint would just be tthe university trying to find every opportunity to charge me money. Despite that, The campus life is amazing, academics are well taught and received, and I feel respected and safe.
i love the campus is very pretty, most people are all very nice. The school has many resources to help you succeed and overall the school sets you up for success.
I have had a great experience attending the University of Idaho. I am glad to be back for my second semester. Love all the events they have for students and the free food or meals as a freshman.

I mainly like the social aspect of University of Idaho. I grew up in a small town so you start to know everyone in your town. I love how the University of Idaho is so diverse. I get to meet new people, get to learn new ideas, and get to have a different aspect on life. I’d like to see change at the University of Idaho is the eatery. Although they do have many options which is good. They tend to stick to certain types of foods. I’d like to see of variety of foods!

I love the U of I. Wherever you go on campus you can find people who are inclusive and welcoming. The campus is also so beautiful. Especially in the fall with all the changing colors and you really get the fall experience. I really love the campus rec center. There is a workout gym, 2 basketball courts, an indoor hockey rink, a rock wall, saunas and showers. I spend most of my free time in the rec center. I would 100% recommend applying to U of I you will make lifelong friendships and memories that you’ll never forget.

The University does a great job of making students feel welcome. Everyone is always very kind and helpful. There are a few professors who aren't great at teaching, making it difficult to learn that subject's material. But overall, most of the professors are great.

I like how the University of Idaho is in a college-town! Everything in Moscow, Idaho is centered around the university, which creates a very involved, community-based environment. The involvement from alumni is superb, which is crucial for our university.
I like the way that the school includes all different kinds of cultures and people into how they do things.

The University of Idaho is not a good college for international students. I would recommend exploring different colleges than this, no housing, no campus life, no good professors(just like you studying by yourself). Not recommended at all.

The University of Idaho is a true hidden gem. I decided to go here because they offered me the biggest scholarship and didn't expect too much from it. However, I was extremely positively surprised when I arrived on campus. Moscow and the Campus are gorgeous, the community is amazing and the food on campus is really good. The housing is way better in real life than it looks on the pictures.

My experience at UI is pretty well so far, the people are nice and the professor are pretty fun. I've been able to make tons of friends within my new community and I'm glad I chose this school to help me pursue my thirst for knowledge.

University of Idaho has been a really fun experience for me. I did dual enrollment through North Idaho College during High school and earned an Associates degree and THEN transferred to the University of Idaho and graduated with my Bachelors Degree in marketing just last year. With only 2 short years at the University, I decided to continue my education and earn my Masters degree to continue my stay in Moscow, which is where I am now!

As a freshman at the University of Idaho, I’ve been really impressed with my experience so far. The campus is beautiful, easy to get around, and feels very welcoming. I’ve especially enjoyed the beginning-of-the-year activities, which made it easy to meet new people and feel part of the Vandal community right away. The town of Moscow is also a huge plus—it’s charming, friendly, and has a great small-town college atmosphere that makes it feel like home. My only complaint is the campus dining; the food could definitely be better. Overall though, I’m happy I chose U of I!

ai_summary: The University of Illinois Chicago (UIC) offers a diverse and affordable college experience , attracting a wide range of students. Many reviews highlight the strong support from professors and faculty , with a commendable emphasis on student resources, including tutoring services and helpful advisors. However, the university's commuter-heavy student body can lead to a diminished sense of community and campus life, with some students noting a lack of social activities and events. While the academic environment is generally praised, there are concerns about large class sizes impacting individual attention and the inconsistency of teaching quality across different courses. The urban setting provides access to the vibrant city of Chicago , with many students enjoying the variety of dining and cultural opportunities nearby. Nevertheless, some reviewers express a desire for improved campus facilities and a more inviting atmosphere, as well as frustrations with financial aid processes and parking issues. Overall, UIC is seen as a good choice for students pursuing a solid education, particularly in urban settings, but may not suit those seeking a more traditional, tightly-knit college experience.

reviews.text: It is a very good university in terms of education provided however there can be some changes in terms of safety as well as more access to more professors and equipment for certain classes such as labs.

I am so grateful to have the privilege to attend this world-class, accessible school near the heart of Chicago. UIC welcomed me with open arms, even after the trials and tribulations I encountered while I pursued my associate's degree.
The professors and faculty are generally helpful; even if they aren't, the school is so big that you can almost always talk to someone else for advice.
Cost of attendance is relatively high compared to other public universities, like in California or New York, but it balances out when you take into account the high cost of living in those other places.
The school's proximity to Little Italy and the West Loop make hanging out around campus really easy and worthwhile.
One of the downsides is... you cannot expect to stumble upon a robust party scene here. It has to be found, especially if you are a commuter student.

I like the diversity in the classroom; my professors made me think differently and challenged me to get better. There are a lot of resources for undergraduate students. If I need help, it doesn't matter what; there is somebody who can help me.

I am a first year, college student who is currently pursuing a degree in biology/ health studies. I live off campus and enjoy the environment in my school and the people there in it. It is a very welcoming space with plenty of things to do in the area and places to eat. Academically it’s a very great school that challenges me to work harder and to further push myself to be the best student I can be.

In general, I describe my education at UIC as "bare bones." What I mean by that is that it is not your average American university with all of the tailgates and campus bars that make college appetizing for fresh 18 year old students. In fact, UIC has no football team. The sports are D1, yet games have very low attendance. Frat and sorority life is not big, but still present.
However, my education was immensely satisfying. In general, I thought my engineering professors were very good. There is a ton of research and professional programs at UIC to take part in. I also took many other classes which I enjoyed, like poetry and Italian language courses.
Above all, my favorite part about my education at UIC is being in Chicago. The students are very diverse and reflect the city. I would also take a Chicago speakeasy or chill B.Y.O.F. bar over a college-town bar any day.
I have had a good time in UIC in the time I was in there. The institution has a lot of resources that can be used really effectively to both determine and pursue a career.
A diverse group of people with various backgrounds and goals, which broadens your mind and helps you appreciate your peers.

Very good professors(especially Dan Subotnik-accounting and Robert Remini-history). Excellent value for the money(which I had little of). Finance classes have paid for themselves hundreds of times over. Passed CPA exam. UIC gets a big thumbs up👍

UIC can honestly feel like the opposite of a traditional college experience. The student life is limited, the campus is spread out in a way that kills any sense of community, and the energy often feels more like a commuter hub than a university. A lot of people go to class and immediately leave, so it’s hard to build a social circle unless you already have one. The campus can feel unsafe at night and even day, especially around certain blocks where there’s barely any student presence. The general vibe is academically fine, but awkward interactions, and an overall “in-and-out” culture that makes the school feel disconnected. It’s not the kind of place where you walk around and feel a college atmosphere.

My college experience was a meaningful period of growth and self-discovery. While navigating classes, meeting new people, and adapting to new responsibilities, I learned more about who I am and what I want for my future. College challenged me to step outside my comfort zone, develop stronger study habits, and become more independent. I grew both academically and personally, gaining confidence in my abilities and a clearer sense of direction. The environment encouraged me to explore new interests, connect with others, and build skills that continue to shape my life. Overall, college played a major role in helping me grow.

It is a very helpful college, but it was a little confusing to get to the point where I am. What I do want to have changed is how the holds work since I cannot sign up for my next classes due to my having tuition I sadly cannot pay for.
I loved my time at the University of Illinois Chicago. The diversity and atmosphere of the University is a such a great microcosm of what it's like to live in Chicago.

My experience at the University of Illinois Chicago has been both challenging and rewarding. I really like how diverse the campus is and how easy it is to meet people from different backgrounds. The professors, especially in Biomedical Engineering, care about what they teach and make the material feel connected to real-world applications. Being in the city also makes UIC feel full of opportunities, with hospitals, labs, and companies nearby. One thing I would like to see change is better support for commuter students since it can be hard to stay involved on campus. Overall, UIC has pushed me to grow, stay disciplined, and take advantage of every opportunity that comes my way.

My experience at UIC has been unique and unforgettable. The campus is beautiful and lively with students constantly engaged in events, sports, and sales. Everyone is friendly, but the campus gets quiet after 6 p.m. and on weekends because UIC is mostly a commuter school. Dorming at the ARC was one of my favorite experiences because of the modern, comfy architecture and the beautifugl view of the city. Although it can be hard to make friends on campus, UIC offers a wide vafriery of clubs and activities. My experience on the UIC dance team was unforgetable, I was given the opportunity to compete at Nationals against other schools all over the country. Our athletics are strong but we lack school spirit and it is unfortunate not many students attend the sports games. I am veyr found of our academics, I have had great experiences with TA's and professors and have enjoyed all my classes. I will say UIC does not compare to big state schools but it is still an exceptional school.

Great College, professors are tough at the school but fair my degree was an applied psychology so my stats classes were naturally the hardest. I finished my degree after transferring my credits. they transferred my credits so wonderfully. I think getting to know the resources and making friends in the Quad was fairly easy, and lots of party on and around the campus. ALSO UIC 1# school for bringing popular performers like Childish Gambino, Kendrick Lamar, and J Cole exclusive to students. I miss UIC priceless memories just walking a touring the campus.

ai_summary: At the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign, students often remark on the vibrant campus life and diverse community , which contribute significantly to their college experience. Many appreciate the dedicated professors who are generally supportive and engage with students, enhancing their academic journey. The campus offers a variety of resources, including career services, numerous student organizations, and social opportunities that cater to a broad range of interests. While some find the academic rigor , particularly in engineering, challenging, they value the growth it fosters. However, opinions differ on aspects like campus safety and the dining options, with a few highlighting concerns about the organization and environment during trying times. Overall, UIUC is recognized for its blend of a large university atmosphere and strong academic programs, making it a dynamic place for personal and professional growth .

reviews.text: The University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign is an amazing institution filled with kind people which makes it easy to make your “home away from home”. The campus has great transportation to travel on campus and outside of campus. You will not be bored while attending because there will be an event on campus. The course work is rigorous but doable with the free on campus tutoring resources. There are mental health resources for students that are struggling with finding their footing in college.

Overall, I've had a really great freshman year experience. I've made many new friends within my classes and RSOs. I plan to take more action within the clubs I am in next year, and hopefully pursue some leadership positions.
This schools got everything you could ever ask for in a school. Even if it doesn’t blow you away you will enjoy your time here. UIUC has something for everyone.

Great campus life with plenty of night life and campus clubs for everyone. Professors are very helpful and know a lot on their selected subject. Campus can be a bit confusing at first but other than that its great!

I love my university because of the community I found of other African American students. If I it wasn’t for me finding my tribe, I would’ve dropped out as UIUC did very little to support their small drop of black students!
It has been an amazing experience! They have great academics and also so much to do on campus! I'd highly recommend, you won't regret it!

If you live on campus, daily life is amazing. You will live with friends or by yourself, go to class, go home and make your own food or go out to eat. You will learn how to live independently just like I did, and the best thing is there is always people to study with you in libraries, especially in the Grainger library which are all ways full, extremely full in midterms or finals. The only thing is that buses were not always on time, sometimes it will be early a couple minutes or late a few minutes, but there’s a pretty high chance for your buses to be delayed for an hour or even disappears!

The institution has allowed me to grow as a person and meet amazing people. I have become more independent as an out-of-state student and being more dedicated towards improvement. I got to know people from multiple countries and backgrounds and I love how the university finds ways to include everyone. Their engineering program is amazing with kind and patient professors and many opportunities for research. The school does a wonderful job in including every student on campus and creating a big family.

I attended an In person college tour to the campus of the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign with my fellow seniors from George Westinghouse College Prep. We were welcomed by a comprehensive power-point presentation on college life from a student of the institution. After a quick game showing briefly how the College considers applicants, we went on a guided tour across the campus into the Main Quad of the campus. We went into one of the buildings around the Quad and got a sneak peak into campus life, it was bustling yet productive. Overall, the campus was absolutely stunning and photogenic, with a wide range of faces that emphasize the schools inclusivity. I loved the tour. Our group sadly didn't get to see the campus halls, so that's the only thing I wold change about my experience at University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign.
I really like the school, it's not too close but not too far from home. I love how the quad is and how most things are walkable distances the school has a great vibe.

The University is in the middle of nowhere and you really have to form a good group of friends otherwise there is not much to do outside. I recommend to join a club where you are curious to learn more so you make good friends.

As a sophomore at the University, I love the diversity and being able to meet so many different people from different backgrounds. I also love how flexible the professors are and how much the TA's work and care for the students.

Overall, I have enjoyed my time at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign immensely. I am a transfer student this year and they have made my transition as smooth as possible. I struggled academically in community college last year and the counselors and advisors have been incredibly encouraging, trying their hardest to get me back on track. I went from failing a class last year to now having straight As and a 4.0 university GPA. I also have many friends within the plethora of clubs and RSOs the campus provides. My creative writing club has kept me feeling so inspired that it has persuaded me to pursue a double major in English and Creative Writing. My self-esteem has increased significantly, my time management is becoming increasingly healthier, and I owe my development within the last few months all to this school. The process for applying for housing is lengthy and stressful, and the food can be mediocre at times, but those things are a small price to pay for a brighter future.
UIUC has a diverse campus that is highly adaptable. There's a lot a student can do here. Though it's not in a big city, champaign urbana makes it feel pretty dense due to the student population size.

UIUC is a great school. The academics are great. Not all professors are good but that is any school. There are so many majors and courses to take. The campus life is amazing, There is always something happening which comes with attending a school this large. There are so many different restaurants, all with amazing food. It is a great opportunity to try new foods with friends. Since UIUC is a D1 school, sports are a very important part of the culture here. Many sports games are free to students except for football and basketball. Sometimes there are cheap basketball tickets the day before games. It can be hard to find your people here because there are just so many students but the more clubs you join, the more people you will meet.

ai_summary: The University of Indianapolis (UIndy) offers a close-knit community atmosphere that many students appreciate, particularly due to its small class sizes allowing for personalized connections with professors. While the quality of academia is consistently praised, with many students enjoying their specific programs and the supportive nature of faculty , there are mixed feelings about the dining options and weekend activities. Some reviews highlight the campus’s challenges with safety and social life, particularly for those seeking a vibrant party culture . On the positive side, the university provides various resources to help students academically and personally, with many feeling welcomed and supported. However, concerns about the campus's location and the need for improved student activities and dining options were frequently mentioned. Overall, UIndy is seen as a solid choice for students seeking a smaller educational experience with dedicated faculty.

reviews.text: My experience at the University of Indianapolis has been mostly positive and rewarding. I appreciate the supportive professors who are approachable and genuinely invested in students’ success, as well as the smaller class sizes that make it easier to build relationships and stay engaged. The campus environment feels welcoming, and there are many opportunities for involvement that help students feel connected. One change I would like to see is improved communication and flexibility around administrative and financial processes, as these can sometimes feel overwhelming or unclear for students. Strengthening these systems would make the overall student experience even better and help students focus more on their academic and personal growth.

Uindy is a place that is so dependent on the person. It is rare to find a good professor that communicates clearly and effectively or actually gives you information instead of reading off slides. If a professor doesn’t like you it’s really hard to get around them as some have a monopoly on certain topics. The cost to go here is insane which makes living on campus out of sight for me. I previously lived on campus and I was miserable. My water smelt, the sides of my bed were wet when I arrived, and people don’t listen to quiet hours. They recently implemented Ace It Book Bundle which is a complete scam as they charge you based on credit hours and not the cost of the book. Events have gotten better over time but some aren’t worth driving. My favorite event so far was Houndsgiving which was really special. If you want to go to parties you need to use an app like Fizz or Instagram to find the location. People are generally nice but it really depends on your major. No one talks in Gen Eds.
I like how the teachers at Indianapolis are very involved in learning and helping you be the best you can be. I would like to see food options be better and healthier.

I have loved my experience at the University of Indianapolis! The campus life is great and the sports are so amazing here. I wouldn't change anything about the campus because they truly do so much for student engagement, academics, and athletics.

My experience has been a great one. The professors are well equipped with the knowledge and expertise in their areas of study. The campus life and student experience has been wonderful thus far. I am looking forward to each semester with enthusiasm. There isn’t any other place I would choose to complete my studies at this time but Uindy
I like the University of Indianapolis because of the smaller class sizes and the engaged learning with the professors.

I love the smaller student to teacher ratio. I can be able to communicate better with professors and cooperate well enough to be able to receive greater amount of feedback and help on my academics within their classes.

While I feel the area around UINDY is very unsafe and not east to change I feel they could be better about reviewing their professors and making it easier for students to get around or feel comfortable.
I transferred in from out of state and felt at home immediately. Everything is close by, an actual "campus" feel.
I love this university. The staff are so welcoming and there are so many great students. My quality of life is amazing here.
So far I have loved how helpful and involved the staff is. There are so many events and activities to choose from and I am loving the gym.

It’s a pretty small campus but everyone is considerable friendly and open to new things and new people. We have a lot of different people going there and many different things for many different people. The sports are very fun to watch and take part in as Uindy has very good sports teams. The professors are great but can be a bit awkward at times but they try their hardest. Thanks to the campus being smaller you pretty much will met everyone once and will make close knit groups but overall everyone is cool even the professors.

The University of Indianapolis is an overall great school. There are great programs and great faculty. However, the faculty and programs can tend to be disorganized. The campus is beautiful, but the immediately surrounding area does not seem very safe.
Overall this has been great so far! I’m excited to continue and start up with great new friends and people!
Everyone id so kind and helpful! I can’t believe I was so lucky to be apart of the community. I love this small community 🩷

ai_summary: At the University of Iowa, there's a strong sense of community and support among both professors and students, which many reviewers appreciated. The academics are generally praised for their rigor and the variety of programs available, with smaller class sizes in upper-level courses allowing for more personalized interactions . Student life is vibrant, with plenty of opportunities for involvement in clubs and activities, and the beautiful campus located in a lively Iowa City adds to the overall appeal. Some reviews noted concerns regarding the out-of-state tuition costs and the availability of parking, along with a mixed response to the social scene, which can be focused heavily on partying. Despite these issues, many students express a deep sense of belonging and pride in their university experience, highlighting the unique combination of academic and social opportunities as a defining feature of the University of Iowa.

reviews.text: My two favorite things are our therapy dog, Finn (he's a handsome and hungry boy), his handler, Anna, and the French Club, because j'adore le français.

I did my undergrad in psychology, not knowing beforehand that there were big names in the psych department in Iowa! While I can't speak for other majors, the psych program is cut above an average public university. Research lab participation, mentorships, and research opportunities were easily accessible. Professors are not only all very knowledgeable in their respective area, they also had excellent teaching and course designing skills! Iowa city had a great food scene as well, given the city size. Very diverse.

I like that the University of Iowa provides an array of resources for students. From academic planning, to tutoring, to student clubs, there is always someone to help you enhance your college experience. As a transfer from a local community college, I do not enjoy the large lecture classes as much as smaller, more intimate learning environments. However, the discussion sections offered as part of some big lecture classes are beneficial for asking questions and clarifying course content. I would like for students to have better access to their professors, instead of defaulting questions to teaching assistants. This would be beneficial for furthering the learning experience of students.

The current professors I've had are all extremely helpful & dedicated to their research. Lectures are hit or nuts depending on who you take, especially in lower level courses.
The campus is almost integrated with the downtown area; while distinctly separate, there's still a lot of overlap. Plenty of supports, for all things, make sure to take advantage of them.

I have had a great experience so far. I enjoy my specific program more than I did electives. My program is small so administration is much more personal and cares about you. When I was taking elective courses before I was accepted into the sonography program, everything was fine, but I wasn't having fun at school. The social life at Iowa is great. There is a ton to do outside of school and many student groups/organizations as well.

What I loved about the University of Iowa is that it’s a small campus, but it has incredible facilities, clubs, theater, and so much more. You don’t need a car to get around—everything is walkable, from classes to the fitness center, the library, and the Ped Mall (where all the bars, restaurants, and cafés are). The teachers were fantastic—down to earth, genuinely helpful, and really there to teach. Some classes had hundreds of students, but most were small, under 35. This makes such a big difference when you're learning new material and need a bit more support from your professor. I would highly recommend the University of Iowa to anyone that would like an excellent education in a small, safe, easy to get around campus.

I love being a student here- the campus life is amazing! I come from a particularly hard major, and hence my classes are tough. With that being said, most of my professors are very kind and helpful! There are definitely academic resources available that really do help!

The University of Iowa not only provides a stable and engaging academic life, but a community that is always there for you. During my short time at Iowa, I've met eclectic people that have welcomed me with open arms. Every where I go, there is a person willing to help and give their support. The professors genuinely care about their student's learning, give incredible feedback, and take time to prepare their lectures. My favorite part of the University of Iowa is living on campus. I live in the living learning center PIE (People In Engineering). I'm constantly surrounded by people that are taking the same classes as me and we work through homework together. It's very nice to be surrounded by students that understand your work load and can help you with it as well. Through PIE, I've also been provided with lots of opportunities like meet and greets with potential employers, study sessions set up by residential assistants, and academic planning nights. I've found my second home.

The University of Iowa is an amazing campus with a thriving student life. The classes are well-rounded to keep you engaged and learning without being overwhelming. The only problem I have is some recent changes to the universities' DEI programs, including the removal and erasure of many of the organizations that drew me to this school in the first place.
Overall pretty great! Good campus life, downtown life, and super diverse. As a student-athlete, just living life, while pursuing in sports and classes!

It's academically strong good overall in all factors.The University of Iowa offers a strong academic foundation and supportive faculty across disciplines. I appreciated the campus resources and inclusive environment. I’d love to see expanded financial support options—more scholarships, flexible payment plans, and clearer guidance for students navigating tuition and aid.
I like being able to visit. It was a good community and I enjoyed my time there. The people are great, and so are the teachers.

I’m in my freshman year and I have had a very good experience so far. Everyone has been so welcoming and supportive. For one, the opportunities are endless here at Iowa. I am studying Business and Finance, however some of the people I have met are in other programs of study.
Though the campus is large, I have not had any problems getting around. My residence hall is well kept and full of friendly people. I believe I made a wise decision in attending this university and I think anyone who attends would be happy with their experience.
I like it theres a lot of places to meet people and there are a lot of resources academically and personally through the school as well.

What sets this program apart is its emphasis on community engagement and hands-on learning. Studio courses connected us directly with Iowa communities, allowing us to tackle real planning challenges while building our portfolios. The program's flexibility enabled me to tailor coursework toward my interests in sustainable development and housing policy.

ai_summary: The University of Kansas offers a vibrant college experience characterized by a strong sense of community and a wide array of academic opportunities. Students appreciate the supportive professors who genuinely care about their success and the various resources available to help them achieve their academic goals. The campus environment is lively, with many events and activities, and the interaction with diverse peers enriches the overall college life. However, some reviews mention concerns about parking difficulties and high tuition costs , particularly for out-of-state students. While the sports culture, particularly around basketball, adds excitement to student life, preferences towards Greek life can sometimes create a divide in social experiences. Overall, students feel a sense of belonging and pride at KU, describing it as a place that prepares them well for the future, despite some challenges.

reviews.text: I love KU! I am from out of state but have always felt so at home in Lawrence. The town is beatiful with so many places to explore. Campus is so special to me there is literally so much to do and join that everyone has a place here. I also love all the people I have met, everyone is so kind and so fun to talk to. I love Wescoe beach and seeing all the fun things that KU offers. KU basketball is a plus as well the field house is an experience in itself. Football is fun too especially when we win. The atmosphere and community that is created here is one of a kind and I think I would want to be apart of anything else. I am so grateful to have chosen to go to KU, I finally found a community and a place I truly feel at home in.

My experience as a first-year student has been interesting to say the least. So far, as a black student i was expecting to see more diversity and people of color than the predominantly white schools I had attended in the past. However, though there is some diversity its only a few students within every hundred white students I see. Life outside the college is great! The community and places to eat are nice to go to on the weekends. But again, the school community hasn't been the greatest, at least for me. I just wish there were more flexibility and care within the school. Especially when it comes to the cost of everything, tuition, housing, food, etc.

My first semester at the University of Kansas has been an incredibly rewarding experience. I have thoroughly enjoyed my business-related courses, as they have provided a foundational understanding of the industry that I am eager to build upon. Additionally, the campus is remarkably accessible and maintains a vibrant, welcoming vibe that makes being a student here a joy. I am currently loving getting involved in clubs, and attending sporting events. I would 100% recommend KU to other students. I am genuinely looking forward to continuing my academic journey at KU and seeing what the next semester holds. Rock Chalk!

ai_summary: At the University of Kentucky, students express a mix of experiences regarding academics, campus life, and support services. Many appreciate the vibrant campus , beautiful surroundings , and strong athletic culture , highlighting the sense of community and opportunities for involvement in various clubs and activities. However, some students feel like just a number, with concerns about the university's prioritization of profit over student welfare. The quality of education appears inconsistent, with supportive professors in some areas, especially nursing and STEM, while others face challenges with poor course organization and communication. Additionally, issues with housing, dining, and parking are noted, along with a desire for more mentorship and inclusion efforts, especially for diverse populations. Overall, while there are positive aspects, several reviews suggest room for improvement in administration support and campus life dynamics.

reviews.text: I have thoroughly enjoyed my first year at UK! I would highly recommend it to anyone. You can truly find who you are!
An amazing university with wonderful classes and environment for all students. The school provides a lot for their students and staff. The school has a pleasant experience.
