I really enjoy the University of Kentucky, it is a great school with plenty of opportunities to learn and grow as an individual and meet lots of new people!

I am a Freshman from Minnisota and I am having an amazing experience! I passed up going to more "elite" schools like Northwestern, Wisconsin, OSU, and Michigan because I loved the vibe at Kentucky and the program (Engineering). I have zero regrets. Kentucky has been so welcoming, even for a liberal from up North! My friends have a variety of viewpoints but we all get along so well. The counselors are very helpful and the alumni network is really strong. I love the Basketball games and the Football tailgating is so much fun! Despite it being a large school, everything on campus is walkable, and Lexington is a really fun city, so much to do...horse races, hiking, nature, lots of great places to eat and a wonderful farmer's market on the weekends. I joined Greek Life and highly recommend. No hazing in the sororities, just great sisterhood (I made so many friends) and it's much more chill than other SEC schools. The dorms are gorgeous and the people are kind.

I'm in a small department, and the combination of large university and small department is a real sweet spot. Lots of resources, passionate professors that really work with their students and want to see them succeed, and incredible diversity on campus so it's hard to NOT make friends. The DRC is also really easy to work with once you're established as a student with accommodations.

I have had such an amazing time here at the university of Kentucky. Everyone in the college of fine arts is a big family, and we all care for each other. I have made so many new friends and continue to make friends as the semester goes on. I wouldn’t give anything up to continue to have this experience.
I just didn't like the way they prioritized grades and deadlines over the student's mental and physical well-being.
I am enjoying it better than I expected and would like to continue my college journey here. Hopefully, I can pay this semester’s tuition off with this.

I like how there is a lot of diversity with professors and students, there are resources for everyone and everything you would just need to look for it. Faculty are here to teach and they love what they do. It is a safe campus .

So far my overall experience at the university hasn't been the best. I have a DRC which allows me to have accommodations inside and outside the classroom, however when contacting professors or teachers about it, they don't see the emails or never respond because of the multiple ways of communication. Also safety and housing are a big concern of mine. With the growing number of student, majority of us have to live off campus now which creates a safety issues for a lot of students especially freshman who have never lived alone or without the safety of their parents. I think the school should try to do something about the safety issue and help students feel more comfortable around this area, since theirs homeless shelters and stuff around the campus.

I am originally from Pittsburgh and I personally loved everything about Kentucky. The community is so welcoming and kind. It is big enough where there is lots to do both city like and rural but it is also small enough where you don't lose yourself. I enjoyed Keeneland on weekdays or even hiking at Red River Gorge. The academics are amazing and facility truly wants you to succeed.

I have a good time in my classes with UK. My only trouble is that I don't get good communication with my professors. My classes are more like "learn it yourself" which is new to me as a freshman and hard to adjust to.
UKY has change my life in some many way it has opened so many opportunities for me. The campus is great the the atmosphere is awesome. Coming from NY the people are so nice.

I've loved every minute at UK. From the day I got here, this place felt like home, and its such a joy to spend four years here. I would choose it again and again. I feel motivated to go to my classes because I enjoy both the subjects and the professors I have. The campus is so welcoming, and I find myself wanting to be on campus for events and socializing. The sports are fun to go to, even though we would have a better football team if it was up to me.

I would love to see a bigger support for the international groups. So more events for them so people can go and experience them. So they might join those clubs and actually appreciate them differently than they have been.

ai_summary: At the University of La Verne, students appreciate the small , intimate campus environment that fosters strong connections with professors and classmates. They often highlight the personalized attention they receive in small class sizes, which enhances their academic experience and overall learning. Many reviews express a sense of community , with an emphasis on inclusivity and support from both faculty and peers. However, some students mention that academic planning can be challenging, particularly for those pursuing unique paths like double majors. While the campus is seen as beautiful and accessible , there are concerns about the limited number of social events , especially for commuters. Additionally, tuition costs and the perceived value of the education for the price are frequent points of discussion, with some feeling that the cost may not align with the academic rigor they expected. Overall, reviewers feel a strong sense of belonging at La Verne, but some desire more robust student life programs and events.

reviews.text: Not very detailed in explaining assignments at times. But overall, it was a good experience. I would like to see more online courses for my degree available.

I attend the University of La Verne, and overall, I have had a very positive experience in my program. One of the best parts of my experience has been how supportive and accessible the faculty are. Professors are knowledgeable and responsive. They truly care about their students and their success. The program is well structured and practical, allowing me to apply what I learn directly to my professional work. I also appreciate the strong sense of community and the emphasis on reflection, growth, and real-world application rather than just theory.
One of the more challenging parts of my experience has been balancing the demands of the program with work and family responsibilities. The coursework can be intense at times, especially while managing a full schedule, and deadlines can feel overwhelming. I am also a new mother who can sometimes feel overwhelmed. However, these challenges have helped me develop stronger time-management skills and resilience.
La Verne is such an amazing area, very small and calm. The class sizes are small as well, meaning you will know everyone and be able to work well with others. It was a great match for me

My overall experiences at the University of La Verne has been positive. I appreciated the welcoming campus community, supportive professors, and small class sizes that allowed me to connect more personally with instructors and classmates. The courses often tied directly to real-world applications, which helped me feel more prepared for my future career. I also enjoyed the many opportunities to get involved in campus activities and the strong sense of belonging among students. Beyond academics, the university offers a strong sense of community and opportunities to get involved, which made my experience even more fulfilling. Overall, I am grateful for the education and support I have received at La Verne, and honestly, there is nothing I would want to change about my experience here at La Verne.

If you're only about education and immersed in your classes and professors, then this school is for you. Other than that, the campus life isn't great. Not a welcoming school; it's hard to make friends unless you already know people who attend here.
it's a pretty great school i feel like the professors are more connected to the students and do a lot to help you out so it's easy to get help. you can tell this school helps you succeed

The University of La Verne offers an exceptional educational experience, supported by dedicated student advisors who are readily available to answer questions and provide guidance throughout your academic journey. The Financial Aid Office is staffed with knowledgeable and supportive professionals who work to ensure students clearly understand their financial responsibilities and available resources.

I have found the the staff and faculty members to be very kind and supportive. I have been treated with dignity and respect and have received helpful information and guidance that I feel will assist me in meeting my educational goals successfully,

University of La Verne is a very diverse university. If you walk around you will see people from different parts of the world which come together to build a community. The university is big on building community and making people feel at home. There are many student led events that are free for students and that is a moment they can use to meet new people and build connections with those people. The food that is given by the school is also amazing, they rotate and have different menus everyday and it has always tasted good.

As an international student, my experience at the University of La Verne was a mix of positive and frustrating moments. On one hand, the campus environment was welcoming, and I met some great people—both classmates and professors—who genuinely wanted to help me succeed. The small class sizes made it easier to ask questions and feel included, even coming from a different country and culture.
However, there were several challenges that really impacted my experience. The biggest one was the student portal system. As an international student, it was incredibly frustrating to navigate. It wasn’t very user-friendly, and when something went wrong—which happened more than once—it took a very long time to get it resolved. Communication was a major issue. Emails often went unanswered for days or even weeks, which is really stressful when you're dealing with time-sensitive problems like class registration, visa requirements, or financial holds.
I like how small and peaceful it is. There is a lot of space and only a few students, so, everyone has their own little niche to hide into!

I was so pleased with time at the University. Despite starting school admits a global pandemic, I felt encouraged, supported, and educated by the quality of professors the school provided. Upon returning back to school, I was so please with the stillness, beauty and campus life of ULV.

I got my Undergrad at the University of La Verne in 2007, had a wonderful experience and the quality of the esucation and student life was so fulfilling. I am just about to return for my masters and I couldnt be happier.

My experience at the University of La Verne has been meaningful and personal. The smaller class sizes made it easy to build strong connections with professors and classmates, and I always felt supported inside and outside the classroom. I’ve had the chance to get involved in clubs, internships, and even plan a study abroad semester, all of which helped me grow beyond academics. One area that could use improvement is academic planning. If you're pursuing something beyond the standard path, like a double major or studying abroad, it can be hard to figure everything out without clear guidance. That said, the sense of community at ULV is real. I’ve met people who truly shaped my experience and supported my goals. I’ve grown a lot here, and I’m grateful for the opportunities the university has given me.

The school is in a quiet neighborhood that has lots of little cafés and restaurants that are within a 5 minute walk. The campus overall is beautiful with lots of greenery. The school does a lot of of events to get students involved.

ai_summary: The University of Louisiana at Lafayette is often described as a welcoming and supportive environment , emphasizing a strong sense of community among students and faculty. Many reviews highlight the dedication and approachability of professors, with students appreciating both the academic rigor and the real-world applications of their studies. The campus, characterized by its beautiful scenery and unique features like a swamp in the middle, plays a significant role in shaping the student experience. While social life, including Greek organizations and campus events, contributes to a vibrant atmosphere , there are mixed feelings about the dining options and shuttle services. Students noted the affordability of tuition and a variety of resources, yet some expressed frustrations with communication and administrative support. Overall, the culture, friendly atmosphere, and ample opportunities for involvement make UL Lafayette a distinctive choice for prospective students.

reviews.text: Expecially in the psychology department the staff and students are wonderful. I enjoy the dedication to getting us into grad schools as well as the on demand aid from advisors

The University of Louisiana at Lafayette is a school that provides many opportunities to succeed not only while attending, but also once you have graduated and entered the professional world. These opportunities include on campus tutoring services, small class sizes, advising, mentoring programs, study spaces, research spaces, etc. Once you have completed your time at Ul and graduated, they also help with career services, internships, networking with alumni and local businesses, and building strong connections with industries around. This school puts on various events and activities throughout the year for students to meet new people and grow relationships with previously known individuals as well. With this, UL tends to always have a very welcoming environment that is diverse and inclusive to all with lots of school spirit. Lastly, This school works especially well with helping students learn time management and responsibility, as well as building confidence and independence!

I enjoyed my undergraduate experience at the University of Louisiana at Lafayette with the help of my friends and family. Although the years were fast and hard, I do not regret attending this school. However, I believe they can do better with the professors.

Being at ULL reminds me everyday that I made the right decision choosing where to go. The people here on campus are like no others that I have ever seen. Everyone is so incredibly kind ], welcoming, and prioritize their education. I never feel the need to worry about something happening to me that's to the on campus police and the blue emergency buttons all around and even off campus. The school has so much opportunity for to be active within the community on and off campus and to explore new places and people. There is always something going on on campus which allows me personally to grow my circle and meet new people.

As long as I've been here, it has been a very decent place. It allows for students to feel safe voicing their opinions, have great opportunities for their students, and make everything as easy as possible for everyone to access.

I loved ULL, I had to leave for unforeseen circumstances, but overall it was the best place I’ve ever attended. The professors were very good and caring, the atmosphere of the school was super fun and welcoming, and the environment of Lafayette was so exciting and gave a home feel. It was the best place I’ve attended and if I could I would go back immediately!
I loved the school and the academic programs available. I wish their were more funding options for graduate students who were not local, but other than that, everything was great.

When I came for a campus visit, I fell in love. The experience was great meeting different people from different areas of Louisiana. While in high school, I wanted to experience this campus because the advisors sold me on coming to the school and I must say I haven't been disappointed yet. I love the people, the atmosphere, food, classes, etc. I really like and enjoy being here and I can't wait to make my family back home in Monroe, Louisiana proud.
So far my experience has been great. The staff and professors have been really helpful. I don't think there's anything that I would change.

When I first started as a freshmen the orientation staff was very helpful with my transition and made it easy for me to fall right in as though I was family. My Dean was so wonderful and walked me through all my steps and made sure I knew what I was doing. Coming to ULL made me realize that I made the right decision.

So far, I am loving the campus and academics as a transfer student. I feel welcome and at home here! I feel valued within my program of study and I feel that I am given ample resources within that program.

This is my first semester at the University of Louisiana at Lafayette, and so far I am enjoying my experience. All the campus staff are very helpful and answer questions when I come to them. All of the other students that I have encountered have been very nice and seem to have a good attitude. I like how large the campus is, but transportation is provided to get around, and I do not have to walk very far. I can catch the shuttle bus. Overall, so far, I am very happy I have decided to come here.

So far it has been pretty fun. I am a commuter so I am not to involved in campus life or parties. Depending on the professor can easily affect performance in a class. Overall the experience has been one I would recommend to others.

Starting my freshman year at the University of Lafayette has been a journey full of excitement, growth, and new beginnings. So far my experience has been nothing but good. I've met new people, the food is delicious, and there's always something to do on campus.

My time here at the University of Louisiana at Lafayette has been nothing short of amazing. Since I've started my journey here, I've grown in so many vital aspects of my development. The diverse student, staff, and faculty community has provided the utmost support and encouragement not only for me, but for other students as well. There are so many resources in place to help students succeed academically, mentally, and socially. With 200+ student organizations, it's inevitable for students to find their place in our big Ragin Cajun Family. The university also hosts a multitude of family-friendly and safe events that promote a positive atmosphere. I've loved my entire experience here at the university.

ai_summary: The University of Louisiana - Monroe offers a close-knit , family-like atmosphere , where many students appreciate the campus's beauty and the friendly demeanor of both staff and fellow students. Academically, experiences vary, with some praising the dedication of professors who are eager to assist students, while others note room for improvement in organization and encouragement. Student life features a range of activities and events aimed at promoting engagement, although some students feel there could be more diverse and inclusive options available. While the campus’s setting provides a peaceful environment conducive to focus, some students express concerns about the proximity of entertainment and the overall liveliness of the surrounding area. Lastly, students value the university's affordability and supportive resources , but they also point out challenges, such as inconsistent administrative support and the need for upgrades in certain facilities.

reviews.text: ULM was a very good school for what I went for, which is music performance. It was specifically my private lessons teacher that was very good. The ensemble experience was not the best, but the music school itself was good. You get out of education what you put into it, so as long as you work hard and use the resources that you are given, it is very difficult to go wrong. When selecting a school, that is the primary thing that is needed in your mind. People who are hiring you do not care so much about what school you went to, rather they care about the skills that you can bring into their business to acquire more profits than they would have gotten without you. Overall, I would highly recommend ULM for those who are interested.

ULM is actually a pretty good school. Just that the envrionment can be boring at times and the campus food is not that good. If you live off campus, you'll be good though. I've never been thought by any problematic professor. They've all been cool so far
Like with anything else, you get out of it what you put into it. I wish I had asked for and received more support for my professors as far as my major was concerned.
I would like them to improve the dorms more and be a little more affordable because they don’t offer scholarships
It has been a very safe and comforting environment while away from home, and I absolutely value my time on campus.
I love it here. Everyone is so welcoming and ULM just feels like home. Student involvement is amazing. The food could be better but I will not complain if someone is cooking for me.

I've lived in Monroe for the last 5 years. While I was a senior in high school, I toured both ULM and LSU as possible colleges for me to attend. Even though LSU has more achievements due to them being a larger school, I felt that ULM was a better fit for me. They seemed to be more interested in me as a person rather than me as a number the way LSU was. Overall, I feel that ULM is the perfect fit for me and anyone who wants to have an individualized education.

I’ve only been here for a week and honestly it is kind of nice the teachers are nice and helpful, there are many resources to help yourself succeed here and many opportunities to make some friends just get out there like I should have go look around campus find your classes and if you are a male and in Madison hall I’m sorry this place is not the best it has really bad internet and although the cheapest definitely feels like it unfortunately but otherwise campus life is quite fun and enjoyable and I believe others would quite like it here.

It's pretty nice there, the fact that it's smaller than tech means each student can get a lot more focus. I think most of the staff I've met are very dedicated to their jobs. Honestly, the only really big changes I would be interested in are the upkeep in two things: The bathrooms (mostly the one in the HUB, it's disastrous) and updating Masur. It's horrible that people live there and it hasn't been updated in so long. It's already one of the furthest dorms out, and it's always having problems. My first semester, one of my friends said the heating was out for over 12 hours in the middle of winter. The social scene is pretty great, I only wish there were more meetups to encourage meeting new people. Maybe movie nights or book discussions. Most of the meetups I see posters for around campus are religious meetings, which there's nothing wrong with, it just shuts a lot of people out regardless of the religion.

I love ULM it’s culture is different lots of different back rounds and it feels like a family. When I started going there this year I wondered what it would be like and I feel welcome the students and staff were very nice to me.
The ability to be included. The great resource and support to succeed. The security and the extra the campus police will do to keep you safe.
It was okay I am currently transferring to Louisiana Tech in Ruston La for career purposes and personal reason
University of Louisiana is one of the best school. You can pursue your degree without having to worry about your financials.

I believe the university is one of the best for allied health programs. This includes physical therapy, occupational therapy, speech-language pathology, nursing, dental hygiene, pharmacy, etc. The university now has a Doctor of Osteopathic Medicine program. The surrounding area is to be desired, but the overall campus experience is very nice.
I would like to see more opportunities for student involvement or engagement on campus. I am involved in multiple student organizations and would like to see more events open to all students.

ai_summary: The University of Louisville receives a mix of positive and critical feedback from students about various aspects of their experience. Academically, many students appreciate the supportive professors who are approachable and genuinely invested in student success, along with a range of helpful resources like tutoring and study workshops. The campus itself is noted for its beauty , cleanliness, and manageable size, allowing for a close-knit community feel despite a larger student body. Student life is lively, particularly with a variety of extracurricular activities and a strong sense of school spirit surrounding athletics. However, some students express concerns regarding campus safety, particularly at night, and a perceived lack of diverse dining options . Additionally, issues with administration responsiveness and the financial aid process have been highlighted as areas needing improvement. Overall, UofL is seen as a welcoming place with opportunities for personal and academic growth, but with some challenges to navigate.

reviews.text: The teachers were all very competent. I enjoyed the classes and I enjoyed my professors. I didn't live on campus, and the commute did kind of suck, but it was enjoyable.

As a student at the University of Louisville, it feels like a place where there’s always something happening and plenty of opportunities to grow. The campus has a strong sense of school pride, especially during game days, and it’s easy to feel connected to other students through clubs, classes, and events. My professors are supportive and genuinely want students to succeed, while the variety of majors and resources makes it easier to explore future career paths. Overall, being a student at Louisville feels both challenging and exciting, with a balance of academics, social life, and real-world preparation.

I like the dining options and the walkability of University of Louisville. I wish there were closer and more free parking options for commuter students. More support and resources for transfer students would be nice.

The University of Louisville is a descent college. The college excels in fields such as engineering. However, there are some lacking aspects such as the food options on campus and some investments in certain departments such as the math and physics department.
I have just visited but the food there is amazing and everyone is very nice and wonderful, I am so excited to start my freshman year this upcoming August !

UofL is a great urban university. I currently major in Public Health and even though it's a small degree with only around 800 students, there is a large number of resources that helped me through my first semester as a freshman. The people here are very friendly and welcoming, even to out-of-state students. The sports scene here is pretty great too. Basketball runs the city, and the arena does get fired up on game day. Louisville as a city is overall pretty decent. Like any other city, it has some problems but there is so much to do here it's fun.

My time at the University of Louisville has mostly been a good one. The campus feels approachable, the class sizes (at least in my program) made it easy to actually know professors, and when faculty are invested, they’re genuinely supportive and willing to help you grow. I appreciated the mix of practical, hands-on work with theory, and the freedom to explore ideas without being boxed into a single lane. Louisville itself is also a plus: affordable, creative, and easy to navigate. That said, there’s room to improve. Communication between departments and administration can feel fragmented, and some processes are way more complicated than they need to be. Facilities and resources vary a lot by program, which can be frustrating. I’d also like to see more consistent funding and visibility for the arts.
Overall, UofL gave me space to learn, experiment, and figure out what I care about. It’s not perfect, but it’s a solid place to put in the work and come out better for it.

My time at the University of Louisville has been both exciting and challenging. I remember walking across the campus for the first time, feeling a mix of nervousness and excitement. The professors were supportive, always encouraging questions, and the campus community was welcoming. Being at UofL has taught me independence, teamwork, and the importance of balancing academics with personal growth.
I love UofL. It has such a homey feeling and i love the people i’ve met there. it has an amazing nursing program and it’s nice being near the city.

University of Louisville is a very homely school; it feels a bit like home but not far from downtown. The professors for the most part are friendly, knowledgeable, and willing to help. The buildings, some are new and some are very much in need of repair. The area in which the school is located is a bit more dangerous because there tends to be ones looking to prey on naive students.

My overall experience as a first-year college student has had its fair share of ups and downs. The thing I struggled with the most is being able to stay on tasks with certain assignments I may have due, however this is the most social I've ever been within my lifetime and I'm incredibly grateful for all of the friends I have made.

In my experience at the University of Louisville, I am primarily enjoyed the campus and athletic scene. One thing I would like to see changed are the food/dining options, as they are very low and not diverse.
I’ve enjoyed my time at louisville within the first two years i’ve been here, campus is fun, sporting events are awesome and there is plenty of resources for students if you need it

University of Louisville is a great experience; it has been exciting to live on campus and further my education daily. This university is a pretty great place to learn and live. The people are great, and the diversity is plentiful here, which is great for an African-American kid such as myself.

If you've never seen uofl's campus during the fall, you're missing out big time. Campus, party scene, and my professors are my favorite part of this school. I truly could not recommend a better school.

ai_summary: At the University of Lynchburg, students appreciate the small campus atmosphere and the close-knit community , which fosters meaningful relationships with professors and peers. Many reviews highlight the caring and approachable faculty , who are willing to support students academically. While the academics receive mixed reviews—with some students finding them challenging and others feeling they lack engagement—there is a consensus that smaller class sizes enhance the learning experience. However, there are criticisms regarding outdated dorms , limited dining options, and inconsistent campus safety, particularly for marginalized groups. Student life is vibrant for those involved in athletics or Greek life, but some note that it can feel isolating for others due to a lack of diverse social options. Overall, the university provides a unique combination of academic support and community, though improvements in facilities and dining could enhance the student experience.

reviews.text: As a freshman at the University of Lynchburg, I'd say my overall experience has been sufficient. Some things I have found enjoyable here are the smaller class sizes and the many activities and clubs to participate in. Although having an acceptable experience so far some changes could be made such as, more help finding job opportunities, parking spaces/regulations, better housing maintenance, and campus security. University of Lynchburg provides a supportive, smaller learning environment.

My time at U of L has been very memorable. Teachers and other faculty are supper supportive and will often work with you to make sure you succeed in your academics and enjoy your time there. I love the small campus because it creates a tighter sense of community. While some of the school facilities may be on the older side, current renovation projects are going to improve that for future students. As a student, there is many things to enjoy. There is two gyms, the library is cozy and has plenty of study spaces, the dining hall and other food places on campus serve great food and are open longer that one would expect them to be (10 pm!). Overall, the school really prioritizes students and their experiences, which I really appreciate. While there has been recent changes in the curriculum due to budget/economic concerns from the school, it seems that they are working with students and faculty to make sure these changes are effective and improving their educational potential.

My college experience has been mostly positive, thanks to the amazing amenities and resources available. I love the fully-equipped gym, the convenience of unlimited meals, and the beautiful campus scenery, which makes studying and relaxing enjoyable. The support provided by the college, whether it's academic or personal assistance, has been a huge benefit. However, it's definitely been difficult at times, and I’ve learned that college is a mental game—you really have to stay focused in order to succeed. Another downside is that some professors can be unresponsive, making it tough to get help when needed. Despite this, the positives outweigh the negatives.

I really enjoy the experience of a small college like this one. My favorite part is being a part of my swim team. My least favorite thing about it is that they are cutting my major, music, and now I'm having to take all my major classes this year.

I am a first year here at the University of Lynchburg. The housing here is good and I'd say the campus life is pretty lively. This area of Lynchburg is beautiful and I like to explore the shops around. The only downside I would say would be the heavy focus on the international students. It is amazing that we have people come here from all over but sometimes, it may feel like the administration cares more about the internationals than others. Overall, great school, great area, pretty good food, nice people, great professors.
I came to Lynchburg as a first generation student, and I had a truly transformational experience. I stayed to get my Master's and even ended up staying in the Lynchburg area. Love Lynchburg!

It’s okay not bad but not amazing either it’s a pwi so it’s not really that diverse but you’ll see sprinkles here and there. Campus is silent but in a peaceful way there isn’t unnecessary ruckus and it’s a small campus so everything on campus is walking distance which makes it nice. The academics so far is good the professors are fair and nice. There’s a lot of athletes they’re actually mostly athletes here so sports activities are a lot there’s multiple games per week across different sports. Food isn’t bad it can get tiring eating at the dining hall but it’s nice that there’s other options on campus like sushi, dominos etc. I feel like it’s a friendly campus and it’s actually really easy to make friends, upperclassmen are really nice and will help so it’s nice. As it is a small campus there’s a lot of familiar faces everywhere you go. The downside is that it’s in a small city in the south of Virginia meaning that there isn’t a lot to do outside of campus there’s one mall that’s it.

University of Lynchburg are a family away from home. University is a close-knit student community where my dreams and ambitions are encouraged and my potential is limitless. The university are committed to helping you succeed in the classroom and every aspect of my college experience. I am so glad be Hornet family.
I’m an incoming freshman and what I heard and seen this school is amazing! I’m excited what this school holds for me

Overall I like the School. If you are looking for a smaller university than it’s perfect. It’s everything a small school should be, you know all the professors and they know you and even care about you. I can’t go more than 2 minutes without seeing someone I know and being able to have a discussion. The dorms and rooms are fairly good but it’s like any other school. While some schools don’t have housing for their students after sophomore year Lynchburg has housing through senior year with dorms, townhouses, apartments and townhomes.
I just think it is a great school and really reliable for anyone and everything. The sports are amazing and some of the strongest sport scenes around and it’s amazing to see and watch.

I like how devoted the Professor are to their students, they really try to make connections. One thing I would like to improve is the food options of campus, there arent alot of options, and it can be expensive.

My overall experience at the University of Lynchburg has been rewarding. From the beginning, I felt welcomed by a supportive community of faculty, staff, and fellow students who genuinely care about your success both inside and outside the classroom. The professors are passionate and approachable, and they go out of their way to provide guidance and encouragement. I've grown not only academically, but personally as well thanks to the wide range of opportunities available, from student organizations and service projects to internships and leadership programs. The campus itself is easy to navigate, which makes it a comfortable and inspiring place to learn. I’ve built meaningful relationships here, and discovered new interests I never expected. I truly feel that the University of Lynchburg has prepared me for the next chapter in my life, and I’m grateful to be a part of this community.

My major program got sacked and turned into a teach-out. Some of my professors left midway through the year. I met nice people: peers and professors alike. People cared on this campus. However, there was an uncomfortable gap between the faculty and the administration. Academics were tricky but often felt like busy work, and progress was slow. I made good connections, but learning was bumpy and frustrating.

The time I have spent at Lynchburg was a personal one, and it was not an easy one. Academically, I struggled more than I expected, and overall the time spent at the school was not an enjoyable one. However, I do understand that everyone’s journey is different.
Through the tough moments, I have learned and is learning how to keep moving and what things are and are not for me. Growth doesn’t always come in comfortable ways. So looking back on the past years of being a student here, I realize it’s shaping me into the person I am becoming.

ai_summary: At the University of Maine, students often highlight the strong sense of community and the friendly atmosphere on campus, which fosters connections among peers and faculty. The support from professors is a common theme, with many emphasizing their accessibility and willingness to help students succeed. Academically, while some courses and professors stand out as particularly beneficial, there are mixed feelings about the consistency of teaching quality and the effectiveness of advising services. The campus life is vibrant, featuring various clubs, activities, and a lively social scene, though some students wish for more off-campus options and improvements in housing and dining. The experience can be impacted by the harsh Maine winters , which some find challenging. Overall, while the University of Maine has its ups and downs, it provides a solid educational environment with ample opportunities for engagement and personal growth.

reviews.text: I enjoyed living on campus (despite the poor quality of dorms), and I loved exploring Orono, Bangor, and Maine as a whole. There's a lot to do if you like to go outside and enjoy nature, and there is some good food off-campus too. The dining halls mildly improved while I was there, but weren't great overall. The Title-IX and Student Conduct is terrible. I unfortunately had to report what happened to me in the dorms to my RA, who had to tell their boss, who said it would be confidential but then gave the person a bulleted list of what I and others reported. The Student Conduct investigation never went anywhere and any attempts to talk to the RA manager went nowhere too (the manager told me they couldn't tell me anything). The Title IX case lasted over 6 months, ruining my entire sophomore year, and had no real results either. The investigator retired in the middle and instead of using a different investigator they just stopped investigating and then had the hearing.
The forestry program is amazing. Everything from the hands-on learning opportunities to the passionate staff makes me glad that I picked UMO

I have loved my experience within the Umaine system, especially UMO. I have been an early college student for the past three years, and I have nothing but good words for the program and all of the advisors, admins, and professors I have encountered. I have only taken one class in person (an incredible experience with wonderful professors), however I have talked to several people in one-on-one meetings, and I have had the opportunities to tour classes, and even be a part of mock class sessions. Everyone has been so welcoming to me, and Umaine at Orono met me where I was and rewarded every instance of me breaking out of my shell and asking for more. I have also taken a total of 32 credits strictly online through the Umaine system, and the support I received from advisors and professors really showed me that Umaine is a truly a place that cares about students and their success.

I am still a new student, but so far everything is good, the professors are very helpful, they try so that you understand the material. The food is good in both dining halls, and there are plenty of varieties. My only okay part is the dorm part, the dorms have public bathrooms per hall, but I am comparing this to the college I used attend, where bathrooms were shared between two rooms. Just do not lose your phone because that is where your id card is, which means you can't get into the dorms or eat without it. It is also cool, and you are less likely to lose it compared to a card.

So far my experience with the University of Maine has been pleasant. They definitely strive for equality and safety on campus. The administration is also very supportive of our life outside of school and is willing to help you out with any problems you might be facing. The amount of resources is endless. The only problem I faced is the teaching method from the professors. While I had many that loved teaching and were amazing, there were some that struggled to clearly explain lessons

As a student-athlete flexibility is important and I appreciate the range of options available. I am grateful for the opportunity to play my favorite sport at this level and receive a world class education as well.
I enjoyed it for the most part, but I've heard that they'll reduce your financial aid by the same amount of outside scholarship money you get

I overall enjoyed my experience at the University of Maine. As it does qualify greatly in research departments and academics, it may be more worthwhile for a focused study. My professors were lovely and truly cared about the education that they were teaching. I highly recommend the philosophy department!

I loved the fact that I was welcomed with love, for starters. Classes were good, and the environment was pretty good considering I was an international student. I adjusted quite quickly. I'd like to see even more diversity though, and maybe a lot more fully funded opportunities

They steal your scholarships, do not come. They steal your scholarships, do not come. They steal your scholarships, do not come. They steal your scholarships, do not come. They steal your scholarships, do not come. They steal your scholarships, do not come. They steal your scholarships, do not come. They steal your scholarships, do not come. They steal your scholarships, do not come. They steal your scholarships, do not come. They steal your scholarships, do not come. They steal your scholarships, do not come. They steal your scholarships, do not come. They steal your scholarships, do not come. They steal your scholarships, do not come.

The food options in student dining could be better and have more choices. Serving pork shouldn't be an option or a limited option due to health and religious reasons. Vegetarian options should be expanded.

I really like the University of Maine. It has good teachers, lots of clubs/extracurriculars, and a good student life. However, the only thing that I would like to see change is the quality of the residence halls, because, some of them don't get cleaned as often as they should, but other than that, they are pretty good.

Going to UMaine has to be the worst decision I've ever made. The facilities are old and uncomfortable (about a billion dollars in deferred maintenance). The Bangor area is depressing with nothing to do. The academic standards are not as thorough as I'd expected so everybody just uses AI without consequences. The university has a problem with retaining professors in many departments, leading to a bad academic experience. The mathematics department is a good example where my professor resigned a week before classes started and his replacement was horrible. The school does a poor job at engaging students without much of a community sense. The party scene was very disappointing. Do not join the Honors College, it is a waste of time. Nobody really cares about varsity sports and there's no school spirit. Housing screwed up my application so I got paired with somebody that wasn't my requested roommate. There are a few positives of the school, but I cannot wait to transfer out of here.

Umaine is a great place to learn; there is always somewhere and someone to help with your education. I am majoring in Civil Engineering, and even though it is the first semester, I am already enjoying my classes, and my professors are very enthusiastic. They also offer great help and have plenty of time to answer my questions. The library is also a wonderful space to do my work, and one of the highlights of the campus.
i’m very happy with my experience at the University of Maine so far! I’ve met any new people from all over the world and have learned so much from my classes.

ai_summary: The University of Mary stands out for its tight-knit community and strong emphasis on Catholic values , creating a supportive environment that's welcoming to all students, regardless of their faith background. Academically, students appreciate the small class sizes and the personal attention they receive from professors, who are described as dedicated, approachable, and genuinely invested in student success. While the campus offers a variety of resources and clubs for engagement, some students feel there's a lack of diversity and wish for more variety in campus activities and food options. The affordability of the university, particularly through scholarships, is a significant advantage for students, especially those from Catholic high schools. Overall, the University of Mary is seen as a place that fosters both personal and academic growth, with a beautiful campus situated just outside Bismarck.

reviews.text: guys the dorms are freaking AMAZING so I would definitely recommend this to everyone who is looking for a Catholic university y'all.
I am an online student at the University of Mary and have had great experiences with my professors and advisor throughout my educational experience thus far.
Very little transparency from administration as to policy. Don't offer spousal health insurance to employees despite being a pro-life university. Excellent professors

Overall, it is a very good university to attend, though it lacks diversity. Most students that are enrolled are either athletes, or extremely religious individuals. The rest usually comprises of people from around the area.
It’s a good school. I wish they were more lenient with living situations and compromising with students

I attended the Univeristy of Mary for my first two years in college. I had moved from it of state to attend and overall the area was lovely because it was so different to where I was from. But after a couple of months I thought the town was a little boring and found there wasn’t much to do. I found that I wasn’t getting a “normal” college experience. This year I will be attending a new university, I transferred out of U Mary because I felt that it was a little expensive to not LOVE where I was at. Also since it’s a private university I couldn’t get much help from outside scholarships. I was an athlete and a part of the band program in high school but found that I wouldn’t be able to do both in college. The music department is my favorite part of the school, even though it’s on the smaller side the professors and students seem to really care for what they do.
The University of Mary is an exceptional school that priorities forming the whole human person. This is achieved through their focus on academics, physical activity, as well as spirituality.

I visited the college and fell in love. It is beautifully located 10min outside of Bismark ND. The faculty and staff made me feel welcome and the overall vibe of the campus was one of community and friendship.

Starting at Umary this past year was definitely nerve wracking but there is absolutely nothing to worry about. I would say this school has the best kindness imaginable. If you are sitting alone someone will always talk to you and ask you how your day is going. If you would like a 4 year program with very accepting professors and peers I would strongly recommend the University of Mary.

My experience with the University of Mary has been enlightening in many ways. I have learned more about myself and those around me, I have learned subjects that I am passionate about, and I have made good friendships that I hope will last for years after graduation. I am looking forward to using my college experience to continue to grow and become a better person, student, and friend.

Being in Bismarck ND, this school does not have much to do off-campus. However, on campus, there are always events going on. Most of the teachers I have had were great, but of course, there are always a couple. The food is unlimited if you live in the dorms and is spectacular compared to other colleges.

I love it here. The community is really great and so friendly. I love my classes and professors so far. The professors I've had really care and will absolutely help you if you have issues with your classes. We have our own Caribou in the cafeteria and it's much appreciated. The cafeteria is open 24/7, except on breaks.

The overall campus is very clean, and safe. I am never worried about being safe on campus. The food is good, clean area and good options. Most housing options are decent, others are not. Definitely do some research on the different options.

It's a very welcoming campus where you quickly make friends and feels like home. The professors want you to succeed and will go out of there way to make sure you learn though you do have to put the effort into it. The winters are cold but most of the buildings are connected making it somewhat easier. The dining area is amazing with great chiefs that cook there. It is also open 24/7 making it perfect to grab a late night snack. If you live on the other end of campus you also have the ability to get food at the cove though it has limited hours. There are tons of perfect study areas and things to do on campus. There are many different types of clubs and organizations to join to keep you busy and have a break from studying.
The University of Mary is full of students and staff that are very welcoming. I was always welcomed with a simple smile or “hi” from someone which made me feel happy being there.

ai_summary: The University of Mary Hardin-Baylor is described as a welcoming and friendly campus where students often feel a strong sense of community . Many reviews highlight the supportive faculty who genuinely care about students' academic success and personal growth. The small class sizes enhance individual attention, making it easier to connect with professors and peers alike. While the campus is praised for its beauty and cleanliness , some students express a desire for increased student life activities and a more vibrant social scene , especially on weekends. The university's Christian atmosphere is appreciated by many, though some students note it can feel restrictive at times. Also, the cost of tuition is frequently mentioned as a concern, with suggestions that it may be prohibitively high for some families. Overall, UMHB is seen as a place where students can thrive academically while being part of a close-knit community.

reviews.text: My experience at UMHB so far has been great! I have loved getting to be in the nursing program here and would highly recommend coming here if that is what you are wanting to do! Our faculty in the BSN program make it a point to care about your education, help support you not only academically, but spiritually as well! Being a christian university has helped strengthen my walk with Christ and prepare me for my future career in Nursing! I have enjoyed my time here and hope to continue to create lasting memories in the last year an a half that I have here!

The best decision I ever made was going to UMHB. I found myself, came closer to Christ, and discovered my passion within the 3 years I attended. I was involved in several campus groups and activities which helped me feel connected to the school. The faculty and staff genuinely care about you- many still reach out to check in on me 3 years post grad. If you’re weighing options for a college, look no further. Belton is also the cutest little town that is close to Waco and Austin. Truly the perfect spot!

I loved my time at UMHB! There were so many different student organizations and traditions to be a part of that I never had to be bored. I also love how much they encourage and open opportunities for students to be plugged into a local church and encouraged in their faith on campus. I would highly recommend taking the time to get out of your comfort zone and attend a new event or activity on campus. You never know what new friend you might meet doing so. I met several life long friends as a student who will be in my life for years to come and am so thankful for the opportunities UMHB gave me, both while in school and in the future.
I loved my time at UMHB! The community and Christian environment is amazing. All of the professors are super personal.

My experience as a transfer student at the University of Mary Hardin-Baylor was very positive and welcoming. From the moment I arrived, the faculty, staff, and students made the transition smooth and helped me feel like I truly belonged on campus. UMHB’s supportive academic environment and strong sense of community allowed me to grow both academically and personally. I truly appreciated the smaller class sizes, approachable professors, and the emphasis on faith and service, all of which have made my time at UMHB meaningful and rewarding.

I loved my time at UMHB!
It was a time where I grew as a person and a child of God! I am so thankful for the way that the Lord used UMHB to encourage my walk with Him!
I absolutely loved it! I appreciated all the opportunities that they had to help me grow in my faith and academically! I got to create life long friendships!

My experience at UMHB was incredible! The classes and professors were so on top of things and cared about their students and the success of the students! For me personally the relationships that were built, the things I learned that have helped me in life, and the irreplaceable memories that were made are what makes UMHB so special!
Fantastic school and fully loved my experience there. I wish that they still had the same student life and experiences that I had when I was there.
I liked the caring professors who wanted every student to succeed and Coaches who wanted to win but also ensured their athletes were healthy to compete.

I loved my time at UMHB. When considering where to go to college, UMHB's Christian values, campus beauty, small classes, and opportunities to get involved were what ultimately drew me there. God definitely blessed my college experience with lifelong friends, amazing professors, leadership and service opportunities, and an education that prepared me well for my career.

I loved my time at UMHB. It was a smaller Christian school so your professors really knew who you were and actually cared about you. They wanted to see you do well in college. There were so many leadership opportunities that I was able to be involved in, that really helped me learn how to work with people, put on fun events and traditions and helped find lasting friendships. Really felt like I belonged at UMHB. Being at a Christian university was something that I was looking for, and loved being at. You could talk about Jesus and learn more about him through out the school in the classroom and outside of the classroom. The local area also has some really great college ministries at local churches, which is also something that was important for me to find as well. UMHB was a great place for me as a student! I loved my time that I got to experience at UMHB!

UMHB felt intentional in all areas of my life: academic, spiritual, and personal. The small class sizes really helped my academic growth along with my advisor who would regularly take time to intentionally support me and my goals. The student life and traditions are amazing and so helpful in grounding the student body together. UMHB really does help students connect their purpose with their spiritual life in a way that I’m not sure other universities have. If that is even a thought in your mind, this should be your first stop.
Going into college, I prayed for a great Christian community, and the Lord abundantly blessed me at UMHB! :)

I graduated from UMHB in 2003, met a fantastic guy, and married him as quickly as possible. I now work at UMHB, and both of my children are students here. It's been interesting to recall the process of registration, housing, and financial aid when I was a student and see how that system has evolved, but one thing hasn't changed. My kids are still dealing with real people who genuinely want to help. They aren't a number; they are known by name, just like I was. Just like every college experience, what you put into it is what you will get out of it. If you want connections, a good education, and solid friendships, you can find them at UMHB. It's distinctly Christian, so you should be aware of that going in. You also need to know - don't look at your financial aid package and assume that's it. TALK TO the financial aid department. Ask questions!

ai_summary: University of Maryland, Baltimore County (UMBC) is described as a school that prioritizes academics and offers a supportive environment with a strong focus on student success . The professors are generally approachable and dedicated, providing valuable resources and attention to students; however, experiences can vary greatly, with some students highlighting a lack of personalized help. The campus is characterized as quiet and pleasant, appealing to those who prefer a peaceful study environment, though it lacks a vibrant nightlife and extensive extracurriculars, leading to some feelings of isolation among students. Many reviews mention the diverse community and numerous clubs available for involvement. Complaints about limited food options and campus accessibility were noted, alongside feedback regarding the administration's responsiveness and the challenges faced in connecting with academic advisors. Overall, UMBC is portrayed as solid for academic pursuits but may require adjustments for a livelier campus life.

reviews.text: Overall all so far my experience has gone pretty well with the adjustment to this new chapter in my life. However personally for me it can be challenging to make new friends as I don’t live on campus since I am a commuter and a lot of events happen in the evening. I do like though how welcoming everyone is to everyone and the clubs there are to join. I would just like to see change the time for certain events since it can be harder to go in the evening.
Some of the clubs here at UMBC aren't that engaging. The food in the commons are not good, I wish we had better food.
Very good school however the on campus life is lacking since alot of people dont live on campus or just comute to the school

I liked the events offered on campus. I loved getting the chance to meet people. I live on campus, and I enjoyed being able to have my own space to go to on campus after my classes ended. I use the gym, or what we call the RAC adn I love it's amenaties. Overall, I love the teachers, opprotunities and resources available to me.

Over all very good, there are so many free resources to students to help pass their classes which is nice. I like how there are studyrooms avabiale. I feel the pricing of food could change for commuters because its to high

School is great known for not having a party scence or stuff to do but you just have to get out and meet the right people and make a initiative to take control of your experience the college experience isn't gonna come to your dorm room.

UMBC is decent. Very few campus life events, even fewer that are for fun and not additional informative events. The Student Event Board repeats the same events every semester, and a lot of them center around straight-to-landfill freebies.
Professors vary. Academic rigor for school of arts majors is average.
Accessibility sucks. Campus is many many stairs. Door openers often don't work.

The campus has multiple different events that can be for professional, academic or for entertainment purposes every week, it is a perfect place for people that like being within a big community but feel that they can have their own way and place to be there.

At UMBC, they put an emphasis on inclusion, a sense of community within the students, and also providing support and resources to their students. I appreciate that UMBC is willing to work with its students who may not have outside support as they go through college. I do like how UMBC has many different organizations for students to join and all the different events they hold for students to come together and just spend time and get to know each other outside of the classroom. I would like to see more preparedness when it comes to inclement weather conditions however; classes have been canceled for multiple days due to snow not being cleared from the parking lots and the campus in general.
Outside of issues with a specific math teacher, My experience with UMBC was generally ok. Nothing particularly good or bad.

pretty good overall. The area is a little quiet, not the place if you want to go to a party school. The academics are a high stanard and require you to work and study hard. Good environment overall if you want to be put in the work on your studies.
I enjoy the campus life and the diversity on campus a lot! I do think the campus food could be better quality, especially in the True Grit Dining Hall. Many people get sick from it.

Being at the University of Maryland, Baltimore County, I did not enjoy the experience of being a college student, but it taught me how to be independent and take responsibility for my own life. Living on campus provides you with these experiences and introduces you to people from other countries. I met several new people who became both good and close friends. This school also provides good opportunities for clubs and jobs if students want to attend and gain experience. The only change I would make is the dining hall food, since they cook decently sometimes. I feel like the main dining hall should improve its cooking style to improve the quality of the food. I feel like, similar to UMD, there should be more food places that we know to provide other opportunities.
They have dedicated professors who actually want to see you succeed. I'm really glad that I selected to go to this campus.

I joined UMBC as a transfer student. I transferred in due to feeling mentally unstimulated at my past university. Since joining every moment has been memorable. The professors are amazing and the campus is beautiful

ai_summary: At the University of Maryland Eastern Shore, students often highlight the strong sense of community and support among peers and faculty, making it feel like a family-oriented environment . The professors are noted for their dedication to student success, often going the extra mile to provide assistance. However, many reviews point out consistent concerns regarding the quality of food services and the cleanliness and condition of dormitories, suggesting areas that need improvement. The campus offers a wide range of clubs and activities, contributing to a vibrant social life , though some students feel the rural setting limits off-campus options. While tuition is considered affordable, there are mentions of organizational issues within administration, especially regarding communication and transfer processes, which can be frustrating. Overall, UMES is described as a supportive place with great potential, valued particularly by those seeking a culturally rich and diverse experience .

reviews.text: Decent school/program (electrical engineering). Good selection of degrees. Equips you for a real world career upon graduation.

University of Meraly isn’t sure out of my experience. It’s a good school. The living is pretty good. It’s not bad depending on the people you live with, but it is a good community though, and I would recommend it to other highschoolers or seniors looking for college.

very poor grounds with outdated materials and classrooms that haven't been updated in years, only one cafeteria that closes at 8 with the nearest food places a 20 min walk. Inconsistent horrible communication, almost charged me for multiple rooms, health center appointment only and nurse practitioner has a schedule no one knows. Shuttle services have no wheelchair accessibility, are in a van with the logo smeared off, and have only been on time for me once in my whole year, does not follow the schedule at all. Disability services are kind and will fight for you, passionate professors who are here because they care. Caring and supportive art department.

My experience at university of Maryland of eastern shore wasn’t what I expected at first, but learning more and adjusting to it, meeting new people, and in being in a new environment changed my perspective of it which makes me feel like I would want to continue my 4 years there.

I am a freshman at UMES and have been here for two months. I would say overall this university is a great school if you are introverted- it has a very quiet, calm vibe and you can stay focused during the day to get stuff done. The people and professors are kind and always willing to help, and everybody here is very friendly. Though I give this school a good rating, I personally will be transferring out because I do not like the location of the school or its size. It is very isolated from everything and you need a car to go into the nearest city whenever you want. I do not recommend coming to this school if you are an extrovert that likes going out and staying busy. If you prefer the small, quiet vibe, this place is perfect for you!

I definitely would never recommend anyone along their child to attend this university especially if they're a female. The university does not provide a safe environment and sticks to a policy where any incident results in both people being guilty. So if you are being physically assaulted or sexually assaulted you are told to never fight back. My daughter was assaulted three times in a row by her roommate and before the assault happened she went to her RA and told her that her roommate was becoming very hostile and they did not do anything thus allowing my daughter to be attack repeatedly in her room. The third time she attempted to fight back to protect herself and we had to call the police but the university without reviewing the police report said that she was guilty for fighting back. This is not a safe environment and this as traumatize my child. I would never allow any child to attend here if you have any concern over their safety and their well-being.
Nice and chill vibe. The university is very diverse and a great HBCU offering a huge range of degrees and programs

I am having a interesting time at University of Maryland Eastern Shore, it is in a rural area with a small town feeling that often puts you at ease. For what my father has told me the food has vastly improved since he was there last and I have to agree with him, there is a large library with supportive people who will point you to where you need to go.

My experience at the University of Maryland Eastern Shore has been a different one for me to say the least. In my first year of going here it taught me how different people are, how many people in life you will meet that are from different places and what you can expect out of others. There is great food but it is quiet compared to where I come from in the city.

My experience at the University of Maryland Eastern Shore has been okay overall, but definitely mixed. The campus is nice and the professors I had were supportive, but it can feel really slow and boring at times, especially if you’re used to a busier environment. There aren’t a lot of things to do on or around campus, so you really have to make your own fun. I think they could improve by offering more student events and activities to keep people engaged. It’s a good place if you want a quiet, focused college experience, but if you like a lot of social life, it might feel dull sometimes.

My freshman year at The university of Maryland eastern shore was more than I could imagine . I met a Great group of people who are like family to me now . I enjoy walking around campus and finding new things everyday. campus events like spring fest and homecoming are really worth going to you get a great experience from it. the only thing I would change at umes is the food in the cafe but there are days that the food is good like soul food Thursdays.

UMES was the worst decision I ever made. I’ve had awful roommates, lived in unsafe housing, and dealt with admin that ignores real issues like break-ins, assaults, and toxic culture. Advisors lied, professors gaslit me, and no one cared whether I graduated or not. They build fancy new buildings for show while students live in fear and chaos. Campus life is empty—nothing but parties, smoke, and empty promises. This school only cares about money and reputation, not students. It drained my family and my spirit. I’ve wasted four years here and gained nothing but regret. Don’t make the same mistake. Do NOT come to UMES.

I have been a student at UMES since August of 2023. Thus far, I have immensely enjoyed attending. I love that my instructors are available to me at convenient times outside of the classroom if needed and many of them will help to push the students to achieve excellence in his/her classes. I would like to see more transportation availability to and from the campus since I am a commuting student. Sometimes, I have to show up to campus very early because the bus may not be available closer to my scheduled class times. Overall, I love the campus and the atmosphere. I enjoy being around such a diverse population when I am there.

I love how open, welcoming and diverse the school is. I knew it was the school for me based on orientation and so much more, everything they said during orientation was lived up to and that is what I love the most. I would like to see more people engaged on campus.

My overall experience with the University of Maryland Eastern Shore has been positive. The campus is welcoming, the professors are supportive, and the community feels like a family. I’ve appreciated the opportunities for academic growth and cultural enrichment.

ai_summary: The University of Maryland - College Park offers a large and diverse environment , with plenty of opportunities for academic and social engagement. Many reviews highlight the rigorous academics , particularly in STEM fields and business, alongside the vibrant campus life complete with numerous extracurricular activities and events. While the campus size can be overwhelming and sometimes leads to feelings of disconnect, students appreciate the supportive faculty and available resources, which help foster personal and academic success. Some concerns include challenges with administration and safety on campus, as well as variability in the dining options and housing. Overall, UMD is recognized for its strong community spirit , proximity to Washington D.C., and the potential for students to find their niche within the large student body.

reviews.text: I think that the University of Maryland offers a diverse experience for all students; however, I think that the administration could do more to care for the students and offering the best experience possible rather than just optics and public opinion of the university.
I feel by attending UMD I am taking the steps to reach my future goals. I enjoy student life and have actively participated in school events.
fun & different. it’s very diverse and it’s plenty of things to do. The athletic facility is phenomenal

I enjoyed attending my courses at the University of Maryland, College Park. I studied under the CMNS College (College of Mathematical and Natural Sciences) and studied for a Bachelor's in Biological Sciences, with a focus of Neurobiology and Physiology!
I really enjoyed my coursework, though tough, but the professors were very supportive and encouraging. I would also highly recommend the Shady Grove Campus in Rockville for your 3rd and 4th year coursework if in this focus; the faculty is amazing, the small, clean, and tight-knit campus is welcoming as well. I loved it so much I was an ambassador for the USG Program and I can't wait for more to experience their wonderful guidance, mentorship, and professorship. I highly encourage transferring to the 2-year program if you are dually enrolled in high school or completing the Early College pathway.
Much love to the UMD professors and campus at USG <3

As a resident within College Park campus, I believe I am getting a full college experience. Life on campus is not bad, with unlimited decent food, and a standard room. However, I do get concerned due to the numerous steam outbreaks, hot water outages. The campus itself is very beautiful and I enjoy studying in libraries and listening to lectures in a big, beautiful lecture hall. Overall, UMD is a great place to learn and study, but I do think I will commute next year as the residence halls can get tiring after a year
Maryland is a great experience for people that are interested in this amazing college. The people here challenge you while also being helpful and inclusive.

I love the campus- its beautiful with a lot of sidewalks and an overall nice area to have classes in. I wish there was at least one more dining hall beacuse there are only three. The sports are not the best to watch.
Is a great school with great people. Made many great friends and learned a lot. Professors are really helpful and understanding if there are any issues.
Like being in a community of friends. Felt alone in a class of 300. Wish exams were more connected to lectures.
I like the university as a whole as there are many different student opportunities for education, careers, and social life.

Housing needs significant updating for the pricing of room and board. Classes are a bit of a toss-up depending on the professor you take a class with. While there are many helpful professors that provide notes and satisfactory learning material there are also professors who have little to zero communication in regards to work and expectations and provide no notes or learning resources.

I like the culture at the University of Maryland. It is close to home so I can commute and/or stay on campus to work out at the gym, study or hang with friends. I appreciate the diversity on campus and learning from fellow students about their cultures, traditions and beliefs.
I don't agree with some of the approaches of the professors to essay writing. We are assigned prompts and when we respond, they try to encourage us to change our opinion/approach. I believe we should write for the prompt assigned; how we feel, what we believe. I also disagree with the finals schedule. Some finals are scheduled on days and times that we aren't even in class or on campus. It doesn't make sense to schedule finals on days/times that aren't in line with our class schedule. Many students have jobs and other commitments that make it difficult to meet the final exam schedule.

The University of Maryland is a beautiful place. There is a lot of fun things to do like go to sporting events, get some food, take walks, travel to Washington DC and much more. The school itself is lovely, there are so many people and a ton of different crowds. There is something for everyone.
I like the school and I am enjoying my time being at the school as well.The classes here are great and the curriculum is very easy to understand. I enjoy being in the UMD environment.

I am currently a freshman at the University of Maryland - College Park, doing computer engineering (letters and sciences- freshman connections). The campus is very beautiful, but also big. The food is pretty good, the academics are rigorous but fair, and the professors are very hit or miss (I have had a few very bad ones and a few very good ones, you just have to check TerpPlanet and RateMyProfessor before enrolling). I think that UMD is an amazing choice for Maryland students who are looking for a good challenge at a very reasonable price.

ai_summary: At the University of Massachusetts Amherst, students frequently highlight the vibrant community and supportive atmosphere , often describing it as a home away from home. Academically, the university is noted for its strong programs and dedicated professors, although experiences with faculty can vary, leading to mixed feelings about the overall quality of education. Students appreciate the extensive dining options , frequently lauding the food as a standout feature of the campus experience. The social scene is energetic, with plenty of activities and clubs available, although some students feel the need to be proactive in finding their niche. Housing can be a concern due to high enrollment numbers leading to occasional overcrowding issues. Overall, UMass Amherst is viewed as a solid choice for in-state students seeking diverse opportunities , a lively campus life, and a strong educational background.

reviews.text: Great school with many opportunities, you have to make sure you take advantage of the opportunities though because it is a very big school. Great social life and campus life outside of parties. There is something for everyone there!

I liked the campus food, definitely is the best in the country. I'd like to see more study spaces for students other than the library. I'd love to see more activities going on around campus like pop ups, fundraisers, events, etc.
