Since it's such a big school, everyone can find friends / community. There is a decent music scene with lots of pop-up concerts and people are generally welcoming. Most professors care about what they are teaching and the university has a lot of tutoring resources to help support students.

UMass Amherst was an amazing school. I graduated in four years with two bachelor's degrees and highest honors from the honors college. The school's size and support systems made that possible. I'll be starting medical school this summer. One warning: don't count on Pre-Health advising at UMass. They wrote my committee letter. Beyond that, I received no support. You'll need to plan your own pre-med journey.

The academics are great but there isn't a lot to do in the area surrounding campus if you don't like going to the frats. The dorms are pretty nice, but housing is not guaranteed after your first year. There are many clubs on campus, so you can find pretty much any extracurriculars here!
The school has been a great academic and social experience. I look forward to seeing the school continue to grow and support all students and people in the community.
I really like the people here. Mostly everyone is welcoming here and it was easy to make friends even though I am introverted. I would like to see a change in professors.

My experience has mostly been welcoming, being able to find and connect with new people. There is a strong sense of community, faculty members, and resources that allow students to grow in regards to academics, dorm life, social aspects, and more.

Overall, the University of Massachusetts Amherst offers a strong academic experience with a wide range of programs and opportunities. The campus has a lively atmosphere, with active student organizations, Division I athletics, and a diverse community that makes it easy to find your place. Professors are generally knowledgeable and supportive, especially for students who take advantage of office hours and campus resources. UMass Amherst also provides excellent research opportunities and career support, making it a solid choice for students looking for both academic rigor and a well-rounded college experience.
A good change from high school. It's full of new opportunities to explore and new people to meet. Lots of good food, and there's always some sort of activity going on during all hours of campus
Very good school that provides a lot for new people. Will definitely give you an experience that you will never forget

The University of Massachusetts Amherst is a well rounded school that provides students with the ability to customize their college experience. The big campus allows room for students to grow while the many clubs offered can make the big university feel smaller.

I transferred to UMass this year and I couldn't be happier with my decision! UMass has a really good food, campus life, and academics. I am also in the swimming team and athletics department is also really good and we have so many resources.

My experience at the University of Massachusetts Amherst has been overwhelmingly positive. The academic environment is challenging in a way that pushes me to grow, especially within the Animal Science program where hands-on labs and supportive professors have strengthened my passion for the field. The campus community is welcoming and full of opportunities, from clubs and research to events that help students feel connected. Overall, UMass Amherst has provided me with a strong foundation academically and personally, and has played a major role in shaping my goals for the future.
UMass is a great school for people who want to both have a good education and have amazing food. The campus is also amazing all year

ai_summary: At the University of Massachusetts Boston, students experience a diverse campus with a strong commitment to inclusion , along with a picturesque waterfront location . Many reviews highlight the accessibility and approachability of professors , with many expressing a genuine interest in student success. The academic programs are generally described as solid, though some students desire more rigor in certain courses. As a predominantly commuter school, students often find social life challenging, with many noting that the campus can feel empty outside of class hours. Housing is a significant concern , with limited options for upperclassmen and high costs for off-campus living. While the campus is undergoing construction and improvements, some facilities and services could use updates. Overall, UMass Boston is perceived as an affordable choice , but those seeking a vibrant campus life may want to consider their fit carefully.

reviews.text: A good experience to connect with others. A great way for me to learn more about the school and build relationships with others.
good professors, good resources, overall a very solid school - I commuted, took most of my classes online, my degree was reasonably affordable

I enjoyed attending Umass Boston. It is a diverse school with a lot of opportunities and majors. I did a 4 year program and my major was criminal justice. there are a ton of classes which you can learn more about the fundamentals of criminal justice. I wish there was a graduate program for criminal justice.

I personally have been having a pleasant experience at the University of Massachusetts Boston. From an academic standpoint, professors are very understanding, and lectures as well as class materials are easy to obtain and are well explained. The student life aspect is also very good so far during my first year. The campus is lively and exciting. There are always events taking place that involve any and everyone from different backgrounds.
So far as a freshman it’s good! I don’t stay here all day so I don’t eat at the cafe instead I just wait to I eat at home. My classes are going pretty well I’m actually thinking about studying abroad

Being a freshman at UMass Boston feels like joining a community that celebrates diversity in every possible way. The campus is large and open, filled with people from countless backgrounds, yet there’s a shared sense of respect and equality that makes everyone feel welcome. Every day brings something new — events for different communities, club activities, or just moments to relax by the water with friends. The campus itself is lively but peaceful, with sports fields spread out and plenty of places to study or unwind. Classes are engaging, and while some professors are more flexible than others, there’s always support when you need it. The dining options are great too — from familiar food chains and cozy coffee spots to places where you can warm up your own meal. Student employment opportunities also make it easy to gain experience while studying. UMass Boston truly feels like a place where you can grow, connect, and find your rhythm in college life.

My experience at the University of Massachusetts Boston has been pretty good so far. Considering I am a commuter student, I don't get to see much of the social and campus life because I am really only on campus for classes.

This place is full of a rhetoric that if you don’t believe the same way you will fail. This school is very unfair and takes things out on you for not having the same beliefs. They are so worried about teaching a liberal ideology, where if you speak up you’re not going to succeed. Ive dealt with this my 4 years here and have told everyone to never come here.

Overall good experience
Clean and safe areas
Nice dorm rooms, not noisy
Students are genuinely nice and helpful
What I like about Umass Boston are the many opportunities and guides given. They help each and every student achieve what they strived for.

The waterfront campus is beautiful. Always one of the things I look forward to. The professors can be a hit or miss with some of them giving their all & others not caring one bit, giving their TA’s all of their work to grade & having disorganized lessons. Academics are good; not too difficult but challenging enough for the student to apply themselves in beneficial way. The support is horrible especially advising. Reaching out feels like a waste of time & often a slap in the face. Student life is good if you’re extroverted. An introvert (especially a black or POC one) will struggle with making friends and joining clubs here.
umb can rea..y be a weird place, no campus life/clubs no great activities the only reason i chose to attend was proximity to boston at a cheap price but then dorms are only for freshmen

My experience at UMB wasn't all the way good nor all the way bad so I could say it was right in the middle. Overall it is a good school to go to I would recommend it to someone if they lived in Boston or in one of the towns close to UMB because it is a commuter school for one and two it's pretty cheap to go here. One thing I cannot stand about the school is the cafeteria options it's the same like 5 things everyday I get so tired of it they haven't changed anything about it since I was a freshman. The parking is also kind weak too.

My experience at UMass Boston has been meaningful and eye-opening. The professors are supportive, and I’ve learned a lot especially in the sciences. What stands out most is the diversity; I’ve met people from so many different backgrounds, and that’s really shaped how I see the world. Since it’s a commuter school, you do have to make an effort to get involved, but once you do, there are great opportunities through clubs and events. The campus by the water is beautiful, and being close to Boston opens a lot of doors. It’s a place that encourages growth if you seek it.
The professors here try their best with the students. I have never felt out of place or that I didn't like the school.

ai_summary: At the University of Massachusetts Dartmouth, students report a mix of experiences that highlight both strengths and weaknesses. Academically, many find the professors supportive and engaged, especially in specific programs like nursing and graphic design, which are frequently praised for their quality and hands-on learning opportunities . However, there are concerns about inconsistency in faculty quality and some bureaucratic issues, such as difficulties with financial aid guidance. Student life appears to be a bit of a mixed bag—while there is a sense of community and various clubs, the campus vibe is described as lacking excitement , with some students feeling bored by the limited social scene. The campus itself is noted for its rural setting and diversity, but many reviewers criticize the food quality and the need for improvements to campus accessibility. Additionally, financial stress due to tuition costs is a common theme, alongside mixed sentiments about the surrounding area's safety. Overall, UMass Dartmouth offers a decent educational experience, but prospective students should consider these factors when deciding if it's the right fit for them.

reviews.text: Campus food is amazing. Great new leadership in culinary. Food is so diverse and delicious. Can't believe that the meals are restaurant quality every single day.
I think that umass Dartmouth is a great school and the experience has been amazing great for meeting new people.
the professors and academic advisor are very welcoming and helpful. they help you through everything step by step with whatever u need

This school is filled with high achievers, as well as interesting and kind people. I enjoyed the bonds I made with my friends on the football team. I feel like those are a group of guys that I’ll never not be in contact with. Furthermore, the athletics are first-class for a division 3 program, and i really enjoy attending sporting events. The people you meet within your classes are also really kind people who are there to learn and assist their fellow peers in anyway they can. The teachers are really helpful and flexible to cramped schedules.
I like how to dorms for first year students are newer and the dining hall has good food. I wish they had more club information fairs and activities to involve the students.
Although I am a commuter student, I find it difficult to socialize with some of the other students. It almost feels like I’m at a community college.

My experience at UMass Dartmouth has been positive overall. The professors in the nursing program are supportive, and I’ve found helpful resources like tutoring and advising. I would like to see more clear communication around scheduling and financial aid, but overall the school has prepared me well and given me a strong foundation for my future in nursing.

The parking situation if you live on campus is horrendous, particularly if you live in the Woodlands. Be prepared to almost never be able to park outside the building you live in, and have to hike in the elements with a backpack or groceries. The administration has been made aware of the parking complaints and the response has basically been “oh well, there’s nothing we can do about it”. In addition, if you have to park and hike from one of the farther lots to your dorm, your options are to walk in the mud/grass right beside a ditch or on the road. They recently added speed bumps, but that doesn’t fully deter people from speeding.

I struggled horribly last semester due to my five year relationship failing and my daughter and I having to learn to survive on our own. The academics team reached out and helped ensure I could continue my pursuit of my degree. We created a plan and they offered help in way of providing people to speak to should my mental health decline. Without this I would have failed and never finished my degree and felt like a failure. The professors here are so helpful and amazing and the entire academic team is what made me see I can accomplish my goals.
Resources are very good. Everyone I have come in contact with has been very helpful and want you to succeed.

UMass Dartmouth became home in a way I never expected. As a Black woman in business, finding community wasn’t always easy, but joining the Black Student Union and living on campus helped me grow. I went from feeling unsure to confident, surrounded by people who pushed me to be better. I loved the late-night study sessions, real conversations in the dining halls, and the support I found in unexpected places. Campus life taught me to be proud of where I come from and who I am becoming. While I wish there were more year-round support and representation for students of color, I’m grateful for the growth, friendships, and strength I’ve gained here. I used to think college wasn’t for people like me. Now I know I belong.

My experince as Umass Dartmouth was ok . I was filled with ups and downs but I felt a little Iscolated. Mostly because It seemed everyone paired up on the first day. I am interverted and have high social anxiety so I was unable to talk with people without feeling like I am intruding.
It's a very diverse school that offers a lot of free activities each week to promote student engagement and retention.

What I love the most about the University of Massachusetts Dartmouth is both is local area as well as its campus architecture and layout, there tons of things to do between classes as well as after as a hard day, things like the local mall, movie theater, and even restaurants, make both the commute there and back enjoyable. However, if there were something I wish would be changed, it would have to be the parking permit issue, with how many people with or without, can barely find on campus parking, especially considering how expensive the permit itself can be. Despite that, I still enjoy the time I have on campus, which is further elevated by the amazing professors I've had, and look forward to meeting as I continue throughout my time there.
After just one year of attending the university I have very few negative comments, the school is great if you're looking for a small enviornment that has many on campus events.

ai_summary: At the University of Massachusetts Lowell, students highlight a strong academic reputation , particularly in engineering, business, and health sciences, with many praising the supportive and knowledgeable faculty . The campus environment is described as welcoming, with a diverse student body and a strong sense of community. While there are plenty of clubs and resources to support student life, some reviews note that campus activities can diminish on weekends, as many students leave. Dining options seem to be a common area of concern, with several reviews expressing a desire for improved food quality . Overall, while UMass Lowell provides a solid educational experience and a variety of extracurricular opportunities, some students feel that engagement in campus life could be stronger.

reviews.text: The Campus is wonderful, Nice and reliable supporting systems to help every student in academic excellency.

Professors are great flexible and understanding of my disabilities. It has been a pleasure taking classes although I have to repeat my research statistics that I have taken previously and passed I do not understand if I received my associates degree from bcc why I would have to retake the class because it isn't a 3000 class because it shouldn't be it was taken in a community College for my associates I don't think I should have to retake the class again.
I really enjoy the staff and level of support here at UMASS Lowell. Everyone is very friendly, always willing to point you in the right direction.

I am only a first year student at UML, but I can absolutely see myself continuing my studies here. One of the most notable things in my expierence has been the professors. They are well-versed in their subjects and therefore are able to properly convey the information to students. I have enjoyed my classes so far and have been exposed to the idea of following criminal justice as a potential minor.
I have attend the University of Massachusetts Lowell for 3 years now. I enjoy studying Civil Engineering. I really enjoyed surveying around campus.

The University of Massachusetts Lowell provides a great experience for first-time college students and their families to attend a higher education institution, providing great student resources and activities.
I transfered to UML and it was a great decision for me and i have really enjoyed my time there. all the professors have been very supportive and wanting me to succeed.

Lowell's the only school in town to my (albeit kinda new) understanding. There are a ton of opportunities to take up and plenty more activities to take part in happening all over campus. Party wise it's only bested among the 4 Umass' by Amherst (Kinda obvious that one) but I find it very enjoyable. Professors tend to be on the respectful end and classes are easy enough to get to while living on campus.
The most important thing to me however is Jobhawk, it can basically guarantee you a part time job if you've got Student Work-study in your financial aid. These positions tend to be lenient and easy-going and give you a some good time and space to get work done or study. Heavily recommend taking advantage of Jobhawk whenever you can.

Food is not good. You come here to earn your degree for cheap. The three campuses are disconnected so to get to your classes you’ll need to walk from residential to classes, or take the bus. It’s not a huge deal but it’s spread out across the city.

Umass Lowell has been extremely welcoming to me. The transfer package was very helpful, and my advisors made sure to help me with my schedule despite staring school late . I think it was a clean transition from my former school to here, I looked forward to my classes , and made sure to pay attention to the material being presented to me .

UMass Lowell has a very reliable online education system and has quite affordable fees. I like the fact that the online courses are asynchronous and I am grateful for their online programs which have afforded me the opportunity to also work and study as I am unable to study on campus.

This university gives first time students a grasp to prepare students for the outside world. It has the programs, events, and opportunities for students to attend and ask questions for what to expect. It can be actually scary, but it would be of help to seek advice and this university provides answers to these questions. I would rate it a 4 out of 5 for its environment and ability to help students achieve their goals.
tuition is too expensive. I had a hard time paying the costs of tuition. The cost of tuition was too expensive.
I do like UMass Lowell I love how there is a whole other city you can visit and almost everything is in a walking distance. I do wish there was more of a variety of classes to take as electives.
Overall the professors are fun and I enjoy learning. However I have a hard time making friends and I would like to join clubs but I don't have lots of time to join them.

ai_summary: Students at the University of Memphis often describe their experience as welcoming and community-focused , highlighting the diverse student body and the supportive nature of professors and staff. Many enjoy the vibrant campus life filled with activities, clubs, and events that promote social interaction outside of academics. Academics are generally viewed as challenging but manageable, with some students mentioning the need for more robust offerings in the STEM fields. While the campus is appreciated for its cleanliness and the variety of resources available, there are concerns about safety , particularly regarding vehicle break-ins and the security measures in place. Food options on campus receive mixed reviews, with calls for more variety. Overall, the sense of community and the support for student involvement stand out as key aspects of life at the University of Memphis, despite some recurring issues related to housing and safety.

reviews.text: It was fun, if you get plugged in. Some professors were hard to understand, but other than that, my professors were great. Safety on campus was a big things for me in this area, and Memphis is sure to have safety precautions present on campus. Housing has been an issue. Dorms are expensive and not nice at all. Off campus housing is available, but also expensive to rent.

The University of Memphis was a wonderful experience that I enjoyed deeply and considered a deeply impactful time of my life. I would say that the overall area of Memphis as well as the general academic and scholastic environment of the school to have a powerful synergistic effect. You grow as a person and as a student.

I love how welcoming it is at the University of Memphis, and how the staff and professors are so kind. I also appreciate that this university offers almost everyone a chance to make changes and earn the degrees they deserve.
Everything at school is not bad but not good either. But I really don't like campus food at all, too boring, too unhealthy but I have to pay for the TigerEats for campus food only anyway.

The University of Memphis provided me equal opportunities for education, campus involvement, and recreation. The Tiger community is one that molded who I became as an adult, and I couldn't be prouder to wear the blue and gray.

My experience as a student at the University of Memphis is awesome. Professors are great at what they do and they make classes fun, and exciting while passing out valuable knowledge to the students. There is so much diversity on campus, and it is easy for students to find friends and groups to be a part of.
The athletics department is amazing, and there is multiple games every semester that students can attend for free, be it football, basketball, volleyball, and others. This is a nice way to feel the campus spirit, attending those games.
Additionally, there is a variety of food available on campus. We have different fast food brands available, and multiple kitchens on campus were you can access food that have been curated to suit your palate.
Lastly, the campus police works day and night to ensure student's safety and they are very reliable. They help stranded students and do all they can to make sure you're not in danger.

With my experiences at the university is overall okay, with some teachers being kind and understanding with my schedule and with other are more strict and unforgiving with deadlines. The administrations with financial aid could use some overall work as well, calling and asking for guidance won't do much good so it's better to do the research yourself or ask others who have experience with dealing along the lines of money problems.

It’s been going pretty great so far!! All I would say is that although sometimes the walking distance from class to class can get pretty fatiguing for me personally; however, the walk is always very pleasant!
The academic programs are in wide variety, and the school offers a wonderful free tutoring program. The professors are very willing to have one on one time to help you with any struggles.

I began my college career at a community college, and after transferring to University of Memphis in my junior year, I felt as though the community college I went to was far nicer than the university. I personally pay $13,000 out of pocket every year to go to the university, and really don’t have anything special to show for it. The campus life is lackluster, the campus food is terrible, the classroom buildings are all old and rundown with many not even having working AC. The school seems to spend all its money on athletics, which does not leave much for academics, which as a student focus on getting the best education I can, is highly frustrating.

My experience at the University of Memphis has been mostly positive. I really like how diverse and welcoming the campus community is, and the professors are usually very supportive and easy to reach. The campus also offers many opportunities to get involved, from student clubs to events that help you meet new people. However, I think the university could improve its dining options and make financial aid communication clearer for students. Overall, it’s a great school that gives you both academic growth and personal experiences.
Life as a college student is great! Some professors don’t explain as well as others but that’s part of life, you don’t always understand the first time.
The University of Memphis is a great spot to get an education. It is the place to be, from the diversity and school events to the many classes designed to instill knowledge.

The University of Memphis – University Campus School offers a strong academic program with dedicated, caring teachers and a close-knit community. As a National Blue Ribbon School, it consistently performs above state averages in reading and math, making it one of the top schools in the area. Parents appreciate the supportive environment and innovative teaching methods tied to the university’s education programs. However, some have noted room for improvement in school-to-family communication and expansion of extracurricular opportunities. Admission is competitive, adding to its reputation for excellence. Overall, it’s a nurturing and academically rigorous place for students to thrive.
I loved being at University of Memphis. I do wish they has advertised more of the events they had around campus that was open to all students and not just those connected to organizations.

ai_summary: The University of Miami offers a vibrant and diverse college experience , marked by its beautiful campus and engaging community. Students appreciate the small class sizes and the dedication of many professors, though experiences with faculty can vary. The university’s location in Miami provides ample opportunities for social activities and networking, influenced by an active athletic and social scene , often centered around Greek life. While the campus is well-maintained, older housing facilities have drawn criticism, and many students express concerns about the high tuition costs and the adequacy of financial aid. Overall, the university promotes a culture of involvement and support , encouraging students to thrive academically and socially, but the active social scene may overshadow quieter student lifestyles.

reviews.text: i loved it when i went there for a visit when my cousins was goin there i loved how the basketball program and the scenery was welcoming

Amazing environment in which people know how to have fun, but there is absolutely still a culture of hard work and studying. I am currently an honors student on a full merit scholarship, and the academics - specifically in the business school - are top tier. The math department (my 2nd major) can be a bit problematic however as it is underappreciated and underfunded. The school invests so much time and effort into working with students to get a better understanding of how they can grow. Our needs are always met, and we have opportunities to speak with administrators frequently. Additionally, our acceptance and research into AI through the business technology department specifically is nationally ranked #1, with our programs accepting AI and turning developing technologies into a learning resource or tool rather than something we must compete with in our job search. Socially, we have so many sports games and the scene in Miami is very extensive.

Amazing welcoming environment! Student body is diverse including all 50 states and internationally. Lots of resources to help students succeed academically, professionally, and in their personal interests.
A good experience i have yet to spend in my institute. It's a memorable experience. Yes I like it . No, i have don't like to change anything from it. It would be a good for us and other students .
The most beautiful campus. Great weather, fantastic food, close to the beach. You can't beat living on campus.

The energy here is high-octane. Whether it’s students wearing orange and green in the Shalala Student Center or people talking about the weekend's football game, "The U" pride is real. However, talking to current students, I realized the "work hard, play hard" reputation is no joke. The nursing curriculum is intense, and because it’s a private school, the cost of living in Miami is a huge factor. Tuition is high, and while the Toppel Career Center is great at helping you find jobs after graduation, you have to be ready for the fast-paced, sometimes flashy social scene that comes with being in such a wealthy area.
The diversity on campus is incredible—you hear different languages everywhere you turn—which is a huge plus if you want to work in a global city like Miami. The classes are small enough that you won’t just be a number, which is a relief when you're tackling tough science pre-reqs.

I had the best experience at the University of Miami in the Master of Science in Media Management program. The cohort was small, which made classes feel very personal and allowed for meaningful relationships with professors. The faculty truly went above and beyond, they were deeply invested, supportive, and brought real-world experience into the classroom. The facilities were excellent, and the overall learning environment was inspiring. I learned so much and genuinely enjoyed every part of the program. What I loved most is how this master’s program uniquely combines the communication and business schools, giving students both creative and strategic perspectives. I had an amazing time and would absolutely recommend this program.
Fantastic University, had a great time, and it prepared me for a very successful career. Best choice for me.

I loved my experience at the University of Miami. I completed the Master of Science in Media Management program, and it was an amazing experience. The courses were up to date, engaging, and truly enriching. It’s an outstanding program led by Dr. Dupagne.
University of Miami was great! It has an awesome athletic scene and so many professional opportunities being in a big city.

You are so exotic!” Dumbfounded, I simply answered with “What?”
Subconsciously, I always knew my upbringing was unique, but never had I felt so different.
Having lived in 3 different countries, speaking languages, and constantly having to adapt to change made me feel I was different. The University of Miami has allowed me to find my home, and fit in. With over 300 different countries on campus, various academic enhancement opportunities, sociable people, and amazing faculty I have felt that I fit in. There are so many people at the University that you are bound to find your voice and your people. There are so many programs both academically and personal that will make you feel at home and happy. I have come to realize that being 'exotic' was an accurate representation of the tapestry that was my life. This is something that makes me different, and that UM supports me through it. There is a combination of various individuals, & passions that allow me to pursue whatever I desire.

While being at the University of Miami, I haven't had any major complaints other than the overall student body. Many students tend to be very party/ night life oriented and since I am more of indoors persons I haven't found a crowd to align myself with but I do enjoy everything else.

I have had a very positive experience with the University of Miami. I appreciate the welcoming campus culture, supportive academic environment, and the wide range of opportunities available to students. The professors and staff are approachable and genuinely invested in student success, which makes a big difference. I also like the vibrant campus life and location, which offers both academic focus and real-world experiences. One thing I would like to see improved is greater transparency and accessibility regarding financial aid and scholarship resources, as navigating costs can be overwhelming. Overall, the University of Miami has made me feel motivated and excited about my future.
Great environment, social life, beautiful campus, easy to make friends but classes were extremely competitive with minimal support

I liked how everyone is super nice and the teachers seem like they actually care about their students. On top of that the school feels vary safe. The only thing I would like to see change would be the food at the dinning hall. The dinning hall closes really early and most of the time the food is either burnt or undercooked.

ai_summary: At the University of Michigan - Ann Arbor, reviews highlight a blend of rigorous academics and vibrant student life that shapes the overall experience. Many students appreciate the high quality of education, noting that professors are passionate and accessible, though some large introductory classes can feel impersonal. While the campus fosters a strong sense of community and school spirit, especially during athletic events, students also express concerns regarding dining options and housing affordability. The campus itself, intertwined with the city of Ann Arbor, is described as beautiful and walkable, providing ample opportunities for extracurricular activities and cultural engagement. Overall, the university is seen as a place of growth, where diverse backgrounds contribute to a rich learning environment .

reviews.text: The University of Michigan is great. The culture, community, and college town set it apart from other Universities. It will also set you up good to start your career.

U-M is a great place to be. It is filled with warm, passionate people, and there is always something to do. While it can be a challenging academic environment, staff and students alike are there to support you.
It was amazing. I had a blast partying and staying up with my friends. I wouldn't trade this expierence for the world.

It's so competitive and snotty here. Not very friendly, everyone is wealthy and privileged. Most students are climbing social ladders. Greek life is full of super rich kids who don't follow the panhel rules because they feel above them. Ann Arbor is a nice city but not tons to do outside of sports and a few bars. Dorms are old. Most classes are large and run by TA or PhD students, not the world class profs. Clubs are very selective and you have to apply and interview to get in...often you are competing against 100 kids for 20 spots, especially the business clubs. Lots of students with anxiety and stress.

I absolutely love going to the University of Michigan. Everyone here wants to see each other succeed, and there are so many opportunities for support. Not to mention, Ann Arbor is a super fun college town with so many things to do and opportunities to meet new people.

I had a positive experience during my time at the university. As a transfer student, things looked a bit different for me, and I wish there were easier ways for me to connect with more than just other transfer students. However, I love Ann Arbor and would choose to go there again if I had the chance.
Beautiful campus and exciting sports community. Athletic energy gets everyone to feel school spirit and want to join in.
Lots of construction and overpriced housing. Dining hall food is typically okay or terrible. Academics are challenging due to the little help available, but it does push you to do things on your own.

Overall, the University of Michigan is a great choice for college. There are various social clubs, you will definitely find a community of people who share the same interests and passions. They have a diverse range of professors that come from various backgrounds and enjoy connecting with the students. Campus life is fun because the school is located in the heart of Ann Arbor, where you can easily walk downtown and hang out. Also, the school is known for having one of the best sports teams in the country. The school needs improvements in areas like diversity, there are low percentages of Black and Brown students on campus. They also should improve the amount of affordable housing for on campus and off campus options.

The people here let their accomplishments get to their heads, leading to an extremely competitive academic and social lifestyle where the majority of people would step on your neck just for a leg up in anything, and I mean literally anything. That being said, if you find a good group of friends, the university offers everything you could need or want from a top public school, besides warm weather.

The college town of Ann Arbor fosters the perfect space to manage continuing your learning while also learning to live on your own. The academics and sports teams are top ranked in the nation which allows for school spirit to come easily. However, you must be built for Michigan's brutal winters as it is common to have to walk to class in negative temperatures and inches of snow.

I have liked all of my classes so far. The transition was tough because classes are taught very fast, but professors want you to succeed. I have liked all of my assigned groups for projects. All of the sporting events are fun, but the walk there is very far because the busses usually aren't running.

I love the work hard, play hard mentality that is embodied by the school. I love the surrounding Ann Arbor area and enjoy the success of the athletic programs. But, I would like to see the football team improving and I would like to see more energy and activity among the students during the cold winter months.
Yes the experience was pretty good I got to study and got a good interaction with both the students and the teachers

The University of Michigan is great for all types of students. There are student events for those who enjoy partying, watching movies, or focusing on academics. I never felt out of place in my values because of this! The one thing I would want to improve is the housing. It can be stressful to search for housing in Ann Arbor and there isn’t dorm space for everyone. Some people I know became RAs to avoid the stress.

ai_summary: At the University of Michigan - Flint, students often highlight the close-knit community and supportive faculty as distinct features of their experience. The approachable professors and small class sizes enhance student engagement and academic success, allowing for personalized interaction and guidance. Many appreciate the flexibility of online courses, which cater to those balancing work and studies. However, students have expressed a desire for improved campus life , particularly regarding student activities outside of academics and more diverse food options . While the campus is generally perceived as safe, some concerns about security and student representation have been noted. Overall, UM-Flint is seen as a solid choice for those seeking a quality education in a more personal setting, though some seek enhancements in student engagement opportunities and resources for various demographics.

reviews.text: U of M-Flint, has been great thus far. One thing I would like to see change is the academic program platform. Canvas is ok, but other work much smoother, you aren't jumping all over, and the cost of textbooks to decrease.

My experience at UM-Flint has been defined by the powerful combination of a close-knit community and the prestige of the University of Michigan system. As a software engineering major, I’ve benefited immensely from small class sizes that allow for direct mentorship from professors who truly know my name. The hands-on labs in the Murchie Science Building have provided the "digital sandbox" I need to bridge theory and practice. UM-Flint offers the resources of a global institution with the personal support of a local community, making it the ideal environment for my growth as an engineer.

There has been targeted bullying and repeated disrespect toward Hindu students and Hindu traditions. Our faith has been mocked and humiliated, while there is little to no institutional support for the protection or respectful recognition of Hindu beliefs and practices.
The Hindu student population is very small, with no meaningful representation or advocacy to ensure our values are understood or safeguarded. As a result, incidents of harassment often go unaddressed.
Additionally, there have been instances of hostility and dehumanization toward Hinduism from individuals who lack basic understanding of our religion. This behavior reflects a broader failure to uphold mutual respect among minority communities and creates an environment where intolerance toward Hindu students is normalized rather than challenged.
The majority of class sizes are smaller, which makes it easier to make closer and deeper relations with professors, giving you more oppurtunities to make connections.
The only thing I would like to see changed is for the university to not take away the pavilion cause it gives students more food choices other than Clint’s
I am a freshman at the University of Michigan - Flint. So far everything has been fantastic. I have no complaints.

This campus is a great alternative for people who want a University of Michigan degree. The univerity is made up of five academic units. These academic units include: College of Arts/Sciences/Education, School of Management, College of Health Sciences, School of Nursing, and the College of Innovation and Technology. My personal experience has been good so far. The campus is safe, with its own police department. Professors are okay, especially in the School of Management. I hope to become more involved on campus.
I would say that UM-Flint experience has been great. I don't really any problems with my school experience. When I was living on campus, everyone seemed great.

Going to the University of Michigan - Flint has been very helpful. Especially some of the professors, I personally had a professor who pushed me constantly throughout the semester which I honestly wouldn't have passed if it wasn't for her pushing me so much. I had fell into just being unmotivated I stopped coming to class and just stopped engaging in class overall. By the end of the semester I was engaging a lot more and my grade for the class overall started to approve so I would say if you needed motivation just for school in general go to the University of Michigan-Flint.

I am transferring into the nursing program and so far the professors have been beyond helpful. I have set up multiple meetings with my advisor and all of my questions have been answered clearly. The support staff works wonders to ensure they are going above and beyond for the students.
So far so good. Im glad I chose this university. Major reason being online and flexibility. No hiccups
I loved U of M flint! I had amazing professors that made me excited to go to class. While the classes were challenging at times I always enjoyed them.
I love that this campus feels like a community college all while being a four year university. I also love the supportive environment from academic advisors to professors.

Although I haven’t started classes yet, being accepted into the MBA program at the University of Michigan–Flint is a major step in my academic and professional journey. I chose UM-Flint because of its strong reputation, flexible learning options, and focus on real-world application. My goal is to build a wellness business that empowers others, and this program will give me the tools to lead with purpose. As someone who values holistic health, entrepreneurship, and community impact, I’m excited to learn from experienced faculty and connect with peers who bring diverse perspectives. UM-Flint represents the future I’m building—one grounded in service, strategy, and self-growth.

Overall, the Bachelor's program offered for respiratory students at the University of Michigan - Flint has been exceptional. I have been a respiratory care practitioner for several years and work a full-time schedule while enrolled in the program, and I can still manage classes despite the workload. The majority of their instructors have been well-established in this program for years, which allows them to provide beneficial courses that truly help to advance the respiratory career. In turn, I have been able to create a balance between work and school while I strive to make my way further into medicine and move out of bedside care in the coming years. I have one year left, but still feel that I have learned so much in my first year of being a part of this program.

ai_summary: At the University of Minnesota Twin Cities, students appreciate the vibrant campus life and the numerous academic opportunities available. Many reviews highlight the diversity of the student body and the engaging extracurricular activities , such as events and clubs that facilitate social connections. However, some students express concerns about the large class sizes , particularly in introductory courses, which can make personal interaction with professors challenging. The professors are generally seen as knowledgeable and dedicated, although opinions vary on their accessibility and investment in student success. Campus amenities are seen as plentiful, but some students point out issues with safety and the variety of dining options. Overall, UMN offers a dynamic college experience set in an urban environment, providing a mix of academic rigor and social engagement, albeit with some areas noted for improvement.

reviews.text: I have attended the University of Minnesota for 2 years before deciding to transfer. During my time at the University, I was exposed to many opportunities to get involved in student groups, and a vibrant student life scene. The University itself really pushes students, especially freshmen, to participate in these groups by having a top notch incoming freshman fair and orientation. Furthermore, it is a Research 1 school so you will have some of the best professors you can meet in the country in their respective fields. Overall, I really enjoyed my time at the University of Minnesota, and I would not have been able to explore new experiences and interests without them.

The university of Minnesota offered me great experiences in both the academic aspect, by giving the chance to connect with student colleagues and professors who opened my eyes to new experiences and points of view, and the social aspect, I met some great people while at the U that help me find myself, connecting on a deeper level.

I enjoyed my experience at the University of Minnesota Twin Cities. I lived at home and was a commuter so I was not on the campus as much as other students but i had a great time and got along well with my classmates. I would like to see some change on making the campus a bit more commuter friendly. It is hard to make friends as a commuter and find places to rest spaces sometimes.

This school is honestly the worst. I’m autistic and the way the students and teachers treated me was straight-up trash. They’d dump assignments on me and expect them done by the next week, totally ignoring the fact that I’m autistic. They even tried to force me into group work on Sundays, even though that’s when I’m non-verbal. (Go Badgers)
If you’re autistic, do yourself a favor and don’t come here. The teachers expect everyone to just grind and ace exams, but because of my autism, I couldn't even do the exams. Instead of helping me, they just expelled me even though they knew exactly what I was dealing with.

This place totally blows. I spent two semesters here before transferring out, and thankfully, I now go to UW-Madison. Nothing is worse than the awful city of Minneapolis, and this school is no better. Someone broke into my car while it was parked in campus parking and stole my stuff from it. The professors here suck, the classes are too big, and they really don't care about you. Don't go here, any other Big 10 school is so much better, I promise.

One thing that I love so far about the University of Minnesota is all of the opportunities it provides you with. The U is a great place to go if you are interested in getting into research, making connections, and joining groups/clubs with similar interests as yours.

I enjoyed the environment when I first got there. It was very different from my high school, in terms of people, academics, and overall the expectations of me as a student. It is challenging, especially as a stem student, but I appreciate having my capabilities tested.

I had a fantastic time at University of Minnesota! I met my people, loved campus, and most of the people are super "Minnesota nice"! I found that for a large campus and university, Minnesota has a lovely homey feel.

I graduated several years ago, and my experience was terrible. Asking for career counseling help for my worthless Liberal Arts degree, and the advisor actually told me to ask my hairdresser to network for me, “because they know a lot of people.” No joke. Previously, when I asked to change my major to International Relations, the advisor, who appeared drunk, told me the major didn’t exist. I had to show him in the catalog that it did. I’ll never recommend this place to anyone. Total waste of money unless you major in IT or go to Carlson School of Management. Incompetent staff, arrogant faculty, worthless degrees.

I love the diversity and size of the University, as well as the countless options for class options and degree opportunities. I am still early in my college career and have so much to learn, but I feel challenged in my academics, my 4 year plan is clear, and there are so many clubs and resources that there is always something to participate in.
University of Minnesota is one of the greatest universities I have toured and learned about. It has very great educational opportunities and it leads you to your success.
Appreciated Welcome Week for new students. Very diverse. Great student life on campus. Lots of clubs.
I’m on my first year, but it’s been a great experience! I’ve enjoyed making new friends, have had great classes & professors, and living in the Twin Cities has been fun!
Great school in a great city. St. Paul campus was quiet and wonderful and the perfect area for CFANS and CBS students. Loved my professors for FNRM. Shoutout Marcella!

A challenging experience with necessary academic obstacles for intellectual and emotional growth. Perspective-broadening professors and classmates, who support open and thoughtful discussions in class.

ai_summary: At the University of Mississippi, students frequently praise the campus atmosphere and the beauty of the surroundings, highlighting it as a welcoming and friendly environment that fosters a strong sense of community. Many reviews emphasize the professors' dedication to student success, with several noting their availability and supportive nature, though experiences can vary depending on the course and professor. The campus offers a diverse array of activities , with a strong emphasis on sports and Greek life, providing numerous social opportunities, although some students express concerns about the prominence of partying and Greek culture. While the academic experience is generally regarded as fulfilling, there are mixed feelings about the effectiveness of academic advisors, with some students finding them unhelpful. While tuition can be a concern, the resources and opportunities available are often described as exceptional. Overall, Ole Miss stands out for its unique combination of a vibrant , close-knit community and a picturesque campus , making it a compelling choice for students seeking both academic and social experiences.

reviews.text: Everything about this school is so amazing! The campus is beautiful and so easy to navigate! This school is extremely diverse, and there is somebody for everybody. There is always something to do! The professors really care about their students and put in an effort to know each of their students. I could go on and on about how amazing not just the school and campus themselves are but also the town as a whole!

The school is great and almost all of the professors are amazing people who really care about your education. Greek life is HUGE here (which I am a member of) but there are hundreds of clubs to get involved in otherwise. The party scene doesn't really exist, it is mostly going to bars on the square(that all have a super high cover). But the square is great for food and shopping too. As for housing, there are way way way way too many students for apartments off campus and those complexes get away with charging insane amounts for rent. Something to be aware of of but overall it's a great school culture and I love it!

It is really nice! I’m having a hard time making friends but other than that I love it! The work load can be very stressful at times especially since we just went through winter storm turn but the campus is amazing and very beautiful and I’ve met really nice people here at ole miss and I don’t think I’d have it any other way!
My university experience so far has been really great. The people on campus are very nice and help me when needed.

I loved my experience at Ole Miss. My professors were great and I feel prepared going in to my graduate program because of them. Student life is amazing and there are a ton of opportunities to get involved on campus. The town is great and is very supportive of the school.
I really loved the walkablilty of campus and how beautiful the scenery is on campus. I am also walking distance from the square and have plenty of options for good food and shopping.
love love love ole miss so so much as it has such an amazing environment and the people are so nice there

My experience at the University of Mississippi has felt like living inside a tradition while also slowly watching it stretch toward something new. Ole Miss does an incredible job of creating a sense of belonging, between the campus beauty, game days in the Grove, and the strong social networks, it’s easy to feel connected and proud to be here. I’ve loved how approachable professors are and how involved students tend to be, whether through Greek life, service organizations, or campus activities. Ole Miss is at its best when it honors its past while being brave enough to grow beyond it.

I have really loved my time so far as a student at the University of Mississippi, I am moving on to my sophomore spring semester and have currently enjoyed everything the school has to offer. The networking events, career days, serving opportunities, and all the sports activities have been fantastic.
I haven’t experienced anything at the University of Mississippi yet, because I am going to be a student there this fall of 2026.
it is great, it is the life of the party over here. highly recommend I have to put 100 characters to submit this so I am just going to put this here.

I fell in love with the University of Mississippi the first time I stepped foot on the campus. Gorgeous landscaping and smiling faces everywhere created an environment in which I could thrive socially and academically. The social life at Ole Miss is boosted by an emphasis on community and involvement and reinforced with vibrant campus event including sporting events. Professors enjoy teaching in a way that makes the learning less stressful and more conducive to growth. If I could see one thing change it would have to be the price. Although many costs are kept low, out-of-state tuition and the cost of living in a small, preppy town certainly create holes in my pockets.

I love the University of Mississippi and would not want to be at school anywhere else. The environment is top tier, as well as student life, there are tons of organizations to join and ways to get involved.

99 percent acceptance rate. Which means there are many really bad people here who don't belong. The academics are sub-par and there are many unqualified professors and service employees. The guys look scruffy and backwoods and not very smart while the women are better dressed. It is more of a party, sex, drugs, and alcohol school than an academic institution. I told my father not to send my sister here because of the masses of misbehaving good ole boys. Class is way too easy and venereal deceases run amok. Diversity is non existent and the N word is heard frequently. I never thought about it until I became a student here but how do black players play for a team named the rebels (confederates who fought to enslave them) and a term OLE MISS that has a racist meaning. There are rural students and very low number of black students and that's it for diversity. Nightlife is common but spiking girls drinks is way too common and even the police will try to get with the female students.
Very fun time with my friends when I finish all my assignments. There is always something to do around campus.

ai_summary: At the University of Missouri, many students appreciate the beautiful and walkable campus , which enhances their college experience and social interactions. The academic programs, particularly in areas like health, education, and journalism, are frequently highlighted for their quality and the supportive nature of professors who are approachable and willing to help. Student life is lively, with numerous events, clubs, and a vibrant party scene , contributing to a strong sense of community. However, some reviews point out a lack of diversity and inclusion , particularly in terms of student representation, and there are mixed feelings about dining options and tuition costs. Overall, Mizzou offers a blend of academic rigor, community engagement, and social opportunities, but also has room for improvement in certain areas.

reviews.text: I plan to major in Business Management and run a not-for-profit agency. My ideal job would be to own a business, like my father. I want to create a positive environment for my employees, and we can work together as a team to help our customers. I believe that we all can learn from each other, and I want to be able to ask others for their input, in order to make the business more efficient and successful. I would like to receive this scholarship, because it will assist me in being able to afford a higher education. I was inspired to continue my education, because my grandfather was able to enjoy his engineering profession, by getting both architectural and civil engineering degrees. That enabled him to have the flexibility to work in various positions throughout his career.
University of Missouri so far has been very easy and great to work with! Especially being a remote online student, they have all been easy to contact and communicate with!
I visited the campus and it was absolutely beautiful and everyone was so friendly and inviting can't wait to join!

I very much like it. It's an open, friendly and diverse university with all kinds of people. I have been able to meet some good people here and the entire campus is very vibrant and open to everyone. There's an expereince for everyone either club, activites and majors there's a lot to do on campus and plenty of places to get involved in. There isn't much I would like to see change besides walking across campus for different classes but college works likes that most of the time.

It is a great school overall. The academics, athletics, and extracurricular activities are great. There are over 200 majors so you can definitely find one you are interested in. They also offer several different minors and certificates depending on your major that I think are really unique and helpful for your future career. Downtown Columbia has a lot of places to go to and many different things to offer to students. However, sometimes it’s not the safest area but they are working to improve the safety downtown with the local community police officers. I’ve had mostly good professors but some are way better than others. I unfortunately lived in one of the older and smallest dorms so my experience with campus living could’ve been better but also could’ve been worse. A lot of the off campus apartments try to get more money out of you but most of them are nice. The majority of campus food is good and they have a lot of variety as far as different kinds of food. Overall I would recommend!

I would say that Mizzou was just kind of okay. I was involved in a few organizations but I just never loved it there. Columbia is a college town so if the undergrads were gone nothing much was going on. I thought some of my professors were amazing, but some of them were down right rude. Worst grade I have ever had a on paper and she specifically wrote that I let my "personal problems" get in the way. Diversity was also not that great, but it is the middle of Missouri.

From the moment I swung the door open of my jenky-but-charming dorm room, I was proud to be a Mizzou Tiger, following in the footsteps of my older siblings. I paved my own way, even getting the chance to take courses at one of the top journalism programs in the country.
I'm forever grateful for my experience at Mizzou (and the amazing professors who guided me through it) because that's what lit the fire in me to dream BIG at graduation. Days after walking across the stage in my cap and gown, I boarded a one-way trip to New York to chase a career in the cutthroat television industry—with no job lined up and only enough money in the bank to last a month.
15 years later, I’m still going strong in this beautiful melting pot! I’ll always credit Mizzou for giving me the courage to dream big, but New York taught me to dream wide. Can you imagine how much stronger Mizzou would be if that kind of exposure to difference was part of our college experience? That’s the change I’m rooting for.

My experience at the University of Missouri has been both challenging and rewarding. One of the things I appreciate most is the sense of community. The campus feels welcoming, and there are many opportunities to get involved, whether through academic programs, student organizations, or professional development activities. The professors are knowledgeable and, in many cases, genuinely invested in student success. I also value the variety of resources available to support students, from advising to tutoring and career services.
Another aspect I really like is the balance between academics and real-world preparation. The university encourages students to think beyond the classroom and explore internships, leadership roles, and service opportunities, which helps build confidence and practical skills.

For how long I've been there I've really enjoyed it. I didn't like that people partied instead of studying, but overall Mizzou is nice. The campus is beautiful and the academic programs I encountered were quite good. The professors were generally knowledgeable and helpful, and the library resources were extensive. There were also many opportunities to get involved in clubs and activities, which was great for meeting people with similar interests.

Carla Allen should legitimately not even be a teacher there!! It goes to show if you put your mind to anything you really can succeed. No matter what occurs, the school has her back and that is absolutely ridiculous and appalling to me.

I have just been accepted to the University of Missouri's Masters of Public Affairs program. So far, I have had a very enjoyable experience working with their online team, financial aid staff, and my advisor has been very attentive. I have all my classes picked out, currently working on a course plan, and I already have a payment plan set up for the semester and on. I have many friends who attend in person and they all have only good experiences to report back on. The party scene is fun but safe, the Greek life is recommended to finding life-long friends, and my mentor who is a lawyer with her own practice and an alum from Mizzou highly recommends the school for higher education, especially.

I had a great experience at the University of Missouri, there was a good combination of academics, personal growth and social opportunities. I would recommend their undergraduate programs for anyone considering this school.
It's hard to get fresh food such as fresh fruits and vegetables. Even if you do find some, it's extremely overpriced.
It is big but when you find your group then it doesn't seem so big. I wish there were more choices to eat.
This place is goated. We are better than the Kansas Jayhawks, (They suck) and the food is good. This place is the best.

ai_summary: At the University of Missouri - Kansas City, students generally appreciate the supportive professors who are dedicated to helping them succeed and are often available for assistance outside of class. Many reviews highlight the campus's friendly and diverse atmosphere , making it easy for students to feel welcomed and find their community. However, some students express concerns about the lack of a vibrant campus life, citing limited social activities and strict regulations that restrict events like parties. The campus location in Kansas City is often valued for its urban advantages , providing access to various resources and opportunities. While tuition is noted to be reasonable, many students suggest that the food options and quality could improve. Overall, UMKC is seen as a solid choice for those focused on academics and looking for a supportive environment.

reviews.text: I have absolutely adored attending UMKC. I am super involved in my community here and I enjoy coming to campus and seeing my friends and attending campus events. It has been an absolute pleasure. I was nervous and shy when I first got here but finding my people has really helped. And so many professors here are great as well.
UMKC is a great school. The professors care to see you succeed and off lots of opportunities to grow educationally. I would like to see better upkeep of the student housing.

At UMKC I am a full time student who lives on Campus. UMKC is a big commuter school and is filled with all sorts of different kinds of students. This is great to be able to find your group. They also have tons of student clubs and activities that are worthwhile.

Very unsatisfied with the mechanical engineering program, everything felt unorganized and cheap. In addition, the fact that almost no one lives on campus makes it difficult to meet and interact with new people, everyone just sits on their phones and goes home.

If you want to go into more debt and have a meaningless degree that takes ridiculously longer than you have planned, than this is the place for you. If you are bringing over electives, chances are they will not count- you're going to spend even more time trying to make up for the time you already invested. This college is a complete scam. The teachers can get away with not grading for ridiculous time periods, yet if you try to drop that class it's your ass. Don't expect much from financial aid either because person A will say one thing while person B says another. This is an ideal place to go into debt. Anyone who enrolls here who is not planning to go into medical, dental, or law school is wasting their time. Don't expect any organization from this school or any honesty. If you are the type of person that trusts corporate entities than this is the place for you. Most people aren't buying into this act any longer and it will be reflected on the current students and the ones to come.

Overall, I have been pleased with my experience so far. I'm enrolled in the MBA program (Online), and I'm also enrolled in the UMKC Doctorate of Pharmacy program but attend the satellite campus in Columbia, MO.

School is pretty good when it comes to STEM majors, campus isn't too bad, main complaint would probably be the dining hall food and how small the food portions are, Kansas City itself is nice, Campus isn't as active as other due to the fact that most people commute.

This University is unique in that it is both a small campus school with many commuter students within the KC metro area attending. I was a commuter student that had just graduated high school. At the time I was impressed with the level of attentiveness the curriculum had on social justice education. There were some standout professors and classes that I enjoyed taking. Overall it was a decent experience. I wish I had taken advantage of the groups and club opportunities that were offered.

My experience with UMKC has been consistently disappointing. The university gives off the impression of being more interested in collecting tuition than providing meaningful support for students. Advising is often disorganized, with students left to figure out requirements on their own, and communication between departments feels non-existent.
Facilities and technology are outdated compared to other schools in the region, which makes the tuition price even harder to justify. The campus environment feels more transactional than supportive—students are treated like numbers rather than individuals. Resources for professional development and networking are limited, and career services don’t do enough to connect students with real opportunities

I went to UMKC for nursing. I would say my overall experience was horrible. They had moronic professors who would post multiple choice tests with no corresponding correct answers available and nearly EVERY test had to be disputed due to there being either an incorrect answer being the “correct answer “ or nonsensical test questions that weren’t even in the texts, made us do group work in which one or two people did ALL the work, and several people either being non involved or incompetent and then took it out on the group with a bad grade, also marked me off for having a bad connection during my final presentation , which was NOT my fault and an absurd reason to deduct points. Had idiotic professors who couldn’t even formulate a sentence and advise me to “rearrange” my sentences to a nonsensical statement that made literally no sense at all, to which I would have to refute the statement and point out basic logic to them which became exhausting. Foreign students given free pass.

The University of Missouri-Kansas City offers unique opportunities for students in both education and the college experience. Its location in the heart of Kansas City, near the Country Club Plaza, gives students access to a wide range of experiences that go beyond the typical college town. Whether that's coffee shop hopping with friends during finals season, attending a Chiefs game during the playoffs, or interning with the Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City, UMKC places you in the center of it all. Unlike many universities that are isolated in small towns, UMKC students can enjoy everything a growing, vibrant city has to offer. Academically, UMKC is a top-rated R1 research institution and the only one of its kind in the Kansas City area. My experience with professors has been a mix of both great and challenging, but overall, they truly work to prepare you for the real world. Many of them also help you build meaningful professional connections that can benefit your future career.

I like how the university has my career path in doing something that I love to do. It’s people that’s there that has the same mindset as you that motivates you to keep going. I also like it’s up to to make your college experience of what you it to be. For instance, if you are a shy person, you put yourself out there to get more of those connections or meeting new people that’s beneficial in your life. What I would like to see change is understanding that everyone has their own path and that the people that’s in or out their life is for a reason. Can’t expect people to be that missing piece for you if you can’t have that peace within yourself.
Getting selected for my number college, was an amazing opportunity. This campus is very welcoming, the professors, staff and the students are very helpful throughout your first year.

I have lived in Kansas City my whole life and always loved UMKC. I have lots of friends that go there so we can study together on the regular. They don't have the best scholarship programs especially for students who grew up in the area, I would like to see them improve that for local students. I would also love to see a little bit more affordable student housing apartments that are not dorms. I love how the school is smaller and has a great health program as that is what I am passionate about. I think for it being a smaller school it should be made a little bit more affordable as well and have it easier to contact advisors. I am so happy to be able to attend college and so blessed I just wish I didn't have to work as much as I do.
The advisors and staff have been incredibly helpful and pleasant to be around. I'm surprised with the diversity in not only race but age too

ai_summary: At the University of Missouri—St. Louis (UMSL), students often describe the campus as a laid-back , commuter-focused environment where academics take precedence over social life. While many appreciate the affordability and the quality of education , there are mixed feelings about faculty experiences, with some professors being highly praised for their dedication, while others received criticism for being less engaging. UMSL has a diverse student body and offers numerous support services, which students find helpful, particularly in navigating academic challenges . However, the campus life tends to feel quieter, especially on weekends, and some students report difficulties in forming connections since many people focus on classes and work rather than social activities. Additionally, while there are resources available, some express frustration with the organization of student services and the need for more extracurricular engagement. Ultimately, UMSL is seen as a good option for students looking to balance academics with work, albeit with limitations in social and recreational opportunities.

reviews.text: I really am sorry but I strongly regret ever going here. Some of the worst advisors ever and have been made to constantly experience the runaround when trying to find opportunities related to my major. Especially the business department advisors which one giggled a lot during a meeting I had when trying to figure out planning for my upcoming semester. Very rude and condescending as well. Expect no help from staff either which you will be left to fend for yourself. Most professors are obsessed with catching ai use or cheating instead of you know, teaching the material to actually learn? University is too expensive to be dealing with egotistical morons who do this and if it is a online class, good luck. Not much communication there if you are forced to deal with faulty proctoring that does not work. Also this school is more than happy to give out loans like candy despite doing well. You would think scholarships exist? Nope. This school is designed to bankrupt you. Low income beware!!

No on campus life, bad housing options, mostly succeed program kids. Athletics programs are competitive but they recently cut some programs. A committee school that’s kinda expensive compared to other low tier universities.

Overall, my experience with the University of Missouri–St. Louis has been positive. The campus is welcoming, and many professors are knowledgeable and genuinely want students to succeed. I appreciated the smaller class sizes because it made it easier to ask questions and build relationships with instructors. The campus also offers helpful academic resources and support services. However, I think there could be improvements in campus life and student engagement, especially when it comes to events and activities. Parking and navigation around campus could also be better. Overall, UMSL is a solid option for students looking for a supportive academic environment.

ai_summary: The University of Montana offers a beautiful campus nestled in a vibrant college town , which students find welcoming and friendly. Academically, many express satisfaction with passionate professors and diverse programs , particularly in areas like wildlife biology and liberal arts, despite concerns about budget cuts affecting course availability and facilities. While some students report positive experiences with extracurricular activities and community engagement, issues like poor parking, outdated buildings, and a need for improved online services surface frequently. A strong emphasis on Griz football fosters school spirit , though some critique the overall focus on athletics over academics. Overall, many students regard UM as a supportive environment , although experiences vary widely, particularly for out-of-state and transfer students.

reviews.text: I like how everyone has a place where they fit in and are excepted no matter if you are a traditional or non traditional student or if you do or don't have a disability. All of the teachers are willing to sit and help you work through any problems that may arise and they are there to see and help you succeed at your goals.

The area- Missoula- is absolutely gorgeous and if you like the outdoors- swimming, hiking, floating the river- you will love it. The academics are fairly good with some teachers really shining- they have an excellent ceramics program that I would suggest getting involved with even if you aren't typically interested in that. They are a bit fanatical about football, and treat the athletes potentially with a bias. Not too much campus life and I would avoid the greek parties at all costs.

I have been familiar with the UM campus since I was in elementary school. Many of my family members attended here, and we are all major Griz fans. I attended basketball camp on campus for many years and learned quickly that this campus is beautiful, clean, and welcoming. I knew from a young age I wanted to attend the University of Montana - even though I had no idea what I wanted to be when I grew up.
