I am currently in the Interior Architecture program and am exposed to amazing professors and well-kept facilities. Each student in the program has their own designated space, making it feel very intentional and beneficial to our success as students in the program. Professors and qualified and take the time to go above and beyond teaching produce good designers. The program encourages internships and building connections for success after graduation.

My overall experience at the University of North Carolina at Greensboro was a good and positive one. The campus environment is welcoming, and there are many resources available to support students both academically and personally. I appreciated the supportive faculty, diverse student body, and opportunities for involvement on campus. The location is convenient, and the university offers a variety of programs that help students grow professionally and academically. One thing I would like to see improve is clearer communication about campus resources and academic advising, as navigating certain processes can sometimes be confusing. Overall, UNCG provides a solid educational experience and a supportive community for students.
I have enjoyed the flexibility I have to pursue what I want and the understanding nature of the professors who are willing to be helpful and supportive in hard times.

I came to UNCG as a non traditional transfer student nearly 11 years ago and I still find myself reflecting on the great things that I experienced there. I have never before or since experienced a place were there were so many different people and the experiences were always positive. Being a student at UNCG guided me towards my current career path…. Higher Education. I owe so much to this institution. I would attend again and I make sure to tell students who are searching for a college that UNCG will be a great choice.
Loved my experience and it got met to where I am today. Great school for networking in the Greensboro Triad. #SpartanAlum

The professors and staff through the Library Sceince department are fantastic. They will walk you through every step of the process from class registration to graduation and licensure. The Library Science Program is a program that I would highly recommend to anyone looking to get their degree or certification in school or public libraries.

ai_summary: At the University of North Carolina - Wilmington (UNCW), the overall college experience has been mixed among students. Many appreciate the beautiful campus and its proximity to the beach, citing a relaxed atmosphere that is conducive to both academic and social engagement. Academics receive praise, particularly for the dedication and accessibility of professors, although some students express frustrations with advising and academic support services. Student life is active, with a variety of clubs and extracurricular opportunities, though many reviews note a lack of diversity and inclusivity on campus. While some students enjoy making lasting friendships and participating in community activities, others feel that making connections can be challenging unless affiliated with specific groups like Greek life or athletics. The campus dining options and housing facilities have drawn criticism for quality and availability. Overall, students often highlight the sense of community and support they have experienced, alongside the need for improvements in diversity and services.

reviews.text: University of North Carolina at Wilmington has been a injoyable experience. Me and my new friends from my dorm, which is very social, visit the beach and like to walk about down town. Overall, UNCW is good for the experience and something laid back and meeting new people.

I enjoy UNCW as a commuter and full-time student. Commuting M-F for my 8am classes can be rough due to morning traffic and a strict parking policy but the campus atmosphere makes it worthwhile. The faculty are generally very supportive, and the campus beauty is a great stress-reliever. However, I would love to see more dedicated parking options or improved shuttle frequency for those of us driving in every day

My time at UNCW has been the best years of my life. I have truly found my home, community, and people there. It is everything I was/am looking for in schooling and experience. I love the community and the area, there's always something to do or somewhere new to try. Living by the beach also has its perks, and I find myself there a lot. Overall I have had exceptional courses and professors, but the availability for classes can be an issue when it comes to registering. The only issue I had wa with parking, there is not a lot offered or spaces at the school. I wouldn't trade my time there for the world.
I love the community and the diversity there. Is a very welcoming and safe community. The people were very friendly. You could always get information and ask question.
Perfect size. Professors who care about their students. New res halls for first year students are amazing. Access to beach and town in 10 min.
I transferred in this semester and have had a great experience so far. I have joined a few clubs and have met so many cool people. There are many events, clubs, and things to do around campus and on.
Great academics and hands-on research with a beautiful campus. I’d love more advising support for pre‑health students.

UNCW is a decent school, if you want to go to a beach school I Imagine its near the top of your lists. The problems with UNCW is everything having to do besides the local area. As a STEM majored student a majority of my professors were honestly terrible, they either were incredibly boring or weren't very helpful in the learning process. I met some truly exceptional professors here, but I can count them all on one hand. Another large problem is parking, parking at UNCW is expensive. I paid 500$ for my library parking pass, only for parking to be impossible to find. At nearly every lot for most of the day, almost every spot is going to be filled. Normally this wouldn't be a large issue but I truly do mean that parking on this campus is borderline hellish. If you want to coast through your college experience without learning much, but having a great time at the beach then honestly UNCW would be your perfect school.

My overall experience at the University of North Carolina Wilmington has been very positive and rewarding. UNCW offers a supportive academic environment with approachable professors and smaller class sizes, which makes it easier to ask questions and build connections. The campus itself is welcoming and well-maintained, and the coastal location creates a relaxed but motivating atmosphere that balances academics and personal life. There are many opportunities to get involved through clubs, organizations, and research, which helps students feel connected and engaged. Wilmington also provides plenty to do off campus, from local restaurants to nearby beaches, adding to the overall college experience. While there can be challenges like parking or adjusting to the workload, the strong sense of community and access to academic and personal growth opportunities make UNCW a place where students can truly thrive.

My overall experience at the University of North Carolina Wilmington has been positive and growth-oriented. UNCW offers a supportive learning environment with professors who are knowledgeable and approachable, especially within health- and science-based programs. I also enjoy the campus atmosphere and its proximity to the beach, which creates a balanced environment for both academic focus and personal well-being. Overall, UNCW has provided me with meaningful academic experiences and opportunities for personal growth, and I believe it is a strong choice for students seeking a supportive, well-rounded university experience.

I would say my experience at University of North Carolina Wilmington has been nothing short of amazing. Wilmington is a very lively and bubbly city and the student life around campus is so much fun! I have really enjoyed meeting new people and all of the opportunities UNCW has brought me over the past couple years and strongly encourage other people to chose this school.

My time at UNCW has allowed me to strengthen both my analytical and leadership skills through hands-on coursework and campus involvement. As Treasurer of the Student Analytics and Supply Chain Association, I’ve managed budgets, organized professional events, and collaborated with peers to build opportunities for career growth. My studies have deepened my interest in using data to drive decision-making in marketing and business strategy.

My experience at the University of North Carolina Wilmington has been mixed with many ups and downs. As I'm sure you may have heard, we had an active shooter threat recently. The way it was handled disappointed majority of the students, including me. Change I'd like to see is more urgency towards student safety and more diversity around campus. I've made amazing friends here and the campus is beautiful, but recent events have been disappointing.
I liked the vibe here and the professors are all amazing, I also enjoy the honors program a lot and I feel like im really set up for success. The only concern I have is with safety

Here at Wilmington I have made some of my lifelong best friends and already through just over 2 years have created memories that will last a lifetime. Being able to have the opportunity to get a great education and also be able to live in this area is just mind boggling. I am so glad that I am this blessed and cared for to be able to be supported to come here and enjoy my years of college. Wilmington has been very good to me and I am excited for the future down here.

ai_summary: The University of North Dakota (UND) offers a vibrant campus atmosphere with a strong focus on community involvement , featuring over 300 organizations, including Greek life and various clubs. Academically, students praise the aviation and nursing programs for their quality and faculty engagement, while experiences with professors range from exceptional support to some inconsistencies in teaching quality. Student life is characterized by social events and activities, although some feel the college is overly focused on sports like hockey. While the campus is generally seen as beautiful and accessible , there are concerns about outdated facilities and parking issues. Tuition is considered reasonable for many programs, particularly aviation. Overall, UND is recognized for its strong community feeling , great opportunities, and a collaborative environment, despite some challenges regarding diversity and academic rigor in certain areas.

reviews.text: They give endless opportunities for social practice such as clubs and events to bring the community together.
I am loving my experience at the University of North Dakota: the people, professors, and all that it has to offer.

My experience with this school is well overall. Most teachers are good at explanations and there are many resources available for tutoring and providing assistance with learning. However, I think these resources should be magnified in a greater scope so more students are aware of them. Another facet of the school include on-campus food which is convenient, even having robot delivery and tastes delicious. There are many fast-food chains on campus and I haven't had any bad experiences. Nevertheless, most on-campus food are card only with ATM's being the outlier where cash can be used. Therefore, an ATM to deposit cash onto your card would be very beneficial.
I have been here at UND for a couple months now and I love it! Besides the freezing weather, it’s been so fun! I really like the campus and how welcoming everyone is.

This school is mainly known for Aviation and it does have a good program where it presents you with opportunities to many airlines in the US. Though the program is good, the school needs to cut down on admissions since there is just not enough flight slots available for current students. Aviation aside, this school is alright, it is out in middle of nowhere so don’t expect too much for fun things to do or good places to eat. The food is alright, the frats are always partying and blacking out when it’s not cold. The mid western winters here are brutal for anyone not from here. And the rest of the academics here are alright, through they do offer a wide variety of subjects to choose from. Like every school, there are good and bad professors and teachers so Ratemyprofessor your way through class registration.
Highly recommended. Staff and academics are amazing. Teachers are always willing to help and they respond very quickly no matter the day or time of day.

This isn’t the best school to go to I recommend going to a different school to be honest. They banned me from that dining halls all because I’m a felon they tried to kick me out because they don’t except people who has been to prison. I only liked one class so far. It’s cold there and they really don’t care to even help you at all. I suggest going to another school. The scenery here is okay but it’s not enough comparing to all the faults this school has. I’m banned from housing put on conduct probation

The school has a good transport system for campus and they claim that you do not need a car. While true a lot of things shut down or slow down on campus on the weekends so I wish I did bring a car just for the ability to have gotten off campus when i wanted to instead of needing to ask friends or timing the trip right with the city wide public transportation.
I have found the instructors to be very understanding and willing to help. I have found the 100-level classes to tend to be on the easier side with having taken advance courses in high school.
For campus a lot of event will happen at the Memorial Union which is on the far East side of campus, also often a lot of times during lunch hours 10am-2pm, which is hard being an aerospace major where the aerospace buildings are on the far West.
The wind is unreal for someone not from Grand Forks, you will need to invest in a good coat if you are coming from a place that does not require you to have one.

It's okay I guess... cold, kind of isolated, a lot of students are online and people aren't very engaged either. I don't think my specific degree program was one they care about much or spend much money on. Things are better if you're in aviation, business, or health sciences.
Super nice! Faculty is nice, buildings are nice and clean, lots of clubs and events. I would maybe change the food and parking costs.

The University of North Dakota is honestly a great place to be. One of the best things is how safe it feels, especially if you decide to go walk around at night. The campus is really well-lit at night, and there are tons of blue safety lights and police cars patrolling around, so you never feel like you’re on your own. Most students say they feel totally comfortable walking back to their dorms after dark. There’s also a lot going on here, so it’s never boring. There are always fun events, too, like tech bingo nights, community movie nights, and spontaneous trivia competitions where you can hang out and meet new people around campus. It’s just one of those campuses that feels really welcoming. You can focus on school, make new friends, and still have plenty of ways to have fun while you’re doing so.
Great atmosphere! So much school spirit and they really go out of their way to provide the resources you need to succeed.

The staff are incredibly friendly and welcoming. There's plenty to do on campus. Numerous festivals and career fairs, as well as weekly activities. There was an opportunity a month back where you got to make your own stuffed animal; it was very fun. Plus, the food is amazing, a different variety every day, and I never really get sick of it. The campus is lovely; lots of flowers and greenery. You might even see some prairie dogs running around.

My experience so far at UND has been very positive. during welcome weekend I met my floor who were all very welcoming, my RA is a very reasonable person and definitely the best fit for me. I'm currently in the first semester of my freshman year and I am currently taking no generals. I am in a meteorology 110 class, Aviation 100 class, Aviation 102 class, and a flight lab for a total of 13/14 credits. during my Aviation 102 class we take ground school for the protocols of flying a small aircraft, AVIT 102 also has a lab portion where we fly a plane and practice maneuvers. My final goal is to become an Airline Pilot, or fly planes like the Canadair CL-415 or others that are similar. It would be a dream of mine to wake up and fly a plane everyday for the good of society and help to save people and property from natural disasters.

My experience at UND was better than I expected. I became a transfer student during my junior year and it was a huge transition, yet I enjoyed it! I found the atmosphere to be welcoming, friendly, and encouraging. My professors were helpful, efficient, and valued student feedback and made the changes they needed to depending on student needs. There are always events going on for students, almost daily, that allows for meeting new people and getting involved on campus. It really is all about students at UND making the college experience fun, productive, and successful!

ai_summary: At the University of North Florida, students frequently highlight the supportive atmosphere fostered by professors and staff, who are described as accessible and genuinely invested in student success. Academics stand out with many praising the quality of education and the intimate class sizes that encourage interaction. The campus itself is noted for its beautiful setting on a nature preserve, with a variety of activities and amenities available, including an impressive gym and community service opportunities. However, some students express disappointment regarding the lack of a football team and vibrant campus spirit , suggesting it feels more like a commuter college. While the cost of attendance is generally seen as manageable, concerns about financial aid services and administrative efficiency are common. Overall, the campus environment is often perceived as welcoming and inclusive , making it a good fit for those focused on academics and building connections.

reviews.text: The life at UNF is pretty great, you have many options in all aspects (academically, personally, campus-life wise, etc.) and there is always something you can be doing here. There are many good professors here and it is always easy to find out who the good ones are. There are many opportunities here and anyone would be lucky to go here.

The University of North Florida was a great college because it helped me tremendously in determining my future career path. UNF also has fantastic and supportive professors and staff that are willing to help you anytime you need. In addition, UNF had plenty of resources to help in an individual's academic journey such as mental health and career services.

I’m only halfway through my first semester at UNF, but I’m already really enjoying the experience. I’m especially excited to start in person this fall. I’m truly grateful for the opportunity to study here, it’s been a dream of mine since I was about 9 years old because of my dad being an alumnus. So far, my online professors have been very understanding and respectful of students’ time, and I really appreciate the curriculum and everything I’ve been learning.
My time was very enjoyable, I had very caring professors and advisors that seemed actually genuine. I met a lot of amazing people and the general students are usually friendly.
I am currently attending UNF and it is very nice. I was quite scared going into it as it is my first big university experience, but all of the professors are nice and always willing to help.
Love my niche school, love duval, love ospreys. Good school if you're antisocial but bad school if you're antisocial because so is everyone else.

During my time at the University of North Florida I have been able to learn so much not just academically but also real life solutions. Studying here has provided me with many opportunities to meet friends and employers. The school works hard for students to be able to find work once they graduate in the field they study, with helping students get internships, research, and more to expand their resume. Some of the teacher are hard to understand and once they are placed into teaching a subject that is not their strong suit most are hard to follow when they are explaining in the classroom. Even though some teacher are hard to understand they are always willing to help the student.

University of North Florida is a great campus to be involved with! From the friendly staff and students to the proximity in distance of each class, it is a great place to not only get your exercise, but also a great way to make good friends and connections in my life. My favorite thing that I've enjoyed about UNF are the people that I've met and the good connections that you can make with your professors. One thing, however, that I would like to see change would be some of the events around campus and the methods that they have to advertise them. For example, for some of the events in the Fall, they just posted it on Instagram without any other methods for advertisement. A recommendation could be to put it on the Student Government board that's over in Alumni Square. This would help with attendance. Another thing is at the events, especially the big ones at the amphitheater, it would be nice to have more food available for the students that attend. Otherwise, great campus to be with!

I hadn't expected to stay at the University of North Florida when I'd first enrolled, but my experience with the courses and their professors, as well as the many research opportunities made me realize that it was just as good a university as other Floridian public universities.

The smaller class sizes made for a more personalized learning experience. The campus is beautiful, and I appreciated the emphasis on conservation. UNF has a garden, numerous walking trails, and a lake. Students can rent recreational items such as kayaks for free, to use on the lake.

I've had a really good experience. I'm in a great program, I have really amazing friends for the first time in my life. UNF's a great place if you know what you want to do and you're open to community.

UNF has been so easy to when with so far. I'm returning after taking some time off to care for my family and they are helping me get right back on track. There are great professors that really care. The advising staff is great too.
I love the university of North Florida for the efficiency. I can go to school and work easily in the same day.

This school is okay overall. Communication will be slow, and you will find yourself being sent in circles—I had to speak with four different people just to get one hold removed, and it still took a week. The events on campus are pretty fun, but if you’re looking for a good party scene, you’ll have to go off campus. The food is decent—not terrible, but nothing mind-blowing. Campus safety is generally fine, with emergency boxes and regular police patrols, though it’s still a good idea to have someone you trust with you at night.

I like being a student at the University of North Florida because it feels like a place where I can grow without getting lost. The campus is big enough to offer a lot of opportunities, but small enough that I still recognize familiar faces when I walk to class. I like how the nature trails, the lake, and the open spaces make the campus feel calm, even on stressful days. When I have a break between classes, I can sit outside and feel like I’m away from the noise of a typical college campus.
My classes also make UNF enjoyable. Most of my professors take time to explain things clearly, and I feel like they actually care about whether I understand the material. I appreciate the smaller class sizes because I feel more comfortable asking questions and talking with my classmates. It helps me stay involved and not just sit in the back of the room. I have noticed that I learn better when I feel like part of the class instead of just another student in a crowded lecture hall.

ai_summary: The University of North Texas offers a diverse and welcoming environment where students feel supported in their academic and social endeavors. Many reviews highlight the quality of professors and the variety of academic programs , especially in music and arts, which contribute to a well-rounded education. Students appreciate the strong sense of community , with opportunities for involvement through events, clubs, and volunteer activities. The campus is noted for its beauty and convenience , as well as its safety, although some mention areas for improvement in dining services and athletics. Overall, UNT is described as a place where students can form meaningful connections and enjoy an enriching college experience.

reviews.text: It is a pretty good university with lively students all around. They offer plenty of good food, activities, and much more.
Great school which offered transfer aid and that helped me a lot. I love everything about UNT, and the professors and staff are great.

I really enjoyed my time at University of North Texas, earning a degree in Media Arts and Photojournalism and leaving with experiences that led to an internship and a job at a Dallas production agency. The most valuable parts were the student-run TV station, where I was Entertainment Director for two years, and the student newspaper, where I contributed for four years. These roles allowed me to attend South by Southwest as press three times, earn a Lone Star Emmy nomination, and build lasting professional connections. While I’m grateful for these experiences, I wish the Media Arts courses had better prepared me for industry work, as I found the journalism courses more directly applicable to a professional career.
I’m a future student of University of North Texas. I hope when I apply for this university, I’ll get accepted and go there.

I graduated from UNT and I really enjoyed pursuing my undergraduate degree here. I've always felt welcomed here by faculty and my peers. UNT offers a lot of opportunities that can expand your experiences in academia and in research.
For being my first year at UNT I have felt very welcome from all my professors. I have enjoyed getting to see some of the fun activities that happen here.
I have only been at UNT for a semester but so far I have really enjoyed studying geography and anthropology.

The University of North Texas is a good school. I do not stay on campus so I can not talk about the campus life. Although, I have heard that the parties and campus life is really good. The school is also really diverse.

University of North Texas Has been an experience that made me grow as a young adult, although it has not always been a great experience usually with housing and parking University of North Texas has been a place that I have been able to express myself.

The specific program I have been admitted to is very personalized and targets clinical skills to create outstanding clinicians. The professors and directors are amazing and make it their duty to help students improve their skills in all areas of the field. The campus is very nice and quite large, giving students access to many places for studying, homework, and meetings. The audiology program is rather affordable for a doctorate degree compared to other major universities.
I'm still here, and its super nice. The people are generally friendly, theres always events to visit throughout the week if you get bored. They heavily promote healthy socialization

University of North Texas is a great school for students wanting to pursue a liberal arts path. I really enjoy their course options and I found plenty of people with the same interests as me. There is a great variety of people you will meet on campus and during classtimes.

Im a transfer student from a different city from Texas. I can tell that UNT its great for their students. Im 41 years old, trying to get the bachelor degree, Im a full time at my job. I totall recommend UNT for your academic success.

3.University of North Texas (UNT) is a busy, medium-sized college with a lot going on. They have a football team, basketball, powerlifting, and dance, but the band isn’t that strong. Their academic scores are good, and the campus is very diverse, although they don’t really focus much on Black students. UNT has a good cost for out-of-state students and a very low crime rate, which makes it feel safer. The music program is decent; however, you need two years of a foreign language to attend. Overall, UNT is kind of in the middle—not too big, not too small—but always active and full of student life. Based on these factors i rate it a 8.5/20
I'm stil currently attending University of North Texas at Denton. So far I've liked attending and studying at this campus. I am a commuter, so I dont spend much time on campus.

ai_summary: The University of Northern Colorado (UNC) offers a welcoming and supportive atmosphere that many students appreciate as they transition from high school to college. The campus is often described as beautiful, manageable in size, and homey, enhancing students' sense of belonging. Academically, small class sizes allow for personal interaction with professors who are generally caring and helpful, contributing positively to the learning experience. Students also highlight the availability of various academic resources, including tutoring and career services. While student life includes a mix of clubs, events, and activities, some feel that the food options and diversity could improve. Concerns about tuition costs and the local environment of Greeley surface in reviews, but UNC is recognized for its strong programs in education , nursing, and performing arts, making it a solid choice for those in these fields. Overall, students value the sense of community and support they find at UNC, although experiences may vary based on individual perspectives and backgrounds.

reviews.text: Overall I greatly enjoyed my time at UNC! If I were to have to make the decision again, I'd still choose to go there.
The campus is so so beautiful. The professors are amazing and are all really passionate about their classes. Tuition is also a fair price.

The diversity and community within the visual arts department was great. Director was always ready to help and solve any concerns or needs of students. There needed to be some updating to equipment which hindered students from being able to complete projects on campus, so updating equipment is important. The professors were all good at their roles and there was always activities and events on campus for students. Food is okay. there are some great small food places nearby but could use more.
Experience was good, professors were passionate. I get to meet tons of new friends during my time off between classes.
pretty good. UNC is def a good school for those interested in MET. it's overall pretty safe too. probably not the best school in the US, but its worth it.

UNCO offers a wide selection of opportunities for many students that are willing to advocate for themselves. Most professors are very willing to help students and have great opportunities for research and academic advancement in different areas.
As an HSI, I would like to see the college focus more on diversity and meeting the needs of its student population.

University of Northern Colorado is a good school, the campus is extremely safe, due to the campus police. The professors are willing to work with you and are there for you to pass. The only thing I would change is the dinning hall hours, since they are not open at 10 am which would be nice but other than that it is a very nice school.

While studying at the University of Northern Colorado I have been forever changed. I began school in Fall of 2023 and it was my first time ever being on my own. I had to focus on time management more then I ever had to at home. However, after the first few weeks I got into a very steady rhythm. I would attend classes, campus activities and so much more. The thing I most enjoyed about the campus was how welcoming everyone was. No one saw anyone as an outcast or a stranger, everyone wanted others to succeed. I spent a lot of my time either in the library with my colleagues studying or at the gym working out with some of my best friends. The professors always cared about their students as well. I specifically remember one of my professors Dr. Pullen who allowed extra one on one time to fully understand a topic I was struggling with in order to bring my grade up in his class. Overall I can not fathom how grateful I am that I decided to attend the University of Northern Colorado.

There are many things I enjoy about my experience so far at UNC. There is a good variety of food every day, not only at the dining hall but as well as other spots on campus that are available. I also like the options for activities outside of education, I am currently in the school's jiu-jitsu club and it has been great. If there is one thing that I would like the see change, it would be longer hours for the spots to eat on campus, but it is understandably easier said than done.

I think UNC is a good school, though I’ve never attended an in person class. My research class I took was well structured and I learned a lot. I’m excited to go full time starting next year after my time at Aims. I think it’s a very walkable area and everything is easily accessible. The only thing I would work on is adding more programs as many students are struggling to find a program at UNC and is leaving for other nearby options.

UNCo, overall was a great college, but for the area, it was way too liberal, and the professors are dependent on the subject matter, proficient. Make sure you make connections with your offices and deans. The finance department had a vindictive professor, and the prices of the were at best at least cheaper than a majority of the state. I would go back but probably not as an undergraduate, but maybe only as a fellow or graduate student when I really only needed too. The police force is very professional as well and the veterans service was a helpful group. The on campus ministry was very beneficial as a community and spiritual growth as well.
I am a transfer student from Adams State University to UNC this year so do not have a lot to rate. The admission process was difficult and not streamlined for a transfer student.

As a sophomore at the University of Northern Colorado (UNC), I am thrilled to share my positive experience at this remarkable institution. From the moment I set foot on campus, I felt welcomed and supported by both faculty and fellow students. The sense of community here is truly unparalleled.
Overall, my experience at the University of Northern Colorado has been incredibly rewarding. I am grateful for the supportive community, excellent education, and countless opportunities for personal and professional growth. I highly recommend UNC to anyone seeking a well-rounded and fulfilling college experience.
Mostly online...so I am limited in my response. Campus is nice. Employees have been kind and helpful so far.

Great music school environment, good graduate/undergrad ratio and interaction. Affordable. Access to Recording Studio at a student rate was great. A little far from Denver and/or Fort Collins if you are trying to play gigs in those cities while in school. Nice community. Occasional preferential treatment given to graduate students in ensemble placement auditions. Had to study both classical and jazz which I liked, but it may not be for everyone (jazz history plus classical history, etc.- orchestration and arranging both required).

ai_summary: The University of Notre Dame is often described as a beautiful campus with a strong sense of community , where traditions and school spirit are integral to the student experience. Academically, it holds a reputation for rigor, with many students noting the opportunities for engaged learning and supportive faculty. However, some reviewers express concern about class sizes and a perceived lack of individual attention from professors. The community is generally seen as welcoming, yet many comments highlight a lack of diversity and an environment that may feel exclusive to certain demographic groups. Student life is enriched by various clubs, sports, and faith-based activities, although some find social dynamics can be cliquey. There are also mixed feelings about the value of tuition relative to the overall experience, with some noting that the costs are high for what is offered, especially outside the academic realm. Overall, the Notre Dame experience is characterized by a blend of challenging academics, strong alumni support, and a vibrant campus culture .

reviews.text: I found the campus community to be phenomenal, especially in undergraduate dorms and surrounding athletics.
The community is by far the best part. Students, faculty, and professors make the place what it is, home.
The culture within the dorms is very strong. Everyone grows very close with each other, and there is a lot of pride in your dorm and in the events your dorm hosts.

Notre Dame had provided more support than I ever expected from a college. Both inside the classrooms and extracurricular there are always to challenge my curiosity and explore my passions. While there are many aspects which are hard to feel proud of (entitlement, ignorance and hypnotically ideals) most are caused by individual members of the community. The university as an establishment has been a net positive throughout my time here.
Fantastic experience, with challenging courses, excellent students, high expectations and an incredible network post graduation

University of Norte Dame is such a unique campus and experience. Everyone is so nice and enthusiastic! Haven't experienced any athletics as of yet, but plan to! Love the dorm structure and the food is pretty good!

My time at the University of Notre Dame was truly transformative. I loved the beautiful campus, the strong sense of community, and the emphasis on values. The professors were amazing, and the classes were challenging yet supportive.
However, I'd like to see more diversity and inclusion efforts, and more flexible academic programs to cater to individual interests. Overall, Notre Dame offered an exceptional education and instilled a deep appreciation for integrity and service. It's a place I highly recommend!

Notre Dame has been a great place to be. I can only speak from my experience in Arts and Letters, but in all my classes I've appreciated that the rigor the University is known for is driven by Professors who care about the topic and want you to be well-informed about it (not just rigor and challenge for the sake of a class being hard).

The University of Notre Dame is a beautiful bubble of life in South Bend. The work in my undergraduate studies was steadily challenging, but the support from professors, advisors, and student leaders made it worth it. The trick is to find groups of people that push you forward to be a better student and person. I found those people in the Gender Studies and Studio Art departments and will forever be grateful for the memories I have with them at Notre Dame. Notre Dame’s future to be a true “force for good” is to stand strong in the face of federal pressure and work in accordance with their mission of valuing human life by supporting their students, faculty, and staff.
I love the way the community comes together. Everyone is very welcoming, and the professors are helpful and genuinely aiming to guide you in your understanding and help you succeed.
Great school with a perfect balance between academic rigor and fun student life. Made some of my best friends and best memories here.
The academics are priority to me but the students tend to all be very similar in terms of background, race, etc which makes it hard to meet lots of new interesting people

I loved going to Notre Dame! I am so grateful for the education I got at Notre Dame - most of my professors were so kind and really care about their students. I enjoyed a lot of my classes. But what makes Notre Dame more special is the people - I felt at home with the community there. I was able to make so many friends through my dorm, classes, and the extracurriculars I was a part of. Can't forget about the football though. Attending football games as a student was definitely a highlight of my undergrad experience

Notre Dame is amazing! The people truly make this university what it is. Alongside incredible academics, the campus community is thriving. There is always someone to talk to or rely on. I am very grateful I attend this school.

I would love to see more diversity on campus, and I would love for advisors to be more on task, especially when it comes to premed students, because we can get lost pretty quickly, not really knowing what to do!

ai_summary: At the University of Oregon, students often appreciate the beautiful campus and friendly atmosphere , highlighting a community feel where many feel safe and included. Academically, the university has strong programs , particularly in business, psychology, and the College of Design, although some students note that the rigor can vary significantly across different courses. The faculty is generally described as supportive and knowledgeable, contributing positively to the learning experience. However, there are concerns about the lack of diversity on campus and the rising tuition costs, which some consider high for the available resources. The social scene can be somewhat limited, focusing heavily on Greek life and sports, particularly football, while the city of Eugene is considered small with limited nightlife options . Overall, while the university offers many opportunities for engagement and growth, students feel they need to actively seek out experiences and connections.

reviews.text: My experience with the university has been really rocky as I struggle to maintain complete focus. My need for financial assistance is big as this is my main concern to completely focus at school. For this reason it has been rocky. Somethings I did like at the university were the great professors, they excell at their job and at times it keeps me motivated. Students as well have been incredibly kind and the overall vibe from people is amazing. Something I would like to see change directly from the school is the cost of tuition and room and board. They restrict students from financial need backgrounds to attend the school, and it shows that they tend to students from a higher income, while low income students and families find other ways to be able to pay for the school, or look elsewhere and turn down a dream. I believe this is one big thing that needs to change. The school needs more diversity, everyone should be welcomed.

My experience at the University of Oregon as an education major has been very positive and rewarding. The education program has given me a strong foundation in both teaching strategies and understanding how to support diverse learners. I’ve especially valued the hands-on field experiences, which allowed me to apply what I’ve learned in real classroom settings and better understand the day-to-day realities of teaching.
The professors have been supportive and knowledgeable, and they’ve created an environment where I feel encouraged to grow and reflect as a future educator. I also appreciate the program’s focus on inclusivity and creating safe, supportive classrooms for all students.
One area for improvement would be offering more consistent and longer-term classroom placements earlier in the program. This would help students build stronger connections and gain more confidence before student teaching. Overall, I feel well-prepared and grateful for my experience.

The university of oregon is a great school full of a lot of diversity in academics, athletics, social and more. Seasonal depression hits really hard over here, but it when the weather is nice, campus is really nice
There are tons of resources on campus.

Go Ducks! Best experience of my life.
Great campus
Lovely dorms
Eugene is great place to live you can find everything you need
I love it. Beyond wonderful. My freshman year was amazing and I am enjoying my sophomore year and look forward to really soaring at U of Oregon

There are a large amount of courses, schools and majors to choose from. I really appreciated the financial aid they were able to give me. There is a lot of focus on sports, business, and architecture, so if that’s what you’re going for it’s a great fit! Some of the sciences and arts could use more funding and priority but you can still have an effective education.

University of Oregon is a great academic campus that cares about its students and offers a wide range of courses. A quarter system can seem intimidating, but there are unique benefits such as access to more course topics and having a diverse experience among teaching faculty and staff that make it worthwhile.

My overall experience with University of Oregon was incredible, the campus life, especially for freshman is very fun and there is so much support for students. From picking out classes to studying abroad, there are advisors who are available to answer questions for everything. The campus is vibrant and the food is so good.

My experience was incredible. Initially ambivalent, I quickly grew to love the UO community. Far from overly competitive and cutthroat, everyone, faculty and students included, was nurturing and fostered both my personal and professional development. I couldn't be more grateful for the opportunities I was amassed.
Love the campus, great resources, both academically and out of the academics ,like extracurriculars and personal well being. Also a very student-led action college.

The class sizes of the initial core classes can be overwhelming being in a room with five hundred people but the professors make the experiences enjoyable and allow for connection outside of lecture time. I am a psychology major so I have been able to participate in research studies which has been wonderful! I don't think I would change anything and I am looking forward to the upper division courses so we can dive deeper into the content of psychology in different careers. I like that the university operated like a small town. There are places to relax, study, eat, and get medical attention if you need it. The clubs and student lead organizations are a dream to be a part of something bigger.

I personally liked the student life when it came to our football games. Whether they were at the stadium or the common area of their dorms, everyone joined together for every game and were united. I usually went with one person to the games, but you would talk to everyone around you and befriend them. Even if it was away games, everyone showed spirit for the game. Additionally, the residence that live nearby had spirit for the games. However, one thing I would like to see changed is the dorm buildings, especially the older ones. Every newer building has more utilities than others. For example, some of the newest building have way more washers and dryers than older ones, and that could affect ones entire day. With over a hundred students living on every floor, small numbers of machines makes the process take way longer than it should. It'll become an entire day affair instead of just a couple hour task.
Overall positive, Eugene can be sketchy and know that if anything happens the university does not prioritize student safety and well-being. They are more concerned about reputation and money.

I'm in my freshman year here as a music major, and I'm enjoying it! I have lots of friends both in my major and outside of my major. The dorms are great, and there are lots of events that happen on campus.
The UO is a great school, but you shouldn't expect too much more than that- the culture is pretty uniform, and you can get what you put into it, with it having good opportunities if you look for them

ai_summary: The University of Pennsylvania offers a mix of rigorous academics and vibrant student life , making it an attractive option for many high school seniors. Reviewers appreciate the beautiful campus and the diverse range of academic programs, particularly highlighting opportunities in research and cross-disciplinary studies. Many students find the competitive environment both motivating and stressful, often describing a "work hard/play hard" culture that heavily emphasizes pre-professionalism. While the professors are generally praised for their expertise and accessibility, some departments, like math, have faced criticism. Social life at Penn is lively, with numerous events and a strong Greek presence, though the dining options and housing conditions receive mixed reviews. Students express a desire for better mental health support and inclusivity initiatives, indicating areas for potential improvement. Overall, Penn provides a robust educational experience enriched by a culturally dynamic setting in Philadelphia.

reviews.text: Top tier professors. The quality of my education here has far exceeded other colleges and universities I have attended. They are a little but under-resourced for fully online students, but they are growing in that capacity every semester.

I'm not sure if I would attend again because of it's very pre-professional environment. I didn't feel like I was there to learn but rather get a job. I will say, the name recognition of Penn and Wharton has really helped me later on as well.

I love the scene and the sense of community at Penn. The people I've met here are genuinely some of the most interesting and kind individuals. My department is also a highlight—it feels very tight-knit and offers amazing resources for students.

The University of Pennsylvania embodies the perfect balance of “work hard, play hard,” supported by an exceptional network that lasts a lifetime. Its rich traditions and Ivy League legacy create a campus culture where ambition and community thrive together. Every school at Penn offers world‑class academics, with Wharton widely regarded as the best business school in the world. Penn’s dual‑degree options—especially the M&T Program, often considered the most elite undergraduate program in the country—set students up for unparalleled long‑term success. Combined with vibrant student life, spirited athletics, and a genuinely fun college experience, Penn stands out as a truly extraordinary place to grow.
Great academics, social life, city life, and a work hard play hard balance. If you love exploring the city and always having something to do you will love Penn

The University university of Pennsylvania is overall a rigorous institution that maintains Ivy League standards in terms of quality of education, environment, and student experience. The university has lots of resources for almost anything that a student can need, whether it be professional outreach, financial assistance, food assistance, and many other benefits. I find it catering all classes of people. However, some may find its diversity a bit lacking. If it’s your first time trying to get there, you might get confused since it’s in the middle of nowhere. But once you step onto campus, you’ll fall in love with it. If you can handle the extreme workload of multiple papers and assignments every few days as well as the tough grading, then UPenn might be the one for you.
I haven't started yet but from my research university of Pennsylvania is an esteemed university that trains leaders those aspiring to be great with all love, patience and equality

UPenn has provided me with more resources and opportunities than I could ever imagine. It seems like every day I learn about a new service offered at Penn. If I ever had an idea I wanted to pursue (which I do, in fact, have many) I'm assured that there are ample opportunities at Penn to help me bring it to fruition. On top of that, the social scene is amazing here. All the faculty are dedicated towards making sure each student is having a great time and is making friends. Many events are hosted each day where we can cool down and socialize after a long day of classes. It's great, I'm having TONS of fun here while being super intellectually stimulated!

My favorite part of attending Penn is its connections to the Philadelphia community. There are many tutoring services that Penn offers to West Philadelphia elementary and middle schools that allow students to gain a sense of what Philadelphia is like outside of campus. Clubs as well, such as Penn Wellness, serve the community of Philadelphia with food and fun events to help provide access to the public's needs. I would like to see change in healthy food options on campus. Penn offers great options for dining halls, but nutritious food is not always available, nor is variety is food that is offered.
Great university and great campus. People here are fascinating and energetic, love the professors here. But Philly needs to be improved
Good school overall. Campus food needs a lot of work. The professors in the STEM department are not the best, and there is definitely grade deflation. But everything else is amazing.
Penn is the right fit for me- I wanted a "work hard, play hard" place with top notch academics but also a vibrant social life!

My experience at the University of Pennsylvania has been both academically challenging and personally enriching. As a student in the architecture program, I’ve had the opportunity to work closely with passionate faculty, collaborate on complex design projects, and engage in coursework that pushes me to think critically about sustainability, space, and social impact. The diverse campus community has exposed me to new perspectives and helped me grow both as a designer and a global citizen.

As an international student, I’ve had a positive experience at Penn. The school proactively supports students through Campus Express and helpful orientation sessions. I appreciated learning early on about Penn’s safe, accessible transportation and the Career Center’s services—resume reviews, mock interviews, and career advising. Advisors and staff respond promptly, and when visa delays impacted many international students this year, the school provided detailed emails, info sessions, and online course options to keep us on track. However, I hope Penn can offer more scholarships for international students, as tuition is high and financial aid limited. Additionally, while many offices respond efficiently, some departments are understaffed, leading to long delays. Finally, on-campus job opportunities are limited, making it difficult for international students with work restrictions to support themselves. More positions and support for student employment would make a meaningful difference.
Work hard, play hard environment. Help is always available but must be sought out. Philly is a great city!

There are several things that I enjoyed during my experience at the University of Pikeville. The thing I liked the most was the community. The University of Pikeville is a small campus, so it provided us with a more hands-on learning experience. Through these experiences I gained lasted connections to both my classmates and professors. My only critique for what I would like to see changed is the campus life. The University of Pikeville is in a small town where student need to make their own fun, however, it would be nice to see continuous improvements into the area for more entertainment that college students could take advantage of.

The University of Pikeville was extremely challenging and rewarding. The professors were both firm and fair, while truly caring for the students in the classes. I always felt safe and secure. The public safety department understood that students were there to get an education and took care of the safety aspect. The administration was always visible and accessible.

When there is already nothing to do in the town, the horrific Wi-Fi makes it worse. Could barely even watch a clear TV show on it and it cuts out at least once or twice a week for the night. Professors are not qualified to teach in most cases and you will probably have to take 3 of the same professors for every semester that you are here. Most I learned here was of Chegg and Chat GPT.

I thoroughly enjoyed the academic side of Pikeville. The professors in my specified major were fantastic. I had psychology and CJ professors. Both did phenomenal and made learning fun. I do know that the Biology major is struggling and many do not like professors.

I’ve loved being at UPike, I’ve met a lot of great people and have created amazing friendships! One thing I love about the school is that it’s very family oriented; everyone feels like one big family!

I am currently enjoying my experience attending University of Pikeville. This school is centered around helping one another succeed and making sure you are doing well. This school will go above and beyond to make you feel safe, welcome, and happy. There are many events held every week that will force you to engage with others and participation in them leads to more bonds being created and more memories made.

At UPIKE, you hear the word FAMILY A LOT! I was skeptical at first with how much I heard about FAMILY. After week 4 this is true, you are FAMILY! You are more than a number, you are somebody. From the Public Safety people to the RAs you are treated like family! During first year orientation the Dean was greeting students. One the first day of classes he was giving fist bumps and greeted me by name. I feel at HOME and I feel SAFE. Best decision was coming to UPIKE.

My overall experience at this university was subpar. The night life was non exsistent. The sports here were not very appreciated. The food never imporved even after many complained. I will say I did always feel safe on this campus, the small class sizes really benifited me, and the professor were always there to help and tried to make outside connections and good relationships with us.

I switched to Upike from another college and was automatically met with great things. They have a disability resource center with an amazing staff
Who will fight the housing and school battles for you. They go out of their way to message your professors and housing staff if you need any accommodations for anything. And they have a whole office dedicated to diversity, as well as an ace office for low income or/and first generation college students who will help navigate college life and will be there for you even after you graduate. I also participate in their theatre program and nothing I have participated in has been negative and very accommodating to my epilepsy.

I attended Undergrad at the University of Pikeville as well. The small class sizes were a great way to build connections and relationships with classmates and professors. There was always events and activities for students to attend, even on the weekends.
I have loved the opportunities at UPIKE. I have yet to have a terrible experiences here. Going to a private University has helped my transitions roll so much easier and quicker!

My experience here at the University of pikeville has been pretty interesting. there has been multiple different events on campus or off-campus. The cafe food is okay. some people are friendly and some are not.

Unbelievably bad instruction combined with patriarchal politics. Student clubs entirely managed by overworked faculty members instead of being managed by the students themselves. Pointless, time-consuming rituals throughout the year, not just at graduation. The Honors ceremony, for instance, goes on for hours and accomplishes nothing. Students encouraged to miss substantial class time for dubious field trips. One student, for instance, demanded excused absences because her church conducted a mission to Paris.

Upike has given me the opportunity to stay close to home while still being able to achieve my academic goals and help benefit the people living in this area as well. With their amazing nursing building and educators, me and my classmates are able to become confident in our skills and team working abilities.

It is a wonderful university that has such a welcoming community. The people are so kind and the professors are so dedicated to the success of their students. There is so much emphasis on mental health that it is a place that any student can be at ease reaching out.

ai_summary: The University of Pittsburgh garners a range of feedback from students focusing on academics, campus life, and facilities. Many students appreciate the vibrant student life , highlighting the variety of clubs, social opportunities, and the friendly community that makes it easy to make friends. However, there are mixed feelings about the academic experience; while some students enjoy supportive professors and the quality of certain programs, others note significant issues with faculty in the STEM fields and problematic grading policies. Campus facilities like housing and dining come under criticism, particularly concerning outdated dorms and subpar food options. Despite these drawbacks, students often describe the campus atmosphere as lively and its location in Pittsburgh as convenient, providing a balanced college experience with access to city amenities. Overall, Pitt is viewed as a solid choice for students eager for both academic and social engagement, particularly for those interested in health-related fields.

reviews.text: I genuinely have enjoyed my experience at this university as the professors and opportunities from my professors as well as for my major have been top tier, making me fall in love with my major and future more every year

Pitt offers a wide range of opportunities for undergraduate students in any field. As Pittsburgh is a sizable city, almost every academic department can connect students with job and internship opportunities outside of campus. Professors are generally very understanding, I rarely encountered professors who were unwilling to talk to me one-on-one and assist with course content. Additionally, average class sizes were between 25 and 35 students. In terms of sports, Pitt is a D1 school, students get free season tickets to football games, which are played at Heinz Field. Volleyball and basketball are also highlights. The Pitt Fitness Center offers free workout classes, and there is no shortage of clubs and student organizations to get involved in. Overall, I think Pitt offers everything you could want from a large university, and was a super positive experience. Of course, there could always be more diversity, and first-year housing could definitely use some renovations.

Academically a great school, but their support for students is almost non-existent. Having a sibling who attended South Carolina and had incredible support and guidance to help them get REU's, advanced lab work, and grad school opportunities. At Pitt I can barely get an adviser to do more than rubber-stamp my class choices.

Overall, Pitt's academics are very good, and they have a lot of opportunities, especially for STEM students. The only thing I have to complain about is the housing; they over-admit, and the current students have fewer places to live on campus to make room for freshmen.

My experience at the University of Pittsburgh was generally positive but there are a few changes that could be made in order to benefit incoming students. The dining halls are quite bad and there is a lack of first year housing which is causing many students to have to live in hotels. The school is also turning upper level housing into first year housing which is making it hard for upperclassmen to find housing on campus and off campus rental prices are high. However, the academic climate is good and I felt I learned a lot in my time here.
Overall, the University of Pittsburgh provided a fun experience with challenging classes, engaging professors, and lots of campus activities.

I love Pitt, from the sports (no matter how terrible they are) to the classes to the city. I worried when I started that being in a big school would make me feel like a number, but I've always felt like an individual here that can do my own thing and stand out.

Overall my experience was good there are a lot of programs and opportunities. I wish they had offered more help in internships and some professors can be unnecessarily tough but most are understanding.

Campus and academic life are both decent. The food is actually awful, especially in the dining halls. A lot of ongoing construction that obstructs sidewalks. Plenty of extracurriculars, although you have to find out yourself when events are happening.

I appreciate the knowledge and education that I have received throughout my years at the University of Pittsburgh. Many professors were brilliant and formed one-on-one relationships with students rather than just lecturing the class as one. I also formed many friends and connections during my time of attendance.
The school is honestly amazing, but the only downside is the food lowk sucks and the dorms can honestly be much better.

My overall experience at the University of Pittsburgh has been both enriching and transformative. Pitt offers a rigorous academic environment that has challenged me to think critically, adapt quickly, and apply what I learn to real-world situations. Through my coursework, I have developed strong analytical, communication, and problem-solving skills that continue to shape my academic and professional growth.
Beyond academics, Pitt provides countless opportunities for involvement, leadership, and community engagement. Being part of a large and diverse campus has allowed me to connect with people from different backgrounds and perspectives, helping me grow both socially and personally. Involvement in student organizations and campus initiatives has strengthened my leadership abilities and sense of responsibility to the community.

I loved my undergrad experience at Pitt. Because it is such a large school, there are so many student organizations to join, so many classes to take, and so many people to meet. And yet, I feel like in all the social circles I was a part of, someone knew someone else in a different circle, so it never felt that huge. My STEM courses were challenging, but they were usually balanced by really interesting and informative humanities courses. Overall, I loved my college expereince at Pitt.

I’ve only finished my first semester but it’s really nice so far! The other students seem to be really caring and there are a lot of resources available to everyone, especially academically and health-wise. They have a strong astronomy department too, even if it’s typically a more niche area of study.

This year, the university over-enrolled. I was accepted with early action in December. I filled out my housing form the day it became available. Everyone else got their housing before me. By two weeks! No one would communicate with us about it. They put me and 200 other students in a Hampton Inn a mile down Forbes Avenue. We pay the most for our housing out of everyone on campus. We pay more than the higher-end dorms at Nordenburg, right next to the school. This has significantly impacted the experiences of my dormmates and me. The eatery has gone downhill, but all the other food options are solid. Professors, for the most part, are solid. You can have a lot of fun with your friends on the weekend, very close to campus, which is nice as well.

ai_summary: The University of Puerto Rico - Rio Piedras offers a diverse and vibrant academic environment , where many students appreciate the dedication of professors and the breadth of academic programs available. There's a strong sense of community that fosters personal growth, especially highlighted during challenging times such as natural disasters and political unrest. However, students frequently mention issues with the administration, citing inefficiencies in services and bureaucratic processes. Facilities show signs of wear, and some students feel improvements are needed in areas like housing and sports amenities. Tuition remains affordable compared to many institutions, but recent increases and budget cuts may pose concerns for accessibility. Overall, the unique blend of cultural richness , academic rigor, and an active student body contributes to a distinctive college experience, though it is marred by certain operational shortcomings.

reviews.text: I absolutely love the University of Puerto Rico Rio Piedras simply because of the diversity of students. I love seeing the diferent types of activities or performance, especially the ones with different culture and ascetic. The student body that I have seen and experience are full of helpful and kind people.

UPR Río Piedras is a fairly average university. While it has strong academics in some areas, the overall experience is often bogged down by bureaucracy. Getting anything done, whether it’s registration, paperwork, or resolving issues, requires jumping through endless hoops. You’ll need multiple documents, signatures, and visits to different offices, and the support you get is minimal at best.
Staff assistance is inconsistent. Some people genuinely try to help, but many seem indifferent or overwhelmed, which makes the process even more frustrating.
Professors vary widely. Some are excellent, engaged, knowledgeable, and supportive. Others are disorganized or disengaged, and the quality of instruction can be hit or miss depending on the course.
If you’re persistent and self-driven, you can succeed here. But be ready to fight for every step.

In my opinion, the University of Puerto Rico (UPR), is the best university in the island. It offers a comprehensive, profound, and enriching education that often exceeds expectations. From the moment I entered it became my place of peace. I truly enjoy being on campus, that even on days when I don’t have classes, I still go to campus because I simply love experiencing university life.
The professors are not only outstanding professionals but also remarkable human beings. Student life is diverse and vibrant, and the overall environment reflects resilience, continuous growth, and a strong sense of community. What I would like to see is that the university’s structure and essence are preserved, that students make the most of every resource available, and that the country as a whole values the wealth of knowledge this institution holds. The UPR is a true treasure, and despite many challenges, it has found ways to move forward. I sincerely hope it continues to do so. UPR, my alma mater.

My experience in UPR Rio Piedras these four years has been incredible. From the awesome professors, to the students, to the opportunities. Everyone is always eager to help you in anything you need. And the professors are always looking for chances to help you grow as a student and as a person. I am really grateful to be studying in UPR Rio Piedras.

It's a great university with great diversity of professors with various backgrounds. You will learn about Puerto Rico with a different lens and the world. They give you the skills you need for success in whichever endeavor, life path or career path.
My classes have been online so I haven’t been much to the campus. Is the best university of Puerto Rico and the professors are excellent.

My experience at the University of Puerto Rico – Río Piedras has been truly transformative. I’ve grown so much, not just academically, but also personally and socially. It’s a space full of diversity, resilience, and critical thinking. I’ve met amazing people and professors who are genuinely passionate about what they teach. While the administrative side can be slow and frustrating at times, the quality of the education makes up for it. Studying at UPR-RP has connected me to my identity and my desire to give back to my country. Here, you don’t just study—you learn how to create change.
Its a good university with bearly campus life. Evrebody goes to class and after go to their houses. The education is excellent and very diversed people.

My years in the University of Puerto Rico were full of excitement. I majored in Natural Sciences and then minored in Science Education, so I ended up working as a Science and Math teacher for the Puerto Rico Department of Education. As any student who wants and comes to college to learn, I enjoyed every moment of my student years in this institution. Graduated with a BS.
My experience at the University of Puerto Rico, Río Piedras Campus, was incredibly enriching in terms of academics, social life, personal growth, and professional development.

My experience at the University of Puerto Rico, Río Piedras, has been both challenging and rewarding. I appreciate the strong academic environment, particularly the opportunities for critical thinking and research within my Interdisciplinary Sciences program. The professors are knowledgeable and passionate, and I have enjoyed engaging discussions that push me to grow intellectually. Additionally, the campus itself is rich in culture and history, creating a unique atmosphere that fosters both academic and personal growth. However, I believe there are areas for improvement. Administrative processes can sometimes be slow and inefficient, making it difficult for students to get timely assistance with enrollment, financial aid, or other university services. Additionally, expanding research opportunities and modernizing facilities would enhance the overall student experience.
I like most of the things of campus. If I had to changes something it will definitely be some professors because some of them I think don’t teach the right way

Being a student at the University of Puerto Rico - Río Piedras has been an enriching experience. The campus is vibrant, filled with a diverse student body and a mix of academic rigor and cultural richness. The professors are knowledgeable and passionate about their subjects, which makes classes engaging. I’ve been able to connect with people from various backgrounds, and the sense of community is strong. There are plenty of extracurricular activities to get involved in, from student organizations to cultural events. Though there are challenges, like bureaucracy and occasional infrastructure issues, the overall experience has shaped me into a more well-rounded person. It’s a unique place where education meets Puerto Rican culture in a meaningful way.

The University of Puerto Rico is a good option for those who, like me, need a good education while still profiting from opportunities like scholarships and financial aid. I think the quality of most if not all bachelors degrees are wonderful.

The University of Puerto Rico is my alma mater. I have been a product of the UPR system since I began studying at UHS in seventh grade. It fills me with pride to belong to the first teaching center in the country and every day I try to honor the centennial academic legacy to which I belong through my studies in the Faculty of Natural Sciences and my participation in university sports. My cultural, intellectual and national identity have been forged over the years on the streets of Río Piedras. I owe this institution a lot and strive to honor it through my academic achievments.

ai_summary: At the University of Rhode Island, students often highlight a mix of positive and negative experiences, particularly regarding academics and campus life. Many appreciate the supportive professors who are dedicated to student success and the diverse range of majors available, especially in programs like marine biology and nursing. The campus is generally considered beautiful, with proximity to beaches enhancing the appeal. However, some students express dissatisfaction with the size of the institution, stating it can feel overwhelming and that social life often hinges on Greek life or organized sports. There are mixed reviews on the quality of services like academic advising and financial aid, with some finding long wait times and limited access frustrating. Dorm facilities and food options receive criticism for being inconsistent, indicating a desire for improvements in these areas. Overall, while URI fosters a sense of community and provides many opportunities, there are notable challenges that prospective students should be aware of.

reviews.text: I intend to be a transfer student at the University of Rhode Island. I am very interested in attending URI but it is currently more expensive than the university I am currently attending. I am trying to find and get scholarships to help afford URI. I have friends who attend the university and have visited a lot. I love the the university and have grown to feel very comfortable there.

The University of Rhode Island is a good school. They are diverse and offer a great variety of courses. Wether you are a business major or a nursing or engineering major or anywhere in between, they got you covered.

My experience at URI was very iffy. There were things I struggled with campus wise. The parking situation is terrible as the student lot can be a 10 minute walk from your dorm and is often times full and there is nowhere else to park. The parking pass is so expensive for there to be these issues. The campus is also not easy to drive around which makes it complicated to pull up near your dorm if you need to load your car. In addition to this, the dorms are very outdated. Lastly, the food options are an issue because on the weekends only 1 dining hall is open but if you're not able to get there during their hours, you'll have to pay for other more accesible options. The classes are typically 25-50 people which isn't bad but there is minimal student and professor connection. The system to sign up for classes or view your schedule is often down and leads to complications.

Don't come here. Campus is boring and miserable. Only way you will have any type of social life is if you join greek life. That being said the greek life people are some of the most obnoxious people I've ever met. Housing is awful and the food is survivable at best. There will probably be times where the dining hall serves shrimp dishes for days straight. As an out of state kid I don't feel like I belong here. The best thing to do in Kingston is leave.

URI was the best undergraduate school experience ever!!! All of my professors and academic team were supportive and intellectual. From freshmen year to graduation I felt like my time at URI was well-worth it
I am a current student, and I enjoy my experiences so far. There are many clubs available for students to take advantage of as well as sports teams, though I'm not familiar with them.

Honestly, for me, what I really liked about URI was making friends and being around like-minded students who share my goals. The student life here is the best, and there are many events happening all around you that you can take advantage of.

I like the supportive nature of everyone and everything. I like the easy access to everything that I need in terms of academics. Something that I wish they did differently is to make it clear if there is a schedule conflict with the professor prior to classes starting.
