I think it is unacceptable that the university should allow students to enroll in a section and possible make semester long plans around THAT, only to have it changed by something out of the student's control. As a second, minor note, I do not think that classes should be allowed to run/begin past 5-pm.

My overall experience at University of Rhode Island was extremely positive and rewarding. The professors were highly knowledgeable, supportive, and genuinely invested in student success. They consistently took the time to explain complex subjects clearly and were always available to address questions or concerns, whether inside or outside the classroom.
I graduated in 2020, a period marked by significant global uncertainty and disruption. Despite these challenges, my time at URI provided a strong academic foundation and, more importantly, the confidence and resilience I needed to continue pursuing my educational goals. The mentorship, rigorous training, and supportive learning environment at URI prepared me well for the next stage of my academic journey.
As a result, I felt well-equipped and motivated to pursue an advanced degree in Biotechnology at Texas Woman’s University, where I continued to build upon the skills and confidence I developed during my undergraduate experience.

As a second-semester senior at The University of Rhode Island, I reflect on my time here fondly. There are many ways to get involved at URI through sports, Greek life, clubs, and more. Being in a sorority gave me a support system of women who hold each other accountable and uplift one another. I have enjoyed each of my courses and have found that the professors in the psychology and HDF departments are exceptional mentors. We are encouraged to participate, ask questions, and seek feedback, which I appreciate in a learning environment. I have formed several close connections with my professors who have acted as mentors and support systems for me. It genuinely feels like the teachers at URI want every student to succeed, and they do everything in their power to ensure that they do. Overall, the academics at URI alone are enough to say it's an amazing school. However, the location and opportunities for growth and connection make it an even better option.

The University of Rhode Island offers a wide variety of classes under all different subject matters with varying degrees of difficulty. The social scene including greek life and different club activity is extremely prevalent. URI uses the application Brightspace for class information, grades, and assignment, however there are often issues with this application and it is difficult to get an accurate reading of your grades throughout the semester.

The University of Rhode Island provides a great atmosphere. The campus itself is midsized and very walkable with various options to eat and hang out. There is not much of a downtown scene, however, buses do run to Providence for students who don't have vehicles. In my own experience the professors have been very accommodating surrounding mental and physical health. There are always options for things to do on the weekends including clubs, sports games, or parties. This is a very Greek life centered school, but as someone who is not affiliated with Greek life, it is still easy to find something to do! The dining hall offers a lot of different options everyday and many of the off campus location accept Ram Account(a part of the dining plan).

Over the three years I have spent at URI (graduating a year early) I have had an exceptional experience. As a commuting student, then living closer off campus in my final year, both the faculty and students have done a tremendous job of making everyone feel included, regardless of living on or off campus. As someone apart of an academic fraternity, I feel as though the clubs, groups, and recreational activities here set students up well in terms of networking, making connections outside of the classroom, and even creating friendships, which I have done through extra curricular activities here. I chose URI for the academics, the flexibility to graduate early, the option to double major, the beautiful campus and the proximity to the coast, which is where I reside, work, and spend my free time fishing, swimming and surfing year round. I am extremely proud to be a soon to be graduate of an awesome institution, a newly crowned R1 school, and a top public university in New England.

When I was first applying to colleges, URI was at the bottom of my list. However, after attending a university in New Hampshire, I’ve come to find attending a university closer to home where I’m able to commute yet still be involved in campus life is the best fit for me. You are right gives me this opportunity and I’m able to continue my education while still being close to home.

While I have only been in University for a few months, I have taken the full opportunity to connect with people in both my social and educational life. When I first arrived in University, I sought to connect with as many people as possible to gain valuable insight into my major and in general find advice for my career. I found work at my institution's computer center, joined clubs that related to my major, and have taken the time to connect with my peers, instructors, and advisors to ensure my success in my education. My job has allowed me to connect with upperclassman in my major allowing me to learn valuable advice to plan out my college career, have participated in hands on work in clubs obtaining experience working on electronics, and furthermore have connected with academic advisors to stay on top of my education. In all, while I am still a freshman who has yet to see the whole college experience, I have taken my best efforts to make it most out of it.

ai_summary: At the University of Rochester, students often highlight the academic rigor and flexibility offered by the open curriculum, allowing for exploration within and beyond their majors. Many appreciate the small campus atmosphere that fosters a close-knit community , yet some express concerns about social integration, particularly among international students and Greek life. There are mixed reviews about campus life; while many enjoy the variety of student clubs and activities, others report a lack of social opportunities and a challenging party scene. Faculty engagement is generally viewed positively, but the accessibility of professors and the quality of instruction can vary. The campus itself is described as beautiful, but students acknowledge issues with dining options, housing availability, and high tuition costs , which contribute to feelings about return on investment. Overall, while the institution provides strong academic experiences and opportunities for research, students express desire for better support services and increased communication from administration regarding student concerns.

reviews.text: Great food. Truly appreciate all of the hard work of our dining staff. They're awesome! Professors are solid. But I have to say, the campus life is a nightmare. Besides school work, there is nothing to do. No entertainment, no shopping, no real friends. Everyone drifted away since my freshmen year.

The university is a small, close-knit community relatively isolated from the broader Rochester city (but not too out of reach). This fosters a studious environment while giving students a lot of opportunity to engage with their interests and grow. In my experience, there was a lot of opportunity for research given the close proximity to the medical school. There was also a lot of leadership opportunities through club involvement. However, be mindful that career resources seem to be limited. You are better off majoring in natural sciences than humanities for later job prospects here.
It was wonderful. The whole vibe of the campus was super collaborative and not competitive in any sense at all. I really do see myself spending my next 8 years there.

Good school academically but I feel like for the amount of money we have to pay in tuition everything could be better. Some professors for the big intro stem classes are actually horrible, and they lowkey made me reconsider my degree. There are good profs but again the bad ones are really bad. Idk I feel like everything is just very mid

The University of Rochester has a strong yet balanced emphasis on academic life and community, which allows for flexibility and freedom to explore interests. Personally, I went through a lot of ups and downs, and the support system was exceptional whenever I had to resolve issues and the like.

The University of Rochester is a fantastic school, by far one of the most underrated in the country. Academics and professors are on par with Ivy+ and the research opportunities as an undergrad are unmatched. Due to its somewhat small size but incredibly robust research output, advanced research opportunities for undergraduates are abundant and as a result this university prepares its undergraduate students very well for graduate school in law, medicine, social sciences, and engineering. Students here end up in the best PhD programs in the world at Caltech, MIT, Stanford, Northwestern, Harvard, etc. The food is also really good compared to most other colleges, it should be ranked much higher than a C. The city of Rochester can be a little sleepy and the going out scene can be more intimate compared to larger universities in bigger cities, but if you're looking for an idyllic northeastern collegiate experience with fantastic STEM academics, I'd highly recommend a visit in the fall.

Academics:
Very hard to get A (course need daily study, few offer bonus point/curve adjustment).
Value:
Tuition=$69k/year, total=$95k/year.
Diversity:
Minorities don't have enough people to form clubs.
Campus:
Snow is salted, grass is NOT mowed, max 15 min walk to class.
Athletics:
Gym>soccer>everything else.
Party Scene:
Greek life only, infrequent, long lines.
Professors:
Will have 1 mandatory course with a terrible professor.
Location:
Winter storm made it even colder for even longer.
Dorms:
Ancient building, pee sprayed toilet, inhuman showers.
Campus Food:
Dining hall=horrible cooking, food court=sometimes undercooked.
Student Life:
Activities not well-advertised, clubs all mostly dead, students too busy with procrastinated deadlines. Friend groups form early in major courses, general courses=0 contact next semester.
Safety:
Smoke alarm will sound at 4 am in a winter, forcing every student out for the 7th time this semester.
I visited the university of Rochester las spring and I thoroughly enjoyed my time there. My favorite part was the architecture of the campus.

Overall the students and friends I made really highlighted the whole experience. Most professors were very welcoming and willing to help students, however not all were very receptive to feedback and seemed not to care very much for students. I think overall the faculty and leadership should be more receptive to student feedback.
Pretty easy asynchronous classes that just provided work to do, no lectures. I’ve heard from other students online classes are good

I think this is a great school. Most of the students here are pretty friendly/nerdy. There are many different types of people here and a thriving LGBTQ+ community. The food staff are so kind and friendly too.

So many opportunities here! It has the vibe of a small town school but I would say we are mid sized because of the grad students sharing some things with undergrads. The food is good, especially for dining halls, just gets a little boring after some time. It's so easy to take classes you are interested in and I love the open curriculum.

Overall, I have enjoyed my time at the University of Rochester, especially with the open curriculum and the flexibility with course choices and scheduling and being able to explore my different passions in various courses. The biggest thing I have struggled with so far is the campus not being in the most amazing place, it is pretty hard to get anywhere without a car, as the shuttle system is unreliable, and many places to go are too far to walk outside of College Town.

I have LOVED my time at U of R! I have genuinely made some of my best friends and met my favorite people. The student culture is probably not for everyone, but it is exactly right for the type of person I am. UR students are incredibly hard working and often overcommitted and over-dedicated to a wide variety of classes, subjects, clubs, and jobs, but everyone genuinely loves the things they do. It's also generally not a competitive community despite it's hard working nature–people are willing to help each other succeed through study help and guidance towards the school's own built-in resources (of which there are many).

I really loved all of the resources available to students to help them excel in class. There are plenty of opportunities to get help ranging from workshops to tutoring hours offered by certain clubs, to tutoring offered by the learning center, to library spaces with librarians to assist in research. There are a tom of clubs that are all easy to get involved in and you can find a club for almost any group you can think of. I also really like the events that they host here, from therapy dog sessions to yellowjacket weekened and meliora weekend, there are plenty of opportunities to remove yourself from academics and just have fun. There are public saftey patrols constantly driving around campus and every day I see a public saftey car patrolling the parking lot that I park in which is really nice for when I have to walk back to my car later in the evening.

ai_summary: At the University of San Diego, students often highlight the beautiful campus and the strong community feel , enhanced by small class sizes that promote close relationships with approachable professors. The academic experience is generally viewed positively, with many appreciating the supportive nature of faculty and the liberal arts curriculum. However, there are mixed feelings about student life; while some find it engaging with various events and activities, others feel the environment can be cliquey and lacks vibrant social activities. Tuition is regarded as high, leading to concerns about the value received versus the cost, and there are some criticisms surrounding diversity within the student body. Overall, students appreciate the unique blend of a beautiful setting, personalized education, and supportive faculty, despite some disappointments in social dynamics and costs.

reviews.text: Campus life offered a variety of food options, events, and even scenery! They're always evolving and making improvements to the school. While expensive, I'd say USD offers a complete experience as a college student -- and being in San Diego is always a plus.

My experience at the University of San Diego has honestly been a mix of growth, challenge, and self-discovery. While USD is a beautiful campus with a lot of opportunities, my time here has not always been easy. I’ve faced moments where the academic pressure felt overwhelming, and balancing coursework, personal responsibilities, and my own mental well-being has been difficult at times. There were semesters where I struggled to stay motivated, questioned my abilities, and felt like I was constantly trying to catch up.
At the same time, those struggles became some of the most important parts of my experience. USD pushed me to develop resilience, discipline, and a stronger sense of who I am. I’ve learned how to manage stress better, how to ask for help when I need it, and how to keep going even when things feel uncertain. I’ve also grown more confident in my goals and in my ability to work through setbacks. Overall, my experience at USD hasn’t been perfect, but it has been meaningful.

I love USD! It is a great balance of academics, extra curriculars, wellness, and fun. There is so much to do and it is in the perfect location - 10 minutes from the beach and city. The smaller size allows for connections with teachers, a strong community on campus, and opportunities that are hard to come across otherwise.
The students here are genuinely happy to be here. There is a strong community. The professors here also want you to succeed.

I am having the time of my life here at the University of San Diego! Not only is this campus filled with incredible academic opportunity and many fantastic individuals, but the city of San Diego as a whole has so much to do and see. I feel so lucky to go here.

USD is the best. Not only does every community emphasize a culture of kindness, the professors show in everything they do how much they care about each and every student. The small class sizes allow for very personalized relationships with professors and administration. The campus is the most beautiful in the nation! The school does an amazing job encouraging study abroad programs and finding ways for students to overcome obstacles to go abroad. The engineering program is super versatile and hands-on. The school's Catholic identity leads everything that we do and speaks to the love and welcomeness that centralizes campus.

I love the small campus size -- many ways to find tight-knit communities on campus and know your professors and peers on a deeper level. The location is great. There are numerous professional and personal opportunities to grow and develop as an individual.
The study abroad program is really what makes USD special. I’ve had great classes and professors as well.

USD is an amazing place for people who prioritize the quality of their environment in order to thrive academically. The people are welcoming and kind, and while there is a good balance of religious students vs. non-religious students, it has created a climate where people want to come together and serve the local community. I have personally never been happier and this school’s tradition of the liberal arts has transformed me completely. It has taught me how to become the kind of person I would look up to and to be immensely grateful for the opportunities that life has brought me. I am much more confident in my worth and ability to understand others. It has challenged my assumptions about the world and has asked me to introspect on my beliefs and attitudes. Although it might not be a perfect match for everyone, USD changed me for the better and I could not be more grateful to be here.

The University of San Diego has a wonderful campus and is situated in the heart of San Diego, which is truly a beautiful area. The university really cares about student well-being and provides many wellness resources to students. The food is very tasty.

USD is the greatest choice I have made in my life. There is something for everyone, and someone for everyone. Professors remember your name, and that's not just for the semester: my freshman year professors still remember me and check in, three years later. Everyone here is intentional with their work and their choices, and have fun while doing it. The academics are rigorous and rewarding, and the facilities are amazing. In a place where the President of the school will sometimes walk with you to class, you never forget that there is always someone looking out for you.
USD has great academics with devoted professors, our lawns are always filled with happy students doing what they love to do (skateboard, surf, talk, etc.)
I love it. I chose this school because I enjoy having smaller classes. I am able to connect more with them and their career opportunities are great.

I really enjoy USD's small class sizes. I have been able to form a connection with many of my teachers. There are always events on campus and the food options are great. Also the campus is very beautiful!

My experience has been great overall! I've met a lot of great people, and have interacted with great professors who are always willing to help and teach. The campus is beautiful and it's so nice to be near the beach.

ai_summary: The University of San Francisco (USF) offers a unique blend of opportunities thanks to its vibrant location in the heart of San Francisco. Students appreciate the small class sizes and approachable professors, which foster a close-knit community and personalized learning experience . The campus's accessibility to city life presents countless extracurricular and professional opportunities, enhancing the overall college experience. However, tuition costs are a significant concern for many students, often cited as high and only partially offset by financial aid. Feedback on the food quality and campus spirit indicates room for improvement in those areas. Overall, USF stands out for its dedication to social justice and diversity, and it is particularly well-regarded among nursing students, although some feel the school's focus on athletics and costs should be better balanced with academic support and resources.

reviews.text: I made lifelong friends at USF. I met amazing Black and Brown professors that had impacted my life and shaped me into the woman I am today. I love the city of San Francisco it holds a special place in my heart.

Overall, my experience with the University of San Francisco has been positive and meaningful. One of the things I appreciated most was the strong sense of community. Students and faculty genuinely care about social justice, service, and making an impact, which creates an environment where people feel supported and motivated. The campus itself is beautiful, and being in the heart of San Francisco adds so much energy and opportunity—whether it’s internships, cultural events, or simply exploring the city.
Overall great experience. I was a transfer student and so I had a different experience than others may have had.

My experience with the University of San Francisco has been overall really good. With a small school, you receive more helo from counselors and professors. The school has a huge psychology program, with classes that are based in diversity to reflect the student body. The campus is also in the heart of San Franscico and allows students to explore using public transit.

The university of San Francisco is a welcoming place and has a lot of nice people and staff. It has everything needed for whatever you want to be and a wide arrange of food. It is also in walking distance of stores you may need to visit and many bus stops. Overall I recommend it and it’s a super welcoming place
As a commuter, the academics feel easy and doable. However, if note that most clubs and events are held at unreasonable times for commuters.
I love it, the campus is so pretty, the people are so nice. I love the weather. I like how the classes are small and it's easy to talk to professors.

Strong emphasis on student wellbeing and belonging.
High tuition and limited funding; more aid needed.
The University of San Francisco is a good campus if you want to major in nursing. However, be ready for a very challenging experience.

The campus is gorgeous, boasting stunning views and exquisite architecture. The academics are rigorous and very demanding. I am a neuroscience and pre-health major and would have a midterm every two weeks in 3 of my classes alongside several assignments. I learned a lot; however, it was incredibly stressful. I lived in dorms, and they were very unkempt. The bathrooms looked disgusting at times, and I actually had an incident where tiles from the ceiling almost fell on my head in the bathrooms. The food was alright, however repetitive.

My major tends to be a bit strict since I’m am taking a architecture but the school is nice overall almost all my professors are very understanding when I dealt with complicated situations and they help me with improving my grades as well with opportunities (which is always nice). Only thing is that the school is expensive it almost feels downright unaffordable, because I live in a low income household and I don’t really have a job per se (taking 18 credits currently.) it feels incredibly stressful to find routes of work around of getting funds for your school.

University of San Francisco was an amazing experience for me. The major I was in, Kinesiology, was a small program. I was fortunate to have my professors several times, which led me to build amazing relationships with them. They were all fantastic people who were ready and willing to help if I was ever confused on a topic. Plus living in the city of SF was a great experience were I could meet people from different backgrounds, explore the city, and try foods all the cultures there had to offer. I highly recommend visiting USF to see if they're the school for you!
It’s been a great experience for me it’s a very lively place there is always something to do and work on completely depends on you San Francisco is really beautiful to be honest.

Overall, my experience here at University of San Francisco has been different but a good different. I have met a lot of people who have different personalities and have different life stories, but every story has impacted who I am today, and I love all my friends here. Sometimes life can be hard because although college is fun and is a great opportunity, money is something I struggle with because I have a windowed mom and 5 other siblings back at home. But I'm happy that I'm surrounded with so much love!
If social justice is your thing this university is your place! They also will help you with internships.

ai_summary: At the University of South Alabama, students generally appreciate the beautiful campus , supportive professors , and the close-knit community feel. Many reviews highlight the engaging academic programs , particularly in nursing, engineering, and business, with faculty often described as dedicated and approachable . However, experiences can vary significantly depending on the department, with some students noting less engaged professors in certain areas. Student life offers various activities, but some feel that a more vibrant campus atmosphere and dining options could enhance the experience. The campus's layout is praised for being easy to navigate, although parking has been a common concern. Overall, students feel that USA offers a solid educational experience with ample opportunities for involvement and personal growth, despite some acknowledged areas for improvement.

reviews.text: I like how young it is compared to other schools it’s better in tech and opportunities. The professors are nice and many scholarship opportunities within the school. I would like to see improvement in housing so just better looking and functioning dorms depending on which one you choose and maybe more scholarship money.
Great school with very good professors. Campus is easy to get around. Police department is friendly and helpful.

Honestly, I wasn't prepared to be very involved since I commute to school; however, South Alabama has led me to be involved regardless of whether or not I stay on Campus. I have had so much fun and already have listened to some great speakers that have come to the school.
I graduated from South with a finance degree and still keep up with some of my professors. My degree jump started my career.

Im part of the Jaguar Marching Band, On the Colorguard. It has brought me more school spirit and seeing all the Jaguar Fans.
The University has so many Opportunities and everyone is very friendly.
For an online course, it was very easy to keep up with and stay in contact with my professor. Any questions I had I was able to get help and guidance.

So far my experience at South Alabama has been nothing but great! The president of the campus Jo Bonner is such a honest man and truly loves what he does and you can see that in everything he does and provides for the campus. I wish that the dinning hall was consistently good but for the most part it’s good and they have lots of options to eat. Campus is very easy to navigate and everyone is truly here to help you succeed in all your classes. With the free tutoring it can help anyone and everyone with that extra push they may need to get that grade they want on a test or exam. Looking forward to spending my next years of college here at South Alabama and i’m looking forward to be more involved around campus.

I love how easy it is to connect with other students and professors . You can get a long way buy just speaking with others. The only thing I would like to see change is all of the sand on the walkways.

The campus is well laid out and easy to get around. I like that it is not so big that it is overly crowded but yet enough people that it is fun and lively. It has everything I need and I feel comfortable there. The only challenge would be finding a parking spot, but I have adjusted the time I arrive to plan for when other students are leaving. This seemed to help. I also love the cost of the parking permit which only around ten dollars. The college I transferred from was hundreds! Overall I love attending the University of South Alabama.

The University of South Alabama overall has been a blessing. The food in the dining hall is very well put together. the friendliness on campus is top tier. The only issue I have is parking but it’s maintainable .

At The University of South Alabama, I have gotten the chance to create so many great friendships and bonds with people I never knew I'd be this close with. I have gotten the chance to join organizations and learn more about the diverse culture at South Alabama, as well as learn more about my school's history. I have grown to love my professors as they try their best to help me succeed, even through the large workload, I have endured great bonds with them. Overall, The University is a great school and a nice home to those of the south, even residents who aren't from here. Go Jags!
overall experience is a 10 out of 10 definitely would recommend this school to friends and family who are wanting to attend a 4 yr college

Move in was super smooth and so much welcome! Everyone is friendly and there for you through whatever. I am a freshman that just moved in and its already clicking for me. I’m so happy to say I go to USA.

My University of South Alabama experience was outstanding. My professors were caring. If I had questions or needed extra help they were always willing to help. During my time there we had amazing basketball and baseball teams. I came to South from a small town in Florida. There were plenty of opportunities to make friends. I would highly recommend the University of South Alabama to anyone looking for a great overall college experience. Go Jags!

I love that south is a diverse school with an unbelievable amount of career options. I, myself, am either doing nursing, or am going to pursue Biomedical Sciences in the future. I love the food court and the liveliness of the students around. I haven’t really met unkind people on campus, personally. I would absolutely like to see a change in the elevators, although I myself am not disabled. It’s disheartening to see elevators stay down so frequently. Other than that, It’s a beautiful school! I absolutely plan to return for my M.D. if I do intend to pursue it later on.

ai_summary: At the University of South Carolina, students appreciate the supportive and engaging academic environment , with many praising their professors as approachable and dedicated. The campus is often described as beautiful and vibrant, offering a multitude of extracurricular activities that help students connect and create friendships. However, some students feel that the strong Greek life and party culture can overshadow academics, making it challenging for those not interested in that scene to make friends. The resources available for first-generation students and various academic programs, including the highly regarded Darla Moore School of Business , are highlighted as significant strengths. While the campus life is lively, with great sports events and social opportunities, there are concerns about housing shortages for upperclassmen , limited parking, and rising living costs in Columbia . Overall, USC offers a blend of academic rigor and vibrant student life that suits a wide range of interests, but the experience can differ significantly based on personal involvement and social preferences.

reviews.text:
Overall, my experience at the University of South Carolina has been really positive. The campus environment is welcoming, and there are a lot of opportunities to get involved both academically and socially. I’ve had professors who are supportive and genuinely want to see students succeed, which has made a big difference in my experience. I also appreciate the resources available, like tutoring and academic advising, which have helped me stay on track and maintain a strong GPA.
One thing I would like to see improved is accessibility to certain opportunities and resources, especially when it comes to affordability and awareness. Sometimes it can be difficult to find or take full advantage of certain programs due to financial limitations or lack of clear information. Overall, though, USC has provided me with a strong foundation, and I feel like it’s a place where I can continue to grow academically and personally.

I am a freshman at the University of South Carolina and I have had a favorable experience so far. I am a sport management major and the academics in this field are excellent. I have had good support from my professors. I have also had a great campus experience. There are numerous food options available and the meal plans are sufficient. There are a lot of options for campus involvement and there are plenty of sporting events to attend. Columbia is a decent sized city, but it is very difficult to get around without a vehicle. There are also times that it does not feel entirely safe, but there are plenty of campus police. Overall, I would rate my experience as very good.

Academics weren't taken as seriously as I would have liked. I decided to transfer after one year. Housing on campus is terrible- full of mold, roaches, and needing updates. I developed food poisoning several times from poorly cooked foods. Surrounding area is not safe walking alone at night.
It has been a great experience so far. I am currently a sophomore majoring in Finance at the Darla Moore School of Business, and I feel as if I have grown as a professional and an individual.

My overall experience with University of South Carolina has been very positive because it offers strong academic opportunities and a supportive learning environment. I especially appreciate the resources available to students, such as academic advising, career support, and opportunities to get involved in extracurricular and professional development activities. The campus environment also encourages students to stay motivated and focused on their future goals.
One thing I would like to see continue to improve is even more support for students pursuing healthcare careers, such as additional shadowing opportunities, networking events, or workshops focused on graduate school preparation. Overall, my experience has helped me grow academically and professionally, and I feel it has helped prepare me for my future career path.

I like that so much stuff is walkable around campus. There are always fun events on Greene St, or even Soda City. A lot of people are super friendly, but the campus is not very diverse. The meal plan swipes are very limiting so if you stay on campus I suggest getting the meal plan dollars that you can spend anywhere and as much as you want. It will force you to be more financially responsible as it's a set amount of money you stretch until the end of the semester. I like that there is a shuttle system because some buildings are just so far that you literally won't make it to your next class if you tried. As a psych major it's really nice that all the buildings you frequent are somewhat in the same area which is convenient.

It was an interesting experience. I have never lived in the south part of America so it was interesting to see the different environment. There were a lot of people coming from different states so it was comforting to know I wasn’t the only one. I did find a place in the school. a community.

My experience at the University of South Carolina has been a mix of growth, challenges, and learning about myself. As a black student, coming to a large university like this can feel a little overwhelming at first. There are moments where you notice that you're one of the few people who look like you in certain classes or spaces, and that can take some getting used to. At the same time, it also pushed me to find communities and organizations where I feel supported and understood. One of the things I appreciate most is the opportunity to connect with the other black students through cultural organizations, student groups, and campus events. Spaces like those make a big difference because they create a sense of belonging and allow people to share similar experiences. I've had professors who genuinely care about their students and make an effort to create inclusive classrooms where different perspectives. I've learned how to speak up and find my community.
I loved all of the professors I have had so far in the Biology department. There are many resources on campus for everything you can think of.

It took a moment to get adjusted to UofSC, but once I did I fell in love with my school. There are a lot of places to meet people of similar interests and many faculty and staff that are dedicated to seeing their students succeed.

I loved the University of South Carolina, I was a transfer student so did had experience at a small technical college. I was nervous about coming into such a big school however, the campus life and professors made things personal so it didn't feel quite that large.

I enjoyed all of the constant opportunities you can participate in as well as attending different sporting events. It's also nice that there are 2 gyms since so many students go here. Plus, it is a relatively close walk from campus to downtown Cola. I would like to see some better food options throughout campus though.

USC has ample resources for students, especially freshmen. If students were just willing to reach out, there would always be someone or some people who are willing to help them succeed. There are resources like Student Success Center that help students with classes, money management, and coaching for building good habits; the Career Center, which helps students with building their resumes and work experience; a minimum of three career fairs every semester with companies that are actually interested in talking to students (and potentially get internships or jobs), and so much more.

Being at the University of South Carolina, I have truly been able to experience life while balancing my school work. All of my professors do their best to help me when I need it, and there are so many other helpful resources throughout campus that are free to all students. I absolutely love the atmosphere here. There is always something to do or going on that is on campus. Dorm life isnt the worst thing, and the RAs try to make it fun. The only thing I would change is the parking situation. For a school with 40,000 students, there aren't many places to park on campus.
I love UofSc. I think the schools is amazing, the school spirit is phenomenal, the professors are great, and the students are great too!

ai_summary: At the University of South Dakota, many students appreciate the welcoming atmosphere and supportive faculty , often noting the small class sizes that foster close connections with professors. The academics are generally well-regarded, especially in fields like education, health sciences, and business, with a strong emphasis on personal attention in courses. Student life offers a variety of clubs and activities , although the campus environment can feel limiting due to its small-town location, leading some to feel that opportunities for social engagement are somewhat lacking outside of Greek life and sports. While the campus is praised for its beauty and accessibility, there are concerns about campus facilities, such as dorm quality and food options, and some students express dissatisfaction with the administration's handling of housing and financial aid. Overall, many students feel a strong sense of community at USD, viewing it as a good value for the education received, though there are notable areas for improvement.

reviews.text: The school as an institution is good. It supports strong academics, has solid professors, and is worthwhile if you’re simply a student. However, when it came to the social atmosphere, it can difficult. If you’re not part of the crowd, people can alienate you. It’s probably not even purposeful. People are just bad sometimes at accepting that others are not like them and aren’t obligated to conform to them. It’s kind of sad. Otherwise, a good school…but for my well-rounded sanity, it would have worked for everyone if I had transferred to another school when I had the chance.

There are good and bad things about USD. If you like small town living, you will likely enjoy USD. There isn't a lot to do in the town so it can get boring. The college has kind of a smaller feel so you are able to build closer relationships with your professors which is always nice.

My experience at USD has been pretty good. I really have enjoyed the classes and think that the campus is great. There is a wide range of food to choose from whenever you are hungry and always something to do. I have found it easy to meet new people there which has made it a good experience for me.
I love it here. I am involved in greek life and a club sport. There are so many opportunities here to grow. Only thing I would like to change is more affordable options that are like coyote housing.

I feel that this school is only made for a specific type of person. One who grew up in the mid-west in an extremely small town that wanted something similar to that. As someone who enjoys lots of time outdoors, this area was very difficult as it's very bland outside. The town is extremely small so there are few options on things to do. Often times people go to the bars because that makes up 90% of the businesses here. That is where many make friends but as I have little money, and have a hard time with underaged drinking, I don't go there. The community is very small and judgy for anyone that doesn't dress and act the same way as them. That is why I'm transferring because its not enjoyable for anyone like me. Many who also go here went because they are close to home, but as I am far from my home it's very difficult to find friends when everyone leaves over the weekend or goes to drink. I would never recommend this school to anyone out of state or from the Black Hills.

The University is great for people that aren’t fans of big cities and is a great location between Sioux Falls and Sioux City. Everyone can find friends and get to know professors with how small some class sizes can be.

I love USD. The campus feels like home and I don't just feel like a number. I am close with my professors and my classmates. We bond over classes and other extracurricular activities that is provided for us on campus. I feel safe and comfortable here at USD.

I think the campus and people here overall are really welcoming. The buildings are very up to date and sleek. The food options are incredible and the sheer number of options makes it easy to not get bored. The professors are very invested in their classes and seem to actually care.
USD is a very nice campus with great student life. I loved my first year as a student there and am very excited for the coming fall.

I really love my school here in South Dakota. I find the campus easy to get around and I am enjoying my classes. I wish there was more affordable housing in the area as some of the more affordable places aren’t very desirable.
It’s not bad school but it needs to be more supportive and willing to help their students. Overall, it could improve in various ways like better food, better activities, more professors, etc.

Overall I love USD. I am a transfer student and I immediantely felt welcome on this campus. I love all of my professors, and I love how many ways there are to get involved. There is a place for everyoen to get connected and feel at home.
I have been extremely satisfied with my experience here at USD! I would strongly recommend attending here, especially if you have interest in the arts programs!

Love it, just don’t have the resources I need to be successful. I’d love to have the chance to go to vermillion or augustana in Sioux Falls, so I can make friends, have the resources I need, and have the chance to be free

I have has a great experience with the University of South Dakota, I plan to be returning to campus next year, I like to go to the gym and workout and walk around. I like that they have convenient stores on campus to get a little treat. I would like to see the cleanliness change. The bathrooms get very dirty and it makes it feel gross to walk in there.

ai_summary: The University of South Florida (USF) receives praise for its supportive faculty and diverse academic offerings , making it a solid choice for many students. Reviewers appreciate the helpfulness of professors and academic advisors, often noting that they genuinely care about student success. The campus is described as beautiful and vibrant , with plenty of opportunities for social engagement, clubs, and activities, contributing to a sense of community . However, some students mention challenges with parking and advising processes, alongside mixed experiences regarding campus life and food options. Overall, USF stands out for its combination of strong academics , a lively campus atmosphere , and a commitment to student services, although some aspects, like navigating administrative matters, could be improved.

reviews.text: Great experience obtaining my bachelor degree. The campus is beautiful and always felt safe. Lived on campus and enjoyed the dorms as they are newer. Skip the meal plans. Parking an issue especially the first few weeks of classes. Area around campus used to be shady but its much better now. GO BULLS!

The University of South Florida’s online paralegal program offers an impressive balance of academic rigor and flexibility. The curriculum is comprehensive, providing a solid foundation in legal research, ethics, and procedural law that feels immediately applicable to the field.
The digital interface is intuitive, making it easy to engage with faculty and peers. However, I would love to see more interactive, real-time networking opportunities or virtual career workshops specifically for distance learners. Overall, it is a high-quality program that effectively prepares students for the transition from the classroom to a professional legal environment.

Overall, I am having a great time at the University of South Florida! The academics are above average, as well as the campus life. This provides a nice, comfortable balance between school and social life. There are plenty of opportunities to participate in many clubs on campus, due to the bull market every Wednesday. This is an organized event in which clubs can advertise themselves, allowing students to ask question pertaining to their interests. The food quality varies, however there are plenty of option besides the dining halls where you can grab a bite to eat. If there was one thing I would like to see change in, it would be the inclusion of Greek life on campus. This change of involvement with Greek life would allow more students to participate in Greek life, which in turn would allow for a betterment throughout the student body.
All of the funding goes to sports and the honors college, no funding for the arts or even the library

Overall, my experience at the University of South Florida has been positive. I like that the school offers many opportunities for students to grow academically and professionally. The professors I’ve had are knowledgeable and willing to help if you reach out. I also like that USF has a strong focus on technology and real-world skills, which is important for students studying information security and computer science. The campus has many resources available, such as tutoring, career services, and student organizations that help students prepare for their future careers.
One thing I would like to see improve is communication and organization in some courses. Sometimes assignment instructions or deadlines can feel unclear, making it harder for students to stay on track. I would also like to see more hands-on labs and practical experiences in technical classes so students can apply what they learn more directly.
I love the campus and the diversity at the school. What I would like to see change is the amount of single dorms there are available to students.

In my experience, USF is a great school and has been great to work with when it comes to academics. The process to reach out to advisors and professors is fairly simple and I have never felt like I was on my own to figure something out.
Beautiful campus. Some older classrooms, but still comfortable. Love the library - easy access from everywhere on campus and a solid collection.
I really enjoyed the sailing team and the community there. I thought that the school really values the athletic programs and pushes students to strive outside of the classroom.

USF is a great school and one of the few schools in the state of Florida that has my major (American Sign Language Interpreting) as a four year program. I enjoy how passionate and involved many of the students and staff are here.
Amazing! I loved campus involvement! The professors are awesome and the online classes are very easy! Highly recommend this university if you want to get invilved
I would commute about 30-40minutes from home and attend classes at the Tampa campus. The campus is nice and huge and I learned a lot from my classes.
Good school, generally good profs. Located in north Tampa it's away from the city, very convenient if you grew up around there.

As an older student going back to school to complete my bachelor's in 2024, I felt included. The wide variety of classes there was to choose from was amazing. I did have some "issues" with having to complete some higher level general education classes. I did not love how not clear this expectation was initially, but once I found a great advisor I was set. He was wonderful and easy to schedule with. He helped me understand what was needed and help me navigate it. Once the slight boring classes were out of the way I could focus on the classes I was eager to take! I did commute to campus, so I do not know anything about housing/ party/ student life.
I haven’t started my enrollment at USF yet, but I’m looking forward to starting in the fall. I’ve heard so many good things about it and I can’t wait to start the college life.

ai_summary: At the University of Southern California, students consistently highlight the supportive and engaging academic environment . Many appreciate the professors, who are described as knowledgeable and accessible, as well as the resources available for career preparation and networking, which are seen as significant advantages . Student life is vibrant, with a strong sense of community fostered by diverse extracurricular activities and spirited events, especially during sports seasons. However, concerns about the cost of tuition and ongoing support for low-income students do emerge. The campus itself, located in Los Angeles, receives high praise for its beauty and safety , contributing to a lively and welcoming atmosphere. Overall, USC stands out for its blend of rigorous academics, an active social scene, and ample opportunities for personal and professional growth.

reviews.text: I liked the networking opportunities... their strong alumni network and high-quality programs that prepare students for future careers. I also loved the Work Hard, Play Hard" culture: students like me often feel challenged academically while also enjoying a fun, active social life. What i would like to see change is probably nothing other than the actual cost of attending USC. It's really nice!

Honestly, I transferred into USC in my sophomore year from the University of California, Irvine. While both in California, they could not be more different. I dropped pledge ship from Greek Life, and honestly feel quite isolated from a lot of the fun that is part of USC's reputation. It was simply too expensive for me as a student to pay out of pocket. I like the campus and I think amenities are good (not the food though) but just wish I felt a bit more integrated.
I live around the area , visited the campus multiple times . Out side the campus it is filled with homeless and addicts .

I really enjoyed the opportunities and people I met at USC; I think it's a great place to try a whole variety of subjects and meet a diverse array of perspectives and people. At the same time, USC's size also means that a student has to constantly advocate for themselves and be outgoing to seize all the possible opportunities, the institution essentially throws you in the deep end in figuring out how to do everything from network to even attending the right classes for your degree.

I enjoyed my time at USC and overall felt academically supported. There were many opportunities and resources to discover more of myself, what career may be right for me, and build meaningful connections with my peers.

USC is a great university with a endless options to study and find your interest. Most of the professors are kind and willing to take time to help all students navigate all courses. I recommend this university to all who seek a diverse course load that will challenge you .

USC is a wonderful university. As a Trojan, I appreciated the various learning opportunities and resources provided by the university. One of the perks of the university is that it has great study and food options for students to benefit from, especially during long days. It also has great study spots such as Leavey Library on their UPC campus and Norris Library on their HSC campus. The bookstore is also amazing and a wonderful place to buy USC merchandise, especially as a Trojan. The university has multiple campuses, which add more to the experience, especially if you use the intercampus shuttle to commute between the UPC and HSC campuses. USC is the place to be.

Overall, I really enjoy the campus life and the social aspects of going to USC. However, I think USC can do better when it comes to guidance in regards to course planning and academics. Sometimes, the guidelines aren't fully clear.
I enjoyed the campus activities, connected with great minds and I had a good friendly research assistant
Good student life. But the university only cares about money. Way too expensive and network will be helpful in california
USC has been the best undergrad experience I could of asked for. From great professors and a wonderful education, to networking and good student life. USC is a work hard party hard school
Live right next to the college and see a lot of dangerous things happen, know people who’ve gone and say the education and students are great. Only problem is safety and housing.

As a student pursuing two degrees in two different schools within USC, I had the opportunity to take classes related to both cinema and English. Overall, I throughly enjoyed these classes and found myself looking forward to attending lectures consistently across the three years I spent at the university. For 2.5 years, I lived in on-campus housing and had little to no issues with it and easy-access to both campus and the village, and I met a few of my closest friends through my classes. The only issues I had with my undergraduate experience were the costs and frequent trouble with communicating with some of the advisors, as well as errors made by the school with my loans and the grace period after I had graduated. Still, it was an experience I will truly never forget and one that I could not have gotten anywhere else, and for that I will always be thankful and continue to encourage those who haven't pursued higher education to do so.
I've definitely loved my time at USC. I've made lifelong friends, and still stay in touch with some of my professors after the semester has ended.
I am a student from China and I highly recommend USC for Chinese people. USC is a university which can provide lots of oppotunities of jobs.

ai_summary: At the University of Southern Mississippi, students appreciate the welcoming and tight-knit community atmosphere, often describing the campus as feeling like a home away from home. The small to medium class sizes allow for meaningful interactions with professors who are noted for their dedication and support in helping students succeed academically. There are numerous opportunities for student involvement through clubs, events, and activities that cater to diverse interests, promoting a vibrant student life . While the campus is praised for its beauty and safety, some students mention the parking situation and food variety as areas needing improvement. Overall, students find the university to be affordable with a strong focus on academics , but some express a desire for greater financial aid options. The environment strikes a balance between a supportive educational experience and an engaging social scene , making the University of Southern Mississippi a distinct choice for many.

reviews.text: My experience at the University of Southern Mississippi has been amazing so far! There are so many ways to be involved and meet people at free campus events, such as our annual Southern Miss Activities Council movie showings, food from around the world showcase, and annual spring festival. The classes are very personalized and close-knit, so you get to know your professor better than you would at an SEC school. There are so many dining options, and you can use meal exchanges at campus restaurants. There are also so many organizations to be a part of, like Eagle Connection tour guides, Student Government Association, Lambda Sigma Honor Society, and Student Alumni Association! Overall, I think Southern Miss is an amazing school and has a thriving campus life that really sets it apart from other universities.
Everyone is extremely nice and if you are confused about any aspect (or everything as I was last year), everyone I have ever talked to has helped me out immensely.

My experience at the University of Southern Mississippi has been pretty good. The professors in my major are very nice, supportive, and always willing to help. Whenever I have had a problem or needed clarification, they have responded quickly and made sure I understood what to do. Their support has made a significant difference on my academic success. The housing on campus could use improvement, especially in terms of maintenance and overall comfort. Some buildings feel outdated, and certain facilities would benefit from upgrades. One thing I really enjoy about USM is the amount of activity on campus. There is always something going on, whether it is student events, club activities, or recreational opportunities. Greek life is also strong and provides many opportunities to get involved, meet new people, and build a sense of community. Overall, even though there are areas that could improve, my time at USM has been enjoyable.

My overall experience at USM Hattiesburg Ms. was awesome. I would describe the campus as welcoming and diverse, with a tight-knit community that balances academic support with social involvement. USM was a journey. It was like home away from home, Atmosphere that blends southern charm with spirited traditions. Through the semesters of professors, life was happening. The university supported and provided services along the way. Every educator, instructor, staff and fellow students would come together to participate in community engagements. It was a great experience and opportunity for me to share with my family. Southern Miss, "To the Top"!!! It is our call to action when we approach a fellow classman.
My experience at USM has been positive overall, but my main complaint refers to the professors that the university as some of them are just blatantly bad at their jobs.
I genueinly enjoy it here, originally I just chose this school because it was close to home but the more time I've spent here I don't really think anywhere else would be for me.

I believe USM is a great place to attend for your undergraduate degree. Hattiesburg is a progressive city for being in Mississippi and the people are kind. USM professors make you feel welcomed and most of them are very kind and supportive. I have had a positive experience with the school as a whole but mostly within the program I am involved with.

I transferred to the University of Southern Mississippi after I did my first two years at Pearl River Community College. I am a Sociology major, and all of my classes are fully online; the sociology program has many useful classes that I believe will help me succeed in my career after I graduate. They are very accommodating of my needs as an online student and provide a wide range of services and support to help students succeed both academically and emotionally. The professors are understanding and do a great job of teaching the class content. I definitely recommend USM to prospective students.

USM is a really diverse campus and has a lot of support for undergraduate students. However, there is not a lot of resources for graduate students (i.e., financial, community, tutoring, etc). There is also a high faculty turnover rate and a lot of faculty leave after a few year of working here. This is pretty concerning, considering graduate students work directly with faculty. Overall, there are a lot of systemic issues that the university has not addressed since my time here.

I love attending the University of Southern Mississippi so far because it offers a strong blend of open arms, hospitality, and beautiful surroundings. The supportive faculty and smaller class sizes promise opportunities for mentorship and individualized attention. USM’s cultural diversity and rich arts and music programs would let me explore multiple interests. The campus’s proximity to museums and the zoo brings both natural beauty and chances for outdoor adventure. Finally, I believe the university’s traditions and warm, friendly atmosphere would help me grow socially, intellectually, and personally.

My experience at the University of Southern Mississippi has been positive and memorable. I’ve enjoyed the welcoming campus atmosphere, supportive professors, and opportunities for involvement in student organizations. The academic programs are well-rounded and offer real-world applications that helped me grow personally and professionally.
However, I believe there’s room for improvement in streamlining administrative services like advising and financial aid. Clearer communication and more user-friendly systems would benefit students.
Overall, USM has provided me with a solid education, valuable connections, and a sense of community. I’m proud to be part of the Golden Eagle family.

As a freshmen living on campus, the beginning of my college experience at the University of Southern Mississippi has been great. There are many ways to get involved on campus and abundance of activities. The only major complaint is parking. There is not enough parking for resident students.
It has been good so far. The staff has been very kind and helpful in getting me enrolled and acclimated.

I’ve attended the University of Southern Mississippi since 2022 as a transfer student, and my experience has been mixed. I lived on campus for a year; Central Park dorms are amazing, but older dorms need major improvements—especially for upperclassmen, who often choose student apartments instead. My professors have been great, and in my program, we typically have the same ones until graduation, allowing them to know each student well. The cafeteria was nice, with regularly changing food options. My biggest issue has been my advisor, who often takes 1–2 months to respond to calls or emails. If students are required to reply within 72 hours, advisors should be held to the same standard.
I absolutely love southern miss. this campus and the love surrounding it is impeccable. I wish i would have gone here all four years

Im am not proud of graduating from here. Worst decision I ever made. If I could go back I would have chosen a trade school or just gotten a certificate at a community college. Didn't find a job the scholarships aren't really enough, not worth the price of admission. Theres jobs that require a high-school diploma that have gotten me better pay. Did not find a job with my degree, I feel like I was gaslighted about my career opportunities here. I am very dissapointed in the education I received and this institution as a whole.

Did not find a job after college, the experience here was horrible. This school is very expensive I wish I wouldn't have wasted all my aid on this school. It was an abominably horrendous return on investment. I would think twice.

I had a wonderful experience during my four years while obtaining my bachelors. They set you up for success with their process of practicing interviews, gaining field experience, and working on your resume- ensuring that upon graduation, you will have the career of your dreams!

ai_summary: The University of St. Thomas - Minnesota offers a unique blend of personalized education and a vibrant community , featuring small class sizes that foster close student-professor relationships. Many students appreciate the supportive faculty and the variety of academic programs, particularly in business and the sciences, though experiences with professors can be inconsistent. The campus is highlighted for its beauty and accessibility , although some students express concerns about the high tuition and living costs. Student life is active, but there are mixed feelings about community inclusivity and post-weekend engagement, with some students noting the campus can feel empty on weekends. The university's commitment to diversity is acknowledged, but many reviews indicate room for improvement in both representation and campus culture surrounding inclusivity. Overall, while many find a strong sense of belonging and opportunity at St. Thomas, concerns regarding cost and a somewhat homogenous student body are common themes.

reviews.text: I am not yet a student at University of St. Thomas but what I have seen in my visits and tours have led me to believe that it is a great school.

My experience at the University of St. Thomas has been super positive! I’ve lots great friends and felt welcomed into their community. Welcome week was the best time to get to know everyone! There are always fun events happening, which makes it easy to stay involved. The professors are helpful, understanding, and care about your well-being.

Life at UST was a truly rewarding experience that helped shape who I am today. Surrounded by a supportive campus community, I had the chance to learn from dedicated professors, build lasting friendships, and explore new ideas both inside and outside the classroom. The balance of academic challenges and personal growth taught me resilience, curiosity, and confidence. Whether it was late‑night study sessions, joining student organizations, or discovering new interests, every moment contributed to a sense of belonging and purpose. Looking back, college wasn’t just about earning a degree—it was about discovering my strengths, expanding my perspective, and creating memories that will stay with me for a lifetime.

I am a current Freshman at the University of St. Thomas. I have begun my second semester, and I have felt welcomed from the moment I came here. Moving from Arizona to Minnesota for school was a big change, I came here without knowing a single person while the majority of students here already had established friend groups. The change was a bit nerve-wracking but because St. Thomas offers many different opportunities to meet people, I soon met some good people, and I have had great friends since. As an engineering student, the resources offered by St. Thomas are incredible, from the woodshop to the 3-D printing lab the opportunities are endless. I even joined the Rugby team here and have met yet another community that strives for greatness and pushes each other to do their best. My experience has been great, and I hope things continue to work well for me both in and outside the classroom.

I am currently a college sophomore at St. Thomas. Coming into my freshman year, I was honestly really nervous because college was just a completely new experience. I was also a little nervous about the diversity. I am an African American woman, and I had heard from multiple peers that St. Thomas severely lacks diversity and is predominantly white. I can say that after being at this university for about two years, that is definitely true. There is a serious lack of minorities within the student body. In this political climate, there have been times where it feels unsafe to be walk around without being looked at and feeling uncomfortable. However, I have found my community and who I feel comfortable around. Education wise, many of the teachers are willing to help and I’ve learned a lot of information since coming to the school!

It is a very vibrant school. There is so much to do, and the housing is very nice. It's way bigger than others. It's also furnished and modernised. also the food is really good. This school is really lively and fun. There’s so much to do, with lots of clubs and activities to join. The housing is great, it's much bigger than at other schools. The actual school buildings are furnished and modern. The food is also really good, with plenty of options. Overall, it's a fun atmosphere, has good living spaces, and great food.

I really like my experience since the day I applied every one has been really nice and helpful. I will say however that it is very dependent on you scheduling your own classes and making sure that you enroll in time to take your required classes. You have to be prepared otherwise it can be hard to get your desired schedule.

I really love and appreciate just about everything at the University of St Thomas. The support, professors, small class sizes, campus are all wonderful and exactly what I wanted. The only thing I don't love is it's in the heart of the city. If it had a more rural location it would literally be perfect.

A very welcoming community, and a lot of student clubs and activities. The professors are great and friendly. The school has every resources a student might need, and one would succeed by making the most of it.

The University of St. Thomas is a great college for those who like a contained campus, with smaller class sizes, and more direct opportunities. Great academics with high regard to attendance and participation, great faculty:student ratio. Campus life is ever growing with the new Lee and Penny Anderson Arena and the D1 athletics. Values are consistent and academics feel personalized.

Campus has allowed me to connect with many students and professors within my first year! I have told everyone who is considering college to look into St.Thomas as they offer a wide variety of majors, minors and opportunities.

University of St. Thomas's nursing program sets their students up to fail. They make empty and false promises that all students with a 3.5 or above GPA will get into the program, but that is not the case. I have never witnessed and experienced such unprofessional behavior at a university, and all I can say to students looking into a nursing degree at St. Thomas is GET AS FAR AWAY FROM THERE AS YOU CAN. The ones that do get into tge program are more miserable than any other nursing program, even those that are more difficult. The instructors DO NOT care about the students and their well-being in any sort of way, and It is frustrating to see such an expensive and Catholic school not care about the people paying tens of thousands of dollars every year to them.

I was scared to go the UST because it was my last choice. I decided late and was scared to go. But when I saw the campus for the first time, I fell in love. It was such a nice campus and it was so pretty. It felt like home.

I have really enjoyed my time at St. Thomas. I’ve found a really great group of friends and the campus is beautiful. Academically, the quality I would say is dependent on the classes one takes, but the professors for the most part are all really passionate about their respective subjects. I would like to see a little more accommodation housing wise considering the growth of the university as well as a little more involvement with the fine arts ok the part of the university.

My experience at the University of St. Thomas in Minnesota has been really positive. The small class sizes and supportive professors make it easy to learn and get help when needed. There’s a strong sense of community, with plenty of clubs and events that help students connect, and the campus itself is beautiful and well-kept. I also appreciate the career opportunities that come from being close to the Twin Cities. However, tuition is expensive, and I’d like to see more diversity and inclusion across campus. Some classes fill up too quickly, parking can be difficult, and weekends can feel quiet. Overall, St. Thomas has great academics and a welcoming environment, and with improvements in affordability and inclusivity, it could be even better.

ai_summary: The University of St. Thomas - Texas is frequently described as a friendly , tight-knit community with a diverse student body, where many appreciate the small class sizes that foster personal relationships with professors. Academically, the university offers strong programs , particularly in nursing and pre-med, with professors generally noted for their enthusiasm and care for students’ success. Reviews highlight the affordability of tuition through various scholarships, though some students express concerns over rising costs and the financial aid process. In terms of campus life, while the supportive atmosphere is acknowledged, many find student life somewhat lacking in vibrancy, with calls for more events and diverse dining options. The location in Houston enhances the experience, offering access to a vibrant urban environment , although some students feel that the college experience could be more engaging.

reviews.text:
Here’s a cleaner, more polished version of your sentence added to your bio:
Diego Escorcia is currently attending the University of St. Thomas, pursuing a degree in Criminology with the goal of one day joining the FBI. The University of St. Thomas is a diverse school that offers many opportunities, and it has supported him in a variety of ways; however, he hopes to see more interactive experiences and engagement across campus. A dedicated and competitive athlete, he has played soccer and volleyball throughout his life, earning recognition as a 2x Defensive Player of the Year. He values discipline, teamwork, and hard work both on and off the field.
This school was definitely worth attending, however it is extremely small and has limited opportunities.

My experience at the University of St. Thomas has been shaped by its small, close‑knit campus and the sense of community that comes with it. The smaller class sizes have made a huge difference in how I learn. Instead of feeling like one student among hundreds, I feel seen. Professors know my name, they notice when I am improving or struggling, and they genuinely care about my goals. It is easy to ask questions, stay after class, or have real conversations that go beyond the syllabus. That kind of support has helped me grow both academically and personally.
Campus life is calm and welcoming, which makes it easy to focus while still feeling connected. You see familiar faces everywhere, and it creates an environment where people look out for one another. Whether it is studying in the library, hanging out on the lawn, or joining small student events, there is a sense of comfort that comes from being part of a community where everyone is within reach.
My experience at University of St. Thomas has been good. I like the small class sizes which helps to know other students.

My journey at the University of St. Thomas has been defined by an exceptional balance of academic rigor and personal support. The professors here are more than just educators; they are mentors who bridge the gap between theory and practice, whether through engaging in-person classes or highly accessible online platforms. This academic foundation is bolstered by a campus life that feels both vibrant and safe, providing a secure environment to pursue extracurricular activities that have honed my leadership skills. From the collaborative atmosphere of the classroom to the diverse community engagement opportunities, my time at St. Thomas has been a transformative investment in my future, equipping me with the tools and the confidence to succeed in the professional world.

UST is a Catholic centered college that brings Christ into the classroom in every class. There’s a chapel on campus, and it’s right in the heart of Houston! It’s a great place for nursing students that want to focus on school while still having a social life thanks to the campus community.

My overall experience at the University of St. Thomas has been positive. I really appreciate the small class sizes and the supportive, approachable professors who genuinely care about student success. The campus has a welcoming community and a calm learning environment. However, I would like to see more student activities and improvements in administrative efficiency and resources. Overall, it’s a great place for personalized education, with some room to grow in campus life and services.

St.Thomas has been great these past few years. I have bonded with professors and made many friends. Overall the experience and learning I have received here have been phenomenal, I am thankful for everything that my professors and staff have done for me. If I had to pick another college I would pick St.Thomas again.

It was good. I don't think I was challenged enough because I do not remember like anything I have learned. I think they need to focus more on us learning rather than just throwing information at us if that makes sense.

The Staff at University of St. Thomas are very supportive and result driven. students are assigned advisors who are always happy to assist or escalate issues to appropriate departments.
There is also the aspect of spirituality which strengthens faith in God, especially in these challenging times. International students are offered a seamless registration process and direction to navigate through immigration requirements.

This college is in the local area so many students are commuters. Therefore, their isn't that many people that stay for social events sometimes. Overall, academics are pretty well and teachers are friendly.

My experience with UST is a relatively good one. I have met many amazing friends there and the food is good too in my opinion. The only thing I would like to see changed is more affordable housing for those of us who cannot commute.

Great school with great connections. There are required core classes that are included for each degree plan but are interesting and a refreshing break from the STEM classes.
