There are often student events on campus that are fun. Class sizes are all small allowing for a better one-on-one experience with professors.

My experience with the University of St. Thomas – Texas has been welcoming, inspiring, and deeply encouraging. From the moment I connected with the admissions and advising teams, I felt supported and seen. The university’s values of driven approach and commitment to academic excellence truly stand out. I’ve appreciated the clear communication, accessible resources, and the genuine care shown by faculty and staff. The small class sizes and community-centered environment create a space where students can thrive. I’m excited to continue growing here and would love to see even more support systems in place for second-degree and nontraditional students like stay at home moms returning to school.

It’s been a good experience and I’m convinced it will continue to be a great school with vast resources. It is a “small” school, but that’s why I prefer it over others, because of the ability to actually be involved in your classes, the capabilities to succeed in all your tasks. It’s been and will be a great school, nothing much to change about it.

ai_summary: The University of Tennessee at Chattanooga (UTC) offers a balanced blend of academics and campus life, contributing to a generally positive experience for students. Many reviews highlight supportive and approachable professors who are genuinely invested in student success, fostering a welcoming environment. The campus is frequently described as beautiful and well-maintained, situated conveniently near downtown Chattanooga and offering numerous outdoor activities. Students appreciate the diversity of majors , extracurricular opportunities, and campus resources such as tutoring and counseling services. However, some common points of concern include high tuition and housing costs , along with issues related to financial aid transparency and limited weekend activities on campus. Overall, UTC strikes a good balance for those seeking a mid-sized university experience in an engaging city.

reviews.text: Chattanooga is a very unique city and very diverse I think many can find their passions and their belonging here.

My experience at the University of Tennessee at Chattanooga has been some of the best four years of my life. The campus cultivated such a strong sense of community and collaboration by constantly hosting fun events and promoting countless opportunities for students. I am forever grateful for my time there.

My experience at Chattanooga has overall been decent. The campus life is good, and there is always stuff to do around campus. The dining hall switches up the food every day, so you have variety. I believe that there is something for everyone at Chattanooga if the mountains and cold are the place for you.

UTC was a great school where I was able to obtain my teaching degree. The professors worked with me and helped me tremendously. They prepared me to pass the test and with a lot of great field experience in my education major.

My experience at the University of Tennessee at Chattanooga has been overall positive. The campus is beautiful and located in a great area that makes it easy to balance school and relaxation. While navigating financial aid and housing can sometimes be a challenge, the professors and the academic environment are strong. I appreciate the practical learning opportunities and the diverse campus community."
I met wonderful professors that inspired me and were willing to help me with any problems I had with school work.

I like that there's a community here. I feel like they really try to hype up clubs and organizations to help you find your place. They have lots of options for clubs -- there's something for everyone! Housing isn't the best. I feel like sometimes it's a scam. They don't really prioritize anyone, even if you were the first person in the school to apply for housing, there's still a high chance you won't get it. Aside from this it's not too bad.

I am a duel enrollment student here at UTC. The opportunity to study here as a college student while still being a high school senior is an amazing opportunity not many colleges have. The many events across campus, nice facilities, food, and overall feel make UTC a great place to call my school.

I love living here in Chattanooga. I’ve lived here since I was born. It’s a beautiful city, with a great beautiful landscape (especially in the fall season). I find it easier to find places to blow off steam and relax whenever I feel myself becoming stressed with schoolwork. Campus is very nice, safe, and beautiful. There are plenty of places to sit and study if need be. The only thing I’d change is the dorm located at Palmetto. I honestly don’t believe the dorm should be located where it is. I’ve heard from many people that’s their only downside when it comes to rooming. I feel like that takes a few stars away from the safety aspect, specifically for women, but other than that, no complaints.

UTC has been extremely beneficial, both in academics and my personal life. It has invited me to branch out and really excel, the professors often say, "you only get as far as you reach" and I think it is so true. My mindset even when it comes to simple day to day tasks has evolved after my time here. I am a current senior with 1.5 semesters left before graduation, and it feels bittersweet to say goodbye.
I enjoy the housing, and I have my own room. I am in a sorority and it is going great. I do not like the area however, because there is very high crime and many homeless right outside my window.

I have enjoyed my time at the University of Chattanooga at Tennessee. The housing options at the university are wonderful. I live in an apartment style dorm with two other people. The dorm is very nice and the process of living there was quite easy.
UTC is super welcoming, and they are so helpful. All my professors are so understanding of my issues that I have with my financial aid

I enjoy going to UTC. The classes are interesting and fun; every semester I'm excited by the classes I get to take, rather than feeling like I am obligated to be there. I went back to school because I believe in myself and developing my talents and interests, and it's a privilege to have support and guidance. The campus is beautiful and all the professors I've had care about what they teach and communicating clearly and effectively.

UTC's got some cool stuff going on. Academically, it's good. The class sizes are decent, so you actually get to know your professors, which is a plus. The campus is gorgeous, right in downtown Chattanooga, so there's always something to do. Student life is pretty active too, with tons of clubs and events.
If I could change a few things, I'd say some of the resources, like career services, could be better. And seriously, the parking situation is a nightmare.

ai_summary: The University of Tennessee, Knoxville offers a vibrant and lively campus atmosphere , where students often describe the community as friendly and welcoming. Many reviews highlight the strong support from faculty , noting their passion for teaching and willingness to assist students in their academic journeys. While the sports culture is a significant draw, with dedicated fan support and energy at events, the social scene, particularly related to partying, is mentioned frequently. However, several students express concerns about housing availability and costs, as well as parking difficulties, which they feel detracts from their overall experience. The campus is regarded as beautiful and walkable, and there's a notable sense of school spirit , yet some students call for improvements in dining options and advising services. Overall, the University of Tennessee stands out for its community feel, extensive extracurricular opportunities, and strong academic programs.

reviews.text: At first, I feel very safety when I walk around the campus, the most important reason because a lot of students also on walking path. There are a lot of yard and also swimming pools. When I finish my classes around evening, I walk through physical buildings and a lot of students playing inside. They were playing pickle ball and volleyball. There are also swimming pool and really convenient for student who want to practice swimming or just basically swim for fun. Next to it is the gym with many varieties of machine that student can work out for their full body. There are even have bathroom inside if they want to take a shower after work out a day long. Beside that, there are also bus routes both day and night for students who don’t have their own car so moving inside the campus is quite convenient. But most of the time, we choose to walk because it is healthier.

I started off not enjoying the university and really wanted to leave; however my opinion changed as I realized my issue was my major and not the university. I realized I didn’t like my major and took a career exploratory class and changed my major. The atmosphere is great at UTK and I haven’t had any bad professors yet. My biggest issue with the university is they need more advisors because of the large student population and it makes it difficult when signing up for classes.

This is a good school. UTK has good facilities and opportunities for any student interests. The social scene is very nice as well. The only major downside is that the University looks mildly ugly due to constant construction.

I really have enjoyed my experience at UT. I transferred here about a year ago after getting my assoiciates degree at a community college and although I was neuroscience for the transition it has been great!

I like UTK, but it does lack diversity in students. There is a lack of diversity when it comes to both race and sexuality and many student of both communities have expressed this. The professors are very kind and mostly helpful. The school does tend to focus on it's athletes and parking has become a very big problem.

The University of Tennessee is an excellent university, specifically for business students like me. There is constant encouragement for student support (resumes, interview prep, career fairs) as well as various clubs and activities you can engage in within your major(s). There is a constant camaraderie that is built during your time; alumni that have graduated years ago still love to say "GO VOLS!" I thoroughly enjoyed my time there, particularly studying within the Hodges Library. Personally, I think it is overpriced as a flagship university and is not worth the cost of tuition, unless you are planning on getting graduate level degrees. Faculty is kind, but you really need to pursue them to maintain a relationship. Athletics are awesome! As for the party scene, there are a lot better choices that students could make on any given night, simply put: not worth it. Overall, I think the University of Tennessee was good choice and I'm thankful for my time there.

I really enjoyed my experience at UTK. It was the first time moving away from home, and I loved being far enough but also close to my family. I was able to earn my degree for communication and science disorders. I loved all the support that was available to me throughout my college experience. I loved being able to go to the rec center and utilize the facilities there. I also enjoyed celebrating school spirit within our sport teams. The only thing that I could think of changing is making it more affordable or offering more scholarships to students. Otherwise, my overall experience was a positive one as I also met my now husband there!

It is a great school and I loved going here. I ended up transferring because though they offered the degree I wanted, the program was niche and not so great. I wanted something more hands on and what they offered was sitting in 15 hours of lectures a day and that is not what I wanted. But I think that would have been very different had I been in a different program that was more common. I loved the school though. Great school with great involvement, people, activities, food, and sports. Expensive though. Get ready for a 400 dollar parking pass to park 2 miles from your class. lol. its rough.

Overall great mix of academically challenging while many opportunities to have fun. Many opportunities to do a variety of different things whether athletics, arts, or outdoor activities, and everything in between.

My time at UTK has been defined by incredible school spirit and a sense of community. There is nothing like a Saturday in Knoxville! I loved how easy it was to get involved in student organizations and find my 'people.' The energy on campus is infectious. One thing I’d like to see change is the parking situation; it can be a real struggle for commuters. Also, some of the older dorms could definitely use some modernizing.
I love it so much! I have met so many amazing people and all of my professors have been very supportive. UTK also offers a variety of extracurriculars which I love as well.
Love SEC Football games, dorm rooms, community, extracurricular sports, and faith community. Do not like the housing situation where you are expected to move off campus after your first year.
Well balanced and growing university with a focus on the health and wellbeing of the overall student experience.
the admin stink here and the classes are alright but the food is really good. there are a lot of different places to eat and it is relatively superior to other campuses i visited
A lot of walking and very little parking spots. A lot of hills to walk. There is buses but traffic does slow it down by a lot. Weekends are always filled with parties.

ai_summary: The University of Tulsa offers a mix of experiences for students, which largely revolves around its small campus and class sizes. Many reviews highlight the dedication of professors and the supportive academic environment , where students value the close interactions with faculty. The school is praised for its diverse student body and numerous extracurricular activities, allowing for a strong sense of community. However, some reviews mention challenges such as expensive tuition , bureaucratic issues , and inconsistent student services. While many students appreciate the vibrant campus life and opportunities for involvement, there are concerns regarding the quality of food options and safety on campus. Overall, TU is seen as a place where committed students can thrive academically in a close-knit setting, though some factors may impact their overall satisfaction.

reviews.text: The University of Tulsa is a vibrant college with a lot to offer. The classes are challenging, but rewarding! The professors are very nice and want to see students succeed! The campuses beautiful and very decorative for holidays. The students here are nice and diverse with a bunch of clubs focused on learning and having fun. The local area has lots to do, but isn’t the safest to explore alone. But everything is always better with friends! The food here is good, but a little pricey, as is most things here. There’s a good selection here and more off campus. If there’s one thing I’d change it would be the amount of money used for more trivial things and focused more on the students. We have a few lawn roombas the require a lot of maintenance and money. But they aren’t really required for a daily use. They already have hired a landscaping company that does a wonderful job. Overall, TU is a nice campus with lots of opportunities.

The University of Tulsa is a great school that truly prepares students for a successful career in nursing. The instructors are knowledgeable, supportive, and genuinely care about helping you grow both academically and professionally. The program is challenging but rewarding, providing hands-on experience that builds real confidence in clinical settings.
If there’s one thing I’d like to see improve, it would be even more opportunities for mentorship and networking with current nurses and alumni. Overall, TU has been an incredible place to learn and develop as a future nurse.

After being enrolled here for almost three years now, the on-campus opportunities, faculty to student ratio, and overall student experience are the top three reasons as to why I have loved my time on campus.

ai_summary: The University of Utah offers a vibrant campus experience with a mix of strong academics and an active social life. Students appreciate the beautiful campus , located near mountains and city amenities, and report a good variety of academic programs, particularly in health sciences and STEM fields. Many reviews highlight a sense of community , with numerous clubs and events that foster social interaction, although some commuters find it challenging to engage fully in campus life. Faculty generally receive favorable feedback for being supportive and dedicated, though experiences with specific professors can vary. However, students express concerns about high tuition costs and disorganized administration , especially regarding financial aid and housing availability. Overall, while the U has many resources and opportunities, some students feel that the larger institution could better address concerns about student well-being and inclusivity.

reviews.text: I LOVED University of Utah. Coming from San Diego it was a culture shock at first, but leaving to go to college is a culture shock in itself. Salt Lake is a great city with plenty to do, see, and explore. I love that you have an urban center with lots of culture but you can escape to the mountains to get out into nature. I met some of my best friends there, some who lived 30 minutes away from me at home so we were able to drive back together, and some who lived on the other side of the world who I can now visit. Salt Lake has something for everyone. The campus is great, the professors want to see you succeed, and will do anything to help you. I struggled in college but I had the resources available to help me overcome them and graduate. The one thing I would like to see changed is parking. University of Utah is known for its lack of parking, unless you are paying an arm and a leg then you are unable to get parking OR are receiving a hefty parking ticket. The best bet is walking.

I really like that the University of Utah has a student life that provides lots of different get togethers. I am a little worried about football tickets and availability for students to get them. I wish the food on campus was a bit better. I'm sure that can be said for most schools. I worry about the new class and parking restrictions. I really enjoy being a Ute and hope to continue. The cost is worrisome.

I came to the Unversity of Utah as a transfer student and overall, it's been a good experience. All my professors have been very nice and they all seem like they enjoy their job. The campus size isn't as big as I thought it would be so I'm glad it's easy to get around. I also love how easy their transportation is with the buses to school. I do wish the Unversity had more opportunities for students to meet people, I feel like it's more of a commuter school then I expected. It's really hard as a transfer student to make friends.

My general experience at the University of Utah was positive. The professors all enjoyed what they did and were always available to help students that needed help. My only gripe with the school is the commute. While the U does provide every student free access to UTA's Trax and Bus system, the parking situation is awful. There are so few available parking spots available anytime past 8-9am, meaning you NEED to be on campus before 7 am to guarantee a parking spot. Due to this, most students opt to use the UTA system, leading to a slight overcrowding issue. Otherwise, the school is great.

I appreciated how supportive most professors were and how the environment encourages students to grow into who they aspire to be. I would like to see increased diversity, as well as additional opportunities for students to pursue and complete their master’s programs. Improving the parking situation would also greatly enhance the overall student experience.

The Fine Arts program is fantastic. The classrooms are smaller, so I'm given more opportunities to interact with my professors and other students. I enjoy the foundation courses, even if they are outside of my emphasis. The Art building is not so great. It leaks on the third floor, and there is hardly any seating. None of the classrooms allows food, so I have to eat on the floor in the hallways if I want to be ontime.
On campus is alright. The university is on a hill, so I often find myself walking uphill. Food prices are high, and there is very little food available around campus.
I've had mixed experiences with the professors. Some have been wonderful, and others have been a nightmare. Unfortunatly the required foundations courses sometimes only have one professor available for a class, and I haven't had good experiences with them.
They spend all of their time on recruiting- once you get in there are no resources. There are no academic advisors. It's a huge factory with no support.

Parking and living is miserable. I recommend finding off campus student affordable housing/apartments that has roommates. I’ve lived on campus and off campus (twice), I don’t recommend paying for a downtown apartment. I recommend a cheap one near campus or sugar house. The academics can be great, but actually being allowed to park and go to class is another story. You’ll pay 200+ for a semester or more parking permit and still get parking permits. Frankly if you go head and you see a parking compliance officer, flip them off and tell them they should hate their life/quit their job. Also if you’re head only for skiing or sports than there’s a good chance you’re not graduating.
I’ve had a good experience at the university of Utah so far. The Honors college is often difficult to handle with amount of hoops that you’re required to jump through.
Great college full of great people. One of the best public schools in the west for a reason. Their academic programs are competitive and professors are experienced.

My overall experience with the University of Utah has been very positive. I appreciate the wide variety of academic programs and the flexibility they offer, which makes it easier to explore different interests while still working toward my degree. The campus is beautiful, especially with the mountain views, and it creates a motivating environment to study and spend time between classes. I also like that there are many resources available to students, such as tutoring, academic advising, and mental health services, which show that the university genuinely cares about student success and well-being.
overall, big student body so less direct interaction with professors or TAs. nice campus overall and safe surrounding area with good student life.

At the University of Utah, I was able to find a degree that I loved and a small community that shaped me into the person I am today. While I was not very involved with campus life, I appreciated the fact that there were constant events and activities throughout the campus that allowed me to meet new people and explore more of what the school had to offer. The student population was not very diverse but the school often tried to show support for minority students through various programs and professors that genuinely cared about their students.

So far, I have liked several things about the University of Utah. Firstly, it's food is amazing and as a gluten free person there are so many options. Secondly, I love the football team, and the home games are just electric. Thirdly, it's very easy to get around campus, as most classes are a walking distance away from my dorm and if need be, I can take the train as students get to ride the public transport of Salt Lake City for free. Finally, there are so many campus activities and clubs every week, so I'm always out and about meeting people. Honestly, nothing much needs changing.
Most of my classes and professors were really good. I completely transformed as a student. Unfortunately there were a couple professors that really left a bad taste in my mouth.

ai_summary: The University of Vermont (UVM) offers a picturesque setting and a range of academic opportunities, particularly strong in environmental studies and sciences, though experiences can vary widely. Many students enjoy the friendly community and supportive professors , noting that small class sizes can foster personal connections. However, some express concerns about grade deflation in selective majors and emphasize the need for more diverse course offerings , especially in the humanities. The campus atmosphere tends to lack a vibrant nightlife or party scene, leading to some feelings of isolation, particularly during harsh winters when social activities dwindle. While Burlington has a lot to offer in terms of outdoor activities and a welcoming environment, students also mention high tuition costs and subpar living conditions, with outdated dorms and food quality often criticized. Overall, UVM is seen as a good fit for students who appreciate nature, a progressive community, and a balance of academics with outdoor pursuits, but potential students are advised to consider their priorities and conduct thorough research before committing.

reviews.text: UVM is a dump with capital D. Professors show up to class less than students, if you are even lucky enough to have a professor. In the Education department, I have had only a couple professors the rest were just unqualified warm bodies to put in front of students. Most teachers are drafted in a pyramid scheme from the Education department. When they show up and don't cancel class, they usually try to rile you up on some BS social justice issues that we really didn't come to college for. Most teachers are arrogant poorly traveled BS artists that just hand out grades to those that follow their social issues like brainless zombies. The campus is an ugly hodgepodge of buildings that neither have ac in hot weather or heat on shoulder seasons. Many students are cool but smoke a lot of weed and spend weekends hung over. The lake looks pretty but it is polluted and every summer they close the beaches. Burlington is full of transients begging for money. Find someplace else.

Courses that should be the simplest to pass required massive time commitments compared to courses directed toward majors. Academic support was loudly proclaimed but stretched extremely thin and overall unhelpful. Serious grade deflation here. Do your research before coming here.
Essentially none of my personal career success is owed to my time at UVM, transferring was one of the best decisions I made.

Everyone is very nice and cool. If you choose wisely, you can get amazing teachers always willing to help you. It's a great university if you are looking for a medium to small sized university, chill vibes, strong values of justice, equity, and acceptance, and just cool people overall. You need to like the cold, though.

The University of Vermont is unapologetically mediocre at everything and exceptional at nothing. As a sophomore who is pursuing transfer options, I would STRONGLY encourage incoming students to AVOID this university. As an bio major I have taken roughly 20 classes through this university point and only 2 classes seemed to have any semblance of time or energy put into their curriculum. As for other departments, I cannot comment, but the Biology and Chemistry as a whole are completely dysfunctional. This institution is a sinking ship.
Furthermore, this university has downright predatory grade deflation. Classes are designed to be unfair because it props-up institutional profit motive. Most graduates from this school are 5th or 6th years which is what this school desires as it pads their balance sheet. The grade deflation also makes transfer opportunities much less likely which keeps students trapped and paying.
Don't make my mistake, go elsewhere, please.

I enjoy how connected the students, professors, and faculty are and how there are always opportunities to learn as a student and person. UVM offers a variety of academic resources, student clubs, and ways to be involved with UVM and the surrounding community. So many single experiences have lead me to bigger and better ones.

I have nothing but good things to say about my past four years at UVM! My professors have been so kind, and have worked really hard to prepare me for a career by connecting me with internships and letting me assist with their research. Burlington is such an amazing area to live - there's always something going on and you never live more than 10 or 15 minutes (walking!) from your friends.
Any time I ran into issues with courses or campus life, my advisors and UVM's administration were always really responsive, which definitely made me feel like the school cares about me as a person. I chose UVM because of it's access to academic and extracurricular opportunities, financial fit, and professional programming. Four years later, I'm so glad I did. While in college, I've gotten to engage with academic research and had multiple internships while living in a really cool college town and meeting many of my best friends. I've never regretted choosing UVM, and highly recommend it!

At UVM I truly love my professors. Every professor I have had here has genuinely seemed to care about me, despite sometimes having 100+ students in a given class. Besides that, I have found great joy in my clubs and activities at UVM. I am in the women in law club, political science club, a member of Kappa Alpha Theta and I am on the club gymnastics team. I am grateful that I have so many opportunities to pursue things I love, while making friends in a very supportive atmosphere! Besides that, I am grateful to have 2 very fulfilling work study positons ,one in the CCRH gym, and one working in the Office of The Registrar.
I believe choosing UVM was the best choice I have ever made! The community here has motivated me to work hard and find my academic and personal interests!

After attending for a month, i can definitely say this is not the school i would’ve picked if i could go back in time and tell myself what it’s actually like. a majority of the people here only came to party, and many of them have already dropped out after the parties the first week were over. it’s impossible to find anybody who wants to do anything but sit in their room or party. I have never been more unhappy anywhere in my life! Many are taught by people who themselves are still students. Actual professors who are professionals are mediocre. Don't waste your time at this university, they don't care about their students and you can't even get into your required classes for your degree.

I really enjoyed my time at UVM. Most of the professors that I had were readily available for help and clearly enjoyed teaching. I also love Burlington. There are plenty of outdoor activities to keep you busy. With Lake Champlain and the mountains so close, everyone at UVM seems to live outdoors. I would say my biggest complaint is the housing. On campus housing is only available to first and second year students. All other students are forced to look at off campus housing options which are very expensive. Overall, UVM was a great fit.
I would like to see more attention and funding put into professors, and athletics at the school. I find nutrition very important and would also love to see improvements there.

UVM has a great culture; it is accepting of others. There are many ways to engage and build community. For students not interested in partying, there are other alternatives such as hiking, skiing, and visiting beaches on Lake Champlain. Academics are strong, but student workloads vary significantly based on major. Campus dining halls are average for college campuses and nothing special. On-campus housing quality varies based on resident halls. The Central Campus Resident Hall and University Heights are by far the nicest options. UVM struggles with keeping resident halls maintained and clean. Lastly, UVM doesn't offer on campus housing for Juniors and Seniors, leaving students to navigate the difficult and expensive Burlington housing market. In my view, UVM needs to improve housing options, allowing students to continue to live on campus by building more high-quality and affordable housing.

My experience here at the university has been really positive. I’ve had a really great time during my first three years here I really enjoyed the community. I think that’s one of the places where Uvm really excels the most. I am also absolutely in love with the curriculum that I’m learning, I feel like I’m challenged the perfect amount and I’m learning a great deal about the major that I am in. I also really enjoy the off campus experience of being in downtown Burlington. I’ve really enjoyed exploring everything that Church Street in the surrounding areas have to offer, including the music scene, great local restaurants, and just hanging out with friends and family the professors that Uvm also genuinely care about who you are as a person which is really refreshing. If I had to choose somewhere to go to college, I would choose Uvm over and over again. I would like some bigger more chain type of restaurants to come to campus, but overall my experience has been amazing.

My time at the University of Vermont has overall been a very positive experience. There are some aspects of campus that could use improvement such as some facilities, but the overall environment that has been cultivated in Burlington is exceptional.
I have really loved it at UVM!! I have gotten so many fun opportunity, I have made lots of friends and I feel like all my professors have been really amazing and want me to succeed

ai_summary: At the University of Virginia, students appreciate the blend of rigorous academics , vibrant social life , and supportive community . Many reviews highlight the challenge of coursework paired with accessible professors who genuinely care about student success, fostering an engaging learning environment . Although the campus culture encourages participation in various clubs and activities, some students express a desire for more informal social opportunities to promote connections. The beautiful campus , coupled with the enthusiasm for school spirit—especially during sporting events—enhances the sense of belonging. However, there are ongoing concerns about the perceived lack of diversity, competitiveness among students, and the quality of dining options. Overall, UVA is recognized for its strong academic reputation and enriching college experience, making it appealing for students who value both challenge and community.

reviews.text: My overall experience with the University of Virginia was positive. The campus was gorgeous and the classes were good.
I loved touring it so much and I am super excited to continue my studies at the campus next fall. Go hoos!

These advancements are the exact reason why I hope to attend University of Virginia and ultimately receive the Jefferson and Walentas Scholarship. I hope to continue to contribute to my community using the advancements of technology. This scholarship will lift any financial burden on my family and myself and allow me to solely focus on my higher education. This scholarship will give me the faculty support to further my dreams of using technology to impact the lives of those in underserved communities.

I loved my time at UVA especially for my academic education. It truly felt like the "public Ivy league" education so many rave about, particularly in the arts&sciences. My social education on "grounds" was more complicated, for many reasons including being a transfer student and being in undergrad during the COVID pandemic. UVA emphazises southern values in an otherwise extremely progressive town, a conjunction that can be confusing for various marginalized people. In this way, it does function like some Ivy leagues historically have/do. And furthermore, these southern values can translate to a certain kind of progress that is extremely valuable and reflective of American history more broadly. However it also translates to problematic and hyper-active greek life dominating most aspects of socializing on campus, as well as certain departments molding students into particular kinds of professionals rather than guiding them in their own unique development.

I have overall enjoyed my time at the University of Virginia so far. I feel as though this institution offers a variety of opportunities for everyone. There are many communities within the school that allow you to find things that you are interested in and connect with your peers to build stronger relationships and make the experience more enjoyable. The school works very hard to give students comfort and provide resources for any academic, social, or mental help needed.
It was a major adjustment from community college; however, I enjoy the atmosphere at UVA. Students and professors are friendly and it's fueled my love of learning.
I love of my time at the University of Virginia. It has provided me with so many opportunity and I will be forever grateful, especially as an out of state student.

UVA has so far been everything I had imagined the college experience would be. The campus is massive and absolutely beautiful, the dorms are comfortable and not too cramped, the dining halls offer a wide range of delicious choices, and there are so many diverse and interesting classes available to take. Everyone that I have spoken to has been friendly, and the parties I have gone to were a blast.

My overall experience have been positive. I have joined UVA as a transfer student in August 2024. Since by then UVA has helped me in providing a number of sources to help me get comfortable, welcomed and feel like I have been a UVA student. The tuitation have been so expensive here but the quality of education and campus life compensate for it. Overall, recommended!
Very pretentious and preppy. Classes were fine. Food was horrible and got worse each year I was here.
I had a really good time at UVA. I made plenty of life long friends. I was able to graduate in 4 years and secure a job in the field I studied in.

I have so far really enjoyed my experience at UVA. I was lucky enough to have my good group of friends also attend but I know that is not the case for everyone. It can feel alienating at times but if you put yourself out there you can find your people!

My experience at the University of Virginia set me up for a career in a niche field where I make contributions far beyond what I thought I could with only 2 years outside of study. As a large research institution, access to funding and research-oriented faculty was the highlight of my UVA experience.
If I could change one thing, it would be the housing and residence life as well as the dining hall experience. In my opinion, the high tuition and required room and board for the first academic year warrant top-tier housing and food.

UVA has been a phenomenal experience! It has literally changed me in the best way possible. It has its downsides but I think our school does its best to accommodate to those challenges. It is such a beautiful thing being here and working alongside such intelligent and driven people! The academic atmosphere is definitely rigorous, but doable with motivation and grit. The social scene is amazing, there are opportunities for anyone here (inc. and exc. greek life). The food is amazing as well as the people. WAHOO WA!
I love how gorgeous the campus is. Walking to class is a breeze and is fun!! There's also a great public transportation system that is free to all students.

ai_summary: The University of Washington (UW) offers a vibrant and diverse community enriched by a beautiful campus and numerous academic opportunities. Many reviews highlight the availability of world-class research , extensive clubs, and student organizations, emphasizing the importance of getting involved to form connections. However, prospective students should be aware of the competitive nature of the university, particularly regarding impacted majors, which often leads to a high-stress environment for many. While professors are generally supportive, the large class sizes can make personalized attention challenging. The campus's location in Seattle adds unique urban advantages , though high housing costs can be a drawback. Overall, UW is seen as a strong choice for resilient students looking for a prestigious education, but it's crucial for potential applicants to ensure that their goals align with what UW offers.

reviews.text: It was a very formative experience overall. But fields that weren't STEM got less resources and opportunities. There were so many career fairs for STEM but barely any for the Humanities. Thankfully, my last two years I got connected to some people in the humanities department who were invested in my success and actively working to get students more resources.

My experience with University of Washington has been both challenging and rewarding. I appreciated the diverse student community, strong academic programs, and access to valuable resources that support personal and professional growth. The campus environment encourages independence and critical thinking, which helped me develop confidence and discipline. What I liked most was the opportunity to connect with people from different backgrounds and gain real-world perspective. However, I would like to see more accessible support services and clearer communication for students navigating programs. Overall, it’s a solid institution that prepares students for real-life opportunities beyond the classroom.

Overall I had a great experience at University of Washington. It was a good educational experience, and once I got into my major I had a better time. The process of getting into a major I didn't enjoy as much. I think it's a lot to ask of a 17-18 year old to know exactly what career they want to have at that age. It's nearly impossible to get into some majors. A lot of the pre-requisite courses are taught by professors that are very clearly only there for research. I would like to see the departments make a better effort towards making sure students are leaving with job opportunities.
I am starting in Spring 2026. So far the onboarding process has been carefully guided. They help through the process.

My experience at the University of Washington was defined by its world-class academic rigor and the sheer beauty of the Rainier-facing campus, but it definitely required a high level of self-advocacy to navigate. I loved the "research powerhouse" atmosphere and the incredible networking opportunities provided by being in the heart of Seattle’s tech and healthcare hubs, which made my degree feel like a solid investment. However, I would love to see a shift away from the high-stress "weed-out" culture of capacity-constrained majors and toward more personalized support for underclassmen. It is a massive, fast-paced environment that rewards independence, but it can occasionally feel impersonal if you don't proactively seek out your own smaller community within the crowd.

My experience at University of Washington was overall very good. Interesting selection of classes and activities and you get what you put in. the campus is beautiful and I loved going to school in Seattle

University of Washington has been good to me so far, the professors have been fantastic, and the opportunities to study abroad, which interest me greatly, are plentiful. I've participated in group rides with the Husky Cycle Club, and they are always welcoming and a great group of people. The campus itself is breathtaking, and the buildings are in good shape. However, if I had to change one thing, it would be the transparency around scholarships and financial aid. I wish it was easier to find scholarships, and that there were more student resources surrounding this greatly important aspect of student life.

This university is considered one of the best public universities in the US, and they know this. That means that they know they can treat their students like trash and still have people attend to get that name on their diploma. We were told at Orientation that students were only the Professors' 3rd priority. I specifically had my graduation threatened because the University did not communicate about changes in major requirements until halfway through the quarter where it starts applying. The campus is huge with so many students that you have to strategize when choosing courses to make sure you'll have the time to walk from one building to the other through walking traffic. It is difficult to make friends because everyone is so focused on getting the best grades, which there's nothing wrong with if that's what you want. But if you want an even somewhat enjoyable College experience, go somewhere else.

The University of Washington offers a rigorous academics, outstanding research opportunities, and access to top-tier clinical and professional experiences. Faculty are knowledgeable and deeply engaged in the fields, encouraging, and the campus culture encourages critical thinking and service.
The administration process can be a bit overwhelming and navigating through resources can be daunting.
I've had a really good experience with this school - there are so many opportunities if you know how and where to look!

Academics : UW's academic rigor pushed me to grow. Professors are knowledgeable and passionate, often leading groundbreaking research. Classes can be challenging, but the quarter system keeps things fast-paced and engaging. I loved diving into topics like computer science or business—resources like the libraries and tech labs were super helpful.
Campus Life : The campus vibe is electric, especially on game days with Husky football! Clubs, Greek life, and events like the Cherry Blossom Festival add flavor to student life. Seattle's outdoorsy culture means hiking, coffee shops, and concerts are always nearby.
Opportunities : UW connects you to Seattle's tech hub. Internships at Amazon, Microsoft, or startups were accessible and shaped my career path. Research opportunities with professors are also a big plus if you're curious.
Challenges :The cost can be steep, but scholarships and part-time work helped. Some classes are huge, so getting prof attention takes effort.

I think overall UW is a really great school, with a beautiful campus, interesting classes, and a really great local area with downtown Seattle being a short distance away. It can be difficult, however, if you are a commuter student as traffic in peak hour is terrible and the cost of parking at UW keeps going up every year. I would say as long as you try your best to make some friends, and there will be plenty of opportunities from classes to events to other situations, then you'll be okay.
It was a very good experience the one time I went to the university of Washington I was treated with respect and the students body made sure I was safe and happy during my visit.

Overall, I really enjoy the school and the community established at the University of Washington. The location and public transportation are really convenient and make the city of Seattle accessible for students.

The University of Washington is a great school with so many passionate professors who genuinely care about students’ growth and learning. The campus is beautiful and offers many excellent spaces to study, and the school has a thriving extracurricular scene with countless RSOs filled with dedicated, enthusiastic students. There are also extensive opportunities for research, including independent projects, even for undergraduates, which makes UW a great place to explore academic interests.
That said, there are some minor drawbacks. TAs are often underpaid, and depending on the class or department, that can affect the level of enthusiasm and support they are able to provide. In many large introductory lecture courses, the high student-to-faculty ratio makes it difficult to receive detailed feedback on qualitative assignments such as essays and research papers. Finally, the on-campus food is both expensive and generally not very good.

ai_summary: The University of West Georgia (UWG) is often described as a welcoming and community-oriented institution where students can easily make friends and engage in a variety of campus activities. The academic experience is generally positive, with many reviews highlighting the helpfulness and accessibility of professors , along with a diverse range of courses and opportunities for professional growth. Student life is vibrant, with numerous events and activities fostering social connections, although some students noted a lack of off-campus entertainment options . While the campus itself is appreciated for its beauty and amenities , some concerns exist regarding parking, food variety, and housing conditions. Overall, UWG offers a supportive environment with a focus on student success, though some areas, like administration and campus resources for students, could use improvement.

reviews.text: I earned my second Master's Degree at this university. It was a wonderful experience. The professors were very helpful and supportive of my educational development. I would recommend this institution to others who are looking for graduate and postgraduate studies.
Wonderful school. Love the professors and the ability to continue my education part-time while pursuing my dream career.
I love it! The professors and faculty are always up and active with students. My most favorite person there is Pepper, the IT desk worker! he is so funny and fun to talk to!

It's an okay school, but I honestly wouldn't recommend it to anyone. Can be ever boring, and I wouldn't want anyone attending and paying for a boring school. If you live near by and would want to commute then definitely go for it. But I honestly wouldn't recommend you coming here if you plan to stay on campus. This school is only good for academics.
I like pretty much every thing honestly. It's a pretty good school and I've learned a lot and met great people in such a short amount of time. I can't wait to finish all four years here.
It was middle of the road. Getting used to Microsoft after years on Google was hard, but the Professors let me know if there were any technical issues.
I love the university of West Georgia. I feel at home and welcomed on campus as well as I feel seen in my classrooms.

My experience here at West Georgia has been great. I love the area and the environment. The campus is small but that is great. I enjoyed the parties they were really full and I also enjoyed the events here.

I think UWG is the best university I could have chosen. Being here makes me feel like our president truly cares about us in the way they feed us, the events they provide, and the quality of our education and food. The campus is pretty, there are many extracurricular activities, and it's relatively safe here. I love UWG.

Most professors are very helpful and actually want to see you succeed. There are many ways to get academic help and I love how you can practically be able to find a space anywhere to study or do some work
The university has some great qualities to in terms of the overall landscape and theme but I would really love a more connected environment as everything is quite separated in terms of living areas
I like it so far. I liked all my classes I've taken here. It has a good computer science department. I like that I get access to certain rooms in the main building on campus.

Although I haven’t yet begun attending the University of West Georgia, I’ve already been impressed by the support and guidance provided by the advisors, front office staff, and financial aid team. Everyone has been incredibly helpful insuring I’m well prepared and on track to achieve my degree.

I took German classes online, and the professor was amazing. He worked with me to be able to take the classes, even when one wasn’t listed as online. I was able to complete an oral exam over the phone and had a great time with the material.

University of West Georgia was a Dual Enrollment student there for 4 years while I was in high school and they made sure I was safe and comfortable while on campus, which I really appreciated. I also enjoyed my 2 years as a full-time student and would highly recommend UWG to anyone interested!

ai_summary: The University of Wisconsin-Madison offers a diverse and vibrant college experience , with a campus celebrated for its beauty and strong community spirit. Students appreciate the rigorous academics , highlighting the opportunity to engage with knowledgeable professors and a variety of interesting courses, although there are some frustrations regarding large class sizes and variability in teaching quality. The campus life is bustling, enriched by ample extracurricular activities, a dynamic social scene , and spirited athletics, especially football. Many reviewers emphasize the welcoming and inclusive atmosphere , while some express concerns over the lack of ethnic diversity and the challenge of navigating a large university. Overall, UW-Madison is seen as a well-rounded institution that fosters both academic growth and a fulfilling social life, making it a great option for students who thrive in an active college environment.

reviews.text: I cannot speak more highly of UW-Madison! Truthfully the best university in the world! I had a wonderful 4 years studying at Madison. The campus, city, and faculty were all awesome. 100000/10

I love Madison. There is truly a bit of something for everyone, and once the main campus starts to bore you, the city itself has so many hidden gems. Even without a car, getting off campus around the city is free for students and is pretty easy if you're comfortable with public transit.

I like the large opportunities offered such as: free tutoring,ethnic clubs,sports. Though i think the school may lack diversity I do see opportunities to connect with people within my ethnicity as well as having the opportunity to make friends from different places. Some things I would want to see change would be the campus food I think a diverse or changing of food selection would have helped me cut back on buying out food.

I love UW-Madison and would recommend it to anyone who is lucky enough to come here! I am currently writing this on Lake Mendota Terrace because it is a warm, sunny, beautiful day in Madison. I am from out of state but my friends and family love to visit here. The campus and student life is incredibly active and the campus is a perfect balance of nature and city with all four seasons!

I have really enjoyed my time at UW-Madison so far. I have made many friends, learned new things about myself, and become a better person. I enjoy the classes and professors here as well as being close to the city. I would like to see slightly better advising resources on my campus, as some advisors do not respond promptly or give inaccurate information.

I loved attending Madison. The city itself is a perfect college town. There is enough to do on and off campus I never felt trapped just going to school. The classes were hard, but the professors always seemed in my opinion to be happy to help. Even after graduation, the Pre-Lw Advising office has been so helpful to my law school application journey.
Love my time in Madison. Lots to do, lots to learn, lots of people. Good academics and social scenes.
UW-Madison is a great school. I really enjoy it because it has great academics all the while being in a beautiful fun city.

While there are some downsides to every college campus I have found little to no downsides here other than having to figure out housing for the following school year in september and october. This posed a challenge freshman year since I did not know anyone. The professors are wonderful and they come from all over to teach, most professors are also very passionate about the subject they are teaching. Another aspect I love about the school is the vast ammenities! I love being able to find new study spots whether its in a campus building or local coffee shops!

Madison is a great community where there are so many different things to do and avenues to explore. I would recommend the University of Wisconsin Madison because the academics are challenging and engaging and the student life is super fun and inviting. You get the best of both worlds in Madison and that is why they call it a "work hard, play hard" school.
really great culture, though in the general classes there are 200ish people for one professor. waters dorm is the best
It was overall a positive experience studying at the University of Wisconsin Madison. I have experienced a lot of great things there

Overall UW Madison is a great school with many knowledgable professors and strong academics. There are many great opportunities to work together with your peers (fellow students) and build strong workplace problem solving and collaborative skills. One drawback is the frequent lack of one on one time with the professor that comes with attending a larger university.

I enjoyed my first semester at UW a lot, there is a lot of great people from staff, to professors and students too. The residence and dining halls are all pretty nice and new and the sporting events are very fun to be apart of.

My classes have been really eye-opening in the sense of learning about different majors and certificates you can get here. The dorms are really nice and they each have things unique about them such as Smith has a Starbucks. The dining halls are spread out nicely that there is always one near all the buildings if you need to grab a quick bite to eat and go to your next class.

ai_summary: At the University of Wisconsin - Milwaukee, many students express a strong sense of community and support from both peers and professors. Reviewers often highlight the caring nature of faculty who are committed to student success, emphasizing accessibility during office hours and open communication. The campus is described as well-kept and easy to navigate , offering a variety of resources, including dining options and event activities that contribute to an engaging student life. The diversity among the student body is frequently mentioned as a unique aspect, allowing for a rich exchange of cultures and ideas . While students appreciate the variety of academic programs and opportunities, there are some criticisms regarding class difficulty and safety concerns on campus and in surrounding areas. Overall, UWM is viewed as a welcoming place that offers a good balance of academics and community involvement, particularly appealing for those looking to connect with a diverse range of students.

reviews.text: My overall experience at the University of Wisconsin–Milwaukee has been positive. Most of my professors have been supportive, approachable, and genuinely invested in student learning. They have been available for questions, provided clear guidance, and created environments where students feel encouraged to succeed. However, not every experience has been the same. Some instructors have shown less engagement or flexibility, which can make certain courses more challenging to navigate. Despite this, the majority of my academic experiences have been meaningful and have contributed to my growth as a student. Overall, I would rate my experience around four out of five stars, as it has been strong and supportive, but not without areas for improvement.

I really enjoy the setup of the campus and how even going onto the grounds a bit really delves one into the college experience. I am currently attending for business so most of my classes are in the Lubar building and I really enjoy the set up and intimacy of the building when I am in there. Some of my classes are fairly well sized but the one with less students makes it feel so much more personal and understandable. There is also the campus social life, where I feel like I have had not a lot of trouble getting to meet people and make friends.
The community is really great and welcoming, there are many events to go to for connections and making friends. There are also many resources for careers and a wide variety of other things.

My experience at UW- Milwaukee has been positive overall . I really like how spread out the campus is, it makes the environment feel less crowded and easier to navigate. The TA's are really supportive and show they really care by offering a helping hand in this new college transition. One thing I'd like to see change is more accecibility for textbooks prices are out of hand in some classes.

I really like the diversity and how close-knit the community is. It's a very fun campus and not too big, which I like. I also love how straightforward it is to get academic accommodations, and the only thing I'd change is more events for commuter students.
The professors in the CEAS College are top of the line in terms of helping out the students with any questions and pushing them to accel and ask questions.

University of Wisconsin Milwaukee offers a strong academic experience with supportive professors and a diverse student community. I appreciated the smaller class sizes, which made it easier to connect with instructors and get help when needed. The campus is in a great location, especially being so close to Lake Michigan, and it also offers good access to internships, research opportunities, and jobs in Milwaukee. Some of the campus buildings are very old, which is noticeable. Professors are overall hit or miss. There are some truly excellent professors who care and teach very well, and some very bad ones, with almost nothing in between, so your experience really depends on the classes and instructors you choose. The good professors definitely outweigh the others, so overall, it is a solid university that provides good value and real opportunities if you take initiative.
I like the campus vibe people are nice things I would like to see is more events and more good campus food.
I enjoyed my education at UWM. We had a great university community and staff and professors really cared about students. I had a lot of experience to get real life applicate for my field.

Good school, I love how simple the campus is and the resources and clubs are so fun. The food is way to expensive for what you pay in the dining hall but it's not the worst. Overall I like it here though, the proximity to the lake is also a plus.
So far Milwaukee has been very interesting, but fun and eventful campus and college. They're many things to do and many organizations and clubs to join.

I graduated from the University of Wisconsin–Milwaukee last spring and had a really positive experience overall. I appreciated the diversity on campus—students came from many different backgrounds, which made classes more engaging and open-minded. My professors were supportive and passionate about their subjects, specifically in the Sociology and Geography departments. Everyone there was so passionate and really knew the material. I enjoyed the opportunities to connect what I learned to the city through required internships and community projects. The campus had a welcoming vibe and a great balance between city life and academics. If anything, I’d like to see more support for students working full-time. Overall, UWM helped me grow academically and personally.
I would like to see UWM incorporate people who are commuters or live off campus in student life. Such as making clubs have an online portion whether its through zoom or something else.
It's a nice school, I love all the events and resources here and I've met some amainzung people. Though price and food quality for what your paying for is difficult but I'm making it work.
I have taken online classes at UWM throughout high school, and the professors are amazing! My older sister went to UWM, and she liked it a lot; she just said it had an older vibe to it.

ai_summary: At the University of Wisconsin - Oshkosh, many students appreciate the sense of community and support they receive from both peers and professors, making it easy to form friendships and feel included. The campus is noted for its welcoming atmosphere , with diverse extracurricular activities and social events that foster connections among students. Academically, while many praise the helpfulness and dedication of the faculty, some express concerns about the limited availability of certain classes and majors, particularly following recent instructor layoffs. The campus is described as a convenient size , allowing for easy navigation between buildings, and it's situated in a relatively safe area, although some students have noted issues with traffic around campus and a desire for more safety measures. Students generally enjoy the food offerings and campus facilities, but there are calls for greater variety and quality. Overall, UWO is seen as a good choice, particularly for those interested in nursing or education, due to its supportive environment and strong programs.

reviews.text: I love the campus is a great size, you have everything in Oshkosh. There’s a lot to do to clubs to games to parties to game nights. I would like to see the advisors change they are not the greatest.

This was a complete waste of my time. They created problems and obstacles for my major in core courses that didn't have anything to do with my major. I am not able to get decent paying jobs due to my affiliation with this place. They do not prioritize the correct things and only care about taking your money.
I was an ESL Education major, who had to complete two teaching clinical. One wasn't even required for my major, but the professor was allowed to require it. I lost two weeks of work pay and had my job schedule in ruins due to the sudden change. Professors should not be allowed to abuse their power as such, but were allowed to here.
The university says that I owe them $50,000 but I never took out that much in loans. They encourage scams and try to ruin everyone's lives. People are spoiled and discouraged from seeking education. They do not value education, anyone's personal well being, or intelligence. This university has handicapped my professional opportunities.

My experience at University of Wisconsin–Oshkosh has been overall positive. The professors are approachable and care about student success, and the smaller class sizes make it easier to build connections. The campus feels welcoming and easy to navigate.
I would like to see more internship and career networking opportunities, along with some updates to facilities and dining options. Overall, it’s a supportive environment where you don’t feel like just a number.

Overall my experience at UW Oshkosh has been very good. I commute to the campus, so the only change I would want to push for is more parking, as it can be difficult to find a spot. However, I've had a positive experience with everything else.

There are many amazing clubs and oppurtunites that they have, and many of the professors are extremely helpful. One thing is that the intro level course work and required classes seem too easy, and not like higher education.

My experience at the University of Wisconsin - Oshkosh was overall mostly positive. The campus feels welcoming, class sizes are manageable, and many professors are approachable and supportive. It’s easy to feel like part of a community.
Some administrative processes and advising could be more consistent, and continued upgrades to facilities and career resources would help. Overall, it’s a solid, affordable school with a good balance of support and opportunity.

My experience at UW-Oshkosh was quite good. As someone who works and is at the radio station extensively, I really love their Radio-TV-Film program. Having also worked on TV, I give high regard to that. I would say that as a college overall, it is great, and even better when it comes to the broadcasting/media departments as well.
The housing could definitely be improved. The rooms and buildings are very outdated. The bathrooms are cleaned pretty frequently but are still very dirty.
great life is awesome dorms could be better always stuff to do everything is close by and great environment

Overall, I really enjoy being a student at UWO. For academics, I always feel as if my professors love what they teach and love teaching it. I can tell they are invested in their fields of study and really enjoy teaching. I do think that I need to take so many classes though, that are not related to my major. I had to add a minor because when I am done with my major classes I still need 17 credits to get to 120 credits, to be able to graduate with a Bachelor's degree. It is completely okay though, because I did want to add a Spanish minor anyway, but I just feel like 17 credits is a "whole extra semester" that I did not fully know I needed until now, which adds roughly eight thousand dollars extra. Besides that, I really enjoy it here and I also have a job here at CopyThis located in Reeve Student Union. I like the environment and there is always something going on at campus. There are so many activities that are available to participate in, and I enjoy going to those with my friends.
I just really like my campus and the freedom i get from living there. The only thing i don't like is that some professors aren't the best, but it's just a personal opinion.

My experience at UWO has been very positive and helpful with preparing me for internships, connections, and other opportunities. The professors here are amazing and care for the students. The student life is okay and it’s very easy to get connected around campus.

As a small school it gives you a lot of opportunities to get involved and enter high level positions for on campus jobs and clubs. The professors and staff on campus are so amazing and understanding for the most part. You get the same if not better education since the class sizes are smaller and some professors have already worked in your field! It is also affordable and a small tight nit community!

After finishing my first year at the University of Wisconsin - Oshkosh, I am glad to say that I had a great experience. The dorms are nice and when you need a work order they are done in a reasonable time. The food isn't bad. It's nice to not have to cook everyday and the dining hall often has different food on their menu. Although on the weekends you are limited to the same 4 options that can get boring over time. Student life is nice, there are a lot of nice people. I found that a lot of people who attend Oshkosh like to party every weekend and this can be fun sometimes. For students who require accessibility needs make sure you check with your advisor to make sure that all things that you will use are accessible and fit your needs. Some buildings on campus are not fully accessible and make you go out of your way to get to a class. Overall this is a decent college.

What I liked about Oshkosh was how it wasn't too big and not too small. You were able to get to know a lot of people and build friendships easily. I also liked how close every building was to each other. The farthest from one side of campus to the other that you had to walk was about 15 min. There was also a lot that you could do around the area. It was just a little under a 20 minute walk to downtown where there are cute little shops.

ai_summary: At the University of Wyoming, students consistently highlight the strong support system and approachable faculty , noting that smaller class sizes facilitate meaningful interactions. The campus fosters a friendly and inclusive community , with a variety of academic resources and extracurricular activities. Many appreciate the beautiful mountain surroundings and the opportunities for outdoor adventures. However, some students express concerns about the limited off-campus activities and the occasional disconnect in communication regarding academic advising. Overall, while the university offers a solid education at an affordable price, the student experience can vary significantly based on personal involvement and engagement with the campus community.

reviews.text: I loved living in Laramie while attending the University of Wyoming. I earned both a bachelor's in mechanical engineering and a minor in entrepreneurship during my studies there.

its a great campus to come travel to and from internationally. With this the campus is highly diverse and welcoming to many people from all over the world. We hold many major types to study with mostly decent professors available. I would like to see them actually focus on the student body demographic and not just go with "what fits best on paper" methods to campus additions. Such as adding new dormatories and removing parking to make them fit, parking has become a nightmare and would really appreciate a revisit to this situation.

The University of Wyoming is a great school for undergrads. I was a student in two different departments and both seem to care about their students. The professors were willing to help you at any time given and other resources were always available. They have housing accommodations available to anyone who needs it including family apartments. Maintenance is reliable and quick to respond. Degree Advising could use some work but it just got switched to the professors job so they are getting used to it. Campus Life seems to always be alive. They offer line dancing, sunrise yoga, water exercises, and much more for active life styles.

Overall I enjoy/enjoyed my experience at UW. The students and professors are all so helpful, kind, and down to earth. My only negative comments have to do with my own social skills rather than the school itself.

The University of Wyoming is a good combination of a state university and a small college. There is a good number of students and extracurricular activities, but you can still make connections. You will end up seeing some of the same people in your classes and get to know your professors. Yet you aren't stuck with the same 10 people; you can branch out and make new friends.
So thankful to be a student at the University of Wyoming! Everyone here has been extraordinarily supportive and helpful when it comes to the success of our students and faculty.

I really enjoy the student and dorm life here, and the academics are top notch, as there is free tutoring for any class. However, the town and university is smaller making it only reach a specific student.
I have only visited the campus. Based on students accounts and what I saw while touring I think UW is very safe and has positive student relationships.

So far, my limited experience at the University has been very positive. Coming into school i was a little leery, as I have two years off to serve as a voluntary full-time missionary for the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints. Getting back into the swing of school has been an adjustment, but the teachers have been extraordinarily willing to lend a hand in helping me catch back up to where I was. Even while many of the classes are lecture based and have large amounts of students, the teachers love to talk individually during office hours after class to provide that help. I have also thoroughly enjoyed the wide variety of clubs and activities across the school. I am currently part of a ministry team for church and absolutely love it. It has been a phenomenal opportunity to make friends and serve others at a time in which I am often focused far too much on myself. The more I utilize what the university has to offer, the greater positive impact it has had on me.
its be very fun so far even tho im only a couple months in I have been provided events to socialize in and many ways to get support no matter the topic
Awesome environment, new dinning hall and two new beautiful dorms. College of business is also beautiful and awesome professors and instructors
i am applying to University currently but the process has been easy and all support staff have been amazing in this journey

I have had a fantastic experience at the University of Wyoming, participating in many academic activities as well as campus activities. They have awesome opportunities to see concerts and get involved in the community.

Overall, I enjoyed my time at UW. I really struggled in the beginning with the advisors leaving and I was a non traditional student so I didn't have that in person connection. I eventually got an advisor who knew what my needs were and the process was pretty smooth after that. I fell like all the classes were valuable. There were some differences with how some teachers used Canvas and those who didn't. So consistency fluctuated from semester to semester because some chose to use multiple platforms to communicate, schedule group assignments, and the syllabus wasn't always up to date. Otherwise, overall, I enjoyed my time there and wish they had an on line masters program in my field.

Overall my undergraduate experience was fine. I met only one person I still speak to. Unfortunately it is very cliquey because students come from high schools around Wyoming and are only stay friends with those they came to college with. The professors are 50/50, they are either amazing and care deeply about students or only care about their research and are forced to teach.

ai_summary: At the University of the Cumberlands, students appreciate the affordability of tuition , including free textbooks and low hourly rates, making it a viable option for many, particularly adult learners. The campus is noted for its beauty and tight-knit community, which fosters supportive relationships among students and faculty. Academics are generally well-regarded, especially for online courses, with professors often described as dedicated and responsive. However, there are criticisms regarding limited public amenities , such as nightlife and dining options, as well as the state of dormitories, which some find lacking. Student life is enhanced by athletics and various events, but some feel that the college leans heavily on sports culture , potentially sidelining non-athletic students. Additionally, the college's Christian ethos is prominent, which might not appeal to everyone. Overall, while many find a nurturing environment at the university, there are areas, like housing and food quality, that students believe need significant improvement.

reviews.text:
My academic experience at the University of the Cumberlands has been very positive overall. The program has worked well with my busy schedule, especially while balancing work and family responsibilities. The flexibility of the courses has allowed me to continue advancing my education while still meeting my professional and personal commitments.
One of the best parts of the experience has been the professors and administration. They have been helpful, responsive, and supportive throughout the program. The courses have also been relevant to my field, which has helped me connect what I am learning directly to my work in cybersecurity.
The most challenging part has simply been balancing school with a full-time job and being a full-time dad. However, the structure of the program and the support from the university have made it manageable and rewarding.
Instructors have all been great, prompt in responding, and helpful. I love that as a student, you get Grammarly Pro. It is so useful when writing papers and responses on discussion boards.
