I love WSU! I am proud to be a Coug alumni. I was only there for two years, but I enjoyed my time there. I felt community there, and I appreciated the support from my professors. I wouldn't have changed my time there for anything!

Moving to Washington State University was one of the most influential decisions I have ever made. Since enrolling and since living here at WSU, I have made countless new connections as well as exploring numerous professionally developing events hosted every week at multiple locations. Not only is it one of the most fun times of my life here, but also one of the best educating experiences.

So far, I like WSU, I was skeptical of living in the dorm my first year, and joining greek life. But now that I am a sophomore student, looking back at it, I loved it, Freshman year was one of the best years of school I had ever experienced. I made so many new friends, connections, broke off some friends and connections, but managed to trudge through freshman year. I would like to see a change in the pizza that they serve at the dining halls though haha.
Washington State University allowed me to grow as a student, leader, and individual. I enjoyed my time at the school!

The culture at WSU is unlike anything else. The community is welcoming and loving to any incoming freshman. I studied hard and felt supported by the university throughout my endeavors. I worked at multiple university sanctioned jobs including the dining hall and gyms, both of which were very accommodating with my schedule.

I loved my time at WSU. It challenged me and pushed me into the academia I am pursing now. Without the constant support and faith that were provided to me by my professors, I doubt I would have been as successful. Especially since my first year at WSU was online due to the pandemic.
Good environment to study and the town is safe for families and students. The only downside about wsu is that there is not much to do for fun

I really like WSU because it has given me the chance to meet a lot of amazing friends and build a strong sense of community. Being here has helped me grow socially and academically, and I appreciate how welcoming the campus environment is. Overall, my experience has been really positive, and I’m grateful for the people I’ve met.

I honestly really enjoy it year, especially being a first year, it’s better than I imagined it to be. I made some great friends and am having a good time. The one thing id like to see change is probably maintaining a clean area in places like the dorm bathrooms and showers.

The academic advisors have ruined a lot of my college career by messing up my schedule. I am consistently told one thing and then my advisor does another thing, against my consent. My professors also teach my to rely on AI for a lot of my homework.

ai_summary: Washington University in St. Louis offers a welcoming and collaborative academic environment , as highlighted by students who appreciate the access to supportive professors and a range of academic resources. The campus is described as beautiful, with appealing dorms and varied dining options, though some students feel the food could be improved. While the rigorous academics are often noted to be challenging, many students thrive due to the availability of tutoring and other forms of support. Student life has a strong focus on collaboration over competition, although the social scene is described as less energetic compared to some peers, largely due to recent restrictions on Greek life and party culture. Students value the diversity and opportunities for engagement with the surrounding community, though some mention a lack of economic diversity within the student body. Overall, WashU is characterized as a great choice for students who seek a balance between rigorous academics and a supportive community, albeit within a relatively quiet social environment.

reviews.text:
One of the most predatory admissions strategies for a Top-20. Loves to admit students with strong tangibles but little signaled interest to attend small Elite Schools as a way to appeal to parents; Poaching students from what probably would've been a better cultural fit.
Non-existent campus life and traditions here mean you do not get a college experience. Career services do not offer any unique value; WashU falls firmly into being one of the worst value options for the tier of student that would be admitted here.
Unsafe city, beautiful campus, overrated food, great chancellor, no school spirit, fairly supportive, strong athletics, poor advising, great for churning out pre meds.

Everything about WashU just feels so genuine. Since orientation week, everyone has been so welcoming. They also make sure to meet all students' financial needs and that's something truly amazing for an institution. Every social event always has the right resources to meet students' needs and makes an enjoyable environment. The housing and meal plans are amazing; you even get a free birthday cake with the platinum plan! The professors are amazing at their work and show interest in students and their academic abilities. The campus life is amazing, with special events organized to get to know the St. Louis area better; I went to Six Flags because of them and currently entered a raffle to go watch the Phantom of the Opera! WashU is great at balancing academic and social life. Their food is just as great and has a wide variety for vegetarians and vegans which can be a challenge for people.
It is an amazing place where I can be myself and feel protected while trying out new things that I have never tried before. It truly is an amazing place and I am really grateful for it.

I’m a current freshman at washu and love it so much!! Super welcoming environment and you genuinely learn so much in your classes. Not a big party or sports school but there are other ways to enjoy campus life and meet people. I would so highly recommend applying!
After a horrible experience in high school, Washington University was wonderful. The students were far more diverse and accepting of differences.

WashU is a very underrated school since often times the only people who know it are the ones familiar with top universities, and recruiters. It's a beautiful campus but MO has a long and cold winter. The school is very safe, although there are local areas outside of Clayton which one should not visit alone. Academics is rigorous, yet I find myself sometimes slightly disappointed by the quality of the teaching being lower than what I was expecting. The professors are all passionate about their subjects are brilliant, but some of them are just horrible in lectures and WashU needs to be more careful selecting their staff for classes. A very fun student culture with many clubs, there are super competitive clubs that are hard to get into but the Greek life culture is decently healthy. Dorms are wonderful, retail food options are great and the only complaint I have is that only the cafeteria is open on weekends. The local area can get boring quickly, so make sure to rent a car and explore.

I never realized how much I would appreciate the quality of life here. Of course Washu has very smart professors and students and an absurd amount of research opportunities, but the campus is also beautiful and easily walkable, the dorms are great, the gym is great, and Forest Park is great. There are some bad parts, like the lack on name recognition and workload and the teaching quality (so many teachers are there for research and struggle to teach you well). Also, so many kids are going free it’s crazy.
If you’re undecided, it’s a great school but make sure that if you’re interested in engineering apply to it because it’s easier to switch out of engineering than to switch in.
Love love love it. More people should know about WashU because it has been such an amazing experience!

My experience at WashU is not unique, although people are typically quiet about having this sort of experience. Academically speaking, the school is fine; you'll have to work hard regardless of what major you choose. However, the campus is extremely left-wing, and as someone who leans in the other direction, it can feel very isolating when hardly anyone shares the same views as you. Conservatives are demonized, rejected and even doxxed on apps like Sidechat. The social scene is abysmal; the administration has cracked down on Greek life and parties, so people walk around on eggshells just to have fun. The campus has fostered a culture of competition and it shows even in clubs—many are extremely selective and in order to be part of the "in" crowd it seems you need to be involved in one of them. There are very few campus traditions left, either; ThurtenE and WILD (both of which take place toward the end of the year) are among the last. I'm very disappointed in my WashU experience overall…
My experience at Washington University St.Louis has been going great overall. I enjoyed the academics, campus life, and professors. I hope they'll improve on the campus food, party scene, etc.

Very cliquey. NOT for undecided majors. Unless you are a member of a special population in a program, expect to do everything for yourself (no lists of contacts/connections nor mentoring for "regular" students). If you do ask for help, expect to be asked who your parents know and who their friends are that can help you (and the university).
WashU is a great college and I’m very happy with my decision to attend WashU. It has great teachers, dorms, and food.

I enjoyed being a student at WashU. The environment was collaborative and professors were open to helping students with classroom material as well as research/work opportunities. The administration was very responsive to students' demands for change. I felt heard and valued by WashU.

It has been an overall amazing experience attending WashU. I have met so many kind and driven people and I truly feel at home there. While the athletic and social life scenes are not amazing, the community and activities available/provided by the school make up for it.

ai_summary: Wayne State University offers a diverse and supportive environment , with a strong sense of community among students and faculty. Many reviews highlight the welcoming atmosphere on campus and the accessibility of resources like tutoring and academic advising. The quality of education is often praised, particularly in programs related to science, business, and medicine, with professors noted for their dedication and helpfulness . The campus's location in Detroit adds to its appeal, providing students with numerous cultural and social opportunities . However, some students express concerns about the condition of campus facilities, including dining options and outdated buildings, as well as challenges related to parking and financial aid availability. Overall, while Wayne State is seen as a good place to learn and grow, some areas for improvement remain evident in the reviews.

reviews.text: I'm a current freshman at Wayne, but from my first two semesters here I would say that this university is pretty good. It's easy to get in touch with professors and your advisors, resources are easy to access and there are many student organizations to participate in.
I really liked the flexibility of Wayne State. I liked that I had options of taking less challenging classes and very challenging classes with lots of research opportunities.
Very diverse,plenty of places to eat, great professors with travellinggrwat backgrounds and experience.
I love the distance from housing and my classes. It is a easy accessible distance to and from. The professors are very understanding and the on campus life is amazing!

Wayne State is a great school with a fantastic Business program. As an incoming student I did not have to apply separately to the business school, which was a great stress relief. Since starting last year, I have received constant support from professors and advisors. It is a great local university that often goes over-looked!

I graduated from WSU in 2009. I played baseball, and majored in Social Studies Education. I am currently in the process of entering the PhD program at Wayne to study philosophy & ethics
The change in the campus & surrounding area has been so stark that it scarcely reminds me of my time at University Towers.
Now that my son is a student at WSU, everything has come full circle, so to speak, he loves the campus, his professors, and his academic program.
He earned a 4.0 his first semester, so yeah, I’m a proud father who raised my son on my own. It wasn’t easy at times, but we have such a close relationship that I wouldn’t change a single thing…it was very cool to hear him say that Wayne State was his first & only real choice for college bc it was my Alma Mater
However, if he continues to earn high marks the remainder of his frosh year, he is going to attempt to transfer to the University of Michigan
I was mislead with my courses, but now I have a new advisor who is leading me on the correct path to graduate.

My college experience has been rewarding and engaging, largely due to campus life and supportive professors. Being on campus allows me to feel connected with fellow students and professors. This connects helps me to stay motivated through my journey. Access to campus resources such as libraries, professors, and academic advisising services has positively impacted my college experience.
Although I enjoy taking courses on campus, I have taken online courses as well. I do not prefer online courses due to the amound of discussions that feel less interactive in my opinion.
Overall, my college experience has been positive and fufilling. I'm glad i have the opportunity to attend on campus courses with supportive professors academic resourses. I have grown so much as far as staying accountable through this process and personal development.

I would like to begin by saying that I don't usually leave reviews for places, but this one called for it. Going to Wayne State for my Masters Degree was one of the worst decisions of my life. The advisors are discombobulated and are extremely difficult to get in touch with. The registrars office is by far thee worst office experience I've ever had in my life. They are constantly redirecting you to different staff who have no idea how to answer your questions. I was under the impression that a remote program would enable you to complete your internship remotely or out of state. Nobody could answer that question until after I was misled & enrolled. They then told me I had to be completely in person. The staff are supposed to make things easier for you, to be helpful and kind. That has not been my experience here at all. They have not helped prepare me for life outside of Wayne State. I will say, the professors were very kind and helpful. Besides that, save your money. Go somewhere else.

Wayne State is overall a very solid school. Campus life is huge, there are many people to interact with, and there are always activities being planned. Wayne State Housing is very inclusive and it's very easy to feel at home and a part of a huge community.

I mainly like Wayne State University because of the diversity, growing up there wasn’t as much representation in the schools that I was attending until much later. I love the fact that everyone comes from a different background yet we all ended up in the same place seeking for similar things, whether that is an education, learning on how to get our goals in life, or the opportunity of learning.

WSU is an amazing school that is only about 10 minutes away from home. The staff was amazing and the campus was very safe, with police officers always there and easily accessible. The campus life was great! I had 2 semesters with 7 classes each, and the teachers were very caring and wanted us to succeed. I had all As for the past 14 classes I took. I worked really hard and there were many quiet spaces for studying.

My experience at Wayne State has been a mix of stressful and really rewarding. It’s definitely not easy, especially as a transfer student, but it’s a school where if you advocate for yourself and actually put yourself out there, you can grow a lot. I like the diversity on campus and how real everything feels. You meet all kinds of people and it pushes you to mature fast. What I don’t like is how confusing things can be, especially financial aid and communication. It feels like you’re constantly calling different offices and getting different answers, which can be overwhelming. I wish there was clearer communication and more support for transfer students so the transition isn’t so rough. Overall though, Wayne State has helped me become more independent, confident, and driven. It’s not perfect, but it’s a place where you can really build yourself if you’re willing to navigate the challenges.

It wasn't a very welcoming place for me. In my time there I was clearly reminded numerous times that I am an outsider and I will never fit in. I was not encouraged. I was told that I should doubt myself. This institution ruined my college experience. It preys on high school students that are about to graduate in the nearby communities to enroll there to secure as much taxpayer funding as possible and does not care if that student graduates.

Wayne State University offers a unique blend of strong academics, real-world opportunities, and the energy of an urban campus. One of the things I appreciate most is the university’s commitment to hands-on learning—whether through research, internships, or community engagement in Detroit. Many professors are passionate about their fields and genuinely invested in student success.
I also enjoyed the diverse student body. You meet people from all backgrounds, which creates a supportive, inclusive environment and adds a lot to classroom discussions and group work. The campus itself has improved greatly in recent years, with updated buildings, expanded study spaces, and more dining options.
What Could Improve
While the experience is overall positive, there are a few areas where the university could grow:
• Administrative processes (financial aid, advising, registration) can sometimes feel slow or confusing. Streamlining communication would make things much easier for students.

ai_summary: West Chester University of Pennsylvania offers a mix of engaging academic and social experiences, though opinions on specific aspects vary widely among students. Many praise the supportive faculty and accessible professors , highlighting their dedication to student success, particularly in programs like psychology, biology, and business. The campus itself is noted for its beauty and small-town charm , with convenient access to local restaurants and a range of student organizations and activities. However, some students express concerns about the social environment feeling cliquey and the limited housing options , especially as upperclassmen often find themselves off-campus. Parking challenges and food quality also receive mixed reviews, indicating room for improvement. Overall, West Chester stands out as a welcoming community with strong academics but faces challenges in student life and resources.

reviews.text: I applied to West Chester back in August of 2025 and still have not heard back about my decision yet. It seems extremely unprofessional to me that this is allowed. West Chester was one of my safety schools so I’m now committed to another university, but to anyone considering applying in the future, I’d think twice. This has completely turned me away from even considering the school. I do not appreciate it.

I have had an amazing experience at West Chester University of Pennsylvania, particularly as a psychology graduate student. The faculty's strong sense of support was one of the things I valued most. In addition to their expertise, professors really care about their students' success, whether it is through career counseling, research mentoring. I also valued the hands-on opportunities available throughout the psychology program. My education felt extremely meaningful, as I worked directly with children and families from underserved communities, participated in research, and gained experience with assessments and data collecting. This experience helped bridge the gap between academics and practical experience, which is incredibly important for students who want to work in the clinical psychology field.
I would love to see more parking options on campus. The current selections include expensive parking passes or paid street parking.
My overall experience at West Chester is good; just a few minor tweaks could be made. For example, more housing options and more affordable housing would be beneficial.

I was studying law there as a transfer student from SHU and still managed to graduate in 3 years. It was an amazing experience and I feel so lucky to have went there and learn more about law. I graduated cum
Laude and had the best teachers! They taught me how to not give up and how important persistence can be!
Overall it is a very nice school, with up yo date tech which can be hard to find. Sometimes the food could be better but you get that from most other colleges and west chesters food is far from bad.
The food isn’t the best at the dining hall. Dorm living is very limited for upperclassmen, but the dorms are nice.

I really like how close everything is. Town is just about 10 minutes away from the main campus. The building are close to everythhing. Its not a very big campus which is nice. But the housing here is horrible. We dont have enought housing for all of our students which means that a lot of them have to either drop out or go homeless.
The town was cute, not too small of a size but not too big, great criminal justice program when i went. Hard to get lost honestly

Overall, the professors are all very kind and want to see you succeed. Everyone goes home on the weekends so it is pretty much a ghost town outside over the weekends. Housing is a complete mess for upperclassmen. Basically no guaranteed housing after freshman year. Food options are not great. Security is extremely strict, you can get charged a fee for being in the same room as someone violating student conduct - even if you did nothing wrong. Bags are checked every night after 8pm when entering the buildings.

West Chester University truly feels like home to me. I love absolutely everything about the campus, the opportunities, the students, the academics, the downtown and surrounding area, the spirit, the events - and I tell every single person I know about that (enthusiastically), which is also helpful when I do my prospective student tours as a WCU Student Ambassador!

I didn't enjoy my experience at West Chester University as much as I hoped. Unfortunately, the school wasn't very proactive when it came to protecting students from dangerous situations. There were always a lot of safely alerts being sent out to students via email called "timely warnings" these were sent on the daily basis. Sometime sent two or three times per day. It created a very alarming and anxiety inducing environment for myself and alot of the other students.
Overall, my experience at West Chester has been positive. My main complaint is the housing lottery system.

The area is beautiful and transportation is decent. You can find places pretty local to enjoy and the bus makes things fair fast as well. The academics are good but I wish the had more online classes to accommodate the commuter students, especially since they took away some commuter parking.

My experience here was satisfactory. Most professors were down to earth, and if not there were many other options for the same classes allowing you to choose someone else. The town is nice and is overall pretty safe.

ai_summary: West Virginia University offers a unique blend of academic rigor and vibrant student life , often described as a welcoming community where students can thrive both socially and academically. Many reviews highlight the supportive and engaging professors who genuinely care about student success, although there are mixed feelings about certain departments and course offerings due to recent budget cuts. The campus, nestled in scenic surroundings , provides a lively environment with plenty of activities, clubs, and Division I sports that foster school spirit. While there are concerns about the quality of facilities, including food and dorm conditions, students appreciate the accessibility of resources and transportation across campuses. Overall, WVU is seen as a place where students can forge lasting friendships while pursuing diverse educational opportunities , despite some challenges related to university administration and infrastructure.

reviews.text: I had a great and beneficial experience involving my academics at WVU. I have gained so many opportunities and chances to grow my skillset and knowledge. I believe I have been properly prepped for post-grad and job experiences.

West Virginia University is located in Morgantown, WV. The town is small but quaint. Most teachers at WVU are passionate about their courses and want to help students to succeed. For those who are interesting in a party scene the frats are very popular and there are many bars that are 18+. For those who aren't interested in those types of things WVU also has an event called UpAllNight, which takes place almost every Friday and Saturday night, that offers students all types of fun ways to spend the night that doesn't have to involve alcohol.

West Virginia University (WVU) is hands down the worst school ever. Classes were unnecessarily rigorous and most were unrelated to the major to get you to spend unnecessary amounts of money. Academics were practically non-existent. Students party on weekdays and are borderline alcoholic degenerates. Professors have relations with students. No diversity at all, very dangerous (frequent shootings, bomb threats, and violence). School is extraordinarily expensive for a bottom tier school in a poor state. Administration is extremely inefficient and unhelpful. Transportation is borderline impossible and inefficient. I can’t stress how bad of a school this is. This is coming from someone who went to two other schools/universities one of which was a community college and was 100000x better than this school. The administration is there to steal your money, and professors are there to have relationships with students. The party scene is there to rob you of your education. Avoid at ALL costs!!

Excellent vibes and student culture.
Tuition is high for out of state students.
Sadly they cut arts and other programs.

I;m not a huge fan of the school, the tution price isnt bad but defintly a big party school. The dorms very, downtown dorms tend to be bigger but communial bathrooms that arent up to date and no AC. newer dorms tend to be nicer but smaller. Academics are okay, many professors have strong accents but very smart. Dinning hall is okay but repeats many options

West Virginia University is a big school with a lot to offer for many different people. I would recommend it to someone who is willing to go out of their way to find their community. Due to the size and party nature of the school, it can be easy to feel isolated if you don’t fit it in with the average WVU student. Otherwise the nature and campus is beautiful and the academics are great. Cost of living is also very managable.

I will be a Senior in the nursing program for the fall of 2026 at West Virginia University (WVU) in Morgantown, WV. It is hard to believe that I will soon be a registered nurse. I was honored to be directly admitted to the nursing program as a freshman, where I completed my first year with a 4.0 GPA and earned a place on the President’s List for both semesters. In April 2024, I was inducted into the National Society of Collegiate Scholars. During the fall of 2024 and 2025, I maintained a 3.91/3.89 GPA and earned Dean’s List recognition. WVU has been a wonderful academic home, and I am excited to continue deepening my nursing education and practical experience. I am so glad I did my nursing experience at WVU. I am well prepared.
Some technical issues I’ve felt with. I believe I have the best room for me, great spot, can see the bus stop, close to the PRT, chill, and community.
Honestly my time so far at WVU has been good aside from my current living situation. To change that I will be living elsewhere next year, but I loved to see more fun clubs for hiking!

When applying to college, I wanted a party scene yet still get the academics I needed. WVU was the perfect place for that. The geography is beautiful and you get a good amount of snow. I did have a mold issue in my dorm but it didn't affect me too bad. The night life is also very enjoyable for those that want to go out on the weekend. Overall, I had a good experience at WVU.

West Virginia has been amazing at making new students feel welcomed and included. It showcases diversity and teamwork that can be used throughout every future job. They offer many different clubs, sports, and opportunities for students to get out there and be engaged. It has great academics during the day, and fun stress relievers at night with different clubs to attend and burn off steam. Overall, West Virginia has been adventurous, knowledgeable, and adaptive for my future career.

West Virginia was amazing for me because I enjoy the nature around and the college life on campus. The food in the dining hall is amazing with chick fil-a and panda express. The transports make it easy to get around campus very easy also. At night there are many bars and clubs to go to for the night and the frats there throw really big house parties. All the classes i need are walking distance and even if they weren't I would be able to take the transport over to which ever campus I needed and whatever part of the city.

Overall, West Virginia University is a great college. There are lots of opportunities for each individual student. One thing I would like to see change on campus is the dinning options. Us students pay a lot of money for a dining plan that is very subpar. The food is cold, going bad, or has no flavor. Other than the food there are no other major complaints I have for this college. It has been a great environment to be in during my freshman year.

I have truely been so blessed to be a student here. I truely have found this place to be my home. I've met some of my best friends here, and I've learned so much from being here the last three months. I've truely found a home here. I can't wait for the next three years here.
Casual racism is rampant I do not recommend please choose another school this is your warning. Transfering after a year and a half. Save the money and choose anywhere else.

ai_summary: Western Carolina University offers a unique blend of academic and outdoor experiences, set against the stunning backdrop of the Blue Ridge Mountains. Students appreciate the friendly community atmosphere , emphasizing small class sizes that foster personal connections with professors and peers. The campus hosts a variety of clubs and events, making it easy for both introverted and social students to find their niche. While the outdoor activities and scenic views are highlights, there's some concern about the food options, parking, and the relatively isolated location with limited off-campus entertainment. Many reviews underscore the supportive faculty and staff , alongside some calls for better diversity and communication from the administration. Overall, WCU presents itself as a welcoming, productive environment for students who value a close-knit community and a strong focus on academics and nature.

reviews.text: I thoroughly enjoy my time at Western Carolina University. The educators really care about you as a student and your success in your decided career field. The experience of being on a smaller campus really allows you to get to know your fellow students and experience student life to the fullest.

This is my first year at western Carolina university and so far I have loved it. I’m an interior design major and love the program. I have learned so much from it just in my first year. I am in one of the older dorms and so it doesn’t have A/C or a kitchen so that would be a big downside for me. Campus is beautiful and makes a wonderful university at whole.

Western Carolina University is a great school that's not huge in size, but has just the right amount of students to not overwhelm someone and still gives you the college experience. Although it is not located in a city, the surrounding area has numerous hikes to explore and a nice main street to walk on. WCU is also close to cities such as Asheville and Charlotte. Education on campus is good and the school is always holding some sort of activity in the University Center. Overall, WCU is a great place to go to college if you don't want a large student population and a lower tuition cost.

I really loved everything about Western. I was very hesitant because it was a smaller campus and I wanted a bigger campus but after orientation in June of 2025 I knew that it really was best for me. I've done a lot of things I didn't expect to do like be in a fraternity my freshman year and ending up being a founding father and the president. The classes and professors are good and most of them truly care. I would say its pretty safe but every now and then we have cases of sexual assault on campus so the safety could be a little better. The food is decent but it has a lot of options for everyone. The dorms are really nice especially the new freshman dorms.
Campus is absolutely beautiful, and I love that Cullowhee is so quiet. If you're ok with a town so small that the nearest McDonald's and Walmart are a whole town over, you'd love it here.
It is a pretty good university full of professors from diverse backgrounds. Its location is a little hard to get used to and the housing is not very good, but if you like the mountains it is great!

Western Carolina University (WCU) offers an amazing campus experience in the heart of North Carolina’s beautiful mountains. The campus is spacious, scenic, and well-maintained, with plenty of green spaces that make it a great place to study or relax. Students and staff are friendly and welcoming, creating a strong sense of community. There are lots of student organizations and events, making it easy to get involved and meet new people. The surrounding area, including Cullowhee and Sylva, offers charming coffee shops, local restaurants, and outdoor activities such as hiking and biking. With its combination of academic excellence, natural beauty, and a supportive environment, WCU is a fantastic place to learn, grow, and enjoy college life.

My overall experience at Western Carolina University has been very positive. The campus has a great atmosphere, and it feels like a place where people genuinely want to succeed. My professors have been supportive and clear with their expectations, which has made the transition into college much easier. I appreciate how many academic resources are available, from tutoring to office hours, and how willing faculty are to help when you reach out.
The campus community is also friendly and easy to connect with, and the location makes it a unique place to study since there are so many outdoor activities nearby. WCU offers a good balance between academics, social life, and personal growth. The only thing I would improve is communication from certain offices, since sometimes updates come late or aren’t fully explained. Overall, WCU has been a welcoming and motivating environment where I feel supported and encouraged to do well.

Western is a smaller university with a homelike feel. The professors are always willing to help. There are plenty of sports, outdoorsy activities, and special events on campus. I am double majoring and the amount of support I am receiving is amazing! Go Cats!

I am currently a WCU cheerleader and I absolutely love it, the team, the coaching, the environment. I also love all the things I have access to here as a student, compared to other colleges WCU is definitely one of the less expensive. But with all the free things we have access to including medical care and all the things it really is nice. The campus itself is beautiful. The professors I have had so far are absolutely amazing as well very helpful and most seem caring and like they actually want to be here and teach us.
Western Carolina university is a beautiful campus to study on and very inclusive of all those who attend.
Just like I expected my experience to be. Great food, studies, music, professors and student life....

Western Carolina University (WCU) is a vibrant and exciting place to be, offering a unique blend of academic excellence, stunning natural beauty, and a lively community spirit. Nestled in the scenic Smoky Mountains of North Carolina, WCU is a haven for outdoor enthusiasts. Students can hike, bike, or explore the great outdoors, all while enjoying breathtaking views. The campus itself is welcoming and full of life. With various events throughout the year, including cultural festivals, concerts, and sports games, there’s always something happening. The sense of community at WCU is palpable; students feel like a part of a big family. The university's strong traditions, like Homecoming and the annual Spring Fling, create cherished memories and foster camaraderie. Academically, WCU excels in various disciplines, from business to the arts and sciences. Students have access to passionate

I love the campus and the people. My classes have been good and not too boring. The food here was good, but it seems to have gone downhill a little; I think it is because of the workers. Overall, this is a great school, and I highly recommend it.
I generally like it, I live off campus so I'm not super involved with everything besides the academic aspect most professors are chill. Some are not but most employees are.

ai_summary: Western Kentucky University offers a unique combination of beauty, community, and academic opportunities that many students appreciate. The campus environment is frequently praised for its peacefulness and stunning scenery , which contributes to a welcoming atmosphere. Students mention a diverse range of majors and supportive professors who genuinely care about student success. However, experiences can vary; some students find the social scene overly focused on parties and express frustration with certain aspects like dorm conditions and financial aid responses. The school fosters a strong sense of community , with numerous clubs and organizations available, though some students feel off-campus activities and resources could improve. Overall, WKU is recommended for its blend of academic engagement and social life, but potential students should be aware of the challenges some face regarding housing and academic support.

reviews.text: Western Kentucky is a very flexible school. They've worked with me and made life easier. The dean of student sent groceries to my house one time

WKU is a disaster. The Professors have no clue as to what’s going on and rarely available to assist you in the classes. I have dealt with multiple advisors who set me up with wrong schedules or poor choice of classes. This horrendous advising caused me to delay my graduation by 2 YEARS! Stay away from WKU if you can or you’ll end up advising yourself, teaching yourself, and going into debt all on yourself. The WKU has zero care for its students.

I have enjoyed how classes that I've taken the professors know my name and they are interested in getting to know you. I have taken the time to get an on campus job which I love so much it is very enjoyable and I get to connect with a bunch of people each day. My best friends are people I've met in classes not in Greek Life. I've never worried about my safety on campus not because things don't happen but because we have PD right near by and they are able to get anywhere on campus within just a few minutes.

My experience at Western Kentucky University (WKU) has been very positive. I really enjoy the beauty of the campus it’s a pleasant place to walk, study, or just spend time. One of the best things about WKU is that you don’t have to be part of a group or organization to enjoy campus life. Even if you’re not very involved, there are plenty of quiet spots and events you can enjoy on your own.

My overall experience at Western Kentucky University has been incredibly positive. I’ve found the campus to be welcoming, the professors supportive, and the academic environment motivating. The Health Sciences program, in particular, has provided me with a strong foundation in healthcare and hands-on learning experiences that have helped me grow both academically and personally. I’ve also appreciated the many opportunities for involvement whether through volunteering, student organizations, or leadership roles that have allowed me to connect with others who share similar goals.
I love western Kentucky online courses. They are very understanding and helping even though we are not on campus.
I enjoy the architecture of the buildings; they're beautiful. I enjoy the night view, especially since it's really pretty.

I've really enjoyed my time at western, the only issue that I've had so far is, due to the fact that I am a minority, I do feel threatened when the school allows extremist groups to do things such as set up booths and have info sessions etc.

I really love my professors and classes, they make my college career really interesting and enjoyable. The disabilities office is also super helpful, I had no issues applying and receiving my accommodations. The food options are good but has incredibly long waits at anywhere that's not the campus buffet.

Overall, WKU is a really good undergraduate school. There are a lot of different majors offered, the food is good, I've heard there's a good party scene (though I don't partake) and it seems that there's always small events going on. I would like to see the advertisement for such events to improve, since I usually only hear about them after they've occurred or from other people.

Western Kentucky University is a great experience. The professors really want to help you learn, there's a decent student community, and the local town is really focused on the athletics, game days and tailgates are a big thing here!

WKU is a great university. Overall, the professors have been good. There are many activities and clubs to be involved in. Students should only be required to live on campus their 1st year not their 2nd year too. I would change the HIGH cost. The fees and tuition went up making it a big struggle for some students to pay. Need more funding and ways for students to pay for college. The university has a lot to offer and provides academic support.

The campus is lovely and my professors are very welcoming. The housing is okay but is being upgraded in the next few years. The food is okay but I’d like to see some improvement there. Overall I’ve had a great time so far

My experience at Western Kentucky University has been very eventful. The campus offers incredible diversity, strong academics, valuable resources, and a vibrant social life that has made my time here truly memorable.

Western Kentucky University has such a beautiful campus, and it gives you such an "at home" feeling. Everyone there is so welcoming and so nice. You always have somebody that is willing to help you and succeed.

ai_summary: Western Michigan University presents a diverse college environment with a blend of solid academics and active student life. Many students appreciate the approachable and helpful professors who prioritize their success, although there are mixed reviews about the consistency in teaching quality. The campus is described as beautiful and well-kept, with a friendly atmosphere that facilitates social connections, although some students note challenges in meeting peers if living off-campus. There are numerous clubs and extracurricular activities to engage in, enhancing the college experience. The dining options receive praise for variety and quality, yet some students express dissatisfaction with specific facilities. Additionally, while the campus is seen as generally safe, concerns about safety off-campus and some outdated dormitory conditions are highlighted. Overall, students enjoy a unique sense of community and a range of resources that support their academic and social endeavors at WMU.

reviews.text: I was an aviation flight science major originally, but I switched to mechanical engineering so I transferred to a better program. The aviation program was world class, aviation just wasn't for me. All of the buildings on campus were amazing especially the aviation campus. I found great resources throughout campus, and being in the honors college gave me a lot of opportunities for social events, as well as smaller class sizes and priority registration. The RSO's were great and I made a lot of friends through them even though I'm not the most social person. The dining hall food was usually pretty good and had a decent amount of variety. The downfalls of the school mostly come with its location and party scene. Kalamazoo doesn't really have much to do and its not the safest area. And other than the frats there aren't a ton of good parties. Kalamazoo is only like an hour from the lake and grand rapids though, and there's the Amtrak to Chicago.

I generally had a good experience! I attended WMU when the Covid-19 pandemic hit; initially, I felt worried about how they would handle the situation, but by Fall 2020 semester the kinks had been mostly smoothed out.
It was fantastic. All the students, teachers, and staff are so opening and kind. It feels like a home away from home

WMU has a very safe student centered campus. Campus was always very full of life and provided lots of opportunity to meet new people. The class sizes for major courses were often 30 or less which made the academic experience very personal and manageable.

I had enrolled as a Non degree student last year and had a great experience. The professors were excellent and knowledgeable, however, i would like to see the professors spend more time with students one on one addressing their issues.
I love the experience that I am having at Western Michigan University. I feel that the University takes me and my education seriously, which has helped me succeed in my courses and stay on track.

I had a great time at western, everything from student life to the professors was good at western, and I'm actually planning on transferring back up there pretty soon. I left originally because I was just unsure of what I really wanted to pursue, but I'm still so sure Western is the best option for me.
Its a nice clean campus the courses are educational and not to hard if you study the teachers are helpful and very supportive.
I like Kalamazoo, I just miss my family. It is hard moving to a new city and struggling to make friends.

I would really love to see Western Michigan University introduce a better online directory. I've watched many students, including myself, struggle to book advising appointments, schedule classes, pay tuition, find housing, and so many more through our GO WMU webpage.

As a recent alum of Western Michigan University, I've had a pretty good overall experience! The campus life and opportunities definitely stand out. That said, I'd be remiss if I didn't mention some inconsistencies with professors—some departments had amazing faculty, while others had issues ranging from inappropriate comments to a bit of a laissez-faire attitude. Just something to keep in mind, but overall still a good journey!
Chose Western Michigan University for its supportive community, strong programs, and vibrant campus life.

It's a fine school. What I like about this college is how easy it is to find friends, as well as the relationships that can be made with the professors and instructors here. What I don't like is how difficult it can be to navigate some of their services. I feel like I go in circles on their website and nothing is easy to find. Another issue is the parking for students. $300 a year and it's always a fight to find space to park. The staff have much more parking than the students do, and there are many times where I see empty spaces for staff parking while there are multiple cars in the student section waiting for people to leave so they can make it to class on time. It's also not a very accessible campus for students who may be disabled, as it is a very hilly campus. It feels like it might be a struggle for those who are wheelchair bound to navigate through the main campus. That being said, I do really enjoy the classes, and I do believe the professors and advisors are extremely helpful.

My experience with WMU has been fairly all good. The student organizations are large in number and have a wide variety of interests to explore. There is a group for everyone and activities to do always. I think the community could have more events but that comes with funding. I appreciate the way students advocate for each other and the services offered by campus. The housing off campus is nice, on campus I had a poor experience. The food is very good, however eating anything consistently will get annoying. Overall college is what you make it and Western is a place you can make great memories.

It's a large school with great resources and wonderful professors. Like any school, however, it has its flaws. The housing here is expensive, and you aren't getting what you're paying for. I'm lucky to be here, but there would be plenty of changes if students had more of a say.

ai_summary: At Western New England University, students appreciate the supportive and friendly community fostered by small class sizes and approachable professors who genuinely care about their success. The campus is noted for its beauty and is well-kept, creating an inviting atmosphere for both study and social activities. Many students enjoy the variety of events and clubs available, although there are calls for more diverse offerings and improvement in campus dining options. Academically, the experience is often praised, especially in programs like Biomedical Engineering and Neuroscience, where personalized learning is emphasized. However, some reviews highlight challenges with administration, financial issues, and a lack of vibrant student life , particularly on weekends. Overall, WNE presents a nurturing environment for those seeking a close-knit college experience, albeit with areas needing attention and growth.

reviews.text: I thoroughly enjoyed my time at Western New England University. I was apart of the schools athletics being on the volleyball team, and loved every minute of it. WNE is a smaller school with about 2500 undergrad students, which makes it feel homey. Every professor I had knew me by name and I felt I had a personal relationship with. I would 100% choose to go to WNE time and time again!
The professors were amazing at working with you to make school work for you. They advocated for me and helped me be the best student I could be

My review on Western New England is that people need to start being more kinder, and the school needs more diversity there’s a lot of racism here!! My 2nd week of college I experienced a white group calling me a racial slur and as well as going to a party two boys asked if I know Jim Crow laws. I feel it’s normalized but it should not be the school needs to do better and give consequences because in the real business world some do not accept people like that.
So far I've had an amazing experience at WNEU. I'm in the 0-6 pharmacy program as well as the MSPGx program. They've been very good at accommodating my needs.

The academics in this school is pretty solid and the professors are very helpful and passionate about their jobs. But outside of that, the school is not as great in terms of social life. It is a small school in a dry city. Also, racial diversity is very low. Nevertheless, I would want improvement in the campus food

My overall experience with Western New England University so far has been pretty great. It is super easy to make friends since the school is much smaller and more tight knit. As a commuter, I personally still find plenty of stuff to do while I'm there and don't feel like I'm missing out too much when I'm not. There isn't much I would personally change on campus. However, it is important to note that there is not much around the area to do.

My experience with Western New England University has been one of the main reasons I feel confident about my future. From the moment I first visited campus, I felt welcomed and supported. Everyone I met—students, faculty, and staff—made the school feel like a place where I could grow both academically and personally.
What stood out to me the most was how much the university cares about its students. Every conversation I had felt genuine, and I could tell that the people there truly want students to succeed. The environment is friendly, and it made me feel comfortable asking questions and exploring everything the school has to offer.
Overall, Western New England University made me feel inspired and supported. It gave me a sense of belonging, even before officially becoming a student. My experience has only strengthened my motivation to continue my education there and to take advantage of everything the university offers to help me reach my goals.

WNE has definitely gone downhill. The school has fired half the campus police and cleaning staff. Loads of favoritism when it comes to applying for ResLife position. Most RAs don’t care enough to do their job or even show up for their shifts. Freshman year I could not study in my dorm because girls would scream up and down the halls until midnight during exam week and our RA didn’t care/was nowhere to be found. I lived in a suite in LaRiv my sophomore year and it was pretty much more of the same. Plus the washers and dryers were almost always broken. Food at dhall is pretty basic too, usually the same options everyday. The school does hold a lot of events which is nice to attend and make friends. Professors are either really good or really bad. I’ve had a few who I loved and were always willing to help me, others who were more strict and unreasonable, but every college has its good and bad. The small class sizes are a huge benefit and allows you to really get to know your professors.
It's a nice college if you like small universities and like to know a lot of people easily. It also is not as fun as intended around campus.

Western New England University is a great school for the price. The professors are very nice and are passionate about what they teach. The staff is efficient and willing to work with you through any problems you may have. The campus is the perfect size, not too small and not too big.

I was a student leader in more ways than one, so I saw and learned a lot more about WNE compared to your average student. From what I've seen and heard since graduating, it appears to be going downhill. Loads of favoritism in different departments, especially in ResLife. Complaints aren't taken seriously, and there's no punishment for misconduct. Sure, freeing, but for those of us there to study and begin our adult lives, it was incredibly frustrating. If you're not a resident student, it's great. But if you plan on being a resident, consider finding housing elsewhere and not getting a dorm on campus. Campus police, last I heard, is down to four full-time officers, and it's now mostly made of community service officers (we love them though). Overall, a solid but expensive option, though I'd mostly like to see admin change how they treat students; treat them like up-and-coming adults rather than children.
usually there is only one place to go on friday and one place to go on Saturday and its always the same people
western new england university is a good school, but people scream in the dorms and are loud for no reason, and very immature. the proffesors are good, but the workload is a lot.

WNEU is a private university in Springfield, Massachusetts. Like many private schools, the sticker price is high, but there is a significant amount of scholarship, merit aid, and financial aid available. Whether it becomes affordable depends a lot on your academic performance, financial need, and whether you take advantage of all the aid options.

Western New England University (WNE) offers a transformative educational experience marked by supportive faculty and a close-knit community. Students thrive in small classes with personalized attention from professors passionate about teaching. The campus exudes warmth and beauty, fostering collaboration and growth. With diverse academic programs and over 70 clubs, students engage deeply in pursuits aligning with their passions. Career services effectively bridge academia and professional success. Located in Springfield, Massachusetts, WNEvbalances academic rigor with opportunities for cultural and professional enrichment, shaping well-rounded graduates poised for success. WNE cultivates an environment where students flourish academically and personally within a nurturing setting.

ai_summary: Wichita State University offers a community-focused academic environment with a diverse student body and a variety of extracurricular activities. Many reviews highlight the supportive faculty and the accessibility of advisors, making it easier for students to navigate their educational paths. However, some students express concerns about rising tuition costs, parking issues, and the need for improved campus facilities . The campus is described as beautiful and walkable, with nearby amenities, though some feel that it lacks a vibrant party scene and strong school spirit. Overall, students appreciate the opportunities for engagement and learning at WSU, particularly in programs like nursing and engineering, though experiences can vary greatly depending on individual professors and specific programs.

reviews.text: My experience so far at Wichita State has been really good so far, though I'm just finishing up my first year at the university. The professors I've had so far are knowledgeable in their subjects. There is a hitch once in while but that's apart of studying and asking questions. The food on average is pretty good. My maintenance requests have been answered and our housing emergency (room flooded) was handled rather quickly. If a resource is required, the university, I believe, does a good job of marketing their services.

WSU is the definition of a middle-ground, average school. It's fine. There's nothing special about it; no party scene, a dreary student life, run of the mill athletic teams and no football. I've had a fairly good experience with all my classes and my professors have all been enjoyable, but outside of that the school itself is so unbearably boring it drives you insane. It's not in a good location, either, and the only entertaining things to do in Wichita are all downtown and you better pray you don't get hit by one of the abysmal Wichita drivers on the highway.

My experience with Wichita State University is great. I like going to some sports events, mostly men basketball with some of my friends. The student section is great overall, where some of still kept on cheering regardless of being down or overall score. My experience with parking varies, depending on what day and what time. I suggest to have earliest class of the day since there are more space to park compare to later classes needing to search for parking spots. The Rhatigan Student Center is pretty neat, nothing too fancy. They have Panda Express, Chick-Fil-A, Freddy's, Starbucks, sushi and some asian foods. For me, they are as good as local restaurants but some menus or coupons are limited on campus. WSU also have different food trucks coming 2-3 times per week, and I highly suggest to check it out for quick order and less people. One thing I love about WSU is diversity, where many international students or exhange students applied here for education purposes and participations.

I enjoyed my college years at Wichita State University. The disability services were very accommodating to me. Professors and students on campus were very friendly. I felt safe when I lived in the dorms and walking on campus to class. The food was good. The only thing I would change was to expand the parking lot, but Wichita State already took care of that after I graduated. Overall, I had a good experience attending WSU and have fond memories of friends and the classes I took there.
I am excited to grown and learn at WSU. I am excited to study abroad. I would like better dining hall food and more connections.
I think my teachers were the most important part of my education. They genuinely cared for all my out of school work related pursuits, and gave me their most thoughtful useful feedback.

Wichita State has been a great place to get my bearings as I prepare for what’s next. They cherish each step of the way, including the alumni step, where they still provide support.
I really loved how diverse the programs and students were and how that helped me to be more prepared as a social worker.
I do wish they had the capacity to do more online or flexible courses, but I knew that coming into it.

I'm happy with my experience at WSU. My teachers are very nice and helpful. The international office, my advisor makes us feel we are like home as well my teachers at BSB. I wish we have scholarships for master's students.

I have been at Wichita State University (WSU) for two years now and I have really enjoyed it. Our campus has a lot of resources for students and many safety tools if needed. The stuff here is very welcoming and eager to help any student that may be struggling with the class. Along with that, tougher classes provide a SI leader for those who need tutoring or need help to better understand the material. I think WSU does an amazing job at hosting events to get people connected and help students get a foot in the door for their future.

I had a blast. It was tremendously formative time in my life. I got involved in Greek Life. Planned and hosted a lot of parties, won some intramural championships, went to a lot of shocker games and a lot more. Made great friends, learned what it meant to be a part of something bigger than myself, to lead and to follow. Met my wife in the dorms. I wouldn't change a thing.

I am an international student studying at Wichita State University, far from my family, and especially my daughter. This time has been emotionally and financially hard for me. However, at Wichita State University, I found a family that supported me every step of the way. My studies, social life, cultural adaptation, and overall adjustment went smoothly, thanks to the support of my friends and professors who guided me through immigration processes and adjustment programs. I met peers from all over the world, which helped me connect with other international students and gain experience in sharing and learning about different cultures.
As an international student, while adjusting to a new academic and cultural environment, there are always financial challenges, which have inspired and motivated me to stay focused to meet my goals and remain determined to succeed despite financial hardships, and it taught me to work hard, remain disciplined, and focus on long-term goals.
The one thing I would like changed is the cafeteria food. Some days it’s good but other days I have trouble finding good food.

My experience at Wichita State University has been very positive overall. The campus feels welcoming, the community is diverse, and there are plenty of opportunities to get hands-on experience through internships and real projects. I’ve appreciated the supportive professors and the modern feel of the Innovation Campus. If anything could improve, it would be having more campus activities on weekends, more consistent advising across departments, and a little easier parking during busy times. Overall, WSU is a friendly place to learn and grow.
Campus has lots of activities going on which is nice, YMCA and heskett offers lots of physical activities
Wichita State University has been a great and absolutely amazing home away from home, the only thing I would change is the food at the dining hall, there aren’t you many options available

ai_summary: Widener University receives a mix of praise and criticism from students. Many appreciate the small class sizes and dedicated professors , which create opportunities for personalized education and meaningful interactions. The sense of community is frequently highlighted, with students feeling welcomed and supported, particularly in groups and activities. However, concerns are raised about the overall cost of attendance and the quality of food, with some students finding it repetitive and below expectations. Campus safety and reliability of services like the shuttle are also points of contention. Despite these challenges, the university is recognized for its strong nursing program and other academic offerings that prepare students for their careers. Some students express that improvements could be made in communication and updating facilities, especially to enhance the overall student experience.

reviews.text: I have had a profound learning experience. After only a year of being a student here, I already feel more knowledgeable. I feel that I am well-rounded because of my general education as they’ve helped me understand the world and societies we live in. The College of Arts and Sciences makes learning very fun. I have met some of the best professors here who are always willing to help.

My experience at Widener University has been very meaningful so far. As a Biology major on the physical therapy pathway and a member of the track and field team, I have grown both academically and personally. Being part of the track team has helped me build strong relationships and develop discipline, time management, and perseverance while balancing athletics and coursework.
One thing I appreciate about Widener is the supportive community. My professors are approachable and willing to help students succeed, and opportunities like serving as a PRIDE Mentor have allowed me to support other students and help them feel connected on campus.
As a commuter student, I would like to see more opportunities and daytime events that help commuter students stay engaged and involved in campus life. Overall, Widener has helped me grow into a more motivated and disciplined individual, and I am grateful for the opportunities the university has given me.

I've enjoyed my experience at Widener so far. They have many resources to help students. Academically, I have been doing well, and the teachers are nice and willing to help when they can. Many teachers are even willing to help after school/office hours. Their student success office has also been helpful as I have been looking to level up career-wise. They have many clubs to get involved in. Overall, I am satisfied with my experience.

I really love attending Widner university, but my parents can’t afford to keep sending me even with the FASFA its very expensive still especially because my father was out of work half of the year due to sickness. This money would truly help me pay for my education.. I want to be the first in my family to graduate college and make my parents proud.

Overall, I think Widener is a great school with great programs and good professors. The people on campus are very welcoming. I think that the cost is very expensive and I would hope to get more money through financial aid.
Good School. Plenty of good professors and very nice campus. Quite boring student life. Food is alright and the general location is pretty nice

My experience at widener so far has been good . The school comes with many recourses to help you archive your goals . The community here is very strong with many weekly events happening. I only attended one party and it was fun wouldn't recommend widener if you're looking for a party school.
I liked the diversity of persons and cultures seen on campus, but disliked the way students acted while partiying or during the night

Widener is full of diversity, opportunities to make friends, and engage in community. Everyone is so inclusive and want the absolute best for you. Everyone wants to see you thrive and succeed. As they say, "We are all Widener." This meaning we are all family. I love how welcoming and caring everyone are. As far as academics go, the professors really appreciate their students and give them their all. They put in effort because they care for their students like they are their own kids. They always greet you with a smile on their faces, an energy ready to make a new day a good day, and an excitement to teach you new things. As well as academic advisors who really help to guide you on the right academic path for your major and interests.

I left Widener University due to serious issues with housing, academics, and overall value. The buildings had rodent problems, and maintenance was slow or unresponsive. For how expensive on-campus living was, the quality didn’t match the price. Meal plans were also overpriced, and the food was consistently low quality. On top of that, staff in housing and admin offices were often unprofessional and unhelpful. I was in the nursing program, and I noticed early on that some professors seemed unqualified. There were also frequent academic errors—like misspelled medications and diagnoses—which are serious problems in a healthcare education setting. When I raised concerns, the response was basically, “They’re human and make mistakes,” which I found unacceptable. Healthcare education demands a high standard of accuracy and accountability, and I couldn’t stay in a program that didn’t reflect that. I highly recommend considering another school for a better experience.

As a proud alumni of Widener, I have to say I had the best experience! I met some of my best friends and created lasting memories. I feel Widener has prepared me for my career through my personal connections I made with my awesome faculty and support staff. I was able to travel internationally for the first time thanks to Widener. The people are friendly and I felt like a really grew as a student and young adult. My best advice to new students is to get involved in as much as possible: sports, clubs, orgs, internships or co-ops, travel, greek life, volunteer work, campus jobs, and more. Doing so will build your resume, gain friends, keep your schedule full, and keep you connected to the campus.

I enjoy attending Widener. The academics are strong, and the professors are knowledgeable and supportive. I’m learning a lot in my classes and my teachers are helping me be successful. When my grades started to slip in one class, my professor reached out. She offered to talk, gave me extensions, and made sure I was aware of the support available on campus.
Outside the classroom, there are a lot of opportunities to get involved. I’m in Activities Council, Black Student Union, a faith-based club, a medical club, and the campus radio station. One of the things I really appreciate is that even underclassmen can take on leadership roles.
If I had one critique, it’s that the campus is small and the surrounding area isn’t lively. There’s not much within walking distance, and sometimes it can get repetitive on campus. Still, I’m really grateful to attend. I still feel like I’m getting the full college experience, it just comes with a few locational limits.

I have a complicated relationship with my school. I love how big on academics my school is due to having a lot of help around campus to make sure students succeed. Although it is a good school some things they can do better at is be more responsive, better food in cafe, more food options, helpful campus safety staff, and many other things.
I enjoy the smaller campus, comfortable environment. However, there is limited housing for the sizes of classes, and can be difficult to find answers to your questions/ self advocate.

This was the school I always wanted to attend and once I got here it did not let me down. This has been a great experience so far when it comes to everything such as education, living on campus , and events. I really hope I can spend the rest of my 4 years here at widener.

ai_summary: Wilkes University offers a tightly-knit campus experience that resonates with students who appreciate smaller class sizes and personalized attention from faculty. Many reviews highlight the supportive environment where professors are dedicated to student success and often know students by name, fostering a community feel. The range of academic programs, including hands-on opportunities , especially in fields like pharmacy and engineering, is praised, though some programs have drawn criticism for lacking organization or clarity. Student life is vibrant, with numerous extracurricular activities and events, though the surrounding city of Wilkes-Barre is noted for safety concerns. On the downside, students point out the high tuition costs and some dissatisfaction with food quality and certain professors. Overall, Wilkes University might appeal to those seeking a supportive, community-oriented college experience with solid academic offerings.

reviews.text: after touring the campus it seems amazing and everyone was so very nice! i absolutely loved the school!
Wilkes is a pretty nice college and has a nice guaranteed seat pharmacy program. It is also close to a lot of places to eat, work or have fun.

I really like this university and I've wanted to go here since I was a kid. I love my The program and all my professors are great. it's a heavy workload however but i think it's just the program I'm in. I do not play sports, but I've heard good things about the athletics here. I also don't dorm, but I've heard that the buildings flood a lot or the fire alarm goes off, which would just make me mad. i love it here though I definitely made a good choice

As a coming freshman, I have enjoyed the convenience on the campus. As a student it is very important to be able to get to your classes, the dormitories and even the athletics department. The Professor's are all willing to help me get to that extra mile.

there are lots of activities. The area around the school is slowly being revitalized but is a little downtrodden. Sports programs are good. Some of the professors are hard to understand due to language accents

I am given many opportunities to expand on my horizons but unfortunately I find that it is hard to receive enough financial help to be able to remotely comfortably go to college there. I am entering into my Junior year and have come face to face with having to consider the possibility of having to drop out. But other than that the General Education professors are wonderful teachers and the student life is so fun. There are often bingo nights with incredible prizes that really brings everyone together.

Wilkes provided me with an extensive opportunity to develop as an individual. The programs and friends I have developed and maintained is wonderful. My coursework was diverse and gave me a chance to learn. I defintely feel I was afforded much education and job growth.

Wilkes is a good college, my first year in college at wilkes was rocky at first, but it overall came along really well at the end. i think i would like to see a little bit more diversity at the college, like more sports (track and field, women's wrestling), student diversity, etc.

As a transfer student from a local community college, Wilkes was the only University I truly wanted to attend. The application process was easy and everyone was so welcoming. From professors to support staff, even current students, their kindness and assistance was extraordinarily helpful! None of my questions went unanswered and there were, and still are, so many opportunities to learn and better yourself at Wilkes. I’m beyond proud to be a Colonel and cannot wait to see where it takes me!

Only just started this summer semester. So far first course is ok, took course cause advisor said others enjoyed it. I guess if you like to do research and learn about research its your thing. Me I find the course boring. Tho I do look forward to seeing what the other course may offer.
Wilkes University offers a lot of fun student events throughout the year and there are different types of student clubs and organizations for making new friends and networking opportunities.

Wilkes University has given me amazing opportunities throughout the past four years. I have grown personally, as a student, and as a leader. I will be able to take all of my new life skills with me in my future job to be the best employee and person I can be. Wilkes also has so many extracurriculars to get involved in that allow for a good study break and some fun!

Wilkes has been, for me, what I want most out of college. Wilkes has catered opportunities and encouraged everyone to carve the paths that work best for them. The campus life and community are amazing, the academic opportunities rigorous and diverse, and overall Wilkes has been one of the best things to happen to me.

Here at Wilkes University, I’m a nursing major, so passing the NCLEX after these 4 years is very important to me. Wilkes has a recent NCLEX pass rate of 100% due to changes in the curriculum. The board for nursing is always trying to find new ways to perfect the curriculum for both student enjoyment and AACN standards. Wilkes offers so much for their students when it comes to events and activities that range from building resumes to general entertainment. On top of career fairs and student development, Wilkes has on campus job opportunities. I’m a part of Admissions as an ambassador and I love the work environment. It’s nice to be a part of families’ first impression of our campus. Things that are getting better include the food in the dining hall. When I was here as a freshman, it was not great. Now that there’s new management, I think the food quality is a lot better. I chose Wilkes because of the nursing program, but I’ve fallen more for this campus every year since.

My time at Wilkes University was filled with numerous opportunities and very helpful resources. The professors were incredibly and compassionate. The advisors wanted the best for me and it showed in every meeting. I would recommend anyone interested in studying hospitality leadership to apply there!

ai_summary: William & Mary offers a unique college experience characterized by a welcoming and tight-knit community . Many students appreciate the rigorous academic environment , citing passionate professors who genuinely care about their students' success and the opportunity for personalized attention due to smaller class sizes. While the scenic campus and the historic charm of Williamsburg enhance the overall experience, concerns about the quality of food and housing persist, with many reviews noting ongoing renovations and improvements. The social scene is described as friendly and inclusive, although some students feel it lacks vibrancy compared to larger universities. Despite mixed reviews about administration responsiveness and campus services, most students express a strong sense of belonging and connection, making William & Mary feel like home.
