reviews.text: I have really enjoyed my William and Mary experience so far. The student life here is great. The dining could definitely be improved but overall good.
Small school but definitely a tribe! Lots of great activities and top tier academics. Great liberal arts education overall and nice tight knit community and beautiful campus.

I love w and m dining
I love w and m dining
I love w and m dining
I love w and m dining
I love w and m dining
Very classical campus with great professors. Students always get opportunities to do research or whatever thing they want to try out.
Very good experience!! I have enjoyed my time here!! I really like it here!! I made lots and friends with many beautiful people!!
I love William & Mary I love William & Mary I love William & Mary I love William & Mary I love William & Mary
It’s very nice here. I really really like it here and love Every day being here. I’m so happy to be here
Loved the school, great opportunity and wonderful time meeting people. Sometimes want more when comparing to VT or JMU, but I am constantly reminded of how happy I am being here
Great diversity. Fun place to be in. Offers lot of activities and interesting research directions with friendly professors
If you are willing to get over the (honestly minor) hurdle of the subpar housing and food experience, you will be treated to an amazing university.

Elite, historic, current. Carnegie R1 research institution, incredible opportunities for political science and journalism in Washington DC, personal relationships with professors, vibrant social community and clubs, and grounded intellectual study body. The new dorms and dining hall are stunning, and campus and surrounding area is pristinely beautiful.

I think it is an incredible institution. Amazing programs, staff, and opportunities for students. They need to work more on bridging the divide between students and the surrounding area, but the campus itself is wonderful!
I enjoy it being a student and going to the college and the teacher are very great and grateful and the food service is very good and I enjoy eating
I enjoy William and Mary because the professors are cool and helpful and the clubs are nice, im also president for one
Very safe, fun and thriving social scene, wonderful professors. Food is a 50/50. Party scene is subpar, but that’s okay.

ai_summary: William Carey University offers a close-knit and supportive learning environment , particularly benefiting students looking for personalized attention from professors in smaller class settings. Many reviews highlight the dedication and approachability of faculty , emphasizing how they genuinely care about student success. Academic programs, especially in nursing and education, are recognized as strong and well-priced for the quality of education received. While the Christian atmosphere and community involvement are seen as positives, some students express a desire for improved campus amenities , such as cafeteria food and more social activities. The campus's small size makes it easy to navigate, though some feel it contributes to a quieter student life on weekends. Tuition is viewed as somewhat expensive, yet many appreciate the financial aid options available. Overall, William Carey is noted for its friendly atmosphere, academic rigor, and community spirit, though some organizational aspects could be improved.

reviews.text: William Carey University is great for students who may be more shy or enjoy much smaller class sizes. The professors seem okay, they could be better. The building is clean and nice to look at. The campus size is smaller.

Administration SUCKS! NEVER REPLY TO YOU. ACT LIKE THEY DON'T WANT YOU TO COME TO WILLIAM CAREY. THEY NEVER ANSWER EMAILS OR PHONE CALLS. THEY ARE ALL OVER THE PLACE. NEED TO HIRE NEW ADMINISTRATION AND REGISTRAR STAFF (BOTH MS CAMPUSES).
Easy to apply with help from such wonderful advisors that is willing to assist. Great parking for students.

I love William Carey university not only this school is not student that jut graduated high school but it’s for the non traditional students like myself a mom of three kids with disabilities. WCU makes education their top priority and they want their students to succeed. The professors explain things for you to understand and they are available if you need to discuss a lesson, tutoring is available also.

William Carey is exactly where I belong. At this university, I found the most incredible group of caring, Godly friends, I have the most incredible professors who care about me and expose me to incredible opportunities. Because of the small size of the campus, my professors know me by name. They know my career goals and are willing to help me reach them.
My experience at William Carey University has been wonderful. I love how flexible the online class is and I’m still able to work as well.

I love online classes because they offer flexibility and convenience. I can learn at my own pace and schedule my study time around other responsibilities. There’s no need to commute, which saves time and money. Online learning provides access to a wide range of resources and tools, making studying more engaging and effective. I can revisit recorded lectures anytime, which helps reinforce my understanding. Virtual discussions also allow more thoughtful participation. Most importantly, online classes empower me to take control of my education and build self-discipline, making learning more personal and productive. It fits perfectly with my lifestyle and goals.

William Carey has so far been a lovely experience, and I intend to continue going here for quite some time. The professors are helpful, the campus is clean and easy to navigate, and the faculty are very friendly.
I am a new student who basically does everything online for now. Which works perfectly for my life right now. What I have encountered so far has been great from my advisor to admissions.

William Carey is a solid choice if you’re looking for a smaller, faith-based university with a strong academic focus. The professors genuinely care about students, and the small class sizes make it easy to get help when you need it. The school is especially known for programs like nursing, education, and business.
Campus life has a close-knit feel, so it’s easy to make connections with both classmates and faculty. While it’s not a big party school, there are still plenty of ways to get involved, whether through clubs, student organizations, or mission work. The Christian atmosphere is a big part of the school’s culture, so it’s a good fit if that’s something you value.
The facilities aren’t as fancy as those at bigger schools, but they’re well-kept and improving over time. One major plus is the affordability—Carey provides a quality education without the massive price tag.
Overall, if you want a supportive, faith-centered college experience, William Carey is definitely worth it.
i’ve found my home here!! i love the carey community and i can’t recommend this college more. i came here for my nursing degre!

As a student at William Carey University, I can confidently say that choosing this school was one of my best decisions. The small class sizes and dedicated professors create a personalized learning experience, where faculty truly care about your success. The university’s Christian atmosphere provides opportunities for spiritual growth alongside academic achievement, making it a place where faith and learning go hand in hand. The campus community is incredibly welcoming, and there are countless ways to get involved, from clubs to service projects. The resources available to students, like the STEM lab, enhance the educational experience. If you’re looking for a supportive environment where you can grow academically, spiritually, and personally, William Carey is the perfect place. It’s a place where you’re not just a student—you’re part of a community that values faith, service, and excellence.

Overall I have had a great experience so far with my education here at William Carey as a transfer student. I am a full time online student, but due to my courses I am taking I am required to be present on campus at least once throughout each term. Even with this minor inconvenience it is still very accommodating for someone who is working full time and attending college at the same time. I would absolutely recommend William Carey University to anyone who is planning to further their education and is having issues with attending classes in person due to their work schedule.
William Carey has been the absolute best! I previously attended another college, and I was very unhappy there, so I left and went to WCU!

I really like they had did different activities going on, especially when it came to test week, and many other activities going on through out the school year. I also like how nice, and inviting everyone is. Also everyone make sure to make you feel comfortable , not matter if you are touring the campus, or if you are just spending your free time socializing in activities, or just exploring and meeting new friends on campus.
I which they give students more grants once they graduates or sone type of scholarship that would help those who are planning to continue on in their career. I wish they would give students qt least a semester without pay, so students could decide if that is something they really want to do, or give them a percentage off of their tuition for that semester.

I really enjoyed the people I’ve met! Most have become my best friends or simply helped me get the most out of my experience here at William Woods University. The life at woods and the people you meet is what really brought me here. The professors are wonderful and truly help you out and want the best for you. Our sports just got in a new conference so the records should be better within the next 5 years, but could make people stray away. Overall, the schools organization with academics, financial aid could be better
Great for online programs! In-person needs significant renovation for the current price, included updated residential halls and food offerings. Not allergy-friendly.

I’ve been trying to re-enroll in the Legal Studies program at William Woods and keep hitting walls. I followed every step—submitted the re-entry form, made payments, reached out to multiple departments—and still can’t get clear answers on when the next term starts, what classes I need, or if my tuition rate is still locked in. Instead of answers, I’m passed around or ignored. Advising, financial aid, and other staff either deflect or don’t reply at all. I’m trying to plan my finances and coursework to stay on track and graduate, but the lack of support is making that impossible. I’ve maintained a 4.0 GPA and stayed committed through personal setbacks, but this has been frustrating and discouraging. At this point, I’m seriously considering transferring. The school claims to support adult learners, but that hasn’t been my experience. If you’re looking for responsiveness or structure, I’d be cautious.
Overall it’s okay, mostly housing issues and people not really caring what we have to say make it not a great choice for someone.
I am attending to start July 14th. I am transferring in credits from Navarro County Community College and Gateway Community College.

So i am actually not a student at William woods yet. I have applied been accepted and done all of that jazz I just need this next school year to start for it to become official. However many of my friends go to Williams woods and throughout my senior year in high school I have asked multiple times what their experience was like and it was all good things. My mother also went to William woods when I was in elementary school so I have been very connected since then. Experienceing that first hand was also nice as I got to talk to some of the people on campus who weren't my friends. Overall an amazing experience anytime I went, in all aspects
The staff is amazing. I like how quickly the teachers and advisors respond. They are very knowledgeable and understanding.

I'm an equestrian here at WWU, the dressage instructor and Hunter/Jumper instructor (Bailey McCallum, and Erin Cardea) have MAJOR personality and professionalism issues. Erin Cardea at one time put her hands on my to physically move m body without consent because "I was not moving fast enough", she also has a favorite joke of "too bad you're a student cause I wish I could fire you".

I like how small the campus is. There is always someone you have interacted with or know wherever you go on campus. A lot of the professors are open and willing to help students especially during office hours. There are events going on at campus almost daily and so there's always something for you to be doing.

Where do I begin. Living on campus in a dorm that you have to share a bathroom with everyone is disgusting and there is no one around on the weekends to help with that. Second, the food is horrible and real chefs needs to be hired for the price we pay for a meal plan. Other schools that I am considering next year have cooler options like Chic Filet, chipotle, and other things within the campus or they have designed a food court with other options. I play athletics and an additional turf field for practice would be better so we can practice outside of practice. The professors are pretty hard especially being a freshman you're still trying to navigate college but miss an assignment and theres no grace

The teachers here are great but I can’t in good consciousness recommend the school unless you’re commuter. The life here sucks. Most of the dorms are awful and only allowed to continue to exist because supposedly they historic which means they just stink and refused to get any updates. Even one of the newer dorms doesn’t have hot water half the time. The food here is so awful and they keep trying to do Mexican even though they can’t do it. They also got rid of the lead point scholarships which were great and one of the only reasons I came to the school for football. The football program sucks and has literally made me think lower of the school. A lot of resources are going to a program that no one really cares for. You can go to literally any other school for football, but you can’t go to every other school for horses and ASL. For the past few years, there've been a lot of fake service animals that I’ve literally pooped in front of my door and no one seemed to care.

I have enjoyed my time at WWU. I have met a lot of friends through activities, throwing for track and my sorority. I have also been thankful for professors who have helped me during struggles with classes so I could maintain better grades.
The only part of campus life is the food quality. It isn't bad, but it could use some improvements. That's probably common at most college campuses.

I really love it, I feel like I’m at home but I’m not. Some things I wish can be change or the bird poop that you have to walk through campus around. I do like how the campus got a nice pond in the middle of it. Implementing innovative teaching methods such as flipped classrooms, project-based learning, and interactive online platforms to engage students actively in their learning. I will like to see more inclusive campus environment that celebrates diversity, equity, and inclusion, and supports students from diverse backgrounds. As well Establishing effective feedback mechanisms such as surveys and focus groups to continuously assess and improve educational quality and student satisfaction. another note Providing ongoing professional development opportunities for faculty and staff to stay current with educational trends and best practices. To sum it up Leveraging technology effectively for virtual learning, simulations, and digital resources that enhance both teaching and learning.

This university is what you make it. You will get out what you put in, if you never leave your dorm or go home every weekend you're never going to get the full experience. The campus hasn't been updated in 20 years, and the campus buildings reflect that. The food is phenomenal for the campus size, and the folks in the dining hall will actively go out of their way to ensure you are satisfied with your meal, you have to talk to them and tell them what you're looking for. The Greek life situation is horrendous, at times, it feels like the directors of campus life and their bosses are trying to strangle and kill Greek life, especially fraternities, on campus. Some people want to see the Greek life system succeed, which makes sense as people in Greek life are significantly more likely to finish all 4 years, but it's very few and far between. Overall this school is growing and changing, if it's for the better, it is still to be seen.

The best equine program I have seen. After visiting the school 4 different times, just to see it again from my junior year in high school. Having knee surgery postponed my fall start, & now I can’t wait to start in a few weeks for Spring semester.

ai_summary: Wilmington University is noted for its flexibility , particularly through its extensive online course offerings that accommodate working adults and those with busy schedules. Many reviews highlight the dedication of professors, who are described as supportive and invested in students' success, often bringing real-world experience to their teaching. The school is also praised for its affordability and the availability of scholarships. However, some students have expressed frustration with administrative issues, particularly concerning financial aid and communication, noting that the bureaucracy can be a hindrance. Overall, Wilmington University caters well to adult learners and provides a diverse range of academic programs, though experiences can vary significantly between courses and instructors. While the lack of on-campus housing and traditional college atmosphere is mentioned, many appreciate the personalized attention and small class sizes that enhance their learning experience.

reviews.text: Wilmington University is a place where education is the first and foremost priority. This is done in a safe environment which feels totally like home. From the staff to the Professors and Doctors, Wilmington University is the place where you will not feel like an outsider. The campuses are designed to promote a peaceful atmosphere in which to work. With various restaurants nearby, you will never want for food or drink. The best thing I found with Wilmington was it's nearness to everything. A place where you will feel welcomed.
Love the academics and the advisors, they help me understand a lot. I was able to change my major and concentration within my second year, which was very helpful in following my chosen path.

I believe that Wilmington University is a great public university overall. But there could be some changes, such as providing more economic options for students. I am an in-state student, and I decided to attend this school because I felt it would fit my financial situation, but that was not true. I also think there should be a more substantial campus experience, such as adding dorms. This will make it easier for students to have a more regular college experience. I also believe that a caffiteria would be a great add on. This will allows students to have somewhere not just to eat but to socialize witch is very important for students mental health.
Being at Wilmington university is a great experience and I hope other people come to this college too. So they can benefit from it just as much as I’ve been.
The online class taking experience has been great and I would recommend this college to others, especially those concerned with pricing of a 4-year University.
Overall Wilmington university was a great experience to understand my field of study for the years I was there for the professors where helpful and fellow students where helpful as well
Admission process was clear and straight forward. Administration is truly invested in helping student succeed.
It has been great the professors have been amazing and they truly care about your education. I would like to improve online class experiences.

I transferred here from a different university and honestly regret it. The way that myWilmU and Canvas are set up compared to my last few schools looks super cluttered and dated. I have had nothing but problems with my admissions despite having no problems at 2 other universities before coming here. I also had an issue with them denying accommodations requests that the other 2 universities granted me no problem because they "don't do those accommodations for higher ed" (which, working in spec ed, makes me never want to recommend this school to my students). I'm continuing the degree because it's necessary for my career and partnered with my work, but I would never voluntarily go to this school ever again.

While pursuing my Undergraduate, it started rocky as a transgender individual who applied under a different name. Once this was settled, I could have inclusive and welcoming professors who were engaging. This created a safe environment to continue my education towards my graduate degree here at Wilmington.

Wilmington University is one of the best colleges in Delaware. It has a helpful faculty, atmosphere, and great resources for success. The whole student body is friendly, open, and accessible in helping all students feel welcomed and safe. The college atmosphere regarding athletics is as awesome as going to a D1 School. Each course is affordable and enjoyable as well. Wilmington University brings about the best college experience ever.
Professors teach high platform and treat test books. All have college degrees, which student are working on.
I am talking classes online and everyone is super friendly. The professor is very helpful and so is the staff. I am glad I chose to attend this university.

This is by far the worse college experience I have ever had! You get what you pay for and since my job is paying for it through Guild I’m not getting the best education. The courses could do with an audit. There are so many errors in the rubrics and resources like copy/paste errors, broken links, and missing deliverables and downloads for tutorials. Software courses have not been updated for years. Some of the course descriptions do not match what is being taught! I am taking the Web Design program and will not have any skills to actually go into a web design or advertising career.

I feel their online course options and overall structure work well for someone like me. I am a returning student who works full-time and has a family, so flexibility and being able to do schoolwork at a time convenient for me is best, which WilmU offers.
The really only downside for me is many of the courses require a group project, which in and of itself is not a problem. However, when you are not a traditional full-time student with many other conflicting things and that other students don’t necessarily have your diligence/work ethic and live in different time zones it makes it difficult to get them done efficiently.

ai_summary: Wingate University offers a college experience characterized by small class sizes and a supportive community , where professors are noted for their approachability and dedication to student success. Students frequently appreciate the beauty of the campus and the camaraderie fostered amongst peers, often describing the environment as welcoming and friendly. However, reviews also highlight several areas for improvement, particularly concerning campus dining options, inadequate housing conditions in some dormitories, and rising tuition costs without corresponding enhancements in student amenities or services. While many find their time at Wingate rewarding and the connections made invaluable, others express dissatisfaction with the overall value for money and limited social activities on campus. The proximity to Charlotte offers some opportunities for off-campus engagement, but a lack of local entertainment is a common concern. Overall, it seems that Wingate's charm lies in its size and community feel, despite some ongoing challenges that students face.

reviews.text: My time at Wingate University although short since I am a freshman has been pretty good. I have made many friends and the campus is very pretty to look at.
My overall experience at Wingate University has been great, the people are really inviting and accepting

Wingate university priorities students and make sure most of their decisions doesn't cause any inconvenience by giving them freedom of speech. They make sure they communicate every step of the application for students to understand clearly what is it that is required from them. Wingate university has a lot of courses for different students to choose from.

my overall experience has been pretty boring so far. Being that i also play football i have already made many connections with people in different fields of all different ages. But i wish i went to a larger school where i could meet many people and connect with others outside of football. Academic wise the school is up there with the best. Most teachers are understanding and engaging and with the small number of students teachers are able to be more hands on with every student.

Going through my Freshman year at Wingate University I learned that everybody can fit in and make friends and we are all apart of a community. At Wingate I love the McGee Center because as a commuter it allows me to hang out and chill and also get to know people which I really enjoy. At Wingate University I would like to see bigger events than there are now so I could meet even more people and have a great time.

I’m currently a sophomore at Wingate University and a commuter student. Overall, the school has a very welcoming atmosphere, and most professors genuinely care about helping you understand the material and succeed in their classes. The campus itself is nice and well-kept, and the people are friendly.
One downside is that the surrounding area of Wingate is pretty lame, there’s not much to do nearby unless you’re willing to drive to Charlotte. Also, if you’re not living on campus, it can be a little harder to stay involved since many campus activities are easier to find when you’re around.
Overall, Wingate is a good school with a supportive environment and great professors, just be ready to get involved if you want the full campus experience.

Wingate has been a great experience so far. There are so many people from different backgrounds here that you’re bound to fit in. The academics and resources are great and besides that there’s tons of on campus activities to do here too. We have street fairs, also a big party scene with the social house. There’s really never a dull moment.
Professors are unprofessional and people are very rude here. they don’t care about their students at this university.
Beautiful, conservative school with a great focus on academics. Not a party school. You come here to learn

Don't ever eat here is there a dining hall especially the Harris one are disgusting there's nothing but flies everywhere hundreds of flies they land on the food they land on the dishes most unsanitary food Hall ive ever eaten

I have been through a lot at wingate and I have felt as if the school has worked against me instead of with me. I have went through extremely stressful and life changing experiences such as SA, a weapon being pulled out on me and being antagonized by the same people I have restraining orders on or no contact orders on. Walking around the campus has been really hard but living has been even worse. I had a total of 3 roommates just my sophomore year because the university continued to put me in extremely unwanted and dangerous positions and then when I began to lack on grades or motivation they decided to cut my financial aid instead of helping. I got in more trouble in situations than the people who did me wrong. I felt as if situations that got reported I was the victim but ended up being the issue or ignored. I am even currently trying my best to receive a $20,000 scholarship or loan so I can pay my debt off to the school and continue to be a student during the spring.

Wingate is a very beautiful campus! As soon as you walk onto campus it feels like home and I’ve never felt unwelcome! Everyone is very nice, from administrators to students. Professors really do care about their students more than any other college I’ve heard about.

My overall experience at Wingate as a first-year student was okay. I found a good circle of friends, but given the area, there aren't many things to do that are close and in a good range of time. One of the professors that I had for my first semester had outlandish expectations and graded everything harshly, as she expected every piece of work to be perfect. The food there isn't bad, but if you don't have a car, be prepared to eat the same rotation every week. If you're a person who likes to party, go places, and have fun, I wouldn't recommend Wingate because it's a small, tight-knit, desolate, and lame place to be. You will be able to find good friends; however, good luck finding something to do on the weekends.

I have loved my experiences at Wingate, with it being a small community of very pleasant people. I love having a Starbucks on campus and many places around campus dedicated to studying. The library is amazing and has a wide variety of studying locations for different learning styles.

The campus is beautiful. They have wonderful professors in the undergraduate program. I am new to the PharmD program, but so far they seem like they are going to be awesome. The program keeps in touch even before the first day. Wingate wants you to succeed.

ai_summary: At Winston-Salem State University (WSSU), students often highlight a strong sense of community and support among peers and faculty. Many reviews praise the dedication of professors who are invested in students' academic success and personal growth. The campus atmosphere is described as friendly and family-oriented, providing a welcoming environment that fosters friendships and extracurricular involvement. Academic programs, particularly in nursing and healthcare, are noted for their rigor and effectiveness, while various student organizations and activities contribute to a vibrant campus life . However, some reviews mention concerns with administrative processes and the need for improvements in facilities, communications, and food options. Overall, WSSU is seen as a place where students can thrive academically and socially, with a unique cultural experience reflective of its HBCU identity.

reviews.text: My experience at WSSU so far has definitely been something for the books. With this being my first year in the beginning I will say I was scared but Winston changed all of that. They know how to make you feel seen and apart of the Ramily here at SU. The professors are nice and understanding when it comes to not understanding or work that you’re stuck on. The food is great throughout the week but many people go home on the weekends so it’s kind of whatever you can find during those times. All in all I love my campus and my Ramily and i wouldn’t change that for anything.
I love how all of the professors are invested in making sure we succeed. I also love how the campus acknowledges the student athletes and the amount of work we put in to be great.

I have enjoyed my first semester experience at Winston-Salem State University. My professors have been extremely accessible and helpful. Campus life has been really great student life has been exceptional. I am involved with our Van and find it extremely exciting.

My experience at WSSU was overwhelmingly negative. The school seems to prioritize nursing students while offering little support to other majors. My biology advisor provided no real guidance on my career plans, concentration, or transfer questions. Whenever concerns were raised, administration dismissed them with, “give it time, this is the best HBCU.” Housing was overpriced and poorly maintained, with work tickets marked “complete” despite no repairs. The campus caters to only a certain demographic of Black students, and the dining hall is only decent when tours are happening. We went nearly two months without hot water and were told to shower in the gym instead of having it fixed. The surrounding area feels unsafe, with concerning activity just steps from campus. Overall, it was a terrible experience.

My overall experience at Winston-Salem State University has been rewarding and full of growth. I really appreciate the sense of community on campus—people here genuinely look out for each other, and that creates an environment where you feel supported both academically and personally. The professors are passionate about what they teach and truly want students to succeed, which makes a huge difference.
If there’s one thing I’d like to see improve, it would be expanding campus resources even more whether that’s additional study spaces, more weekend activities, or extended hours for certain services. Overall, though, WSSU has given me opportunities, knowledge, and connections that I value and won’t forget.

As a freshman at Winston Salem State University, I have adjusted to personal responsibilities, academics and social life. I’ve felt the support from family, friends and the Ram Pride. During this challenging shift, I’ve found balance, embraced new experiences and working toward building a solid foundation of lifelong success. WSSU has afforded me the opportunity of experience a diverse student body, student clubs, Greek life, athletics, and service opportunities that foster a sense of belonging and personal growth. I’m glad to be apart of the Ramily at WSSU.

One thing I liked is being able to exert some independence. Even though I still may need help or ask questions there are a lot of things I have to do on my own. One thing I want to change is having the opportunity to live on campus to experience on campus life.

I was a transfer student at Winston-Salem state university. I did my Junior and Senior year at Winston as a biology major and I really enjoyed my time there. I feel as though my professors really cared about my learning experience. They tried their best to ensure the student there had a good understanding of the material being learned. Most of my professor provided outside materials that could help student grasp different topics. Furthermore, most of my professors took the time to have review sessions and meet with students whom where struggling for certain chapters throughout the semester to hopefully help the student get a better understanding.

The college is great in my opinion, they have a a lot of resources that can really help anybody no matter what major they have. Also, they are very welcoming. The vibe of the college is so great like it’s a lot of great energy around you. The weather is good. The food is good. I just love the school honestly. Also, it being an HBCU they really give you great opportunities to pursue the career that you want.

It’s just amazing they are the number one college to have the best nursing program which I am majoring. In so That’s one of the things I like j would like to see be more security around the place though however other than that it’s great college
The food was not terrible but due to my diet I couldn't eat a lot of it. On "Fried-Chicken" Wednesday's I can't eat anything but a sandwich or a salad. I would like better options.
I've only been here for a semester, but I have found it to be such a fun and lively place. I have met so many different people and my academics have improved since I've been here!

I like it a lot all honesty it’s a great school It’s a smaller camp but I think that’s okay, I like how everyone can just get together and have fun just the college life in general it’s a great experience the community the connections with people is great as well.

My overall experience at WSSU was amazing! The academic scale is great, teachers do everything to make sure you can pass, and the staff is overall outstanding with the care they have for their students.

Attending Winston-Salem State was a great time, but I am currently transferring out. Academically, they are an attentive school, meaning they do care about their students. Socially, as an HBCU, the culture was there, but there was not much diversity. The culture made it too comfortable to want to stay. Everyone knew everyone, which is a good thing, but not always. The housing was great, but on the downside, so many incidents and maintenance requests went unnoticed. There is only one cafe which time to time changed their schedule without notice, and sometimes they barely have anything to eat, I would like for that to change. Bring back Ram Shack for the students who don't have the money to spend on Starbucks, Chick-fil-A, or Subway. Overall great school.

ai_summary: Worcester Polytechnic Institute (WPI) offers a distinctive educational experience centered on a project-based curriculum that prepares students for real-world challenges, particularly in STEM fields. Many reviews highlight the intensity of the academic workload , driven by a fast-paced 7-week term system, which allows for focused study but can also be overwhelming at times. The professors are generally regarded as supportive and engaging, fostering a collaborative learning environment. Student life is described as vibrant, with a mix of social activities, including Greek life, though some students note that campus life is more skewed towards engineering culture. Dining options receive mixed feedback, with calls for better variety, and while housing can be hit or miss, the campus itself is often praised for its beauty. However, concerns have also been raised about the rising tuition costs , disparity in support for minority students, and the need for improved mental health resources. For prospective students, WPI appears to be a solid choice if they are passionate about STEM and project work while being prepared for a rigorous academic environment.

reviews.text: I absolutely loved going to WPI and constantly recommend it to others. I was challenged in my engineering courses, while the school emphasized well-roundedness, like being able to travel abroad for my IQP.
Pretty good! Professors are kind and willing to help out whenever you need it. The academics are rigorous but very worth it.

I am a huge fan of WPI. The term system is brutal sometimes, but if you manage your time well you will do well. Professors are good for the most part, academics are pretty solid if you can keep up with the pace, and the social scene is alright but not great. Dorms are hit or miss, but most are pretty solid, and most of campus is really nice. It's a very nerdy school, so don't expect sports or greek life to be a big part of life for most people, but for me that's fine. Classes can feel a little rushed but it's fine if you prefer more independent studying. Opportunities are great as well. The only part I really don't like is the food, though it's been slowly improving.
Good school, very diverse student population, alwasy something fun goin on around every corner. Professors care and want to see their students succeed.

WPI is a university with passable academic rigor, many professors who love what they do, a wide array of clubs, and essentially nothing else noteworthy or special about it. The athletics are poor, the tuition is expensive, the IQP/MQP system is horribly run and mismanaged by an incompetent administration, and the admin as a whole is impossible to reach, thoroughly unconcerned with their students beyond what money they can squeeze from them, and painfully incompetent. WPI gestures towards concepts of student health and empathetic practices but misses the mark at a systemic level in every conceivable way that requires any sort of substantial action. It's truly an awful system carried heavily by the people within it.

Worcester Polytechnic Institute is a really great school. Because of the term system, everything moves at a fast pace, but that actually works to students’ advantage. Classes are short and focused, so you can dive deeply into a subject without juggling it for an entire semester, and then move on to something new. That structure makes it easier to explore different interests, get involved in research earlier, and have time to get involved in clubs or leadership roles. It also helps you integrate into the community faster, since you’re constantly starting new courses, meeting new people, and building connections throughout the year.
WPI has opened so many doors for me, not only academically but also socially. I’ve had the opportunity to have so many resources at my disposal that have positively impacted my career as a student.

I can confidently say I would choose WPI again if I could do it all over again. The people I have met and the community this school fosters has become invaluable to me. The campus culture has pushed me to be the best version of myself while still allowing me to explore my niche interests all while pursuing my academics.

I chose to go to WPI because it was one of three schools that I was accepted to in a very disappointing college process. My grades and test scores made me think I was better than the other people at WPI and my goal was to graduate as fast as possible so I could move on as quickly as possible. Within a week of being at WPI I had to change my attitude because it was actually taking effort to be unhappy there. The students and faculty are so incredibly supportive and passionate. Any competitive spirit is also incredibly positive. Each person hopes to share their interests and cultures with everyone else, and students want to learn as much as they can from each other. I never knew I had the potential to grow into the person I am today. Those changes are all thanks to me being at WPI.
Truly the gold standard of STEM schools. All of the competence and focus of other schools like MIT but without the overly competitive student bodies. Very extroverted campus for a tech school.
Great school academically with a heavy focus on project based learning. You can really dive deep into something you’re passionate about and you’ll find resources to help you.

I really like the project based learning, its a super effective way to learn that is nonstandard to a typical college experience. It does a good job making sure that students of all learning styles have a good chance to do well

I have loved being apart of greek life at WPI. Definitely way safer and more inclusive than other schools. In terms of academics, WPI’s project based learning and term system are so unique to the school. If you’re a hands on learner who loves labs and projects, this is definitely the place for you. Smaller school with a well knit community, especially among the 1/3 student body in greek life.

Worcester Polytechnic Institute is a welcoming community that supports all people, regardless of race, gender or ethnicity. The community is driven, creating new experiences for the world, state or local community. While the classes are rigorous, students are willing to assist whenever possible.
So far I really love it. I liked how there are so many resources to help students if they are struggling. I would like to see them help more financially as it si very expensive.

ai_summary: Wright State University has a lot going for it, particularly in academics and student support. Many reviews highlight the small class sizes , which foster personal connections with professors who genuinely care about student success. The campus is described as beautiful and welcoming , featuring convenient underground tunnels that help navigate inclement weather. While the university offers a wide array of clubs and activities, a significant number of students commute, which can lead to a quieter campus life on weekends. Some students express concerns about high on-campus living costs and parking difficulties, while others appreciate the diverse community and supportive staff . Overall, students value the educational opportunities and the potential for personal growth within a friendly environment, even if the social scene isn't as vibrant as some larger institutions.

reviews.text: Wright State has such an in-depth system of majors, all of which you can have specific focuses. It makes finding your major and interest so easy.

I haven’t start any courses yet, but I have been to wright state university multiple time,and I have seen the life on campus it such a great experience for me so far, and I’m very interested on schooling there.
Wright State overall is a good campus to go to college all professors are good, they stay on topic and teach well. The campus overall is well kept. They keep the campus clean

Wright State has a ton of potential. It has a beautiful campus with great green spaces. It looks really good in the summer especially. It is very safe and is located in a suburban area. I would love the campus to continue to look into greek housing, and develop the area across from Cornel Glenn Highway. I live in California now and I know that high school students here would be very interested in something like Wright State. Let's keep up the momentum Raiders!

My experience at Wright State University has been positive and meaningful. As a student in the College of Education, I have benefited from a strong balance of coursework and hands-on learning. Completing Phase I, Phase I Part II, and currently Phase II field experiences has allowed me to apply what I learn in real classrooms and confirm my passion for teaching. Faculty members are supportive, knowledgeable, and committed to student success, and they emphasize inclusive and culturally responsive teaching practices. I value the sense of community within the education program and the opportunity to collaborate with peers who share similar goals. Balancing coursework field placements, and part-time work has challenged me to grow in time management and responsibility. While I would like to see increased flexibility and financial support for students completing field experiences, I am grateful for the preparation Wright State has provided overall. It has prepared me well for my future career

Overall WSU is a great university. They have many resources that students can take advantage of it. During winter the tunnels system is great. There is a lot of information, spaces to study, events going on all academic year around. For the tunnels system during winter they could try to make it a little safer, I don’t feel like those floors and the snow are a good thing together!
Overall the school is decent, this is my freshman year so don't have much to compare it to. Cost wise isn't bad according to my parents and I feel safe while I'm there.
It's good in here but there are something like there is no scholarships for international students. I mean there are very less to none for them.

My freshman year at Wright State University was, in a word, a transformative contradiction. It was a year defined by the simultaneous feelings of profound disorientation and profound belonging, of grappling with the expected academic challenges and discovering unexpected pockets of community. As a first-generation college student from a small Ohio town, Wright State wasn’t just a new school; it was a new universe. And while that universe had its share of bureaucratic black holes, it also provided the gravity that held my fledgling ambitions together.
What I liked most was the authentic, unpretentious atmosphere. Wright State lacks the cutthroat, ultra-competitive vibe of some larger state schools. In my introductory courses particularly my University College seminars and English composition classes the professors seemed genuinely invested in the transition from high school to college.
It is a medium sized campus with good, friendly people. The professors are helpful. It is convenient and not too far from home.

My overall experience at Wright State University I would say is a pretty good experience. I do like the campus's scenery and the recreational activities the school has to offer. There's plenty of places to meet up with friends on and off campus. What I would like to see change is parking over at housing.
Overall, it is a good experience for commuter students who are working part-time and getting their degrees.

Great experience! Everyone is so helpful and you can tell everyone wants you to succeed. It is peaceful and gorgeous. The campus is on one side of the road but you can get it seems like almost anywhere within 5 minutes.

I love how the professors are able to work with you and help you get involved and invested in your future as early as you work towards it. I think the one thing I would like to see changed is maybe just a few more opportunities for events because sometimes the times just don't work very well

So far, as a freshman attending Wright State University, I'm very satisfied. The classes here are engaging and taught in a digestible way, and the professors are very kind/passionate people. My only critique is that the advisors here are very flippant. Some will tell you one thing and another will tell you something completely different in regards to the same question/problem.

ai_summary: Xavier University offers a close-knit, welcoming campus atmosphere with small class sizes that enhance student-faculty connections, making it easier to seek help and build relationships. Many students appreciate the supportive faculty and variety of resources available for academic and personal challenges. While there are numerous activities and opportunities for involvement, some students find the social scene limited, particularly for commuters and during the weekends, often leading to feelings of cliquishness or boredom. The campus's proximity to downtown Cincinnati provides opportunities for off-campus engagement, although concerns about safety in the surrounding area are frequently mentioned. Additionally, while most reviews praise the faculty and educational quality, opinions vary on the administration's responsiveness and the diversity within the student body. Some students also express concerns about tuition costs and the overall value of their education compared to their needs and experiences.

reviews.text: Xavier University is a great school, I genuinely believe that choosing this college shaped me into who I am today. I think there are things that, of course, could be better.

My experience at Xavier has been overwhelmingly positive, especially because of the strong sense of community on campus. The students, faculty, and staff genuinely make the university feel welcoming and supportive, and it's easy to feel like you belong here. At the same time, the rising cost of attendance is a real concern for me. while I love the environment and the opportunities Xavier offers, I worry the financial burden may become too heavy. I hope the university continues to expand scholarships and financial support so more students can fully enjoy the community that makes Xavier so special.
I am enjoying my experience so far and enjoy the coursework for my major. I also believe that this university helps me to strive for more.
I really liked seeing how the tour guides really showed us what we needed to see and that we were able to see the actual classes we would be taking. The campus was really neat.

Xavier have great professors and staff members. They are all very helpful and want you to learn and succeed in life. The students there are also very nice and the classrooms are also very good. Something I would like to change is the events. Xavier changes money for events yet there almost never seems to be any events and even if there are, it is not worth what we payed for. I would also like it if there are more diversity in Xavier such as more diverse clubs, events, etc.
I really enjoy Xavier and the WCB because of the real world experience you receive. It allows you to talk to professional and build relationships with them

Xavier University is the perfect college for me. The small class sizes create a personalized learning environment where professors truly get to know each student as an individual. This close connection fosters strong academic support and meaningful relationships. The campus community is welcoming and tight-knit, making it easy to feel at home. Xavier's commitment to academic excellence, personal growth, and service aligns with my goals and values. Opportunities for involvement, leadership, and real-world experience are everywhere. At Xavier, I won't just be another face in the crowd, I'll be a part of a community that supports my success inside and outside the classroom.

I enjoy attending Xavier University. There is something for everyone to know, explore, and love. The campus is beautiful and vibrant with many activities and events to enjoy. I have never been on a campus that has so many joyful and kind people to be around. The cafe food and workers are a pleasant experience always and a breath of fresh air to walk into. It is a home away from home.
I chose Xavier because of their accessibility services as well as the community feeling you get when getting involved.

Xavier University is an amazing place for a student looking for a close knit community. It is just next to downtown Cincinnati which has a lot of fun things to do for everyone. So if you’re this kind of person, just go to Xavier and enjoy your 4 years

Xavier is a perfect school for people who want smaller class sizes and personal relationships with professors while still being in a big city/suburban area. There is a lot to do outside of campus if you have a car and the campus is just about perfect. The athletics are decent and going to the games is probably the best experience I've had on campus. The only thing most people would say to improve the school is better night life closer to campus, however there is still plenty of that in Cincinnati.

I have really enjoyed going to Xavier University. It has provided a lot of opportunities and fun experiences that are very unique to Xavier. They are very inclusive to everyone and have a wonderful student life on campus. Everyday a new activity, food, or pop up shop is provided on campus which allows for students to mingle and step away from academics. In all the university is wonderful, however, I would like to see an upgrade to the food provided. As someone who has a lot of food allergies, the food options are very limited. The dining hall does have an allergen station, however, it is very lacking. More than half the time, I cannot eat at the station. In addition, the food provided is a lot of the times undercooked/raw.

I have finished two years at Xavier and my experience has been very good. I have liked my classes, most professors, and my daily life here. The dorms are clean and updated and any time I needed anything I was able to get help. The campus is manageable to get around to classes, dining hall and dorms. There are many places you are able to get help between class schedule, career or major help. You are given a career advisor and they help you with various question you have and making sure you are on the correct track. Basketball is a big part of campus and the Cintas Center the best place to see a game! I very much am enjoying being in Cincinnati, there are many thing to do and the food scene is great. CVG airport is a quick drive way so very easy to get back to campus, and for me leaving near the east coast I have another choice besides driving. I am very happy with my choice to come to Xavier!
Xavier is a great school on paper, but I wished they cared more about students than profit and donors.

Xavier University has been a wonderful experience for me, as I am a current senior. All of my professors and friends I've made during my time here have been lovely. I have been able to do many extracurricular activities during my time here, and after moving into the apartments at Xavier, I have really enjoyed my living experience. I studied abroad, which was a wonderful opportunity for me to connect my International Business major and French minor. Overall, Xavier has a tight-knit community, with a lot of support around you, and I recommend Xavier University!

ai_summary: Yale University offers a compelling mix of rigorous academics and a vibrant student life , appealing to a diverse student body. Many reviews emphasize the supportive community fostered by the residential college system, which encourages close bonds among students and promotes engagement across various extracurricular activities. The professors are frequently noted for their dedication to student success, providing both academic support and mentorship, although some reviews highlight inconsistencies in teaching quality within certain departments, particularly in STEM. Students appreciate the extensive resources available, including peer tutoring and a wide array of clubs and activities, but note the workload can be demanding. The campus is described as both beautiful and inviting, with options for energetic or tranquil environments. While most students cherish their experience, some initial culture shock and social dynamics are noted, especially for those from underrepresented backgrounds. Overall, Yale is recognized for its rich academic culture and strong sense of community, making it a unique and rewarding place to study.

reviews.text: Academics are tough, but there is plenty of support - you just need to ask. My favorite part of Yale is that everyone here takes their academics seriously and I have access to so many opportunities and professors at the top of their field. , but there are also plenty of people like me who also are very social.

This is my first semester at Yale and I am incredibly impressed by all that this school offers. Excellent academics, student life, and endless resources. The infrastructure they have in place for students in terms professional and academic development is quite impressive. As a student, you really feel like you have everything at your fingertips to succeed and thrive, and endless opportunities to explore ideas. The campus is incredible, such beautiful architecture and facilities, and not to mention they cook great food.
The community at Yale is unlike anything I've ever seen before. People here are so kind. I truly feel at home here.
I love it. The classes, professors, academic, extracurricular and professional opportunities are unmatched. The school has the best people and is so much fun and fulfilling.

A few months ago, a friend invited me to sit in on Professor Marci Shore's class, "European Intellectual History Since Nietzsche." After her lecture on Heidegger, I shared an amusing conversation with her students:
"I feel like Instagram is the modern-day Das-man."
"Totally—do you think AI can experience Dasein?"
And as we laughed over lunch about whether Olivia Rodrigo was "authentic" or "inauthentic" by Heidegger's definition, I realized Yalies don't compromise breadth for depth. Obtaining a liberal arts education within a research university framework, I have the unique opportunity to become both a scientist and a citizen. As a student, I'm surrounded by passionate, intellectually and ideologically diverse students at every moment, which makes thought-provoking discussions inevitable.

Attending Yale has been a great opportunity for me. I am from a small town so I've been able to meet people from all different walks of life going to school at Yale. The academic rigor at the school is also definitely present and I've had to change as a student to best fit the academic challenge in my classes.

I've learned that Yale feels a lot smaller than other colleges, for better or worse. It makes for a more intimate college experience, but if you want to really explore and get out into the world, it might not be for you. There are obviously so many resources available to you, but it still feels supported by alumni than other colleges like Harvard and MIT.

Yale has been the best experience of my life. I love the residential college system. The professors are amazing. There are so many activities to do on campus. Nothing but great things to say about my life at Yale and can’t wait for the rest of my time there.
I liked all the resources that were available for first year students. The residential colleges make you feel like you have a family on campus

Yale was a great experience for me as an undergraduate student. I had an excellent academic experience, studying physics and history. I made lifelong friends and tried to make the best of all of the amazing opportunities I was offered through the university!

Great community spirit.
Breadth of courses
All inclusive and welcoming community
Great gym facilities

What I love most about Yale is the access to world-class resources and the inspiring community of peers and professors. The faculty are genuinely invested in students’ success, and the residential college system creates a close-knit, supportive environment.

The overall experience is good. The vibe is very friendly. Everyone is busy but nobody shows off how much they are doing. People genuinely see each other as individual humans and treat each other with kindness.

Positive and a good start to college life. I found the campus to be safe and inviting. I liked how I was able to walk in one direction and find quiet and quaint spaces. If I walked in the other direction I was able to be in the heart of the city. It allowed someone like me to get a mix of high energy or quiet campus life.

being part of the Yale Young Global Scholar from this summer I really appreciate the diversity Yale brings to this campus and the university as a whole. The professors and students that helped with this program have been very welcoming and have showed me a different way of thinking. Showing us the life of a student and lecturing us about interesting topics you would not get taught in high school, Yale has been a great influence in my life.
I am happy with yeshiva university for its values and opportunities for my future. I am an Orthodox Jew and yeshiva offers me the opportunity to practice my religion and keep up with the real world.
The classes can have more times and days. Overall great programs and student assistance from advisors.
Very accodomating and supportive educational environment. Excellent staff and student body. Classes are highly informative and stimulating, while also being managable.
Amazing way to complete college and continue to grow religiously. The religious resources here are unmatched.

The men's section is done for a jewish student who wants to develop his studies both in the secular and religious sector. It will depend a lot on your major how classes and professors will be. Overall recommended only if you want to really use your time effectively, but it will also depend on yourself, the major you're studying and where you live, and how religious you are, as well as how serious do you take it. Definitely, a place to grow and dedicate in both religious and professional aspects.
I will be starting to attend Yeshiva University's social work program for Fall semester. I am super excited to start towards the career of my dreams.
Amazing university for Orthodox students, great business school and small classes that make it easy to learn!
Yeshiva University so far seems to be very warm and accepting. I'm looking forward to my time here learning under the professors' guidances.

Yeshiva University is an amazing place. The campus feels like an Oasis in the city. The people there have formed a great community. The classes are interesting and challenging. The Torah learning is unparalleled. I would appreciate a more vibrant weekend, with more guys staying in school.
It's really great, I get to learn all day and get credits. I would prefer more interaction with staff

Yeshiva University feels like home.
As a religious Jew- I transferred from my I initial university to be able to feel safe in my religious observance during these difficult times. I was anxious about the transfer and I went from having from in my old dorm to being a new face again. While it was a big decision, by transferring I have made friends for life, enjoying The City while taking my studies. Yeshiva University has a bustling student life with many clubs and opportunities outside the classroom. The environment is always respectful and welcoming. I personally come from a background where the women don’t pursue advanced education, and was anxious about keeping up with my undergrad. Yeshiva University has many resources so that the students don’t fall behind. People often say Yeshiva University feels like a family and it’s because we are. From the security guards (shout out to Ms. Santiago!) and to my peers we are all in it together!
I cant really say as I am going to start my second undergraduate degree there during the fall 2025 semester
Food is pretty bad (run for profit), dorms are passable to good, and professors are for the most part excellent.
Challenging academics with limited student life, but a great place for focused learners! Not super diverse as it is a female yeshiva.
The city is the campus, but allowing me to study aboard and receive full credits is amazing. Learning from different cultures I can bring back to the classroom
